Futurise is a company under the Ministry of Finance, that plays an active role as the Public Policy Advisor to Ministries and industries in shaping Malaysia for the Future Economy. Futurise has been mandated to lead the National Regulatory Sandbox initiative. The aim of this initiative is to regulate and deploy innovations, and increase Malaysia’s overall competitiveness through innovation-driven economy and adaptive policy-making. This encourages collaborations among entrepreneurs, corporations, regulators and the government, which allows to create and expand innovation ecosystems.

The Futurise Centre as pioneer in future innovation seeks to act as: 1) one-stop centre to strengthen the shared development of prototype products between private and public sector, 2) adaptive policy maker to enable lab-to-market adaptation, in particular for local technology solutions, 3) doing business easer to intensify the sandbox initiative by conducting industry studies and recommend strategic policies.
Novelty: First governmental initiative to accelerate the national regulatory sandbox across multiple areas of the economy and society with an innovation and future perspective.
Impact & Results: Example of impact of the Sandbox: https://futurise.com.my/myav

Organisation: São Paulo State Court of Auditors
Description:
The São Paulo State Court of Auditors (TCESP) built a new analysis methodology, the School-Effect Indicator (IEE), which evaluates school effectiveness based on the analysis of factors related to both school and extra-school factors, such as socioeconomic status from the students. Using Artificial Intelligence (AI), it is calculated how much the school has added to the proficiency of its students, bringing out the principle of equity, recognizing the needs of each school group and working to reduce the impact of these differences.

The project stems from the evidence that schools of richer areas achieve a higher average grades in the national exam called National Basic Education Assessment System (SAEB). With AI, considering only socioeconomic data, the expected grade is calculated. The difference between the observed SAEB and expected one is considered the value added by schools to the performance of their students. This value, that is the School-Effect Indicator (IEE), is an estimate of the portion of the grade that falls to school factors, a measure of school effectiveness.

The IEE evaluation methodology allows the auditor to select the schools that stood out the most (highest IEE) to identify the good practices that possibly influenced the above-expected performance. On the other hand, schools with the worst IEE scores are also selected for evaluation and possible identification of the factors that determined a lower-than-expected performance. As the suggestions are included in the audit report, the public manager becomes aware of the notes and can work to suppress the factors causing the low scores and disseminate the good practices recognized by the assessment. The IEE brings numerous opportunities for conducting research on the learning environment, including good practices and effective processes, that explain why certain schools differ from others operating in similar conditions.
Novelty:
The School-Effect Indicator is innovative because:
- it brings to light the analysis, by the external control, of public policies in education to be inclusive and equitable,
- it allows TCESP to achieve greater impact on public management, reinforcing the value of public auditing by expanding the forecast of recommendations based on the analysis of strategic public policies in education, promoting good governance through the dissemination of knowledge and good practices
- it uses AI to synthesize a significant mass of data and find solutions to a critical problem such as the evaluation of school performance. The partnership with the University of São Paulo, through its Institute of Mathematics and Computer Science, provides a new perspective for improving the methodology and dissemination of the IEE, for managers across the country and for the academic environment.
Impact & Results:
The School-Effect Indicator (IEE) was used for the first time in 2021 and it helped the TCESP audit to identify good and bad practices. These audit findings were compiled in a report that came to the attention of state public managers in the form of 13 recommendations that can contribute to better student performance. Based on these recommendations, TCESP monitors and controls eventual corrections and adoption of measures by government management.
The IEE methodology will be extended to municipal schools, under the management of the 644 municipalities in São Paulo (except for the capital of São Paulo, which is governed by the Court of Auditors of the Municipality of SP). To this end, TCESP has a partnership with University of São Paulo to improve the model and hierarchical analysis to include the comparison between different education networks.

Organisation: Organismo Supervisor de las Contrataciones del Estado - OSCE
Description:
The scattered information of supplier's background makes difficult to identify suitable suppliers for public procurement and this affects goods, services and infrastructure project delivery. The State Supplier Finder, providing a single supplier sheet and big data analysis, integrates and validates providers to assess their suitability to be suppliers of the state. With this tool, interested users can make decisions with information from more than 2 million state suppliers and historical information on their contracts, composition of companies, partners, shareholders, legal representatives and administrative bodies, disqualifications established by court order and administrative disqualifications arranged by other regulatory bodies, and other aspects that can qualify as impediments.

The State Supplier Finder brings benefits to both demand and supply. It can modify buyer’s behavior considering due diligence and improving the choice. For suppliers, it is a way to present their record and experience information on public contracting in a transparent way. Further, for citizens it represents contracting supervision empowerment with quality, reliable and timely information, promoting transparency and citizen oversight. In general, State Supplier Finder strengthens competitiveness, efficiency, and integrity of public procurement.

There is a proposal to incorporate more information sources to enrich the searching and information transparency. The current data are retrieved from the databases on public sector personnel, financial system, and regulatory and compliance databases, and others. Later and gradually, there will be a design of State suppliers performance measurement model with a clear objectivity and wide-ranging scope.
Novelty:
The State Supplier Finder provide allows users to access an unprecedented amount on information and, doing so, take more informed and transparent decisions. This tool integrates information sources from various sectors and using big data for data analytics, generating automatic alerts for public entities and restricting absolute impediments.
The digital tool's development took place in line with an innovation framework, agile methodologies and with user experience (UX) approach. In the development process, public actors responsible for procurement were involved in the co-creation and experimentation process.
Impact & Results:
The most relevant impact has been the time saving (from 42 to 3 minutes) on searching suppliers impediments before state contract awards, it represents an investment savings up to 6 million soles in man hours; and also, the acceptance of 84% of logistics operators.

To date, the digital tool is available for use. It provides information on more than 2 million 500 thousand State suppliers is available and more than 300'000 electronic offers have been validated since its launching. Using this tool, more than 20'000 impediments to contracting with the State were detected.

Its impact is currently being further measured qualitatively with interviews and reviews from stakeholder that use it.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:
The State Supplier Finder: https://apps.osce.gob.pe/perfilprov-ui/ 

Other resources
- Ficha Única del Proveedor - YouTube (1, short)
- Ficha Única del Proveedor - YouTube (2, short)
- Ficha unica del proveedor - YouTube (3, long)

Description:
Prioritization of projects and initiatives is often challenging and consists of a traditionally heavy administrative investment. The Town of Aurora has created a process that automates the prioritization of projects through an algorithm that scores projects based on Service Improvement, Financial Viability, Reputation Improvement & Ease of Implementation. Thanks to this innovation, all project information can be found in one channel and the heavy administrative process of prioritizing projects is automated, allowing the management team to focus more on strategic discussions.

The main objectives of the integrated business planning process innovation are to achieve:
- Better coordination of project information across the enterprise
- Speeding up the decision making process
- Ensuring strategic alignment of projects
- Enhancing financial and project accountabilities
Novelty: The project is innovative as it helps to overcome the highly complex and costly process of prioritizing project investment decisions. Rather than focusing on just the financial benefits, the integrated business planning process considers service improvement, reputational development and ease of implementation through a in-house developed algorithm. The entire prioritization process is completed instantly and results are automatically displayed for the management group. Previously the prioritization took months of meetings and debate.
Impact & Results: The innovation is already institutionalized and has been used for the prioritization of projects for the last 3 years. The prioritization approach is now being transferred to the new Enterprise Resource Planning system for continued integration with core business processes. Until now, the main impacts of the process are (i) the reduction of administrative burden on the management group who could avoid spending time and attention to administrative details and unnecessary debate (ii) the increase in levels of collaboration across the organization, (iii) the improvement of projects' execution, including a greater focus on citizen experience and (iv) the enhancement of the effect of the Town's expenditures.

Organisation: Cardiff Capital Region – Local Wealth Building Challenge Fund
Description:
Public services face increased pressures to find solutions to wicked problems, but the traditional R&D route to finding solutions often fail to provide the incentives for deeper experimentation, new collaborations, scale and ROI. The Cardiff Capital Region (CCR) Challenge Fund provides a route for public services to identify and develop innovative responses to challenges while stimulating jobs and economic growth in the region. As a Challenge Fund serving the needs of public services across the region, this fund provides a space through which public servants connect with solution providers who may find innovative solutions to ongoing challenges. The objective is not just to develop an innovation, but that the innovation will benefit the region’s economy and its citizens.

The partnership between CCR and Cardiff University has facilitated the development of a bespoke and responsive model for the fund. This approach has been developed through ongoing interaction between the local authorities of the CCR and innovation experts in the university. To date, during the first two years of the scheme, challenges that address health and well-being and sustainability issues have been developed and delivered. The overall programme runs for 3.5 years and has a budget of £10M.

The first addressed the challenge of training for clinical procedures during the pandemic through the application of simulation technologies to develop new training products for medical staff. Two small businesses were successful with their initial proposals and gained two rounds of funding to develop, prototype and market test innovative products. These are currently undergoing further testing in hospitals in both Wales and England. Subsequent challenges addressed Net Zero issues, waiting times for endoscopy, and food innovation challenge looking at issues of localising food supply and resilience.
Novelty: Challenge Funds have been used to prompt innovation in both the private and the public sector. What makes this an innovative use of the Challenge Fund model is the focus on developing innovations that will be procured by the challenge holder and will stimulate place-based economic activity and bring local wealth benefits. The expectation that the innovation be adopted, procured and scaled up by the challenge holder adds to the motivation to develop a truly useful and innovative product. Furthermore, the anticipation that the innovation will be of economic benefit to the region prompts reflection on how private sector providers might stimulate economic activity in the region. This combination of public (social value) and economic value is comparatively rare in the design of challenge funds and is further made distinctive through the nurturing of a community of practice that has brought in participants from across sectors in the region.
Impact & Results:
One challenge has come to a successful completion. The Fund is currently supporting six challenges that are at various stages of progression. The Fund is currently looking at the longer term strategic direction for mission-driven innovation while simultaneously scoping challenges to target resource towards burning issues facing local authorities, with the urgent need for Social Care innovation and innovation which supports businesses and citizens through the cost of living crisis being major areas of focus.
Ultimately, the Fund will assess impacts based on regional economic and social benefits, the successful scaling of innovations, and will seek details on the economic impact in respect of jobs created or sustained by bringing the innovation to the market.

Project/Initiative Name: PASTRES - Pastoralism, Uncertainty and Resilience: Global Lessons from the Margins
Organisation: PASTRES, FAO, Coalition of European Lobbies for Eastern African Pastoralism, International Livestock Research Institute
Description:
From climate change to financial markets, from disease epidemics to changes in rules, territories or boundaries, pastoralists (sheep or cattle farmers) are experts at living with uncertainty.

The PASTRES programme (Pastoralism, Uncertainty and Resilience: Global Lessons from the Margins) has set out to learn from how pastoralists deal with uncertainty and resilience, drawing out lessons for responding global challenges.

The stories and audioimages on its platform explore how pastoralists understand, experience and respond to uncertainty. It draws on six different regions around the world where pastoralists are living and working in often variable and uncertain conditions.

Approaches to uncertainty from pastoralist terms: https://seeingpastoralism.org/an-uncertain-world
Novelty: Initiative to explore the concept and understanding of uncertainty from the pastoralists perspective.
Impact & Results: Policy upscaling through its partners network: FAO Pastolarist Knowledge hub, the Coalition of european lobbies for Eastern African Pastoralism and the International Livestock Research Institute.

Project/Initiative Name: Spanish Financial and AI Regulatory Sandbox
Description:
The Financial Sandbox allows the testing of technological innovations applicable to the financial system in a test environment controlled by the supervisory authorities. The sandbox regulates the controlled testing space, i.e. with its own characteristics, what is known in Europe and internationally as the regulatory sandbox. It is a set of provisions covering the controlled and limited conduct of tests within a project that may lead to a financial innovation. a project that can bring a technology-based financial innovation applicable to the financial system, or that may give rise to new business models, or different types of financial and ancillary services or the performance of public performance of public functions in the financial field

Three aspects function as the keystone of the test environment: it is a controlled space; it is a supervisory instrument; and it is governed by a law-protocol scheme. law-protocol scheme. The sandbox is a way to connect innovators and regulators and provide a controlled environment for them to cooperate. Such collaboration experiences in the financial sector should facilitate the development, testing and validation of innovative AI system with a view to ensuring compliance according to the requirements of the AI regulation. 

The AI sandbox initiative is expected to generate easy-to-follow, future-proof best practice guidelines and other supporting materials. The pilot will look at operationalising the requirements of the future AI regulation as well as other features such as conformity assessments or post-market activities.
Novelty: First AI regulatory sandbox in Spain and in the European Union - this is based on the regulatory (and innovation) sandbox of the financial sector.
Impact & Results: The pilot will look at operationalising the requirements of the future AI regulation as well as other features such as conformity assessments or post-market activities.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:
Sandbox in regulation and finance: https://sandboxspain.com/ 

https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/news/first-regulatory-sandbox-artificial-intelligence-presented 
https://www.tesoro.es/sandbox/solicitudes-para-el-espacio-controlado-de-pruebas
https://www.tesoro.es/sandbox/cohortes/cuarta-cohorte
https://www.boe.es/boe/dias/2020/11/14/pdfs/BOE-A-2020-14205.pdf

Description:
ActEarly is a Bradford city's collaborators approach to  improving the health and life chances of children from deprived communities. Moreover, it focused on upstream early life interventions to improve the health and opportunities for children living in two contrasting areas with high levels of child poverty: Bradford, Yorkshire and Tower Hamlets - London. In that sense, ActEarly aims to improve the life chances of children by focusing on improving the environments that influence their health. 

Three themes are examined: (i) on healthy places, considering the environments in which children live and attend school, and examining how these could increase physical activity, reduce obesity, and improve mental wellbeing, for example; (ii) healthy learning, focussing on how learning is linked to place and community interactions that facilitate learning; and (iii) healthy livelihoods, considering what is good for children’s learning and social engagement.
Novelty: Initiative focused on upstreaming early life interventions to improve the health and opportunities for children.
Impact & Results: No details about impacts

Organisation: Network of Acute Hospitals, Duke-NUS Academic Medical Centre
Description:
The SingHealth Duke-NUS Academic Medical Centre draws on the collective strengths of SingHealth and Duke-NUS Medical School to drive the transformation of healthcare and provide affordable, accessible and quality healthcare. During 2020 SingHealth has redesigned three services around patients’ needs and improved remote care:

- First, patients in public hospitals can access their medical information and recovery schedule from the MyCare app at their bedside.  Isolated patients can check their vitals, medication, test results and communicate with hospital staff, while maintaining physical distances.

- The second innovation is digital medical certificates, ensuring that doctors’ letters are not misplaced. The switch offers patients greater convenience, streamlines clinical and administrative workflows. 

- Thirdly, SingHealth has pulled services to manage medical appointments and payments into an app, and has piloted two new services on this platform this year. Patients can save documents like prescriptions, and record voice instructions from doctors and nurses. Doctors can assign articles and videos for diabetic patients to better understand their health.

Another focus in SingHealth’s digital vision is wearables and IoT. Hospitals are trialling sensors that can alert staff if patients’ vital signs go beyond the normal range, he says. They continuously monitor vitals like heart rates, respiration and temperature. It uses analytics to predict, for instance, whether a patient is likely to miss an appointment, or whether they are at risk of developing acute symptoms. And it is using artificial intelligence and robotic process automation (RPA) to make patient support and clinical services more efficient.
Novelty: A Portfolio of four innovations that incorporate health by design, the implementation of digital technologies (AI) and device-based application and sensors to make use of IoT.
Impact & Results: Not possible to identify specific impacts.

Project/Initiative Name: Australia Post Information Management Workflows with RPA Bots
Description:
The company operates within a vast network of 4,356 post offices countrywide and distributes packages to more than 190 countries worldwide. As one of Australia’s oldest government services, Australia Post aimed to automate repetitive manual tasks to bolster the workflow across accounting services and offer employees the opportunity to develop their skillsets.

Australia Post’s accounting team identified Robotic Process Automation (RPA) as the ideal solution to improve business efficiencies and redirect employees towards more value-driven work. Australia Post kicked off their digital transformation journey with a free trial of Automation Anywhere RPA and a pilot project. After the initial success, Australia Post partnered with Automation Anywhere to automate 25 additional processes, including maintaining financial journals, managing credit uploads, facilitating automatic agency set up and pricing, as well as material maintenance, which involves creating and changing the details of all items sold at different postal outlets across Australia. By introducing RPA, Australia Post’s accounting services team was able to restructure workflows and foster a culture of innovation and efficiency.

Critical to implementing automation was the education of staff members at the start of their journey. Approximately 90 Australia Post team members completed self-learning modules on the Automation Anywhere University platform, and a number of staff have taken their education further and became RPA-certified. Excited by the opportunities automation presented, some of the members at Australia Post have even begun developing their own automation processes and are suggesting new ideas for scaling RPA in the office.
Novelty: Implementation of Robotic Process Automation to several Australia Post's internal processes.
Impact & Results:
120 Bots deployed
18,000 hours saved annually
15% cost reduction within accounting services

Organisation: Prime Minister on Social Protection and Poverty Alleviation Office
Description:
Ehsaas App and platform is one of the boldest initiatives of its sorts developed by the Government of Pakistan with the objective of poverty alleviation and social safety for the most financially vulnerable segments. This initiative aims to connect the people wanting to donate food rations and those in need. Additionally, there is a version that works for charities (also available on Facebook) to assist people in need through creating linkage between them, the philanthropists and the charities. 

Two links have been given on the Facebook page of Ehsaas; one for those who want to donate rations and the other for those who are in need of ration. Ehsaas Kiryana will also provide a digital platform to all non-banking Kiryana owners who will have the option to open a digital bank account with National bank of Pakistan. Moreover, there is an option for using geolocation to track areas where charities are more active and/or there are more people in need.
Novelty: First App and platform in Pakistan with focus on poverty alleviation and social safety by connecting the people in need with charities and philanthropists.
Impact & Results: On Google Play the App has been downloaded >100 thousand times and scores 4.9/5 in performance reviews (almost 5 thousand reviews).

Description:
Sarawak Digital Economy Corporation (SDEC) has created digital community centres (DCC) to teach introductory courses on the use of digital tools and platforms, as well as social media. Nowadays there are more than 20 DCC and 7 Innovation Hubs across the State/Region and they provide advanced training programs "to move to the next step of digitalization". The DCC and Innovation Hubs aims are: 
- To provide ICT service platform & support.
- To enhance community digital skills & building talent capacity.
- To offer local entrepreneur with socio-economy development & supply chains.
- To increase awareness & digital readiness for current & future needs
- Catalyse Community's Socio-Economy through Digital Inclusivity

Sarawak DCC and Innovation Hubs have also created multiple testbeds to test emerging technologies such as 5G and IoT at its Centre of Excellence. These platforms simulate how the application might be used, and enable SDEC to tweak the applications before it is rolled out commercially. Malaysia’s first 5G testbed in Sarawak went online in August 2020 as the first IoT testbed. 

Finally, the agency is planning to roll out SME Digitise packages through DCC and the Innovation Hubs to support the digitization of SMEs up to two years on their entrepreneurial journey.
Novelty: Creation of Digital Community Centres and innovation hubs across the State/Region to advance in the incorporation of digital solutions and new technologies in the public and private sector, as well as to provide citizens capacity building.
Impact & Results: No impacts available.

Project/Initiative Name: Providing Electronic Information on Drugs for People with Disabilities
Organisation: Ministry of Food and Drug Safety
Description:
About 690,000 people in Korea who live with sight or hearing loss face challenges when they try to purchase, use, distinguish, and understand pharmaceutical drugs. Through an amendment to the Korean Pharmaceutical Act, the Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) made it mandatory to provide braille and code and laid the groundwork for securing access to drug information for people with sight or hearing loss. The MFDS is working closely with a public institution, organizations for people with disabilities, pharmaceutical companies, academic research institution, and pharmaceutical associations to build a digital service for people with sight or hearing loss to easily access drug information using their mobile phone.
The objectives of this new digital service are two-fold:
1. To build an easy-to-use electronic information service model using mobile phone to scan code for people with sight or hearing loss,
2. To provide essential drug information while minimizing the economicburden of the pharmaceutical industry.
To ensure these objectives are met, focus group interview and surveys with people with sight or hearing loss, and a public-private joint body meetings are in progress. The public-private joint body consists of MFDS, a public institution, organizations for people with disabilities, pharmaceutical companies, research institution, and Korean pharmaceutical associations.
The service allows the information code on drug packaging to be scanned with a mobile phone to easily connect to a website with a sign language video with voice and subtitles. Through this digital service, people with sight or hearing loss can check drug information including drug indication, directions, and precautions. We expect this service will contribute to the prevention of drug misuse in people with sight or hearing loss.
Novelty:
The electronic information service is an innovation in public services that ensures access to drug information for people with sight or hearing loss. To implement this innovative digital service, the MFDS established a legal basis through the amendment to the Pharmaceutical Act, and formed a public-private joint body for robust discussions on developing the digital service.
By scanning a code using a mobile phone, drug informations on drug indication, directions, precautions, and others are provided with this service. It is expected that this service will improve public services and raise awareness to the right to access to information for people with disabilities.
Impact & Results:
At the moment, impacts and results are not available yet. Still, the implementation for the innovative digital service is expected to secure the right to access to drug information for people with disabilities. The information code on drug packaging can be scanned with a mobile phone to easily connect to a website with a sign language video with voice and subtitles. Through this digital service, people with sight or hearing loss can check drug information including drug indication, directions, and precautions. We expect this service will contribute to the prevention of drug misuse in people with sight or hearing loss.
The government can ensure that the service is reflective of demands from both users and stakeholders as the MFDS works closely with the public-private joint body. This will have a significant impact on strengthening public services and raise awareness in rights of people with disabilities.

Description:
The provision of European funding for economic recovery after the pandemic favored the provision of significant aid to citizens that needed to be managed quickly to ensure financial sustainability. Previously, this was done manually, which caused difficulties in resolving them in due time and form. To counter this, the Digital Agency of Andalusia (ADA), through the Intelligent Automation Unit (UAI) developed an aid distribution automation process to achieve efficiency and transparency in the public services of the Andalusian Regional Government.

Thanks to this automated process, citizens can obtain aid more quickly, given that the agile resolution of the process allows distributing the entire budget in the adequate manner and within the appropriate timeframe. Thanks to the new model, the Andalusian administration has greater precision, as it can also avoid human errors, and efficiency in the most routine administrative tasks. This allows to delegate repetitive tasks so that the public employee can focus on improving the citizen's experience.

Robotization in aid management is also complemented by an IDP (intelligent document processing) system for the evaluation of documentation requirements, which allows the processes to be fully automated, without the need for the intervention of external companies or third parties, strengthening the security of the process and reducing the associated costs.
Novelty:
The Andalusian Digital Agency makes an innovative use of robotization technologies and intelligent document processing (IDP), articulating a new management model based on the reuse of components for greater scalability, reliability and increased performance. It contributes to the motivation of civil servants by reducing monotonous tasks, allowing them to focus on more valuable activities, based on their specific knowledge. It is a work pattern that supports increased capacity in the face of peaks of activity and is a non-invasive technology compatible with current systems.
The aid management allows to homogenize manual processing, minimize start-up time and optimize costs. The manual work is replaced by the robotization, resulting in more efficient and error-free processes. It is not just about the application of a transforming technology, but also the consolidation of a centralized model for the entire Andalusian Administration that reuses it for an agile development of new public services.
Lastly, many benefits are provided to citizens, that gain greater simplicity and accessibility to the public service of aid distribution.
Impact & Results:
The robotization process of aid is already working for strategic sectors. In total, more than 800,000 operations have been managed so far for 9 projects. Specifically, these would be the following:
In the area of employment, more than 400,000 applications have been processed:
- The Solvency Plan processed 100,000 applications.
- 70,000 applications for direct aid to the self-employed were processed, and 46,000 applications for aid for the payment of the self-employed quota.
- For the rental of premises, 16,000 applications processed.
- In the hospitality sector, more than 100,000 applications have been processed to date to reactivate and maintain their activity.
- 70,000 more applications processed for aid to companies in ERTE.
Also, more than 3,300 aids for the promotion of the commerce sector have been automated, and more than 360 applications for transport aids have been processed.

Description:
Fuse Classroom is an AI-enabled education platform brought to the users by Fusemachines. Its goal is to transform the delivery of education and to democratize AI on that field. In that sense, the platform helps transform universities and colleges into online learning institutions by enabling online-live classroom experiences, tutoring powered by AI, student progress tracking, and more AI features for all besides.

The virtual classroom can inform teachers how students in their classes are doing, what material they’re struggling with, and what they should study to improve. It’s being sold in some markets for as cheap as a dollar, and students loge half a million minutes a day on average across all its platforms. Furthermore, the platform brings high-quality AI-education programs as well, so that the users can learn how to build AI themselves.
Novelty: An AI-enabled education platform to transform learning institutions by integrating the student, teacher and mentor experiences and track their interaction and progress.
Impact & Results:
- 40+ AI Patents for education and technology. 
- 12+ Languages Spoken applied to the systems 
More information about the implementation in the classroom: https://fuseclassroom.com/platform/student-information-system

Project/Initiative Name: Community Acquired Pneumonia Predictive Engine - CAPE
Description:
CAPE is an AI-enabled tool that can predict the severity of pneumonia in patients, including COVID-19 patients, based on a chest x-ray image. This Smart Health AI predictive engine enables closer monitoring and treatment of patients with severe pneumonia for improved patient outcomes through timely triaging and treatment.

The tool was co-developed by a multi-disciplinary team and equipped with the capabilities to utilise a single end-to-end machine learning model. Using more than 3,000 CXR images and 200,000 data points including lab results and clinical history, CAPE was trained to generate a score based on indicators of pneumonia severity from CXR images: a) low-risk pneumonia with anticipated short inpatient hospitalization (2 days or less), b) the risk of mortality (death), c) the risk of requiring critical care support (ICU). The risk score generated by CAPE can serve as a decision support for doctors, so that patients who are likely to require critical care can be more closely monitored and treated. 

Initial validation tests at CGH shows that CAPE has an approximate accuracy of 80% in predicting severe pneumonia. This is comparable to traditional pneumonia severity tools that are scored manually
Novelty: AI-enabled tool that can predict the severity of pneumonia in patients, including COVID-19 patients, based on a chest x-ray image
Impact & Results: CAPE has an approximate accuracy of 80% in predicting severe pneumonia and other lung-related hazards.

Project/Initiative Name: BOOSTER: Broadband Observatory Overview Shared with Territorial authorities
Description:
Ultrabroadband is now an essential requirement for citizens to stay connected and for businesses to be competitive on the market. For this reason and to counter the digital divide that affects remote areas, Emilia-Romagna’s Digital Agenda has structured a co-design process with local public administrators in order to offer a complete, simple and integrated tool to the community: the first and only Observatory on ultra-broadband in Italy. Within the framework of the Thematic Communities, sites of co-learning and co-construction activities, there was a perceived need to access information related to ultra-broadband in order to understand present and future opportunities in their territory and thus orientate future policies.

The co-design phase of the Observatory was concluded in May 2022 with the involvement and participation of key stakeholders, such as Mayors, Deputy Mayors, operational technicians, and citizens. The participatory process was structured with the objective of optimising the usability and quality of the Observatory thanks to the feedbacks from testers. The suggestions were collected through the holding of four plenary meetings and the successful completion of a questionnaire structured in different sections: Data Quality, Data Representation, Tool Usability, Main Functionality, User Experience. 20 testers among Mayors, Deputy Mayors and operational technicians and a total of 476,485 citizens participated in the co-design process.

The Observatory allows institutions, citizens and businesses to consult data on the actual state of connectivity in every civic number across the region. The data represented come from official sources, both governmental and private telecommunications operators. Emilia-Romagna’s Digital Agenda is already in the act of bringing the Thematic Communities together again to optimize the current version of the Observatory with new ideas and evolutionary changes with a new co-desing process.
Novelty: The project's started from the needs identified in the Thematic Communities, an innovative forum for co-learning and co-construction. Further, with the mission of mapping ultra-broadband, a participatory process was held involving public actors, technical experts, and citizens. Differently from other similar projects, the data are gathered from many different sources.
Impact & Results: The Observatory was published online in July 2022. To date, there were almost 2000 accesses to the tool and 100 warnings. The team is currently in evaluation phase willing to understand what and how to optimise the tool's functions, along with other suggestions for improvement. Another co-design process is being organised.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:
The BOOSTER tool: https://osscon.lepida.it 
Description of the tool: https://digitale.regione.emilia-romagna.it/emilia-romagna-in-connessione/osservatorio-della-connettivita 
Presentation: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dkqw43k3aCU and Reti, connettività e banda ultralarga — Agenda Digitale — dell'Emilia-Romagna (regione.emilia-romagna.it)

Description:
Barev Balik is a digital and AI-based helper platform of information and tools, created for parents that covers the stages from the desire to conceive to the children turning six. The project brings together healthcare experts, e-health data, and the mobile ID technology of to develop a database, understand user interaction, and monitor and evaluate the progress of the project. Additionally, findings from the project will turn into policy recommendations.

The platform acts too as one-stop-shop for different services besides maternal health information. For example, a user would be informed whether you they are a beneficiary of any program or not, or what kind of financial support they can claim. Similarly, would receive reminders about vaccination times according to the national immunization program. The platform creates data about the first journey of its citizens. 

The platform is part of the "Hello, Balik " project, which is implemented by the UNDP office in Armenia and the Ministry of Health. It is planned to modernize the public healthcare system in Armenia, to improve the quality and availability of services and information provided in the field of reproductive and maternal and child health, as well as to promote the culture of fact-based decision-making.
Novelty: First digital and AI-based helper to plan, inform and support (soon-to-be) families based on the journey of conception, birth and early childhood.
Impact & Results:
Still under implementation, the platform acts too as one-stop-shop for different services besides maternal health information, and creates data about the first journey of its citizens. 

BarevBalik platform has over 20,000 website visitors and around 500 application downloads. The overall number of registered users are around 2,200.

Organisation: Tourism Committee of the Republic of Armenia
Novelty: An artificial intelligence (AI) tool to help improve the Armenia’s emerging tourism industry by gathering real-time perceptions of services and popular sites to bring evidence into policy-making. Also the first AI-based tool for tourism - as per their knowledge.
Impact & Results: The platform visualizes the results of sentiment analysis of over 174,000 touristic reviews about Armenia, scraped from Booking.com, TripAdvisor and Facebook.com, with disaggregation of 25 detailed aspects per 4 categories.

Description:
SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) drives and coordinates the implementation of the national SkillsFuture movement and promotes lifelong learning through the pursuit of digital skills mastery, and strengthens the ecosystem of quality education and training in Singapore. It plays a key role in the quality assurance for private education institutions and adult training centres. At the same time, ensures high quality, industry-relevant training, as well as in continuing education and training (CET) and pre-employment training (PET). 

The agency's platform has redesigned the jobs supply and processes to adapt to a pandemic-hit economy. This meant streamlining manpower, creating new roles, and implementing new tech. For example, traineeships and mid-career switchers can benefit from these programs and gain from future-forward employment agreements when business conditions stabilize.
Novelty: Initiative that coordinates the implementation of the national SkillsFuture movement and promotes lifelong learning through the pursuit of digital skills mastery, and strengthens the ecosystem of quality education and training in Singapore
Impact & Results: The government created 100,000 jobs, attachments and training opportunities by the end of 2020 with WSG’s support. It has increased the number of jobs by bringing forward hiring plans in the public sector, and is reskilling workers in the hardest-hit industries: retail, tourism and aviation.

Description:
MedeINN arose from the need to innovate in the government sector. Under Innovative State premises, the municipality seek to connect the challenges of the Mayor's Office with entrepreneurs and researchers' capacities. This has been achieved after a redesign of a Public Procurement for Innovation methodology, which enables Open Innovation in the government sector.

MedeINN´s methodology begins with the construction and consolidation of databases with challenges faced by the different organization areas. Subsequently, challenges are prioritized based on different variables: available data, available equipment, available resource, possibility of a solution through technology. Prioritized challenges are launched through an open innovation call through the MedeINN’s platform. Research groups, teams of innovators and start ups can present their technology-mediated solution proposals at these calls. Some proposals are chosen and they participate in a follow-up and prototyping phase to guarantee solution's novelty. Finally, monitoring phase results are evaluated in field and winners execute their solution in a monitored pilot.

Currently, MedeINN has carried out innovation projects in the following areas: connectivity, mobility, zero paper, management of urban green areas, solid waste, and prevention of teenage pregnancy. Seeking to take the laboratory to a greater dimension, the municipality will launch its Regulatory Design Center, with the aim of designing regulatory instruments that facilitate new technologies implementation in the government sector, institutionalising and strengthening MedeINN.
Novelty: MedeINN's mission-oriented approach bolster innovation, creating the possibility for adoption and commercialization of new technologies. It supports digital government with an innovative procurement methodology that secures that the winners are those with the greatest impact and capacity to improve citizen's experience.
Impact & Results: MedeINN has launched 4 open innovation process with the objective of solving 11 public innovation challenges. The results of the solutions found are: 350 citizens connected to internet in rural areas where never have arrived connection before, between 19 and 35 tons of paper saved per year, 33 parking lots with IoT sensor and data analytics to make intelligent decisions, 1 predictive model that optimize pruning of green area. The municipality is continuously trying to improve the methodology through pilot tests, and evaluations with technical analysis, interviews, and surveys.

Project/Initiative Name: Young Entrepreneurship Mentors - Education to Learn and Transform
Organisation: Federal Institute of Education, Science and Technology of São Paulo IFSP Campus Registro
Description:
Young Entrepreneurship Mentors is an education project to disseminate the culture of innovative and impact entrepreneurship through agile methodologies in order to empower students from integrated high school in the Federal Network of Science and Technology Education to undertake sustainable solutions for their community through science, technology, and innovation.

So far, about a thousand students from technical courses have gone through the project. The project's actions address the students' families as well, who are involved in the process of identifying the problem, ideas for the solution, and market research. At the end of the project, a business expo is held. In this final activity, family members are also invited to participate, and students present their projects to a panel of local investors and entrepreneurs.

The project has been taking place since 2018 on the Registro campus in São Paulo's Vale do Ribeira, the region with the most preserved Atlantic Forest continuum and the lowest economic development index, with little attraction to attract young talent to the region. All planned actions are based on the vision of multiplying active entrepreneurial and innovation methodologies through peer mentoring, that is, the formation of a team of young entrepreneurship mentors who offer mentoring to other young students and teenagers in the development of entrepreneurial projects, stimulating them to the business and technological world, promoting exchanges and mutual enrichment among peers.
Novelty: The project empowers students to develop solutions for their community through science, technology and innovation, breaking down barriers and making connections between students and their community. More than 7 business convergences have already been held. The main objective is to spread the culture of innovative and impact entrepreneurship through innovative and inclusive methodologies in the entrepreneurial process, with the help of mentoring teams formed by young teenagers. All planned actions are based on the vision of multiplying active entrepreneurial and innovation methodologies through peer mentoring.
Impact & Results: This project succesfully led to the training of 1000 students in the federal network, the creation 5 companies, the development of organizational innovation projects with 5 companies in the region, the accomplishment of 7 business meetings. The project received support from more than 4000 entrepreneurs. The project successfully strengthened inclusion as 70% of the students who were scholarship holders in the Young Mentors project.

Organisation: Accessibility Enabling (A11y) Team (Government Technology Agency of Singapore)
Description:
Purple HATS is a customisable, automated web accessibility testing tool that allows software development teams to find and fix accessibility problems, to ensure all users have access to inclusive digital services, especially the elderly and persons with disabilities (PWDs). It is an open-sourced solution to automate periodic tasks and the testing of government digital services.

Purple HATS runs a bot that scans through webpages and analyses the markup language that displays webpages. For example, the bot will check whether buttons are properly labelled and how those buttons are read by screen readers and whether pictures or images are labelled correctly, with alternate text (alt text) provided. The bot then scores the impact of the identified accessibility issue using the open-sourced AXE accessibility testing engin. Purple HATS results are compiled in JSON format, after which a HTML report is generated. 

The scoring system has been augmented with a local factor that allows for more fine-grained categorisation of accessibility problems, reflecting whether an accessibility issue is regarded as an existing functional issue by elderly and PWD users in each contextual inquiry. Thus, the results generated by Purple HATS enables software development teams to prioritise their accessibility fixes based on the specific needs of affected users (e.g. low vision, deaf-blind, colour blindness, mobility, sighted keyboard user).
Novelty: Traditionally, it can be time-consuming and costly to conduct regression testing. Purple HATS automates code-testing of digital services across various web browsers to identify code-level issues so that they can be immediately rectified. It is the first tool of its kind to be developed in-house by the Singapore public service.
Impact & Results:
Purple HATS was first piloted during a government digital service bug bounty in 2020; GovTech used it successfully to identify bugs across active, public-facing government digital services. The respective product teams were notified of the bugs and were then able to rectify them within the year.
While it originated in GovTech, Purple HATS can now be used by any software development team across Singapore’s public service and private sector, as it is open source.

Description:
The Singapore Government developed ‘OpenAttestation’, an open-source framework to support endorsement and verification of documents using the blockchain. With this, an easy and reliable way to issue and validate tamper-proof certificates is readily available to the Government as well as any adopters locally or internationally.

OpenAttestation is part of Singapore’s Smart Nation Initiative. It is built upon the Ethereum blockchain. It enables trusted provenance to ensure that the document is validated against the authority of issuance. This makes it a secure solution for issuance, authentication, and verification without the need for proprietary software of equipment. The fundamental framework was developed by a lean team of six developers, designers, and managers in a span of three months through agile methodology and application of cryptography, that was inspired by the possibilities of blockchain.

Many innovations catalysed and were built upon the OpenAttestation framework such as OpenCerts, tamper-proof educational and vocational certificates, TradeTrust, simplifying the exhange of digital bills of lading between government and business, and HealthCerts, which authenticates and endorses Covid-related certificates.

As OA is open source, the private sector can easily incorporate the technology into its products, driving usage outside the public sector (as Trybe.ID did). The objectives of OpenAttestation are to (i) reduce transaction and submission of fraudulent documents, (ii) ensure document integrity, and (iii) check issuance status.
Novelty: This is the first blockchain or cryptographic-based innovation in the Singapore Government at scale and is also open-source. Compared to other emerging solutions, OA allows for decentralised verification, and there is no need for integration with a public key directory. It contains advanced features that also allow selective disclosure and rendering documents with custom templates.
Impact & Results: GovTech has observed good results and impact of the innovation, purely by product development foray into major or critical industries ranging from education to health and to maritime which is a bedrock to Singapore’ successes. Successes have been measured in terms of adoption by end-users as well as private companies. The interest from foreign nations as well as media coverage amplifies the positive results and successes of the initiative.

Organisation: Swedish Center for Digital Innovation, University of Gothenburg
Description:
The Swedish Center for Digital Innovation, through a partnership with a small software development firm, has created a digital maturity assessment (DiMiOS) that has been nationally scaled in Sweden. The digital service DiMiOS was launched in the fall of 2018 and since then over 400 organizations have used it to assess and monitor the development of their digital maturity, i.e., the organizations ability to utilize the benefits of digital transformation. 

DiMiOS is sold and maintained by the software firm, in close collaboration with the researchers who constantly evolve the underlying model as new insight is created. In 2021, the Swedish Association for Local Authorities and Regions (www.skr.se) initiated an ESF funded project to scale DiMiOS to all its members, making it the de-facto standard for assessment and monitoring of digital maturity in municipalities and regions
Novelty:
DiMiOS is an innovation because:
1. It is the first scientifically developed model for digital maturity to be nationally scaled.
2. It is developed through a strong partnership between universities, the private- and public sector.
3. It challenges current underlying assumptions within the public sector, directly leading to new behavior.
4. It is a novel form of research engagement, where the digital solution pumps data directly to researchers.
Impact & Results:
DiMiOS is now accepted as the main model for assessing digital maturity in the regions and municipalities in Sweden, yet adoption in the central agencies still lag. 
A significant increase in the level of maturity for organizations that have used DiMiOS for more than one year can be seen. The national level of maturity is currently increasing. With over 40 000 respondents, this also translates into increased involvement of internal stakeholders in digital transformation. Further, the e-learning accompanying DiMiOS is distributed to 150 000 people in the sector.

Description:
In 2020, the Buenos Aires launched BA Climate Action: an online platform that opens information on environmental management in clean energy, sustainable mobility, comprehensive waste management, and other indicators of climate change. In this way, it fosters public dialogue and contributes to raising awareness of the urgent nature of climate change. In addition, it gathers activation and participation actions so that citizens, civil society organizations, the private sector, and academia add their contribution to make Buenos Aires a carbon-neutral, resilient, sustainable, and inclusive city by 2050.

This site was the first site co-created with more than 600 citizens, experts, and civil society organizations. Through multiple instances of dialogue and participation, a process of opening and visualization of more than 30 data sets was designed, such as the location of green points in the city or aerial photos that locate the solar roofs. BA Climate Action aims at combining public information, accountability of government actions and civic activation, while supporting the democratization of information and achieving the active participation of citizens.
Novelty: With this innovation, Buenos Aires is the first city in the world to link the open government and climate agendas to involve citizens in climate action while being accountable for its actions and commitments. Further, it does so with co-creation processes, which allowed the government to generate a platform adapted to the specific needs of citizens, experts and civil society organizations.
Impact & Results: As the first result of the initiative, BA Climate Action has received a total of 87,600 unique and organic visits since its implementation. Further, it paved the way for the City Government to develop new Open Government policies such as green employment indicators in order to identify urban environmental jobs and identify companies whose traditionally non-environmental activities could transition towards more sustainable forms of production. This process seeks to identify opportunities for the creation of new green jobs, existing training needs, public promotion policies, experiences, good practices and lessons learned in the matter.

Description:
The Portal Mais Transparência is a digital platform of the Portuguese state, where citizens can access easy-to-read and updated information on public spending in an accessible way, combining data science and storytelling. Launched in 2021, the platform is now a reference resource for monitoring European funds, State and local budgets, and public contracts. In a world often perceived through lens of mis/disinformation, Transparency+ is building trust by nurturing civic engagement and open government.

The Portal provides deep insights into government key areas in the form of (i) relevant indicators that are in line with the nature, structure, and complexity of the data, (ii) effective communication strategies grounded in modern data visualization and storytelling techniques, creating a space for active auditing of public activities and fomenting active citizenship. The Portal was developed as a citizen-centric solution, as its entire design process and implementation relied on diversified civic participation (with surveys, interviews, co-creation workshops, persona reference models, among others), to guarantee that the roadmap, areas, and information available according to citizens’ and users’ demands instead of displaying the information and data in the way government wishes or envisions.

The Portal is managed by a cross-sectoral team from several different areas of the Portuguese public sector under the coordination of the AMA – Administrative Modernization Agency. Includes also sub-contractors from the private sector. It has characteristics of a complex Program and is aligned with best practices regarding governance and management recommended by PMI – Project Management Institute, also using Agile approaches in development.
Novelty: Transparency+ stands out due to its storytelling and easy-to-read presentation, sharing information in a simple and intelligible way, minimising difficult technical terms for the ordinary citizen. The involvement of citizens with special needs guarantees accessibility to all. It shares clear government information with civil society while providing better management and use of data previously scattered across various state platforms, reinforcing planning and supervision digital administration capacity
Impact & Results: The Portal has reached over a quarter of a million views since its launch and continues to grow as new areas and features are developed and delivered. Further, it strengthens trust in institutions, allowing citizens and companies to access valuable information for different purposes, including raising literacy levels or promoting informed public participation. It has raised awareness in society and more reflexion about public policy often featuring in media coverage.

Description:
Zombis Run App is a UK-funded smartphone game with a techno-thriller story that helps people walk 10,000 steps every day, while they're on the run. This is an ultra-immersive running game and audio adventure for iPhone and Android where over five million players worldwide run to the perfect mix of heart-pumping audio drama and pulse-pounding songs from their own playlists. 

Season 8 of Zombies, Run was launched on 2019/2020, introducing new features including new missions, and a major redesign to make the app improve geolocation accuracy and imaging. Right now on its version 11.0 they are testing language features with crowdsourced platforms. 

They also have developed training series: "Expert 5K Training for the Zombie Apocalypse. Discover secrets and escape zombies in our 8-week workout experience that combines storytelling and fitness. It's time to become a Runner!"
Novelty: Ultra-immersive game/app that helps people walk 10,000 steps every day, while they're on the run.
Impact & Results: - More than 5 million downloads since its re-launch version 8.0 in 2019/2020.

Description:
There are nearly 8,500 document-based forms published on GOV.UK, many of which are not fully accessible. These forms can be either very difficult or impossible to fill out for users with certain disabilities. They’re also harder and take longer to process for government teams. The aim of this Beta platform is to help public teams to create and publish online forms that are quicker and easier to process and use.

The GOV.UK Forms is supporting a number of partners to easily create accessible, digital forms in minutes. The main users are people working in government who build and use forms, typically to collect information which enables somebody to access a government service. This is a really common need across the civil service. So it’s crucial that they do not need to have any technical background to create online forms.

The platform will be web-based, with no coding required. The platform code though will be open-sourced, and you can already see the accessible forms code we use in the components section of the GOV.UK Design System.
Novelty: Beta platform to help public teams to create and publish online forms that are quicker and easier to process and use based on a large document-based forms previously used by the government.
Impact & Results: For 2022 GDS aims for at least 60% of UK public partners to have published a form by the end of private beta. Then, building off this learning, they aim to launch the platform for central government use in 2023.

Description:
The Museum of Future History is curated by experimental philosopher Jonathon Keats and timed to debut at the UNESCO High-Level Futures Literacy Summit. Under the belief that the future is influenced by futuristic visions from the past, the Museum is conceived as a global cultural institution supporting open-ended and inclusive approaches to confronting the challenges of tomorrow. 

The idea was sparked by a growing concern among futurists that people have been “colonizing the future” with visions and predictions of what it will bring, and that those visions limit the opportunities or possibilities of those future generations. Considering how these toys may have shaped our future is only one purpose of the museum. The other is to foster more open-ended forward-thinking visions—to challenge designers, toy makers, and society in general to create futures without limitations imposed by the past. 

The Museum displays historic objects representing past visions of the future that have shaped the present. The collection is complemented with a selection of new objects representing present-day visions of the future, as well as an archive of past and present written forecasts and predictions. The aim behind the historic objects - most of them toys - is to change the ways in which toys are conceptualized and to explore its potential to liberate future generations from the burden of present-day assumptions.
Novelty: A museum conceived as a global cultural institution supporting open-ended and inclusive approaches to confronting the challenges of tomorrow.
Impact & Results: No impacts available.

Description:
Antikythera is a new program to examine the implications of an unfolding radical philosophical event: the emergence of planetary-scale computation. The purpose is to use these emergence as an opportunity to rethink the fundamental categories that have long been used to make sense of the world: economics, politics, society, intelligence and even the very idea of the human as distinct from both machines and nature. 

This is a program reorienting planetary-scale computation as a philosophical, technological and geopolitical force. Through research, design and debate, Antikythera is convening 75+ leading technologists, engineers, designers, philosophers, and writers to develop new models, programs, and speculative projects that steer computation towards a viable future.

Antikythera’s work in speculative philosophy and design focuses is based in several research themes:
- Synthetic Intelligence: The long-term implications of machine intelligence, particularly focused on NLP and large language models.
- Hemispherical Stacks: The geopolitics of planetary computation, particularly focused on platform infrastructures.
- Recursive Simulations: The emergence and development of worlds in response to simulations across domains, from scientific to VR/AR.
- Synthetic Catallaxy: The ongoing and potential organization of computational economics, pricing, and planning.
- Planetary Sapience: The long-term evolutionary emergence of natural and artificial intelligence, and how it must now conceive and compose a viable planetarity.
Novelty: First program worldwide to examine the implications of an unfolding radical philosophical event: the emergence of planetary-scale computation
Impact & Results: The first Antikythera studio will begin in February 2023 in Los Angeles, with fieldwork in locations including Seoul and Mexico City.

Description:
In World Bank project the standard procedure is to invite constituents of the region receiving aid to meet in the large cities of their countries for a one- or two-day event in which World Bank facilitators listen to their concerns. If concerned citizens cannot make it to that event, they cannot voice their concerns.

Beginning at the end of 2019, the Your Priorities platform started to be piloted instead of face-to-face citizen engagement meetings, first in Kosovo, and when Covid-19 hit the platform became the primary citizen engagement method for a large project in Kyrgyzstan, in collaboration with ARIS, a local development agency, and the World Bank. This method allowed more citizens affected by an investment program to voice their comments and concerns, share input, and raise objections.

In partnership with the World Bank, ARIS Online Platform has been used for several project. Overall, the platform has made projects more transparent and accountable through participatory monitoring and reporting. Further, ARIS developed an ingenious plan where local village leaders subscribe to specific WhatsApp channels to be informed about new opportunities to participate and let people know in the village. In this way thousands of Kyrgyzstan citizens could be heard.
Novelty:
The ARIS Online Platform is innovative because:
1) It uses state-of-the-art open source citizen engagement social networking technology with the Your Priorities platform.
2) The Your Priorities platform was developed further, especially with the needs of individual villagers in Kyrgistan using their mobile phones to participate in decisions regarding how to spend World Bank funds in their village. 
3) One of the biggest global challenges for any engagement project is letting citizens know about those opportunities. ARIS and World Bank developed an ingenious method of using WhatsApp Groups to notify village leaders, promoting engagement opportunities in their village.
Impact & Results: Over 160'000 citizens participated to this innovation thanks to its use of technological tools to support participation. PeoplePowered is working on an in-depth funded case study about this project that will include much more detailed data.

Project/Initiative Name: Integrating Machine Learning Techniques to 2021 Census of Population Coding
Description:
In 2021 Statistics Canada, Canada’s national statistical agency, successfully implemented a new strategy for coding write-in responses to questions asked on the Census of population. 

Fasttext, a natural language processing algorithm, was applied to 31 questions and approximately 7 million write-in responses that would have in the past been completed by human coders. This innovation significantly increased the coding efficiency by decreasing the time and cost required to code the 2021 Census. Approximately $4M was saved using machine learning instead of human coders.

Going forward, we are undertaking a series of research projects to improve model performance with a view to increasing the amount of variables and responses that will be coded by machine learning in the future. It is our current planning assumption that for the 2026 Census almost no interactive human coders will be required.
Novelty: Using natural language processing to code survey responses is an entirely novel technique at Statistics Canada. Implementing this into the process was complex and required reinventing and modernizing the way Statistics Canada does all its Census coding
Impact & Results: Thanks to this machine learning application, Statistics Canada coded 6,933,081 records that would have otherwise been coded by humans for a savings of approximately $4M in labour costs. This innovation drastically furthered the standardization of coding and increased the speed at which Statistics Canada was able to get coded responses to subject matter experts for review.

Organisation: Ministry of Education, European Commission, Officine Italia
Description:
Officina is a lab for innovation in the public sector whose main objective is to catalyse the energy of young talents by offering them a transformative training programme. Officina was developed to address a triple urgency: future decision makers not perceiving the public sector as an attractive workplace; the public sector having high average age workforce and lack of innovative approaches; society at large needing a more modern and appealing public sector in this key historical moment.

Officina is both an innovation lab, a hands-on working experience and a learning journey that allows young talents to experience a professional and personal development opportunity within some of the key Italian Ministries acting for the recovery of Italy and the implementation of the National Resilience and Recovery Plan (NRRP). Considering the incredible opportunity of the Next Generation EU, the Public Administration is today one of the key enablers to change the outlook of our country and the right place to launch the first edition of the Officina and start our transformative change.

By July 2022, 15 young talents with diverse and complementary backgrounds (e.g., social sciences, economics and engineering) were selected as participants in the first edition of Officina. In October 2022, the programme started with a 2-weeks intense training Bootcamp. From October 2022 to January 2023, the 15 participants will work in teams on strategic projects and dossiers (e.g. evidence-based and data-driven policymaking, capacity building, multilevel governance projects) defined by department and offices of the Italian Ministries involved together with Officine Italia and other key partners.
Novelty: Formative and educational program youth-oriented to support public institutions on the design and implementation of solutions of the National Recovery and Resilience Plan using agile methods and policy by design.
Impact & Results: No impacts available as Officina has been just launched. In order to monitor the achievement of the project's objectives, the team designed a Theory of Change and evaluation model based on Key Performance Indicators related to the three project’s stakeholders, whose outcomes will be mutually influenced: the Participants, the PA and the Society/Country.

Description: A multi-platform application for computers, tablets and VR devices , which allows users to gather in 3D environments with colleagues from different Arab countries. These environments allow users to interact directly and enhance virtual learning and also provide access to various scientific resources across the web in the world, division into small groups, use of gestures and interactive effects, and exploration. You can either use our pre-made environments, or the educator or content
Novelty:
-             Easy access across various computers and tablets
-             It is not linked to place and time. It can be accessed from anywhere and at any time that suits the user
-             Freedom of movement, interaction and control during use
-             A combination of interest and fun while learning, speed of response, and transfer of knowledge
-             Gain first-hand knowledge via application and simulations of various operations
-             Saving the high costs of equipment and teaching aids and the physical needs of the educational process
-             Raising the level of education, keeping pace with the aspirations of the times, and creating a generation capable of dealing with modern
Impact & Results: Unclear - not certain how implemented this is

Project/Initiative Name: Training Needs Analysis of Public Institutions
Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
Training Needs Analysis is a systematic analysis used to determine the necessary training for employees' personal and professional development needs in line with the mission, vision, and goals of public institutions. More than 560,000 public employees participated and stated their needs according to Ministry-specific duties and responsibilities, competencies, legislation, and computer systems.

Training Needs Analysis Project includes all Ministries and their affiliated, related, and coordinated institutions. The main aims of the project was to scientifically determine both the on-line and face-to-face training programs based on their priority and make the public institutions aware of the importance of training needs analysis, to better increase the efficiency and effectiveness of related activities. 

All Ministries and their employees benefited from the reports prepared with the needs analysis results. In addition, ministries create training programs and training contents according to their specialty by using the results of these analyses.
Novelty: The analysis aims to identify the priority areas for training to mitigate the impacts of technology-driven changes in the workplace and prepare public employees for the current and future workforce needs. For this purpose, the need for developing 21st-century competencies was also evaluated with the analysis mentioned above. The innovative methods used to accomplish the project resulted in easy-to-use training and development priority lists based on six different occupational title groups in each of 17 Ministries.
Impact & Results:
The Training Needs Analysis of Public Institutions was conducted by the participation of more than 560.000 public employees and results of the analysis can be scientifically generalized for 4 million public employees. 

Training Needs Analysis reports were prepared for 17 Ministries. These reports, which include priority training needs under four main headings for six different title groups, significantly contributed to the training plans of the ministries. Ministries planned their 2022 training programs according to the results of the analysis.

Description:
The Accountability Incubator is a creative peer learning program for young civic activists and change-makers who want to fight corruption and build accountability. It was developed to provide long-term support, networks and skills to people who are often overlooked by or left out of traditional civil society programs. It is innovative in that it uses creative tools, a long-term approach and the very latest thinking to shape governance globally.

The Accountability Incubator provides tailored, long-term and hands-on support to 100+ activists and change-makers (that we call “accountapreneurs”) from civil society around the world. The support they receive takes a hybrid format (online/through bite-size Whatsapp content) and in person with Accountability Labs in 8 countries- Belize, Kenya, Liberia, Nepal, Nigeria, Pakistan, Uganda and Zimbabwe. The year long program provides these accountapreneurs with everything they need- from learning opportunities to communications support to stipends to pilot ideas- to super-charge accountability within their communities and find new ways to solve entrenched problems of governance.

The overarching objective of the innovation is to build a new generation of accountability change-makers that can create a more prosperous, inclusive and fair society.
Novelty:
The Accountability Incubator is innovative in 3 key ways:
i)                Model- it blends flexible, virtual content with hands-on, sustained support on the ground, meaning that civic changemakers receive the support they need to push local and national/global reforms;
ii)               Approach- the Incubator is centered in locally driven, locally owned and locally sustained efforts. It brings in those that are least included; and supports them to make their voices heard in new ways.
iii)             Scale- the Incubator is an approach as much as a method. It can be scaled (and adapted to context) easily as we have shown; and it can be reframed according to issues/context- to focus on specific accountability and governance challenges.
Impact & Results:
We have supported over 200 accountapreneurs who have developed more than 150 new ideas for accountability. These change-makers have raised on average- with our support- often tens or hundreds of thousands of dollars to grow their efforts, with many of the leading donor organizations globally. They have done everything from setting up the 1st tool to crowdsource information on public services in Nepal; to setting up a film school for women to fight corruption in Liberia; to building a local media outlet for verified news on issues of democracy in Pakistan. We measure results through qualitative surveys, check-ins and data collection; and longer-term evaluation efforts. In the future we are expecting the Incubator to grow further and for the networks it generates to expand to the point that anyone with a concrete ideas for governance change in the participating countries has been through the program.  

The Incubator continues to grow- with another 5-10 countries set to launch over the next 5 years. The process is also being adapted in new ways to additional contexts.

Project/Initiative Name: A versatile molecular tool to help global communities safeguard cities against urban mosquito-borne diseases
Description:
