For the first time ever, authorities can process more than 340,000 asset declarations using an algorithm.
For the first time ever data on public procurement from 2017-2021 is public and standardized using the Open Contracting Data Standard.
The PDN has more than 10,000 visits per month and 120,000 visits per year, which translate in strategic data consultation form authorities and citizens.

Project/Initiative Name: Designing a battery of indicators of policy coherence for development
Organisation: Sant Boi de Llobregat Town Council
Description:
With the intention of building on the policies already in place in the different areas of the city council, work has been carried out to validate a set of 51 indicators that make it possible to systematise the information linked to the commitments of the 2030 Agenda and to accompany the reflection of the teams working in the municipal sphere to transfer the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) from a global and strategic scale to a local and operational scale. 

The aim is to involve all departments in the process in a cross-cutting manner, taking into account the complexity and interdependence required for policy coherence for development (PCD), and to set up a monitoring framework - understood as a set of tasks necessary to collect data, define evaluation criteria and present the results in public spaces - as an essential part of the policy coherence system.

This implementation makes it possible, on the one hand, to have an updated x-ray of the impacts of the projects that are already being developed in terms of sustainable development and global justice in each specific area. On the other hand, it brings together different policies, which are usually deployed in a stagnant manner, to generate synergies and optimise resources, without forgetting the possibility of transforming the same policies in order to improve them.

This report will not only provide more detailed information on the current situation of the policies to the technical teams in charge of deploying them and gauging the degree to which the objectives proposed for each area are being met, but will also allow the public to receive feedback on these processes. Currently, the data collection mechanism has been systematised and shared with all the specific units and departments, and the first data dump has been carried out to work on the starting photograph or baseline, which will serve as a reference for the preparation of the first report with the data obtained for the following year.
Novelty:
It involves all the council's teams in a cross-cutting manner, facilitating the incorporation of the PCD approach, even in those areas in which it does not usually work. 
It transfers commitments from the global to the local level, specifying the strategic lines of the 2030 Agenda in specific measurable policies, bringing them closer to citizens. It is a strategy for putting the SDGs on the ground.
It allows the administration to advance in the processes of evaluating the impact of public policies and to connect the lessons learned with the possibilities for improvement in a complex, interconnected way and from a process perspective.
It takes the form of the creation of a tool that enables the systematisation of data collection and analysis, making the process continuous and tangible. This tool has repercussions on the improvement of public policies, from a perspective of coherence, efficiency, impact and transparency, helping to set short and medium-term milestones.
Impact & Results:
The most notable impact of the implementation of the battery of indicators has been the systematisation of those data that are significant for each technical team of the local administration in order to be able to evaluate the policies they implement. 
As it is a battery of indicators, the information collection system itself allows the extent to which the implementation of the PCD approach has been consolidated in each specific area to be gauged, being in itself a process of evaluation and analysis of the policies. In the first data collection, 70% of the indicators have been completed. 
The consolidation of the initiative will provide regular information on the main social, economic and environmental impacts that public policies generate on global public goods linked to sustainable development, while generating evidence that can be used to design new policies and improve existing ones.

Project/Initiative Name: Mobile Madrid - Digital Services for Citizens
Organisation: Madrid City Council - Directorate General of the Digital Office
Description:
The Madrid Móvil application is a way of prioritising Mobile First design, which seeks to promote and facilitate the easy use of services by citizens from their mobile terminals, improving their usability and accessibility, involving citizens and enabling citizen leadership. With Madrid Móvil, Madrid citizens can carry out all their administrative procedures: consult data and access "Mi Carpeta", obtain personal certificates, apply for grants, pay taxes, etc. Citizens also have a direct line to the Citizen Service and can make an appointment if they wish to carry out any of these procedures in person.

Electronic administrative procedures are carried out with the maximum guarantees, using a general electronic identification system valid for any Spanish administration.
They can also apply for services at municipal sports centres, such as reserving tickets for swimming pools, taking part in sports classes, renting courts, or renting a bicycle from the BiciMad service. Citizens can also apply for a place in municipal workshops and cultural activities through the specific section of the app.

Madrid Móvil also provides transparency and accountability by allowing citizens to report warnings and incidents - geolocated on a map of Madrid, with accompanying information such as photographs (optional), and a brief description - related to street furniture, lighting, cleaning and urban waste, green areas or trees, etc.
Novelty:
Madrid Móvil represents a new way of providing public services and also a new channel of communication with citizens. The initiative has evolved over the years to include new services and functionalities, including the ability to carry out administrative procedures. 
Madrid Móvil is an easy way to contact the Administration and also a way to propose new functionalities or report malfunctioning of the solution through first level options in the app. 
The app itself represents a clear incentive for the use of digital services thanks to the ease and ubiquity of participants to chat and stay in touch with their network of contacts in the app. Thanks to the ease and possibility of use from anywhere and in any situation.
Impact & Results:
The Madrid mobile application has had more than one million downloads and close to 300,000 users in two years.
The commitment to digital transformation and the provision of digital services, which a large proportion of citizens access via mobile interfaces (phones and tablets) is enabling a significant improvement in the management of our services, such that:
-Electronic processing is going to lead to overall estimated savings of more than €100 million per year in administrative costs for citizens and businesses over the next 2 years.
The use of the Madrid e-Office has increased 6-fold since 2019.
Face-to-face processing in offices has gone from 85% to 35% and the target for 2023 is to reduce it to 20%.
-80% reduction of paper in internal administrative management.
-The decrease in the number of people in attendance at registers has allowed specialised attention without prior appointment for the elderly in the Citizen's Advice Bureaux.

Organisation: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation
Novelty: Today, birth and marriage certificate extracts, digital vaccination passes, certificates of electricity supply and e-payslips of Government officials have been made available on MoKloud. More documents will be covered soon by MoKloud, bolstering the digital economy, reducing environmental impact, and supporting citizens' needs.
Impact & Results: No impacts available.

Project/Initiative Name: National Intelligence for Skills, Education, Employment & Entrepreneurship (NISE)
Organisation: Aspire to Innovate - a2i, ICT Division
Description:
In Bangladesh, mismatch between the demand and supply sides is considered to be the key reason behind unemployment and inefficiencies of the labour market. NISE, a one-stop data driven matchmaking platform, bridges the gap between demand & supply sides & thus benefits all relevant stakeholders. This unique platform caters for the needs of youth, policymakers, skills service providers and industry by enabling real-time data driven decision making.

The objectives of the NISE platform are as follows:
1. matchmake among Youth, Skills Providers, Industries
2. contribute to achieve SDG 4 (4.3, 4.4) & SDG 8 (8.5, 8.6)
3. facilitate real-time data driven decision making and policy formulation
4. create central database for youth
5. provide Career Counselling and Guidance
6. reduce time-cost-visit (TCV) for receiving skills
7. forecast future occupations
8. track employment records
9. track initiatives on 4IR

NISE aims to minimize youth unemployment crisis through the following innovations:
1.           Making real-time data available for the policy makers and decision makers to make evidence-based informed decisions regarding youth skills and employment.
2.           Bringing together 32 Govt. Departments relevant to skills development under 23 Ministries into one platform so that they can use platform’s future job forecasting feature to ensure market driven skills development. 
3.           Connecting 42 industry associations into a single platform to make sure the HR demands from their member industries can be visualized in real time.
4.           Integrating with all existing job portals 
5.           Providing career guidance, suggestions on business and entrepreneurship, apprenticeship opportunities, migration opportunities for the youth.
6.           Securing meaningful decent employment.
7.           The platform offers specialized services for the people with disabilities - i.e., voice command, reading guide, font customization, cursor customization
Impact & Results:
1.	0.4 M unemployed youth registered in NISE
2.	80 skills service providers under the Department of Social Service supporting real time data driven decision making
3.	27 skills service providers under the Department of Youth Development supporting real time data driven decision making
4.	6 Skills Development Centres under the Bangladesh Industrial Technical Assistance Centre supporting real time data driven decision making
5.	Piloting started by SME Foundation to reach 7.8 M CMSMEs in Bangladesh to ensure Access to Skills 
6.	Government of Somalia has replicated NISE in the name of “Shaqo Abuur” for the skills development & decent employment of 5.32 M Youth
7.	Government of Jordan has replicated NISE in the name of “Digi Maharath” to provide skills development & employment support to 2.8 M Youth and refugees
These results have been measured through the real-time data available in the platform.

Description:
Many areas in Palestine face the challenge of the accumulation of waste in commercial areas, which affects the citizens who use the area, and causes traffic congestion during the waste collection process by municipality employees due to the lack of specific paths that take into account the shortest road, priority streets and waste collection times. This increases the effort and cost on all municipalities, including Nablus Municipality, in which this innovative solution was piloted in..

This intervention is based on a human-centred design methodology to create an application that solves the problem of waste accumulation in the city of Nablus, targeting citizens and drivers of municipal cars affiliated with the Solid Waste Department. A digital solution that organizes the process of collecting waste throughout the day with the least possible time, effort and cost, which helps determine the routes of waste collection vehicles and determine the shortest paths to them (traveling salesman problem) and classify neighborhoods according to priority for collection based on land uses and population density.
Novelty:
The challenge that this solution resolved was identified along with citizen and municipal staff, following a human-centred design (HCD) process. The students were trained on the HCD methodology and the available tools, to apply the process in identifying the challenge, solution and rollout the solution in an iterative manner, while involving the citizens and users at all stages of the process. 
The digital solution is new and was never applied before in the Palestinian context.
Impact & Results: One month after the launch, there are already 180 users on the platform. User registration and satisfaction is tracked through the app.

Description: Latvia has developed an Innovation lab for user centric policy making via diverse innovative methodologies. We integrated innovative approaches to policy making at the very center of public administration, aiming to tackle complex horizontal issues via cross sectoral co-creation in development and testing of solution prototypes. InLab had become a national live testbed for new policy planning approaches with a vision of extending its impact to regional level.
Novelty: The novelty of the project is the way how problem solving is being carried out in public sector in Latvia, which is the most important aspect to context of innovation. It is not usual to organize design thinking workshops by involving stakeholders from very beginning (even before a problem is defined) within the project in order to find solutions that could be later implemented.
Impact & Results:
Some of the results to mention: 
-Over 108 co-creating sessions and 1 hackathon; 
47 prototypes or solution designs were developed that were tested in the laboratory and which had potential to be implemented in a real environment in the form of a -pilot project over 300 people involved in various roles during sprints. 
-fostering overall innovation culture in public sector of Latvia, ensuring proper stakeholder involvement as well as demonstrating the way, how the problems should be solved using user-centric approach.
-Innovation expert network established across public sector 
-Multiple prototypes implemented

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
YTNK TV is a free-to-use digital training platform that supports young people's career development within the framework of equal opportunity through various training programs and live broadcast recordings. It raises their awareness about their careers and enables them to make career choices per their qualifications. Live broadcast recordings and various training contents are currently available on YTNK TV.

The entirely free platform fosters young people's adaptation to the skills required in the 21st century and provides them with quality and unique training and activities.

In line with the Office's research during the platform's preparation phase, it has been revealed that not every student has an equal opportunity to access the resources to have sufficient information about career planning. For this reason, the Career Planning Course is the first training program implemented on YTNK TV to create career awareness among the youth and equip them with the knowledge, skills, abilities, and competencies they will need in their professional life.
Novelty: The innovative aspect of the platform is that it includes content that will directly impact young people's careers, supports skills development in preparation for future jobs, and offers a wide range of training types such as asynchronous, synchronous, or interactive.
Impact & Results: As of September 2022, YTNK TV has been used by hundreds of thousands of students and viewed more than 17 million times. As a result of the efforts and collaborations, more than 90 educational content and more than 20 live broadcasts and program recordings were added to the system. Considering that it has been in use for nearly two years, it can be stated that YTNK TV, offered free to contribute to the career development of young people, has a broad target audience and attracts great interest.

Project/Initiative Name: The Machinery Price Catalogue - an electronic tool designed for customer convenience: no bureaucratic burden, no procurement procedure
Organisation: Rural Support Service of Latvia (the Paying Agency of Latvia)
Description:
The purpose of this innovative tool was to make purchase of machinery and equipment for farmers more simple and convenient. The Catalogue is available as an electronic tool at the Rural Support Service of Latvia website and is linked with the Electronic Application System (EAS).
Electronic tool - the Machinery Price Catalogue - was designed considering all the benefits for farmers. The main benefit - no procurement procedure is required.  In addition, time spent by farmer filling in the project application is noticeably reduced, as well as it is easy to choose the appropriate machinery for the farm, evaluating its capacity and pricing.  This approach also reduces time spent for the Rural Support Service of Latvia specialists to evaluate a particular project.
Novelty: Streamlined process with no procurement needed.
Impact & Results: The implementation of the Machinery Price Catalogue has significantly reduced amount of the work required for implementation of European Union co-financed projects for traders, buyers and controllers. The process is incomparably simpler, fully transparent and clear for all involved parts.

Description:
Louth County Council has piloted a new training resource ‘HIDI’, which assists cross-departmental staff to apply the principles and goals of ‘universal design’ to their local economic and community planning activities.  Taking a ‘universal design’ approach provides a ‘awareness framework’ to align innovation across the varying strands of local service and activity planning. As a result, staff can better plan for the integrated health, wellbeing, and participation of all our citizens. 

The overall learning objective of the HIDI training resource is to raise awareness, up-skill, and foster a deeper appreciation of Universal Design (UD) principles, design goals, and application guidelines across many areas of integrated local county and community development as they relate to promoting health and wellbeing for all.
Novelty:
HIDI aligns three innovative aspects to address this unmet need:

-             Governance: to ensure that the voices of the many stakeholders are taken on board in shaping it’s development
-             Research: to ensure that the resource is based upon the most up-to-date information sources
-             Design Development and Pilot Testing: to ensure that the resource is appropriately designed to be successfully delivered in the diverse operating contexts of relevant stakeholders
Impact & Results:
28 participants took part in the pilot. The impact was most obvious for those with no previous understanding of UD. Those with previous experience mentioned the value as a reminder of the benefits of applying UD in their work. Feedback was very positive with participants noting the specific value of the practices module.

It will soon be rolled out across Ireland's County and City Management Association (CCMA) for adaption/replication by others.

Project/Initiative Name: Consejo Nuevo León para la Planeación Estratégica (Nuevo León Council for Strategic Planning)
Organisation: Consejo Nuevo León para la Planeación Estratégica (Nuevo León Council for Strategic Planning)
Description:
The Nuevo León Council for Strategic Planning is an advisory and propositional body which transcends political terms and party politics. The Council promotes sustainable development and the well-being of all inhabitants in the state of Nuevo León through the planning, monitoring and evaluation of public policies. With a long-term vision, the Council seeks to make the state of Nuevo León a leader in economic, social, human and environmental development, even comparable with developed countries.

Consejo Nuevo León’s work approach is unprecedented, since it directly promotes collaboration among public, private and social sectors to ensure accountability in three particular areas:
- Acting as a bridge between the academic, private and social sectors with the local government, harnessing applied research and experience to make policy and long-term strategy recommendations, advise the local government, and generate policy-oriented research and analysis.
- Integrating citizen’s perspectives in the public policy cycle through participatory methodologies to ensure consensus and inclusion within society.
- Coordinating citizen evaluation of government performance and results using the goals established by the Plan.

Consejo Nuevo León work’s approach is sustainable and fosters a generalized legitimacy in society due to, on one hand, its institutional and collaborative nature since its creation 8 years ago; which allows it to be a representative of key stakeholders for development. On the other hand, the hybrid funding model, which entails the incoming of resources from both public and private funds (50-50), secures its flexibility and longevity.

Some examples of initiatives currently promoted by the Council are:
- Coalición Nuevo León Abierto (Open Nuevo León Coalition), a public and social sector consortium created to promote effective and open government following the methodology from the Open Government Partnership.
- Tablero de Indicadores (Indicators Dashboard), a digital platform with free access which allows the users to consult, analyze and monitor the progress of the indicators of the Strategic Plan in different disaggregation levels such as: sustainable development goals (SDGs), aspirations, strategic objectives, strategic lines and also complementary indicators.
- Red de Conocimiento (Knowledge Network), an effort to promote applied research in each of the priorities set by the Strategic Plan to 2030 through research calls, thematic forums and continuing education opportunities.
- Observatorio de Seguridad (Security Observatory) a digital platform for analysis and diagnosis of the criminal phenomenon and the public security system in Nuevo León which provides quality statistical information to help understand, measure and control crime in the state.
Novelty: Consejo Nuevo León’s entire working structure is based on collaboration and partnerships with the private sector, public sector, academia and social sector. The Council has worked also with international actors such as the OECD, the UNDP, the Open Government Partnership, among others.
Impact & Results: First, the Council contributed to the creation of the Strategic Plan 2030 in 2020-2021, through an innovative participatory process which included: a public survey, a review process with actors from all sectors and a final expert-validation exercise. Then, the Indicators' Dashboard (launched in March 2021), as a platform to concentrate information from various sources to facilitate the monitoring of the Strategic Plan and the SDG, to broaden the access to key information, connect multiple outlets of knowledge, promote data literacy and facilitate evidence-based policymaking. Regarding public problems, the Council coordinated different stakeholders in a State Strategy for Air Quality, which represents a significant effort to put climate change as a topic in the state government’s agenda. Also, it coordinated a project to make the pension system more sustainable in the long-term. Its implementation alleviated pressure on public finances by 800 million pesos per year.

Project/Initiative Name: e-insan: Integrated and People Oriented Mobile Platform
Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
e-insan integrates innovative career tools developed by the Human Resources Office of the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye (The Office) inside a mobile application to increase accessibility and equal opportunity. In this sense, it enables users to benefit from a wide range of human-centered, career and development-based services from a single point, and it is a unique mobile application worldwide. Furthermore, it will benefit from cutting-edge technology such as artificial intelligence and blockchain.

e-insan has five main tools as follows:
1. Career Gate: shows all part-time and full-time job postings of public institutions
2. Talent Gate: enables university-student-employer interaction easily and conveniently for career development purposes. 
3. Distance Learning Gate: developed to equip public employees with appropriate knowledge, skills, and competencies. It is aimed at increasing the quality of training
4. National Internship Program: ensures equal opportunity for all university students accessing career openings with transparent, merit-based, competency-based, traceable, and innovative evaluation methods in cooperation with all public institutions and voluntary employers from the private sector. In addition, the program guarantees anonymity as the hiring institutions cannot see the name or other personal data of students other than their professional qualifications.
5. YTNK.TV:  free-to-use digital training tool that supports young people's career development through various training programs and live broadcast recordings.
Novelty: e-insan is innovative because it provides a one-stop-shop for careers inside public institutions increasing transparency and equality of opportunity
Impact & Results: The number of users demostrated the great impact of this innovation. 3.8 million public sector employees use the Distance Learning Gate tool, hundreds of thousands of university students use the National Internship Program tool to find an internship. The Career Gate tool is focused on job-seekers in the public sector, and millions of citizens are using it to apply for jobs and monitor the processes. More than one million students and graduates use the Talent Gate tool to find jobs/internships in the private sector.

Description:
HighEd is a modern education platform. This platform, developed by the "Digital Finance" Incubation and Acceleration Center at the Tashkent Financial Institute, allows all types of young people who want to study abroad to submit their documents to universities online and conveniently through our attached mentors. The platform provides steps not only for filling out documents, but also for winning large amounts of grants. The information about successful students of applicants is provided directly to the Ministry of Higher Education.

 Currently, the platform aims to cover all young people in Uzbekistan, and in the next stages it aims to enter the global markets of Central Asia, the Commonwealth of Central States and Asian countries. The platform takes into account the addition of news and additions every year, having studied the opinion of users.
Novelty: The innovativeness of the platform is that the applicant can apply for studies based on his total budget, network with international students through the system, and become a student by watching online video lessons from mentors. In addition, the platform constantly provides the government with the latest statistics on students, thereby also serving to protect the rights of citizens abroad.
Impact & Results: Currently, through the HighEd project, more than 500 young people are studying at the most prestigious international higher education institutions on a grant basis.

Description:
The Bureau Ouvert is a community that started with regular meetings in Paula Forteza's office in the French Parliament. Throughout the parliamentary term, developers, data scientists, economists and committed citizens worked with her team on projects aimed at opening up Parliament.

From 2017 to 2022, Paula Forteza, French MP opens up her office to developers, data scientists, economists and engaged citizens to work on projects aimed at opening up Parliament. Throughout the parliamentary term, a community is born that developed several platforms, data visualisations and tools for citizens to participate in the fabric of law and parliamenterians to become more transparent and accessible. Developments were based on free software and open data, to facilitate resource sharing.''

Paula Forteza's term in office came to an end in June 2022. The project is no longer runnning. Is has nevertheless been thoroughly documented. The team and the community involved wish their experience can be passed on to new members of Parliament in France and abroad. A lot of the platforms, tools and data visualisations are "ready to use" since they were developed with free software and open data.
Novelty:
The French Parliament is a very traditional institution. It is very hard to establish long-term relations with citizens or civil society because the institution doesn't open up easily and parlamentarians are usually very busy.
The fact that this place for informal collaboration, innovation and sharing lasted the whole term in office and had a prolific production all along, with absolutely no resources, is close to a miracle.
It is thanks to the good will and the engagment of civil society and volunteers, as well as the expertise of Paula Forteza's team in multi-stakeholder project management, that this initiative was possible.
Impact & Results:
The results can be measured in terms of :
- the several tools and platforms that have been developed 
- citizen participation in different initiatives that could go from between 15 and 30 participants for regular meetings, 100 to 200 participants for the hackathons and around 10.000 participants for the online consultations
- community building : several actors have met and have get in touch with Members of Parliaments and National Assembly administrations during our events and continue collaborating nowadays

Project/Initiative Name: Intelligent Virtual Contact Center Platform - IRENA
Description:
The substantial refugee waves brought the need to quickly react to various information and convey it as effectively as possible to those who need it. That is why Deloitte launched and configured a virtual communication platform IRENA that helps overloaded volunteers of non-profit organisations manage requests and respond to questions from Ukrainian refugees. IRENA is an omnichannel platform that includes: voicebot, chatbot, call centre, tools for task management and planning and knowledge base.

This platform combines three basic components. The first one is the voicebot and chatbot handling basic repeating inquiries. A second part is an online call centre with human operators who deal with questions too complicated for the voicebot or chatbot. And on the background of all that, there are systems to store and manage requests that help operators organise tasks and create a database of solutions to given situations, which can be used as a starting point for later assistance. All of it is online; volunteers do not have to install anything – they simply open an online application and make phone calls, take over and solve tasks, or edit the voicebot themselves if needed.

IRENA was developed and is primarily configured for the needs of non-profit organisations in relation to the immigration crisis. However, its further possible uses are many – in state administration for instance. During the COVID-19 crisis, the need for the services of public institutions to be digital rose sharply, and IRENA is one of the platforms that can be used for this purpose. There are many questions and requests that can be handled through a voicebot or a chatbot. IRENA can be used by authorities but also hospitals for instance. Its operation is essentially the same as with non-profits; the only difference is in the content of the interactions.
Novelty: There are several differences compared to other voice/chatbots, but 3 of them stand out. First, scalability which non-profits themselves can enhance. Deloitte delivers, implements and provide all the necessary support. The deployment for NGOs only takes a few hours. But further development, NGOs themselves can modify the system to suit their needs. Second, connectivity. There is no problem to connect the systems and share data of two non-profit organisations using the IRENA platform. Organizations in Poland, Hungary, Czechia and Slovakia are already working together in this way. Third, IRENA is highly versatile. It is a package of several tools in one. IRENA essentially needs no prerequisite; you just need to deploy the platform, and you can immediately start using it
Impact & Results: First NGO was onboarded only 72 hours after the first launch of the platform. After two weeks, 10 NGOs from 5 countries were onboarded, answering about 9000 calls a day, employing 170 operators supporting refugees. Now, IRENA operates in 7 languages that are spoken by refugees. The biggest impact of IRENA was saving financial resources that otherwise would have been answering repetitive questions and covering this service efficiently. No less impressive is the increase in first line support automation for refugee FAQs from 20% to 80% in 1 month.

Description:
Seoul Metropolitan Government (SMG) has launched the Seoul Safety Income Project (SSIP), which provides cash assistance to households earning income below a minimum threshold for the three years from 2022 to 2024. SSIP is designed to provide more income support to low-income households rather than providing the same level of support to all families regardless of their financial need. The amount of cash assistance a household receives varies by the number of family members and income.

SSIP will provide income support for 800 households for the next three years. The range of participating households will be expanded from 500 households with income not exceeding 50% of the standard median income in the first phase (2022-2024) to a total of 800 households, by including 300 additional households with income between 50% and 85% of the standard median income, in the second phase (2023-2024). All participating households will be selected randomly. SSIP also includes a research study aiming to examine the effects of the cash assistance. The participating households will be the treatment group that is monitored and compared with their control group, over the next five years. The treatment group participants will receive a debit card whose balance is updated monthly. They are required to participate in an income and means test at least once a year during the three-year period to ensure that the monthly cash payments reflect the changes in their income and assets.

SMG received applications for phase 1 participants from March 28 to April 8, 2022 and announced its final selection of the participating households on June 29, 2022 along with households assigned to the control group. Finally, 484 households confirmed to participate as the treatment group, and another 1,039 households agreed to participate as the control group. The selected treatment group participants will receive monthly income support for the next three years.
Novelty: Unlike many other income support programs carried out in and outside Korea, the amount of SSIP payment provided to participants varies based on their respective levels of income. Further, a research is organised to measure its impact with randomised control trial.
Impact & Results: The impact of the project is not visible yet. To evaluate the outcome, the team will use the mixed-method approach, comparing the differences between the treatment group and the control group, using both qualitative and quantitative indicators to track their changes before and after participating in SSIP.

Project/Initiative Name: The Gateway to the Global Market
Description:
At Lingopass, we created training journeys with adaptive and engaging methodology, offering an English, French, Spanish and Portuguese for foreigners training platform with a focus on the public and educational market. We sell our solutions to all central and regional administrations, as well as schools and universities. 

Connecting the best of pedagogy, neuroscience and artificial intelligence we offered to the Brazilian Army and to the National Police Academy (Academia Nacional de Polícia - ANP)  adaptive learning paths that shaped the student's personality type, solving the problem of engagement and course completion. 

The methodology was a disruptive innovation that allowed the startup to record very high rates of engagement, progress and performance of all the 1300 public servants trained since 2021 even when applied to students without any previous experience of learning foreign languages. In parallel, we fed a large database - socio-demographic, personality profile and behavioral data -that began to be analyzed with different algorithms in order to create a mechanism for recommending study strategies based on historical data of access and engagement of students

Lingopass is also a true education platform focused on the professional growth of its students, developing talents far beyond language, nurturing communication skills, cultural openness and self-confidence with a new, adaptive teaching concept that matches student's personality type and allows us to leverage motivation, engagement and results. The methodology is a disruptive innovation that allowed the startup to record very high rates of engagement, progress and performance.
Novelty:
Connecting the best of pedagogy, neuroscience and artificial intelligence we offered to the Brazilian Army and to the National Police Academy (Academia Nacional de Polícia - ANP)  adaptive learning paths that shaped the student's personality type, solving the problem of engagement and course completion. 
The methodology was a disruptive innovation that allowed the startup to record very high rates of engagement, progress and performance of all the 1300 public servants trained since 2021 even when applied to students without any previous experience of learning foreign languages. In parallel, we fed a large database - socio-demographic, personality profile and behavioral data -that began to be analyzed.
Impact & Results: With this, the programme has even generated cost reductions for the contracting administrations, leading to upsell (+25% increase in the contracting of licences after 3 months of contract), renewal and expansion of the scope of contracts, showing the quality of our services, measured by the effectiveness of the learning.

Description:
Marea Digital allows citizens to report local problems to the government, as well as local initiatives that are working for the communities’ well-being. Contrary to similar kind initiatives, it’s innovative because it uses tech to allow the citizens to aggregate data about their communities’ problems, but also includes an offline strategy for the local government to design solutions with the citizens to solve the problems reported, fostering dialogue and collaboration to solve people’s needs.

The main objective of Marea Digital is to identify and prioritize the community’s problems through an exercise of community driven data and follow up on citizen requirements to achieve effective citizen advocacy with the local government. In addition, it has the potential to organize and manage citizen actions of political influence and community self-management, as well as the harmonization of citizen requirements in accordance with local political agendas. The platform has created an offline movement of leaders which are working through a solid network for the wellbeing of the community, thus the platform allows also to identify good practices making them more visible.

To date, thousand reports have been updated to the platform and public agendas are prioritized by the citizens. Accountability and the sense of co-responsibility regarding the decision-making process has been a fundamental pillar for the achievements of the initiative.
Novelty: Before Marea, citizens lacked a space that allowed them to collectively, visually, in real time and with access of the whole world, to map the problems they face, from lack of paved streets to clean water. With Marea, citizens not only have a space where they can share their community’s problems, but they also have an offline strategy of dialogue with local authorities to solve the problems.
Impact & Results: Marea Digital has favored and increased citizen participation and democracy in Buenaventura. Citizens have reported more than 1,000 problems. Thanks to the follow-up and monitoring instrument developed to measure the impact, 9 self-managed activities and 27 institutional responses have been involved in advocacy processes, and 17 positive actions by decision makers stemmed from the advocacy actions developed on the platform. To date, 66 community-based organizations, 22 public agencies and 104 citizens actively participate in the process.

Project/Initiative Name: Catalog of Information on Corruption in Mexico (CICM)
Organisation: Executive Secretariat of the National Anticorruption System
Description:
The CICM was created to make visible, socialize and make known to different actors interested in the phenomenon of corruption, the universe of variables that allow monitoring anticorruption, integrity, auditing, control of public resources and other information related to aforementioned phenomenon. It standardizes and converts to plain language more than 3000 variables on the phenomenon of corruption in Mexico. 

For CICM creation, the National Anticorruption System(I) identified key government institutions that generated relevant information on the topic, (ii) processed, downloaded and standarized the data, (iii) classified this data by taking into account key categories included in the National Anticorruption Policy aimed at tackling corruption, impunity and power abuse from prevention to punishment, (iv) elaborated a variable description to facilitate its comprehension to non-expert audience, and (v) published this data, which is constantly updated, in a simple and interactive website.

The CICM promotes transparency, accountability and citizen participation. It has become a dynamic tool that provides clear information about the accomplishments and challenges in anticorruption policies to the general public. For example, recently work has been oriented to creating a variable viewer for the creation of maps with the information available in the Catalog to ease the way in which information is presented. Finally, the CICM has the potential to create alliances with strategic actors in the sector, like civil society organizations and government institutions, which have resulted in collaborations that go beyond the scope of the Catalog
Novelty: CICM provides an innovative way to monitor anticorruption policies in Mexico. In this way it helps policymakers to identify deficiencies, better implement policies thanks to its data collection service, and it halps the public to hold policymakers accountable.
Impact & Results:
The impact of the Catalog is evaluated through the traffic it generates and the degree of usage by stakeholders. According to statistics from Google Analytics, the CICM has registered users from the United States, Ireland, Ecuador, Cuba, Germany and Spain and, since the launch in April 2022, it has had over 1,000 visits.
By using the historic data that will be generated over time, the CICM would help to develop evaluation athat can compare two different periods to assess policy results also related to the implementation of CICM.

Description:
The goal of the project was to understand the needs of an adult language learner on the learning journey and how to organize language education for them in such a way that it helps them acquire the Estonian language better. As a result, a common agreement was reached that unites ministries and different sub-institutions and improved cooperation. A prototype of a solution combining various language learning opportunities and a new language learning format - Language Roulette - were developed.

The project started considering the lack of awareness of language learning opportunities, the importance of the language environment, the fact that barriers to language use are often high, and the fact that learners’ ownership and skills to support their language learning outside the language course are low. Based on these, the following innovations solutions were developed:
- The Gateway to Estonian Language Learning - a place that centralises Estonian language learning opportunities offered by both the state and the private sector
- Language Roulette - a chat room where to have an hour-long discussion in Estonian in a free and pleasant atmosphere under the guidance of a mentor
- Cross-sectoral cooperation to provide language learning based on the needs of the client.
Novelty: The innovation provided a way to diffuse the knowledge of Estonian by making learning opportunities more visible and creating new opportunities also with new technologies.
Impact & Results: Today, Keelerulett has become a year-round service of the Integration Foundation, with participants from every corner of Estonia and from all over the world. To date, the Keelerulett has been held 48 times, bringing together 1,628 friends of the Estonian language.

Organisation: Secretariat of Higher Education, Science, Technology and Innovation of Ecuador - Senescyt
Description:
Thinkia is the first Citizen Laboratory in Ecuador built with values of collaboration and innovation and governed by a multi-stakeholder team that brings together the voices and views of the private and public sectors, academia, civil society and cooperation, to generate well-being for citizens. The main goal of innovation is to consolidate the relationship between innovative agents to generate an articulated national innovation ecosystem and to promote within each sector that internal challenges can be solved with open processes of public participation that seek effective and agile solutions that contribute to social welfare.

the Laboratory promotes the fulfilment of the following objectives: i) to improve the well-being and prosperity of citizens by working on social, cultural, environmental and economic challenges; ii) to improve public services; and iii) to strengthen democracy and citizens' trust in public administration, contributing to the legitimisation of its management. 

The Laboratory has a management model that involves two instances of participation: the Core Team and the Extended Team. Both bodies are made up of actors from the public and private sectors, civil society organisations, academia and international organisations in order to guarantee governance, representation and a plurality of voices.

It is hoped that this management model, and the Laboratory's collaborative way of working, can be adopted as a good innovation practice that can be replicated by other communities in the national innovation ecosystem.
Novelty: Thinkia is a collaborative platform built by several active members that foster the articulation of different actors of the innovation ecosystem. We implement the principles of the public innovation hexagon as the methodological basis for all activities. We invite other actors to use open innovation through innovation challenges, capacity building and other methodologies.
Impact & Results:
First, the most tangible, is that there is a Citizen Laboratory operating at the national level in Ecuador.

Second, we are putting the existence of this laboratory into practice. In the last year we have implemented an innovation challenge on cannabis and technology where more than 100 people were trained in innovative methodologies, in addition to strengthening the relationship between the actors of the innovation ecosystem based on collaborative work. 

Thirdly, we are playing a role as a trainer of trainers, with Thinkia now being a national benchmark for guiding and providing technical and methodological support to other public institutions on issues of open, public and social innovation.

Description:
BESA is an online tool that monitors, from start to finish, the performance of contracts for goods and services. It seeks to identify and eliminate malpractices by the public sector during the execution of procurement contracts.

It originated from the need to promote best practices, to reduce corruption, impunity and inefficiency in public procurement, generated largely by systematic failures in contracting, generating uncontrolled purchasing processes, directing or rigging public tenders and assigning contracts to companies with irregularities, facts that facilitate the formation of corruption networks to divert public resources through government purchases.

The aim is to take advantage of the BESA system as a public procurement data tool to identify and mitigate corruption risk practices, carrying out audits in real time, which will make it possible to identify and sanction bad practices, making the control, preparation and monitoring of public sector procurement, leasing and services contracts and operations more efficient, which will serve as a source of information to identify the method, subject and object to be audited, as well as to define the scope of the reviews.

The implementation of the BESA system is by means of an online computer system that will allow for the real-time monitoring, control and auditing of acquisitions, from the formalisation of the contract to its finalisation, in large volume. It is designed in three stages: 1) The first consists of the registration and consultation of public procurement, 2) The second stage corresponds to the monitoring of procurement contracts and the alert system, 3) The third and last stage corresponds to the generation of reports, which is currently under development.
Novelty: The BESA system has been oriented towards reducing corruption and impunity in the exercise of public procurement carried out by agencies, entities, state and municipal public bodies, with total or partial federal public resources, whose expected impact is to increase citizens' trust in public institutions, in terms of the application and exercise of public resources.
Impact & Results:
There is a record of more than 2,900 contracts formalised by agencies, APF public entities, APE and APM, for an amount of more than 300,000.0 million pesos, in the Electronic Logbook for Procurement Follow-up. 

Six monitoring, evaluation and information validation visits are being carried out to institutions of the Federal Public Administration, with the information contained in the BESA system, with respect to registered contacts.

Project/Initiative Name: Co-creation of public spaces for the improvement of neighbourhoods in Bogota
Description:
Through the application of the entity's social management manual and co-design sessions with the inhabitants of all age groups in the neighbourhood, the aim is to find that factor of local identity that inspires popular creativity and contributes to the design proposals.

This experience has been replicated in 12 other neighbourhoods and it is hoped that the initiative will be improved, adapted and applied in other regional and national institutions involved in the construction of public spaces in the country.

It is not a radical innovation in the world or in the country, but an incremental innovation in our institution and in the sectors we are intervening. We take reference from practices and methodologies such as tactical urbanism, urban acupuncture, placemaking, design thinking, social mapping and other tools that allow us to know the territory and co-create its improvement.
Novelty: We have modified the scope and methodology of design, focusing on the active participation of people in the conception and effective transformation of the public space that surrounds them. In addition, we provide communities with creative, expressive, associative and practical tools, using concepts and methods from architecture, engineering, design, art, social work, playfulness and traditional crafts, which they can then use to solve the urban challenges of their context.
Impact & Results: 4 neighbourhoods have been intervened in the sector, with around 1.000m2 of co-designed public space, with alternative urban furniture, wall and floor murals, children's games, gardening, mats and meeting spaces (geodesic dome).

Project/Initiative Name: The role of the park ranger in times of pandemic - Adopt a Taricaya
Description:
Due to Covid-19 and the social isolation measures, tourism and conservation activities in nature reserves in Peru came to a halt. As a consequence, the management groups of the Pacaya Samiria National Reserve saw their main sources of income affected. The project aims to generate resources to support families and guarantee their food security during the pandemic.

With the initiative "Sponsor a taricaya, conserve Pacaya" the aim is to help park ranger families and achieve the release of 43,000 taricaya turtles (an at-risk species of turtle) into their natural habitat. The initiative consists of sponsoring a taricaya, donating S/. 4.00 (1 EUR) for each one, through the National Conservation Authority (Profonanpe) accounts. In return, all participants receive a donation certificate in digital format that accredits their support for this action in favour of nature. 

As a result, in the first year, 564 vulnerable families benefited from the initiative. The experience was the winner of the Effective Environmental Management category of the 2021 Good Practices in Public Management Award, an annual recognition organised by Ciudadanos al Día since 2005. The project showcases the value to incorporate mechanisms of proof of donation (which are legally recognized for tax alleviation) with conservation initiatives.
Novelty: The experience is innovative because it is an alternative and/or complementary way to state subsidies in emergency situations. It generates the involvement of other actors (citizens, private companies, social organizations, among others) to support the conservation and proper management of natural resources by families living in protected natural areas.
Impact & Results: It has been possible to support the local economy of 564 member families, guaranteeing their food security. More than 5,000 taricayas have been conserved in the first year of the project

Project/Initiative Name: Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Service Desk Classification of Requests
Description:
Automate the classification of service desk requests (support for users and organisations) using Artificial Intelligence techniques (NLP, Natural Language Processing) and open source tools to reduce costs, optimise resources and improve response times.

The solution, developed by Agesic and in production for more than a year, consists of several algorithms responsible for assigning different categories to the requests that reach the management system.

The solution has successfully automated the classification of 82% of Agesic's Service Desk requests. It is a project developed "end-to-end" at the Agency and has been in production for more than a year, automatically processing more than 33,000 requests. 

Thanks to a control panel, the service desk manager reviews its operation in real time, based on different performance indicators; in turn, the desk's technicians collaborate in the detection of misassigned requests so that the models used can be trained in a matter of minutes using the resources available at the National Supercomputing Centre of Uruguay (ClusterUY).
Novelty: The NLP techniques used in the new solution allow us to adapt much better to this changing reality and the possibility of re-training the algorithms according to the response of the technicians accentuates this virtue. The use of performance indicators allows us to permanently review the results, suggesting improvements and extensions that correspond to the new needs and uses of the tool in a frequently changing environment. In this sense, the solution proposed by the AI frees us from repeated adjustments that require increasingly scarce effort and resources.
Impact & Results: Good reviews and feedback have been obtained from all sectors consulted. It is considered a success story in the use of AI for the improvement of public services with the possibility of quick and efficient replication in other agencies, some of which have already shown interest in implementing it.

Project/Initiative Name: Transforming Public Procurement through the Electronic Catalogues of Framework Agreements of PERU COMPRAS
Description:
The tool allows a significant reduction in procurement times and use of resources compared to traditional procurement, minimises the risk of corruption, and benefits entities and suppliers. It also integrates technologies such as blockchain, business intelligence, and promotes citizen oversight through access to information with open data.

The PERU COMPRAS Electronic Catalogues have a unique design in the world, applying Blockchain technology for the first time in public procurement, which allows the registration of each purchase order and their respective offers in multiple servers (nodes), which ensures that the information is not adulterated. Each purchase order has a QR code, which allows access to the original PDF file, verifying the authenticity of the document. It also has two types of contracting: Ordinary Purchases, (for smaller purchases), and the Large Purchase, (in which the application chooses the supplier, eliminating the buyer's discretion).

The PERU COMPRAS electronic catalogues are also integrated with the financial and administrative systems SIAF (financial management tool) and SIGA (administrative management tool), which allow the reservation of resources of the requirements, as well as the traceability of the contract.

Currently, more than 120,000 different products are offered, grouped in 40 electronic catalogues and 253 categories; in addition, products with environmental sustainability criteria are available through the tool.

-Generate savings for the state: Since its launch in April 2017 until June 2022, more than US$ 468 million in savings for the state have been generated.
-Reduce corruption by reducing (or eliminating) discretionality.
- It significantly reduces the risk of corruption on the part of the entities. It also increases the participation of bidders by obtaining a greater number of bids in the results, and increases transparency with the OECD-standard open data portal.
Novelty:
Apply Blockchain technology for the first time in public procurement. This model minimises corruption risks, eliminating buyer discretion in the Big Buy; moreover, the application only shows the buyer the products (no supplier data or prices), so that the buyer chooses what he/she really and effectively needs. 

It also uses Business Intelligence tools to show indicators that promote compliance with the obligations of entities and suppliers as a "Traffic Light Alert", and makes information transparent through its portal according to the Open Contracting Data Standard (OCDS) and Peru Observes.
Impact & Results:
'- US$ 2019 million traded through the tool (period 2017-June-2022).
- US$ 468 million in savings for Public Entities (period 2017-June-2022).
- Reduction of the procurement process time to 7.4 days on average compared to traditional procurement procedures such as public tenders or public bids, where the average time is 143 days.
- 90.9% entity satisfaction and 89.1% supplier satisfaction with the tool by the end of 2021.
- Improved competition to 33.5 bids on average per procurement, compared to traditional procurement procedures that do not exceed an average of 3 bids per process,
- The risk of corruption in entities is minimised. Our processes are also certified by the UNE ISO 37001:2017 standard.

Description:
The Women in Rural Brazil website offers gender-strategic information to subsidize innovation projects and development initiatives whose goal is to improve the quality of life of women living in rural areas of Brazil. Approximately one million women manage agricultural enterprises and 4,5 million work in the agricultural and livestock sector. 

The goal of the Women in Rural Brazil website was to create a database of quantitative and qualitative information on the lives of women managing and working in rural establishments. The expectation was that such a database could minimize gender-based exclusion in research and development projects in agriculture. 

The creation of this website involved technicians from the Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock and Supply (‘‘MAPA’’), the United Nations Organization for Food and Agriculture (‘‘FAO’’), and Brazilian Agricultural Research Corporation (“Embrapa”), with strong female participation across these three institutions. The first interactive graphics were built on data from the census carried out by the Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics (‘‘IBGE’’) from 1995 onwards, when the collection of disaggregated data pertaining to sex began. 

Women in Rural Brazil was created as a tool for researchers and actors within the public sector working to define governmental programs and public policies. Its indirect beneficiaries are the women at whom the studies, actions, and technological innovations are aimed; apart from society in general, as gender equality contributes to the reduction of poverty and higher levels of human capital.
Novelty:
The website is a public tool dedicated to facilitating access to strategic information on women currently dispersed in the data banks of different governmental entities, such as the Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics (‘‘IBGE’’), the Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock and Supply (‘‘MAPA’’), the National Supply Company (‘‘CONAB’’), the National Institute for Colonization and Agrarian Reform (‘‘INCRA’’), and the Ministry of the Environment (‘‘MMA’’), among others. 
The idea is to collect this information and make it easily accessible through interactive graphics that display information on a national and regional level. Furthermore, it includes information on public policies oriented towards women in rural areas since the Federal Constitution of 1988, a special section with public notices for contracting projects, as well as publications and news.
Impact & Results: In the first phase the main result was the synergy generated among the partner institutions and Embrapa’s researchers and analysts, who were formally invited to participate in this effort. Given Brazil´s continental dimensions and great spatial heterogeneity, a network ‘‘Rede Embrapa Mulheres Rurais’’ was created with the participation of 70 researchers from all regions of Brazil and different fields. The first publications will be available from 2023 onwards. Five of them are currently under review and will focus on women in livestock farming, coffee farming, vegetable oil extraction in the Amazon, organic cotton cultivation and macaúba extractivism. Another 15 are already being prepared.

Project/Initiative Name: Guide for Heads of Single-Parent Families
Organisation: Guarantor Body of the Right of Access to Information (OGDAI) of the Government of the City of Buenos Aires
Description:
The objective of the Guide for Heads of Single-Parent Families is to train and empower this group in the exercise of the right to access public information, by providing them with a tool that facilitates the solution of some of their daily problems. It systematises and concentrates useful and necessary information for this group: frequent procedures, medical care, free legal assistance, vocational training and job placement programmes, support spaces and other services.

We produced a Guide in digital and physical format, which is useful, adopts clear language and condenses relevant information to help solve everyday problems of female heads of single-parent families. It is a practical and innovative solution that does not need many resources to be executed and implemented. The target group, in this case women heads of single-parent families from all social classes, benefits from the innovation.

The Guide for Women Heads of Single-Parent Families of the C.A.B.A. concentrates and systematises useful information for a group that, although specific and identifiable, does not have centralised or clearly defined channels of access to information and points of reference.
Novelty: This is an unprecedented experience of targeted transparency. We selected a group in a situation of vulnerability that had not been considered before for the production of this type of product. Then, we observed whether the information they needed was already produced and, immediately afterwards, we systematised and centralised it in a clear and easy-to-use guide to facilitate access and give it visibility.
Impact & Results: The total number of guides that were distributed as of September 2022 is 1200 physical units. Currently, we are still working on dissemination and distribution. We plan to replicate this targeted transparency initiative by considering other vulnerable groups that do not yet have channels or reference sites to access specific, relevant and useful information for them.

Project/Initiative Name: Technology for an Irreversible Transformation in Indonesia’s Education System
Organisation: GovTech Edu (in partnership with the Ministry of Education, Culture, Research & Technology (MoECRT))
Description:
GovTech Edu works with the Ministry of Education, Culture, Research & Technology (MoECRT) to build a technology ecosystem to create an irreversible transformation for Indonesia’s education system. GovTech Edu works as the thought and development partner to the Directorate Generals in the Ministry, bringing holistic technology product experience and resources. The Ministry officials act as the primary vision owner – bringing problem statements observed in Indonesia’s education system – in which GovTech Edu will then be responsible for clarifying and defining the appropriate technological interventions to enable the desired transformation.

It includes tech platforms made available for teachers, school principals, university students & practitioners, through products like Teacher SuperApp, Education Scorecard & University Internship Matchmaking Platform. Further, two integrated, easy, and accountable school financial management systems were launched. With these tools, the transformation to improve student learning outcomes becomes more inclusive, data driven & impact-enabling at scale.

All of the above platforms are made accessible for free through a single sign-on account called “belajar.id”, allowing teachers, school administrators, local governments & students to benefit from the rolled-out digital products.
Novelty: The MoECRT collaboration with GovTech Edu is one of Indonesia's first public-tech sector partnerships. It focuses on delivering tech solutions by co-designing them with teachers, schools principals, local governments and university students, supported with apt user insights. Users’ involvement is ensured through a robust immersion and research process. The close collaboration with the Ministry’s stakeholders has enabled a first-of-its-kind and innovative way of working, where the conventional top-down government direction is decoupled through a bottom-up, data-backed, and user-centric approach.
Impact & Results:
Teacher: As of September 2022, the Teacher SuperApp has reached 2+ million downloads, with 4.8 rating on Google Play Store. A total of more than 3,500 learning communities have contributed to the app, and more than 92K upskilling and inspirational content were included. It has been used by more than 1.6 million teachers nationwide and has become one of Indonesia's top 5 education apps.

School principals: More than 200K schools have utilized the school financial management tool & a total of more than US$3BN funds were managed through the tool. Over 140K schools have used the Education Scorecard.

Students: The university internship matchmaking platform has also been accessed by more than 700K university students, of which approximately ~25% have been matched with industry & company participants.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:

https://youtu.be/WIO1fSXfPlY (pitch)

- https://siplah.kemdikbud.go.id/ 
- https://belajar.id/

Description:
The team has developed an innovative GDP forecasting application based on Explainable Machine Learning (XML). It allows users to generate accurate and explicable economic forecasts from data set with multivariate time-series. The application displays novel prediction changes for temporally ordered variable values, which largely increases the explainability of predictions. It also includes a hybrid ML model that seamlessly combines all algorithms which outperforms ESV’s own pre-pandemic GDP forecasts.

The application provides any users – politicians, researchers, journalists and citizens – a tool to perform accurate forecasts that continuously learns from historical changes in variable impacts. After closer evaluation, the application could potentially be integrated into any ordinary economic forecasts performed by different bodies of government and used as basis of governmental policy-making. It gives evidence on how to use existing ML algorithms without jeopardizing the level of explainability of predictions.

This application was developed in the Data lab at ESV. The purpose of the Data lab is to create a national arena for data on financial management to further the development of a data driven performance culture in the government administration and advance decision support at various levels. To fulfil the purpose, the laboratory develops several applications to conduct queries, computer processing, analysis and visualization with the support of machine learning and other AI methods. The work is carried out together with need owners, AI researchers, IT consultants and remunerated students.
Novelty: GDP predictions with ML outperform traditional statistical techniques but this innovation provides the benefits of ML with novel explainability. Further, as all ML GDP forecasting, it overcomes the limitations of theory-based forecasting.
Impact & Results: This innovation confirms that ML algorithms are likely to outperform humans in making economic forecasts on the short and mid-term. In this way this new forecasting model is in line with OECD’s appeal for data driven innovation. Lastly, it encourages the furthering of Explainable ML (XML) and gives a concrete example of its benefits.

Description:
The process of formulating the Innovation Policy began in July 2019, when a multidisciplinary committee was formed with representation from all levels of care in the OSH network, with the aim of drafting the guiding principles of the declaration on public innovation in health, an opportunity highlighted in the institutional strategic plan. 

The policy seeks to develop a culture of innovation in our healthcare network in order to generate public value and quality care for users of the OSH. - Guiding principles: network integration; environmental awareness and sustainable development; user satisfaction and social participation. 

Action Objectives: a) Development of a culture of innovation: a.1) Communications; a.2) Education and Training. b) Development of innovation projects: b.1) Project bank; b.2) Competitions and awards; b.3) Internships and good practices. c) Structure and process for innovation: c.1) Analysis and (re)design of innovation; c.2) Definition of a structure for innovation; c.3) Processes and procedures; c.4) Evaluation of results.
Novelty:
This project is innovative because it is unique at the national level in the Health Services, providing a framework and structure for the innovation process in public health. 
It also responds to a management of the local application area, involving and generating an ecosystem with all the establishments that make up the health care network of the Talcahuano Health Service, whose jurisdiction corresponds to the municipalities of Hualpén, Penco, Talcahuano and Tomé.
Impact & Results: 1- 40 civil servants trained in innovation strategies, which resulted in 6 innovation projects. During the month of October 2022, a new training will be given to 60 more civil servants. Formulation of the first collaborative innovation project with Universidad San Sebastián and DAS Hualpén, on integral approach to oncology patients and their families, which has allowed 31 families to enter the pilot programme. Development of a digitalisation project (with internal development) of the food and nutrition process of the hospitals in our network, which has a positive impact on work teams made up of around 20 people. It is currently in the IT testing stage. Formation of the Innovation Committee, whose mission is to advise the Talcahuano Health Service network on the implementation of innovation. 5. Creation of a space on the website dedicated to the innovation carried out by the Service.

Description:
Chatico is a tool available on the Web and Whatsapp that identifies, unifies, centralises and rationalises information of interest to citizens, procedures, services and enables citizen participation.

In the conceptualisation of the project, the strategic decision was taken to deploy it on websites, but also on WhatsApp because it is understood that this communication mechanism is very appropriate among citizens of different ages, socioeconomic levels and technical capacities. This makes it an effective communication tool with transactional potential.

The chatbot, Chatico is a service channel that identifies, unifies, centralizes and rationalizes information of interest to citizens, procedures, services and enables citizen voting, through digital democracy mechanisms such as citizen causes, making use of artificial intelligence and interoperability between different sources of information of the District, in a clear, intuitive, accessible and frequently updated language.

It is aligned with agile methodologies, in particular a hybrid methodology between Scrum and Kanban, which together allow greater flexibility and the ability to modify products and services throughout the project in search of an incremental development that enhances Chatico's capabilities. In addition, it is supported by artificial intelligence and natural language processing, and provides 7/24 attention on procedures, services and, in general, strategic information about the city. It is periodically updated according to the current situation, but also to what its users request, always verifying beforehand the relevance and possible impact of each implementation.
Novelty:
Chatico's objective is to bring citizens closer to and improve their trust in the District Administration, through: 
 
1. Streamlining of procedures and services: access to information, procedures and services in a simple way, optimizing time and searches in different portals of the District Administration under a mechanism of daily dialogue. It is a unified and transactional channel that generates answers to the demands or procedures most requested by citizens.
2. Promotion of citizen participation: It is consolidated as a channel for advocacy, citizen participation and digital democracy in which citizens can consult, identify, compare and decide on programs such as: participatory budgets in the localities and citizen causes (environmental, territorial, educational, among others). 
3. Allow sending information, with prior authorization of the citizen, on topics that interest him/her and facilitate his/her life in the relationship with the city.
Impact & Results:
From 25 September to 24 October 2021, a total of 5,145 votes were cast. For this term, from 24 August to 22 September 2022, more than 32,000 votes were obtained. 
 
Quantitative results: 
Total interactions with the tool: 45,000 interactions/conversations. 
In 2021, using only the website, 5,400 interactions were received. 
Satisfaction: Survey result: 91%. 
Average bot response time: 0.38 seconds

Project/Initiative Name: Learning Trajectories: Laying the foundations for an Early Warning System to reduce school drop-out rates
Description:
With the help of Big Data and Artificial Intelligence, student trajectories are identified in order to create strategies and public policies to increase the educational level of the population.
These efforts lay the groundwork for an Early Warning System to identify students at risk of dropping out of school. These efforts add to the commitment of the Guanajuato Ministry of Education (SEG) to find innovative solutions through the use of ICTs and to improve its data-driven decision-making.

Through the 'Learning Analytics' project, the SEG has been working on the harmonisation of different databases with relevant information, as well as the development of use cases and identification of patterns and variables, with the aim of having valid, reliable and up-to-date data and information on the current state of the education system. In addition, within the pioneering initiative 'Educational Trajectories', the products emanating from Learning Analytics allowed for the development of predictive analyses on individual student trajectories in the state.

The application of predictive analytics allows the identification of multifactorial aspects of school dropout, resulting in intelligence for the development of effective care strategies. As next steps, we seek to employ data science and analytical techniques and tools for the early identification of vocational trajectories of secondary and high school students in the state.
Novelty:
Trayectorias Educativas is an innovative initiative because it not only uses Information and Communication Technologies (ICTs) to inform courses of action and offer solutions according to the needs of the users of the education system. It implements predictive analytics and AI methods to streamline government performance and decisions for the benefit of citizens.

Learning Trajectories presents a public innovation approach that puts students at the centre, influencing multiple factors beyond the classroom.
Impact & Results:
At the moment, thanks to the data analytics consolidated by the SEG, strategies for the Social Pact for Education have been generated:
- Guanajuato became one of the few entities in the country with a measurement of the expected loss of basic knowledge for primary school students, associated with COVID-19 containment measures.
- The number of students who dropped out of school as a result of the pandemic was identified, allowing efforts to be redirected towards the recovery of students and their learning.
With the SAT in operation, it is expected to have a list of students who are at risk of dropping out in each school cycle, which will allow for the establishment of preventive and targeted measures in each case.

Project/Initiative Name: Preparing Civil Servants for the Fourth Industrial Revolution
Description: The Fourth industrial revolution brings many opportunities, but also new challenges for public administration.
