The online self-paced training "Fourth Industrial Revolution: New Technologies" teaches the basics of AI, Blockchain, Virtual Reality, IoT, 3D printing, Robots, Drones and the Digital Economy. Through quizzes, games and many illustrative examples, participants can immerse themselves into the modern world of emerging technologies, which is crucial in order to create more innovative policies.
Novelty: This innovation makes public servants learn about benefits and challenges of new technologies. It does so with an online tool that is built following the idea of gamification.
Impact & Results: Over 100.000 public servants have access to Moodle training platform of the National Academy for Public Administration. No other impacts available.

Organisation: Ministry of Labour and Employment Promotion
Description:
the Ministry created the Labour Market Information System (SIMEL) as a network of public and private institutions that provides technical, institutional and technological support to integrate, standardise, process and analyse labour market information from both public and private sources to meet the different information needs of actors in the labour ecosystem.

Specifically, SIMEL classified its actors into three groups based on i) their degree of access to restricted information (subject to personal data protection laws); ii) their characteristics of consumption and production of labour data; and iii) their needs for information, management and understanding of content.

Through SIMEL, the aim is to provide differentiated labour information services and tools to private users, external public users and internal public users. SIMEL works on the basis of the interaction of three components. First, there is the governance component, which brings together the institutional framework that underpins the system (this includes the identification of information providers, roles, rules, protocols and flows). Secondly, there is the content and design component of the information services, which brings together all the generation and integration activities. The third component is the technological architecture (hardware and software) that the system requires for its operation.

Currently, the system is in phase 1 of implementation, in which the following tools have been deployed (some in pilot phase): 1) Labour Information Consultation Platform (PCIL), 2) Lmis. Stat, which is a web repository of indicators and a platform for the production and dissemination of statistical data in the process of implementation, 3) Labour Innovation Laboratory, which is a SIMEL mechanism to generate rigorous evidence for the improvement of services and policies, 4) GIS Viewer: A tool that allows us to visualise and consult labour market information in a spatial manner, 5) Extranet: A private network with controlled access, in which data can be shared and linked in a collaborative manner between different units and entities that generate and use labour information.
Novelty:
Until the establishment of SIMEL, Peru did not have a complete labour market information system, understood as a system that manages to integrate all the country's labour information. This implied:
1. The unification and standardisation of criteria in the reporting of indicators on the basis of a national consensus.
2. Transparency in the definitions and metadata of indicators.
3. Formal institutionalisation of mechanisms for the exchange and transfer of information between institutions.
4. Updated contents, adjusted to the information needs of heterogeneous users.
5. Contribute to evidence-based policy formulation.
6. Expanding the labour market research and analysis agenda.
7. Promoting the use of information through mass-use technological tools.
Impact & Results:
'- The PCIL is deployed and used by sector officials. This version has modules: i) Occupational Guidance, with information and trends by occupation, training offer and resources for labour development and job search; ii) Labour Overview, which contains historical series of indicators, as well as interactive options and specific thematic dashboards built from surveys and records; iii) Specialised publications and microdata. 
- The first call for proposals for the Labour Innovation Laboratory was launched and three initiatives proposed by research centres were selected, with whom the pilots are being worked on in coordination with the organic units of the Ministry of Labour that will implement them.

Project/Initiative Name: Marketplace for training in public innovation
Organisation: Laboratory of Public Innovation of Bogota iBO / Mayor's Office of Bogota
Description:
We created a virtual Market-Place where public servants can have all the information at hand, identify which courses suit their needs, time and resources.

In Colombia and especially in Bogotá, public entities are developing a significant amount of training in public innovation for public servants. Likewise, public servants are very interested in learning about and applying public innovation concepts (in Bogotá there are approximately 2,200 public servants, the community of public innovators is made up of almost 500 public servants).

It is a web platform hosted in the cloud that has different functionalities to facilitate the search for courses on public innovation. When courses are registered on the platform (either by the entities developing the training or through iBO) they go through a text indexing process that allows public servants to filter very easily from a word search bar or from a series of categorical filters (name of the entity, local-national-international level, virtual-presential-hybrid format), and target population. For each course, details of start dates as well as requirements are then presented in a standard view that facilitates the visualisation and comparison of the options.
Novelty:
This innovation is different because it integrates modern technologies in the development of the web platform so that it is easily scalable and adaptive, making it easier for the usability of the platform to improve according to the use given to it by public servants.
On the other hand, we consider it innovative for the public sector to design exclusive products and services for public servants to improve their training capacities.
Finally, it is an innovative process in its methodology as we have developed several validation workshops with public servants to constantly improve usability and experience.
Impact & Results:
Currently, several validation exercises have been carried out with public servants to receive feedback for adjustments. However, the indicators that we consider relevant to measure success in the future are:
Increase in the number of visits to the market place.
Increased number of click-throughs to view course information
Increase in the number of people enrolled in the courses offered.
User satisfaction with the usability of the marketplace.
Increase in the number of entities that want to include their courses and training in the marketplace.

Description:
Datálogo Colombia was developed in response to the dispersion of sources for consulting information on the documents produced by the national government. In addition, it leaves an institutional memory for open consultation on different aspects that the outgoing government hands over to the incoming one. The tool is innovative in applying machine learning technologies to process, organise and present a large volume of documentary information in an agile and simple way for Colombians.

Datálogo Colombia is a digital tool in which citizens can consult all the documentation generated during the last period of government in a quick and easy way, as well as the sectoral management reports and the reports of all the entities that make up the executive branch of the national government (around 190 entities). The information includes normative, documentary and statistical records (around 30,000), and is a space of transparency for citizens in general, as well as for the new government's team. In this way it takes advantage of available data and generates accessibility.

The main objective was to create a web repository with all the documentation produced by the national government between 2018 and 2022, and make it available to citizens through a search engine that presents it in an organised way, using filters so that citizens can quickly find what they are looking for. It is hoped that with the new government the Datálogo will become part of Colombian legislation, through an article in the new National Development Plan Law.
Novelty: Datálogo Colombia facilitates the consultation of documentation generated by the national government through a search engine that uses machine learning algorithms to organise, classify and present the information and make it useful and accessible to all. The algorithm is capable of transforming images into text, enhancing the use of files that have a photo format; in addition, the search has a functionality that creates a word cloud for each document indexed in the database, giving the user previous ideas about their query.
Impact & Results: The results of the implementation of Datálogo Colombia are evident through web analytics metrics: number of visits, number of unique users, bounce rate, pages visited, browsing time and traffic sources. Since its opening on 17 June 2022 to date, it has had close to 194,000 visitors (142,000 mobile and 51,000 web). It is expected to become the most consulted government website in Colombia.

Project/Initiative Name: Strategy to promote public innovation in Bogota (Experimental and iBO talks)
Description:
iBO identified that Bogotá is eager to learn more about public innovation to address city challenges. For this reason, two large spaces were created to generate conversation about innovation in different audiences. Experimental and iBO Talks were born.

In iBO we seek to generate conversation around public innovation. For this reason, incremental innovation strategies were designed to create and consolidate community among the actors of the Public Innovation Ecosystem of Bogota. In the District we have more than 120 public servants, 235 actors in the Ecosystem, strategic allies from public and private entities, organisations from the private sector and academia.

On the one hand, Experimental aims to share good practices in innovation exercises for multiple audiences: people close to innovation (public servants) and those who want to contribute from their daily work, have an innovative spirit and see innovation as an opportunity. In this sense, it is a space that is open to any type of public that is interested in learning, getting to know and replicating innovation tools, practices and exercises in their communities or in their immediate environment. 

Experimental currently has an impact of 50 live connections and has more than 4,600 views. In the short term, our main goal is to continue to generate conversation and obtain more than 8,000 reproductions to continue strengthening and generating innovation capacities in the actors within the Ecosystem.
Novelty:
Experimental is a space for informal conversation with high-level guests to share their experiences and lessons learned in the development of public innovation projects on various topics such as: culture of innovation, behavioural science, innovation for peace, among others. Its format is unique because it is live and its call is open so that different actors of the ecosystem such as students, entrepreneurs, social organisations can participate and ask questions in real time to the guests.

iBO Talks proposes short spaces of maximum 30 minutes as Ted Talks for public servants of Bogotá to present initiatives that they have implemented from their entities and in this way, inspire and connect with other public servants, thus strengthening inter-institutional knowledge management and the network of public servants of Bogotá.
Impact & Results: We have around 5000 views in both spaces (Experimental and iBO Talks) which allows us to demonstrate that we are generating valuable and interesting conversations around public innovation, and thus consolidating an Innovation Ecosystem through a community that is woven around shared knowledge. To date, 10 experiments and 8 iBO Talks have been carried out.

Project/Initiative Name: Platform for digital applications of the Peruvian State - Facilita Perú
Organisation: Presidency of the Council of Ministers - Secretariat of Government and Digital Transformation.
Description:
Facilita was created as a platform that allows entities to digitise procedures in just a few steps and, in this way, attend to citizens' requests without the need for them to go in person. 

Facilita has four objectives that respond to the aforementioned problems and seek to offer an efficient experience to its users. First, to reduce the digital divide between public entities by offering a free service that does not require installation or specialised programming staff. Second, to improve the experience of public servants in their interaction with the platform, as it does not require a lot of training or an installation process as it is ready to use versus other platforms that do require it. The third objective is to strengthen the capacities of the digital leaders of the entities to foster the continuous use of the platform, so that the use of the platform is relevant for the whole entity. Finally, to improve services and continue to grow based on the real needs of users, whether they are public entities or citizens, so we prioritise the identification of their real needs for the process of designing experiences and interfaces.

We want the process of creating forms to be as simple as putting together a jigsaw puzzle, which is why we provide different components and tools to facilitate your task. In case new functionalities need to be incorporated or incidents need to be solved, we work with SCRUM methodology, which allows us to put the user at the centre and leads to solve problems and incorporate improvements in an agile way.
Although Facilita was born with a forms creator as a minimum viable product, in its two years of implementation it has incorporated new components such as standardised templates for procedures, digital payments and consultation services. With the creation of Facilita, an important component was born, which is the process management component that will allow the modelling of a process with several interactions from start to finish.
Novelty:
Facilita is an innovative project because it facilitates the work and management of public servants so that they can serve citizens more simply and quickly, through reusable components that can be put to use without the need for knowledge of web development.
In addition, it is implemented with agile and innovation methodologies such as SCRUM, design thinking and lean UX, an unusual way of working in state entities, but which allows for rapid and relevant growth for users. Unlike the more traditional ways of working in the public sector, agile and innovation methodologies allow the focus to be on the user as the protagonist and to make partial deliveries of components and functionalities that are perfected according to their validation. In this way, the platform is built gradually and remains relevant.
Impact & Results:
To date, there are more than 500 public entities users, including municipalities, regional governments, public enterprises and ministries, which have created more than 2,400 digital forms. In addition, the demand for these services has been high, as more than 470,000 requests have been received from citizens through Facilita Peru's forms.
In the future, it is expected to continue implementing new functionalities in the platform. By 2023, priorities are to incorporate more digital payment services, develop a section for statistics on requests so that public servants can generate reports, interoperate with other digital platforms of the state to ensure a complete digital flow, and incorporate elements that allow compliance with an accessibility approach in the platform.

Description:
The Portfolio of Instruments was developed in response to the dispersion of information on multiple portals regarding the types of support provided by the National Government to promote competitiveness and innovation projects for citizens, entrepreneurs and academics.

An interactive tool that seeks to facilitate user access to the National Government's public interventions in the areas of competitiveness, productivity, entrepreneurship and science, technology and innovation (STI). It generates timely and quality information that allows for an effective connection between supply and demand. This tool stems from the implementation of the "Articulation for Competitiveness" (ArCo) methodology, an initiative that promotes the articulation and optimisation of programmes, projects, calls for proposals and other public interventions of the National Government, in order to achieve greater efficiency in the use of public resources. 

This tool, together with the ArCo Methodology, has a systematic process for the collection of information, using ex ante evaluation tools that assess the suitability of the instruments, generating recommendations for articulation and consolidating the instruments in a single portal, disaggregating public interventions by type of user and categorising the instruments according to user need.

With the Competitiveness and Innovation Instrument Portfolios published in 2021 and 2022 with 497 and 376 policy interventions respectively, access to citizens was facilitated and open government and accountability tools were provided, publishing the results of ArCo's implementation in terms of concentration, coverage and scale of instruments.
Novelty:
The Portfolio of Instruments is an improvement born out of the iterative process of experimentation and learning from the implementation of the "Articulation for Competitiveness" (ArCo) methodology. It is of great value as it is based on an empirical approach in which the overlapping of functions and efforts between entities, the dispersion of public resources and the targeting of supply are assessed. ArCo offers solutions to achieve a results-oriented budget, promote the efficient use of public spending and foster synergies between entities according to the needs of their users.

The Portfolio of Instruments innovates in governance by solving the lack of knowledge of society regarding the institutional offer on competitiveness and innovation issues.
Impact & Results:
The Instruments Portfolio website for August 2022 had 33,090 visits and mapped 376 instruments in 70 entities belonging to 20 administrative sectors of the National Government for a value of $7.5 billion. This figure for the second iteration of the analysis of the General Budget of the Nation - 2022 was earmarked for the attention of entrepreneurs, businessmen, academics and other beneficiary users of the productive development policy and the science, technology and innovation policy.

By the end of 2022, the portfolio website is expected to reach 52,000 visits, based on visits for the same period in 2021 and visits up to August 2022.

Description:
Data Mexico was developed to integrate and transform public and private sector data to enable a better understanding of the economic and social context. The platform facilitates strategic decision making through key evidence, integrating databases with information aggregation, filtering and reorganisation technologies. 

DataMéxico aims to solve the growing need to access and visualise the economic and social information collected in Mexico during the last years, allowing to articulate an economic promotion strategy of the Federal Government. There was no project close to the economic community that was able to bring together the functionality, analysis and information necessary for the understanding of the context in the sector. 

The databases are integrated through Datawheel's own technologies called Tesseract and Bamboo, which allow the aggregation, filtering, search and transformation of the files into elements that are then understandable by the platform's Content Management System (CMS); thus, generating thousands of connection points with the data and automatically filling in the aforementioned profiles.

DataMexico integrates a wide range of data on trade, production, employment, education and demographics, among others, for the whole country, with high spatial resolution at regional and municipal level, which has allowed optimising resources to inform actors from industry, business, local government and civil society among others. The users can also build their own visualisations with the data available through the "Vizbuilder", customising different metrics, filters, selecting datasets of interest and finally being able to download all the necessary information for their analysis.
Novelty:
A fundamental element of the platform relates to the analysis of Economic Complexity in Mexico. This methodology allows using the data available in a given geography to predict development dynamics. Complexity considers and relates capabilities, resources, technologies, human capital and infrastructure to their development potential.

The calculation can also be performed for Product Economic Complexity (PCI) by analysing manufacturing, sales or export data; and for Economic Activities (ACI) by considering employment or production data.
The platform is already implemented and has proven to be of great help to the economic sector in Mexico, becoming a reliable source of data, especially for institutions.
Impact & Results: Nearly 2,000 people (citizens, public servants, investors and researchers) have been trained to learn how to use and take advantage of Data Mexico. Also, from January to September 2022, the platform has received 1.9 million visits. Among the impacts is access to more information for decision-making. Companies from different sectors have shared with the Ministry of Economy that Data Mexico allowed them to identify data to evaluate their investment projects.

Project/Initiative Name: Promoting Compliance through Effective Communication: An Application of the Framing Effect in the Removal of Barriers to Competition
Organisation: INDECOPI - National Institute for the Defense of Free Competition and the Protection of Intellectual Property
Description:
INDECOPI, the agency of competition in Peru, has created a specialized unit called “Effective Compliance Team”, which incorporates behavioural insights (framework effect) in their communication with other government institutions. Through this innovation, the government has a cost-effective tool to incentivize compliance, ensure the understanding of other institutions about bureaucratic barriers to competition, and benefit economic agents in the market and overall the citizens of Peru.

The Unit has three main objectives:
1) Act as a delivery unit for eliminating barriers to competition by prioritizing persuasion before sanctioning other government institutions.
2) Communicate the benefits for government institutions if they eliminate regulatory barriers to competition considering the framing effect.
3) Increase the use of behavioural tools to solve problems related to conduct cost-effectively.

To tackle the problem, we identified that non-compliance responds to a lack of knowledge. In this way, we use the framing effect to effectively communicate the scheme of sanctions and incentives of the system of bureaucratic barriers, as well as the steps to be taken to definitely eliminate the regulatory barriers.
Novelty:
This project is innovative in law enforcement because:
1) It implements a delivery unit specialized in law enforcement by persuasion before sanctioning. 
2) It uses behavioural economics concepts such as the framework effect to achieve compliance with institutions that have created barriers to competition. 
3) It facilitates the general understanding of the steps to be taken to achieve compliance and provides support through a personalized assistance contact.
Impact & Results: Preliminary results show that the average adaptation time for those denounced public entities that received the new document design is 59 working days, while for those denounced public entities that received documents with the previous design, the average time is more than 200 working days. Likewise, 44 bureaucratic barriers have been effectively eliminated, which is more than two times the number of barriers that were removed by cases before this project. For the impact evaluation, we propose to use propensity score matching, once the minimum sample size necessary is reached in order to obtain statistically valid results.

Project/Initiative Name: Voluntary Removal of Regulatory Barriers to Competition: Using Social Norms to Nudge Government Institutions Towards a High Quality Regulation
Organisation: INDECOPI - National Institute for the Defense of Free Competition and the Protection of Intellectual Property
Description:
INDECOPI has implemented a new project called “Voluntary Removal of Regulatory Barriers to Competition” based on the use of social norms in official documents and data dashboards to monitor the removal of bureaucratic barriers across the country. Through this, civil servants compare their performance in eliminating barriers with their peers and adapt their behavior towards high-quality regulation. 

The objectives of this project are: (i) to include social norm messages in official documents to modify the behavior of civil servants by comparing them to their peers in the elimination of regulatory barriers to competition, (ii) to decrease the average time for eliminating bureaucratic barriers to competition and support the economic recovery in Peru, and (iii) to communicate and take advantage of the incentives for the elimination of bureaucratic barriers in Peru.

Regarding the civil servant's incentives for the elimination of bureaucratic barriers, the project proposes to enhance the three positive rankings for entities and public servitors in the first document sent to the entity: (i) ranking of institutions (ii) ranking of preventive actions, and (iii) ranking of civil servants, which includes a public award at a national ceremony. The ranking of institutions seeks to encourage the desire for general participation by rewarding with national recognition and official certification to entities that participate in the project. The ranking of preventive actions proposes that the final product delivered from the project should be reported internally to the different areas of the entities for the socialization of the new norms of their administrative procedures. Finally, the ranking of civil servants is intended to generate healthy competition among them for inclusion in an official ranking that will benefit their curriculum. In this way, we benefit not only citizens but also public servitors involved in the elimination process since they are going to receive public recognition for the elimination of bureaucratic barriers.
Novelty: The project implemented by INDECOPI is an innovation because it aims to minimize biases civil servants can have about their peers and nudge institutions to voluntarily remove regulatory barriers to competition. In the context of an economic recovery, we employ behavioral economics tools and data science to accomplish objectives more efficiently and effectively. Through behavioral insights, we nudge civil servants to change their behavior, and as result, we remove bureaucratic barriers and create better conditions for the market. In this context, the project designed and sent documents with a new design that include messages of social norms. Through data science, we recollect evidence about the behavior of other entities and we found the perfect comparison for every government institution. We design dashboards to monitor the voluntary removal and evaluate the effect of comparing an institution with others with better performance and close geographically.
Impact & Results: According to the preliminary results of the project, there was a considerable reduction in the average time for eliminating bureaucratic barriers, from 90 to 17 working days. This number decreased since it was possible to implement a new design in the official documents comparing institutions. It should be noted that during the project implementation a total of 787 bureaucratic barriers of 28 institutions were eliminated. To carry out the impact evaluation, we propose to use propensity score matching, considering the minimum sample size necessary to obtain statistically valid results. In addition, to monitor the project and how it develops throughout time, we have implemented the use of dashboards and pivot tables to track, measure, review, and control the results and also prioritize based on evidence the new institutions to treat with the social norms.

Project/Initiative Name: Implementation of DYPA Training Programs through the VOUCHER.GOV.GR electronic platform
Description:
The object of this innovation is the digital support of the provision of theoretical training services that will be addressed to beneficiaries (i.e., unemployed), as they are registered in the Public Employment Service (DYPA) in Greece. This innovation is part of the national effort to upgrade Human Resources skills, mainly in digital and "green" skills with the aim of effectively connecting them with the labor market.

The training programs are supported by a digital platform through which high-quality electronic services are provided to citizens, businesses and DYPA, directly related to the management of the vocational training life cycle.
The digital platform consists of individual specialized subsystems, which are properly configured to support the intended workflow and data management. The digital platform exchanges online data with other public bodies here in Greece., thus minimizing the exchange of documents and paperwork.

Through the digital platform, DYPA monitors in real time and fully supervises the procedures provided for the training programs, greatly reducing the administrative costs, as well as the completion times of the implementation, monitoring and control procedures of the training programs. Currently, the digital platform is in fully operational mode and its administration is carried out by the Computer Technology Institute and Press “Diophantus”.
Novelty: In contrast to 'traditional' training programmes, beneficiaries (i.e., unemployed) can choose from a range of training programs in high-demand sectors, with an emphasis on digital and 'green' skills. For the first time, the Ministry of Labour, Social Security and Welfare in Greece, through DYPA, is introducing the methodology of paying providers based on results, with the aim of optimally utilizing the funds of the Recovery and Resilience Fund (RRF).
Impact & Results: At the end of September, more than 81.000 beneficiaries have already registered in the digital platform and their training process has started. In general, the main impact of this innovation is in terms of: a) efficiency improvement, b) user’s satisfaction improvement, c) accessibility and response/waiting time, d) reliability – safety. The voucher management platform provides added-value digital services to all stakeholders (i.e, citizens, businesses, training providers, etc.) and allows the effective and optimal utilization of the RRF funds (i.e., payment by results for training providers).

Description:
CRUT has redefined public transit through its user-centric services. CRUT’s Mo E-Ride (E-Rickshaw) service provides feeder services for its primary Mo Bus (city bus) routes, forging ways for integrated multimodal mobility solutions. CRUT has consciously invested in holistic policy decisions, infrastructure development, data management and capacity building that have been instrumental in efficiently implementing strategies for creation of a sustainable public transport ecosystem, with focus on gender inclusivity.

CRUT has a policy of 50% reservation for women for Mo Bus Guides (conductors) and 100% reservation for women, transgender and HIV positive people for Sarathis (drivers) of Mo E-Ride.  Starting from extensive outreach programmes, to identification and meeting the beneficiaries, to providing them with regular training for two months, to providing them with stipend and lunch to encourage them to join the initiative and finally facilitating in them in the getting the Light Motor Vehicle (LMV) driving license; CRUT has been instrumental in upskilling these people belonging to the marginalised sections of the society. 

To push for women empowerment in the Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) driving profession, CRUT recently recruited woman Captain (driver of Mo Bus). vision for establishing a State-of-the-Art Training and Capacity Building Centre for drivers and conductors of bus and drivers of e-rickshaws along with having dedicated research and development centres of the OEMs in its premises.
Novelty:
CRUT has already laid the foundation of making the vision of gender inclusivity in public transport a reality by employing women Guides (conductors) in Mo Bus (city bus service) and has been successfully able to have 100% of its Mo E-Ride (E-Rickshaw) ‘Sarathis’ (Drivers) who are women, transgender and HIV positive people.
CRUT is the first public transport Authority in India to have disaggregated ticketing system. The Integrated Transport Management System enabled Automatic Fare Collection System is equipped with collection of data on the type of commuter using Mo Bus and Mo E-Ride services. Through the disaggregated ticketing, the organization has been able to plan and execute better to make its Mo Bus and Mo E-Ride services truly inclusive and universally accessible.
Impact & Results:
According to a survey:
• 98% of elderly Mo Bus users rated ease of boarding and alighting as very good
• 95% of Mo Bus users rated availability of priority seats as very good
• 93% of women commuters rated Mo Bus as the safest mode of public transport
• 91% of Mo Bus users rated behaviour of on-board staff as very good
• 90% of Mo Bus users rated the bus service to be punctual
• 57% of passengers have shifted from other modes of transport to Mo Bus
• 40% of female guides in the crew make the female commuters feel safer

Organisation: Ministry of Education, Science and Sport
Description:
The Knowledge Platform is a community space for dialogue on current EU initiatives between policy makers and stakeholders in Slovenia in the fields of Higher Education, Research and Innovation. In this way, we aim to strengthen the co-design and joint implementation of policies in these areas, to support cooperation of Slovenian stakeholders in EU initiatives and effectively promote synergies between the fields.

The Knowledge Platform is conceived as a community in the form of:
• regular meetings/consultations (virtual or in-person), which take place 4 times a year;
• a digital platform offering relevant information and direct communication with stakeholders. The Digital Knowledge Platform is being developed at the present.

The Knowledge Platform facilitates the flow of information between policy makers and a wider range of stakeholders on current initiatives at the EU level and the exchange of views and positions on these initiatives, in an effort to maximise the opportunities offered by EU actions to Slovenian organisations and to keep them up-to-date on EU initiatives and actions and to involve them to the greatest extent possible.
Novelty: The Knowledge Platform is a social innovation that aims to establish an open space for regular dialogue with stakeholders on topical issues and initiatives at the EU level which will fuel further development of the Slovenian Higher Education, Research and Innovation. The initiative differs from similar mechanisms at the national level because it goes beyond one-way transfer of information/opinion, and strives for active co-design of the Slovenian positions on EU-level initiatives, i.e. participatory co-design of policies.
Impact & Results:
The Knowledge Platform has:
• strengthened dialogue between policy-makers and stakeholders (2 virtual and 1 in-person consultation with around 170 participants)
• informed the preparation national responses/positions on EU initiatives and acts
• contributed to the implementation of national strategies in the fields of higher education, research and innovation.
• facilitated breaking down departmental silos.

Description:
Project Sampoorna was launched targeting the mothers of Severe Acute Malnutrition (SAM) and Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM) children, the tagline being ‘Empowered Mothers, Healthy Children’. The mother of a healthy child of the same Anganwadi Centre (AWC) was identified and paired with the target mother, that is the mother of SAM/MAM children; they would be called ‘Buddy Mothers’ (2,416 pairs). They were usually neighbors and shared similar socio-economic backgrounds.

The pairs were given diet charts to indicate the daily food intake of their children. Buddy mothers would have discussions about this on all Tuesdays at the AWC. The local practices related to nutrition would also be discussed. Active intervention under Project Sampoorna was done only for first 3 months by providing protein rich food in the form of 100 milliliters of milk and an egg on alternate days was arranged for all 2,416 malnourished children giving time for their mothers to stabilize themselves in their newly found jobs. The mothers who became financially independent by end of 3 months through the Self Help Groups continued to provide nutritious & protein rich food to their children

2416 pairs of Buddy Mothers were created. Huge public participation as 69,828 people were directly involved in Sampoorna.
Novelty: Project Sampoorna employs a decentralized, technology-based model using community participation and women empowerment as the positive deviance factor for achieving result: an impressive reduction of children malnutrition.
Impact & Results: This project has resulted in reduction of malnutrition in children by 95.6 % within 1 year in Bongaigaon district using very little economic investment. Further, project has yielded encouraging results and by March 2021, 84.96% of SAM children and 97.3% MAM children were normal.

Project/Initiative Name: Tertius - an online construction marketplace that easily connects residential and commercial property owners and managers with certified and approved inspectors at their convenience and within their budget
Organisation: District of Columbia Department of Buildings
Description:
Due to the high level of demand, the Department of Buildings (DOB) was experiencing lengthy turnaround times for construction inspections. Tertius was developed applying multi-sided marketplace approaches to the problem. With this innovation it is possible to identify independent skilled inspectors within the local community and to create a marketing platform that allows them to be easily found, booked and paid. Geolocation check-in for inspectors brings transparency confirming that they were onsite at the time of the inspection, rather than signing off remotely without carrying out proper inspections as had been the accusation frequently in the space.

Government agency benefits from Tertius as it represents a potential profit revenue as volume of users increase and as it boosts efficiency and transparency for both internal and third-party inspections. Developers are happy in that they can book an inspector in minutes, receive quotes, and pay in one motion, and have inspections carried out in days rather than weeks. Inspection agencies and contractors save time and admin costs in quoting, finalising proposals and being selected for work, scheduling, and reporting findings thereby increasing their overall throughput of paid inspections.

DOB released a policy that required all inspections for building developments to be requested/booked via Tertius. This required consultation at the highest level, including the Mayor of Washington DC, Muriel Bowser. The initiative has been fully supported by the DC government due to the demonstrative productivity gains being made, and transparency being brought to the building inspections process.
Novelty: Leveraging modern multi-sided platforms, Tertius could help to better connect demand and supply, increasing transparency, efficiency, thereby creating stronger communities and increasing the level of service. Further, taking a Design Thinking & Agile development led to a purpose-built MVP being developed in just six months.
Impact & Results: Tertius brought far more efficient matching of demand & supply for permit inspections, substantially reducing turnaround times. Further, it has driven revenue to the taxpayers, increased regulatory oversight, increased safety in the building community, saving property owners and developers thousands of dollars.

Project/Initiative Name: STEM Mode: Inspiring STEM vocations with a gender perspective and a focus on women
Description:
Initiative that seeks to develop a national awareness-raising strategy that unifies and articulates the actions of various institutional and social actors to encourage the participation of more adolescent girls and young women in STEM-related careers. 

STEM mode finds in this an opportunity to bring together diverse actors to collaborate in favour of adolescent and young women in Mexico, through the following Objectives: 
1) Establish channels of accompaniment and collaboration between educational, economic and labour institutions of the three levels of government, to raise awareness and position the relevance of STEM areas of knowledge in achieving the objectives of inclusive education. 
2) Analysis, strengthening and/or generation of vocational guidance contents, 
3) Generate a mentoring and tutoring programme in STEM areas, 
4) Increase the enrolment, permanence and terminal efficiency of women who choose to pursue higher studies in STEM careers, 
5) Guarantee the right of women to work in professional fields in STEM areas in contexts free of violence and with equal opportunities.
Novelty:
This is the first national initiative in which a civil society organisation manages to unify the objectives of two Ministries of State around a current issue such as the promotion of STEM.
STEM Mode has a methodology developed and applied through federal bodies with the following Action Plan: 1) Working tables and articulation of key actors from relevant sectors. 2) Launch of the campaign "Get into STEM Mode and go beyond", hosted on a web portal, with vocational guidance content, participation of women, didactic resources and "STEM Heroines" capsules. 3) Events led by the heads of government of the 32 states, with vocational orientation talks, panel of experts, and presentation of the STEM Heroines capsules.
Impact & Results: STEM Mode was launched in August 2022; social media campaign components, the initiative's platform and dialogues with state actors to engage in the initiative are currently active.

Project/Initiative Name: Open Platform for Innovation and Development of Jalisco (PLAi)
Organisation: Open Platform for Innovation and Development of Jalisco (PLAi)
Description:
PLAi seeks to develop the competencies required for Jalisco to consolidate its position as an innovation hub. It offers training to the economically active population through partnerships with national and international institutions. 

PLAi was created as a public policy strategy to offer quality education and contribute to improve and accelerate the training of human resources through an innovative and flexible educational model, in accordance with the needs of the various regions and strategic economic sectors of Jalisco. 

It is aimed primarily at the Economically Active Population of Jalisco, comprising 4,039,130 people in total. It also includes entrepreneurs of micro, small, medium and large enterprises; students, teachers and academic staff of Higher Education Institutions; as well as public service personnel.

In the future, it is envisaged that PLAi's scope will be greater in the coming years and that it will be recognised as a leading organisation in talent development. Thus, the consolidation of the PLAi Virtual Campus will allow both public and private institutions to train their staff through specialisation courses and digital content. In line with the Jalisco Government's strategy to maintain and strengthen Guadalajara as the Mexican Silicon Valley, PLAi will continue to offer programmes in alliance with companies such as Amazon and Cisco.
Novelty:
It has established partnerships with actors from government, industry, academia and civil society, allowing for an educational offer that responds to the needs of the labour market in the digital age and was ready for it before COVID-19 was presented.

It offers short and flexible programmes that contribute to lifelong learning. It has physical spaces in Ciudad Creativa Digital, with adaptable furniture and state-of-the-art equipment, as well as a Virtual Campus, to access various educational and technological services.
Impact & Results: Since 2020, more than 74,400 people have been impacted through educational programmes and technological services. 175 training activities have been delivered, in which 16,122 people have participated. This is possible thanks to the 87 partnerships established with various institutions, 37 of which have been consolidated through legal instruments. In terms of technological services, PLAi makes 160 videoconferencing licences available to government agencies and HEIs, accounting for 40,975 sessions in total. 105 courses have been virtualised on the learning management platform, which has 10,621 users. The PLAi spaces in Ciudad Creativa Digital have been used for 518 events that have been technically supported.

Description:
The innovation was developed because the government of Bogotá had not territorialised innovation in the 20 localities, thereby limiting the governance capacity of local administrations, thus widening the gap between the citizen and the government, the problem was addressed by strengthening the skills of public servants, creating a synergy between central and local government, adding value to the relationship with citizens.

- A programme for public servants in innovation called Innovation Agents was created.
- A local innovation node or micro-laboratory was created.
- An innovative community was created to develop public challenges aimed at solving local problems.
Novelty: No entity in the district has decentralised innovation to its maximum expression, turning each local Mayor's Office into a Start Up of innovation.
Impact & Results:
- 7 micro laboratorios a nivel local
- más de 200 servidores públicos formados
- 20 lideres de innovación constituidos

Organisation: Comptroller's Office of the State of Jalisco
Description:
It is a Distinction awarded to companies that accredit having an integrity policy defined as the set of elements of self-regulation and internal controls that allow mitigating and reducing corruption risks, as well as guaranteeing a culture of integrity within companies and organisations.

It was developed mainly to strengthen the implementation of mechanisms to prevent administrative misconduct and acts of corruption, fostering a culture of integrity based on the adoption of principles and values in a co-responsible manner in both the public and private sectors, all for the benefit of the citizens of the State of Jalisco.

The innovation is based on the creation of an integrity policy, which has at least the following elements: organisation and procedures manual; code of conduct and/or code of ethics; control, monitoring and auditing systems; whistleblowing and whistleblowing promotion systems; training and capacity building systems and processes.

The main objective is to strengthen the implementation of mechanisms that prevent administrative misconduct and acts of corruption, fostering a culture of integrity based on the adoption of principles and values in a co-responsible manner in both the public and private sectors, all for the benefit of the citizens of the State of Jalisco.
Novelty:
Having the Pro Integrity Label registration provides companies with competitive advantages, as they are seen as a better option for doing business. 

Integral Development: Having the Pro Integrity Label registration will be an indicator of the integral development of the company's employees, as constant training is guaranteed, as well as the highest hiring standards.
Impact & Results:
Companies have been encouraged to create their own integrity policy in line with the 7 elements of article 25 of the General Law on Administrative Responsibilities. 

In the first edition of the Pro Integrity Label, 86 eighty-six companies were pre-registered, of which 40 forty completed the required procedures. 

In 2023, the Pro Integrity Label is expected to be awarded to a greater number of companies (200% more).
(200% more)

Project/Initiative Name: Identification Card for People with Autistic Spectrum Disorders in Minas Gerais (CIPTEA)
Organisation: Secretaria de Estado de Planejamento e Gestão de Minas Gerais
Description:
Implemented in 2021, the Identification Card for Persons with Autism Spectrum Disorders (Ciptea) is a digital and/or physical document, which aims to facilitate and enable people with autism to receive full attention and priority treatment in public and private services. The Card is a fundamental instrument to guarantee rights, since it avoids embarrassment due to different characteristics and behaviours that are not conventionally known.

The implementation of the Ciptea in MG, aimed at the following points: (a) to ensure and protect the exercise of the rights and fundamental freedoms of people with autism; (b) to eliminate discrimination based on disability and increase the quality of life and well-being of the citizen with ASD; (c) to make official the issuance of the Ciptea throughout the territory of the state in a standardized, secure manner and in compliance with the General Law of Data Protection; (d) to ensure access to the issuance of the CIPTEA for all regions of the state; (e) to create a database and information on people with autism in MG. 

The elaboration of the request and issuing process of the CIPTEA in Minas Gerais is innovative because the service was co-created together with its target audience, in a technological tool that allowed the service to be provided virtually and in person, which generates a greater reach of the service by the target audience throughout the state. The methodology used in the project, developed by Lab.mg, was based on Design Thinking, which places the user of the services at the centre of the discussion and values the joint construction of solutions. 

The use of the process automation tool, ProMGBPMS, enabled the creation of an innovative system for planning and implementing the service, online access to the process, without the need to travel, and the standardization of the process both virtual and in-person, meeting the needs and specificities of the different realities of the municipalities of Minas Gerais. In addition, the tool uses the Gov.br signature to issue the card and the document, in turn, has a QR Code that indicates its validity, preventing fraud and misuse of the service.
Novelty:
The elaboration of the process of requesting and issuing the Ciptea in Minas Gerais is innovative because the service was co-created together with its target audience, in a technological tool that enabled the service to be provided virtually and in person. The methodology used in the project, developed by Lab.mg, was based on Design Thinking, which puts the user of the services at the centre of the discussion and values the joint construction of solutions. Added to this, the development of the system was done using the agile methodology, considering small deliveries.

The use of the process automation tool, ProMGBPMS, made it possible to create a unique and innovative system to implement the service and standardize the process, meeting the needs and specificities of the different realities of the municipalities.
Impact & Results:
The development of a statewide electronic system to request and issue the Ciptea in a 100% digital way and the qualification of 32 Integrated Care Units (Uai) to issue the document in person, allowed people with autism anywhere in Minas Gerais to have access to the document. 

In 6 months, 5,517 requests for cards were registered, 1,926 online and 3,136 in person. In the meantime, 3,954 documents have already been issued, 830 online and 3,124 in person. As far as results are concerned, this means that after only six months of the service's operation, Minas Gerais already has 343 municipalities with at least one citizen carrying the Ciptea, representing approximately 40% of all municipalities in the State.

Project/Initiative Name: Spreading digital literacy across the nation, with the "K-Digital Platform (KDP)"
Description:
The Korean government recently piloted the KDP to enhance national digital literacy and foster manpower suitable for digital convergence as digital transformation and low-carbon economy transition accelerated in all areas of the labor market. This is to strengthen the digital capabilities of various consumers, including workers, young job seekers, and the self-employed, and to preemptively and systematically respond to structural changes in future industries.

The KDP is an open platform where all training in the field of digital convergence is conducted and all resources can be used by various members in the regions to carry out projects centered on idea sharing and problem solving. There are two main functions of the KDP : 1)Hub of Digital Training 2) Sharing and Opening of Digital Infrastructure. By performing these two key functions, the purpose of the KDP project is to enhance the digital capabilities of the entire nation and support the digital transformation of the entire industry.

Further, the KDP can provide digital training programs to meet the needs of the companies through linkage between local companies and local governments, and joint programs with local governments can also be operated using the platform's infrastructure. In addition, the KDP provides digital lectures for future talents and programs to support activities of student clubs regarding new technologies.
Novelty:
The KDP is innovative for the following reasons:
1) The KDP is operated in cooperation between the government and the private sector
2) KDP programs are flexible so that they can be customized based on the needs of participants. 
3) The KDP operators have regional and in-house councils. The councils provide future directions for the operation of each platform and suggestions for improvement.
Impact & Results: For the past two years, 7,238 people have attended digital vocational training programs. A total of 10,116 people have also participated in the sharing of digital infrastructure, with the utilization time of 87,922 hours. Companies developed projects to enhance the benefits of using KDP both for themselves and for workers.

Project/Initiative Name: Challenge Tenders - An innovative method of implementing digital solutions to solve urging health problems
Description:
Challenge tenders are a new model designed to solve urging needs of the health system which usually aren't attractive enough for the start-up industry to engage in, such as fall prevention among elders, improvement of geriatric care in long term facilities and others. In order to attract digital based solutions to enter the health system, we initiated an innovative method of intervention which allows quick testing of technologies in health organization with the support of the Ministry of Health for fast proven outcomes.

First, we define a problem, one that is generally not attractive for startups, and invite startups to intervene trying to find a solution. We do not limit the number of solutions nor the kind of technology. The selected solutions are fully funded and accompanied by the MOH in executing a pilot in a health organization. The funding is given through all the pilot's stages for support of licensing, developments and necessary adjustments of the product to the health organization. The pilot is executed in a health organization for 6-12 months in which the product is being tested for efficiency, clinical results and compatibility with workflow. In this period the health organization is measuring the outcomes of the pilot in order to establish whether it meets its needs and solves the problem we defined.

Our first pilot executed this way was in the field of fall prevention of elders above 65. Falls are one of our most urgent concern regarding elders being the 6th cause of death among elders. We received a few dozens of solutions from which we chose two technologies to test in health organizations. One of these two technologies was an evidence-based digital-therapeutic solution for customized motor-cognitive training, walking rehabilitation and fall prevention named "GAITBETTER". Maccabi, the second large HMO in Israel was chosen to be the pilot's test site. The pilot involved 200 elder patients that were considered in the 'medium –to high-risk' category for falls. They participated in a 15-visit training program averaging 40 minutes per visit, for 6~7 weeks.  Following the successful pilot, Maccabi rolled this system to dozens of sites throughout Israel, making it available to its relevant patients.
Novelty: This challenge tender method allows us to be more open to solutions and technologies we may didn't even know exist that can answer our problems. The method of funding and co running a pilot alongside a health organization in order to examine the technology and how it is executed in the health organization, allows us and the health organization to fully measure outcomes, usability and weather the problem is being solved
Impact & Results: The first outcome using the challenge tender method was in the field of fall prevention among elders. The results after comparing 6 months before and after intervention showed 71% reduction in the number of falls; 46% reduction in ER visits; 34% reduction in hospitalization days; 28% reduction in FAS; 19% reduction in TUG. These amazing results led to deployment of this solution to dozens of sites throughout Israel.

Project/Initiative Name: Personalized feedback service for national qualification test result - "Digital Mentor Service of National qualification (DMS)"
Description:
The National Technical Qualification is a system through which the Korean government recognizes an individual's vocational ability. Candidates can improve their skills while preparing for the qualification, which can be used for employment, starting a business, and further education. In Korea, more than 3.5 million people participate in national technical qualifications every year, and about 700,000 have been certified. However, after taking an exam, examinees were only told whether they passed or not and had no clue how much they scored in each subject.

The Digital Mentor Service of National Qualification was developed to address such concerns. It leverages massive data on national qualification tests to identify each individual's weaknesses and why they fail. It also provides personalized information by matching examination history with jobs and training offered by government agencies. Supporting test takers from skills assessment and development to job placement.

The service was introduced in 2021 as a pilot program. In 2021, the DMS served 14,094 people who tested for five qualification subjects, and as of 2022, it is already supporting job seekers, with 2,784 of the 14,904 users having landed new jobs (20% got a new job within 12 months of service use). The Digital Mentor Service of National Qualification has been recognized by the Korean government for its innovativeness and has secured a budget (KRW 3.9 billion) for future expansion.
Novelty:
The Digital Mentor Service of National Qualification (DMS) is innovative for the following reasons:
1) A Paradigm Shift in National Qualification Services: Going beyond simply reporting test results as pass or fail, the DMS sees qualifications as the beginning of the journey. 
2) Comprehensive HRD Service: The DMS is a comprehensive service encompassing skills assessment, skills development, and employment support. 
3) Convenience: The DMS integrates useful information scattered across different employment and labor services and big data and provides personalized feedback so that users can easily access quality services.
Impact & Results:
(1) Service innovation: The DMS is the first big data-based HRD service that analyzes the reason why examinees fail national qualification exams (internal development saved development costs by $78,500). The pilot provided high-quality services to 14,094 examinees of five test qualifications (User satisfaction: 4.3/5 points)
(2) Job search: Out of 14,094 pilot users, 2,784 found a new job after using the DMS for qualification, training, and job search (20% found a new job within 12 months of using the service).
(3) Improved convenience: The one-stop comprehensive HRD service reduces annual social costs wasted due to fragmented services by KRW 350m (20,464 people X 2 hours (time saved) X KRW 9,160 (minimum wage).

Project/Initiative Name: Bigger and Louder: Breaking barriers to offer Evidence Based Therapy for patients with progressive degenerative conditions
Description:
A needs analysis to determine the availability of rehabilitation services for patients with neurological progressive disorders in a South African public health setting was conducted. It was deduced that no designated service was available for people with neurological progressive disorders thus, making the neurological progressive service unique to CHBAH and the public health sector.

The service was initiated in 2019 by Speech therapy as no patients were being referred for rehabilitation from Neurology Outpatient Department (NOPD). A speech therapy screening service was then initiated. Results from screening over a 6-week period, identified an overwhelming 96 patients requiring speech therapy services. A focus group was conducted targeting patient input on a speech therapy package of care as well as challenges in accessing the service.

Our screening service allowed us to identify and provide a rehabilitation plan for 196 patients thus far. It also facilitated raising awareness of the rehabilitation team involved with this population of patients and building infinite relationships with our neurologists ensuring the sustainability of new referrals to our service.

LSVT for patients with Parkinson’s disease, requires additional postgraduate certification and licensing and is therefore, not widely used in the public sector in South Africa. Therapists ensured upskilling on the principles of LSVT BIG and LOUD and created an adapted program for our patients.
Novelty: This is the only rehabilitation service that provided a sense of hope and belonging for our patients with Parkinson’s disease. It has been successful in adapting and implementing evidence-based literature by considering our patients socio demographic circumstances. Our flexible hybrid model of care reduces the patient’s travel and hospital costs.
Impact & Results: Our screening service allowed an identification of 196 new patients. Multidisciplinary care has reduced the patient’s financial burden. The hybrid model ensured greater buy in and compliance from patients. An analysis of our statistics indicated that 204 rehabilitation sessions were provided since 2020. Patients indicated that the service has positively impacted their quality of life. Tele rehabilitation included patients who were previously unable to attend daily therapy for 4 weeks.

Organisation: Agencia Nacional de Investigacion e Innovacion (ANII)
Description:
ANII, in collaboration with the private sector and the support of the Ministry of Health, is piloting a novel Open Innovation Initiative (MH2030). Through this new co-creative approach, local entrepreneurs work collaboratively with partnering healthcare institutions to tailor and implement digital health solutions. This not only promotes growth of the local healthtech ecosystem but also increases the capacity of healthcare institutions to innovate and solve healthcare problems.

MH2030 Open Innovation Initiative in Uruguay challenges the traditional method of incubating and accelerating start-ups by involving relevant stakeholders with potential users (e.g. hospital directors, physicians, IT heads, etc.), from problem definition and conceptualization to the development/prototyping and implementation of pilot solutions.

The objectives of this initiative are three-fold:
1) Promote entrepreneurial growth in the health tech sector
2) Foster the development of a creative and innovative culture within healthcare organisations
3) Showcase value of innovation (i.e.. optimisation of processes and resources, improvement of health outcomes).

The implementation of this Initiative in Uruguay is a 2-year multistep process designed to achieve the aforementioned goals.
Novelty:
MH2030 is a local innovation in entrepreneurship programs:

1) Customer-centric approach to innovation. 
2) Cooperation is a core principle. 
3) It originates from multi-sectoral partnerships.
4) By being part of MH2030 Global Community, this initiative benefits from knowledge-exchange of international partners.
Impact & Results:
USD 120k secured in funding (MoD signed by ANII and partners)
3 healthcare institutions participating in the end-to-end process (Collaboration agreement signed)
Over 15 experts (physicians, managers, IT) actively involved (registered in roster).
7 entrepreneurs in health working collaboratively, signed ToR (out of over 20 applications).
Over 6h of collaborative work in a 15-week period (weekly activity report).
2 masterclass training sessions - 50 participants 
Monday Project Manager Software and Miro used as follow-up and collaboration tools.

Project/Initiative Name: Pilot with the Municipality District of Ate Vitarte - Lima
Description:
In May 2021, the Municipality of Ate, was facing issues with their service to its neighbour's due to the Covid-19 pandemic. Because of this, our strategic partner FEMULP connected us with Ate, area that requested GovLink the implementation of a pilot to perform public opinion poll, in order to gather relevant information about priority issues for the community, so as to improve the municipal responses facing the Covid-19 and focus the attention on women to generate a solidarity economy.

GovLink is a platform that helps to articulate alliances between the GovTech’s ecosystem actors and solve asymmetries between the supply-demand, allowing the civil servants to know and get acquaintance to the technological supply, through pilot programmes, so that they can be aware of which kind of technology they could buy, before thinking about the Public Purchase of Innovation (PPI) processes. In this way, the civil servants get trained and retain relevant information that will allow them to produce functional requirements. All this occurs within the framework of blind selection exercises that favor the fight against corruption, inclusion and identity. 

The specific objectives of the pilot:
-  (1) To boost the abilities in the civil servants selected by the Ate Municipality in the use of the innovation methods; 
- (2) Link the Municipality of Ate with one or various international GovTech start-ups with validated solutions and perform a blind selection to choose one to carry out the proposed pilot exercise; and 
- (3) Implement a validation exercise of small scale, that incorporates the layout, the demonstration and test of the technological solution, to be able to know the basic configuration, content and other characteristics that prove the functionality and performance of the solution.
Novelty: The way it focuses on training public servants, and how its design mitigates risk of corruption.
Impact & Results:
Positive feedback was received in the satisfaction surveys 
100% of the civil servants were able to know, at least one new technological solution that they didn’t know before. 
100% of the trained officials are experts today in at least one new methodology for innovation or in the framing of problems
90% of the trained civil servants have expressed a high level of satisfaction 
80% of the trained civil servants claim to have the ability to apply the new methodology learnt.
80% of the civil servants consider that they can elaborate a functional technical requirement
100% of the planned activities for the implementation of the pilot were successfully completed.

Project/Initiative Name: FDIC - FinCEN Digital Identity Tech Sprint
Organisation: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN), U.S. Department of the Treasury
Description:
On April 4, 2022, the FDIC and FinCEN Digital Identity Tech Sprint concluded with a Demonstration Day where 8 teams demonstrated their solutions to experts. The FDIC and FinCEN challenged them to develop solutions in 3 weeks to help measure the effectiveness of digital identity proofing, the first step in customer onboarding. The event broke new ground, crowd sourcing solutions from multi-disciplinary teams to address a shared problem fundamental to building trust in remote financial services.

Digital identity proofing is a foundational element to enable digital financial services to function properly. This element is challenged by the proliferation of compromised personally identifiable information (PII), the increasing use of synthetic identities, and the presence of multiple, varied approaches for identity proofing.
Novelty: The event broke new ground, crowd sourcing solutions from multi-disciplinary teams over a 3 week period, with minimal training to address a shared problem for both the public and private sector.
Impact & Results: The Key Takeaways and Summaries are found here https://www.fincen.gov/news/news-releases/fdic-fincen-digital-identity-tech-sprint-key-takeaways-and-solution-summaries

Description:
There are some structural challenges that characterize the Italian tourism sector. As first, tourism offer is independently managed at regional level and this is a limiting factor to integrated, cross-regional experiences. Next, stakeholders are characterized by a wide digital divide and capability to penetrate markets and attract new demand. Finally, seasonality and overtourism have impacts on the tourism flows, which require a re-distribution over the year and across destinations. In light of this, the creationof the Tourism Digital Hub enables the connection of the entire tourism ecosystem to enhance, integrate and foster the service offer. The investment aims at creating a digital ecosystem, adopting a data-driven approach. The Hub will provide a minimum set of digital services and tools enabling operators to increase the quality of the digital offer.

Tourism Digital Hub aims at 3 strategical objectives:
• Hub as a bridge among tourist and the whole ecosystem
• Hub as big data aggregator and data asset enhancement in an open data approach.
• Hub as an integrated system to increase the digitization of the tourism offer.

The real key to the success of TDH is entrusted to data enhancement and interoperability, which is intended as the key enabler to support collaboration, data sharing and data driven approaches among stakeholders. To this regard, we have been developing an interoperability framework – called TDH022 – to set the standard for our program partners. Key components of the framework include common tourism ontologies and guidelines of semantic management.
Novelty: As opposed to the previously tried “static” websites, the project, for the first time, combines and connects the most up-to-date tourism content in a capillary and interoperable way, fully enhancing the richness and cross-cutting character of Italy's tourism heritage, leading to a strong push toward digitalisation of its many stakeholders, particularly SMEs. It is a dynamic and ever-evolving platform thanks to the constant input of content and continuous users’ feedback.
Impact & Results: To accelerate the return of value and investment, the Tourism Digital Hub is under development by adopting an Agile delivery methodology. The awarding of contracts for the development of the portal was successfully completed in 2021 and the project has now entered its implementation phase. No measurement of impact available yet.

Project/Initiative Name: E-Surv: Sustainable Data Collection Through Community Engagement
Organisation: Ministry of Health and Human Services, Ekiti State
Description:
The Government of Ekiti State working with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology Governance Lab (MIT/GOVLAB) has developed a community-based reporting system called “E-Surv”. E-Surv enables the participation of community members in public health surveillance by reporting public health events/emergencies, to activate a time-efficient government-led response. This E-Surv which has been piloted in the State will create an early warning system for disease surveillance and laboratory diagnosis.

E-surv is a web application that consists of a USSD and WhatsApp Chatbot interface for post-vaccination tracking (PVT) and it also contains a Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) to monitor diagnosis in the same web application. The post-vaccination tracking system (PVT) ensures the effective follow-up of persons who have received the COVID-19 vaccine for any side effects. The Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS allows for effective management of the flow of laboratory samples and associated data to improve lab efficiency and turnaround time while providing accurate controls of the process. The PVT and LIMS are the first phase of a broader plan to fully deploy the technology as a tool that engages members of the community in disease outbreak surveillance not just for COVID-19 but for all other infectious diseases.

Working under the leadership of the Honourable Commissioner, E-Surv innovation was developed by Civil Servants in the Ekiti State Ministry of Health who participated in the Governance Innovation Lifecycle Challenge and Accelerator. The Governance Innovation Lifecycle Challenge and Accelerator is a partnership between MIT/GOVLAB and the Ekiti State government. This innovation is already institutionalised as it is domiciled in the Ministry of Health’s Department of Public Health. Its administration is led by the State Epidemiologist who oversees the Public Health Emergency Operations Centre (PHEOC), responsible for coordinating all disease response activities including surveillance, laboratory, monitoring and evaluation among other pillars. The E-Surv has been piloted among 37,884 persons in the State and lessons learned from this pilot phase will be used to scale the project to the entire State, leveraging the existing State PHEOC structure.
Novelty:
The E-Surv community-based reporting system is innovative because:
1. It was conceptualised and designed by the Honourable Commissioner and developed by Civil Servants in Ekiti State using the Governance Innovation Framework that is grounded in human-centred design.
2. The E-Surv is the first of its kind in Nigeria that attempts to improve government effectiveness and efficiency for disease outbreak response by using technology to expedite laboratory diagnosis while engaging citizens in disease reporting and surveillance.
3. The USSD code and WhatsApp chatbot used by the E-Surv are simple technologies that are widely available in low-resource settings.
Impact & Results:
The E-Surve was introduced in January 2022. As of July 2022, the USSD and WhatsApp chatbots had been piloted among 3.2% (37,884) of the total vaccinated population. Of this pilot group, 2,251 persons responded to the USSD code and 48 persons responded to the WhatsApp chatbot making a total of 2,299 persons (6.1% of the pilot group) who responded. Of this number, 744 persons (32%) reported AEFIs. Using this data, the State surveillance team has been able to provide post-vaccination follow-up to identify severe AEFI cases and refer them to hospitals for observation and treatment. This follow-up is also used as an opportunity to provide correct information on AEFIs, to avoid the spread of false information to persons yet to be vaccinated. This data is recorded in the State surveillance system and will be used to monitor trends in AEFIs for use in research and decision making.
It is expected that by December 2022, there will be a 50% increase among the pilot group who utilise the E-Surv.

Project/Initiative Name: Implementation of the Centro-Gare creative district
Description:
To recover the social fabric and revitalise the pioneering urban space in the city, the City Council collectively promoted the creation of the creative district Centro-Gare. The choice of this innovation habitat aims to transform the area through creative entrepreneurship, culture and innovation while improving the environment for the local population. This is a unique initiative, as the leadership of the process was carried out by the initiative of the public authority together with its ecosystem.

This work triggered the launch of the creative district in the year 2022 and the institutionalisation of its respective governance. At present, a collective task force composed of various institutions from the different helixes of the local ecosystem (Government - University - Productive Sector - Society) has been implementing all the actions foreseen in the collective plan under the leadership of the City Hall. 

The method developed was based on the principles of design thinking, with 06 online workshops, electronic questionnaires and collection points where the various representatives of the local ecosystem exposed the challenges of the territory and possibilities for solutions, triggering at the end the action plan with a view to implementing the Centro-Gare creative district. The whole process was based on four fundamental pillars for the constitution of a creative district: natural and built environment, creative economy, identity and cultural resources, and governance and public policies.
Novelty: This is a unique initiative of revitalisation of abandoned urban spaces led by a municipal government in partnership with the other actors of the local innovation and creative ecosystem. In European cases, movements to form innovation and creative districts were initiated by groups of entrepreneurs, and later adopted by public policies, also in these cases having a very strong appeal to boost tourism activities and that ended up provoking gentrification processes.
Impact & Results: The main and initial impact of the innovation was the official launch of the creative district in 2022, together with the website and social networks of the innovation habitat.
