Furthermore, the governance of the creative district has already been constituted and, from there, the process of implementation of the actions established in the collective plan has been conducted in each of the basic pillars of the process (Natural and built environment, Creative Economy, Identity and cultural resources, and governance and public policies).

Project/Initiative Name: Transforming the City Council-taxpayer relationship through Behavioural Sciences: the IPTU (property tax) case and the tax compliance agenda
Organisation: São Paulo City Hall, Municipal Secretariat for Innovation and Technology (SMIT), Innovation in Government Laboratory
Description:
Currently the relationship between City Hall and taxpayers is based on repressive inspections, difficult-to-understand communications and complex payment flows. Just the reformulation of the IPTU (property tax) collection has increased municipal revenue by R$60 million/year. It is estimated to increase collection by around R$46 million per year in the ISS collection experiments. This project was carried out in partnership with the Municipal Finance Department (SF), started in August 2021.

The IPTU is issued annually and collected through letters sent to the taxpayer's property. The taxpayer has the option to pay in a single installment, obtaining a 3% discount on the amount, or to pay the tax in 2 to 10 installments. Despite the notice, a little less than half of the debtors regularise their situation within the deadline. As a result, the taxpayer registered in CADIN is prevented from receiving aid or tax incentives and from carrying out any business with the City Council. 

A standard letter was produced, which reorganised and deleted information and simplified the language used. From this version, different behavioural principles were applied to understand what would work best in the context of the municipality of São Paulo. The following behavioural principles were used: social norm, deliberate choice, salience of consequences, visual illustration. There were then 5 treatment letters and 1 control letter (original). Notices were sent to 12,310 taxpayers, totaling 25,000 letters. Payment was followed up within 30 days of receipt of the letter.
Novelty:
1. it employs a new theme in the Brazilian context and in the global south. In the literature review that supported the project, there were few initiatives carried out outside developed countries.  
2. It applies a robust and rigorous methodology for causal analysis. With this, it is possible to prove the impact of the proposed new prototype. 
3. Contributes to the culture of evidence-based public policies, experimentation and innovation in the public sector.The result of the experimentation on IPTU opens the door to other more challenging and risky projects.
4. Transforms the tax-payer relationship by taking into account the payment profile of citizens based on tax compliance behaviour.
Impact & Results:
The innovation brought short, medium and long-term results. 
In the short term, the pilot test alone was able to increase tax collection by R$ 950 thousand (195,000 EUR)
In the medium term, the impact calculations of the implementation of the more effective card on a large scale indicated an increase in tax collection of R$ 60 millio (>11,5 million EUR).

Description:
The language in the Brazilian public sector has a history of being complex, full of acronyms, technical terms, etc. This makes the population unable to understand the language used by the government. The Simple Language Programme is the first Brazilian public policy aimed at simplifying the language of government, training public servants to communicate in a simpler way and supporting the simplification of documents. 

The Municipal Plain Language Program seeks to promote change in the entire language of public administration of the city of São Paulo, with more objective communications reducing comprehension noises and rework, increasing the agility and efficiency of public management. 

The Program is divided into the following fronts:
- Train City Hall servers to communicate in a simpler way, through face-to-face and remote workshops with their own methodology;
- Mobilising and publicising the plain language agenda at City Hall, in order to strengthen the importance of the topic, through participation in events and conferences.
- Simplify public documents so that anyone can locate, understand and use the information transmitted by the Public Administration, through simplification projects and mentoring with city hall teams.

The construction of the programme benefits from the assembly of teams with different professional backgrounds and experiences, which in addition to bringing the necessary diversity, makes it possible to think in different dimensions and make the project more robust.

As a result, LAW No. 17,316 of March 6, 2020 was implemented, which institutes the Municipal Policy for Plain Language in the bodies of the direct and indirect administration, in addition to Decree No. 59,067 of November 11, 2019, which established the Programme. Since then, more than 5000 male and female public servants have been trained, adding the scope of training workshops offered in the face-to-face and virtual models, and the scope of the Programme's EAD Course, which adds up to more than 16,000 enrolments. In addition, 8 public documents of the City Hall of São Paulo were simplified. 

This has an impact both on the population's understanding of the documents and services offered by the City Hall, and on the civil servants, whose work processes have been made easier because they are hindered by communication.
Novelty: The simple language programme becomes innovative because it uses structured and applied experimentation methods throughout the programme cycle. Adopting experimentation as a process allows exploring, experimenting, evaluating and proposing incremental changes in all actions, bringing the target audience into the process of designing the actions and planning the programme. It is also the first public policy in Brazil with the specific objective of simplifying the language of government to generate improvement in public services and public value for the population. As an innovation, there was the elaboration in the construction of the decree that reflects what is done in practice within the programme, generating an integrated approach that enhances the value and increases the scope of the programme by engaging different secretariats, public agencies and civil society in the agenda.
Impact & Results:
In 2021 alone, two years after its launch, the programme had 7,000 enrolled in the distance learning course, 247 trained public servants, 10 training workshops held, 40 bodies impacted, and 14 secretariats reached.
These results were measured based on the registration of the people who attended the workshops, using a form that collects data such as the agency in which the person works, secretary, among others. The data related to the distance learning course is measured from the ENAP website.

Project/Initiative Name: TRENSURB ESG Network - Inclusion of the remote ones.
Organisation: Porto Alegre's Urban Railway Enterprise - TRENSURB
Description:
The inclusion of people living in distant areas is one of the main purposes of Porto's strategy towards 2030. The Municipality created the TRENSURB ESG NETWORK; where the metropolitan trains and their networks integrate and enhance local agents together with local users to find solutions and the co-creation of practical actions oriented to connect people living in distant areas, with emphasis on the low-income population.

This dynamic has focused on the metropolitan trains as an important key element, because at least twice a day, the network concentrates people living in distant areas in significant quantity. This situation allows for a unique and valuable opportunity to  involve these communities in quick rounds of information, knowledge and co-creation. Improved connectivity is part of Porto's SDG agenda. 

The program has the following features as highlights:
1) It highlights the importance of distant people as indispensable agents to co-produce solutions related to connectivity. 
2) Identifies metropolitan trains as an important key element, because at least 2 times a day, it concentrates distant people in significant quantity, in a timely and valuable circumstance to motivate participation by creating typical forms of information, knowledge and learning.
3) Creates solutions to genuine problems through practical actions decided by the participation of groups involved in critical situations.
4) Conceives a model managed through the discipline of Risk Management.
Novelty: The ESG TRENSURB NETWORK - Inclusion of the remote ones is an innovative project because of the way we have chosen and intertwined the elements to create an active and diligent dynamic that addresses specific solutions related to connectivity issues. These elements are the local train networks around Porto, users living in distant areas, and other stakeholders involved in the mobility and operations within the area.

Description:
The "startuphubmaroc.ma" platform was launched to respond, in a progressive manner, to the complaints expressed by Moroccan startups.
It allows to enhance and increase the visibility of startups and initiatives carried by the actors of the Ecosystem. And to propose an end-to-end online support offer.
This platform provides online access to consolidated information and features that were previously focused on physical interaction.

he platform has been developed using an agile methodology in co-construction with the different actors of the ecosystem to progressively respond to the major part of the grievances of the startup ecosystem.

To meet the challenge of access to information, the platform has set up 
- A repository of initiatives, structured by startup development phase and by type of initiative. The repository provides simple and fluid access to information on the support measures offered by the various organisations;
- A directory of startups, listing startups and increasing their visibility among initiative holders and principals, via voluntary registration and 100% personalised profiles. 
- An ecosystem observatory for a summary of the platform's statistics and the startup ecosystem (number of startups listed, regional distribution of startups, number of initiatives and initiative holders, etc.).

In a second phase, the platform has set up :
- A video library section: to structure the different media related to the startup ecosystem
- A mentoring section: to provide online support to aspiring entrepreneurs
- A co-founder section
- An Innovation Centre section
- A recruitment section
Novelty: The innovation is that the platform allows, in co-construction with the different actors of the startup ecosystem, to respond in a structured and progressive way to the grievances of the startup ecosystem and to ensure visibility for the said actors and online support for the startups from start to finish (awareness and acculturation, training, financing, technical and functional support...)
Impact & Results:
To date, the platform has more than 280 referenced startups and more than 240 initiatives led by 30 initiative holders. It also has 50 mentors mobilised to support future entrepreneurs.
The number of users in 2022 to date is : 4732
The platform aims to register more than 60 startups per year.

Description:
Register of Administrative procedures (RAP) and its public portal provide information about more than 2300 procedures for citizens and businesses within competences of more than 90 authorities in Serbia – at one place. RAP's Portal is a single window for services of public authorities in Serbia. It makes public services easy, transparent, predictable and accessible. By modernization of public services, we have ensured savings for businesses of 33 million euros annually. 

RAP’s model, as example of good practice, has raised interest in the countries of the region, especially in Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia and Montenegro. Croatia is currently working on establishment of similar register, based on the RAP's model. In 2023, RAP will further expand, as it will include all administrative procedures for citizens, administrative procedures delivered by local authorities and administrative requests, in order to provide comprehensive catalogue of requirements for conducting business in a legal manner.
Novelty:
RAP is a unique place with all information about public services. Service users no longer have to call service providers or go to institutions to find out what they need for a permit, license, etc. Now, they can simply and easily find all up-to-date information in one place.
RAP offers user-friendly, relevant information about the procedures, their workflow and cost. RAP makes procedures predictable and transparent. Through optimization process, we make procedures clear and easy, avoiding legal uncertainty. Such symbiosis proved that we make services more accessible and time- and money-saving for end users. Also, RAP enables public administration to harmonize service delivery by following established standards and increase efficiency and transparency of its work.
The ePermit BPM Platform enables fast and efficient digitization of services, as well as the processing of submitted requests in a unique way for public administration.
Impact & Results: So far (in the last 8 months), RAP's Portal recorded 26 000 visits, and 4 700 new users. Together with implemented recommendations for optimization of administrative procedures (as part of Program ePAPIR), we provided annual savings for businesses amounting € 30 million. In the process of optimizing procedures, 21 procedures were abolished, 345 procedures were simplified and 64 procedures were digitized. These joint efforts led to reduction of administrative burden as a share of GDP, from 3,26 in 2016 to 3,01% in 2021

Project/Initiative Name: Improvement of the methodology for monitoring the area under coca cultivation
Organisation: National Commission for Development and Life without Drugs (DEVIDA)
Description:
The monitoring involves a methodology that includes 4 phases. The first phase consists of the collection and selection of satellite images in the monitoring area. In the second phase, the selected images undergo a restoration process using geographic software to improve their quality. Once the restored images are available, the third phase begins, which consists of the visual detection, delimitation and quantification of coca cultivation. This phase involves cross-checking with other sources of territorial information such as the audiovisual record of coca plots, the contrast with other satellite images, and the reduction of hectares due to the eradication of illicit crops. Finally, in the fourth phase, a process of revision, cross-checking and adjustment of the results obtained in the previous phase is carried out. 

The application of the methodology requires a specialised technical team and access to technological resources. In terms of technological resources, monitoring makes use of very high and high resolution satellite images (PeruSAT, SPOT 6/7, KompSAT, Pleiades, Gaofen and KazeoSAT), remote sensing software and specialised hardware (such as work stations, spectroradiometers, servers and other storage systems).

Relying on these same human and technological resources, in 2022, improvements were made to the monitoring methodology. Two of the main improvements made were the creation of an early warning mechanism that broadened the scope of monitoring, and the creation of an algorithm that automated the restoration phase of the satellite images, optimising the time of the process. With regard to early warnings, a procedure was implemented that took advantage of information provided by a series of actors with a presence in the territory, outside the 14 zones monitored in the previous year. This information came from indigenous organisations and public institutions responsible for natural protected areas and indigenous peoples. The early warning process involved the standardisation of the information to maps, as well as its contrast with freely available satellite images (such as Sentinel-2 and Google Earth), with complementary information and information collected in the field. The results confirmed the existence of 5 new monitoring areas (Amazonas, Lower Ucayali, Lower Huallaga, Camanti and Madre de Dios).

Regarding the automation of the satellite image restoration phase, the improvement consisted of the development and application of an algorithm that allowed the processes of import, orthorectification, radiometric calibration, atmospheric correction, fusion and mosaic, which were previously done manually, to be carried out. The first version of the algorithm was developed in 2021 as part of a pilot study. This allowed the algorithm to be improved and applied to images from the PeruSAT, SPOT6/7, Pleiades 1A/1B and Kompsat-3 satellites used in the areas monitored in 2022. Whereas in 2021, a total of 300 scenes were manually restored by 3 people in approximately 100 days, in 2022, 300 scenes were restored in approximately 20 days by automated means.
Novelty:
The improvements implemented to the methodological process of coca cultivation monitoring implemented by Devida. 1) the creation of a standardised procedure that allowed the verification and incorporation of information provided by citizens and public institutions with presence in territories to which Devida does not have access. 
2) the creation of a unique algorithm that automates the satellite image restoration phase. Both improvements, applied with the same human and technological resources, have resulted in the production of information in less time, with a wider territorial scope, and without generating greater expense for the State.
Impact & Results:
In summary, these two improvements in the methodology applied in 2022, allowed to extend the scope of the monitoring (from 14 to 19 zones), and to reduce the data delivery time (from 10 to 7 months).

The result was achieved through the application of two innovations. First, the creation of an early warning procedure and its incorporation into the monitoring methodology. Second, the creation of an algorithm that allowed the automation of the second phase of the methodology.

Organisation: Secretariat of Innovation and Digital Transformation of the City of Buenos Aires
Description:
The objective is to build an ecosystem of digital interactions on the self-sovereign digital identity protocol agreed with the community. Facilitate the use of the protocol by a critical mass of users so that the ecosystem takes on a decentralised life of its own.

The expected benefits: greater sovereignty of individuals over their information, return of time currently spent on paperwork and bureaucracy to individuals and reduction of transactional costs to organisations. The existence of a digital identity governed by individuals and backed by the state will enable new usage scenarios and, among other things, increase reliability and sustainability over time.

It is a protocol that will facilitate and streamline the completion of procedures and all kinds of actions associated with identity, and at the same time enable new ways of sharing information and providing access to services provided by all kinds of institutions, public and private. It should be decentralised, public, non-permissive, open, extensible and capable of interoperating with other similar protocols.

The GCABA (Government of the City of Buenos Aires) will provide a critical mass of digital identities that will serve as a "bootstrapping" of the digital ecosystem and give it enough relevance to attract both new users and new institutions and companies, thus achieving a dynamic that will allow it to sustain and grow autonomously.

A first application is being developed, sponsored by the GCBA, which allows access and interoperability between all the services provided by the government through common standards, so that users can perceive concrete benefits from a very early stage. It will start to be used by the end of 2022, and will gradually bring a critical mass of users of more than 5,000,000 people gradually during 2023-2024.
Novelty:
The project is innovative, because it is a co-creation between government, business and ecosystem, where the government provides the critical mass for adoption, ensuring its success.
- It uses the most modern Blocchain technology, DID (decentralised identifiers), DAPS (decentralised autonomous applications) among others.
- It is multi-network compatible, meaning that it can be used in different existing Blockchain networks and interrelate with other existing and future applications and ecosystems.
- Governance and its operation are decentralised, it is not in the hands of any actor.
- Identity information remains in the hands and control of the individual, changing the current paradigm and providing new rights.
Impact & Results:
We expect the ecosystem to have more than 5,000,000 users by 2022/2023/2024.
-As a result, individuals are empowered to reach an open, trusted, secure and transparent ecosystem, built on a decentralised architecture, where trust is backed by the network and not by centralised entities.
-Enhancing relationships between individuals and institutions (public and private) based on trust, with technological safeguards backed by the users themselves.
-Streamline transaction times, simplify the process of identity verification and compliance requirements, thus giving time back to individuals and reducing costs for businesses.

Organisation: Convergence.Tech (working with the Australian Taxation Office)
Description: In response to the Australian Government’s National Blockchain Roadmap, the Blockchain Excise Platform digitises and tracks a taxable commodity (in this case pure litres of alcohol). It connects producers and regulator with real-time access to new data, generating productivity benefits and reducing illicit activity.  A first of its kind blockchain ecosystem connecting the regulator to industry to more effectively control the movement, transfer and payment of liabilities for an excise commodity.
Novelty:
Excise duty collection hasn’t changed much in its 100-year history. As the regulator lacks visibility over the creation, storage and movement of spirit, it must impose stringent administrative requirements to ensure compliance. Without trusted data the regulator needs strict rules to ensure dishonest businesses aren’t competing unfairly, in the process creating an administrative burden for honest operators.
 
The BEP generates this data via a real-time ledger, digitising and tracking the excisable commodity across production and distribution. Whilst the Pilot focused on alcohol, it is extensible to other excisable commodities (e.g. beer, fuel, tobacco, hydrogen), delivering significant benefits for regulators, industry and taxpayers.

The foundational infrastructure provides digital identities for a list of “Trusted Distillers” registered with the government, enabling digitization of verifiable certifications, licenses, and documents to further reduce administrative burden and paper.
Impact & Results:
The expected benefits from a full roll-out were estimated by KPMG Australia, for government:
- Generate an additional $45m in tax revenue p.a. by reducing illicit activity
- Improve detection of non-compliance by access to granular commodity production and supply chain data
- Automate and reduce manual compliance activities to enable more efficient use of resources through data-driven insights and industry transparency
For industry
- Improved traceability enhances provenance, brand protection, and levels the competitive playing field by reducing the ability for bad actors to evade paying excise
- Removal & automation of compliance processes reduces red tape
- The digitisation of assets on the blockchain creates new revenue opportunities

Project/Initiative Name: Not just an App, but an ecosystem: JAKI as a digital public space in Jakarta
Description:
Bureaucracy in Jakarta faced 3 main problems: (1) scattered & unstandardized data, (2) siloed systems, and (3) unintegrated services. This made public service inefficient and costly, eventually decreasing public service quality. JAKI is a one-stop digital platform that integrates various public services and accelerates digital transformations for its all. It is developed with citizen-design services and customer centricity, personalised services, and provides open data ecosystem aims to co-create with stakeholders. 

In the future, JAKI aims to integrate all public services in 1 app, including inclusive finance, public information and public service interactions as well as to become an ecosystem that is applicable and can be developed by other regions in the context of transforming government and responding to digital challenges in city development.
Impact & Results:
JAKI Impact:
General (SDG 16)
- User growth with 4 Mil users (Aug 31st, 2022)
- Save $14,351,913 opportunity cost yearly
- >60 integrated services with >80 features with aim to integrate all public services in 1 app including inclusive finance, public information and public service interactions

Digital Governance (SDGs 3, 11 & 16)
- Active citizen participation in public complaints. JAKI is a primary reporting system with 97% resolution rate & reduced submission time (1 day to 2-3 mins) & resolution time (approx. 5 days, compared to 30 days regulated time). Citizens can directly monitor & evaluate the results online
- Increased vaccination participation by reducing registration waiting time from 30 days to 2-3 mins

Digital Economy (SDGs 1 & 2)
- Food commodity prices can be monitored in real time, enabling social assistance delivery for low-income citizens as well as giving assistance to 300,000 MSMEs

Digital Society (SDGs 17)
- 9,302 public Wi-Fi hotspots in Jakarta to support remote activity

Description:
The PANAVAC19 information system was created in response to the country challenge of controlling the entire vaccination process against COVID-19 and the requirements of availability, reliability and traceability of information imposed by the response to the pandemic. The initiative was awarded by UN Public Service Awards. 

The population was massively summoned taking into account prioritisation factors and proximity to authorised vaccination centres, and appointments were notified to the population through emails and a web portal (https://panamasolidario.gob.pa/pag/vacunas) searchable by ID number. 

In April, the portal provided the option to manage appointments on a personal basis by selecting the centre, date and time. Between May and June, the procedures module went into production from www.panamadigital.gob.pa, closing the lifecycle of vaccination data by giving citizens the ability to manage their information digitally. 

In October, vaccination by spontaneous demand was opened at fixed points throughout the country. And finally, in September, the European Parliament declared Panama's Covid-19 information system interoperable, reliable and equivalent to the EU DCC standard, facilitating cross-border movement to and from Europe. 

Recently, in November, the AIG and MINSA have started a process of transferring the knowledge, processes and technology of the information system to be managed and operated by the Ministry of Health. The work is completed with a baseline diagnostic document prior to project implementation and an ex-post evaluation.
Novelty: The system offers several previously unheard-of services: notifying each person of all their immunisation information within minutes of being vaccinated, offering the state the ability to see in near real time the inventory of each brand and batch of vaccine in the various biological repositories, and finally, it has revolutionised the management and presentation of Covid-19 immunisation information by ensuring the privacy, security and cross-border interoperability of the digital certificates and QR codes that are issued. It has favoured economic reactivation by generating "100% vaccinated" spaces, and free-to-use mobile applications to check it.
Impact & Results: The evaluation revealed progress and opportunities for improvement in the following areas: "preparation" is analysed from the perspective of the internal user and states that investment should be made both in training competencies in health human capital and in IT infrastructure. The "quality" sub-index assesses the value added by the system in information management from the perspective of the external user. The "impact" sub-index assesses that despite the social, economic and political impact achieved, there are risks such as the lack of a regulatory framework to guarantee the adoption and sustainability of the project.

Organisation: Auditor General of the Republic of Colombia
Description:
In order to address the recent constitutional reform, a methodology was designed and implemented based on the evaluation of management indicators to measure and qualify the capacities of the 66 territorial comptrollers' offices. 

The purpose is to achieve continuous improvement in the objective and efficient fulfilment of the constitutional and legal functions of the territorial comptrollers' offices. It aims to: 
o Effectively characterise the management and results of the territorial comptrollers' offices.
o Define the criteria, processes and indicators that support a comprehensive evaluation of territorial comptrollers' offices.
o Improve the quality, timeliness and consistency of the information generated by territorial comptrollers' offices as an input for decision-making in fiscal control.
o Unify the legal, technical and methodological criteria for the exercise of fiscal control in Colombia.
o Increase efficiency and effectiveness in the value chain in territorial fiscal control and promote the exercise of social control.

The formula created by the AGR to annually establish the critical score (minimum score to obtain certification), which is not static, suggests improvement because it is based on statistical facts from the overall performance of the comptrollers' offices obtained during the previous year.

Each year the critical score is expected to increase as the average overall performance of the comptrollers' offices improves and the dispersion of their scores (standard deviation) decreases, which means that the comptrollers' offices would be sustainably improving their capacities for the objective and efficient performance of their functions and, therefore, strengthening territorial fiscal control.
Novelty: The experience in itself does not replicate or adopt any existing model; the innovation lies in the possibility of real-time monitoring of the management of territorial comptrollers' offices, highlighting their deficiencies, so that comptrollers can direct resources and efforts to the continuous improvement of their management, through qualitative and quantitative variables, and an Integral Performance Index, which allows measuring and rating, leading to a better public function aimed at the protection of public resources.
Impact & Results:
1. The estimate of the final performance assessment (Comprehensive Performance Index) of the territorial comptrollers' offices increased on average from one measurement period to another; in this sense, for the cut-off on 31 December 2020 it was 77.29, while for 31 December 2021 it was 78.8, a situation that represents an improvement in management and results.
2. The number of process indicators located in the lowest risk level increased from 63.5% to 64.2% between each year's evaluations.
3. Despite the effects generated by Covid19 , the AGR issued the first annual certification within the established legal term, demonstrating efficiency and effectiveness in the construction and implementation of this new evaluation method.

Project/Initiative Name: MAC Express: Bringing public services closer to citizens
Description:
To bridge the gap between citizens and digitalised procedures, the PCM decided that the solution was to implement the "MAC Express" (Mejor Atención al Ciudadano Express) service platform, which integrates a set of digitalised procedures of public entities in an IT solution, set up in a physical space for citizen attention, with the presence of an advisor who assists citizens in carrying out the procedure in their own locality.

1st stage, the incorporation of digitised procedures into MAC Express was negotiated with public entities at a multi-sectoral level.

2nd stage: 150 technical assistance meetings were held to set up MAC Express, with 25 public entities (24 municipalities and the Ministry of Development and Social Inclusion) that provided physical spaces for citizen attention, located in strategic places.

From 2018 to date, the results obtained as a result of the strategic inter-institutional articulation have been 78 Express MACs implemented in 20 regions of the country, with 70% of the Express MACs located in the highlands or jungle, and 51% of the districts with Express MACs are rural and 49% are urban. There are also legal and administrative tools in place to ensure the sustainability of the MAC Express.
Novelty: MAC Express facilitates quick and easy access to procedures whose only provider is the State. The lack of access to state procedures in the most remote districts of the country has been a major problem for citizens, due to the geographic characteristics of Peru and the technological gaps that exist. In response to this, the PCM with MAC Express has opened up a space to bring citizens closer to a model of cooperation at a multisectoral and intergovernmental level to take advantage of the technological advances that allow the use of digitalised procedures and at the same time eliminate the barriers of geographic access.
Impact & Results:
By 2022, the MAC Express will be in 20 regions of Peru. 70% of the MAC Express are in the highlands or jungle. Of the total number of Express MACs, 14% are in the jungle and 56% in the highlands. 49% of the districts served by MAC Express are rural and 51% are urban.

The average monthly number of MAC Express visits is 5,694. Between November 2018 and 25 September 2022, 5,887 services were provided.

Expenditure on travel to carry out a procedure:
The citizens of La Selva have eliminated the expense of transfers thanks to the MAC Express service at PIAS and Tambos.

Description:
In 2020, as a result of the pandemic, the National Customs Service was unable to carry out the traditional auctions, given the sanitary restrictions, and so work began on the idea of creating a digital system that would allow it to develop electronically what was done in physical form. This led to the creation of the platform www.subastaaduanera.cl. The white march of the electronic auctions took place in December 2020, and its implementation will last from 2021 to the present day.

This project not only represents an evolution of the system, but has also helped to position the image of Chilean Customs as a public body that is at the forefront of its modernisation, bringing the institution closer to the public in an agile, egalitarian and transparent manner.
Novelty:
Auctions are now conducted 100% online at www.subastaaduanera.cl, leaving behind the traditional auction system. This major institutional change has allowed the transformation of this system, giving equal opportunities to auction participants throughout the country.

In order to operate, the dates of the auctions are publicised in different media, in order to reach the largest possible number of participants. At the end of the auction bidding period, the lot will be awarded to the highest bid.
Impact & Results:
The results to date are as follows:
1. 74,420 unique users registered on the www.subastaaduanera.cl website.
2. The auctions have resulted in a total income of $2,839,658,999 Chilean pesos.
3. The total number of lots auctioned to date amounts to 1.774.
4. The total number of bids made amounts to 42,175.

Therefore, our result is not only from an economic point of view, but it also offers the possibility for citizens to take part in customs auctions from anywhere in the country, thus opening up to groups that were limited to participate due to lack of knowledge of these auctions or because they did not have the possibility to travel to the region where the auctions were held in person.

Project/Initiative Name: National and integrative e-learning platform "Academia Raqmya"
Description:
"Academia Raqmya" is a national and inclusive e-learning platform that offers a holistic training catalog in the field of Digital, and this to accompany the national effort to implement the digital transformation roadmap, and which is in favor of three important targets; the General Public, the civil servants of the Public Administrations as well as employees from the private sector.

The national and inclusive e-learning platform "Academia Raqmya" offers a training catalog that covers the main digital disciplines: Programming, Design, Infrastructure, Networks, Artificial Intelligence, Big Data, Digital Transformation & Change Management, IoT, Industry 4.0, and more. 

For employees of the Public and Private Sectors, several courses have been designed to meet the different expectations, digital skill levels, and needs of their entities in terms of digitalization. More than 170 topics in the field of Digital will allow beneficiaries to access a continuous training content, allowing them to improve and develop new digital skills, giving access to recognized certificates.

"Academia Raqmya" is a fun and user-friendly e-learning platform, which aims to train 12,500 learners in its first year of launch, to continue to develop instructive and innovative content and to reach several targets throughout the territory for the next 5 years.
Novelty:
The national and inclusive e-learning platform "Academia Raqmya", is a first in Morocco, as it offers on a single platform, hundreds of online training courses in Asynchronous mode, covering a holistic catalog in terms of training courses and Digital themes. 

This platform aims at several targets, in several ways, namely the improvement of the skills of the collaborators of the Public and Private Sector, in addition to the Digital Acculturation of the General Public and finally the facilitation and the curation of online technological training content through a selection of very high quality in terms of renowned international technological partners.
Impact & Results:
Demystification of digital learning, growing interest in digital themes and professions, duplication of this experience at central and local levels
Learning analytics are continuously collected to monitor the impact of the platform. Beneficiary surveys are conducted to measure the Return on Experience.

2458 beneficiaries from May to August 2022 with a satisfaction rate on content and functionalities exceeding 65% of beneficiaries. Expected impacts: to establish a solid digital culture among citizens, and to contribute to the increase in digital skills of employees from the private and public sectors.

Description:
The compensation scheme applied to the period from 1 September 2020 to 28 February 2022. The Ministry of Trade, Industry and Fisheries gave the Bronnoysund Register Centre (BRC) the responsibility for developing and managing the scheme. BRC was the grant authority and thus responsible for establishing requirements regarding checks, which the auditor or authorized accountant must perform when verifying the information provided by the applicant. The key objective for the initiative was to prevent bankruptcy, unemployment, and stagnation in the Norwegian economy.

The scheme was based on common IT-solutions as Altinn and the new register platform developed by BRC, and with approval by an auditor or authorized accountant in advance of admitting the application. When the application was submitted into our system, it went through an automated process to check if the applicant met the conditions according to regulations. This enabled us to avoid spending great resources on checking applications afterwards, or reclaiming payments based on false documentation by the applicants. If the applicant met the terms, the application would be automatically processed in just a few minutes, and they would have their grant in their account within 72 hours at the latest. 

In total, 45000 applications have been processed. 97 percent of the applicants that met the terms received their grant in their account the same working day or the day after the application was submitted. The scheme was developed in less than nine weeks, together with the regulatory development. Agile system- and regulatory development methodology was used during the project and the project had a very slim project management.
Novelty: Usage of the shared public trust-infrastructure in Norway to pre-check applications as well as in guidance is new, as well as the use of external auditors to do checks and the ability to scale up as well as the automated checking of applications beforehand. The project invented a new risk model, where different sources combined is seen as a risk model to stop applications that was not eligible. In case of suspicion during the automated check, the scheme was sent to manual review. Furthermore, the new register platform BRsys enabled fast development of services.
Impact & Results: Office of the Auditor General has in its review of the compensation scheme indicated that the model is sustainable, and as such should be re-used in similar cases where compensation payouts is needed.

Project/Initiative Name: The anti-corruption approach in public procurement
Organisation: Central Office of Public Procurement (PERÚ COMPRAS)
Description:
PERU COMPRAS led the implementation of an anti-corruption approach based on Robert Klitgaard's formula, with which we have reduced buyer discretion, increased competition (average of 33.5 bids per token-product) and generated a 100% transparent environment supported by disruptive technologies, such as Blockchain, resulting in the reduction of corruption risks and the optimization of the public procurement process.

The risk of corruption within the procurement process occurs in two moments, when the buyer chooses the product (risk of directing the purchase) and when the buyer chooses the supplier (risk of undue favoritism).

To reduce the risk of corruption at the first moment we apply the standardization that allows us to standardize the technical characteristics of the goods and/or services to be acquired. The risk at the second stage is mitigated through electronic contracting, preventing the buyer from making a non-objective choice of the goods and/or service and increasing the participation of suppliers.

A 100% transparent environment is created by means of innovative technology such as Blockchain, which preserves the immutability and transparency of the procurement process information generated through the Electronic Catalogues of Framework Agreements platform, and the implementation of an Anti-Bribery Management System (ISO 37001 standard) that ensures that bribery risks are minimized.
Novelty:
We are the first country in the world to apply blockchain technology in public procurement. With blockchain, the integrity of the information of electronic purchase orders, quotations and deliveries is preserved, in order to minimize the risks of corruption that may occur during the process, since this technology, due to its characteristics, is very secure and verifiable from a quick response code (QR). 

We are the only organization in Peru that publishes its open data in the Open Contracting Data Standard, OCDS.
Impact & Results: Since its implementation, in April 2019, until the end of July of this year, we have immutated 684,505 documents, guaranteeing their immutability and transparency.

Project/Initiative Name: Modernization and digitization of the family visitation service for inmates in correctional facilities
Organisation:
ADD : Digital Development Agency
DGAPR: General Directorate for Prison Administration and Rehabilitation
Description:
ADD and DGAPR have collaborated to develop a digital platform 'Ziara' that aims to simplify and dematerialize interactions between visitors and prisons in order to deliver quality public service and strengthen family ties for inmates.

This solution will improve and optimize the visitor experience, dematerialize paper-based procedures, lighten the load on the agents involved and reduce the waiting time for visitors.

**Problem to be solved:
The Ziara project simplifies and dematerializes the interactions between visitors and prisons in order to deliver a quality public service to visitors, based on digital.
This need is justified by:
- A major social issue around the visit path: 85k inmates, 2.5 million visits made per year ;
- A large-scale prison administration (DGAPR): 77 prison directors and members of the central administration, ~20,000 DGAPR employees in Morocco ;
- A visit pathway experience suffering from several constraints: Paper-based procedures, non-optimized physical routes and spaces, understaffing of mobilized officers, significant waiting time before the visit.

The platform will be enriched by other functionalities, namely:
- Implementation of an online food payment solution
- Implementation of an online money transfer solution.
- Implementation of a facial recognition module to accelerate the identification process of visitors
- Implementation of a digital space for the benefit of inmates: access via armored terminals, purchase of canteen, react to visit appointments, get information)
- Dematerialize the process of visits by organizations and associations
Novelty:
'- Adoption of the agile methodology & Design thinking for the implementation of the solution. Several personas were identified and involved (Managers, Agents, Visitors, ...) to design the platform.
- Use of an innovative technical foundation based on recent and innovative technologies.
- The project is multidimensional and deals with the following aspects 
    - Digital transformation of the public administration.
    - Social inclusion and minimization of the digital divide.
    - Facilitation of the reintegration of prisoners.
Impact & Results:
For the pilot site: 
- Number of enrolled visitors, as of 9/29/2022: 9106.
- Number of appointments made on the platform: 6448.
- Number of food purchases ordered on the platform: 2721.
- Number of agent accounts created: 51 users.

Demonstration of a strong desire on the part of visitors to benefit from the system's functionalities.
**Dematerialization of business processes:
Dematerialization of 80% of the processes for managing the family visit of inmates.
**Increased visibility and traceability of operations related to the visit.

Description:
The digital map book of the District Mobility Secretariat is an ideal tool to represent all the information related to mobility and to facilitate the analysis of Bogotá's mobility problems from geographic perspectives.

This digital book is a geographic tool that was created through the Experience Builder tool of the Esri company, which also allows citizens in general to have open information related to mobility and current scenarios, as well as information on infrastructure and transportation projects that are being analyzed and developed in the short, medium and long term. This allows citizens to visualize, know, interact and relate to mobility data in the city, contributing to open government and urban competitiveness.

One of the great features of the map book is that it is designed to work with different integrated databases, thus allowing to generate useful information for decision making and facilitate finding spatial patterns that help determine the phenomena of interest such as congestion, noise, acquisition of combustion vehicles, among others.

The current maps are divided into two formats: GIF and "static", and are supported by audiovisual material and images of the different topics addressed in this book. The map book is in a platform of the District Secretariat called Observatory of Mobility of Bogota, which among its functions is to promote the creation of spaces for discussion and exchange of knowledge and experiences with other entities of the District Administration, citizens and/or relevant actors in the field of urban mobility with the Observatory. In a second version, it is desired to integrate real-time information on the behavior of the different indicators in the city.
Novelty:
The map book is innovative in the way it integrates different indicators related to Bogota's mobility. By using Esri's Experience Builder tool, it was possible to integrate dynamic maps and audiovisual content that reinforces the geographic material, in addition to linking already developed platforms that integrate with the information presented.

An important and innovative achievement is the geographic representation of previously unexplored databases, in which there was no detailed analysis of indicator behavior.
Impact & Results:
An increase in geographic information sources, as well as teaching users of the digital map book to understand and interpret the information in a different way, achieving an advantage in the way in which the analyses are presented, publishing information of great interest to citizens through the platform at a very low cost.

Integrate and centralize geographically the information of indicators, projects, analysis, among others, related to mobility in Bogota in a single platform.

Project/Initiative Name: Remote Presentation System via Facial Recognition (SAREF)
Organisation: Court of Justice of the Federal District and Territories (TJDFT)
Description:
The structuring pillars of SAREF sought alignment with: (a) ease of access and non-dependence on private components; (b) encrypted (secure) data transfer between client and server and other security issues, such as session tokens and random data; (c) observance of the precepts of the General Law of Data Protection; d) simple and easy-to-use language; e) compliance with interoperability requirements defined by the CNJ, such as agnostic microservices orchestration; f) use of software and libraries with no licensing costs; and g) communication with an execution system standardized by the CNJ.

Initially implemented in a Court (Open Regime), the solution was made more flexible and expanded to serve the Alternative Measures Court of the Federal District, which deals with open regime sentences with weekend limitations for the convicts, who must remain at their homes. The remote submission captures the location of the convict by means of the cell phone's GPS, which must be enabled for the procedure to be accepted and successful.

With the SAREF operational in two courts in the Federal District, the focus of the work is currently on the constant growth and improvement of the tool, based on its use by the courts, seeking to add more relevant features and make it more flexible for adoption in different realities.
Novelty:
The SAREF was the first tool in Brazil to solve the impediment of prisoner presentations due to the pandemic, without modifying the work flow of the Courts. The remote form of presentation brought convenience, saving time and money to the convicts by avoiding displacement, in some cases over long distances and in public transportation. The system was developed entirely by TJDFT's internal team, which means that no extra costs were incurred by the Court.

Although other solutions have subsequently emerged to address the same problem, SAREF is the most complete system developed in this context. Besides the mobile module, it has a Court administrator module, which allows a complete management, with reports, registration of new convicts and of presentation periods, validation of the presentations (consulting the recognized photo, GPS map with the convict's location). All integrated to the National Execution System of the CNJ.
Impact & Results:
SAREF began operating in June 2021 for open regime and in February 2022 for alternative measures. We have the following consolidated data so far: more than 15,000 registered convicts; more than 59,000 homologated submissions; about 40,000 carried out by cell phone, which shows the wide adoption of the remote module. Each record described is persisted for queries and audits, and a consolidated statistical panel was developed from the Kibana tool.
A future perspective is the implementation of the subpoena warrant, allowing communication with the convict from the system.

Description:
The Perspectivas - Innovative Program for Retirement Preparation - is intended to help public servants who are about to retire, and who have lived their entire lives at the disposal of their work, to make this transition with more clarity with a view to an active retirement and a longer life.

The Program innovates in its methodology, as it is humanized, centered on the employee and based on self-knowledge, aiming to rescue the identity beyond the professional aspect. Scientific tools for human development are used, in addition to the most renowned coaching and mentoring techniques. 

The program aims to increase the level of maturity of the staff member in order to have an active and fulfilling retirement, according to the WHO (World Health Organization) guidelines on active aging.

The degree of Maturity for Retirement of the employees participating in the Program was measured before and after their participation by means of the "Maturity for Retirement" tool, having broadened the perspectives of the pre-retired professional, restoring their identity as a human being and seeking a safe, clear and planned transition to the next level of their lives. The employees learned, mainly, that they are still useful and able to impact other lives with their work in retirement, whether paid or unpaid. The employees were instructed to assign a score for each item using a scale of 0 to 10, where 0 - does not represent them and 10 - fully represents them.
Novelty: The Perspectives was created in line with the UN's guidelines for active aging. It is different from other programs, both offered by private companies and created by the public institutions themselves, because it is humanized, centered on the human being and is based on self-knowledge, aiming to rescue the identity of the public servant, providing inputs for him to seek an answer to the question "What will I do when I retire? This generates clarity and reduces the fears and the sense of uselessness that are so present in pre-retired public service employees. The expected result is an active retirement, in which the servant will work in an activity that fulfills him or her.
Impact & Results:
The Program was measured with the tool "Retirement Maturity" with a scale from 0 to 10 (being 0 - does not represent me and 10 - totally represents me) and presented the following main results, considering the class average:
- Security about retiring: from 6 to 8.86
- Recognition as an individual outside the workplace: from 7.14 to 9.43
- Knowledge of options for retirement: from 7.43 to 9.14 
- Having a career plan for post retirement: from 4.57 to 8
- Having strategies for the post retirement period: from 5.57 to 7.71

This way, the program's effectiveness regarding the proposed objectives is evident.
For the future, we aim to take the methodology beyond borders and help more and more employees to prepare for this new phase of life in an active and fulfilled way.

Project/Initiative Name: Alice - Bid, Contract and Notice Analyser
Description:
Alice. This is a Continuous Audit program that uses artificial intelligence to fight corruption, with very expressive results. It automatically collects public procurement data on a daily basis, assesses risks, and issues alerts about possible fraud and non-conformities.

Considering the relevance, the values and the risks involved, the Office of the Comptroller General created the Bidding, Contracts and Tender Analysis (Alice) in order to add value to public management with a preventive and timely action regarding public procurement. The system automatically collects daily information about the processes underway in the Federal Government's main public procurement platforms, assesses a set of risks, and issues alerts to direct the attention of the auditors and managers involved to situations that are outside the benchmark.

The tool searches for data from the main national public procurement systems, Comprasnet - the Federal Government's official system, also used by some states and municipalities, and Licitações-e, a Banco do Brasil system, also used by other state-owned companies and states and municipalities.

The crucial point for the tool's success was the integration of its results into the ordinary work process of the House, with strong top management sponsorship. The tool is now evolving to add more functionalities that use artificial intelligence techniques and to add new databases.
Impact & Results:
In 2021, 139,566 procurement processes were evaluated. Of these, 35,461 were processes related to the activities of the CGU (Federal Executive Branch, with the exception of units audited by other sectorial bodies of the internal control system). Of this total, 4,528 alerts were issued by the tool. Due to CGU's operational capacity, 646 alerts were analyzed.

With this type of action, from 2018 to 2022, R$ 9.7 billion (Approximately 1.9 billion dollars) in suspended bids with evidence of error or fraud were recorded. Of this amount, CGU recorded R$1.3 billion (approximately $250 million) in financial benefits from its action, indicating that the related purchases were either canceled by managers for being improper or the reference value estimates used in the purchases were lowered, generating savings to public coffers.

Project/Initiative Name: 2030 Agenda at the PGE (Attorney General's Office of the State of Bahia)
Organisation: Procuradoria Geral do Estado da Bahia
Description:
The Project "Agenda 2030 in the PGE" seeks to collaborate with the intelligent management of information within the State Attorney General of Bahia (PGE/BA) and consequently in the implementation of the agenda at the regional level, through monitoring and dissemination of the manifestations of the Legal Consulting that have adherence to the sustainable development goals (SDGs) of Agenda 2030. This monitoring occurs through the analysis and indexing of the manifestations of the PGE's Legal Consulting, performed in the electronic administrative process environment. 

In addition, public observatories were created to provide data and diagnostics on the situation of municipalities and/or the state, related to the achievement of SDG targets and good practices implemented, as well as the state mechanisms to disseminate and reward good practices that contribute to the achievement of SDG targets by public and private managers were not established" 

The monitoring of the manifestations of PGE/BA regarding the SDGs helps both in the follow-up of the actions of the State of Bahia that seek to meet the goals established for each of the SDGs, and in the governance of PGE/BA itself, since the results contribute to the direction of the activities of the Legal Consultancy.

Furthermore, the aforementioned project resonates both with PGE/BA's mission (to practice public law, acting actively in the construction and implementation of state policies with a view to promoting justice and social harmony) and its vision (to be a reference of excellence in the search for innovative and efficient legal solutions), since it will contribute to the achievement of the goals associated with the SDGs of Agenda 2030, and consequently will also have repercussions on the implementation of state public policies and the promotion of social peace.
Novelty: The "Project Agenda 2030 at the PGE" is innovative because it seeks to contribute to improving the governance of the PGE/BA in interlocution with other state agencies, as well as to increase the transparency of state actions, allowing greater interaction with the public. The project creates an intelligent and innovative system of data governance that can be replicated in other organs of the Public Attorney's Office, generating a large network of information usable by different state agencies.
Impact & Results: The first practical effect was the publication of ordinance 109/2022, which institutionalizes the 2030 Agenda at PGE/BA. This ordinance creates a multi-sectorial Committee with the competency to coordinate actions with the purpose of implementing the SDGs at PGE. Other state agencies were sensitized by sending reports linking the activities of PGE/BA to the SDGs. PGE/BA's employees (about 1000) are stimulated through the dissemination of informative material on social networks and institutional e-mail.

Description:
The Moroccan interoperability platform reinforces the modernization of the Moroccan public administration by setting up a secure technical base for data exchange between administrations. The platform aims, therefore, the interconnection of all the information systems of Moroccan institutions in order to simplify the administrative procedures of citizens / companies.

The establishment of a platform for data exchange between administrations is a basic foundation for the interconnection of information systems of different administrations and public institutions for the benefit of simplifying the administrative procedures of citizens and businesses.

Thus, this solution will come to answer two major brakes of the fluidity of public services:
1.	The minimization of supporting documents to produce a citizen/company service;
2.	The digitization of data exchanges between administrations in a standard, secure and efficient way (no intermediary intervention, from the service requester, between administrations).
Novelty:
The innovative character of the proposed solution is present by the fact that the platform is based on an API Management solution interconnecting the Moroccan administrations without any storage of business data at the platform level to respect the principle of confidentiality and security of the exchanged data.

The API is a tool for operational efficiency and is used as a tool for modernizing information systems to meet a technical need to optimize the architecture. It is an approach that allows to be more agile, to improve the efficiency of internal processes and to reduce development costs.

Also, the micro-service architecture, which is becoming more and more widespread, makes massive use of APIs to ensure communications between the user interface and the micro-services.
Impact & Results:
The platform has reduced the processing time of citizen/business requests and reduced their interactions with related administrations.
Also, it has allowed more than 30 administrations and public institutions to interconnect and exchange data on citizens to verify their eligibility for social programs or to make the data declared by citizens (in the framework of administrative procedures) more reliable, thus digitizing inter-administration exchanges.

Project/Initiative Name: REUSE Communities | Multimaterial Circularity in the Greater Porto Region
Organisation: LIPOR - Intermunicipal Waste Management Service of Greater Porto
Description:
LIPOR has created the present strategy to promote the reuse of used (non-food) products, reducing waste and benefiting the local Community. Within this scope, it has been promoting the creation of Reuse Centres based on the synergic work between local partners. In parallel, it promotes education, awareness and empowerment of the community and the collection and monitoring of data.

LIPOR's Integrated Municipal Waste Management System has created a strategy to promote multi-material circularity at local level, aiming at the reuse of end-of-life products and sustainable consumption. It aims to boost operations dedicated to reuse in a broad sense - creative recovery, repair, among others - maximizing the extension of the useful life of products and avoiding their disposal as waste. 

This Strategy for the promotion of the circularity of end-of-life products in the local community opens the opportunity to create Reuse Centres and other collaborative models (e.g. exchange & share) that are disseminated in other European countries, but do not exist in LIPOR's area of influence, representing radical innovation and breaking away from traditional paradigms related to the use of used products. Furthermore, the ongoing study to identify local entities that operate in the social reuse sector allows for the creation of networks, community and synergic work, thus increasing results.
Novelty: It promotes access to products and goods for people/families in need and contributes to meeting environmental challenges regarding waste management and promotion of the circular economy.
Impact & Results:
From 2020 to 2022 (1st semester) with the implemented actions, 13 tons of waste were diverted from treatment, which is equivalent to 30 tons of CO2eq emissions and 582 euros avoided with the treatment. 36 workshops, 3 trainings, 5 visits to the reuse center and 2 exchange markets were carried out, reaching 750 people. 

Partnerships were created with 14 entities. The impact of the work carried out by LIPOR and partners is reflected in figures in the reduction and reuse area of LIPOR's Waste Observatory. Foram publicadas 47 notícias nos orgãos de comunicação social, correspondendo a um media value de 35.631 €.

Novelty:
ANAC's scope of activity is one of the largest among the Federal Regulatory Agencies. ANAC regulates all aspects of the airline industry, except airspace control (DECEA's competence). Thus, Simple Flights affects practically all aspects of civil aviation.

Simple Flights is the largest program ever launched for Brazilian civil aviation and one of the largest reviews of regulatory practices and public services provided by Regulatory Agencies in Brazil. Another innovation is in the way the program was created. For the first time in the history of Brazilian civil aviation, a program was structured where the starting point was to listen to the problems from the perspective of those regulated by ANAC and the focus was to look at the practical application of regulation in civil aviation.
Impact & Results: The Simple Flight actions resulted in the revocation of 279 outdated norms and in the simplification of the Civil Aviation Supervisory Fee (TFAC) that is charged for services rendered by the Agency. From 342 generating factors, there are now only 25. In addition, the values charged were revised and rationalized, significantly reducing costs for companies and civil aviation workers.

Project/Initiative Name: Reducing the Impact of Covid-19 on Civil Aviation
Description:
The National Civil Aviation Agency (ANAC) adopted in a short period of time almost 100 regulatory measures to reduce the impacts of Covid-19 on civil aviation, obtaining results that impacted the lives of millions of people among air transport passengers, air transport workers. passenger and cargo transport companies, airport operators, such as:

Maintenance of the logistical interconnection of the entire country, ensuring that no Unit of the Federation was left without at least one air connection. The emergency network maintained 1,241 weekly flights even during the most critical period of the pandemic, one of the highest domestic frequencies in the world during the Covid-19 crisis.

Short-term economic-financial rebalancing of the 10 airport concessionaires in the amount of R$2 billion, which is equivalent to approximately 39% of their total revenues in 2019. The approval of the requests for financial rebalancing and the postponement of concession payments avoided the worsening of the crisis caused by the pandemic, preserving tens of thousands of direct and indirect jobs and ensuring high standards of service quality for millions of passengers.

ANAC's regulatory changes regarding the transport of hospital supplies on aircraft resulted in more than a billion items such as respirators, medicines, Covid tests, vaccine production supplies, and vaccination material (such as needles and syringes) arriving throughout Brazil via air transport. Just in the period from 05/03 to 07/19/2020 a single Brazilian airline transported 240 million masks.
Novelty:
ANAC's actions to safeguard civil aviation were unprecedented. Changes in regulations at various levels: operational, training, passenger rights, economic regulation have changed the landscape of civil aviation to avoid the complete interruption of scheduled air transport and without renouncing to the safety and excellence of civil aviation. 

Dozens of new regulations have allowed for novelties in civil aviation such as new possibilities for aero medical transport, training based on technological resources, and meeting specific economic needs of passengers and airlines arising from Covid-19. ANAC's timely actions have brought about a positive change never before seen in civil aviation, making it more agile while maintaining strict safety standards.
Impact & Results:
113 measures, which were discussed with the industry and resulted in the implementation of 95 measures in various areas within ANAC.
- More than 1 billion items and medical supplies and almost 500 million vaccines transported.
- Maintenance of the logistic interconnection of the whole country, guaranteeing that no Federation Unit was left without at least one air connection. The emergency network maintained 1,241 weekly flights.
- Short-term economic-financial rebalancing of the 10 airport concessionaires in the amount of R$ 2 billion reais, equivalent to approximately 39% of their total revenues in 2019.
- Brazilian airports transported, in July 2022, more than 7.6 million passengers in the domestic market. This was the highest value for the month in the last three years. In comparison with the same period last year, the value was 30% higher.

Description:
The city of Florianópolis and TopMed have innovated health care with the creation of Hello Health Floripa. The service fulfills the principle of universality, guaranteeing access to health care for 100% of the population. Using digital channels (telephone or application), it connects any citizen to a health professional, 24 hours a day, without any displacement. The innovation also organizes access to health care, generates savings and balance for public health. The proposal is disruptive in the relationship of citizens with health.

The innovation of Hello Health Floripa is the first pre-clinical care service for users of the Brazilian Unified Health System, developed by TopMed in partnership with the City of Florianópolis, and which has proven to be of immense scope and social relevance, serving as an example, especially in the period of the Covid-19 pandemic, in the biennium 2020/2021, and in the post-pandemic period. 

Remote assistance is divided into two levels: the first contact, called 'level 1', occurs through a smartphone application, tablets, computers, or by calling a toll-free number. The user is assisted by a nursing professional who, with clinical algorithms, i.e., a neural tree of questions and answers about the reported problem, provides the most appropriate referral; the service at 'level 2', the responsibility of a nurse, occurs with the presentation of symptoms and can generate from self-care guidelines, even referral to Health Units. To have access to a health professional, the user waits no longer than 60 seconds. Each service lasts, on average, 10 minutes for triage and another 5 minutes for health information.

Alô Saúde Floripa turned out to be a national reference on how to welcome all its users, without subjecting them to risks and allocating each case to its respective and adequate resource for that moment. The service was responsible for more than 32% of the COVID cases registered in the city of Florianópolis.
Novelty:
The project emerged as a unique and exclusive proposal in public health in Brazil. Offering the users of the Unified Health System (SUS) a digital gateway, i.e., anywhere with access to telephone or internet and that operates 24 hours a day, after the Pandemic may seem obvious, but this project predates COVID-19. It was an innovation tested in practice, in an extreme moment, which proved its effectiveness and resilience.

Furthermore, we believe that the proposal is innovative when it offers conditions to organize the demand for care and, consequently, effectiveness and resoluteness in assistance, guiding the patient to the right place at the right time, without risks due to lack of timely care and without crowding.
Impact & Results: In total there are 596,914 registered users and 354,115 users seen so far, the majority being female - 60.61%, with an NPS (net promoter score) of 93 points, and a resolutivity of 95%. The services are recorded and made available to the Municipality's management by means of dashboards for real-time follow-up and measurement of results.

Description:
Leaders Who Change is a professionalization program for senior public management in Brazil. Inspired by the best international practices and using an innovative methodology of assessment and certification of behavioral competencies, supported by artificial intelligence, the Leaders Program helps the government select the best talents for positions of free employment. A qualified, agile and transparent process of leadership recruitment and selection to transform the future of public service.

Its main objective is to contribute to the improvement of the procedures for choosing professionals who will occupy senior public management positions, through the adoption of prior, clear, and isonomic recruitment and pre-selection processes, providing more transparency in the choice and legitimizing the leadership's performance. 1) Two possibilities are offered - recruitment and pre-selection, 2) Customized selection process: used in complementary membership, 3) Profile Bank: used for standard membership.

The management of the Program employs technologies that are widely validated in the market, such as the Platform that manages the stages of the selection process, the Artificial Intelligence tool, customized to meet the specificities of the Program's selections, and the instrument for the certification of behavioral competencies, developed in a customized way for Enap.

The initiative has proven to be very successful and has contributed to the generation of public value. It is possible to perceive a direct gain for the institutions, which can now count on the right talents for the positions; for the selected leaders, who see their careers valorized, achieving yet another career achievement, and for the teams, which now have a technical, qualified, and legitimately chosen leader.
Novelty:
It makes it possible to carry out selection processes in a personalized way or via a Profile Bank. Adhesion and permanence is voluntary, but it counts on a system of incentives that encourages the adhesion of agencies from the three levels of government, and includes the possibility of full financing of the selection costs, as well as follow-up and development actions for the selected leaders and People Management areas.

It professionalizes and transforms the culture of occupation of positions that can be freely filled, changing the political appointment process for a qualified choice aligned with the competencies and challenges of the Public Administration, without removing the decision-making autonomy of the managers.
Impact & Results:
The Program has already pre-selected more than 240 people, counts on a bank of 4,000 registered resumes, and has already 40 leaders nominated via the selection process. 

The evaluation of people, candidates, and the requesting agencies, measured by surveys applied throughout the selection stages, show a 90% rate of satisfaction with the quality of the process.

The Special Secretary of Treasury and Budget and the Special Secretary of Productivity and Competitiveness (both linked to the Ministry of the Economy), the Ministry of the Environment, IBAMA and ICMBio have already officially joined the Program.

Description:
Challenges is an initiative of GNova Open Innovation - a unit of the Innovation Directorate of the National School of Public Administration (Enap), which offers help to public managers to innovate together with society, taking advantage of collective intelligence to reduce the insecurities of the innovation process and increase the chances of success.

The strategy of inviting society to propose ideas and solutions is not exactly a novelty and, throughout history, has provided great technological leaps. However, competitions and awards are experiencing a renaissance. In the last 10 years, governments around the world have instituted open innovation programs and platforms. The experiences prove that this is an effective way to increase the level of knowledge and engagement on a topic, accelerate change, and create new alternatives to complex problems.

With the documents produced and the resources mobilized, it is time to launch the challenge. We mobilize the innovation ecosystems that we identified in the definition of the target audience and wait for the receipt of proposals from the participants. From there, we select and evaluate the proposals for innovative solutions according to the criteria defined in the calls for proposals, choose the winners, and award the prizes.

In 2022 we launched version 2.0 of the platform, which now has an environment for the public agent to navigate through the toolkit and templates and better understand the steps. In addition, it is possible to publish and launch a competition through the platform, without the direct involvement of Gnova/Enap. Expanding the vision of government as a platform, in which the government agencies themselves can engage in the open innovation agenda that stimulates intersectoral innovation at different levels of government.
Novelty:
For Enap, the launching of the Platform represented an advance in a new frontier in the innovation agenda, which had not yet been explored in the federal government. And this frontier not only brings us closer to citizens, the private sector, and academia, but also promotes the breaking of the informational asymmetry, fosters the exchange of ideas and technology among sectors, and searches for the best solutions to public problems.

After three years of the project we were able to generate impact and place Enap as a promoter of open innovation challenges. Nevertheless, in the current phase of the platform, the aim is to gain scale in the platform itself, building more standardized journeys and providing content that allows autonomy to those who wish to hold their competitions autonomously or with minimal Enap participation.
Impact & Results:
Since its launch, 55 challenges have been launched, with more than 2,000 participants from all over the country, who sent more than 750 proposals, of which more than 50 were awarded. Around 3 million reais in prizes were mobilized. 

The platform recognizes the best proposals for solutions to a problem; builds technological capabilities; incorporates solutions; and promotes new businesses.
