But the main impact generated by the platform was the search for a change in the environment and culture of innovation and public procurement in Brazil.

Organisation: Barueri Center for Innovation and Technology
Description:
Disseminate courses/training and training for employees and the general population, in addition to broadcasting live material inherent to technology, entrepreneurship and innovation. 
 
+9 programs and podcasts available in + 06 platforms and application in google play. 
 Making content so widely available in conjunction with the inclusion of a new digital format to broaden access to Innovation initiatives in the city.

On July 02, 2020 the TV and podcast Innovation Barueri was launched by with the presence of the current Minister of MCTI, Paulo Guedes, and the illustrious astronaut and Minister Marcos Pontes of the Ministry of Science, Technology and Innovations, in its first program had the reach of more than 17 thousand people. Since then, it has been playing its role with the goal of disseminating courses/training and training for employees and the general population, in addition to transmitting live material inherent to technology, entrepreneurship and innovation, we broadcast the program "Innovation Barueri" live on youtube: https://youtu.be/EMHQiPu2wdM.
Novelty: The availability of content in such a broad way together with the inclusion of a new digital format to expand access to Innovation initiatives in the city. Among them we can mention: programs in Podcasts present in more than 6 different platforms, being them: Deezer, Spotify, Apple podcasts, Breaker, Google Podcasts, Pocket Casts, RadioPublic and Anchor. We can also mention the Tv Inovação Barueri application that facilitates the access to the programs and works like a TV set, because when you open the application the program that is live at that moment is exhibited, without the need to send or share links.

Project/Initiative Name: School-Effect Indicator - AN ANALYSIS OF SCHOOLS, USING AI
Organisation: Audit Court of the State of São Paulo (TCESP)
Description:
TCESP has built a new analysis methodology, the "School Effect Indicator" (IEE), which evaluates school effectiveness based on the analysis of factors related both to school and extra-school factors, such as the socioeconomic status of students. The work done was inspired by an article published in 2007 called School Effect Indicator: a methodology for the identification of school successes based on data from the Prova Brasil, authored by the researchers Amaury Patrick Gremaud, Fabiana de Felicio and Roberta Loboda Biondi.

The hypothesis of the methodology predicts that schools located in neighborhoods/districts whose population has higher purchasing power achieve a higher average result for their students on the national exam called Sistema de Avaliação da Educação Básica (SAEB), formerly Prova Brasil. Meanwhile, schools whose students belong to the poorer socioeconomic strata of the population tend to present lower results in the final score of the exam. 

The project uses Artificial Intelligence to calculate the expected score of the students in each school and compares it with the observed score on the SAEB exam. The difference between the observed and expected value becomes the school's added value, that is, how much it has added to the proficiency of its students. Using the average score of the schools and the socioeconomic data, we are able to estimate the portion of the score that is due to socioeconomic factors and, consequently, the residual value added by schools to their students' performance (IEE - School Effect Indicator).

The IEE evaluation methodology allows the auditor to select the schools that stood out the most (highest IEE) in order to identify the good practices that possibly influenced the performance above expectations and thus, such actions can be disseminated to other schools. On the other hand, the schools with the worst EEI scores are also selected for evaluation and possible identification of the factors that determined a lower than expected performance. As the suggestions are included in the audit report, the public manager is aware of the findings and can work to suppress the factors that caused the low scores and disseminate the good practices recognized by the TCESP evaluation.
Novelty:
The methodology of the School Effect Indicator is innovative because:
1) It brings to light the analysis, by external control, of public policies in the area of education, so that it is inclusive, of quality and equitable. 
2) It allows TCESP to achieve a greater impact on public management, reinforcing the value of public auditing by expanding the forecast of recommendations based on the analysis of strategic public policies in education, promoting good governance through the dissemination of knowledge and good practices;
3) Using Artificial Intelligence techniques allows TCESP to synthesize a significant mass of data and find solutions to critical problems, such as school performance evaluation. The partnership with the University of São Paulo, through its Institute of Mathematical and Computer Sciences, provides a new perspective for improving the methodology and dissemination of the IEE, for managers from all over the country and for the academic world.
Impact & Results:
The state education system in the state of São Paulo has 5,351 teaching units, of which 5,044 are regular schools, 38 Indian education units, 4 quilombola schools, 16 in rural settlements, 153 prison classes, and 96 centers for socio-educational assistance. This universe corresponds to a total of 3,466,547 students attended in 2021, according to data from the Secretariat of Education.

The School-Effect Indicator (IEE) was used for the first time in 2021 by TCESP as a benchmark for the evaluation of public policies in the area of education. The IEE was able to help the TCESP audit to select schools considered more or less effective by the proposed methodology, identifying educational policies with greater evidence of impact on school performance. These audit findings were compiled in a report that came to the attention of state public managers in the form of 13 recommendations that can contribute to better school performance of students.

Description:
The fully digitized service throughout the municipal administration, including internal document management system, digital signature, and taxpayer service. 
The technological infrastructure that we have, allowed the implementation of an IT park, formed by servers and software, which allows the accommodation of these scanned images. The project presented a value proposition that, besides low cost, also impacted the environment in an assertive way.

Smart and Sustainable City - one of the first sectors to be worked on, in Barueri, was Health: where medical records started to be shared among the health units of the network. The process of document collection and organization of this operation began in November 2020, and the digitization of documents started on January 4, 2021, focusing on the inclusion of sustainable ways to save inputs, public spending and a new mentality for the preservation of the environment. 

Today, the service is fully digitalized throughout the municipal administration, including an internal document management system, digital signature, taxpayer service, and interconnected services in the areas of Health, Education, Security, and procurement. To make these changes effective, the civil service workforce is being prepared through meetings, lectures, and workshops. 
 
The technological infrastructure we have allowed the implementation of a computer park, composed of servers and software, which allows the hosting of these digitalized images. Barueri has a cloud structure to store and facilitate access to this digital collection.
Novelty:
The City of Barueri in partnership with CIT - Center for Innovation and Technology, on January 21, 2020, implemented the CIT Portal in order to promote greater transparency in public services by publishing the catalog of more than 200 smart city projects in Barueri, serving more than 20 departments, keeps citizens informed of all systems, APPS, infrastructure, events and awards of the city, through the CIT Portal available to all. 

A "Hotpage" is available, a page designed to highlight the main information about Barueri's progress in becoming a smart city, along with all the news published in national and international media about the city's technology projects. 

There is a Free Agents space in Barueri for the server and team to develop pilot projects in a proper environment, the Inovação Barueri, the possibility to learn, design and produce several types of objects, has 3D printers, plotter, etc.
Impact & Results:
'-Live Cameras: 53 monitoring cameras spread around the city that can be accessed by citizens through a website to observe the city in real time. 
-Monitoring Center: 455 cameras in operation, resulting in agility in the monitoring of occurrences, a reduction in thefts and robberies, an increase in the population's sense of security (a 12% decrease in crime), the expansion of the monitoring performance area and a decrease in the response time to occurrences. 
-Social Internet: Free wi-fi internet in squares and in housing complex buildings - The aim is to install it in 2,700 points in the city.

Description:
The new regulatory architecture overcomes flaws observed from the diagnosis of the concessions program, with the objective of improving service provision to users. The RCR intends, in an innovative way, to promote the migration from contractual regulation to a predominantly normative regulation model, based on the precepts of responsive regulation. The proposal contemplates 5 normative acts: a) CRR 1: general guidelines; b) CRR 2: goods, works and services; c) CRR 3: economic and financial management; d) CRR 4: inspection and penalties; e) CRR 5: contract termination and controversy resolution. 

The main objectives of the RCR are the simplification of the regulatory process, the increase in competition among concessionaires for the best service through the discrimination obtained by the classification of the concessionaires, the decrease in regulatory and transaction costs in future updates, the generation of an incentive environment and the reduction of barriers to entry of new businesses and technologies.

The classification of the concessionaires is among its main innovations. The classification is based on the main aspects of the provision of quality service to users, covering the quality of maintenance of road infrastructure (sidewalk, signs, bridges, viaducts and drainage), the execution of works (annual and accumulated) and the operation of services (medical assistance, mechanical assistance and customer service on the consumidor.gov.br platform). 

The RCR also introduces other innovations in service regulatory aspects, by instituting, for example, the system of toll collection in free flow, the integration between management systems of the concession, among other disciplines. 

From an institutional point of view, the RCR represents the reconstruction of the architecture of the regulatory environment, currently characterized by several dysfunctionalities that imply high management costs of the concessions program by the Agency.
Novelty: The new regulatory framework absorbs and improves the innovations tried out in the most recent contracts, such as: free flow fare collection, with sophisticated interoperability between traffic sensing equipment and means of payment; application of fare discounts for frequent users; escrow account mechanism, with a reserve of resources to guarantee future payments and project sustainability; accredited inspection of projects and independent verifiers to support inspection; among others.
Impact & Results: In substitution of the analytical cost appraisal model, RCR 2 instituted a table with pre-established rates to remunerate the indirect costs of the work. This table is already being adopted as a guideline in the appreciation of projects, reducing regulatory cost and analysis time.

Organisation: Public Ministry of the State of Santa Catarina (MPSC)
Description:
The goal is to promote a complete approach of the population, especially the neediest, to the services of the Public Ministry, benefiting all 7 million citizens of Santa Catarina, especially those who need to be attended to by the Public Ministry (as a rule, the needy) and do not have digital inclusion (in order to get the service by digital means) or the means to travel to the headquarters of the MPSC.

The project was conceived from Design Thinking workshops of the Innovation Lab (ImpulsoLAB), when an innovation event was held at the headquarters of the MPSC and it was decided to focus on the problem of expanding access to the citizens of Santa Catarina who do not have digital inclusion in their lives (a reality still very present in Brazil). Problems were cited in the needy areas of the state capital (Florianopolis), as well as the problem of municipalities where the MPSC is not physically present, forcing the citizen to travel to the County's headquarters in order to receive the effective service (removing from the MPSC's action, therefore, that citizen who is more needy - without digital inclusion and with difficulties of displacement, which can reach up to 100km away).

This innovation was inspired by the digitalization of the assistance instituted in the MPSC, which started to provide assistance through messaging applications, videoconferences, etc., which allowed an exponential approximation of the Prosecutors with the citizens, remaining, however, solutions that could approach that citizen who does not have a digital inclusion (a large portion of the Brazilian population still) and who have difficulty to appear in person at the headquarters of the MPSC.
Novelty:
The Brazilian Public Ministry is composed of 30 Units, the Judiciary even more.
Thus, the great innovation is to make an institution that is part of this traditional reality, change its conception to, through technology and partnerships, be present in all municipalities and also in distant places of larger municipalities, thus promoting a democratization of access to those who need it most.
Impact & Results: Results and impacts: Increase in the demand for MPSC services by citizens who had difficulty in accessing them, which is measured through the statistics of services recorded in the Citizen Service Sector - all services are recorded and integrated into the monitoring systems.

Organisation: Regional Council of Engineering, Agronomy and Geosciences (CREA-SP)
Description:
The transformation of CREA-SP is born from the opportunity to bring the professional to the center of our actions and make the Council a reference in quality of services in the public sector and more active in the safety of society. Through building an agile and collaborative culture, strengthening connections with startups, educational institutions, businesses and governments, transparency in management, expansion of digital enforcement and professional training.

Crea-SP has started its transformation process and the proposals for this project were developed with the participation of employees, professionals, and leaders who represent the council. They are supported by the pillars of Modernization, Efficiency, Valuing of Professions and Transparency in Management. The goal is to make Crea-SP a reference in innovation and efficiency in services, offering a complete platform of solutions that serve professionals in Engineering, Agronomy, Geosciences, and Technologists. 

Another goal is to access innovation hubs to connect with startups and cutting-edge technological solutions. Besides capturing external knowledge for the implementation of strategic projects. To update management technologies, replacing them with the most advanced ones used in today's most innovative companies. The project also wants to promote the creation of model companies, with a program that must be focused on the incentive, teaching, and development of Engineering, Agronomy, and Geosciences. 

To implement a new organizational culture based on innovation and collaboration developing the necessary skills to make employees able to work in a new collaborative management approach. Upgrading management technologies, replacing them with the most advanced ones used in today's most innovative companies. Develop an Innovation advisory committee. Implement Crea-Lab, the innovation lab for the execution of digital transformation projects.
Novelty:
With technological advances, society expects to access public services with the same ease, agility and quality as private services. This social pressure, coupled with the reduction in public revenue with the pandemic, imposes on CREA-SP the need to accelerate its digital transformation and, at the same time, requires public managers trained and with appropriate tools to implement innovations to improve operational efficiency.

It seeks to understand the needs of its stakeholders and develops, in an agile and propositional way, solutions and services to meet their expectations, becoming recognized as a reference in its category. The proposal is to develop a service platform that is a reference in the public sector, with the professional as the strategic pillar of management.
Impact & Results: For the first time in the borough's history, the Council brought together the main people impacted by its work routine to discuss ideas and find new solutions to common problems. From this format, which we call open innovation, emerged initiatives that have already been implemented and have proven to be relevant, such as the digital professional card, the Minerva chatbot, the new website and the services portal, in addition to the digitization of 100% of our processes. In parallel to these deliveries, the Crea-SP has advanced towards other important areas, such as professional training, providing an educational environment for professionals to update themselves with hybrid courses in partnership with renowned educational institutions, the Crea-SP Capacita.

Description:
The electronic polls are the new proposal for popular participation in the legislative branch of the House of Representatives. The goal is to allow citizens to have a quick and simple way to participate, while at the same time providing information from society so that parliamentarians can reflect on the proposals in progress and consider the city's concerns when drafting new proposals, when amending proposals in progress, and when voting for the deputies.

The electronic polls were chosen for the short time required for participation and for their high viralization power in social media, helping to broaden citizen participation. To facilitate participation, a journalist prepares an explanatory summary of the proposition, using simple language to allow the citizen to have a better chance of understanding the justification, what the proposition is about, and the impacts expected by the author. For the sake of transparency, access to the original text is also maintained.

Citizens can participate by voting among five options, and can also leave comments indicating what they consider to be positive or negative about the proposition. These comments left by citizens are the richest source of information for the authors and other parliamentarians, and are also the project's greatest innovation. As many comments can be posted by citizens, especially on proposals that are broadcast in the mass media or that go viral in social media, a Machine Learning algorithm of text clustering is applied, aiming to reduce hundreds of comments to a few dozen similar ideas.

This algorithm, currently in operation, uses techniques from Natural Language Processing (NLP) and was developed, and continues to be improved, through a partnership with the University of São Paulo. The polls present themselves as a way to attract citizens to learn about the legislative process and draw their attention to the discussions taking place in parliament.

It is a quick and simple way to participate, allowing educational actions to be applied, contributing to a better understanding of democracy by citizens. This is especially important in a country like Brazil, with more than 200 million inhabitants that generate, on average, 30,000 comments per quarter, a number that tends to increase with the gradual popularization of the solution.
Novelty: This project presents two innovations with the goal of encouraging and expanding citizen participation: 1) the use of polls as a form of participation facilitates viralization on social networks by requiring little time to participate, besides facilitating its use on mobile devices; 2) the use of Machine Learning algorithms to group the hundreds or even thousands of comments into a few similar ideas, allowing parliamentarians to get to know society's desires by reading only the representative ideas of the groups of citizens' contributions.
Impact & Results: Collecting the number results between 2019 and 2022 (through July, Q2), we have a quarterly average of 30,000 comments and 288,000 votes submitted by Brazilian citizens. Participation preference for mobile devices (66%). Comparing the first solution, we had 163,387 comments in 2020 in Enquetes versus 633 comments in eDemocracia, and 1,579,653 votes in Enquetes in 2020 versus 5,579 in eDemocracia.

Organisation: ÍRIS - Innovation and Data Laboratory of the Ceará State Government
Description:
The objective of the project is to improve the agency's levels of engagement in order to achieve better organizational results. Among the beneficiaries are: leaders of the organization, their teams, and society as a whole, by receiving better public services. 
The model used for application provides management information for public managers about the engagement of their teams at work, considering that there are 10 factors that are essential for team engagement in the public sector, which are: strategic clarity, honest mistakes or mistakes as learning, autonomy, equity, execution capacity, trust and transparency, clarity of expectations, a welcoming environment, psychological security, and appreciation.

The step-by-step of the project started with the engagement diagnosis, the first stage of the pilot implementation - as indicated by the methodology of the Practical Guide for Team Engagement. With the adhesion of approximately 83% of the organization's leaders, it was possible to draw up the Sefaz engagement maturity guide. The Engagement Map of Sefaz-CE showed that the factors with the highest scores were "Strategic Clarity" and "Fairness", and with the lowest score "Execution Capacity". In one of the training sessions with SEFAZ managers, the ELO factor was identified - considered a factor that generates connection among the other factors - being "Honest Mistakes" or "Mistakes as Learning".

The construction of the intervention started from the following hypothesis: the example can motivate others and the opening for an open environment for feedback, mistakes, and tests as learning. To strengthen and open an environment conducive to feedback, it is important to make the team comfortable to talk openly about their achievements, points to develop, learnings, and apply a selffeedback on what they can and expect to improve. 

The initiative is something new for the institution, and is also an innovative practice for the public sector in Brazil. At the end of the first cycle of the project, a qualitative and quantitative research was carried out to measure the initiative's impacts. The project is a novelty, which was implemented and had its impacts measured
Novelty:
The project is an innovation because:
- Engagement is a key factor for continuous improvement of both the way professionals work and public services, applied for the first time in Ceará.
- The factors related to engagement are manageable, and the organization can act for its evolution and improvement through the use of data. 
- The interventions are easy to implement and low complexity, with real positive impact results. 
- It is based on a robust methodology, which is anchored in the concepts and methods of behavioral economics.
Impact & Results:
The results obtained, so far, through quantitative research are divided into two perspectives: that of the managers who applied the intervention and that of the participants involved in the application of the intervention.

From the managers' point of view, the data collected showed that: 69% of managers understood that sharing highlights and learnings has an impact on group engagement; 58% understand that the more the group tries new things, the greater the chance of significant learnings appearing.

From the employees' point of view: 58% understood that sharing highlights and learnings has an impact on group engagement; 60% understood that the more the group tries new things, the greater the chance of significant learnings appearing.

Description:
BoraLab is the Municipal Government of Maceió's Open Innovation Lab focused on developing innovative projects and spreading innovative culture using agile and itinerant methodologies that allow interaction and co-creation, involving several sectors interested in the public problem. It is replicable and constantly improved . BoraLab is focused on the problems presented both by public management and by the population, raised through data, research, and citizen experience.

The collective sharing of ideas is essential to obtain more effective results in the approach and solution of public problems. Innovation management requires the opening of multi-sector dialogue spaces and the interaction, co-creation, and exchange of knowledge among representatives of the public and private sectors, civil society, and academia. According to this concept, it was identified the need to create an innovation lab in Maceió, to leverage the culture of innovation in the public sector with the creation of a space in which to stimulate creativity, reduction of bureaucracy and better experiences. BoraLab - Maceió's Municipal Open Innovation Lab - was then created.

BoraLab aims to develop innovative projects and spread the innovative culture in the Maceio Municipality, through the use of agile methodologies that allow the interaction, co-creation and exchange of knowledge, involving the actors of the various sectors interested in the public problem explored.

For BoraLab, a mobile laboratory format was programmed, in which the team travels to the location of the key problem to apply the methodology previously explored by the members. It is aimed at departments in different sectors of the City Hall, as well as issuing challenges to universities and companies.
Novelty: Our main value as a laboratory is in the ability to identify and connect the actors that are in the day-to-day of the problem with the main actors that have the technical and practical capacity to solve the problem according to the reality of the scenario. Through a fluid framework developed by the lab team, the itinerant workshops become adaptable to the determined flow and to the final and real application capabilities of the prototype, that is, the workshops are designed so that the prototype can be developed based on the funding capacity of grants, challenge awards, and previously agreed practical programs that we provide as a public entity.
Impact & Results:
We are an mobile laboratory and we go where the problem is. We look for solutions to internal and external problems, from short to long term. We were able to observe that the solutions developed during our BoraSprint sessions solved open issues and taught a new solution method for the future. The challenges thrown to society were able to group the best solutions according to what the population expects for an even better future.
We used dashboards created from a database where we stored our results. We collected feedbacks and analyzed the post-workshop progress in order to understand how the lab was or was not successful in solving the key problem of the objective.

Description:
In July 2022, with the hiring of Colab (collaborative public management tool), the Secretariat of Environment and Sustainable Development suggested the project to the company to identify the feasibility of development. With the understanding of the need, Colab made a first navigable sketch of the service and thus began a pilot project, which initially was launched in only one neighborhood, Valparaiso, during the Tree Week. The expectation is that at least 100 trees will be mapped in this first phase and that every month new neighborhoods will be added to the platform. 

The work process also included prototyping the service so that moderate and non-moderate usability tests could be applied to refine the user experience. It is the first time that Serra promotes a collaborative action with this scope in relation to the environment, in which the citizen helps in the quantitative survey of trees, their characteristics and conditions and this is made public in a publication feed in the app, so that other people can support, comment and follow the outcome.

The ultimate goal is to consolidate the database and offer an interactive digital map so that the population can have access to information, conditions, and history of all the trees, and be able to continuously contribute with updates. It is hoped that this pilot will identify the main points of improvement necessary for a successful expansion not only to other neighborhoods, but also that with the "packaging" of the solution other cities can join in a faster and simpler way - either through Colab or other platforms.
Novelty: It is the first time that the Municipality of Serra has envisioned a massive call for the population to have, in loco, the collaborative mapping of the city's trees, in a 100% end-to-end digital way. The solution uses geospatial technology to map the trees and their conditions, going through a digital quality review process of these records to ensure an accurate inventory of the trees. As a second phase of the project, an artificial intelligence is expected to be trained to help citizens identify tree species, further qualifying the information record.
Impact & Results:
The pilot project is in the implementation phase, having been made available to users on 09/22. During this period, it was possible to observe a significant increase in the number of new Colab users, verified by means of the platform's management report.

With this, we expect the tree inventory to be done in a shorter time and the management actions to be effective, bringing more quality of life for the citizen and savings for the public management. It is expected a greater involvement of the population in relation to afforestation.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital outcasts: innovation for equal service to citizens.
Organisation: General Ombudsman of the Secretariat for Internal Control of the General Secretariat of the Presidency of the Republic
Description:
The innovation solves the problem of unequal and non-isonomic treatment of handwritten manifestations coming from digitally excluded citizens, since it serves a wide audience, diverse subjects and transversal issues, coming from letters, e-mails and other means. In this context, OUVPR has the challenge of fulfilling the requirement of manually inserting all manifestations received through other channels in the Integrated Ombudsman and Information Access Platform (Fala.BR). It receives, registers, analyzes and distinguishes the different issues addressed in the same letter, identifies the responsible body to direct each typified content, and then offers feedback. This sequence of operational and repetitive tasks used to be done manually. The result was a lengthy process, with expired deadlines and subject to human error. 

The action allowed the treatment of the demands of the "digitally excluded", ensuring compliance with the legislation. The decision also made it possible to launch the content in Fala.BR received in digital media, using a form for prior authorization for the use of personal data, giving legal security to the forwarding of manifestations. Automations - Sustaining the articulated management mechanisms would only be viable with the implementation of automations. Thus, CISET/PR, through the integrated work of the General Coordination of Continuous Auditing and the General Ombudsman Office of the Presidency of the Republic, designed the technological solution. 

The mapping of work patterns was carried out to define tags and classifications that led to the first phase of automation in the process, content screening, and the consequent development of the first automated solution. The ARA robot automatically fills in, registers, and launches the manifestation on the Fala.BR platform, generating the protocol number and the access code that will be made available to the citizen by means of a letter. 

The ARA made possible the accessibility of the "digitally excluded" to the rights provided in Law 13,460/2017. With the success of the experience, a second solution was developed - POLO robot - to handle manifestations coming from other digital means. The solution allows protesters to receive, directly, in their emails, the protocol number and the access code for follow-up.
Novelty: The initiative is innovative from process to result, with stimulus to creativity and decision-making for the construction of an unprecedented technology in the treatment of handwritten communications of citizens. The diagnosis innovated by understanding the reasons for the accumulation of demands and identifying the causes of ineffective treatment of the demands of the "digitally excluded". The approach innovated by applying systemic analysis techniques for understanding multiple events and transformation opportunities; the storyboard of the journeys and empathy maps for awareness and solution vision. The development of the ARA and POLO robots resulted in the innovative technological model, with high impact on procedural speed and delivery of measurable results.
Impact & Results: Timely attendance of 100% of the digitally excluded citizen's plea, previously only 18,6% that proceeded to treatment; synchronism between the receipt of the manifestation, the registration within 48 hours and the treatment of 100% of the demands, previously it was done in more than 20 days; savings of 75% in mailing issuance to the digitally excluded; saving of 293 days of work of a server per year; production of organized data potentially useful for the sectors of control and monitoring of public policies indicating profile and subjects with disaggregated variants; information about "digitally excluded" are statistically measurable; definition of channel with institutions that did not adhere to Fala. BR.

Organisation: FEDERAL JUSTICE OF FIRST DEGREE IN SÃO PAULO
Description:
The Technological Solutions Incubator of iJuspLab is a space for prospecting, prototyping and collaborative development of technological solutions at the Federal Justice of São Paulo, where innovation projects in technology are conducted with institutional support by the business area. Thus, users who do not work in the IT sector can actively contribute, in a safe experimentation environment, to the realization of their ideas, without offering any risk to information security.

iJuspLab - Innovation Lab of the Federal Justice of São Paulo - is the first innovation lab in Brazil's Judiciary Branch, created to disseminate the culture of innovation within the Federal Justice of São Paulo. The lab is part of an innovation framework called iNovaJusp. 

The Incubator is an institutional tool available to users to enable the drive of technological innovations without creating a "Parallel IT". It provides a solution prototyping and MVP development environment defined together with and supervised by the IT area. This secure environment allows the user to actively and safely contribute to the realization of his ideas without posing any risk to information security. 

It also aims to act as a "bridge" between users and IT. By knowing both worlds, it is able to "filter" the users' demands, so that only those that really need attention reach the IT department. It can also help users formulate these demands in a way that is more easily understood by IT technicians. Therefore, the Incubator is not a "competitor" of the IT department, but an important ally of the latter when it comes to managing internal demands for technological innovation and improvements in computerized systems.
Novelty:
The history of the Technological Solutions Incubator of iJuspLab can be understood as a successful case in the movement of reconciliation between innovative initiatives in the development of digital solutions promoted by the business areas and the need to maintain the controls of integrity, security and availability of services by the IT department. 
The Incubator's projects are always considered in a holistic way, taking into account, first, the real needs of all the people involved and their work processes, and only then reflecting on how technology can contribute. In this process of integral analysis, it is sometimes concluded that the solution is not technological.
Impact & Results:
The Incubator was established as an environment to receive innovation demands from users that could not reach the IT sectors. It uses agile development methodologies focused on the user. The intention is to provide the rapid development of software solution proposals, ready or not for implementation, to be taken to the IT area. Sectorial initiatives have been brought to be polished in the laboratory and expanded...(regional inspectors' system). 

Decrease in the development time of new systems, avoiding that by the time of delivery the product is already obsolete; dissemination of a new culture of information technology projects in the institution, agile and focused on the user. The qualitative results have no defined indicators because all the work is based on prototyping, but two practices were classified as best practices for the entire Federal Court (Building Information System and the Incubator itself)

Organisation: Civil House of the Government of Ceará State
Description:
ÍRIS understands that this tool, in the game modality, can help several institutions and their managers to create and develop innovative projects, expanding the culture of innovation in the public sector.
The game was designed to be applied in a physical way as a board game and played by leaders of the public service. The game's materials and manuals will also be made available online, allowing for easy replication in other institutions in the states.
Novelty:
The game instructs managers in a playful and light way to develop their projects, using innovative methodologies that bring a different perspective from the immersive visualization of the problem to the creation of an assertive solution.
This dynamic model helps in the conduction of effective solutions, encouraging cooperation among the teams involved in the project, indirectly generating impacts on management.
Impact & Results:
The impacts and results can be seen at different levels: 
At first level, one can perceive the elaboration of more detailed and assertive projects from an already known problem. We also foresee a greater engagement of leaders, considering that to start the game it is only necessary to know the problem, and at the end, obtain a structured project.

Organisation: ÍRIS - Innovation and Data Laboratory of the Government of Ceará
Description:
ÍRIS created a methodology for the (re)design of public legal and administrative documents, applying the techniques of Plain Language, Visual Law, and Editorial Design. This methodology innovates by bringing the citizen to the center of the communication process; proposing a public communication under the logic of use, rather than processes; and promoting empathy.

The main objective of this methodology is to guarantee citizens' rights through the delivery of public administrative and legal documents that are accessible and enable them to exercise their autonomy and their rights of understanding. There are also specific objectives, which are:
1) Increase the awareness of servers about the need to simplify public documents,
2) To promote empathetic listening to the citizen-user during the construction process of simplified governmental technical and legal documents;
3) Improve citizen-centric legal and administrative document templates and speed up citizens' understanding of legal and administrative public orders and communications.

The methodology has the application of three combined techniques: 
- Plain Language: to make public information more accessible, inclusive and understandable to the population;
- Visual Law: graphic elements applied to increase the understanding and didactics of legal documents. 

The process follows the following steps, subsequent and in a collaborative manner with the partner: understanding of the problem and identification of opportunities; planning (diagnosis of the document to be simplified and identification of the target audience); application of Plain Language; revision by the Legal Innovation team; application of Visual Law and Design; round of tests with citizen-users and validations; final revision, delivery, and publication.
Novelty:
Adopting a culture of simplified language in the public sector, expanding the use of communication and design techniques, breaks with the complex logic of document production in the administration and moves to the logic of the citizen. 
With this rupture, a document with words that are difficult for the population to understand becomes an instrument for the democratization of information, much more human, accessible, and inclusive. This guarantees rights and the exercise of citizenship.
Impact & Results:
Positive impacts were identified as follows:
1) For management: adoption of a more empathetic mentality on the part of servers after training cycles; increased awareness of servers about the need to simplify public documents; unburdening of teams to ask questions and clarify documents; greater approximation between the government and the citizen-user of public services.
2) For the citizen-user: greater understanding and clarity about the steps of the process to access services and public policies; increased access of the population to public policies; increased satisfaction of the population with government public services.

Project/Initiative Name: Data Lake for Decision Support in Healthcare Management
Description:
The Data Lake centralizes data in a single format and automatically updates as new data deliveries are released. It also feeds dashboards, systems, and analyses. The beneficiaries are researchers and public managers who work on the issues of education and health work management. It is an innovation, as it adds quality and efficiency to the data consumption and decision making process.

The innovation is a single data repository of approximately 260 GB of data. The data lake unifies the source of access, which contributes to quality and consistency of results. In addition, the data lake increases the efficiency of analyses, as researchers and managers do not have to resort to multiple data files to conduct their analyses. 

Health policy-making must be supported by data. However, it is observed that there is a wide diversity of data, dispersed in multiple sources, with different formats (.csv, .xlsx, .dbf, .dbc). Facing this problem, a data lake was developed that gathers public data from different sources in a single repository, under a single format. The data lake is also constantly updated as new data from the official databases becomes available

The innovation serves three projects. The projects develop artifacts that make use of data, such as dashboards, information systems, and reports. These artifacts are used by public health managers for decision making. It is hoped that the innovation can serve a larger number of projects and researchers. The innovation was developed by researchers at the Federal University of Goiás, which owns the technology. The tool may expand its scale of use as tools are created to improve the experience of the common user. Currently, access is via SQL language. The creation of a query interface could expand its use to users who do not have an information technology background.
Novelty: The data lake standardizes the source of access, which contributes to the quality and consistency of results. In other words, the innovation is unique and different because it has routines for extracting, processing and storing public health data, and delivers a centralized and integrated platform with data organized by topics of interest (demographics, epidemiology, health workforce, etc.).
Impact & Results: So far, the data lake has supplied four different projects. More than 20 researchers consume their data daily for building dashboards, system and analysis. More than 15 dashboards have been created from their data. A health workforce planning information system has been feeding off the tool. In addition, a team of Artificial Intelligence researchers have been using the data to build predictive models.

Project/Initiative Name: Interactive map: largest construction sites stopped by the Justice system
Description:
The data visualization shows the real role of the judiciary in Brazil's infrastructure. Years of stagnation have fed the thesis that courts would be a bottleneck to the sector. Judges, however, have only stopped 1.2% of the largest public works in the country, revealed a study by the National Council of Justice (CNJ). The map shows what they are and where they are. Thus, every citizen has access to data such as the number of the lawsuit and the value of the work. The innovative and intuitive infographic is the only one of its kind in Brazilian Justice.

The map brings a visual solution to the Diagnosis on Paralyzed Construction Works, carried out by the National Council of Justice (CNJ). Released on November 19th, the study revealed that only 1.2% of the largest public works stopped in the country is due to a court order. The infographic, an unprecedented data visualization in the Judiciary, shows how they are distributed.
Two circles compare the total number of halted construction projects with how many of them are due to the Judiciary in each unit of the Federation: the smaller the difference, the greater the role of judicial decisions. For this purpose, the data had to be cast on two layers of the map, overlaid in the final version.

The project sought to illustrate, in an innovative way, the real role of the Judiciary in stalled works and to qualify the public debate. Translating the results into a visual medium communicates the major findings of the study in an intuitive and interactive way. It makes clear, for example, that judicial measures are a negligible cause of project delays and provides local scenarios. Subsidiary graphs have broadened the analysis, focusing on the public interest. Another goal was to integrate data dissemination between sectors of the agency (SCS and DPJ).
Novelty:
The map brings a visual solution to the Diagnosis on Paralyzed Construction Works, conducted by the National Council of Justice (CNJ). Released on November 19, the study revealed that only 1.2% of the largest public works stopped in the country are due to a court order. The infographic, an unprecedented data visualization in the Judiciary, shows where they are.

Two circles compare the total number of halted construction projects with how many of them are due to the Judiciary in each unit of the Federation: the smaller the difference, the greater the role of judicial decisions. For this purpose, the data had to be cast on two layers of the map, juxtaposed in the final version.
Impact & Results: The project illustrates, in an innovative way, the real role of the Judiciary in stalled construction projects. It thus qualifies the civic debate. The material also helped broaden the media reach of the study. It also translated into visual media the major findings in an intuitive and interactive way. It makes it clear, for example, that judicial measures are an insignificant cause for project delays, and brings up local scenarios. Subsidiary graphs have broadened the analysis, focusing on the public interest. Another gain was to integrate the partnership between sectors of the agency, such as communication and statistics.

Description:
The InfoGOV is the first Data Science website of the Brazilian Federal Government. The platform goes a step further concerning government transparency and openness. With a friendly design, the InfoGOV includes easy access to information about government personnel, procurements, and voluntary transfers. Inside the InfoGOV, the citizens can access the RegBR, a framework that improves legislative transparency, allowing citizens to monitor the Brazilian regulatory flow and its characteristics.

Each of these systems is presented in the section “Surf Data”, and they are question-driven. Also related to those systems, InfoGOV has the section “DataStories” that brings interactive data analyses made with the InfoGOV database.

Concerned about legislative data, InfoGOV hosts the RegBR. The RegBR was conceived as a framework to improve legislative transparency, allowing citizens to observe and monitor the Brazilian regulatory process and its characteristics. In this context, RegBR uses structured information to both create a historical regulatory flow and several regulatory metrics, which present how relevant are different sectors of the economy, how restricted are the normative acts, how linguistically complex are the regulations, and, finally, how popular are these normative acts through the eyes of the citizen and for the government.
Novelty:
The website https://dados.gov.br, “Brazilian Open Data Portal”, allows citizens to access a lot of data from the federal government, but most citizens have no skills in data analytics. 
On the other hand, as can be seen at the painelpep.qvw (planejamento.gov.br), “Personnel Statistic Panel”, the B.I panel aims at decision makers as their users, requiring expertise to understand the information.
Inside the InfoGOV there is the RegBR initiative. The ReBr was inspired by the RegData initiative, from https://www.quantgov.org. RegData has metrics about restrictiveness, but RegBR goes further bringing metrics related to sectors of the economy relevancy, linguistical complexity, and popularity (in terms of google research and appearance on Official Gazette).
Impact & Results: We have had in InfoGOV in the last month approximately a thousand users interacting with the platform. Also, there is a great demand for the use of a product that is derived from InfoGOV: the search environment. The research environment allows researchers who have experience with data to access the infoGOV data and do their own analysis.

Project/Initiative Name: AGERSA - the Use of Drones in Sanitation Inspection
Organisation: AGERSA - Sanitation Regulatory Agency of the State of Bahia
Description:
AGERSA - Basic Sanitation Regulatory Agency of the State of Bahia, keeps within its attributions the realization of on-site inspections of the sanitation structures and all of its components. 

The innovation is the use of Drones (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle - UAV), a remotely controlled aircraft, in surveillance actions.

The objective of this innovation is to bring security to the inspections; Production of reports with complete diagnoses and; Optimization of time, showing gains in terms of quality, efficiency, safety, and cost reduction.
Novelty: By implementing the use of Drones (Unmanned Aerial Vehicle - UAV), in inspections it became possible to obtain images that before the use of this technology, would be impossible to acquire in the inspection act, providing more detailed and assertive knowledge of all structures of the water supply system and the sanitary sewage system. Without bringing risks to the technical team and making the inspection activity more efficient.
Impact & Results: As the objective of the innovation is to use the DRONE, the first goal was to qualify the technical team, and through the course offered by GRAER, the goal was successfully completed, obtaining 100% of the qualified team. The use of this technology brought another issue, the systematic use of the DRONE, being necessary the Confection of the Use Manual, another goal successfully accomplished, thus it was possible to reach the main result, 32 flights in inspections obtaining efficiency in the time of the activity without risks to the technician.

Project/Initiative Name: Electronic System of Agendas of the Federal Executive Branch - e-Agendas
Description:
Lobbying is a legitimate and relevant activity for the promotion of quality public policies that adhere to social needs. It is necessary, however, to provide transparency to this practice in order to strengthen the activities of interest groups, social participation, and prevent undue influence. To this end, the e-Agenda System was launched - the first integrated platform that allows the registration, analysis, monitoring and transparency of lobbying activities in the Brazilian federal government.

The e-Agenda System - is a unique platform for the registration and publication on the internet of information on hearings between public agents and lobbyists - participants, institutions represented and detailed agenda. The system has mandatory and structured fields, which will allow any interested party to analyze the available data in order to identify, for example, which private institutions and interest groups have influenced which agendas in a particular public institution. 

The institution of the e-Agenda System is one of the Plan's actions. Since August 2022, public institutions have been accessing the training environment in order to prepare for the effective use of the System as of October 9. Likewise, the delivery of the Lobbying Bill to the National Congress was also a goal achieved as part of this commitment by the federal government.
Novelty: The e-Agendas platform is aligned with the pillars of digital government, and is innovative, in that i) it is integrated, since it centralizes lobbying information from all federal government agencies; ii) it is data-driven, since the way information is structured allows for the monitoring, analysis and promotion of public policies; iii) it is transparent, since data is available to any citizen; iv) it is essentially digital, since it was conceived considering the full potential of digital technologies; and v) it is disruptive, since it profoundly and substantially changes the way lobbying is done in Brazil.
Impact & Results: Awareness about the obligation to use e-Agendas as of October 9 began in April 2022. In August the System was made available to public institutions for training. Monitoring has been done by tracking accesses to the System and institutions are being contacted for guidance. It is expected that the entire federal executive branch will use the system correctly in order to build a database that is true to reality.

Organisation: Public Ministry of the State of Rio de Janeiro
Description:
Mosaic is an experimental project on the tactics and organizational designs of the Public Prosecutor's Office (MP), conducted by the Innovation Lab (Inova_MPRJ). Its focus is to improve - with innovation, technology and evidence - the results of the institution, especially in the bodies charged with protecting the public and environmental heritage, preventing and fighting acts of corruption and defending fundamental rights.

The Public Prosecutor's Office is a controlling institution whose mission is to defend the legal order, the democratic system, and the inalienable social and individual interests. Among its functions to achieve these objectives, two stand out for their relevance: 
- Defense of the public patrimony (including the environmental), in particular through the prevention and repression of corruption. 
- Defense of fundamental rights, especially by inspecting government programs and public policies to make them more effective, economical and capable of reducing inequalities of opportunity. 

The Public Prosecutors' Offices are differentiated mainly by their attributions - which are the delimitation of themes and (currently) instruments (of investigation and judicial measures) that each office can act on. The attributions are organized in two large groups: civil and criminal. The instruments of action, on the other hand, are the type of action and investigation that each attribution entails. 

Mosaic presented a proposal for change: the possibility of integrated use of criminal and civil attributions by a single body - the PJTC. The implementation of the proposal follows an incremental-experimental logic. In other words, starting with voluntary prosecutors - for this, it is enough to have the manifestation of will from some PJTC and the agreement of the criminal prosecutors with attribution for crimes committed against the Public Administration in the same territorial region.
Novelty: There are already public prosecutors' offices with hybrid attribution (civil and criminal) in some units of the Public Prosecutor's Office. The initiatives, however, have not achieved the objectives of greater effectiveness. Mosaic's differential is its focus on incremental and experimental change, building together with expert prosecutors best practices for action and disseminating institutional knowledge.
Impact & Results: The project's partner prosecutor participated in an experiment, testing the proposal imagined as a product of Mosaic. The case concerned a report of irregular disposal of solid waste on a rural property. Following this path, the expected conclusion of the case would be an average of 3.8 years. On an experimental basis, the partner PJTC made use of the criminal attribution, initiating a criminal investigation procedure (PIC). Because of this, the PJTC was able to use the criminal transaction as a tool to present a proposal to stop the damage and restore the area. With a greater incentive (criminal liability) than a Conduct Adjustment Term (TAC) - a civil instrument. The result was a satisfactory outcome within two months of the opening of the procedure.

Organisation: Secretariat for Personnel Management and Performance (SGP) Central Org of the Civilian Personnel System (SIPEC) of the Ministry of Economy (ME)
Description:
ConectaGente brings together managers of people management units and professionals in the area and has removed the levels of distancing between the agencies, whose communication was hierarchical and very bureaucratic. The core rethought the ways of approaching and interacting with the network, transforming this relationship into a more collaborative, direct, interactive, and inclusive one.

ConectaGente is the SIPEC network's governance center. Its mission is to bring the Public Administration's people management network closer to ensure that the purposes, guidelines, norms, plans and policies for human capital management reach the federal civil servants, generating greater public value.

ConectaGente - CG has alternative and innovative models to guarantee the exchange of information among the SIPEC network. What was previously done in a bureaucratic and indirect way is now done directly, without bureaucracy, with the participation of more agencies, virtually, and with reduced costs for the public administration. 

ConectaGente uses empathy, simple language, assertive communication, active listening, collaborative and affectionate management, and systemic vision in the search for greater efficiency in achieving its goals. The GC wants to have an impact not only on the improvement of results, but also on the experience of the employees working in the people management area, bringing more purpose and engagement to their work.
Novelty: ConectaGente wants to have an impact not only on the improvement of results, but also on the experience of the public servants working in the area of people management, bringing more purpose and engagement to their work.
Impact & Results:
ConectaGente (CG) carried out several actions aimed at integrating and improving communication and the relationship between the SIPEC Network. ConectaGente (virtual) meetings were held, reaching 4,000 views, and 23 on-call sessions with more than 3,000 online participants and more than 7,000 views in partnership with the SGP departments.

Promoted Webinars with the SouGov team, reaching 254,000 views on Youtube and created tutorial videos guiding the use of the SouGov.br app, with more than 200,000 views. In the Perception of SIPEC Network Members Survey (2021) about the Impact of ConectaGente in their work processes reached 67 public servants and 48 organs of the SIPEC network:
- 87% considered themselves satisfied with ConectaGente and 67% have some sense of belonging to the network
- 86.5% stated that the network helps them solve problems in their work processes.

Project/Initiative Name: PROFOCOS - RONDONIENSE PROGRAM TO STRENGTHEN SOCIAL CONTROL
Organisation: CONTROLLER GENERAL OF THE STATE OF RONDÔNIA-CGE/RO
Description:
The government of the state of Rondônia, has developed an action program that consists of a set of measures to face crises through the strengthening of social control. With this, through integrated actions with society and local governments, it is possible to seek solutions to extraordinary problems such as inefficient public spending like the Covid-19 or the improvement of vaccination test procedures, as well as its extension to the fight against forest fires in the Amazon.

The PROFOCOS - Rondonian Program for the Strengthening of Social Control, which is a series of measures instituted by the Comptroller General of the State of Rondonia - CGE/RO, which consists in calling on society to act as the state's inspector, as well as to propose solutions. 

Thus, the program consists in the launching, by CGE/RO, of a calendar of coordinated actions due to urgent needs as they arise. Among the coordinated actions one can highlight: i) EFAS - training school of social auditors ; ii) integrated control, iii) audit 360. 
The program's objective was:
- be a quick governmental response to emerging problems.
- To encourage social participation in the search for solutions to emerging problems.
- To be a control instrument of governmental action, avoiding abuses and irregular measures.

So far the program has had about 450 citizens trained on line for the most diverse topics and more than 1700 municipal managers (authorities and public servants) were able to exchange experiences with the authorities of the state of Rondônia in the search for coordinated solutions.
Novelty:
The PROFOCOS program is innovative in fostering social control in the Amazon because:
(i) It has the capacity to contribute to the critical sense of the citizen without leaving home and in distant areas;
ii) It can be used and parameterized for emerging and extraordinary crises 
iii) It makes it possible to bring together local and regional governments and society in a quick way to find solutions and there is, simultaneously, the development of the concept of citizenship.
The Great innovation is the possibility that everyone, regardless of locality and adversity, can be the controller of public power and inducer of solutions to complex problems.
Impact & Results: Of the 52 municipalities of the State of Rondônia, the initiative had the participation of 35 and more than 2500 people joining the program. PROFOCOS was also recommended as a good practice by the National Strategy to Fight Corruption and Money Laundering in Brazil - Enccla, the Public Prosecutor's Office at the Audit Court - MPC, and the Court of Justice of the State of Rondônia - TJ/RO. It is important to remember that the program also had as interested persons and participants citizens from other states of the federation such as Acre, Amazonas, Mato Grosso, Minas Gerais, Pernambuco, Espírito Santo, São Paulo, Paraná and Santa Catarina. The participation of such entities may have been due to the comprehensiveness of the spontaneous media coverage in more than 60 insertions.

Description:
The management of school feeding is a complex task for Brazilian municipalities. Besides the challenge of managing the entire diversity of foodstuffs, quantities and deadlines. In this context, the startup Lemobs developed a technological solution that provides support to professionals involved with school meals. 

The solution consists of integrated software and applications that allow the management of school meals according to the PNAE guidelines, including the management of menus, nutritional per capita, inventory and cost management, control and approval of orders requested/received, acceptability and food quality tests, as well as tools for nutritional monitoring of students, which allow the recording of health data, allergies, intolerances, and other conditions that assist the planning and implementation of the food supply in schools.

The software automates the various stages of the process, respecting the legal framework of the National School Feeding Program - PNAE, which has more than 80 regulations, from nutritional tables to accountability. With the identification of the students' profiles, the nutritionists can prepare and make available menus and recipes for schools to choose from, which then place their orders in an automated way through the application, generating more transparency and ease of management by allowing the control and visualization of the whole process by those involved (Secretary of Education, nutrition team, and school board), from the availability of the menus to the delivery by the suppliers.
Novelty: The innovative impact directly affects family farming, since PNAE requires that 30% of the resources allocated to school meals by the Federal Government be reserved for family farming. The system is able to identify the farmers' supply and the needs of each school, and generates the orders automatically based on the menu definitions and stock control. It automates the distribution of orders for vegetables, fruits, legumes, eggs, among other family farming products, simplifying the work of school principals. In this way, the Secretariat of Education increases its support to farmers, facilitating the direct purchase of more volumes and varieties of their products.
Impact & Results: After the implementation of the AEI, 58 preparation sheets and 48 menus were prepared for the Tengatuí Marangatú Indian School, which reduced food waste by almost 80% in 10 weeks. To this end, analyses were made of the rest-ingesta index, which assesses the quantities wasted (leftover dishes). Training sessions were held with the cooks to adapt the menus, portioning and presentation of dishes, and the integral use of food in order to ensure the legacy of the implemented changes.

Organisation: Public Ministry of the State of Mato Grosso
Description:
The scope of the MPMT's Workforce Sizing Project was: 1. to measure the distribution of the workforce among members and public servants of the MP/MT per region, per district and per enforcement agency, pointing out potential gaps of undersizing and oversizing. 2. to evaluate the need for the creation or extinction of an enforcement agency of the finalistic area. 3) To provide subsidies for the distribution of positions and place of employment in an eventual public competition. 4) To guide the development or improvement of applications and collaborative systems. 

To this end, it developed a new form of analysis of the social diagnosis, measurement, comparisons and methods for proposing changes in the institution's staff based on reliable and realistic criteria according to the institutional unit: Prosecutor's Offices, District Attorney's Offices, auxiliary organs of the higher administration and administrative departments.

In the development of its methodology, the project carried out an analysis of the current reality of the work force in the institution, as well as a projection of demand for the coming years, which will be made available in Business Intelligence in an interactive way, and can be replicated to several institutional realities, considering that it has three macro stages: 1. analysis of socioeconomic data. 2. survey and systematization of personnel data. 3. analysis of final productivity data and with it the item 4. dimensioning results.
Novelty: It is innovative for attributing a managerial vision around the workforce expended in the institution, as opposed to the previous method based on empirical knowledge. Today the institution has a vision of all its work posts in terms of workforce, that is, the manager can visualize exactly the surplus and the lack of human resources in all the administrative units and execution organs, without losing sight of society's needs, once the measurement of the workforce is weighted by socioeconomic indicators, by population size, besides considering the demand that arrives at the public prosecutors' offices.
Impact & Results: Development of a methodology that enables the analysis from structured data, transforming this stock of data into opportunities and insights for decision making in human resources management. Identification of administrative units of final performance with surplus or lack of human resources. Extraction of patterns, recurrences and trends correlating the socioeconomic indicators of the Prosecutor's Offices, human resources and productivity of the location, which generates the dimensioning result, comparing them in the respective groups. It triggered the construction of administrative acts in order to reorganize work processes.

Organisation: Attorney General of the National Treasury - PGFN
Description:
Brazil has more than R$ 2.7 trillion in evaded taxes. Part of the debt is secured by more than 100,000 assets that should be sold at public auction, which has not been happening effectively.

These situations will be monetized by Comprei through interaction/negotiation with debtors using behavioral theories, or by promoting the sale of the asset in a business model based on the theory of games.

The scope of the proposal is to solve the problem of the Brazilian federal tax agency, PGFN. However, nothing prevents the proposal from being extended to other federal entities and, subsequently, to subnational agencies that have problems related to the judicial auction of assets that guarantee debts with the Brazilian government.
- First, Comprei innovates in mass data-driven analysis. Auctions, especially tax justice auctions, are driven by casuistry, and it is common for assets to not reach the value of the procedural cost or simply not be sold at all. 
- Second, when Comprei negotiates by modeling customized approaches to various debtor and asset modality.
Novelty:
Before Comprei, isolated attempts by judicial branch units and executive agencies had limited success due to territorial restrictions or technological shortcomings.

Comprei is an innovation in that it is guided by data processing to choose the strategy, by the output monitoring network indicating the program's evolution path, by technology in the development of the web tool, by the use of economic theories in modeling the negotiation with the debtor or the sale of the asset, and by the integration with other stakeholders and veto agents based on institutional agreements.
Impact & Results:
Initially, the web program was developed, regulatory changes were edited and new institutional arrangements were implemented in PGFN. 
The next step was to expand the database of assets. From 2021 to 2022, there was a qualitative expansion (depuration of the stock with the removal of unserviceable or no longer valid guarantees) and quantitative expansion (approximately 27%). This expansion allowed the Comprei to be inserted in the PGFN's strategy ruler, to be taken to the Judiciary by the organization's more than 2,000 Prosecutors.

Description:
The innovation was developed in view of the Brazilian challenges in promoting public integrity and tackling the misspending and misappropriation of public funds. It is based on artificial intelligence with more than 9 robots that capture open public data with a focus on forensics and traceability having its impact on public management and its effect on society. We were pioneers in Brazil in AI for the public sector, being recognized nationally and internationally.

The goal is to promote compliance and integrity in the public sector, ensuring an environment of free competition among suppliers, mitigating the externalities in the public procurement process. The benefit is the direct impact on public management through the quality of public hiring and monitoring of public spending. The effect on society through the promotion of quality public services and monitoring of public actions through transparency and open data. One of the examples is the action at the local level with the empowerment of the Municipal Mayors in the decision-making process.

The innovation has a high replicability potential in more than 5,000 Brazilian municipalities since the delivery to the municipal mayor is in the form of a report in simple and accessible language, without increasing the number of municipal employees and fixed costs.
Novelty: Our differential is in the use of Artificial Intelligence allied to the capture and cross-referencing of public data from different databases, with ample capacity for mining and creating insights in plain language, not requiring the input of data by the public servant. We can perform in seconds the work of months of a qualified control analyst, generating as a result reports of 8 years of analysis of 100% of payments made.
Impact & Results:
-More than 982 billion Reais in public spending monitored and analyzed.
- More than 40 million people impacted.
- More than 18 agencies served in 15 different federative entities and branches of government.
- More than 95 million reais of irregularities pointed out.
- The results were measured through mathematical calculation and evaluation indicators
- We will impact more than 2,000 Brazilian municipalities in the next 5 years, impacting more than 100 million people.

Description:
Direito Ágil, a university group and later a startup, created Maria da Penha Virtual, a web application that seeks to guarantee women in situations of domestic and family violence access to effective justice by automatically generating the request for emergency protective measures and sending it directly to the competent court (Rio de Janeiro's Court of Justice), without having to leave home, through any electronic device, with accessibility for women with visual impairment.

Direito Ágil has developed Maria da Penha Virtual, a web application that seeks to realize the access to justice, the capacity of women in situations of domestic violence (articles 19 and 27 of Maria da Penha Law) and their protection through the automation of the request for urgent protective measures and direct sending to the court, in a fast, efficient and unbureaucratic way. 

The tool has already been implemented in the entire state of Rio de Janeiro, through an agreement with the Rio de Janeiro court, and has generated more than 2,000 requests for protective measures, and now we are seeking its expansion and improvement for the other courts, including negotiations already advanced with the TJPB, and expression of interest from other TJs such as TJTO, TJSE and TJAM. In this sense, the free implementation in the other courts means expanding the access to effective justice through the application, which has been proven to facilitate the protection of victims, reduce the time lapse between the request and the decision, make the process more accessible.

With a multidisciplinary background, we have developed our own methodology, which combines legal and evidentiary density, automation of procedural flows, and replicability with specific design for the protection of vulnerable groups. The Maria da Penha Virtual can be replicated on a large scale and customized with a specific design for the protection of vulnerable groups such as children/adolescents, the elderly, the LGBTQIA+ population, and people who suffer prejudice of other kinds; as well as to meet various other demands in which technology is envisioned as a solution.
Novelty: The Maria da Penha Virtual application is the concrete validation of the theoretical model for the realization of human rights conceived by the group that can be replicated on a large scale and customized for the protection of vulnerable groups. Moreover, it is observed the unprecedented automated generation of the request for urgent protective measures and the automatic sending to the Judiciary in order to guarantee to women in situations of domestic violence access to justice and their protection by judicial means, in an agile, efficient and unbureaucratic way. It seeks to make effective the postulatory capacity of the victim to request a protective measure, in the terms of articles 19 and 27 of the Maria da Penha Law.
Impact & Results:
Through an agreement via UFRJ, we implemented it in the TJRJ on 11/26/2020, having generated more than 2,000 requests for protective measures throughout the state of Rio de Janeiro, especially for women living in areas that lack public services, as shown in the Analytics created and in the Observatory of the court. 

Soon the court of Paraíba will also receive the tool, which won the Judge Viviane Vieira do Amaral Award, from the National Council of Justice (CNJ), having obtained the highest average score in the "Courts" category. On 03/08/2022, the application was expanded to all RJ.

Description:
Even in countries with high levels of digital literacy and where there is already widespread interaction with the State by electronic means, the telephone channel continues to be important as a quick and convenient means of clarifying doubts about public services, in addition to the information available through other channels.

Citizen Line provides a single phone number that functions as a gateway for citizens and companies to the Public Administration's services. The structure of the line is built by service and not by entity, allowing a unique user experience, without the need to know the competent bodies for each service. The delivery of telephone calls is done automatically, after the selection of the service on Citizen Line.

The creation of a single, centralized hotline for the entire Public Administration facilitates a holistic and detailed understanding of citizens' needs and also allows for centralized statistical processing of interactions with the public service.
Novelty: This innovation created a unique telephone line for citizens that is built with a user-centered approach, that makes it possible to make the relationship between citizens and public administration more direct and accessible.
Impact & Results: The Line integrates 5 entities: Agency for Administrative Modernization; Institute of Registration and Notary Affairs; Institute of Social Security; Tax and Customs Authority; Foreigners and Borders Service. Since the establishment of the Citizen Line, it processed more than 500'000 calls.
