Instead of presenting numerous individual journeys (CJM), the tool can process a significant amount of data, i.e. journeys.
Impact & Results: The testing and evaluation of the toolkit showed that its present content and format offer relevant and useful support to public organisations during their co-creation endeavours. For wider impact at the level of public sectors and/or identification of additional basis for improvement, the toolkit needs better promotion so public organisations become aware of it.

Project/Initiative Name: A Guide to Data Storytelling in the Public Sector. Communicating Public Finances Effectively
Organisation: Institute of Public Management of the Zurich University of Applied Sciences (ZHAW)
Description:
The Guide supports public finance professionals in preparing and communicating government financial information so that the key insight derived from it can be understood by internal and external addressees, irrespective of their levels of financial literacy. It is therefore the missing link to making better use of the rapidly growing volume of public financial management data. It creates public value by enabling well-informed decision making and promoting transparency of public finances.

The “Guide to Data Storytelling in the Public Sector "Communicating Public Finances Effectively” is designed to translate facts, numbers, and expert knowledge into understandable and relatable stories. Processing, preparing, and communicating financial information derived from the PFM system in a comprehensible and user-friendly way is the prime aim of this Guide. It presents PFM specialists with a menu of options to realize their communication project by offering tips, tricks, a helpful toolbox for each element of a data-driven story as well as a repository of practice examples. It also includes a framework that outlines the process and individual steps when telling a story. In the final chapter, the Guide provides some thoughts on dealing with ethical aspects and delineates the boundaries between storytelling and manipulation.

While Data Storytelling is not a new approach, it has never been contextualized for the public sector systematically. The Guide is the first knowledge product that elaborates a comprehensive framework to be readily applied particularly in the area of PFM. The content was tested and refined in continuing education trainings developed by the ZHAW Institute of Public Management to build capacity of PFM experts in the German speaking Europe. It is envisaged that the Guide will not only be disseminated but will also serve as a basis for structured trainings in other languages.
Novelty:
While Data Storytelling is not a new approach, it has never been contextualized for the public sector systematically. The Guide is the first knowledge product that elaborates a comprehensive framework to be readily applied by PFM experts.

The Guide in and of itself employs an innovative design concept: it takes the form of a print publication as well as a downloadable e-document. It incorporates several technologies and multimedia, such as augmented reality applications to deliver video content, and QR codes linking to an online repository of resources and use-cases. The repository can be regularly updated and ensures an agile product.
Impact & Results: The Guide will be finalized in the coming weeks, translated into four languages, and presented at the World Bank’s Ministerial Conference in Vienna, Austria at end of November 2022.

Organisation: DIRECTORATE GENERAL OF LABOUR RELATIONS/MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND SOCIAL AFFAIRS
Description:
GNSI develops a culture of experimentation, connects SI communities, facilitates networking innovation in managing authorities, policy makers, civil servants, public orgs, social innovation ecosystem. Provides institutional support for developing competences, scaling, social initiatives evaluation criteria and instruments. Finally, transfers knowledge, know-how, tools throughout the ecosystem and countries' competence centres with a less developed social innovation ecosystem. Tackles dual transition.

GSNI paves the way for developing the Greek SI competence center to assist the ecosystem to:
- develop a common definition and a corresponding vision of SI as a form of innovation with an orientation to societal challenges, based on long terms sustainability and for new new products, services, processes to be codesign and co produced by networks of stakeholder, including beneficiaries with active role and Public Sector and policy makers as SI promoter and co-producer;
- Actively work with key stakeholders within and beyond the national ecosystems to create a more harmonised and coordinated framework and agenda for social innovation in each country.
- Further empower policymakers to use the tools and principles of social innovation to make better, more innovative policy.
Novelty: The use of multi-dimensional and multi-scientific cross-knowledge / backgrounds, collaborative methods, to combine human knowledge, through technology tools and open innovation approaches, to create public and open infrastructures capable of assisting national managerial authorities and social entrepreneurs to take advantage of funds to mainstream SI as a form of Public Sector Innovation to tackle societal challenges.
Impact & Results:
Results: i) Digital Query, creation of database for Greek SI ecosystem. Relevant mapping exercise in social economy and social start-ups domain. ii) Public and private-public entities' mapping in SI. iii) Collaboration platform established iv) higher awareness level for SI achieved v) creation of the 1st SI National Registry vi) six National Capacity Building webinars completed.
Measured by: kpi index (number of SI members in Registry, number of SI activities, measures and initiatives, range and size of beneficiaries, UI/UX features, duration of activities, sustainability, probabilities, social impact figures, beneficiaries' satisfaction indicators etc). Results based management framework, instant feedback digital tools.

Description:
The Evaluation Task Force (ETF) is an innovative unit, created in response to this issue as part of the UK government’s landmark Declaration on Government Reform. As a joint Cabinet Office-HM Treasury unit, the ETF’s objective is to put evaluation evidence at the heart of government decisions, so that HM Government (HMG) can have confidence the money it spends is delivering better outcomes for the British public, and delivers value for money. The ETF aims to ensure that government programmes put robust evaluations in place to learn lessons and continually improve outcomes for the public.

The ETF apply the methods of evaluation and experimentation that are common in medicine to the £1 trillion that HM Government spends each year on public services. We would never consider rolling out a vaccine that had not been rigorously tested and the ETF is trying to extend that approach to all important policies and programmes. The ETF has a headcount of 15 employees and delivers a range of activities to tackle the main barriers to robust evaluations in government and foster a culture of evaluation and experimentation. This includes providing advice and support to (i) HM Treasury Spending Teams on the evidence and evaluation plans underpinning departments’ spending proposals, and (ii) departments on how to design and deliver robust impact evaluation.

During the 2021 Spending Review, we reviewed 86 bids worth over £10 billion in three weeks. The team worked with HM Treasury to consider the quality of evidence underpinning the spending proposals; check whether proposals used counterfactuals to test if their idea worked; and set evaluation conditions in departments’ spending settlements to improve the quality of evaluation for funded programmes. This is driving a change in the very culture of government.
Novelty: The ETF is unique in that it is staffed almost exclusively by senior evaluation specialists. Its structure was informed by international lessons learned and best-practice evidence on improving public policy evaluation. Further, the ETF’s status as a joint Cabinet Office-HM Treasury unit, and its integration into Treasury processes, gives it leverage to improve the quality and quantity of evaluation across government.
Impact & Results: Since its inception, the Evaluation Task Force has advised on over 160 programmes covering £48 billion of spend. During the 2021 Spending Review, we reviewed 86 bids, worth in excess of £10 billion, over three weeks. This informed a range of spending decisions in high priority areas, including youth services, criminal justice, education and COVID-19 recovery. We introduced evaluation-specific conditions into departments’ spending settlements for the first time. As a result, departments have published comprehensive evaluation strategies outlining a long-term approach to monitoring and evaluating their programmes.

Project/Initiative Name: Management Accountability Framework – Innovation Area of Management
Description:
A new Area of Management (AoM) has been integrated into the Management Accountability Framework (MAF). This is a tool used to promote management excellence in innovation for Canadians by assessing an organization’s ability to plan, generate and use rigorous evidence to inform decision-making on high-impact innovations. This is innovative in that it provides a clear roadmap to building thriving, innovative organizations and outlines a distinct role for executives in the process.

The objectives of this innovation are to Incentivize departments and agencies to:
1) commit resources to innovation and rigorous testing
2) generate reliable evidence through the rigorous testing of innovation
3) use rigorous evidence to inform decisions on high impact innovations.

This innovative reporting mechanism benefits departments as they build or expand their organizational innovation capacity. By conducting an evidence intake and assessment, the MAF allows for close monitoring of innovation activities across government, builds incentives for deputy heads and executives to promote, support and implement innovation within their respective organisations and disseminates best practices across the federal public service.
Novelty:
The Innovation Area of Management is innovative because it shifts the dialogue on public sector innovation from the periphery to the mainstream, by focusing on the following elements:
1) Innovation management excellence
2) High-impact innovations
3) High-quality monitoring information
Impact & Results: The Innovation AoM has been implemented for the 2022-2023 MAF reporting cycle. The Innovation Area of Management is helping to shape the dialogue on innovation both across and within federal departments. Through our consultation work with users, we have already started to see the benefits of this renewed dialogue (e.g., renewed enthusiasm for the MAF, relationships formed with new stakeholders, strategic long-term planning, etc.).

Project/Initiative Name: 'Seoul Learn' for Restoring Education as a Social Ladder
Description:
The Seoul Metropolitan Government (SMG) is pursuing three goals and following basic plans that were set while establishing 'Seoul Learn' policies. The three goals are: (1) Creating “a fair city of education” by focusing on supporting low-income families and tackling educational disparities induced by income levels, (2) Creating “a sustainable city of education” by offering educational content customized for each life cycle, and (3) Creating “an innovative city of educational” by building innovative educational platforms that combine different educational technologies.

Thanks to this innovation, teenage Seoul citizens belonging to the vulnerable group can take an online qualification test and join Seoul Learn. Member students can choose up to three educational content providers (1 regular school subject, 1 language and certificate, and 1 reading) and enjoy all of their content without any limits. In addition, members can apply for 1:1 online and offline mentoring services affiliated with prestigious universities in Korea. Mentors from each college are helping teenage students with their learning plans, emotional stability, and career design. Private companies are also joining the project by providing learning materials, such as textbooks, as part of their corporate social responsibility activities.

Seoul aims to develop the website of Seoul Learn into an educational platform combining educational technologies by July 2023. This will allow the SMG to manage learning history, make it into data sets, and provide tailored education. The city government also plans to prepare interactive, hands-on remote educational programs.
Novelty:
'Seoul Learn' is an innovation in educational policy for the following reasons:
1) By leveraging private educational content, Seoul Learn overcomes the traditional approach that tries to tackle the educational gap only through public education. The use of private content dramatically increases the vulnerable class’ access to education.
2) By providing private educational content on a public platform free of charge, Seoul Learn alleviates the burden of private education fees for the underprivileged class and prevents education from causing further inequalities.
3) By using a new educational platform applying innovative technologies, such as AI and big data, members of Seoul Learn will be able to take advantage of individually customized educational services regardless of time and place.
Impact & Results:
Since its launch, more than 16,000 people have joined Seoul Learn within a year. However, to evaluate the outcome more objectively, the SMG asked Hankook Research for more detailed surveys. After three rounds of assessment from September to December 2021, Seoul Learn turned out to have gained positive feedback from the public, scoring 85 in overall satisfaction and 88 in willingness to continue using the service.\

Although there is not enough objective data as Seoul Learn is at its early stage, enough cases proved that it helped students with college admissions, career decisions, and academic achievements. Notably, some previous members of Seoul Learn are now contributing to the project as mentors, showing the virtuous cycle of this program. Seoul believes that educational content provided on public platforms can help tackle unequal access to education and contribute to social integration by creating a fair society where anyone can succeed.

Description:
Less than 5% of Small Business (SBs) in Australia take out formal intellectual property protection. TM Embed aims to engage these small businesses at critical points of their business journey by building their awareness, educating them about trademarks, and simplifying the way in which they engage with the trademark system. We do so through our easy-to-use AI-assisted trademark self-service tool, embedded at must-use business partner touch points.

Small businesses themselves can now search whether their brand name is available for free and apply themselves, all within 15 minutes. This can help equip small businesses with the confidence to decide whether a trade mark is right for them. And the goal to TM embed was to democratise access to intellectual property protection and make it 10 times easier for small business owners to access brand protection.

The team tackled three jobs to be done throughout our build. (1) Maximise reach and awareness with the approximately 666 thousand existing SMEs and 108K yearly business target for IP protection. (2) Educating the right SMEs at the right time in their start-up journey and help them understand the benefits for them. (3) Help self-filers start and succeed with their IP applications.
Novelty: The product is a digital first approach to a currently under-served segment of Australian businesses. The AI-assisted tool expands the degree to which users have visibility of the trade mark application process; it has semantic search to help users find the relevant goods and services for them and their brand; and it applies a ‘bucketing approach’ to help the AI compare like trade marks with like.
Impact & Results: To date, almost 100 small business owners who did not have intellectual property protection have engaged with TM Embed and submitted applications to add a trade mark to their business portfolio and over 9 thousand have used it to check a trade mark availability.

Project/Initiative Name: TISK-IKT (the system of digital solutions to help citizens, health professionals and authorities to handle the pandemic)
Description:
TISK-IKT is a system of digital solutions set up in short time to help citizens, health professionals and authorities to handle the pandemic, such as "unique ID to citizen", "booking of test time", "portal for registering rapid test results by health professionals", "test results to citizen via messaging", "test results to citizen in national health portal", "combined entry data into Norway and test results for local pandemic tracking" and "covid-19 passport".

TISK IKT is of course a technical innovation. More than that, it is an organisational innovation, bringing together agencies - 9 government agencies and the medical association- owning pieces of the solution and stimulating cooperation between them. Each part was developed according to a broad plan, with agile project management, and with detailed architecture only were strictly needed.

The cooperation established through organisational innovation in TISK IKT has already been re-used to set up integrated digital solutions for helping Ukrainian refugees.
Novelty: TISK-IKT is innovative because it is the result of the cooperation of nine organisations belonging to different sections of the Norwegian public administration and a medical association. This cooperation produced efficient tools to manage the Covid-19 pandemic, including solutions that are completely new such as the system for local pandemic tracking with travel and test data, as well as the covid-19 certificate.
Impact & Results: A digital solution on the portal "National online health services in Norway" (Helsenorge) became the main channel for sending and explaining more than 11 million test results to citizens (the entire population in Norway is 5,4 mill) which would otherwise require communication in a different way or a different handling of the result. Citizens have booked time for more than 750,000 corona tests electronically, saving time both for them and healtcare personnel. The proportion of e-consultations with GPs went from approx. 5% before the pandemic to almost 30% during and after the pandemic. More than 46 million covid-19 certificates have been downloaded.

Description:
Japan is the least digitalized country among the developed nations. To tackle this, our city has been building the foundation that allows us to digitalize public services simultaneously. Shibuya was one of the first in Japan to digitize administrative services, such as applying for a resident's card with a smartphone. The city carried out many projects to lay the foundation of digital government.

First, we started a project called Co-Creation Hub in which Shibuya collects city data and make it open so that innovators could collaborate with us. Second, as digitization requires that its users have the literacy to use the latest services, we educate our people and provide digital devices to children and senior citizens. Third, Shibuya offers a proof-of-concept program called "Innovation for New Normal" where we let startups to test their product in the city. More innovations include the fact that residents can receive various public services using the messaging app "LINE." Further, the digital local currency “Hachi-pay” will be launched in earnest this autumn and is expected to promote local economy and serve as a driving force for social activities. 

Finally, we will institutionalize the actions. In Shibuya, we have formed a consortium called "Shibuya Startup Deck" to create innovation through public-private partnerships and are creating projects through public-private partnerships. In such way, we are creating a system that supports innovation together with companies.
Novelty: The reason why Shibuya is unique is that it does not focus on the digitization of just one service but creates a foundation for the future in which government services are digitized simultaneously. For that reason, it is unique not only to extract data and issues, but also to frequently provide opportunities for education and training to residents so that they can respond to digitalization. In addition, we are taking measures to retain a large number of excellent human resources.
Impact & Results:
Of the 43,000 people aged 65 and over in Shibuya Ward, one in four does not own a smartphone. In this project, smartphones were provided to 1,413 people, and 1,748 people participated in the smartphone training. More than 80% answered that using smartphones had a positive impact on their lives. In the tablet supply project for junior high school students and younger, we supply tablets to over 7,000 students every year.

Over 1,000 Shibuya residents have registered as testers and will try out the startup's products. Through this program, nearly 50 startups have tested their products in Shibuya and grown their businesses.

In the LINE project, about 30 functions have been implemented, such as applications for resident cards and tax certificates, and reservations for various courses and interviews.

Description:
Gov Buys Innovation is a modern version of public procurement, axed on the active search for innovative solutions available on the market. By reconsidering the way of interaction between the public and the private sector, we aspire to transform public entities into pioneers for new creative ideas, modernising countries in the benefit of private companies and all citizens.

We start by posting “challenges”, which are clear open questions, determining all necessary specifications, but leaving enough space for creativity. The civil servant calling for tenders will take on a new role of expert of the problem, instead of expert of solutions. To reduce the administrative burden for the participants, application processes are organised in three phases. First, businesses introduce a brief description of their idea and an explanation of their vision on how it could solve the problem. The second stage consists of a pitch with a discussion and if possible, a proof of concept. The final round is the actual introduction of a negotiable offer. After every phase all solutions are evaluated and graded, and businesses receive extensive feedback on how their idea was perceived - regardless of being successfully selected.

A procedure via Gov Buys Innovation allows to only buy experimentation. To participate in a call for tenders, businesses therefore do not need the in-house capability to produce their product on a large scale. Evidently, tested solutions can be further developed and eventually be implemented if information gathered during experimentation proves to be successful.
Novelty: This innovation forms a point of contact for companies wanting to help the government innovate and provide their innovative solutions and it promotes experimenting within public administrations to avoid big projects failing and tax money being wasted. It does so with a experimentation-oriented tender mechanism.
Impact & Results:
In the last two years Gov Buys Innovation has managed to attract more than 50 businesses and accompanied six public procurement projects. Four challenges have fully passed the evaluation and experimentation stage. A challenge that was published both on our platform and the classic procurement way, has leas to 25 submissions by 14 businesses through our approach and to two submissions by one business the classic way.

Public authorities as well as companies are increasingly open to our project. We are receiving more requests from government agencies for exploratory talks to start a challenge and former challenge-owners are coming back for further cooperation. Also government decision-makers help us to find the right group of officials for a specific project. We anticipate the number of challenges to double in the next year.

Project/Initiative Name: Achieving Cyber Security Resilience of Public Sector IT Systems and Services
Description:
Recognising the need for a high level of Cyber Security across the public sector, the National Cyber Security Strategy (NCSS) called for the creation of a Common Cyber Security Baseline Standard across all Public Sector Bodies and for the establishment a “CORE Network” to share best practice for cyber security across Government. This project embodies the Irish Civil Service Renewal 2030 Strategy by delivering evidence-informed policy and services, harnessing digital technology and innovation.

The project aimed to create a single, comprehensive and robust cyber security standard across the entire public service. It aimed to increase the level of cyber security in the public sector, as well as developing a level of mutual trust between Public Service Bodies s by outlining the baseline measures all should achieve. In addition, through the creation of the CORE network the project aimed to pool the cyber security resources and expertise of the Government to a single point, providing synergies and strengthening the individual capabilities of each member
Novelty: The project aimed to create a single, comprehensive and robust cyber security standard across the entire public service and, innovatively, it is the result of the adoption of a whole-og-government approach and the cooperation of 13 public agencies.
Impact & Results: The publication of the Cyber Security Baseline Standards gives over 800 Public Sector Bodies a process, procedure, a common language and a risk-based approach to Cyber Security. The main goal of improving resilience and security of Public Sector IT systems has been achieved with feedback both publicly and privately to the NCSC and the Government has been overwhelmingly positive. There have been numerous examples of the Cyber Security Baseline Standards being adopted by both Public Sector Bodies and private organisations to develop their ability to defend against malicious activity and cyber threats.

Description:
Interactive action Agenda (eiAA) is an innovative solution, which was created to support the evaluation of the Training programe for managers in LSG units for 2021.The eiAA also support the implementation of the Public Administration Reform Strategy and accompanying public policy documents, especially in the part of using data for management purposes, providing better services to citizens, as well as better communication within the system.

During the Training Programme, participants acquired basic knowledge from the eight areas: Public policies, Public services, Local self-government system, Public Finance, Public procurement, Roma inclusion and Youth policy. After the program, NAPA’s team designed eiAA so that mayors could be more oganized and have better control of monitoring the implementation of the most important occupations and general activities.

The eiAA enables easier monitoring of planned and implemented activities. It is based on an application that is filled out by all users individually, and then in the reporting section, different reports are generated. The eiAA enables the NAPA’s team, primeminister and relevant ministries also to monitor and evaluate the application of acquired knowledge.

The main origin of the initiative is certainly easier monitoring of the training program’s effects. If knowledge is not applied, it can never lead to change or reform. Only a trained administration that applies new knowledge can provide better services to citizens and the economy. The basic idea of the project arose spontaneously, during the preparation of the program evaluation, it was noticed that it is difficult to monitor the application of the acquired knowledge in practice. In that sense, the project enables easier monitoring of the entire process and managing the planned activities
Novelty: The eiAA introduces an innovative, modern way of doing business that enables the involvement of all stakeholders and decision makers in the process of reporting on the achieved results, monitoring and decision making based on objective data.
Impact & Results: The launch of the application is expected with the constitution of the new Government.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital business model ecosystem of EKOME S.A.
Organisation: National Centre of Audiovisual Media and Communication (EKOME)
Description:
The ICT Directorate of EKOME, a new public company operating under the supervision and strategy of the Greek Ministry of Digital Governance, designed and developed a digital business model ecosystem ensuring business continuity and a flexible remote working environment. Based on cloud solutions, it is designed to encapsulate procedures to coordinate 15 regional Film Offices in Greece and support daily operations. The aim is to provide entirely online services not only to the company’s staff and directorates, but also to film producers, individuals, citizens, organizations, regional communities.

Firstly, we proceeded with the supply of portable equipment (laptops, peripheral devices, and software). Secondly, we installed docking stations, computer screens and peripheral equipment, so that employees could move easily between available office space, in line with their daily work plan and the availability working stations. In relation to the latter, a close collaboration with organizations and companies from both the public and private sector was of major importance. Cloud technology has played an important role in this plan, as all the data and information of EKOME can be available remotely, anytime, and anywhere. Further, hosting all systems on a governmental cloud would ensure continuous operation, in compliance with the desired standards of cyber security.

Through the portal EKOME, aims to coordinate 15 remote film offices and enable film production companies to look for film locations, professionals, and agents. Technologies like Microsoft Azure, Office 365 and online security software are some of the basic parts of this model to enhance communication between staff, handle file storages, tiered roles to designate control of file systems and access from all portable devices, and disaster recovery plans in compliance with the cloud policy and with lower costs.
Novelty: EKOME managed to operate in full digital mode, with the aid of technologies that enhance mobility of its employees.
Impact & Results: The innovation of our digital business model has improved the effectiveness and efficiency of the company’s staff, has modernized procedures, has improved quality of services provided, while reducing the time of workflows within a safe and reliable integrated system.

Organisation: Cabinet Secretary for Finance and the Economy
Description:
The Scottish AI strategy sets out a vision for AI in Scotland, the principles that will guide the region, and the actions they’ll take to develop and strengthen its AI ecosystem over the next five years.

This is the result of extensive consultation with the public, industry, the public sector and academia on artificial intelligence and its place in the Scottish society. It gives the government a roadmap detailing the horizon to go and what is needed to do to get there. 
The Playbook, an open and practical guide to how Scotland do AI in Scotland, introduces our vision, our principles and the practices we will adopt to realise our vision. Scotland will become a leader in the development and use of trustworthy, ethical and inclusive AI
Novelty: First AI strategy for Scotland, and independent one from UK's already existing one.
Impact & Results:
The strategy will be able to:
- Fairer : Ensuring that Scottish values are embedded in the approach to adoption of AI
- Greener : Enabling to make better use of resources and develop new, low carbon industries
- More Prosperous : Empowering innovation and building on a foundation of public trust, empowering the workforce and creating high value jobs
- Outward Looking : Engaging with global partners that share Scottish values, forging partnerships and contributing to tackling shared challenges

Organisation: Ministry of Communication and Information Technology
Description:
The Digital Transformation Units (DTUs) are a qualitative leap in government performance to become a participatory, smart, paperless government, during which communication and information exchange takes place through digital mechanisms. The DTUs also act as a key player in every government agency and represent the front line of technical support in the Egyptian government.

MCIT provided technical support and necessary training to develop and activate the digital transformation units at ministries, governorates, and public entities. The necessary measures were taken in terms of assessments and tests to select the best candidates to work in the digital transformation departments at ministries, governorates and public entities. An integrated plan was implemented to provide technical support to employees and leaders of the digital transformation departments at ministries, governorates and public entities. 

The plan includes building an operational structure for the mentioned units, providing programs, workshops and dialogue meetings to build the digital capacities of the leaders and employees, offering academic support activities in digital transformation and developing a Standard Operating Procedure manual (SOP) in the mentioned units. The DTUs are a good lesson of a sustainable digital transformation for public entities which is easy to be replicated across other entities.
Novelty: The DTU provides technical support and necessary training to develop and active the digital transformation units across the different levels of government. Innovative in government because: DTUs are agile, their action is focused on building digital leadership skills, not only hard digital technical skills, DTUs emphasis is on mass, large scale capacity building with the aim of no employee being left behind or “digitally illiterate”.
Impact & Results: Port Said was where the first project took place - with 5 others currently in place. Digital transformation projects have been absorbed more efficiently, and successfully. More has been achieved with less, more citizens adopt digital government services, resistance to digital tools and means has decreased both among employees and citizens and increasingly paper-system are being eradicated. Most importantly, trust in digital has increased with more government employees adopting digital tools and platforms smoothly. Results have been measured using impact analysis with a quantitative method being used to analyse the data of trainees throughout the various milestones of the project in terms of training hours but also platforms usage.

Description:
The city of Bochum has developed a comprehensive and future-oriented Smart City concept together with the municipal companies, the Ruhr Commerce Chamber and the UniverCity network. Broadband expansion, the digital city hall, climate-friendly mobility and the development of a smart city app for Bochum - this and much more can be found in the concept.

In order to achieve the core objectives of the Smart City Bochum concept, the guiding themes are each subdivided into three central fields of action, which in turn are underpinned by concrete implementation projects. The Smart City Bochum concept defines five key topics in which the city of Bochum is striving for greater digitization: 1) Intelligent city management, 2) Sustainable infrastructure, 3) Innovative economy & Science, 4) Digital Society, 5) Sustainable Mobility & Environment

The city of Bochum's funding application includes, for example, the development of a municipal smart city data platform, the introduction of digital participation tools or the increased development of intelligent sensor technology in the city area. In order to bring the citizens along with it in the ever faster advancing digitalization, the city is also relying heavily on the House of Knowledge, which will represent the heart of the Bochum Smart City as a digital-analog educational space. 

Furthermore, the Smart City Innovation Unit (SCIU) was founded in May 2021 to manage and implement the Smart City concept. The team of the unit consists of employees of the city and the municipal companies, who are delegated to the unit as Smart City managers.
Novelty: The city of Bochum has developed a comprehensive and future-oriented Smart City concept and conceptualized a Smart City Innovation Unit to manage the strategic aims.
Impact & Results:
- 5 guiding themes
- 15 fields of action
- 87 projects

Description:
Vikua develops technology that allows capturing and integrating data from cities, helping governments in the management of urban services and in their decision-making processes. At Vikua they implemented a digital government data room, to manage city services, from anywhere.

The impact/benefits are: 
- Real-time city information
- Control of resources
- Reduction in execution time
- Improved citizen perception

Their purpose is to transform emerging cities into smart ones and support the digital transition of local governments and their officials.
Novelty: The platform implements a digital government data room, to manage city services, from anywhere.
Impact & Results:
Impact in El Salvador: https://vikua.com/caso-de-exito-salvador/ 
Impact in Huancayo (Peru): https://vikua.com/caso-de-exito-huancayo/

Project/Initiative Name: AKSADRON - National Academy for Drone Sports Excellence
Description:
Under the Budget 2022 plan, the Finance Minister of Malaysia announced a RM5 million allocation to establish the National Academy for Drone Sports Excellence (AKSADRON). Through a gamification process, the Government is encouraging the youth to explore drone sports as a career of choice to propel the country’s economic growth in the foreseeable future. AKSADRON is expected to be a one stop center for both drone air sport enthusiasts and drone innovators nationwide with complete facilities and equipment. 

We foresee the AKSADRON to have 2 components such as young drone training programme and competitions. To chart the direction of Drone Sports, AKSADRON has been tasked to undertake industry studies via the National Drone Sports Strategic Roadmap (NADSAR) that outlines the direction of AKSADRON as the centre for drone sports excellence. The purpose of the NADSAR is to set the national direction and implementation roadmap in establishing a robust sports drone ecosystem in Malaysia. As a centre for drone sports excellence, AKSADRON will be establishing initiatives on regulatory framework, standardising drone sports training modules & certification catering from beginners to professional levels, as well as working on talents and career development in this vertical. 

AKSADRON will address the lack of coordination with which the diffusion of drone sports has taken place in Malaysia. Further, it is supposed to be a way to tackle the lack of interest in STEM subjects in the Malaysian youth. To address these issues, the Malaysian Government is trying new ways to incite excitement amongst the youth and sees an opportunity in drone sports as it combines sports and technology.
Novelty: AKSADRON is the first drone sports centre of excellence at least regionally if not the world, supported by the government to ensure the successful roll out of the drone sports initiatives in the country. It supports the diffusion of STEM disciplines, and it will standardize all the training programmes and certification pertaining to drone technology.
Impact & Results: We are in the midst constructing the AKSADRON facility which is expected to be completed by end of the year 2022. The results have yet to be identified but the potential impact of AKSADRON will be encouraging.

Project/Initiative Name: Flood Relief Scheme Climate Change Adaptation Planning
Description:
The Irish government are making substantial investments in infrastructure to manage flood risk while that risk is increasing due to the impacts of climate change. To ensure the effectiveness and sustainability of this investment for future, as well as current, generations we have developed a method to plan, assess, and design flood risk infrastructure that is adaptable to the potential impacts of climate change. This assessment will take the form of Scheme Climate Change Adaptation Plans (SCCAPs) that will contribute to the design process and inform the future planning of adaptation measures.

The project piloted the potential use and application of SCCAPs on an ongoing flood relief scheme in Midleton, Cork.  Through this pilot project a set of viable climate change adaptation pathways have been identified to mitigate future increases in flood risk in the pilot study area. The SCCAP for Midleton has been used to inform the design of the flood relief scheme to safeguard all identified potential adaptation pathways and to ensure it can be adapted in a cost-effective manner when the need arises in the future.

SCCAPs assess the potential for an increase in flood risk under a range of potential climate change scenarios, identify the need to adapt, and consider what incremental or transformative adaptation actions may be necessary and when. The viability of each adaptation option is assessed and used to construct adaptation pathways, presented as a decision tree or route map, which are subsequently monitored and reviewed as climate change impacts are realised.

As of 2021, the OPW had completed 51 major flood protection infrastructure projects and a further 119 are due to be completed under the government’s National Development Plan. All future flood relief schemes will have a SCCAP in place which will be used to help proactively inform the long-term strategic management of potential future flood risk before it materialises. This represents a step-change in flood risk management in Ireland.
Novelty: SCCAPs enable flood protection infrastructure to be planned, assessed, and designed in a manner that safeguards, and enables cost-effective, future adaptation – maximising flexibility in the decision-making of future generations and allowing them to deal with climate change effectively when the need arises.
Impact & Results:
The project has resulted in the completion of Ireland’s first ever SCCAP, adopting the innovative use of adaptation pathways, a practice only known to have been adopted by nine countries globally and one country in the European Union. Subsequently technical guidance has been produced to capture lessons learned and ensure national consistency in the future development of SCCAPs for all existing and planned flood relief schemes.

The innovation, once rolled out on all flood relief schemes, will enable strategic and proactive management of future increases in flood risk at the national level, to ensure Ireland is resilient in the face of climate change impacts including sea level rise and increased rainfall.

Organisation: Regional Electoral Committee of the State of Minas Gerais
Description:
The Sustainable Elections Campaign aims to sensitize candidates and political parties to forward advertisement billboards to recyclable material associations in the state of Minas Gerais, benefiting workers from recyclable material associations. The initiative also aims to help reduce the pollution generated by the electoral campaign, and to enhance the work of associations and cooperatives of recyclable material workers in the state of Minas Gerais. 

In the initial phase of the campaign, the TRE-MG contacts associations and cooperatives in Minas Gerais, and asks for information about their interest in receiving the material. It then invites the political parties and candidates to send the leftover electoral propaganda from the first and second rounds, such as paper, plastic, canvas, to the nearest associations and cooperatives of recyclable material workers, and provides in the invitation a link to the TRE-MG website to consult the project's information.

The Sustainable Elections Campaign page publishes the list of cooperatives and associations of recyclable material collectors, as well as guidelines for candidates and political parties. At the end of the campaign, the recycling associations and cooperatives forward photos and information about the weight of the leftover electoral advertising material received, for the dissemination of the results of the initiative.

In the first edition of the Sustainable Elections Campaign, in 2018, more than 11 tons of advertising material (stickers, cardboard, banners and pamphlets) were delivered to recycling associations and cooperatives in ten municipalities in Minas Gerais. In 2020, more than 28 tons of material was collected in 21 municipalities. In 2022, the record of associations and cooperatives that participated in the initiative was reached: 186, with about 40 tons of materials delivered so far in 34 municipalities.
Novelty:
The Sustainable Elections Campaign, institutionalized in TRE-MG, has been carried out since 2018. Each election, it has been possible to give the environmentally correct disposal of a larger amount of electoral advertising, as can be seen in the results.

The TRE-MG has shared information with other regions that are interested in carrying out this initiative, since the wider scope of this campaign brings benefits to society as a whole. In 2022, it is possible to verify that some Regional Electoral Courts are carrying out the campaign for the first time, as is the case of TRE-MS and TRE-PI.
Impact & Results:
This activity was developed with simple initiatives, achieved its objectives with environmentally appropriate disposal of more than 80 tons of electoral leftovers, generated participation and good results throughout the state of Minas Gerais, contributed to improving the income of workers from cooperatives and/or associations of recyclable materials, and did not incur costs to the Court.

After the campaign, the recycling associations and cooperatives forward to the TRE-MG photos and information about the weight of the leftover electoral advertising material received.

Project/Initiative Name: Course for Management and Preparation of Polling Station Staff (Gestão de Mesários)
Organisation: Regional Electoral Committee of the State of Minas Gerais
Description:
The Management of Poll Clerks course is an innovative practice, as it presents a managerial approach to the issues involved in the work process of calling, appointing, training, and certifying poll clerks, as well as logistical support. The goal is to allow the electoral registries to develop action plans so that the whole process related to the mesary people can be carried out on time, efficiently.

With a systemic vision of the entire work process, the course allows the heads of the registry to plan, organize, and orient, so that the clerks have an experience that encourages volunteerism and that those summoned to work perform their jobs safely and transparently, with good guidance.
The course was developed together with the Judiciary School with the application of diversified technological resources in order to promote various stimuli.

Thus, with a systematized vision, a diversity of stimuli, and innovative themes, the course aims to ensure that the work process on clerks is highlighted with the relevance it deserves, since the clerks are the closest contact between the Electoral Justice and the voter on voting day, and their actions are directly relevant to the credibility of the Electoral Justice before society. This course can be improved for the next cycles based on the knowledge construction that was created in this first implementation.
Novelty: The course was innovative because it brought a systematic vision of the entire work process that had not yet been presented in other training courses. Bringing the training from the planning to the end of the work process promotes the engagement of the server in the process that takes responsibility and ownership of the action, with differentiated service delivery.
Impact & Results: The work process of convocation had greater standardization, which impacts directly on the electoral registry and data consolidation.

Organisation: Zorg-Saam ZKJ (non-profit organisation for residential elderly care) with the support of the Flemish Programme for Innovation Procurement (PIP)
Description:
More than 80% of the residents in residential elderly care suffer from unwanted urine loss. This is why care staff check on them regularly, day and night. In order to minimize these disturbing and time-consuming incontinence rounds, Zorg-Saam (a non-profit group of 15 residential care centres in Flanders) wanted to test new incontinence solutions, equipped with sensor technologies that detect when bandages are saturated and alert care staff. In this way we can offer more comfort to our residents, free our staff from unnecessary handling, and reduce waste.

The Programme for Innovation Procurement of the Government of Flanders supported Zorg-Saam with this innovation procurement, also financially. A thorough needs analysis by means of workshops, a prior open market consultation with potentially interested suppliers, and confidential face-to-face interviews with supplier were carried out. Then, a call for participation in pilots was opened and two companies were selected with the help of an external neutral evaluator.

After a short run-in period of 'trial and error', the smart solutions have proven to add value for both Zorg-Saam's residents and personnel: more comfort, less work. Moreover, they enable incontinence policies to be tailored to each resident’s needs, and at the same time also reducing waste. Convinced of the added value, Zorg-Saam wishes to further explore the use of smart incontinence solutions in its care centres in the near future.
Novelty: This project is innovative both because it provide an innovative solution to citizens but also because it it the result of innovative governance and procurement practices.
Impact & Results:
The results and impacts were monitored systematically by a neutral evaluator (Kunigi) based on a priorly defined set of criteria. After a run-in period the results demonstrated that the solutions lead to: (i) less handling (and thus more workable work), (ii) less unnecessary changing (and thus more sleep and comfort), (iii) - Less waste
- Additional data allowing for a personalised and tailor-made incontinence policy

Project/Initiative Name: Providing Independent Living and Safe Aging Services for the Elderly
Description:
Innovation creates a central support service for elderly people who need care and monitoring (elderly people who live alone and/or far from health & care facilities, are chronic sufferers). It operates for this purpose on a 24-hour basis a Call Center with properly trained staff of social workers and psychologists, who have direct access to each elderly person's file and respond to the elderly person's emergency calls by telephone.

In the event that the elderly face a problem, the 24-hour service immediately sends for help, whoever is needed (a volunteer neighbor, a member of his family, an ambulance, the Immediate Action, the Fire Department.) The service includes the 24-hour monitoring of the elderly through special devices ( devices home monitoring with fall detection & panic button, outdoor tracking and locator devices) which are given and installed to the elderly. 

This innovation is a telecare social service that aims to support independent living at home, people at risk due to age, health problems or social isolation, offering personalized attention that can improve safety conditions, reduce the feeling of loneliness in everyday life, strengthen independence, detect and prevent dangerous situations.
Novelty: Its innovative character results from its main objective, which is to improve the quality of life of senior citizens through the development and use of age-sensitive ICT (ICT: Information and Communication Technologies) solutions.
Impact & Results: Τhe innovation is fully operational today, providing 3,000 seniors with an emergency notification service as well as 24-hour service and communication with psychologists and other health professionals. This innovation saved lives in case of emergency.

Organisation: Audit Court of the State of São Paulo
Description:
The App Portal TCESP brings together in a single digital platform the main services of the TCESP for citizens and jurisdiction. The innovation is the possibility of allowing citizens to act as inspectors of public money, as well as interact with the Court. 

One of the motivations for the development of the TCESP Portal App is the current scenario, in which the use of smartphone phones is increasing and, moreover, it is verified that many homes do not have computers or notebooks, but do have cell phones. It was considered a strategy of the organization to make services available in mobile applications, in such a way that the information is more accessible to any citizen.

The TCESP Portal innovated by allowing access to the main TCESP services, using the typical features of mobile devices, such as notifications and geotagging, with a pleasant and friendly interface, in addition to usability. Before the launch of the TCESP Portal, services were only available through browsers and there were two applications with specific functions that were incorporated in the TCESP Portal. 

In addition, a new product was launched in the TCESP Portal, which is the Municipal Manager, concentrating TCESP information about a municipality in a single place, making it easier for any citizen to assess the situation in his municipality. The goal is to allow the citizen to be an inspector of public money, providing him with all kinds of information about his municipality.
Novelty:
The development of the application for cell phones using resources of mobile devices, such as georeferencing, notifications, visual resources such as accordion, alerts, is in itself an innovation in the organization. There was the incorporation of services that previously only existed on the web, such as the monitoring of sessions, news, events, live broadcasts, fiscalize with TCESP, digital protocol, among others.
In addition, a totally new product was developed in the application, which did not exist until then, which is the Municipal Manager. With the Municipal Manager, all the information on any theme in the municipality is centralized, presenting a "photograph" of the municipality's situation and making it easier for the citizen to analyze.
Impact & Results:
To our surprise, the application, which was developed mainly for the use of citizens and jurisdiction, is being used by the TCESP agents themselves, not only to receive news, watch the broadcasts, among others, but also to analyze how the supervised municipalities are doing, because as the data are centralized in a single place, it serves as a tool for auditing.
More than 160 reports of possible irregularities have already been received and analyzed by the inspection teams. These numbers, in just three months, show that the app has generated a positive effect for society.

Project/Initiative Name: Integrated Regional Bedside Health Management System “PANACEA”
Description:
PANACEA is an in-house, web-based system built to cater for the needs of healthcare professionals working in public hospitals in the region of Crete, Greece, while incorporating sophisticated and innovative quality assurance algorithms. It manages all electronic health record data around all clinical departments and laboratories creating a paperless environment. Patient Data is accessible at regional level creating a regional common data space, while patients have access to lab tests via web.

Thanks to PANACEA, medical staff performs visits with a tablet and has access to any information it needs by the patient’s bed. Further, staff can preview and update health files, laboratory tests, disease course, vital signs. Also, it can view graphs and select comparison graphs of vitals/laboratory tests. IT can also preview predictable scores for the incident, the interaction between prescribed drugs based on substances and patient data like age, gender, allergies and diseases.

PANACEA can provide the basis for the development of additional electronic health services, as it implies the creation of the database required for the development/application of new technologies that will promote research, the evaluation of health services and the creation of a health map, such as data analysis (big data analysis) and the application of Artificial Intelligence algorithms.
Novelty: PANACEA is the only electronic health record system operating at regional level in Greece. It was developed by a small group of IT professionals (public servants) with no cost. Its development and deployment around hospitals was based on innovative technical and organizational tools, such as open-source ticketing and user requirements collection tools, on peer health professional groups, on key-users identification, on-the-job and online training by other expert users, on incorporating quality assurance controls like drug interactions, nursing scales, adverse events registration etc.
Impact & Results: From a study carried out at the Pathology Clinic of the General Hospital of Rethymno, the use of PANACEA has essentially "saved" them, on a daily basis, around 10 to 16 hours of medical nursing time. This saved time by transforming bureaucratic processing into real medical/nursing time. Therefore, its use ensures more hours for the medical and nursing staff for patient care and not for bureaucratic procedures, due to the fact that most of them are done automatically. The system actually provides the guarantees to better and qualitative healthcare services.

Project/Initiative Name: Solar photovoltaic energy and food production: educating young people committed to the SDGs
Organisation: Secretariat of Institutional and International Relations
Description:
The Catholic University of Salta (UCASAL) and the German company SOLPRIME wish to contribute to the expansion of photovoltaic systems (solar panel systems) and electric mobility in Argentina through practice-oriented training and research achievements from the training center built at UCASAL.

All this, with the aim of promoting technological integration of photovoltaic power generation, as part of concrete solutions to various problems: food production, mobility, and compensation in electricity consumption from the contribution of energy generated by the greenhouse. We also sought to compare the productivity in relation to the traditional greenhouse with which the school already had; thus contributing to environmental care and to the technical and economic evaluation of the implementation, generating different attitudes and aptitudes in young people regarding the value of clean energy and the importance of mitigating the effects of climate change.

The innovation could be completed in two lines of action in the future, referring on the one hand, to clean and renewable energies, from the development of derived projects such as hydroponics and aquaponics incorporated to the photovoltaic greenhouse, and on the other hand, becoming a policy of social and educational inclusion, which generates greater equality of opportunities and an improvement in the quality of life of the population, from its replicability in two schools of the governmental Public Sector: Primary School No. 4526 "El Rosal", located in the rural area of the same name, in the town of Rosario de Lerma, 130 km. away from the city of Salta. and in the Technical Education School No. 3155 located in the town of Cafayate, a city 196 km. away from Salta city.
Novelty: The innovation sought to transfer knowledge on photovoltaic energy generation to students, as part of a productive solution of energy-food association; adding to the generation of energy through solar panels the production of vegetables under cover, thus being the first institution in Salta to incorporate the Food and Energy concept. In this sense, the cross-border cooperation developed with Germany, through the company SOLPRIME and the University, was of vital importance, both for the transfer of good practices and knowledge exchange, as well as for the financing of the infrastructure necessary for the project.
Impact & Results:
The innovation allowed the empowerment of the young people of the school in the use of new technologies that motivate the enthusiastic realization of new projects, and during the quarantine for the Covid-19 Pandemic. The periodic records of the Vegetable Garden Workshop and the comparison of the two greenhouses showed a greater soil cover and a growth of 20-30% in height of the plants in the solar greenhouse.

In the future, once the authorization procedures for the connection to the electricity grid are completed, it is expected to inject into the grid an amount that will reduce the annual energy consumption of the school by 40%, thanks to the contribution of 6000 kWh from the solar greenhouse, as well as the replicability of the project in the Public Schools mentioned above.

Project/Initiative Name: More Digital São Paulo - Data Structured Government for the execution public policies
Organisation: Sub Secretary of Citizen Services, Technology and Innovation
Description:
The "SP Mais Digital" Project refers to an Innovation in structuring and transversal actions in the digital transformation of the Government to increase satisfaction and generate savings for citizens in the use of public services, having as requirements: Expand and enhance access to and use of digital public services; Improve the efficiency of public management through the expansion of digital transformation; Improve the transparency of public management through technological tools. It considers two aspects:

- First, implementation and strengthening of CDESP - Central Data of the State of São Paulo and Development of a State Data Strategy, expanding the systemic capacity to store and cross-reference data, monitor and control the execution of processes and issue alerts of non-compliance.
- Second, the beneficiary is the Population of the State of São Paulo around 46 million inhabitants, or 22% of the population of Brazil, focused on the following aspects: (i) modernize the Digital Poupatempo citizen services platform which is a Platform that provides several digital services to the Citizen; (ii) digitalize state public services, including universal access features for people with disabilities; (iii) modernize the single access solution

The Government acts in the dissemination of open data, in order to make it accessible in its processes supported by digital technologies (including algorithms), available to the public to promote social engagement, within the limits of existing legislation and in balance with the public interest; and also, treating data as a strategic asset, being key in generating public value, through its application in planning, decision making, execution and monitoring of public policies, besides adopting rules and ethical principles for its use in a reliable and safe way.
Novelty: The Government establishes a single repository for improvement and innovation in data management, aiming at greater efficiency and effectiveness in the formulation, implementation and evaluation of Public Policies aimed at Citizens, Civil Society and Companies. All access to the data at CDESP must be made through a single platform, with traceability, auditing, governance and management, with the operationalization being the responsibility of the Information and Data Governance Steering Committee of the State of São Paulo. The Government acts as a promoter of Public Polices and holds data that make possible, in a structured, automated, fast, efficient way, lower costs, in the management of a group of selected 126 public organs.

Project/Initiative Name: The Early Hearing Detection and Intervention (EHDI) Programme at Chris Hani Baragwanath Academic Hospital (CHBAH)
Description:
There are approximately 16-17 infants born with congenital hearing loss daily in South Africa (SA). The goal of the programme is to strive for early hearing detection and intervention services (EHDI). 

Against the lack of audiologists available to perform Universal Newborn Hearing Screening (UNHS), the learners helped bridge this gap. The learners required further skills development training on communication, conflict management, neonatal high-risk factors and various patient-related difficulties that they struggled to understand. To meet this, training was done by the coordinating Audiologist. There was a lack of equipment so we looked at how many learners we could get based on existing equipment.

Our initiative of using non audiology personnel to achieve UNHS also creates employment opportunities, is the first innovation of this kind in South Africa. Through collaboration and extensive training, this programme has been successfully implemented, it is a sustainable programme and can serve to motivate for government mandate.
Novelty: This project aims at training personnel able to perform Universal Newborn Hearing Screening with the goal of striving for early hearing detection and intervention services (EHDI).
Impact & Results:
The department used to only achieve NHS for 4% of the population prior the introduction of hearing screeners into the UNHS programme. Since the UNHS programme the highest percentage achieved was 24% in July 2022.
The programme has improved our stakeholder engagement, leadership and training skills. Newborn Hearing Screeners get a qualification, experience and sense of purpose.
We have submitted a research article on this initiative with the hopes of other healthcare facilities implement a similar programme and it can also motivate for UNHS government mandate.

Project/Initiative Name: e-DLA: Online registration of applications for the competence of Port Police
Organisation: Ministry of Maritime Affairs & Insular Policy/ Hellenic Coast Guard
Description:
The e-DLA system aims at modernising the services provided, creating a single electronic database for recording and processing processes, digitising them, providing immediate access to statistics and speeding up inspections by Port Authorities (DLA). At the same time, it aims to provide remote services to interested parties (natural or legal persons) and allows information and services to be obtained immediately, saving considerable resources and man-hours.

The e-DLA information system is used for the registration of applications addressed to the Port Police Directorate by interested parties and the complete management of them by the Port Authorities without the requirement of the physical presence of the citizen. Through the system, the interested party navigates through the available applications, is informed about the current legislative framework and the necessary supporting documents. Subsequently, he can submit the application he wishes to the Port Authority of his choice, attaching the required supporting documents.

Currently in e-DLA, there are twelve (12) categories of administrative proceedings, in which several types of applications are included. The use of e-DLA benefits the general population and port authorities, while from putting the system into production mode, have benefited by using this over fifteen thousand people.
Novelty: Through e-DLA,the citizen, after being authenticated, can register his application and submit the required supporting documents in electronic form, so that it can be fully processed electronically by the competent Port Authorities. At no stage is the citizen's personal presence required
Impact & Results: Currently, via of e-DLA, twelve (12) administrative ones are processed DLA competence procedures, while there is possibility of adding others. To date, over than fifteen thousand (15,000) applications have been completed.

Project/Initiative Name: Becoming Agile; a way to deliver high quality products and solve long standing reform challenges in Government
Organisation: Office of the Prime Minister of Croatia
Description:
All Government have policy challenges that seem too complex to be solved. These challenges span across several Departments, have some areas where jurisdiction of Departments in unclear and any intervention would have serious impact on all citizens. We introduced Agile methodology and Agile teams, led by the Special adviser to the PM, Mrs Ivana Vukov, which mimicked startups, in using iterations and learning to inform their next move in solving a problem or delivering a better product. 

The PA Agile team had a profound impact on the development of the e-Citizens portal in Croatia. The team delivered a new portal that replaced four different websites and two different registries. It introduced user perspective and delivered a document "Standards for the development of public services" that was adopted by the Government session in April 2021. This document formalized the approach and introduced agile teams as a mechanism of developing new digital services.

The new e-Citizens portal was launched in May 2021, as a system that caters to all life roles (parent, business owner, guardian etc.) our citizens might hold. All in one place for all life roles and situations. The number of e-Citizen users grew 30% since. On the other side, the teams are proud of their work. The Agile approach allowed them to break down institutional silos.

We are in the process of drafting the Digital Croatia Strategy 2022-2032. In this strategy, we rely heavily on restructuring the way we manage digital transformation in Croatia and how the results are delivered operationally. Agile teams are now embedded, not only in the Standards for the development of e-services but also in delivery of our digital policy through an improved project management model.
Novelty: This was the first time that agile methodology was applied in Croatian Government.
Impact & Results:
The PA Agile team delivered e/Citizens portal that replaced four different websites and two registries. It became a central place for both citizens and business entities. The new e-authorization system allowed for services to be offered for all life situations.

The Agile team provided evidence that the Government should stop generating 13 million paper referrals/year. This had a positive impact on citizens and their access to hospital care. It also had a positive impact on the nurses who did not have to deal with two systems (physical and digital).

Project/Initiative Name: Metropolitan Commitment 2030: strategic planning to generate new governance
Description:
After three decades of experience with strategic plans in Barcelona, and in the face of the exhaustion of traditional processes, the Metropolitan Commitment 2030 represents a new conceptual and methodological model. The mission-driven innovation approach has been used as a means to determine a limited number of strategic objectives while generating new capacities and building governance in the metropolitan region, which does not have a specific level of government.

In this sense, the aim was to introduce innovations that would make it possible to: 1) adopt a more comprehensive and plural approach to the metropolitan reality that would make it possible to understand its complexity; 2) establish very specific and transformative medium-term objectives in a collaborative manner; 3) build capacities for action on a territorial scale that does not have a specific level of government; 4) combine strategy with the co-creation of solutions and the involvement of the widest possible number of actors in its development.

Thus, the main innovations are as follows:
1. challenge to overcome: introducing a more systemic look at the region's reality; 2. 
2. Challenge to overcome: difficulty in reaching the associative fabric of the metropolitan region and, beyond that, getting its involvement in a strategic plan of this scope. 
3.	Challenge to overcome: excess of strategic plans and feeling of lack of concreteness. 
4.	Challenge to overcome: a strategic plan for a territory that does not have a specific government. 
5.	Challenge to overcome: Deployment of the strategic plan beyond the major institutions. 

These innovations have sought to benefit municipalities and organizations in the peripheral areas of the Barcelona metropolitan region, which are fully affected by metropolitan dynamics and share its challenges, but do not have the tools or capacities to face them in the same way as the municipalities of the metropolitan core, which have the Barcelona Metropolitan Area as a second-tier local government for resources, technical support and spaces for coordination and collaboration.

It is interesting to note that the process was built using design, facilitation and prototyping methodologies from which the functioning of the successive stages was defined.
Novelty: Defining the strategy for a metropolitan region that does not have its own governing body involves a great effort to convince the large number and diversity of potentially involved stakeholders of the usefulness of the process and its outcome. It implies multiplying efforts to establish new alliances, create new tools and design new joint work processes to co-create policies and projects, as well as to convince these actors that the commitment acquired will lead to concrete actions that will lead to real transformations towards a better quality of life and of the environment. Finally, it implies rethinking the usual participation processes and reconsidering the role of the citizenry, mainly through small local organizations and citizen laboratories, bearing in mind that this is an aspect that had never been foreseen in previous strategic plans, whose development was entrusted to large institutions.
Impact & Results:
The innovation introduced have made it possible to define missions and the corresponding concrete commitments shared by all stakeholders. They have also laid the groundwork for the establishment of new partnerships, brought certain stakeholders closer to processes and instruments that were not part of their toolbox, and succeeded in involving other stakeholders who were not usually involved in this type of far-reaching plan. 

483 organizations of all types have been involved in the process. 493 people representing public administrations (25%), the private sector (28%), academia (22%), citizens' organizations (18%) and the media (7%) took part in the generation of knowledge and in the debates. Fifty-one percent were women.

Project/Initiative Name: Development of Pork Origin Discrimination Kit and Utilization in Policy
Organisation: National Agricultural Products Quality Management Service
Description:
The Korean Institute (NAQS) has developed a new pork-origin discrimination kit. The use of this kit has revolutionized the management of pork origin labeling in the Republic of Korea. The efficiency of enforcement for the indication of the origin of pork has been increased and the cost of analysis has been reduced. Because anyone can use this kit easily and the result is clear. This kit can be continuously used for the policy of origin indication and the quality management of pork.

Government officials can discriminate domestic or imported pork within 5 minutes at the site, such as marts or butchers. When government officials investigate violations of the mark of origin of pork, this kit makes it easy to obtain evidence, enabling a quick and efficient investigation suggesting scientific evidence. If the butcher shop’s owner did not admit the violation of the country-of-origin mark at the site of the country-of-origin mark, government officials filled out confirmation documents and collected samples before using the kits, but now the dispute can be easily resolved by showing the results of this kit.

Consumers, farmers and distributors benefit from this innovative pork origin discrimination kit. Consumers who purchase at a reasonable price can be guaranteed their right to know the origin of products by managing the origin labeling of pork well. And correct labeling of country origin can increase consumers' confidence in the labeling of the origin of domestic pork, thereby preserving the income of farmers.
Novelty: Government officials can check the origin of domestic or imported pork within 5 minutes at the investigation site. Unlike the physicochemical analysis method, it is possible to easily and conveniently determine the origin without the expertise and expensive equipment. From 7 days in the laboratory to 5 minutes in the field. Further, it significantly reduced sample amount: from 2kg to 0.3g. Most importantly, the price dropped from more than USD 200 to USD 7.
Impact & Results: By using this kit to manage the country-of-origin labeling, many violations have been corrected. The kit is also used by local governments, school meals, military units and large private retailers to check their own country of origin in terms of quality control when buying pork.

Description:
The solution, CONECTA PJUD, is innovative because it unified the means of digital service, benefiting users and court officials, and made it possible to redesign public service in the context of the health crisis.

In the first instance, multiple channels of attention were enabled, most of them diverging from one court to another, which generated a lot of confusion among users when they wanted to contact a particular court. Tools such as Zoom, Webex, Teams and Meet, in addition to the telephone service, were the mechanisms through which the courts tried to maintain the attention of the public. On the other hand, from the back office, officials had to face a multichannel service system where they had to manage a wide variety of tools, most of them unknown in the Judiciary at that time.

Conecta PJUD is an omnichannel platform, which aims to enable multiple possibilities and means of communication with court users through a single mechanism, which seeks to simplify the consultation and service processes by offering a unified experience regardless of the means of contact. In its origin, Conecta PJUD integrated videoconferencing, chat and WhatsApp, allowing the user to choose the most comfortable format and adjusted to their needs.
The design of the minimum viable product (MVP) was accompanied by a commitment of the courts participating in the development of the concept, to be part of a pilot that would test the already validated prototype.

The courts' motivation led to a series of ideas and measures aimed at strengthening the functional aspects of the platform: the statistical record of the service was consolidated for better management of the administrators; new functional elements were incorporated, such as case consultation, which allowed officials to access the user's history while resolving the service; a functionality was incorporated to help those who lack the technological means and sufficient digital literacy to file briefs with the help of the public service officer, among several other proposals.
