Brazil’s gov.br website offers almost 5.000 public services and has more than 200 million monthly page views on average. With so many options, it’s paramount to make them easily available. With that mindset, we developed two important improvements to its usability: the first is the use of AI to recommend services based on the citizen's browsing history. The second was creating User profiles as a way of finding services based on the citizens’ journeys.

The recommendation of public services using Artificial Intelligence in the Gov.BR Portal aims for the government to be proactive in relation to the real needs of the citizen, reducing the time spent finding public services and increasing their satisfaction. The initiative to create User Profiles aimed to improve the experience of finding public services, organizing offers based on specific moments in the lives of our citizens in order to facilitate the use of relevant services at each moment. So far, 7 profiles have been made available: Retired, Student, Entrepreneur, Driver, Worker, Farmer and Tourist.

The project offers a large-scale benefit and potentially reaches every citizen who accesses public services through the gov.br portal. In the future, our intention is to evolve the project on both fronts: on one hand, improve the intelligence behind service recommendations and make it multichannel, including proactive notifications in the gov.br App and other available communication channels.
Novelty:
The project is innovative in improving Brazil’s citizens experience because:
(1) It uses Artificial Intelligence to recommend public services. Despite being a relatively common practice in e-commerce sites, there is nothing similar in the Brazilian government. 
(2) The creation of user profiles on the gov.br portal is innovative compared to the previous model centered around the government's structure (like transportation, work, etc).
Impact & Results:
Currently, the project is in the implementation phase. Among the results achieved, we highlight:
(1) A 220% increase in the gov.br portal's conversion rate, which is measured by total service requests divided by total page views. We went from 2.8% in May 2021 to 9% in October 2022;
(2) The services recommended using AI have been viewed more than 51 million times. Around 25% of service requests through the gov.br portal's homepage originate from a service recommendation.
(3) 7 profiles have been made available: Retired, Student, Entrepreneur, Driver, Worker, Farmer and Tourist. As of October 2022, services appearing in this section were viewed about 2.4 million times, of which 1.3 million were requested, with a conversion rate of 53%, which demonstrates its high utility.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital fingerprint of deceased for identity verification
Description:
Thinking out of the box, Forensic Pathology Service Gauteng use a digital fingerprint scanner to capture deceased fingerprints and verified it against information at Dep Home Affairs, through a Know Your Client service provider. This empowers FPS to quickly identify mutilated, decomposed or burned bodies on a scientific manner when information and fingerprints are available. Giving a voice to the voiceless and render swift closure to the debriefed families. This is a first for FPS Gauteng.

In 2018 the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research South Africa took hands with FPS to test their OPTICAL COHERENCE TOMOGRAPHY (OCT) in capturing fingerprint biometrics with an optic laser. This system use laser to capture the dermis and epidermis fingerprint. I requested them to test if a normal biometric scanner can capture deceased fingerprints. They excepted the challenge and to my surprise it worked with an old scanner from MR Van Der Merwe. This spark to flame for the innovation and took it to the next level.

The objective of this innovation is to put the identification process in the center of the service rendered to the deceased and the family by making identification scientific , effective and efficient by improving quality of collection of biometric data to verify as quickly as possible. To reduce the trauma for the family. Also improving the trust in FPS process.
Novelty: This innovation by collecting name, surname and id number then capture left thumb and right thumb fingerprint to match it directly with information on HANIS to receive report of direct matching one to one was never done in this environment.
Impact & Results: Using the CSIR Software and electronic biometric scanner to scan 10 fingerprint of unknown deceased. This improve the quality of fingerprint produced for better Hit on database the improve positive identification. This innovation makes identity verification much cheaper as well.

Description:
The Academy developed a generic model called Metropolitan Municipality Governance Scorecard in order to measure the prevailing governance standards at metropolitan governments/municipalities. Our innovation serves a multi-dimensional conceptualisation of good governance as a culture, identification of the principles or good governance, and more importantly the operationalisation of related phenomena such as accountability or representation for institutions and the public.

We have publicly shared the results of the application of our model for the metropolitan municipalities in Turkey. We organised a workshop open to public and discussed the findings and worked on how to use and improve out innovative tool with a variety of participants. We also visited public officials from the central government and enlarged our networks for cooperation in local governance reforms. We also held meetings with the media and our research has been widely covered in local and national media.

Our outreach strategies target different actors and actor-specific goals. Cooperation with civil society organisations will focus on specific thematic areas such as development of social policies for children. City councils demand further cooperation on improvement of representation and participation in municipal governance. For public officials and municipalities, we have presented our work both as a tool for self-assessment and as a contribution to reform in local governance. We have developed certain policy recommendations for areas of governance scored lower in our research and potential underlying problems.
Novelty: First, while good governance is frequently measured through public perceptions of it, our model focuses on the use of publicly available information and documents from a citizen-centric perspective. Second, our operationalisation of good governance is based on a distinct approach that defines good governance both in terms of certain principles such as consistency and transparency, and as part of different governing processes from agenda setting to policy implementation, and of institutional learning for municipalities.
Impact & Results:
As of today, we concluded our research, held a public conference to release the results, a workshop with participants from civil society organizations, municipal employees and city councils, and published an online Guide in Turkish summarising our approach and model, research, main findings and policy recommendations. 

We conducted a survey for conference participants. Most of the participants in the survey represent Civil Society Organizations. Approximately 70% of the respondents stated that they find the project very useful and all of them expressed that the information presented during the conference is clear.

Project/Initiative Name: Centralized digital business license module, to get started faster and anywhere in Chile
Organisation: Ministry of Economy, Development and Tourism
Description:
The Ministry of Economy, has piloted a web-based module, that integrates a Business License application into the national Business Registry, to incentivize businesses to apply for a license online, right after incorporating and obtaining a tax number. This innovation provides undigitized municipalities an online channel with standard requirements for business in their jurisdictions, while gaining a free web-based backoffice workflow, with trusted digital legal and tax info to ease processing.

The module was designed and implemented gradually in stages. Phase 0: A Minimum Viable Product (MVP) designed and implemented as a QA/QC test site, with the minimum busienss license requirements by law and most requested by most municipalities, tested amongst 12 undigitalized municipalitie.  Phase 1: A fully functional and publicly available pilot with 26 municipalities, with Digital ID and interoperability features connected directly to the Business Registry ad Tax Agency website. This pilot was designed and implemented to scale to over 100 municipalities, as an estimation of jurisdictions in remote regions and locations, in which businesses were not applying for licenses.

Agile methodology was used to design and implement the MVP and successive improvements and final Pilot, along with functionalities including national Digital ID and interoperation for transfering oficial legal and tax information and data safely. This innovation was inspired by an innovative service in the Business Registry website, which included similar online services that would help business owners send legal and tax documents directly to anyone with an email, in an attached folder.
Novelty: This project provides the first centralized digital module, for businesses to apply online for their obligatory business license, connected to the national Business Registry website, no matter a business’s location or if their municipality is digitalised or not.
Impact & Results:
Results and impacts observed from the innovation:
- Businesses follow standard and transparent instructions, avoid confusion and in-person submissions.
- Processing time is reduced from over 200 days to 90 days.
- Municipalities/businesses reduce the use of paper.
- Municipalities have more certainty of legal and tax information, sent directly from a trusted source.

Throughout the business user and public servant user workflow, all actions and interactions are registered in a database in realtime. Feedback is collected and analysed, to include in technology, operational and regulatory improvements.

Project/Initiative Name: Lisbon Procurement Planning Platform for Innovation and Sustainability (Lx PPP-IS)
Description:
Lisbon Municipality aims for more innovation and sustainability in Public Procurement. Urgency and specifications' complexity are excuses for not considering sustainability in tenders and barriers to attracting innovative SMEs and startups. The innovative approach involved users designing and developing the Procurement Planning Platform, using a rapid development tool, cloud-based, and agile methods, which selects and supports a project-led approach for deploying new procurement strategies.

It supports the city's budget planning and the Procurement Annual Planning, aligning with SDGs. It is an innovative approach to the Public Procurement Pre-tendering stage, turning the annual Planning into a strategic action plan to promote innovation. Finally, it creates an Annual PP Plan and provides a baseline for monitoring outcomes and impacts. The development of Lx PPP-IS followed best practices of open data, using the OCDS standard, aiming to publish the Annual Procurement Plan before the start of the upcoming year, which is not the practice in Portuguese Public Administration.

The innovation is also about the software used, a low-code rapid development tool from Outsystems, a Portuguese-born unicorn. Lisbon Municipality has established an internal Outsystems Factory, run by our IT Department. Our Lx PPP-IS team defined the functional requirements involving end-users and then, using an agile project management approach, aligned with the scrum methodologies.
Novelty:
This innovation is innovative because:
1. Procurement Improvement: embeds both e-Procurement and Digital Procurement for medium-term Planning and sourcing strategy set-up for Public Procurement. 
2. Solution design: users are involved in the development waves (UX/UI, service design); the procurement unit leads and takes ownership of the development strategy; creating a competitive space for developers; the solution is compliant with open data (OCDS) at its core, and it is cloud-based and able to integrate with other systems.
3. Innovative approach: recognised as innovative by entities such as the local Public Procurement Regulator (IMPIC) and ICLEI (https://procuraplus.org)
Impact & Results: The Lx PPP-IS was piloted through the second half of 2021 and rolled out in April 2022. It currently supports the 2023 budget and 2023 Public Procurement Annual Plan. There was wide adoption of the community of +500 users, which usually had no other tool that Office and the e-Tendering platform for the procurement set-up.

Description:
This project funded culturally-appropriate programs to increase water-safety knowledge and skills, and provide opportunities for physical activity for refugees.  The project also offered an employment pathway into the aquatic industry, which in turn helped create culturally safe and welcoming environments at local swimming facilities. 

From 2018-2022, Metro South Health worked with multiple non-government and government partners to overcome the barriers to aquatic participation and helped over 2500 culturally diverse people complete beginner learn to swim and water safety programs. Participants were recruited via partnerships with local refugee settlement agencies, a refugee trauma service and the government-run Loganlea TAFE (Technical and Further Education) Adult Migrant English Program.

These organisations utilised culturally diverse instructors, to deliver the one-hour sessions. Hurricane Stars were specifically commissioned to deliver subsidised women-only programs, in privately-owned public pools, for Muslim women. Culturally sensitive swimming costumes were also supplied to those women who needed them and childcare was provided. Demand for these innovative courses from the culturally diverse community was significant, unlike other learn to swim programs that existed locally, but were not considered culturally safe.

Participants who wanted to pursue employment in the aquatic industry were supported to complete a modified 12 month swim instructor certification. This certification was provided by  the same 2 registered training organisations and were delivered over an extended time to ensure proper comprehension of all curriculum.
Novelty:
1) It addressed multiple issues for refugees through one intervention. i.e It has increased health behaviours (swimming for physical activity), it has resulted in employment at local swim facilities through a uniquely modified and supported instructor training, and it has created more welcoming and inclusive public environments in the local community. 
2) It was a collaboration of cross-sector partnerships: private swimming pools, local council (government), health department, local settlement agencies, state education agencies, private registered training organisations and community leaders.
Impact & Results:
Between 2018-2022, over 2500 culturally diverse people have completed learn to swim programs. In 2020, 72 women-only sessions inclusive of learn to swim and aqua aerobics were delivered.  98 women from 29 countries completed the program. Participants reported via pre and post survey, increased frequency and duration of physical activity.  

The programs have helped participants overcome the barriers to aquatic participation, including language, access to culturally sensitive swimwear, provision of a welcoming venue, cost, and childcare.

Project/Initiative Name: Simple rules of thumb that improve school management - Behavioural insights in education
Description:
Providing school principals with simple rules of thumb improves school management practices and support to teachers in cost-effective and scalable ways. Evidence from an impact evaluation by the Ministry of Education in Guatemala and the Behavioural Insights Team showed that, an intervention inspired by the behavioural science literature, increased the frequency and quality of school management practices by principals  and the satisfaction of teachers in Guatemala. 

We tested the impact of this intervention through a randomised control trial with  4,124 high schools and 2,892 principals. We found that the ‘rules of thumb’ intervention increased the frequency and quality of school management practices by principals across a broad range of indicators. Teachers who were in schools in the treatment group reported more planning sessions and class observations conducted by their principals.

The main beneficiaries from the intervention were principals and teachers in Guatemala. The intervention also made it easier for the Minsitry of Education to provide support to their staff with evidence-based practices in a cost-effective way. Beyond Guatemala, we would like to adapt and test this intervention in other countries to assess whether its impact replicates across different contexts.
Novelty: Our intervention aimed to create a cost-effective, scalable, evidence-based solution that addressed some of the key behavioural barriers to better school management practices. It was inspired by the work of Roland Fryer, which shows that school management can have a crucial effect on student performance i.e. a 300-hour training for principals in some basic management practices (including training on lesson planning, data-driven instruction, and teacher observation and coaching) can improve scores on standardised maths and tests by at least 0.08 standard deviations.
Impact & Results:
We surveyed teachers from each school at the end of the school year about management practices in their school (i.e. by their principal). The two primary outcomes were the frequency of formal planning sessions and frequency of classroom observations. Secondary outcomes included whether principals spoke to teachers about their teaching, helped them to set class objectives. 
The guidance resulted in an increase of 2.3 percentage points in the proportion of principals who were reported to conduct formal planning sessions every two weeks or more over a baseline of 24.1% (a 10% increase). We also find an increase of 2.0 percentage points in the proportion of principals who observed classrooms every two weeks over a 36.1% baseline (a 6% increase).

Project/Initiative Name: Document Drafting App for the Public Sector
Organisation: National Center of Audiovisual Media and Communication
Description:
The National Center of Audiovisual Media and Communication has developed a digital application which functions as a central database of relevant standard documents which are being produced on everyday basis. Each user can produce these documents by filling-in blank fields in a friendly and simple enviroment.  The app, apart from the creation of the document undertakes their storage as well as their naming title according to the information provided inside the document. 

Based on the needs and the advancements on the modern public administration for further activation and use of new technologies in the workplace in everyday life, the development of personalized interactive Application was implemented using the PowerApps platform. 

After thorough observation during management and drafting of documents, where the editor is asked to either draft one document from scratch, or, in cases of similar documents, replace multiple items in multi-page documents, errors, inconsistencies, difficulty in finding templates alongside other challenges were noticed.

Time increased directly for untrained employees and indirectly for the trained employees who will be asked to train the new ones. Due to the above, applications were developed within the PowerApps environment, applications developed entirely on of the aforementioned issues, on behalf of the "Financial Management & Human Resources Directorate" of EKOME, Directorate in which the need to produce documents is one of the its basic elements.
Novelty: The application manages the large volume of documents and minimizes the time required for their drafting. It standarizes the procedures, creates multiple documents at once, eliminates the margins of error in user friendly environment and it can be used by anyone regardless their digital skills.
Impact & Results: From the day the program pilot started, organization's employees who used the application managed their productive hours better, as the average drafting of each document decreased from ~15' minutes ~3', allowing them to return to their established subject matter and other urgent and current issues.

Description:
The project was born from the need to redesign the Ministry of Education (MI) decision-making processes. The ITeR application is an organizational and management tool built to suit this purpose. The beneficiaries of the innovation are the MI actors involved in the process, but also the citizens who benefit from the measures issued more quickly.  ITeR is innovative because it enables the cooperation of all actors, keeps track of all documents and greatly simplifies the decision-making process. 

The project aims to redesign the MI decision-making processes with a global approach focused on the overall consistency of the actions that lead to specific outputs in the various levels of government. ITeR conceived as an innovative approach has been designed to create a lasting impact, especially in institutional models subject to structural turbulence deriving from continuous political turnarounds. Furthermore, ITeR, also in order not to create divisions between "old" and "new", is an integrated system, or better, harmonized, with the existing systems.

ITeR was created to re-engineer processes focusing on medium-long term sustainability in terms of capitalization of information, activities, processes, and valorization of: informal documents; regulatory frameworks; identification keywords; summaries of processes. During the pandemic ITeR allowed employees to remotely sign relevant documents without the necessity to do it physically and avoid possible service interruptions due to pandemic restrictions.
Novelty:
The implementation of ITeR is not just a step towards dematerialization, it is, above all, an innovation in the governance of decision-making processes according to a public management approach. 

ITeR aims to create a virtuous relationship between the initial moment of needs analysis behind the decision-making processes and the final decisions in terms of institutional communication. So, ITeR aims to manage and monitor document flows in signature and for examination of the Head of Cabinet, Heads of Department, General Directorates and Offices Directorates.
Impact & Results:
This project fully digitalized the Ministry of Education sign process of relevant documents to increase the administrative machine’s productivity and inter-institutional (Minister and Cabinet Office)  and organizational ( Departments, Directions, Organizational Units) cooperation. 

The solution managed to better the reliability of the entire process too, even thanks to the possibility to track the status of the documents and create a digital repository which can be consulted ( also for future decisions) by the users.

Project/Initiative Name: Municipality of Palaio Faliro: Development of IOT Platform - Energy Upgrade
Description:
Τhe Municipality proceeded with the development of a centralized IOT management platform with the ability to control, record and analyze power consumption data and other resources in buildings and other infrastructures, which can function as a smart city base for the Municipality. Τhe Local Authorities will be able to obtain documented data and prepare relevant analyzes for the purpose of certifying the Municipality   and submit proposals for financing projects to reduce energy consumption.

The Municipality of Palaio Faliro, in collaboration with an external partner, developed a centralized IOT management platform with the ability to control, record and analyze power consumption data and other resources in buildings and other infrastructures (e.g. Municipal buildings, School facilities, KAPI , Sports facilities , street lighting, etc.). Τhe Service has the ability to obtain documented data and prepare relevant analyzes for the purpose of certifying  and submit proposals for the financing of actions regarding the energy upgrade of the Municipality to various bodies.

The goal of innovation is to improve technological knowledge, address environmental problems, create sustainable patterns of production and consumption, improve the quality of life, exploit resources, protect the environment, create bases for sustainable development of the city, increase municipal revenue through the use of financial tools, collection-evaluation of data for drawing up a strategic policy in the areas of cleanliness, the environment, the management of the municipality's property, the use of electric vehicles, the management of water resources.

The system has the ability to: 
1.	Central management of all potential energy resources. 
2.	Creation of reports formulating energy data and indicators (e.g. by group of connection type, by local community, by type of building use, etc.), key performance indicators (KPI), thermographs, etc. 
3.	Potential connection with appropriate measuring equipment to create interaction with sources of energy and not only consumption. 
4.	Potential connection with appropriate software for the utilization of past and current energy data. 
5.	Registration of all electricity supplies of the Municipality.
Novelty:
The IoT platform (SenseOne IoT) is a universal system for managing the energy behavior of the municipality's building infrastructure. The system has the ability to: 
1.	Central management of all potential energy resources 
2.	Creation of reports formulating energy data and indicators (e.g. by group of connection type, by type of building use, etc.), key performance indicators (KPI), thermographs, etc 
3.	Potential connection with appropriate measuring equipment to create interaction with sources of energy and not only consumption 
4.	Potential connection with appropriate software for the utilization of past and current energy data 
5.	Registration of all electricity supplies of the Municipality 
6.	Creating events, so that there is an automated notification process in case of non-normal behavior of the facilities (e.g. unexpected consumption from lighting, particularly increased consumption compared to the corresponding previous period, etc.).
Impact & Results:
- Collection of data for the optimal energy and operational efficiency of the municipality's infrastructures   
- Usefulness of smart meters  where there are large consumptions 
- Certification of the Municipality  
- Replacement of old street lighting lamps with led technology. 
- Highlighting the unnecessary consumption of energy for the subsequent reduction of operating costs

Project/Initiative Name: INTEGRATED MEDICAL RECORD SYSTEM (EMR/EHR) IN THE GENERAL HOSPITAL OF VERIA
Description:
Four years ago, the Medical File was added to the Hospital's information system, which is a digital information structure .The electronic medical record, unlike the handwritten medical record which was used before, can now be used simultaneously by several users and access is quick and easy. In addition, the medical decision is facilitated  since it collects all the necessary data for the patient and gives an overall picture of his health condition. 

The differences between the classic handwritten medical record and the electronic medical record are remarkable as they are also the reason why day by day the latter is gaining ground in the field of medicine. The handwritten medical file gives freedom of expression, does not require special training of the medical staff, can be easily transferred to the premises of a hospital without the use of a computer and has minimal cost. However, the required storage space of classic folders is now minimized as well as the fear of losing important data about the status of the patient. Moreover, the cost of such information systems was prohibitive so far.  

They are currently fully operational 
- the Administrative - Financial Subsystem and 
- the Patient Management subsystem 
- Laboratory Information System (L.I.S). 

Medical File now operates in all parts of the Hospital (Emergency Department, Clinics,) while it was connected (HL7) with the Laboratory Information System (LIS) upgrading the Hospital's services and facilitating at the same time Medical, Nursing and Administrative staff.
Novelty: The handwritten medical file gave freedom of expression, it did not require special training of the medical staff, it coul be easily transferred to the premises of a hospital without the use of a computer and it had a minimal cost. However, the electronic medical record can be used simultaneously by many users and access is fast and easy, there are no issues of readability as it supports a structured input of the data, it facilitates the medical decision since it gathers all the necessary information for the patient and gives an overall picture of his health status and is also given the possibility of complex analysis and fast data exchange. In addition, the required storage space of classic folders is minimized as well as the fear of losing important data about its status.
Impact & Results:
The impacts observed so far are the following: 
- Medical history, examinations and disease course (access to the doctor from any point of the Hospital) 
- Electronic Ticket - Electronic Discharge - Issuance of Medical Certificates. 
- Patient management by reducing time-consuming procedures and eliminating errors. 
- The medical information from the secretariat of the clinics, the nursing staff, the medical staff, etc. is immediately accessible thus facilitating personalized interventions for the patient. 
- Saving of Work Hours (human resources do not deal with paperwork, etc.). 
- Patient Identification (printing information on an Identification Bracelet).

Description:
The inability to interoperate between different information technology (IT) systems has urged the Cambodian government to develop Cambodia Data eXchange (CamDX).  CamDX is a unified yet decentralized data exchange layer between IT systems which offers a secure way to provide and consume services, ensures confidentiality, integrity and interoperability between many different data exchange parties. Whether you are a service consumer or provider, you benefit from CamDX in one way or another.

The Royal Government of Cambodia has embraced digital technology aiming to offer better public services to citizens. To name a few, Cambodia’s public services consist of the population database of the Ministry of Interior (MOI), e-Tax of the General Department of Taxation (GDT), the business registration IT system of the Ministry of Commerce (MOC), and vehicle registration IT system of the Ministry of Public Works and Transport (MPWT). However, many databases come with the absence of interoperability of the IT systems.  

CamDX stands for Cambodia Data eXchange, which adopts the model of X-Road of Estonia. The main goal of CamDX is to build an infrastructure that allows for establishing effortless access to data in government databases without compromising the security and ownership of the data with minimal technical changes in the existing information systems. The CamDX ecosystem also has an authentication system called CamDigiKey, a mobile application that requires users to input only information that reflects their real identity. Enabled by CamDX, any request to create an account will be reviewed against the population registry shared by the Ministry of Interior.
Novelty:
CamDX acts as a distributed system with no central database performing only data exchange between members. It is also designed to host the data exchange of both public and private sector services. Since there is no central database, CamDX has no single point of failure.

Moreover, it connects different IT systems built on any technology of choice. It also does not prescribe any tool or technology for the internal use of an organization. It provides secure data exchange between IT systems and does not own the data. The data still belongs to the organization who services the data.
Impact & Results: At the time of writing, CamDX has 438,532 monthly average number of transactions and has exchanged data for 4 services, namely OBR, GTAS, VAPGI, and the CamDigiKey e-KYC solution for the banking sector. With the presence of the data exchange layer in just 28 months of operation, the OBR system has been able to register 18,568 businesses, reserve 12,105 business names and grant 328 specialized business licenses.

Organisation: TDRA  - Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority
Description:
TDRA Career platform supports Talented Job Seekers to apply for a technological based jobs by leveraging emerging technologies such as Artificial Intelligence to enhance the selection process. The Project Could be implemented and adopted in diverse entities across the world. it was initiated to shift to a digital platform, support Government strategic goals toward a green environment, to cope up and adapt with pandemic and enhance user experience. 

TDRA has launched with “TDRA Careers Platform” – one of its kind innovation, after having many benchmark studies and research and development with both private and government sector, locally, regionally, and internationally to utilize the platform on the best manner. Also, through the user experience approach by testing real case scenarios from top brand name entities.

TDRA Careers Portal is a tool that centralizes a talent management database of information, automates routine tasks and simplifies recruitment processes. The end goal in using this tool is to optimize operations and free up recruiters’ time to work on more instinctual tasks. These objectives can lead to an increase in revenue margins and efficiencies. A system like TDRA Careers Platform helps achieve higher efficiency by eliminating the need for repetitive manual data entry.

With TDRA Careers Portal, not only is all of the relevant data available to you at all times but each department’s efficiency is also easily determinable via a central dashboard. An integrated dashboard will have access to the central data store. It will allow you to view relevant data as needed, making your analytical or performance reports more meaningful.
Novelty: “TDRA Careers Platform” is the first career platform integrated with “UAE Pass” -- the UAE Pass app is the first national digital identity and signature solution that enables users to identify themselves to government service providers in all emirates through a smartphone-based authentication. It also enables users to sign documents digitally with a high level of security -- this allows the candidate to have single sign on. Moreover, the TDRA Career Platform uses the feature of Evalufy (Read Body Language impression using AI) where it enhances the process of selection -- body language provides an amazing amount of information on what other people are thinking if you know what to look for -- Bringing artificial intelligence into your service organization is a prime example of the power of digital transformation.
Impact & Results: TDRA reduced the recruitment expenses 21% from AED 240,000 to AED 190,000.  continuous discipline to drive spending and cost reduction, while maximizing business value, where TDRA excels in it. Time to fill and time to hire are extremely important to highlight when mentioning the relevant metrics and outcomes, 14 days were difference after the implementation of “TDRA Careers Portal”, before it was 57 days to fill and hire where it became only 49 days afterwards. TDRA makes sure to measure candidates’ satisfaction throughout the full recruitment cycle, we call it candidates’ full experience.

Organisation: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation
Description:
MauPass has been developed for citizens and businesses to transact easily and securely with Government online. This initiative follows an urgent need for better identity and access management for a trusted ecosystem. This digital identity system supports a 2-factor authentication (2FA) for e-services requiring higher level of assurance and mechanisms featuring non-repudiation.It has a multi-channel protocol that allows integration with third party applications of public and private institutions. 

MauPass has been developed as a single window of authentication service to provide a layered approach towards e-Authentication implementation for all Government and other e-services. It also provides 2FA for e-services requiring higher level of assurance and mechanisms that support non-repudiation: Level 0 - No assurance of identity is required, Level 1 - Minimal level of assurance of identity is required - MauPass provides for username/password based authentication (1FA), Level 2 - Significant level of assurance of identity is required - MauPass provides for 2FA (SMS, Mobile App and e-mail), Level 3 - Substantial level of assurance of identity is required - MauPass may provide for Biometrics in the future. 

Maupass allows Government to confidently deploy sensitive e-services that handle personal information of citizens (e.g. enable citizens to access online birth certificates, online marriage certificates, digital vaccination passes, to perform e-signing, etc.). When users log in using their MauPass accounts, their data (name, surname, address, ID No., etc.) are validated by MauPass through the  Central Population Database. Once the users are authenticated, the various e-services can securely pull their data through MauPass. Consequently, all online forms become pre-populated and the users do not have to fill in bulky forms. 

Maupass uses MSSQL Server relational database management system. The system also interfaces with the Acuant Trusted Identity Platform Identity Verification and Document Authentication.
Novelty: MauPass is a single window authentication service which can be integrated in any application to authenticate the user. Service providers are thus relieved of the task of having to develop the interface for authentication within their applications. In addition, the risk of impersonation is eliminated, especially for systems requiring high level of assurance, since prior KYC registration of the user has already been carried out.   Furthermore, users no longer have to remember numerous credentials for accessing the various online services.
Impact & Results: The take-up of online services has increased considerably resulting in cost savings for Government, citizens and businesses. For instance, statistical records of the Government Online Centre reveal that the hit rate of access to the MoKloud digital safe has increased from 2067 in January 2022 to 12,172 in September 2022. In addition, the risk of impersonation in the usage of online services has been greatly minimized. No filing of such cases have been recorded on the Mauritian Cybercrime Reporting System (MAUCORS). The introduction of e-KYC registration will further enhance the MauPass service for foreigners and Mauritian citizens abroad.

Project/Initiative Name: Blockchain-based open source digital relief distribution management platform for humanitarian and public agencies
Description:
We are a blockchain company focused on social impact. With Rahat, we envision a world where aid distribution is inclusive and accessible for everyone. Rahat is an open-source blockchain-based cash and voucher assistance platform that supports vulnerable communities. This tool has helped local government bodies and humanitarian agencies manage cash distribution programs efficiently. 

Rahat platform issues, manages and monitors relief distribution in the form of digital tokens. It creates a transparent, efficient and cheaper way to distribute cash or goods. It also mobilizes local communities encouraging financial resilience and freedom. Rahat  uses a digital tokenized cash distribution technology dependent on a blockchain-based network for an immutable process to transfer aid efficiently ensuring a secure and fast payment.  

In Rahat, we include a population who still doesn’t have access to formal financial institutions. Unlike other e-payment systems, unbanked populations can easily be onboarded to the system without any proof of identity, making the process inclusive, accessible and accountable for everyone. With tokens, unbanked beneficiaries can determine how much money they want to spend to the vendor without needing to reveal payment account information. This will ensure that vendors cannot over charge beneficiaries without their consent.
Novelty: Rahat uses blockchain technology to record and monitor transactions in real-time. Because of the blockchain technology the ledger of transaction is immutable and secure hence, easily auditable lowering cost for program implentors. Rahat is a collection of open source projects which can help avoid vendor lock in for implementing agencies. We also have a crowdfunding tool which enables people to donate directly to individuals in need without any intermediaries.
Impact & Results:
- Completed the development of the digital CVA management system, enabling
- humanitarian aid efforts by building greater financial inclusion and increased
- accountability and transparency throughout the cash transfer process
- Ran a series of mini-pilots reaching 5,000+ beneficiaries aiming to reach 702K beneficiaries by 2026
- Certified Digital Public Good

Organisation: Telecommunications and digital government regulatory authority (TDRA)
Description:
The innovation is a platform where all the government sectors and private sectors could acquire the UAEPASS (Federal Digital Identity)  log-in Application programming interface(API)  and other services from a single platform and the end-user could install the API without interference with the UAEPASS Operation team. 

The UAE API Marketplace is a government platform that enables the UAE’s private sector to leverage on the Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) of the UAE government and use them to provide seamless services to the UAE’s citizens and residents. 

The API Marketplace aims to boost partnerships among government entities, and between the government and the private sectors, to provide interconnected digital services to customers around the clock and facilitate the provision of safe and reliable data and information. It will further facilitate business and e-commerce procedures. 
  
The first partnership on the platform was between Federal Tax Authority, Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority (TDRA) and eCommerce site Amazon. Through this partnership, services of Federal Tax Authority will be linked directly to the Amazon systems, thus promoting operations’ automation and accelerating sellers’ registration in the Amazon store in the UAE. 

The goal of the innovation is to : 
- Reduce the operation of on-borning entities that are willing to use UAEPASS services  
- A comprehensive platform that guides end-user to install the API without human interference
Novelty: because its solves huge operation challenges with a small process that is provided with a single platform. Moreover, because the innovation has been through the internal innovation process cycle.
Impact & Results:
'- Number of API’s Onboarded: 19 (Federal - 10, Federal (Upcoming) – 6, Digital Enablers -3) 
- Number of requests per service: 3 (MOI – 2, MOHRE – 1) 
- Number of Private Entities requesting APIs: 3 (Private entities requesting APIs – Standard Chartered, Data Value & Blue logic) 
- Number of requests for Contact Us: 8 (Business Clarifications – 4, Onboarding Requests – 4, Issues – 0) 
- All the private sector and government sectors will use UAEPASS for internal use and also for customer service requests.  
- Private sector will levergage the API to reduce their logitics costs.

Project/Initiative Name: Elaboration of electric vehicle charging plan  of the Municipality of Palaio Faliro
Description:
The Electric Vehicle Charging Plan is defined as the zoning program for publicly accessible electric vehicle recharging points and E/V parking spaces. The EVCP supports the spread of electrification in Municipalities creating an integrated and useful network with the aim of serving citizens and services. The Municipality of Palaio Faliro completed the preparation of the EVCP andi it was the second in the prefecture of Attica that was posted on the website of the Ministry of Environment and Energy.

The aim of the initiative is the developing of the EVCP of the Municipality of Palaio Faliro with the main aim of creating a masterplan, which concerns the planning of the installation of charging stations with an implementation horizon of three (3) years in order to cover the charging needs of all categories E/V for the next five (5) years. 

Development of a road map for continuous monitoring of the study and identification of risks Utilization of financial tools / the action was implemented with funding from the GREEN FUND. This action promotes to the public the spirit of environmental protection and the message of the development of green transportation in the daily life of citizens / reduction of pollution-noise pollution, reduction of greenhouse gas emissions, more efficient use of energy and in addition, electric mobility shows macroeconomic benefits & achieves a reduction mobility costs.
Novelty:
The developing of EVCP supports the infiltration of electric mobility within the administrative boundaries of each Municipality creating an integrated and usable network with the aim of serving citizens as well as visitors and related services. It is a new field as the use and operation of E/Vs is significantly different from those with thermal engines 
The implementation of the innovation also includes:
- Cost-benefit analysis and choice of implementation methodology of the EVCP-defined E/V recharging infrastructure network  
- Plan and time planning for positioning/licensing of E/V recharging points
- Specifications (technical, interoperability, etc.) of the proposed E/V Recharging infrastructure network
- Digital files with EVCP geospatial data. The digital files will concern the zoning proposal where the recharging points and E/V parking spaces will be depicted, with all of their necessary thematic properties.
Impact & Results:
Expected results: improvement of the traffic and environmental conditions of its territorial jurisdiction, development of electric mobility, development of entrepreneurship. 

Possible implementation methodology models. 
Supply costs approximately €150,000, connection costs approximately €63,000, operating costs and maintenance (from the 3rd year) approximately €31,000/year. 

1. Case 1: Construction and operation by the Municipality estimated return of approximately 4.8% for 15 years of use 
2. Case 2: Construction by the Municipality and operation by a Private estimated return of approximately 4.3% for 15 years of use 
3. Case 3: Construction and operation by an Individual estimated return of approximately €8,000/year for 15 years of use

Organisation: Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority (TDRA)
Description:
The UAE Pass includes a ‘digital vault’ for storing users’ digital documents and sharing them with government departments for completing their transactions quickly without the need to provide paper documents. Moreover, Digital-Vault aims to ensures availability of authentic digital documents, enable users to request and share them with service providers and ensures trust across all parties resulting in seamless digital experience. 

Digital-Vault enables users to request and share official digital documents to avail of services online. A key innovative factor is that Digital-Vault is part of an ecosystem which includes the National Digital Identity (UAE-PASS) and Digital Trusted Documents system (UAE-Verify). Blockchain technology provided Digital-Vault with the ability to ensure immutable records of transactions resulting in creating trust across service providers and users. Resulting in increasing the digital security of national data and reducing operational costs by reducing paper transactions. 

The Digital-Vault is part of an ecosystem which includes the National Digital Identity (UAE-PASS) and Digital Trusted Documents system (UAE-Verify).  The compounded effect of having verified digital identifies coupled with a means to access trusted digital documents while empowering service providers with the capability to verify documents; resulted in the complete elimination of the need for physical identity verification, the need for physical document citation, providing higher data quality and enabling service automation and an excellent experience. 

Digital Vault next target is to include new sectors such as insurance, education, health, and judicial. For examples one of the current targets is to have 45% increase in service provider’s adoption in the identified priority sectors by September 2023.
Novelty: The Digital-Vault is part of an ecosystem which includes the National Digital Identity (UAE-PASS) and Digital Trusted Documents system (UAE-Verify).  The compounded effect of having verified digital identifies coupled with a means to access trusted digital documents while empowering service providers with the capability to verify documents; resulted in the complete elimination of the need for physical identity verification, the need for physical document citation, providing higher data quality and enabling service automation and an excellent experience.
Impact & Results:
Digital Vault contributed to the effectiveness of the government workforce and operations by saving 21.9 million man-hours, and 3.5 billion dirhams annually spent on employees productivity time, printing documents, and cost of transportation and physical citations. 

Digital Vault environmental contribution and alignment with the SDs is measured by the elimination of the need to print 33.4 million documents and the need to physical citation visit to service centers. This saves around 4,000 tree per year, and reduces the CO2 emissions by 76,000 tons per year.  

Digital Vault economic impact and ROI were realized by exponential increase in digital document sharing ( 236,000+) across service providers and users. In the financial sector, over 80,000 new bank accounts were opened for customers online without visiting the bank physically.

Project/Initiative Name: Trusted Official Statistics for Good Governance and Evidence Based Decision Making in Vanuatu
Description:
The Parliament of Vanuatu has struggled to fulfil its legislative, budget, oversight, and representation functions due to limited capacity to utilise trusted statistics. The Vanuatu Bureau of Statistics addressed this capacity gap through targeted training with Members of Parliament (MPs) and parliamentary civil servants to improve policy making. This is the first focused effort in Vanuatu to introduce data for sustainable development monitoring to MPs with the aim of enhancing good governance. 

To address this capacity gap, the Vanuatu Bureau of Statistics (VBoS) developed the first ever Constituency Indicator Profiles in the country and trained MPs to use this information for the effective representation of their constituents. Additionally, VBoS delivered a training programme for PCS that centred on the development of evidence-based policy briefs for MPs. 

A first of its kind capacity development initiative in Vanuatu, spearheaded by VBoS, the innovation focuses on training and engagement with Parliament to promote data use for better decision-making. The programme was co-created with key stakeholders and a needs assessment identified the capacity gaps related to finding, interpreting, and using trusted statistic for decision-making. The assessment and collaborative design of the programme ensured the content of training sessions for MPs and PCS was sufficiently targeted to enable meaningful results.  

This is the first time that statisticians, policy analysts, expenditure analysts, Ministries and the Parliamentary Secretariat have worked together, improving the links within the Vanuatu National Statistical System, and enhancing coordination between data producers and users. Parliaments combined individual, organisational and system level capacity to use trusted statistics for decision-making was enhanced through targeted engagements and trainings. The VBoS also conducted a pre & post assessment to measure PCS progress. The results of this showed an increase in scores against 17 learning objectives
Novelty: This Innovation has supported inclusive governance by building capacity among different groups to use data, and forged closer ties between VBoS and decision-makers. It has provided a platform for stakeholders to share ideas on how to use data to improve public service delivery in a context where the interaction between parliament and the statistics office has been historically minimal.
Impact & Results: MPs and Parliamentary Civil Servants (PCS) have enhanced their skills to use data for decision-making. Their awareness about the importance of data and the statistical system has been significantly increased too. This has been shown by the interest to scale-up the initiative and VBoS being invited to speak in Parliament for the first time. Additionally, participating MPs used VBoS data to gazette a parliamentary bill increasing import duties on products related to noncommunicable diseases. The VBoS conducted a pre & post assessment to measure PCS progress.

Description:
The "EXTREMA Global" application aims at the personalized risk assessment of thermal stresses. Specifically, it is a smartphone application that uses weather data for a specific geographic location and provides personalized information to city residents and visitors about heat-related health threats. Athens was the first city in Greece to use this application. 

The Municipality of Athens is preparing and shielding itself through a series of innovative policies such as the "EXTREMA Global" application. It aims to inform the citizens and visitors of the city about of the high temperatures by providing them directions to reach the cool spots available in the city. 

The user enters the application environment and by entering information such as their location they can retrieve useful information such as where the cool spots around them are (air-conditioned rooms, parks, shady streets, fountains and more) and where there is water availability (e.g. public taps, springs, animal watering holes, etc.). 

Also, in 2022, the option for cool routes was added where the user can choose the coolest route to their reach its destination. Thus, a broader objective of the application concerns the information and education of the population about the most dangerous extreme weather phenomenon for our health and the provision of infrastructure for the protection of the population.
Novelty: The city of Athens was the first city in Greece to use this app as part of the Treasure project, which gave rise to the TreasureApp, which provides the user with a personalized heat risk assessment for a specific location and which was the beginning of the "EXTREMAGlobal". Other cities that followed and implemented "EXTREMA Global" were Paris, Milan, Rotterdam and London. The application is already provided in 4 languages making it particularly useful for foreign visitors as well.
Impact & Results: This specific innovation is an integral part of a wider framework for the development of digital innovations in the Municipality of Athens, in the context of data management for adaptation to climate change. This effort requires employees’ continuous training and upskilling, with programs to learn or improve their digital skills. In particular, the application has significantly improved the employees' knowledge of geospatial data processing (GIS) while contributing drastically to the smooth and constructive cooperation of the Municipality's departments for the exchange of know-how and data.

Project/Initiative Name: CAREER DAYS - REGULAR ANNUAL NATIONWIDE DIRECT JOB MATCHING SERVICES
Organisation: DYPA (Hellenic Public Employment Service former OAED)
Description:
DYPA, the Hellenic Public Employment Service organises the DYPA CAREER DAYS through the Service Unit for Medium & Large Enterprises aiming to promote direct contact between the business world and jobseekers. Beneficiaries of the action are businesses and jobseekers. It is a pioneering idea for the Public Employment Service because for the first time these events are now being carried out in a systematic way covering the entire country. 

DYPA aspires to effectively manage the acute social problem of unemployment, placing particular emphasis on strengthening active policies to combat it. The main challenge was to encounter and reverse the mistrust of employers and jobseekers, since, until now the Public Employment Service had been identified in the world’s consciousness with passive policies for unemployment.

The specific idea was carried out in the context of the strategic and operational objectives of DYPA and is an adaptation to the new needs of users, i.e. job seekers, who wish to come into direct contact with employers who wish to fill vacant positions. A big number of DYPA staff works in the planning and implementation of the Career Days institution, including staff from Central Administration (with a coordinatory and supervisory role) and staff at regional and local level, e.g. Counselors of DYPA’s local Employment Promotion Centres are mobilised for business outreach and provision of information to the public regarding the events.  

The specific initiative contributed to the improvement of DYPA’s results regarding the original objectives. In particular, it contributed to the improvement of users' satisfaction in their interactions with DYPA, and their trust levels in DYPA. To date both qualitative and quantitative results suggest success in meeting the objectives:  
- More than 300 companies participated. 
- More than 8000 jobs were offered (all educational levels of experience and expertise) 
- More than 7000 prospective job seekers attended. 
- The organisation of Career Days events has been evaluated as “excellent” by more than 90% of the participants.
Novelty:
The main advantages of DYPA’s Career Days are the broad promotion of jobs and the targeted attraction of interested parties in a process that has the characteristics of simultaneous, flexible and direct interpersonal contact. For the first time, business representatives communicate directly with job seekers, at no cost and with mutual benefit. 
Moreover, through the reformed Career Days, DYPA’s executive staff have been oriented towards a different mentality and a different way of work, exhibiting extroversion and flexibility.
Impact & Results:
Τo date, both qualitative and quantitative results suggest success in meeting the objectives, these results are described below:  
- More than 300 companies participated. 
- More than 8000 jobs were offered (all educational levels of experience and expertise) 
- More than 7000 prospective job seekers attended. 
- The organisation of Career Days events has been evaluated as “excellent” by more than 90% of the participants. 
- Confirmed recruitment data lead to the conclusion that within just 20 days after each event, more than 50 jobseekers are recruited, and at average at least 15-20% of the jobs that are actually offered are covered.

Organisation: Office of the Prime Minister, Government of Serbia
Description:
My High School Portal is dedicated to all services related to completing primary education and enrollment in secondary education. It enables parents to perform all activities paperless online, while previously they had to go to 7 different places. This year, it provided electronic service for 67,000 pupils and parents, who completed all essential steps of the final examination from primary education, apply for enrolment tests, access results and perform enrolment, entirely online. 

On the My High School Portal, the users (pupils and their parents) can check all the recorded data on individual pupils, and find complete statistical data on elementary and high school, as well as other information that can be helpful for adequate choice of future education. On a daily level, the following of the main current events is enabled in accordance with the Calendar of enrolment activities, which is also available on this Portal. 

My High School Portal is intended for all the pupils at the end of primary education who are enrolling in high school (approximately 67,000 pupils) and their parents, schools (1,817 schools in the system records, 1,266 of which are elementary, 62 elementary and secondary, and 489 secondary), as well as to the authorities in the field of education on the local and central levels. It also enables decisions and educational policies based on data on pupils’ success, process and coverage in transition between two education levels, which are its integral parts.

Services available on this Portal are classified according to various criteria, but the majority of the users fall in the category of Family/Family life, which offers a wide range of options for faster acquiring of all the necessary certificates and other documents required for family life and family members, from the child’s birth and acquiring of the birth certificate, enrolment in preschool institutions, primary and secondary education, finding employment, to retirement.
Novelty:
All the important pieces of information and access to digital services related to the final exam and pupils’ enrolment at high school have been provided in one place. Inspection of the test and submission of objections can be made electronically. 

The option to electronically submit and correct the wish lists for enrolment, instead of going to school to submit the wish list in paper form.  Enrolment to high school without the need to go to the school or to prior obtain the necessary documentation in paper form.  
Linking of the databases of different authorities (local self-government units, Ministry of Health – local healthcare centres, achieved success and the results in the final exam – primary schools, high schools, entry exams for talented pupils – specialized high school).

Project/Initiative Name: The prosvasis.dypa.gov.gr platform: A digital platform for persons with disabilities
Description:
The Public Employment Service (DYPA) in Greece has launched the PROSVASIS (https://prosvasis.dypa.gov.gr) platform, a new digital platform which is based on the latest WCAG 2.1 AAΑ international standard. With the use of this platform persons with disabilities and especially persons with visual and hearing difficulties, physical and cognitive/mental disabilities have access to the content of the DYPA website thus promoting inclusiveness for these vulnerable social groups. 

This innovation was developed in collaboration with the contractor company LIONCODE WEB & MOBILE SOLUTIONS. A technical report was drafted first to evaluate the digital accessibility of the content of the updated website www.oaed.gr (the former DYPA website) launched in April by the Speech and Accessibility Laboratory, Department of Informatics and Telecommunications, National and Kapodistrian University of Athens in order to identify possible designing and programming errors or errors in the content and to comply to the WCAG 2.0 AA accessibility standard.

The implementation was extended on two basic pillars: 
    1. Design pillar: the design of the platform (UI and UX) was carried out based on all, modern rules closely following the standard. 
    2. Operational pillar: all functionalities available on the platform serve persons with disabilities and many of them have been implemented in such a way to foresee and avert possible problems. 

The especially designed website, which is adapted to the needs of persons with disabilities, is a measure to overcome the barriers vulnerable social groups face such as access to information for all actions and services offered by DYPA as well as difficulty in dealing with the DYPA local services. Via the website and the features offered persons with disabilities can now navigate its content with ease.
Novelty:
The innovation’s added value is that it was designed according to the new WCAG 2.1 AAA accessibility standard and was implemented according to the latest internationally recognized rules. This makes it unique for both DYPA and the Public Administration in Greece. Moreover, it is an upgraded version of the current DYPA website with additional features for persons with disabilities. 

The most important result produced to this day is that it facilitates the access of persons with disabilities to information providing the following: 
- Customisation of font size/ colour combinations 
- Text instead of images, text to speech conversion 
- Text of Audio files/ video navigation via the keyboard/ mouse
- Simplified content layout for persons with visual impairments/ difficulties
Impact & Results: This specific action has multiple advantages for persons with disabilities and the whole society in general. Users are facilitated in locating information and navigating, they feel safe to fill in data and in general they refer to the internal help mechanism easily and quickly.

Organisation: Telecommunications and Digital Government Regulatory Authority
Description:
We had to adopt the “Online Learning and Work from Home (WFH)” policy to make sure that the educational process is not interrupted during the prolonged closure. Distance learning was the option to embrace by the majority of countries all over the world; the UAE was not an exception. The virtual learning with its verity learning style was adopted during 2019 and when Pandemic hit, TDRA was ready and had no issue with its employees grow and learning plan. 

At HR level, TDRA developed the HR Department capabilities to keep pace with development of telecommunications and digital government. It developed mechanisms/systems according to best practices and international standards (ISO) in HR management, reviewed its operations, and developed HR applied information systems. TDRA developed high priority policies/systems for professional talents, such as defining job roles through job descriptions for individuals/departments, determining job groups/families, developing continuous improvement mechanisms and career paths, evaluating career goals, developing talents identification methodologies within TDRA and evaluating their performance. 

TDRA developed training and empowerment plans that support building employees' technical and soft capabilities according to their tasks and initiatives linked to strategic alignment framework that defines TDRA's vision and supportive enablers. TDRA applies HR management guidelines, including specialized competencies framework, to manage HR related knowledge/skills, ensure efficient completion of tasks/responsibilities, and follow-up on development/implementation of projects/initiatives within the strategic plan. 

The learning styles adopted by TDRA varied including but limited to self-paced, instructor paced, blended, reading and articles based approach, interaction with experts, webinar and seminar.
Novelty:
TDRA launched a program to provide consultants in cooperation with a number of international bodies in the various areas, such as Gartner for technical projects, ITU for telecommunications and international policies/laws, RIPE-NCC for IP and domain names, Chartered Institute of Procurement and Supply (CIPS) for contracting and procurement, Chartered Institute of Personnel and Development(CIPD) and the Society for Human Resource Management (SHRM) for the development of the HR team capabilities, and Global Innovation Institute (GInI) for supporting the innovation team and processes. 

TDRA provided knowledge platforms for its employees such as TDRA Virtual Academy, FAHR's Al MAWRID, Udemy for Governments as educational tool rich in training materials in various fields, Tdreeb platform, LinkedIn training platform, as well as partners' platforms such as the ITU Academy.
Impact & Results: Helped to form special teams ie:the advisory committee and the Director General's Councilors committee TDRAenhanced programs to refine expertise and to interact with various groups and global cultures by attending international conferences,training,familiarizing them with ITU,participating in field teams and leading business within their specialties to ensure continuity of the UAE exceptional representation in ITU conferences and/or committees.

Description:
Sandbox is part of HCC’s effort to contribute to green transformation of the Greek economy according to the principles of free competition. There is no similar effort in any other EU country (or elsewhere) and its development and operation is expected to contribute significantly to the further development of the Greek entrepreneurship and the Greek market overall (green standard). The Sandbox is designed to benefit not only the entrepreneurial word but also consumers and the Greek Economy.

According to the Europe Sustainable Development Report 2021, Greece ranks 27th among 33 European Countries concerning the overall score that measures the total progress towards achieving all 17 SDGs (Europe Sustainable Development Report 2021).  Furthermore, it has been reported that competition law may have been a barrier to sustainability initiatives.

The HCC is an independent public Authority, responsible for fostering competition in markets and has recently refocused its activity, promoting innovation and Green Growth due to new market need for sustainable innovative development. Various surveys have reported that competition law may have been a barrier to sustainability initiatives. Therefore, HCC's proposal concerns a project that is innovative, and which will undergo a sui generis development and evaluation. Sandbox platform was officially launched on 3/10/2022, as a prompt response to market requirements for sustainable growth, to ensure an effective competitive environment by promoting sustainable initiatives.

The Sustainability Sandbox platform establishes a unique digital environment where businesses may submit their projects to be assessed.  Also, the Greek economy consists of many SMEs with low productivity, that makes hard from them to achieve economies of scale and thus the capability to invest in new (more sustainable) technologies. Hence, their collaboration on specific projects relating to Sustainable Development Goals, both in terms of funding/design and operation, may become necessary. 

The primary objectives of the HCC’s sandbox are: Promoting and enhancing competition, Avoidance of anti-competitive practices, Strengthening the culture for promoting innovative business proposals.
Novelty:
Competition law can be used as a sword and shield to achieve sustainability in a healthy competitive environment. Businesses could use sustainability as an excuse (known as green washing), to hide cartels. For that reason, HCC took the lead and created the tool that can access business projects that come as a result of agreements between competitors that may bear greater societal benefits within the remit of a competition authority.

Sandbox indicates what opportunities market participants have for making sustainability agreements, but also indicates what boundaries competition law sets in order to protect consumers better and to combat misleading practices and unfair competition. Undertakings can contact HCC through Sandbox, to discuss their ideas and find solutions.
Impact & Results: Sandbox is a great initiative for business to start operating in a more sustainable way and thus raise their corporate social responsibility.  It is expected to boost industry to experiment with new business formats and thus realize more quickly and efficiently its sustainability goals. It involves cooperation between competing undertakings that may lead to efficient changes in market structure to be accomplished and that is one more innovation that Sandbox wants to bring on to the market, by securing, at the same time that all these activities will be done under conditions and close supervision by HCC.

Project/Initiative Name: MediCapt: Scaling rights-based technology for forensic documentation and improving justice for survivors of sexual violence
Description:
MediCapt is an innovative digital application that enables clinicians to document forensic medical evidence in sexual violence cases, capture forensic photographs of injuries sustained, and store them securely. For survivors of sexual violence, timely and high-quality forensic examination, evidence collection, and documentation is a vital part of comprehensive care and access to justice. However, in many countries, including Kenya, the quality of medico-legal documentation is severely limited.

Sexual violence is an urgent crisis that affects millions worldwide, impacting people of all genders, ages, and sexual orientation. It is estimated that over 45% of women in Kenya will experience physical and/or sexual violence by an intimate partner or non-partner in their lifetime. Timely and high-quality forensic medical examination, evidence collection, and documentation are all part of comprehensive care for survivors of sexual violence and are crucial to ensuring survivors of SGBV can access justice. 

To help address persistent shortcomings in forensic documentation and to improve access to justice for survivors in Kenya, Physicians for Human Rights (PHR) – in close partnership with colleagues in the medical, law enforcement, and justice sectors in Kenya, the Democratic Republic of Congo, and beyond – collaboratively designed and developed MediCapt, an award-winning application that enables clinicians to document medical evidence of sexual violence cases on a digital medical certificate (such as the PRC form in Kenya), capture forensic photographs of the injuries sustained, and store this crucial evidence securely in the cloud. 

MediCapt offers a new way to collect and preserve forensic medical evidence to inform police investigations and prosecutions of alleged perpetrators of sexual violence crimes. As a new digital documentation platform, MediCapt sits at the intersection of mHealth technology and legal evidence documentation as a solution for yielding stronger evidence, preserving chain of custody, and improving data security and privacy in cases of sexual violence while minimizing the chances of loss, tampering, or theft of medical evidence.
Novelty:
MediCapt is a two-pronged system: first a mobile application that converts paper forensic medical certificates to a digital platform that prompts the collection of standardized forensic evidence; and second, a web-based online dashboard, currently in development, that will allow police officers, lawyers, and judges to securely access pertinent case information.  

MediCapt collects information digitally and facilitates the way health facilities report SGBV statistics to national health information systems. It is adaptable and ready to be used in different contexts. MediCapt’s form builder function can adapt the app’s documentation capability to collect information required in different legal jurisdictions and can accommodate local languages.
Impact & Results:
To help solidify our evidence base demonstrating the effectiveness of MediCapt, PHR completed a formal evaluation in Kenya to determine whether using the application results in clinicians collecting more complete and higher quality standard medical forensic documentation compared to using paper forms. 

The application’s use has been regarded as feasible and sustainable, both standardizing and improving the quality of documentation across sites. It is anticipated that this improvement will increase the likelihood of successful prosecutions, strengthening accountability for alleged perpetrators and improving survivors’ access to redress and justice.

Organisation: DYPA (Hellenic Public Employment Service former OAED)
Description:
DYPA, the Hellenic Public Employment Service (former OAED) has developed myDYPAapp, a mobile application that provides citizens with access to DYPA’s online services from their mobile phones. It was launched in February 2021, as an adaptation of DYPA’s services to new needs and modern technology that require the digitization of services, interaction and immediacy in serving citizens’ needs. myDYPAapp is innovative as it includes a wide range of user interaction possibilities with DYPA. 

On this basis, myDYPAapp mobile application has been created and launched in February 2021 providing citizens with access to DYPA’s electronic services from their mobile phones. Apple (iphone), Google (Android) and Huawei platforms are supported by the application. So the main objectives for myDYPAapp is to facilitate the access of users to DYPA’s services featuring the best possible provision of services and user experience to citizens.

The application is addressed to all citizens (for specific purposes), but mainly the job seekers. It is user friendly and familiar especially to the young people who use mobile phones extensively in the daily lives.  
Citizens can use myDYPAapp in order to:  
- Issue their unemployment card 
- Renew their unemployment card easily and quickly 
- Find the record/history of unemployment cards 
- Apply for official documents or certifications with the abilty to receive them in their personal e-mails in order to have immediate access to documents they need.

The services provided by myDYPAapp initially concerned e-services provided for PCs. It was deemed useful as well as necessary to redesign these e-services in order to become more user-friendly as well as suitable for mobile use.
Novelty: myDYPAapp is a unique innovation for public service standards because it allows many functions to be performed at the same time, e.g. submission of applications, renewal of unemployment cards, receipt of supporting documents, update of Cvs. The added value of the innovation is the fact that it makes the entire DYPA information system for the job seekers available in citizens’ mobile phones. Moreover, myDYPAapp provides the possility of connection to the data of taxisnet (the information system of the Hellenic Taxation Authorities) and access to a multitude of information and functions.
Impact & Results:
An indication of the positive response the application has received from the public is the increasing number of installations of the application in the last two years, as follows: 
- 30.000 installations in 2021,
- 59.000 installations in 2022 by now 
It is noted that in the context of DYPA’s planning, the implementation of a user satisfaction measurement mechanism is included, regarding the services provided to citizens/businesses. This mechanism measures - also user satisfaction regarding the functions of myDYPAapp.

Project/Initiative Name: Social Launderette-Social Hair Salon of Chania Municipality
Description:
