The structure of Social Laundrette-Hair Salon of Chania Municipality was one of the first to operate in Greece. It is developed to help vulnerable people and people with financial problems. It provides socially vulnerable citizens access to social facilities. It is a place where  people can wash and dry their clothes, take care of their personal hygiene, cut their hair, in a safe environment that promotes the sense of self-worth and belonging. 
 
In March 2021, the first Social Launderette in Greece began operating in the Municipality of Chania. Additionally in June 2022 in the same place, the Social Hair Salon began its operation. This new facility provides vulnerable citizens with access to a variety of hygiene services. Through the free provision of these services, the Municipality of Chania aims to improve the living conditions for socially vulnerable groups, as well as their quality of life, self- image and self-respect. 

The aim of the Social Launderette of the Municipality of Chania is to serve vulnerable citizens – residents of Chania who either have problems accessing basic amenities such as electricity or water, or do not have specific household appliances. Moreover, these free social services include shower facilities for personal hygiene needs, washing and dryer machines for cleaning and drying clothes, and a municipal clothing store that supplies free clothing, footwear, and linen.

In the same place, the Social Hair Salon has been operating since June 2022. The Social Hair Salon of the Municipality of Chania, a model service, is the first to be operated by a municipality in Crete and the second in Greece. Local hairdressers provide their services in this facility, voluntarily.
Novelty: We are in the middle of the first truly global energy crisis, which affects severely vulnerable people.  Social Laundrette- Hair Salon helps people that either have problems accessing basic amenities such as electricity or water or do not have certain household appliances. Gives them a chance to obtain their self-dignity in their lives. Beneficiaries are people with a low income as well as Roma groups refugees or immigrants and students of our city who face difficulties
Impact & Results:
The majority of the vulnerable citizens feel the Social Launderette-Hair Salon like their home. They have a stable assistant to trust and to give them advice.  Municipality’s aim is to strengthen and empower them and reduce the number of people that need social services.  
                                                                                  
Already the Social Launderette-Hair Salon has 2.173 uses of the services of washing and drying machines. The uses of social baths are 1.860 and 1.418 are the uses of clothing. Regarding the uses of Hair-Salon are 464.

Project/Initiative Name: New National Interoperability Framework of Public Services
Description:
The development of this innovation was deemed necessary in order to harmonize the country with national and European legislation and actions, so as to accelerate the country's digital transformation. It is designed with an innovative approach, as its main axes are co-creation, public participation and sustainability, with the aid of a dynamic governance model and the establishment of an interoperability community.

The project "Development of a new National Interoperability Framework of Public Services" is a flagship project of the Digital Transformation Bible 2020-2025, which will create the conditions for the design and development of effective civil-centric interoperable services and ICT projects that will incorporate one of the main guiding principles for the successful implementation of digital transformation, that of "interoperability-by-design".  

Its aim is to be a catalyst for the digital transformation of the country, while it is expected to contribute to the better coordination of the actions of all actors to achieve high interoperable maturity (governance), to create common reference points at all levels (institutional, organizational, semantic, technical), as well as to constitute a roadmap of actions to achieve integrated interoperable digital services in a practical and effective way. 

It is an innovative project, as it is a "living" organization including a continuous updating mechanism that ensures its sustainability. For the first time, it is accompanied by a governance model that creates the conditions for the operation of a participatory Interoperability Community, as well as an Implementation Guide for integrated interoperable services.
Novelty: It is an innovative project designed with co-creation procedures to take into account the experience of public and private sector experts on interoperability issues. Its main axes are co-creation, public participation and sustainability. For the first time, it is accompanied by a governance model which ensures its sustainability and creates the conditions for the operation of a participatory Interoperability Community. urthermore, an Implementation Guide for integrated interoperable services is available for all stakeholders to achieve the expected interoperability for the services they develop or evaluate.
Impact & Results:
The main result achieved so far is the activation of the involved members and the collection of their views and suggestions, which will lead to the more effective establishment of the new National Interoperability Framework.

Since we do not know the exact number of beneficiaries in the year of submission of the innovation, we estimate that this number will amount to about 1000 people in the public sector (IT departments ministries, supervised bodies dealing with IT projects, managing authorities, etc.).

Project/Initiative Name: Solar Panel Efficiency Per Building-Solar Map
Description:
Through this innovation which is used for first time in Greece, users can locate the building of interest and be informed aboutthe PV installation potential, the estimated energy production, the estimated installation cost and the estimated reduction of greenhouse gas emissions. Main purposeof the project is the promotion of photovoltaic systems in order to ensure energy autonomy and saving of energy resources from the residences of the Municipality, especially in that period of energy crisis.

The Municipality of Athens, in collaboration with the C40 Network and the construction company ARUP, developed a study regarding the possibility of installing photovoltaic systems on the roofs of buildings in Athens. As part of this study, data on the solar energy performance of each roof was produced in the form of an electronic map. More specifically, the innovation concerns the development of a map of all buildings (municipal and private) within the administrative boundaries of the Municipality of Athens that provides information on the potential of installing photovoltaic systems to all citizens for each building roof. 

Basic information derived from the GIS map, which was posted on the geoportal of the Municipality of Athens, is: 
a) the estimated energy production, b) the estimated installation cost, c) the estimated reduction in emissions, d) the resulting energy and money savings, etc. The broader goal of this innovation is to promote the installation ofphotovoltaic systems. Renewable Energy Sources (with photovoltaics currently accounting for the largest percentage of them), is one of the solutions proposed to reduce Greenhouse Gases, a tool to confront Climate Change. The goal by 2030 is the installation of photovoltaic systems with a total power of 28MWp in 10% of the city's buildings.
Novelty: The promotion of green energy through the installation of photovoltaics in 50 school complexes was an action of the Municipality of Athens before the Solar Map. With the implementation of the Solar Map, additional emphasis was given not only towards the active participation of citizens in green energy, but also on the utilization of every building of the municipal community, not just the Municipal ones.
Impact & Results: Easy and fast access to building solar potential data within administrative boundaries for the Municipality of Athens provided specialized knowledge and experience to users as it simplified the process of finding data (one of the most difficult and demanding processes in terms of solving a problem).

Project/Initiative Name: Modernizing the commencement and functioning of health-regulated establishments within the City of Athens – A co-operation with the Ministry of Culture and Sports
Description:
The City of Athens and the Ministry of Culture & Sports, realizing how time-consuming the commencement and functioning of health-regulated establishments was, introduced a new, modernized framework  for this procedure. The new framework has been implemented using the capabilities of new technologies, safeguarding the procedure transparency, minimizing the processing time and encouraging new investments within the City of Athens. Both stakeholders and public employees benefit from this framework. 

Through this innovation, public employees responsible for this procedure, minimize their decision-making time. The innovation has been technically supported by the Department of the City's Geospatial Data Management (Division of Strategic Planning, Resilience, Innovation and Documentation). The cutting-edge technology of Geographical Information Systems (GIS) has been used to digitize and map the areas protected by the Ministry of Culture and Sports.

Both static and dynamic maps have been created, representing features with appropriate symbols by category. Public employees of the City use QGIS, an open-source GIS Software, in order to handle the stakeholders’ requests for health-regulated establishments’ functioning and public space occupations. Printed documents and plans have been replaced by a GIS platform, where all geospatial and tabular data required have been loaded and displayed.

Through this framework, introduced for the first time in Greece, all archaeological sites / monuments protected and the restrictions / prohibitions applied to the surrounding areas supervised by the Ministry of Culture and Sports (Ephorate of Antiquities of the City of Athens/Service of Modern Monuments and Technical Works of Attica, East Central Greece and Cyclades) have been clearly defined. A reduce of the processing time by 50%, through a more transparent and user-friendly framework, has been achieved.
Novelty: The new framework has been implemented using GIS technology. For the first time a procedure within the Organization has been transformed through an in-house solution, based on its own resources. The GIS platform introduced has been incorporated into public employees’ daily framework, aiming in the development of additional  digital skills.
Impact & Results: According to the employees being responsible for handling relative requests, processing time has been reduced by approximately 50%. The workflow has been modernized safeguarding the transparency and quality of services provided. The data produced can be integrated into a health-regulated establishments management system accessible to all citizens.

Project/Initiative Name: A network-based Competence Centre for Sustainable and Innovative public procurement in Finland
Organisation: Motiva Oy, Coordinator of the Competence Centre in collaboration with 4 other public organizations
Description:
Government established a network-based Competence Centre for Sustainable and Innovative Public Procurement (KEINO) in 2018. The Centre provides, free of charge, E.g. advisory services to public procurers, promotes the strategic importance of procurement competence in public management, facilitates creation of buyers’ groups among procurers, disseminates information and good practices. network consists of five public organisations. 
 
KEINO is innovative because 1) it combines both innovative and sustainable aspects of procurement 2) Network model brings together diverse and complementary competencies for benefit of public procurers.  Network based KEINO was established to support the implementation of government’s policy objectives relating to sustainable and innovative public procurement. E.g. government’s objective is to increase the share of innovative public procurement to 10 percent. KEINO’s mission is to strengthen public sector organisations’ procurement capabilities, facilitate collaboration between public procurers and companies.  

The value added in the network-based models is to connect the diverse and complementary expertise from several public organisations to provide advisory services to central, regional and local government organisations and facilitate their collaboration. KEINO operates as a virtual organisation without fixed institutional structures with funding from the government of Finland.  

KEINO competence centre has been given a strong mandate to further develop the service portfolio, innovate new forms of services, strengthen its regional outreach (the regional ‘agents’ network), and further elaborate the measurement of outcomes. After an external evaluation in 2021, the present virtual mode of organisation based on partnership was considered superior to establishing a new permanent institution. KEINO looks forward to consolidating the partnership and streamlining activities towards measurable outcomes.
Novelty: The development of KEINO’s service portfolio would not have been possible with the expertise of one organisation only. Further, it would have been very difficult to recruit sufficiently diverse and high-quality experts to one organisation. Network based models allows experts to work in a flexible way with KEINO activities along with their regular activities.
Impact & Results:
The first three years of KEINO’s operations were evaluated in 2020. According the forward looking evalution the KEINO competence centre was assessed to be a nationally significant body for the development of sustainable and innovative in the promotion of procurement.
Further the evaluation stated that KEINO produces relevant and appropriate servicers for the benefit of public procurer’s. Network-based model combined with orientation for continous learning was seen to include many benefits and positive effects.

Organisation: Office of the Prime Minister, Government of Serbia
Description:
A single information system represents the foundation for the modernisation of the educational system. Analysis from the data obtained from this system will contribute to the creation of educational policies based on the needs of the labor market. 

Two main goals: 1) Building a unique single information system in education and integrating individual efforts and solutions, 2) Raising the information basis for the modernization of governance, that is, making decisions based on reliable and verified information. 

Single Information System of Education is intended for all employees of educational institutions, higher education institutions and institutions of school student and university student standards. Also, the data logged in the system is available to the public through the Open Data Portal https://opendata.mpn.gov.rs , where it is possible to inspect the data if I download the available data in a machine-readable format (Excel, Json).
Novelty:
1.	Integration with other information systems aims to exchange the data necessary for process realization, quality control and decision-making. Obtaining data from source systems contributes to data accuracy and reliability. Two-way communication between integrated systems contributes to real-time data updating and is not conditioned by human resources engagement. 
2.	The ability to monitor development indicators in education, the education system, meeting standards, the effects of education in the labour market, and therefore supporting the country's European integration.
Impact & Results:
The system enabled decisions to be made regarding graduates and students in the labor market in accordance with the assessments of the report. The financial institutions as well as the rationalisation of maternal expenses. Control of business processes by competent institutions. Faster processing of data and use of sites by users.

The reduction in the number of unemployed compared to the educational policies that are being tailored from the report. Improving the quality of equipment of institutions with equipment. Rationalization and better organization of the teaching staff. Reforming the education system and creating new models for the realization of teaching.

Organisation: Digital Transformation Department, Presidency of the Council of Ministers
Description:
“Sperimentazione Italia” is a horizontal sandbox that allows companies, universities, research bodies, university startups and spin-offs  from any sector (except excluded areas of application) to test pilot projects in the field of digitalisation and technological innovation, by derogating regulatory constraints. The main objective is to conduct live experiments in a controlled environment under the regulator’s supervision and collect data to promote future-proof regulations. 

“Sperimentazione Italia” is a horizontal sandbox that grants the possibility for companies, universities, research bodies, university startups and spin-offs  from any sector (except excluded areas of application), to test pilot projects in the field of digitalisation and technological innovation, derogating regulatory constraints.  The main objective is to conduct live experiments in a controlled environment under regulator’s supervision and collect quantitative and qualitative data regarding opportunities and risks of a particular technology application to promote future-proof regulations.

The horizontal approach is based on two main assumptions: 
- boundaries between industry sectors are blurring. Innovations may impact a wider range of law under different agencies.  
- innovation has an exponential pace and regulators should keep the pace. The horizontal approach does not set a specific law to derogate (vertical approach), but it could be seen as an “antenna” to capture innovation and generate specific sandboxes based on innovator requests.  

Additionally, Italy has created a Sperimentazione Italia Welcome Office to support innovators in their application process. The welcome office is a great opportunity to interact with innovators and intercept in the early stage potential law constraints.
Novelty:
“Sperimentazione Italia” was the first Italian sandbox in Italy, therefore it was unique in its kind at national level. Moreover some of its features are arousing interest in the international community.  
Please find below some of the peculiarities that are arousing more interest:  

Two of the peculiarities of Sperimentazione Italia are the horizontal approach of the sandbox and the clear timeline for proposing law amendments (90 days after the positive certification of the experimentation report). 

The horizontal approach is based on two main assumptions: 
- boundaries between industry sectors are blurring. Innovations may impact a wider range of law under different agencies.  
- innovation has an exponential pace and regulator should keep the pace.
Impact & Results:
The initiative is constantly evolving both in terms of openness and involvement of other institutions. Much has also been done to improve the innovator experience. For example, the Welcome Office set up a tool that aims to dialogue and support innovators in the preliminary phase of the application, also promoting a dialogue with the competent ministries. 

Two concrete examples of ongoing trials are: 
- GTT(Gruppo Torinese Trasporti): they are experimenting a self driving fleet of shuttles,  
- Yape: the company is experimenting a self-driving droid for more efficient and sustainable last mile deliveries.

Project/Initiative Name: Easy-to-Read: Enhancing Information Accessibility for People With Individual Needs in the Public Sector
Organisation: Ministry of Social Security and Labor of the Republic of Lithuania and programme "Create Lithuania"
Description:
Lithuanian Ministry of Social Security and Labor wants to make sure that all the residents get equal access to the needed information. People with intellectual disabilities, seniors with dementia and others experiencing difficulties to comprehend texts can feel included in the society with Easy-to-Read, a method that simplifies any type of information. 

Easy-to-Read (also known as easy language, EtR or E2R) is a method for creating accessible information. Many EU and other countries around the globe use it for written communication. For example, Sweden started using Easy-to-Read as early as 1970s. The method is based on rules and recommendations created by experts, such as linguists and social workers. These rules allow adapting an already existing text of any genre (e.g. legal documents, articles) into a more comprehensible version. It is also possible to create a completely new easy-to-read text – anything from a simple information leaflet to a novel. 

Lithuania is going to be the very first country globally that will have a national Easy-to-Read model combining both components:  
1) officially confirmed national guidelines for Lithuanian easy language: exclusively designed by Vilnius University linguists according to the state language.
2) legislation: minimal national requirements and recommendations to use Easy-to-Read in the public sector (websites, emails, social media).

Importantly, it is crucial for 35.000 Lithuanian residents with intellectual disabilities. Easy-to-Read in the public sector will enable them to make independent decisions and take more opportunities to participate in public life. E.g., it is already mandatory to use Easy-to-Read to adapt texts presenting political parties and candidates during the national election.
Novelty: Easy-to-Read includes people with intellectual disabilities (or other target groups, such as people with dementia or non-native speakers) in the text preparation process as validators. Validators are people who read the text, comment on its clarity and suggest changes. This stage cannot be skipped: not only does it enable the target group to do intellectual work, but it also allows to maintain the high text quality. Lithuania is the first country that will confirm legal regulations stating that it is mandatory to pay for validators' work, which is problematic in many countries.
Impact & Results:
The national legislation for Easy-to-Read in Lithuanian public institutions is already in the process of approval by the Parliament. National model of Easy-to-Read also consists of other interconnected components: 
Workshops for public sector employees (featuring the Minister of Social Security and Labor) on how to adapt texts in Easy-to-Read. Many of them come from Communication departments of their institution. Practical tool-website with advice has already been launched. Center for consultations (single window concept) is being created. 

International illustration database with a Slovenian NGO is going to be available by winter. Illustrators in Lithuania have already generated new pictures for the online database during workshops.

Project/Initiative Name: Selection and Training of Public Sector Executives: The open innovation project implemented by the EKDDA
Organisation: National Centre for Public Administration and Local Government (EKDDA)
Description:
The innovation concerns the new working method adopted by the EKDDA, regarding the development of an educational methodology for the training of the employees who will staff the newly established inter-ministerial executive branch. Under an open innovation network, for the first time, EKDDA worked towards exploring the required set of skills that an executive is required to possess which then formed the basis for the formulation of an appropriate training program for their development. 

EKDDA attempts to respond a) to the government's priority key policy axes, b) to the current needs of the public administration, as well as c) to the career prospects of civil servants, through a practical and applied orientation of the knowledge provided. The goal is for the successful completion of this training process to provide highly qualified people with the necessary prerequisites to serve the public interest from specialized positions. 

The first training programme will produce the first 315 executives of the Greek public administration, who will fill key positions in the country's ministries. We envision the expansion of this programme in order to enrich the pool of executives with skilled employees to staff public services across the country.
Novelty:
EKDDA's new training program constitutes a type of administrative process innovation (De Vries et al., 2016) that combines the use of new technologies and exploits internal and external information flows by expanding its organisational boundaries.

For the first time in the Greek public administration, the selection of the executives was based on a written examination conducted remotely (online), with supervision being carried out by combining human presence with modern technological tools using artificial intelligence. 

The proctoring software solution features among others, the following qualities: 
1. AI Proctoring with recording of user actions during the exam 
2. Live proctoring so that a proctor of the EKDDA can monitor the examinees in real time during the examination 
3. Screen feed of candidate so that the EKDDA proctor is informed in real time about the candidate's suspicious actions and to intervene.
Impact & Results:
Through the new training methodology, developed within an open innovation network, we anticipate the production of executives equipped with a portfolio of skills, have greater expertise, mobility and fluency in developing external relations and therefore demonstrate a low degree of identification with the specific organizational context of their employment. 

In addition, three new professional paths are created, as Law 4622/2019 provided for three new specialties within the newly created inter-ministerial branch: public policy analysts, lawmakers and digital policy analysts.

Description:
A mobile first solution for citizens to report service delivery issues whilst giving government representatives the ability to interact and educate citizens via the app. The Let’s Talk Mobile app that aims to connect all municipalities across South Africa with their respective communities to engage in service delivery and community problems.At the same time as local and provincial government are face an aging workforce and budget reductions, new consumer-grade expectations from citizens. 
 
Optimize data collection, consolidation and indexing to set up all process with the right information right away. Let's Talk App designed to deliver key basic-service and service delivery, challenges of yesterday’s, todays, and generations to come. For citizens Enabling positive active citizenship by ensuring that local communities voices are heard. People now have a mobile first way to engage with their local government and to ensure that service delivery issues are identified, shared and tracked. And that any vital information is shared to the public.  

Positive: the ability to provide better building services and tenant experiences. Negative: the pace of technology adoption is slightly faster than the pace of training workers to use and care for this new tech.
Novelty: Our technology uses hyperautomation, known by some as end-to-end process automation, intelligent automation or digital process automation, is a strategy to identify, analyze, and improve end-to-end business processes with technology. Without completely removing people from the equation, it is used to support citizens data driven decision making, helping to reduce instances of human error, improve operational efficiency, and enhance employee performance.
Impact & Results:
“It is through this app that citizens will be able to take photos of any type of government-related concern,for example,potholes or unfinished RDP houses, upload and send it to the service directory of the relevant government department”.Our observation since we first launched Let’s Talk Gov App version 1.16.0 is the amount of demand from the users to enable more basic services beyond reporting potholes and unfinished RDPs.

The impact was measured on the basis of travelling distance between the municipal offices and its resident's,looking at the walking distance of 45km-105km for them to apply,report an incident.

Project/Initiative Name: NHS Flu and COVID-19 Vaccines Chatbot
Organisation: Department of Health and Social Care
Description:
Survey figures spoke an alarming truth about a lack of awareness in the public of the risks of flu and Covid-19, especially when they were circulating at the same time, alongside the essential protection that the vaccines provide. This meant that there was a need for a tool that provided instant access to a trusted voice (the NHS) to cut through misinformation and confusion caused online and to encourage vaccination. This was the first conversational tool of it's kind developed for vaccines. 

Our mission was to offer vaccination education more seamlessly, encourage to get the jabs they were eligible for and offer them the chance to book. Our broader coronavirus search strategy had 3 specific strands: Own, Be There & Support. Vaccines fitted into the OWN strand focusing on maximising impressions for full coverage. OWN - These are the most important keywords where we want to maximise coverage on all searches possible.	The bid strategy was 95% impression share and key word groups included - Vaccines – Flu & Covid, NHS & PHE, Testing, Rules, Covid 19 App, and Test & Trace	 

Our concept was simple - Turn one search into a conversation. 
With search results being limited one result, yet citizen questions often being multitudinal, a ‘One Stop Vaccination Shop’ was the answer all the public’s questions about both the Flu and Covid-19 vaccines with the functionality to drive users to book their jab. This took form in the ‘NHS Winter Vaccines Virtual Helper ‘, a chatbot using the latest Google ad product development, allowed citizens to answer subsequent questions based off their original search and see additional information for each vaccine.  

The UK Government was the first government to market with Conversational Ads and first in the UK on the Search beta.
Novelty: Our concept was simple - Turn one search into a conversation. With search results being limited one result, yet citizen questions often being multitudinal, a ‘One Stop Vaccination Shop’ was the answer all the public’s questions about both the Flu and Covid-19 vaccines with the functionality to drive users to book their jab. This took form in the ‘NHS Winter Vaccines Virtual Helper ‘, a chatbot using the latest Google ad product development, allowed citizens to answer subsequent questions based off their original search and see additional information for each vaccine.
Impact & Results:
'-	The Search copy used was clear and concise, properly setting expectations for users that they were entering a messaging experience and simultaneously encouraging them to chat, resulting in a very high CTR of 7.3% (25% higher than average) 
-	The welcome message of the chatbot enticed people to engage in the conversation and delivered outstanding results as around 53% of users responded upon entering the messaging experience (2.6x higher than benchmark message conversion rate) 
-	On average, users sent 3.5 messages per conversation, representing a valuable exchange of critical information, and meaning conversations.

Organisation: Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs and Housing (MoMRAH)
Description:
Madinati platform helps entities to introduce accountabilities on the municipal waste generators in all sectors against the waste they produce. It helps in building a comprehensive knowledgebase with much higher accuracy to support strategical decision making. 
 
One of the main problems in MOMRAH  currently is the random disposal of waste, as well as the adoption of improper practices in its management, such as mixing waste, and its negative environmental and health effects on society. Madinati platform project aims to: 
-	Monitor, control and manage waste movement form its sources. 
-	Monitor vehicles, and equipment of the municipalities projects, and cities infrastructure projects. 
-	Registration of waste producers in smart electronic system. 
-	Unifying the requirements of tracking service providers according to the highest technical standards. 
-	Ensure the quality of service, and compliance through electronic contract documentation. 

Madinati platform system includes tracking waste from the generation points to the approved final disposal points during the waste life cycle, and includes the process of transportation and tracking of waste vehicles using tracking and monitoring devices. It also includes monitoring and tracking the vehicles of all Municipalities projects operating in cities in order to serve as a supportive and complementary tool for the Municipalities’s work strategy and plans.
Novelty:
Madinati platform helps entities to introduce accountabilities on the municipal waste generators in all sectors against the waste they produce and make sure they comply to the governmental regulations through applying and involving them in the following functions: 
-E- Registration of waste producers and transporters. 
-Documentation of e-contract between waste producer and transporter 
-Tracking for waste management plans and project equipment movement inside the cities 
-Tracking system and report analysis for entering illegal zones. 

The e-contract solution is the right tool to gain feasibility and full tractability of what actually happens on ground 24/7. 
All system outputs are reflected on live tracking enterprise dashboard fixed on a technical control room giving an immediate, sustainable reports and statistics satisfying the requirements of decision-makers.
Impact & Results:
- Waste Transporter registration: Where the procedures for registering carriers have been automated, and service providers have been increasing the transfer of waste to 100 carriers. 
- Control oversight of the equipment of the secretariat projects: Through the use of IoT technology, oversight of contractual and non-contractual equipment has been increased and its behavior has been monitored to reduce the impact of random throwing in the region and visual distortion, as 8636 violations have been detected so far and covers the annual platform operating costs in addition to 18% 
- Controlling waste transport contracts: The electronic contracting procedures between the commercial license holder and the waste carrier have been automated. Madinati platform, which led to the development of this sector and made it easier for citizens to find a service provider with high efficiency and lower price

Project/Initiative Name: Quality management system (QMS) in public sector in Croatia: Digital repository and improvement tool for public administration business processes and quality management (self)assessment (eSUK)
Organisation: Ministry of Justice and Public Administration
Description:
In order to increase the efficiency and quality of the public administration (ministries, local self-government and agencies) in Croatia, e SUK, a digital repository of public administration processes and quality management (self)assessment tool was developed. For the first time, public administration bodies are provided with methodology and tools for optimizing and standardizing business processes and introducing a quality management system. 

The established IT system e SUK provides the possibility of storing and processing data related to business processes and quality management in public administration bodies. It is a unique open source tool in which it is possible to map business processes in BPMN 2.0 tool (open source programme Camunda), and review them for the purpose of optimizing or even abandoning certain processes in order to reduce the administrative burden and achieve more effective public administration. Simplification and elimination of unnecessary processes is also necessary for the efficient digitalization of public administration processes.  

All public administration bodies in Croatia can use the IT system, after completing training that qualifies them to work on processes using the IT system's training environment. Thus, the system is currently used by 124 public administration bodies: 20 agencies, 16 ministries, 14 state administrative organizations, 16 counties, 26 cities and 32 municipalities.
Novelty:
Quality management system in Croatian public administration (PA) is innovation by itself as new paradigm to move from bureaucracy to client oriented PA. There was no methodological and digital approach in this filed in PA before. In general, innovation is joint knowledge and change management (digital) system for all PA bodies, as well as mapping, optimization and standardization of business processes, particularly those affecting citizens.  

For the first time, government officials are trained to upload and map processes they work on in IT system. Also, participating in online training they are learning how to optimise and standardise processes of their PA body.
Impact & Results:
Starting with training in April 2022, so far 300 trainees (government officials) have completed training in process mapping, optimization and standardization using training environment of the digital repository developed for the training purpose, with examples of processes defined for training.  

Currently, in digital repository are 1347 mapped real processes (and their subprocesses) in BPMN 2.0 modeling standard of 124 public administration bodies. Government officials are still undergo training in the terms of ESF project Implementing quality management system in Croatian PA by the end of March 2023.

Project/Initiative Name: Electronic Appointments with the Services of the Municipality of Palaio Faliro
Description:
The innovation is an electronic service that contributes to the immediate service of the citizen/business without physical presence, reduction of bureaucracy, targeted management of requests. Via the online address https://palaiofaliro.gr/egwebapps/rantevou-app/rantevou/ the citizen  makes an appointment with the service he wishes on the day and time it serves him and can be informed about the required supporting documents. Services organize and complete their work avoiding inconvenience. 

This innovation enables the citizen to schedule/make electronic appointments with the services of the Municipality through the "Electronic Appointments" application from the website of the municipality. Quickly and easily with one click from his own equipment wherever he is without physical presence, avoiding inconvenience and crowding. 

This electronic service contributes to the reduction of bureaucracy, to the simplification of procedures, to the targeted management of requests and to the digital transformation of the municipality. This specific initiative is one of the implementable actions of the strategic planning of the Municipality's Operational Program 2019-2023 in the Pillar "IMPROVEMENT OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE AND FINANCIAL CAPACITY OF THE MUNICIPALITY" and in particular in the Measure: Upgrading of e-government services provided - Digital transformation and in the Goal: Improvement of electronic services provided to citizens.  

The aim of the innovation is to provide quality services utilizing modern digital technology and to gradually implement the digital transformation of the municipality so that it is able to respond to the new data of the time and the new needs of society, improving and developing the services provided and to achieve the modernization of the municipality by adopting innovative actions.
Novelty:
The conditions created by the pandemic led the municipality to accelerate the  implementation work. 

Of course, there were reports and requests from citizens and the issue of the time they had to allocate in order to go to the municipality in order to be granted a certificate or certificate had been strongly raised. One of the problems they encountered was waiting, which created dissatisfaction and irritation. Another problem was the lack of complete information on the required supporting documents that they had to present to the municipality service.
Impact & Results:
The application facilitates citizens in the procedures of their requests to municipal services, offers immediate and easy service without physical presence, offers better quality services to everyone, reduces bureaucracy, simplifies the process of communicating with municipal services and contributes to the digital transformation of the municipality 
Particularly: 
•	The number of citizens waiting decreased by 15% and number of phone calls by 10% 
•	Saving employees' time by 15% and utilizing it for internal work

Organisation: K8 Institut fuer strategische Aesthetik gGmbH
Description:
A data literacy toolkit based on a transformation design model as well as the MyData Global approach to data literacy. Co-developed with members of the Saarland Ministry for the Economy, Innovation, Digitalization and Energy, it provides a low-threshold introduction to data literacy as cross-sectoral concern and key area of public sector innovation. It is part of an ongoing co-creative data strategy process exploring the role of data / data governance in the dynamics of regional transformation. 

This is an online toolkit based on K8's transformation-design approach that explores the role of data in five complenentary fields of action. The objectives are to leverage the cooperation with MyData Global (through the MyData Saar hub) to facilitate engagement with a simple-yet-comprehensive approach to data-driven transformation. 

The ones to direct benefit would be members of the state government - from MWIDE (ministry) as well as the State Office for Surveying, Geoinformation and Land Development (which was encouraged to co-develop an Interreg funding proposal focusing on the role of geodata literacy in regional transformation processes, with a particular focus on the use of digital twins), everyone involved in the research and design process (as that process built knowledge and capacity to more effectively engage with the dynamics of public sector innovation).
Novelty: First-time cooperation to build an interactive education and onboarding tool to build a design conversation on data-driven transformation.
Impact & Results:
'- highlighted he role of data literacy in the regional transformation process 
- facilitated the co-development of follow-up applied research and innovation projects, linking regional public sector actors to a wide range of like-minded public sector actors in other parts of Europe (outcome: draft proposal for Interreg NWE to be submitted early 2023) 
- co-shaped use case development for public sector ai deployment (use case: ai for a dynamic solar cataster, full proposal submitted to federal funding agency in 2022)

Project/Initiative Name: Sustainable Waste Management Guide of the Municipality of Central Corfu and Diapontia Islands
Organisation: Municipality of Central Corfu and Diapontia Islands
Description:
The recycle.corfu.gr is the 1st municipal website in the history of the island of Corfu which is dedicated exclusively to issues related to sustainable waste management as an easy-to-use online guide, it aims to be the main tool of valid online information and awareness for the citizens of our Municipality and help them participate more effectively at the critical stage of "Separation of Waste at Source" which will increase the quality and quantity of the separately collected waste streams. 

The recycle.corfu.gr is the first municipal website in the history of the island of Corfu dedicated exclusively to issues related to sustainable waste management (prevention, reuse, recycling with "Sorting at Source") but also to the available solutions offered for separate collection of the different waste streams in the Municipality of Central Corfu and Diapontia Islands.  The recycle.corfu.gr was created in the form of an easy-to-use online guide in March 2021, with the aim of being the essential digital "tool" to inform and raise awareness among citizens, offering them updated and valid information.

Although the main purpose of recycle.corfu.gr is to be the main digital "tool" to inform and raise awareness among citizens about the sustainable management of waste in the Municipality of Central Corfu and Diapontia Islands, at the same time it is also the place of publicity for the overall efforts that paid by the Municipality for the reduction of its environmental footprint and its gradual transformation into a "green", "smart" and "digital" Municipality, which are listed in the form of informative articles or Press Releases in the "Articles" field.
Novelty: The recycle.corfu.gr is the first municipal website in the history of the island of Corfu dedicated exclusively to issues related to sustainable waste management (prevention, reuse, recycling with "Sorting at Source") but also to the available solutions that are offered for separate collection of the different waste streams in the Municipality of Central Corfu and Diapontia Islands.
Impact & Results: The recycle.corfu.gr from March 2021 until the application submission period, has more than 16,000 views as total traffic of all of its web pages.  Recycle.corfu.gr has contributed significantly to this success and has already successfully responded to its main role and helped the citizens to change their behavior and hope to continue the same.

Organisation: e-Government Center for Social Security Services (IDIKA SA)
Description:
As part of a comprehensive strategy for the Digital Transformation of Health services in Greece, IDIKA which is a supervised body of the Ministry of Digital Governance, developed the myHealth mobile application. Via myHealth mobile app, citizens have access to their health data. 

MyHealth app introduces a set of modern health services to Greek citizens, reduces bureaucracy, ensures transparency, and lays the foundations for the development of new health and social security digital services. ΜyHealth mobile app is an innovation as it is the first public mobile application in Greece, as an integrated service to the citizen, facilitating access to health services by providing ease of use and transparency and laying the foundations for the development of new digital health and social security services. 

Citizens can receive information through push notifications about the next available medicine prescription and diagnostic tests referral, while they have access to information regarding their diagnosis and medication through the prescription. Parents and guardians have access to children's digital prescriptions and their regular vaccination schedule. It also provides reliable and valuable information such as notifications for preventive medical tests and medical appointments. 

It is the first time that citizens have access to their own health data via their mobile device and our vision is to enrich further these data, so that eventually myHealth will evolve so that citizens can access their entire electronic health record via their mobile device.
Novelty:
This is the first time in our country that the citizens have access via a mobile application to their health data and they can 
- manage their Drug Prescriptions
- manage their Diagnostic Tests Referrals
- be facilitated in their interface with healthcare providers
- receive important information and push notification concerning their prescriptions, vaccinations and medical appointments.

MyHealth mobile application has been implemented in such a way that it can be easily enriched by other sources of health data, through appropriate interoperability, so that citizens can gradually access more information, and ultimately via myHealth citizens can access their entire individual electronic health record.
Impact & Results:
MyHealth provides access to citizens to their health data and specifically to their drug prescriptions, diagnostic tests referrals, medical certificates and their children's vaccinations. 

Citizens receive as well significant notifications (push notifications) about the about the expiration of their drug prescriptions and their diagnostic tests referrals and the  schedullng of their children's vaccinations and therefore myhealth mobile app is an important tool for them to better managing their health.

Description:
Integrated Municipality Governance Model offers a comprehensive scope and observable criteria to implement and develop good governance principles at municipalities. It is an implementation model for continues development  of local good governance. There is not any other model in similar fashion with comprehensive scope and practical steps. The closest model was developed by Argüden Governance Academy (Municipal Governance Scorecard). 
 
Municipalities play important roles in furthering sustainable development and quality of life for various social groups. To optimize their roles and outputs, municipalities need to enact good governance in holistic manner at their overall structures and governing stages. In general, municipalities are obliged to function and serve in accordance with good governance principles due domestic legislations. However, the implementation of these principles are mostly patchy and dependent on the will of municipal leaders and actors. The Model provides an integrated approach to implement good governance culture and principles at different governing stages and steps. It also provides specific mechanisms and tools to evaluate and continuously improve the quality of good governance at municipalities.
Novelty: The Academy created a Model and implement two pilot municipalities to operationalize the twelve good governance principles of the Council of Europe at every stage of municipal governing processes. This Model designed first time in the world which is innovative because it provides an integrated approach to implement and continuously improve the quality of institutional good governance mechanisms. It is consisted of mechanisms, standards and tools for the use of decision makers and practitioners at different levels in order to apply and receive the European Label of Governance Excellence.
Impact & Results:
A report analysis of the current municipal governing mode and progress points for improvement. Based on objective data observation, surveys and interviews with the relevant actors.  

An integrated municipal governance model incorporating mechanisms, tools, standards and measurement criteria are developed according to the principles and legislative framework.

Project/Initiative Name: Governance structure of sustainable development in Romania and innovative sustainable development initiatives
Organisation: Department for Sustainable Development, Government of Romania
Description:
The Department of Sustainable Development (DSD), created in 2017 at the Centre of the Romanian Government, in charge of coordinating the implementation of Romania’s National Sustainable Development Strategy (ROSDS) 2030, has put in place an ambitious institutional framework and instruments for implementing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Principles for policy coherence for sustainable development (PCSD) are guiding institutional and policy making processes.

A National Action Plan (NAP) for the implementation of the Strategy was adopted in 2022.  The actions included in the NAP are mainly aimed at inter-institutional coordination, so that line ministries and other institutions are actively involved in the implementation of the ROSDS. 

DSD has undertaken concrete actions for setting up and operationalizing the Romanian institutional framework that fosters a whole-of-government approach for sustainable development, including monitoring and evaluation. The key institution is the Interdepartmental Committee for Sustainable Development, ICSD, which is a consultative body that comprises ministers, headed by the Prime Minister and ensures, among others, that the principles and objectives of sustainable development are included in all national policies, programs, plans and strategies.  

These institutional and strategical developments are complemented by a series of instruments that help horizontal and vertical implementation: 
- a postgraduate training and continuous professional development program "Public Administration for Sustainable Development"; 
- the Romanian Sustainability Code, a virtual platform for transparent reporting of the activity of a certain company,
- a Center of Excellence for Public Administration in the field of Sustainable Development will be put in place until 2025, with EU funds.
Novelty:
For the implementation of ROSDS, the DSD began the professionalization of 150 civil servants from the central public institutions in the field of sustainable development. Until 2026, 2000 persons more, 80% from local level, will be trained as sustainable development experts.  

At the end of a process which started in 2018, in May 2022 DSD managed to update the specific legislative framework so that public institutions can include in organizational charts and adapt job descriptions with duties for "sustainable development expert".

Description:
Today's complex problems challenge us to organize differently. We must collaborate to make sense of our interconnected problems and envision new solutions. The Local Innovators Program is redefining participatory innovation offering local government leaders and social entrepreneurs a powerful 6-month experience in which, through a systemic innovation methodology they co-design and develop with the community a solution to a high-priori  problem in their cities. 

 RIL is promoting democratic practices by creating a new participatory governance model at the local level through which multiple actors who are impacting a problem or are being impacted by the problem work collaboratively to design and implement collective change initiatives that address structural problems in a holistic manner.

To strengthen democracy in a world that is increasingly interconnected, we need to develop holistic solutions that address these interdependent challenges, be it vertical (within the sector) or horizontal (across several sectors). Furthermore, even though civil society views the local government as a critical strategic partner for scaling up and institutionalizing successful solutions, many civil society organizations and community leaders typically engage the government last or do not engage it at all due to a lack of trust.
Novelty:
The Local Innovators Program’s methodology combines human-centered design, systems thinking, and participative governance models. The Program builds on the Double Diamond Framework by adapting it to collaborative processes and by embedding systems change tools. 

Teams go through an iterative process of eight stages: (1) Develop systems thinking and leadership skills with social equity and justice lens; (2) Create the Design Team; (3) Develop a deep understanding of the problem by conducting fieldwork and participatory validation to better comprehend the different dimensions of the problem; (4) Define the areas of intervention and the shared long-term goal, and develop a strategy roadmap; (5) Ideate and prioritize solutions and approaches; (6) Prototype ideas; (7) Develop action plan; (8) Present and implement “+I project”
Impact & Results: In its eight-year trajectory, the organization has supported more than 280 local governments globally,  working to strengthen democratic practices through collaborative processes, management, network development, innovation, and the development of knowledge tools. More than 400 local government leaders and social innovators took part in past editions.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital Maturity Index for local governments
Description:
Digital transformation is a long-term journey that involves complex changes. One of the challenges is how we evaluate the progress. We are inspired by the quote, "If you cannot measure it, you cannot improve it". Most of Catalonia's municipalities cannot do a digital assessment. The Digital Maturity Index provides an evaluation turnkey solution for local governments. It is based on open data, and it has a clear focus on public value impact. 

The Open Government of Catalonia (AOC) is a public agency with the mission to foster the digital transformation of Catalonia's public sector, which includes more than 2,000 authorities: local, regional, universities, and agencies. It provides e-government services about shared digital platforms, interoperability, digital identity, analytics, and change management. AOC has collaborative governance, a citizen-centric strategy, and a turnkey approach to provide technology, support, data privacy assistance, legal advice, training, and best practices. 

Digital transformation is a long-term journey involving complex technology, processes, organization, and culture changes. One of the key challenges is how we evaluate the public value it generates. Most of Catalonia's municipalities are small and have many limitations in assessing their digitization process on their own. The Digital Maturity Index (DMI) for local governments provides a turnkey solution to evaluate their digital maturity. It is based on open data, and it has a clear focus on public value impact. DMI is a composite of 30 key indicators that assess the degree of digital transformation of 988 local authorities: 947 municipalities and 41 counties.

DMI has become a powerful tool in Catalonia to build a data-driven culture for local governments. It provides a dashboard to assess the overall digital transformation in a territory to deliver insights for policymakers, analysts, and stakeholders. And a balanced scorecard the key indicator for a specific local government to support self-evaluation and better decisions of elected and government officials.
Novelty:
Developing an instrument to assess the degree of digital maturity of local governments based primarily on open data is a unique and innovative initiative, both nationally and internationally. Several digital government indexes are applied at the federal or state government level, which does not fit for conducting assessments at the local level. Moreover, most composite indexes are based on statistical data or surveys. 

DMI is also innovative because it delivers a comprehensive set of visual dashboards using business intelligence tools that are addressed to the different types of users.
Impact & Results:
The achievements are the following: 
- DMI is currently widely used by the Catalan government (state), provincial governments, county governments, and municipalities. The number of visits is 5.000 for the current edition. 
- DMI supports the evidence of improvements. Some of them are: 
    - The DMI has increased by 8 points since previous year.  
    - 76%  of local governments have improved from previous year 
    - The usage of e-Services by citizens has multiplied by three, mainly because of the pandemic. 
- Satisfaction of using digital services has increased 2 points from previous years. We use the Net Promoter Score methodology adapted to the public sector. 
- AOC provides a consulting service to local governments to advise and support their digital transformation initiatives. Since the last years, the number of requests has increased by 10% to improve their DMI.

Description:
Bangladesh identified its first COVID positive case on 8th March 2020. Being a densely populated country with inadequate healthcare infrastructure, it was a mammoth task to provide treatment to the daily increasing number of COVID patients keeping them in the hospital while maintaining social distance which could also lead to rapid community level transmission.

This initiative started it’s journey in June 2020 for covid-19 positive patients.  
1. Medical assessment, Doctor Consultancy, Counselling and treatment 
2. Medical follow-up, counselling, treatment facility 
3. Health information Service 
4. Service through incoming call 
5. Emergency service coordination (Ambulance info, hospital bed info, food & medicine buying support, funeral support etc.) 

Patients were treated depending on 3 types of treatment plans. 
-Treatment Plan A: Patients with Mild Symptoms 
-Treatment Plan B: Patients with Medium Symptoms 
-Treatment Plan C: Patients with Severe Symptoms
Novelty:
The most innovative catch of Covid-19 Telehealth Center was that it was the quickest and most cost effective possible solution available at that critical pandemic situation.  

The objective of this initiative was to ensure effective and quick healthcare for citizens in every corner of the country during this crisis situation irrespective of their economic/ social status or physical location (urban or rural) so that no one is deprived from the basic healthcare right.
Impact & Results: Till 30 November 2021, COVID-19 Telehealth Center has provided 15,61,068 telehealth services to about 7,04,890 patients involving 50 Doctors and 30 Health Information officers.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital Vaults for Citizens, Businesses and Public Administration
Description:
The newly founded Greek Single Digital Gateway "gov.gr" is already extending its operation by enabling citizens and businesses to: 
i) submit e-applications for public services that are not offered end-to-end online, but are processed by a paper-based back office, as well as 
ii) receive the output of the service in a personal "inbox". 
The main benefit is an instant, seamless and user-friendly transformation of any public service to e-service, regardless of the way the procedure is processed by the competent authority.  

The vaults are an incremental, frugal innovation not only seen as a quick-win but also undergoing continuous improvement with new functionalities. They are a well-documented implementation of agile methodology, able to be a springboard for more innovations. The implementation reflects, for the first time in the organization, a design logic that captures the applications generated as process-agnostic, so they can be interoperable both between vaults and between vaults and third-party Information Systems (IS).

The PA vaults are a digital environment for receiving and managing digital documents and citizens' requests sent via gov.gr. They are uniquely identified by code and name, provide authorized public servants with role-based authenticated access, interoperate with other IS through an application programming interface, and offer a comprehensive menu of management options. 

This new system of transactions through vaults offers immediacy, accuracy, and transparency of the whole process. Citizens and businesses can access seamless e-services and follow their request's progress. At the same time, the benefits for the PA are particularly significant: bureaucracy is combated horizontally and drastically and the transfer of hard-copied, manually-signed documents is significantly reduced.
Novelty:
1. Are a frugal way of reducing the number of the paper-based, time-consuming and costly administrative procedures to a great degree. 
2. Reflect a new era in the public services design and provision inside the Ministry. This tool was more than a custom-made ICT project. It was a concept intended to facilitate online access to administrative procedures and cater to the operational needs of any public authority, embracing the Agile Methodology, modular architecture, and digital governance principles (such as interoperability by design). 
3. Are an innovation developed in-house, supported by a committed, centralized cross-departmental team. 
The innovative core of the project is a turn to sustainable, transversal, and yet customizable tools
Impact & Results:
Till now, have activated and operate their digital vaults: 
- 8.104.167 citizens, 
- 150 municipalities, 
- 1.000 citizens' service centers, 
- 1.000 Departments of the Hellenic Police, 
- 15.000 Schools and Universities. 
As of September 2022, more than 62 million e-documents have been exchanged via the vaults (mainly about covid19).

Organisation: Ministry of Human Resources and Emiratisation
Description:
The insurance system for the rights of workers registered with the Ministry was launched as an alternative and optional system to the bank guarantee to provide a decent life for workers, ensure market stability and enhance the country’s position with global competitiveness indicators Ensuring the provision of entitlements such as end-of-service gratuity, leave allowance, unpaid salaries, economy-class return flight tickets to the homeland, and compensation for work injuries and illnesses.

Based on global digital changes, government trends in the Emirates and international requirements related to workers' rights and the need to link them to updated systems and policies, and in view of the rapid growth of the economy in which there are about 350 thousand establishments and 5.47 million workers registered in the Ministry. The importance of providing a system to protect workers in the private sector, quick to respond to urgent changes, has emerged. 

The insurance product covers any financial labor entitlements contained in Federal Law No. 33 of 2021 regarding the organization of labor relations and its amendments and the decisions issued to implement its provisions for the workers of establishments. It covers the obligations of recruitment offices for domestic workers in the event of a breach of the obligations contained in Federal Law No. 10 of 2017 regarding domestic workers and its executive regulations.

The purchase of the insurance product includes the provision of consultancy services and labor claims, which is a free service that can be obtained by calling the number 04/6659999 and working hours are from 8 am to 8 pm on all days of the week except for Fridays and public holidays, by providing a reception service for workers’ claims and a service hot line.

All awareness, promotion and marketing campaigns are planned and implemented with the aim of reducing labor disputes housing, food, or any other needs that the ministry deems appropriate are provided in case the establishments falter and are unable to pay the workers' dues, even if there is no labor insurance.
Novelty:
The initiative is considered innovative at the level of the state and the region in protecting the rights of workers, as it is unique in enabling workers to claim their rights and provide insurance coverage for them, as the system covers the workers working in the state and registered in the ministry’s database.

On the regional level, it is considered a unique initiative and the first of its kind in terms of having insurance coverage to protect the rights of workers, which called for the free zones in the country, the Gulf states and the countries of the region to learn about the experience and benefit from it, with the praise of the insurance companies in the United States of America, which reviewed the experience and stated that it is unique at the level of the scientist.
Impact & Results:
A bank with a value of 3000 dirhams, which will inject approximately 14 billion dirhams of bank guarantees into the state’s labor market gradually, which constitutes a qualitative leap in reducing labor costs, reducing burdens on employers and the positive impact in reducing complaints and labor strikes

A team specialized in strategy and performance measures the results by following up on the results with a central system of government performance and presents them to the leadership periodically and is subject to internal and external audits in accordance with operating procedures, monitors complaints, and follows up on the views of those concerned. The following results were recorded:
- 17.11 rate of work stoppages per 1000 establishments in 2021 compared to 18.2 in 2019
- A decrease in the number of labor complaints received by the Ministry in 2021, reaching 210,618 compared to 2020, which recorded 308,343
- 9.7 million insurance certificates (8.6 million for establishment workers and 1.1 million certificates for domestic workers)
- 279,000 total number of heads of families who benefited from insurance

Project/Initiative Name: Self-guided methodology for textual simplification and accessible communication for government agencies
Organisation: SEGER - Management and Human Resources Secretariat
Description:
The innovation goes from the collaborative creation and testing of the framework, to the possibility to scale up with little intervention from the small project team, to the expansion with Design in accessible communication. 

The solution developed was the construction and implementation of a methodology based on virtual workshops for engagement, realization and strengthening of textual simplification actions and accessible communication in the government of ES. The framework brings a comprehensive model (with a comprehensive view of plain language), focused on results (achieving strategic objectives), scalable (replicable) and focused on innovation actions in public service. 

The project incorporated three lines of action associated with specific objectives for each of the gaps. The actions involved several agents, under the perspective that the communicational process involves the State and civil society. To incorporate the use of simple language in the digital transformation initiative led by Subges for the entire government, the team created the "Workshop on Service Simplification" and a manual for the servers that enter the information on the portal of services provided by the government to citizens. 

With a duration of 10 hours, 15 civil servants were trained at the same time as they reformulated the public notice. Linked to the workshop, a second stage consisted of visual simplification, focusing on content hierarchy and a more functional and attractive design. The framework consisting of the three workshops and the manual, under construction, generates a model for facilitating and increasing understanding of government communication with incremental and iterative experiences, which are being replicated and scaled in a self-instructional manner.
Novelty:
The plain language approach in the country is already being implemented in a few federated entities, but still creating consistency. The differential of the methodology developed is the self-instructional and replicable framework with autonomy by any agency that wishes to do so. It was built collaboratively, tested, and implemented in two agencies and is based on a hands on logic - training while solving the problem - through workshops and a manual. 

It is based on the bottom up construction by the team and the transfer of technology and training of multipliers during the process, and not on the dependence on a team of experts to gain scale in the agencies.
Impact & Results:
Simplification of all the Inoves Award processes, reducing doubts by more than 60% (2020 - 2022) and impacting 254 projects and 1453 integrants; 

Simplification of 2 editais and automatic replication for 11 other editais of the Secretariat of Culture; Interest from top management in creating the State Policy for Simple Language (bottom up)

Organisation: Tribunal Regional Eleitoral de Minas Gerais
Description:
The Local Legal Project was developed with the objective of diagnosing the accessibility situation of the more than 10,000 voting locations in the state of Minas Gerais and, in partnership with the various agencies, promote the adaptation of the properties to the accessibility requirements. The goal is to enable the full participation of everyone in the electoral process, voters with or without disabilities, obese people, the elderly, and others, with autonomy and safety.

The Local Legal Project has the challenge of mapping the situation of voting locations in the state and proposing, through partnerships with public/private power, location owners, universities and/or interested parties, the adequacy of the locations to the accessibility criteria. The adaptations, including the "reasonable" ones (CNJ Resolution 401/2021), according to the reality of each place. 

The polling places required for the assembly of polling stations are usually state and municipal schools that are often in a precarious state of repair. The development of the project, besides enabling access to voting for all voters, provides accessibility for regular users, students with reduced mobility, in short, for the entire community. Accessibility at TRE-MG is a value in the Court's Strategic Planning, and accessibility in polling places is one of PETRE's indicators.
Novelty: The project is innovative because the Electoral Justice, although not responsible for the polling places, engages in the cause of inclusion and uses its structure to help promote accessibility in the polling places. The project is effective because, due to the capillarity of the Electoral Justice and the reality of many places, especially municipal and state schools that lack a minimum accessibility structure, a large number of people can benefit from this action. Currently, a system to register these more than 10,000 voting locations in Minas Gerais has been developed by the Court's Information Technology Secretariat, so that we can issue reports, monitor the implementation of improvements, and thus propose new actions.
Impact & Results:
The results of the project are the creation of more sections with accessibility and, it intends to promote places (schools, gymnasiums, banks, etc.) suitable to the accessibility criteria under the law. We know that, although it is a legal requirement, many buildings do not even have the possibility to adapt to the legislation. The mapping, however, enables the proposition of actions to minimize the limitations of access to voting. 

The management of this information can avoid the numerous situations in which the voter has his or her right to vote precluded by existing barriers. The desired impact is that no voter has his or her right to vote restricted by possible barriers, and that the community with disabilities or reduced mobility can benefit from the actions after the elections.

Project/Initiative Name: Automatic Renewal of the Citizen's Card
Organisation: IRN - Institute of Registration and Notary Affairs
Description:
The Portuguese Ministry of Justice has developed a service, during the pandemic period, which allows citizens over 25 to automatically renew their Citizen's Card and receive it at home, without having to go to the services. With this measure it was possible to continue to ensure the renewal of identification documents for Portuguese citizens and ensure the satisfaction of essential needs for life in society, such as the need to identify and prove identity.

The Citizen's Card is one of the most ambitious projects of a vision of digital transformation and simplification of public administration that since 2007 has opened the door to the development of online services and dematerialization of processes. 

The project "Automatic renewal of the Citizen's Card for people over 25" has therefore made it possible, within a framework of severe limitations imposed on face-to-face service during the pandemic situation, to ensure the regular renewal of the civil identification documents of Portuguese citizens, without the need to travel, by adopting non-presential service mechanisms, supported by online services for consulting the state, which allow citizens to follow the stages of renewal (Digital Justice Platform - https://justica.gov.pt/), online information services and helpdesk services of the dedicated telephone line - Citizen's Card Line. 

This modality allows people 25 years of age or older, who have their card expiring and wish to keep the same data, to automatically renew it and receive it at home by mail, conveniently, without the need to travel to the services.

Project/Initiative Name: Intelligent management of aid to citizens and companies
Description:
The provision of European funding for economic recovery after the pandemic favored the provision of significant aid to citizens that needed to be managed quickly to ensure financial sustainability. Previously, this was done manually, which caused difficulties in resolving them in due time and form. For this reason, the aid robotization process was implemented to speed up the procedures, releasing administrative burdens and responding to citizens' requests in an agile and efficient way. 

The aid automation process is part of the initiatives developed by the Digital Agency of Andalusia (ADA), through the Intelligent Automation Unit (UAI) to achieve efficiency and transparency in the public services of the Andalusian Regional Government. Specifically, this initiative is a benchmark in the automation of aid, which has a direct impact on citizens, as they can obtain these aids more quickly, given that the agile resolution of the process allows distributing the entire budget in the adequate manner and within the appropriate timeframe.

The management of aid is one of the most common and complex processes carried out by the Andalusian administration. It involves the processing of a high volume of files, for which a large number of requirements must be checked in each application. This automation solution seeks to become a reference model for the entire Andalusian Regional Government, based on the reuse of components for greater efficiency, reducing costs and start-up times, standardizing and homogenizing the management of aid. 

Robotization in aid management is also complemented by an IDP (intelligent document processing) system for the evaluation of documentation requirements, which allows the processes to be fully automated, without the need for the intervention of external companies or third parties, strengthening the security of the process and reducing the associated costs. This initiative is aligned with the acceleration of the use of disruptive technologies and the development of skills of all employees for the modernization of the Public Administration.
Novelty:
The Andalusian Digital Agency makes an innovative use of robotization technologies and intelligent document processing, articulating a new management model based on the reuse of components for greater scalability, reliability and increased performance. It contributes to the motivation of the manager by reducing monotonous tasks, allowing him to focus on more valuable activities, based on his specific knowledge. It is a work pattern that supports increased capacity in the face of peaks of activity and is a non-invasive technology compatible with current systems.  

The aid management allows to homogenize manual processing, minimize start-up time and optimize costs. The manual work is replaced by the robotization, resulting in more efficient and error-free processes. It is not just about the application of a transforming technology, but also the consolidation of a centralized model for the entire Andalusian Administration that reuses it for an agile development of new public services.
Impact & Results:
