Finnish government provides these tools free of charge to all public, private and third sector organisations.
Impact & Results:
The innovation has raised a lot of interest among potential customer organisations. In September 2022, 10 organisations and 20 services are using the tools, including big central government organisations (i.e. Finnish Tax Administration, Social Insurance Institution of Finland, Police of Finland) and big municipalities (cities of Turku and Lahti).
First user experiences (user interviews and tests) are positive. The self-assessment tool is seen as a useful check list and tool to identify pain points for the development of services. The customer feedback tool is appreciated for its simplicity and possibility to give anonymous feedback, lowering the threshold of citizens to give feedback.

Project/Initiative Name: Integrated System of Care for Victims of Gender-based Violence (using AI)
Organisation: Information Society for the Americas Foundation
Description:
The innovation was born in the COVID-19 pandemic, when thousands of women were locked up with their aggressors. In 2020, 108,402 communications were received on the 144 line. We developed a comprehensive system of care for victims of gender-based violence in order to improve the state's response to the problem. The innovation consisted not only in gathering and systematising information but also in assessing the risk of femicide present in each case through the use of artificial intelligence.

The solution consists of an Integrated System of Care for Victims of Gender-based Violence with Artificial Intelligence (SIAVIGia). The objective of the platform is to provide governments with a tool that allows for the comprehensive management of care for victims of gender-based violence, and to have a common Anonymous Data Registry in order to produce standardised statistical information of quality as input for the design of public policies to address the problem. This is a platform that integrates a Risk Assessment system with Artificial Intelligence that allows to evaluate the probability of each case becoming a case of greater conflict and even escalate to femicide.

The evaluation allows for the entry of the risk factors present, and the intensity with which they are present, to then weight these variables and assign scores that allow for the identification and prevention of possible cases in which the aggressor ends up murdering the victim.

SIAVIGia has modules designed mainly for seven areas involved: 1) Social Security, 2) Citizen Security, 3) Health, 4) Diversity and Gender, 5) Justice, 6) Victim Assistance, and 7) Social Development.
Novelty: The innovation lies in the development of a Femicide Risk Assessment: a system that through Artificial Intelligence learns to assess the risk of femicide present in a case of gender-based violence better and better, assigning different levels of priority in the follow-up of each case accordingly.
Impact & Results:
The impact of the solution is measured by assessing the variation in the level of reporting and state agility in the response to gender issues, in the evolution of cases of gender-based violence, and in the evolution of femicides within the territory where the solution is implemented.
From December 2019 to November 2020, a total of 1147 admissions of victims of gender-based violence were made to the Municipal Directorate. Of these 1147 admissions, a total of 3926 registered interventions were given; giving an average of 3.5 interventions per person attended.

Description:
ETAPAS aims to improve public service delivery for citizens by facilitating the ethical adoption of Disruptive Technologies in compliance with European and national strategies and guidelines. It is carried out by a Consortium of 14 partners from 8 European countries including public administrations, digital innovation hubs, research institutions, and IT companies. The project has received funding from Horizon 2020.

To deal with the implementation of Artificial Intelligence, Robotics or Big data in the Public Sector, ETAPAS intends to develop three tools:
- a Responsible Disruptive Technology Framework (RDT Framework) including a Code of Conduct (CoC) setting out ethical principles, an overview of ethical risks and social impacts of DTs, RDT indicators to practically measure those risks and impacts, and a European legal framework;
- a Governance model; and
- a prototypical software platform.

ETAPAS approach aims at making these tools as practical as possible to support PAs with a responsible adoption of DTs through a careful assessment of the trustworthiness of their development and use. To this end, these tools will be co-designed with public bodies and the involvement of Digital Innovation Hubs (DIHs) and universities. Moreover, the framework will be tested and validated on four Use Cases, where public organisations will carry out the pilots covering different set of PA services and outreach levels.
Novelty: The structure of the team is innovative: 14 organisations spread around Europe and funded by the European Commission's Horizon 2020. The project is innovative because it aims at developing recognised tools able to shape the relationship between disruptive technologies and PAs at the international level.
Impact & Results: The project has delivered the Code of Conduct for Disruptive Technologies and it has developed links with other innovative projects around the world. The four PA in which to carry out the pilots have already been identified but the results of these experiences are not available yet.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:
ETAPAS Project (overview)
ETAPAS Project (who we are)

Project/Initiative Name: First Blockchain Technology Experimentation Space of the Colombian State
Organisation: Ministry of Information and Communication Technologies (MICT)
Description:
It allows territorial and national public entities to access free of charge to infrastructure, technical support, technology appropriation, development of proofs of concept and testing of solutions.

The pilot has so far been implemented in two phases: Phase 1, 3 entities were selected that had a landed and viable idea for this technology. The 3 entities selected for this phase were prioritised by the ICT Ministry according to the need of the national order to generate public value in terms of transparency, fight against corruption and optimisation of processes within the entities. Phase 2 was a series of workshops and talks with the aim of teaching about blockchain, resolving doubts, and accompanying the entities to turn their ideas into viable projects for this technology. It was a phase of appropriation of the technology. The 14 entities selected for this phase were chosen through an open call where each entity sent the required documentation according to their needs. 

The main objective is to learn from the subject, experiment, make mistakes and continue learning in order to generate lessons learned and encourage other entities to implement real and innovative solutions for their needs. The second objective is to facilitate a friendly space through experimentation, where entities feel free to learn and discover a new technology.

This guide comprises two parts. The first corresponds to the description of Blockchain technology, the importance of promoting open government, the identification of ownership initiatives in the country and some use cases with usability. The second is the implementation of the technology in concrete projects, presenting a step-by-step approach to present a principled project and a governance process for planning, building a prototype, scaling a minimum viable product and managing change.
Novelty:
It is innovative because it reinvents the mission processes carried out by the participating entities with the use of disruptive technology such as Blockchain. 
The Pilot allows to explore and learn the technology, generating valuable lessons for its subsequent implementation and encouraging more entities to experiment as well, but also applies a technological mechanism to provide significant improvements and a new organisational mode in the Colombian State. The objective is to learn, develop functional solutions and sow the seeds of public innovation in other entities.
Impact & Results:
We proposed developing proofs of concept, and the use of collaborative spaces was of such an impact that two prototypes were consolidated for phase 1, which are ready to be deployed in production.
In phase 2, gaps in appropriation of blockchain were overcome, and a Phase 3 became necessary, in which we expect to advance in consultancy in order to consolidate minimum viable products.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres: https://gobiernodigital.mintic.gov.co/portal/Centro-de-Innovacion-Publica-Digital/Proyectos    (Click on "Piloto Blockchain" to drop-down the cell)

Organisation: Agency for e-Government and the Information and Knowledge Society (Agesic)
Description:
The solution generates a controlled virtual environment very similar to real conditions and circumstances. It seeks to train brigadistas (people in charge of guiding the evacuation of officials and visitors present in the Executive Tower, Presidency of the Republic Building) on the emergency procedures to be followed in the event of an evacuation of the Presidency Building.

The project is the first case of its kind in the Uruguayan state, it emerged as a proof of technology, evolved into a proof of concept and is currently a functional solution, which must continue to evolve, as it is working on ideas that will not only allow it to remain in the training and evaluation of the brigadistas, but also to use the models generated as a replica of reality, entering into the framework of other concepts such as "Digital Twins".
Being a project born from the division of emerging technologies and going through all the stages within the cycle of technological surveillance of this work team, which proposes on a given technology: Identify, Search, Analyse, Value, Disseminate and Orient; it is immersed in a scheme of continuous improvement, with emphasis on the last 2 stages (Disseminate and Orient), seeking to publicise the work done and promoting it as a driving force for other areas and organisations to innovate with a solid knowledge base, and the certainty that this technology is mature enough to embark on projects that provide value to citizens and civil servants in their service.
Novelty:
Using virtual reality, the Uruguayan state combined different actors to build the first VR case, immersed in a cycle of technological surveillance created within the organisation's own framework. 
There was an exchange between the development team, management, brigade members, firefighters, the team of trainers and other interested parties, all working and contributing from their knowledge base to achieve a solution that adds value.
Impact & Results:
'- Possibility of saving funds, each virtual practice does not require more expenditure than the time that the person can be immersed, with infinite possibilities in the use of virtual resources.
- Evaluate its use considering the people involved and their roles: Firefighters (trainers), Brigadiers (Trained), civil servants and people visiting the Executive Tower.
- Reduction of the time dedicated to the tour of the Tower, with the possibility of interacting with all the objects necessary for the movement of fire extinguishers, waistcoats, helmets, etc. (if necessary).

Description:
Bogotá Local was developed due to the economic crisis of Covid-19, which generated great difficulties for the most vulnerable population. It is a reactive response in the short term, recovery in the medium term and consolidation of the popular economy in the long term.

It is innovative because the Government Secretariat and the local mayor's offices addressed the popular economy, which was not the focus of the national government. It caters for women, young people and the elderly.

The innovation is about making public management more flexible so that local municipalities, under the coordination of the Government Secretariat, can invest resources in their popular economies and thus attend to their own differential needs in comparison with other sectors of the economy.

Its primary objective is to strengthen and consolidate the popular economy, which is all those economic activities that are carried out outside the formal economy. Its specific objectives are oriented towards leveraging productive niches, payroll incentives that allow them to maintain their workers and/or link new people, and generate training processes in business and digital skills.

The main method was to join the efforts of the local mayor's offices by facilitating flexible programmes that meet the needs of the people, this was a process of breaking down barriers and facing the complications that public management entails. Implementation and monitoring mechanisms were also adapted. The main one is the Local Government Centre as a tool for consolidating implementation and monitoring data from Bogotá Local.
Novelty: It is innovative in that it is not based on an economy of large companies and enterprises. In addition, it is focused on the population that has more difficulties to access formal employment, therefore, besides being a strategy with a strong emphasis on the economic strengthening of Bogotá, it seeks to benefit the most vulnerable people in the economy, which means that there was a change in investment priorities and this form of economy became one of the main ones.
Impact & Results:
Both quantitative and qualitative data were used to measure the results. Bogotá Local by 2021 had the following results: 32,383 people with jobs, 22,734 productive units or productive family enterprises with incentives. This benefited: 12,656 women, 10,928 men, 8,799 young people, 1,230 rural producers and 949 cultural incentives. During this period a total investment of $194,134,431,273 was made, corresponding to 51,658,975 USD (TRM June 2020). 
In 2022, the aim is to continue with the consolidation of the popular economy, where it is expected to benefit 23,000 micro-businesses.

Description:
Until 2017, the Government and Ministries took individual and uncoordinated initiatives to digitize public services. But most initiatives were facing challenges to provide services due to unplanned design and implementation. To overcome this, Digital Service Design Lab (DSDL) is an innovative method where service providers, service recipients and IT experts design digital services and prepare digitization masterplan together for their respective institutional organisation within just 6 days.

DSDL acts with 6-7 days long residential workshop for every ministry or division of the government of Bangladesh. Implementing DSDL first requires the service of the concerned ministries to be analyzed and groups created based on the analyzed service grouping. Every group consists of one team leader from the Deputy Secretary/Joint Secretary/Director level who leads the team, 2 domain experts who have the knowledge of the relevant services, 1 or 2 service relevant IT expert, 2 service recipients, and 1 Digital Service analyst from Digital Service Accelerator. 

So far, 30 Ministries/Divisions and 61 Govt. Organizations have completed DSDL. However, conducting DSDL isn’t the end. Digital Service Accelerator also provides support after DSDL. From Procurement to Implementation, in the whole Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).
Novelty: In the Government of Bangladesh, no such method exists other than DSDL, which assists and supports the Government extensively to achieve the “Digital Bangladesh” Goal. DSDL 6-days approach is novel as well, as it works with novel tools of project development.
Impact & Results: 30 Ministries and 61 Organizations have designed & planned 225 software covering 1624 services through DSDL. As a result, 11 Ministries/organizations are developing 117 software (Procurement Worth Tk. 100 Cr.+). 80+ software have already been developed till date.

Description:
Bogotá-Colombia is administered by 20 local mayor's offices. Before, it was the mayors who allocated local resources according to their own concept, without taking into account the knowledge and experience of the community, which is why roads were built where they were not needed or social programmes were developed without recognising the diverse populations. With the Participatory Budgeting Civic Laboratories, the community began to decide where to invest their local budgets.

The methodology of the civic laboratories of participatory budgeting was designed, the public servants of the local mayor's offices were trained, the citizens were invited, the tool of divergence called Carnival of Ideas was used, a convergence and agreement was made with the citizens and they defined up to 50% of the budget of the mayor's offices.
Novelty: The difference with this innovative project lies in the methodological process based on divergence, convergence, concertation and agreement through creative tools.
Impact & Results: More than 132,000 participants in the civic laboratories of participatory budgeting, more than 56,000 proposals agreed and agreed, for the measurement of the results were taken into account the analysis of data from the participants vs. agreed proposals, after the agreed proposals will be convened to the proponents in a process called local builders from there those ideas or solutions raised will be incubated and developed with the resources of local municipalities. this implies that about a billion Colombian pesos will be administered with proposals from citizens.

Description:
A problem for companies and owners of companies is that they need to engage with many different authorities to conduct their business. There is also a problem with lack of trust between companies, especially cross border, since there are no standardized way for digital identity for companies and relevant credentials/data that the companies want to show each other before and when they conduct business. There might also be lack of trust between institutions in different countries as they do not understand each other's semantics and systems.

Instead of building solutions in a siloed approach we aim to build into the ecosystem by integrating different actors and enabling the business to control their own data using blockchains and digital wallets. This enables companies to conduct cross border business digitally in a much smother way as they can use trusted data/verifiable credentials and effective the business registers work cross border as we can automize work that we do when it comes to guaranteeing correct data. Our test case is focused on procurement but the techstack and possibilities can be used whenever you need to prove something digitally that has to do with a company.

This is both a new approach and use of a combination of new technologies that benefits the companies. We are now creating services in a cross-border pilot between Finland, Norway and Sweden. Our case has also gained a lot of attraction which enables us to lead a consortium for an EU large scale pilot on digital wallets for organisations which is how we envisage that we will scale the project. In this we partner with many private companies such as VISA, Nextline, Open Peppol, BankID, IDUnion.
Novelty: We are working agile in the way that we contentiously add new functionalities (and do away with ones that are not needed). We are working very outreaching and using foresight (especially on regulation and technologies) with make us fast moving. Also we do integrate new technologies, in a test-environment, rapidly in a way the normally would not happen.
Impact & Results:
The impact is quite clear as we now spearhead the ecosystem in Europe when it comes to digital wallets for organisations. But the real results will come after the regulations and infrastructures are set into place. 

When the cross-border pilot and the EU-pilot is ready we will see the numbers but we are sure (from our analysis) that we will reduce the time used for compliance in B-B and B-G very much and that is the minimum positive results.

Organisation: VLAIO - Flemish Innovation and Entrepreneurship Agency
Description:
Becoming a circular economy entails a deep transformation of industry, consumer behavior and policy. Traditional innovation funding focuses on technology, punctual projects and (single) companies, which alone fail to bring the required systemic change. Hence, the Flemish government launches the subsidy programme Living Labs Circular Economy for projects tackling complex challenges, with a high ambition level, in co-creation with very diverse stakeholders and employing system innovation methods.

The open call ‘Living Labs Circular Economy’ funds ‘living lab’ projects in manufacturing and construction. This innovation does so in a particular way. First, traditional grants often minimise risk, rather than managing it. VLAIO proposes a different risk management strategy, aiming to maximise exposure to positive upsides. To accomplish this, VLAIO uses theory of change approaches, reflexive monitoring, and manages a portfolio of projects to share risk. Second, VLAIO aims at having diverse partnerships, hence it is open to innovative collaborations. The submission process has three steps: i) projects pitch to our team and get feedback; ii) optional collective or individual coaching sessions, eg. on system innovation tools; iii) submit final proposal.

The call was overwhelmingly popular. We had 700 participants at info sessions and a large inflow of projects, in a period of declining interest in traditional instruments. A project runs for up to 3 years and can request €300k to €1 M at 80% funding rate. Design and roll-out of the call was a co-creation between several VLAIO teams and a consultant for the coaching. Our main beneficiaries are circular pioneers from the ‘quintuple helix’: business, government, researchers, citizens, financiers.
Novelty: This innovation funds circular economy projects trying to overcome the risk aversion of other grants mechanisms. Further, it aims at a diversity of partnerships: if in line with the theory of change, each stakeholder relevant for reaching the goal is eligible.
Impact & Results:
Our call was overwhelmingly popular: 107 pitches and 39 final proposals, while traditional calls suffered declining inflow. Qualitative interviews with candidates confirm that the innovative elements of the call are a good match with their needs. Our coaching programme improved their knowledge on system innovation and facilitated new alliances, strengthening the ecosystem. The coaching sessions received a 9.2/10 rating (n = 17)
22 projects are to receive €18 million in 2022, with 174 unique organisations as project partners. Hundreds more are suppliers or partake in advisory boards or focus groups. Tens of thousands will be directly impacted through project outcomes (eg. industry standards). The ripple effect in small and tightly connected Flanders will be substantial: more businesses will go circular. We will measure the impact on CO2, material use and trends in the respective sectors.

Project/Initiative Name: La Araña: Bogotá's Public Innovation Strategy
Organisation: Alcaldía Mayor de Bogotá / iBO Public Innovation Laboratory
Description:
Our "ARAÑA" strategy seeks to strengthen and make public innovation sustainable over time, based on the creation of networks/ecosystems and the non-exclusive dependence on innovation units.

This is a strategy that aims to create a network-ecosystem of public innovation that is sustainable over time. This is due to the dependence on innovation laboratories (which are increasingly closing down on a recurring basis) as the only means for the generation of public innovation.

Each of the parts of the "Spider" has a fundamental sense of purpose: Its legs as a fundamental base: The first leg represents the "institutional framework" that sustains innovation in Bogotá; The second leg is represented by the "Governance Model of Public Innovation"; The third leg is the "innovation services" that this model provides to public entities and citizens; And the fourth leg is related to activities related to Knowledge Management and the Generation of Indicators.

The body of the spider is formed by our methodology based on the double diamond (based on systemic thinking, behavioural sciences) that permeates the whole Spider, seeking that all the actions developed by the members of the ecosystem never lack user-centricity, collaboration and experimentation.
The eyes of the Spider are the representation of the work done by the Bogotá Public Innovation Lab (IBO), as an articulating entity of actors, which seeks to support the activities of other entities, as well as to generate knowledge management actions for continuous improvement. 

The purpose of the network is to enable entities to interrelate and generate joint actions, but it also aims to "capture" them so that it is very difficult for them to leave the network. On the other hand, an analogy is also made with the eyes of the spider, to the extent that there is not just one eye but several, seeking that any entity in the future can take the position that iBO occupies as an articulator of the ecosystem.
Novelty:
It contains a relevant and varied structure for its sustainability over time. It leaves aside the traditional hierarchies within public entities; it raises the importance of solid institutional lines in the short and long term; it distributes services and actions by entities; it is responsible to the extent that it proposes actions for knowledge management and evaluation. 
It is also innovative because it shows public innovation in a more comprehensive and wide-ranging way than if it were only led by an innovation laboratory.
Impact & Results:
Some figures on Bogotá's public innovation ecosystem
+50 public servants participate in the governance model
+40 innovation goals to be met by 2022
+50 activities to be developed to meet goals in 2022
+17 "flagship" projects to be carried out in 2022
50% fulfilment of goals by July 2022
Bogota's public innovation index increased from 36.7 to 41.28

Description:
Anticipate is a tech-enhanced tool for context monitoring and early warning of conflict. Anticipate fights bias in analysis by encouraging analysts to be forward-looking and removing echo-chambers. It hosts data collection, political-economic analysis, and anticipation in one place. As such, it is a one-stop shop for the public sector to develop policy.

The app combines data collection, analysis and early warning/anticipation in one place. It supports all processes of context monitoring from data collection to the dissemination of output.

To aid data collection, Anticipate provides the tools for networks of on-the-ground collectors to compile and categorise data. Anticipate also supports data collection from open sources through tools like the news aggregator, which scans and selects news pieces that are reliable and relevant to the project, and the indicators page, which provides real-time information on metrics tailored to needs of the project. 

In order to assist analysts to develop accurate and relevant analysis, Anticipate separates noise from subtle paradigm shifts with computer-assisted tools. For example, actor mapping describes the interactions of relevant actors over a period of time. Additionally, a location map shows the geographical distribution of events on 2-D and 3-D maps.

Equipped with these tools, experts (and users) are able to position themselves on future events based on forecast questions
Novelty: Anticipate gathers all processes of context monitoring in one place, making a one-stop shop for public policy makers. This includes providing users with high-quality data, which saves them time and avoids selection bias. Moreover, the entire data review process happens in the app. This avoids multiple channels of communication and scattered flows of information, which can lead to data inaccuracy. Anticipate also fights bias by utilising forecast questions, to which experts respond with probabilities after using tech-enhanced tools for analysis.
Impact & Results: Anticipate has experienced a high adoption rate among those involved in a public sector project on the platform. This means that many processes of context monitoring have been carried out on the platform with the aid of its tech-enhanced functionalities.

Description:
Companies in Emilia-Romagna in Italy are in strong need of personnel with ICT skills. Unfortunately, the education system is currently unable to meet this demand: there are still many young people who do not follow STEM courses. In particular, few girls approach university and specialist courses in science and technology. Digital Girls ER is an active orientation project whose main objective is to bring girls closer to computer science and programming through an innovative method.

The project consisted in the organization of 8 free Summer Camps lasting 3 weeks, of a laboratory and operational nature, in all the universities of Emilia-Romagna, during which, under the supervision of university teachers and tutors, the girls attending the last years of high school were able to learn the basics of a programming language and work in a group on the practical application of what they have learned, to create software and / or other types of products.

Project/Initiative Name: Pink Passes – Enhancing Women’s Mobility and Workforce Participation through Improved Public Transport
Organisation: Dialogue and Development Commission of Delhi
Description:
With this innovation, women are provided pink passes to travel in all public buses (total 5589) serving the city of Delhi free of cost. In addition to free passes, the government also implemented an initiative to attain 100% coverage of bus marshals, who are unarmed civil defense volunteers and home guards who acquire formal training for this job and are mandated to promote safety, including reporting and responding to cases of sexual harassment. To support a positive environment, many of the new marshals were women.

Further, the buses were also equipped with modern ICT features which include 24*7 surveillance through CCTV camera, Panic buttons, live tracking of buses, two-way connection with command-and-control center and e-ticketing facility to increase accessibility and safety for women passengers. The implementation of many initiatives such as relaxation of recruitment norms for women drivers within buses of the city transportation fleet has resulted in hiring of more than 20 women drivers for heavy motor vehicles. 

The initiative initially envisioned as a scheme for a shorter period has been lauded and has continued till date due to its efficacy in improving women’s mobility systems. Similar schemes are also being conceptualized for other modes of public transportation to ensure multi-model last mile connectivity to commuters. Such initiatives were extremely helpful in supporting people of economically weaker sections as well. Realizing the benefits of such initiatives, a similar scheme of free passes was also started for contract laborers traveling frequently from one part of the state to another.
Novelty: The innovation provides free and safe public transports to women. As such, it addresses the lack of women’s mobility, which limits their access in becoming part of the economy and potentially leveraging the various opportunities that might be available in an urban growing economy.
Impact & Results: A considerable proportion of bus travelers are now women, which has gone as high as 80% during non-covid lockdown periods. For the period between March 2021 and July 2021 (after the first wave of COVID pandemic), 2,014,650 ticketed journeys were recorded, of which 1,543,986 (75%) were pink tickets issued to women passengers. Overall, the implementation of the pink passes policy has increased women ridership in buses from 33% to a sustained average of 40% currently.

Description:
The Austin Civilian Conservation Corps (ACCC) began as a pandemic response program to help residents earn income and access green careers, and has evolved into a leading model for equitable, climate-focused workforce development. The ACCC, a collaboration with 10+ City departments and multiple community partners, has provided over 125 living-wage opportunities with supportive services, training, and career pathways for Austin’s underserved residents, and is actively shaping the green economy.

The program continued to gain momentum and exposure throughout 2021, and one of the team members was even asked to testify to the US House of Representatives about the ACCC’s successful model.

In 2022 the City provided an additional $6M of funding and two new full-time positions to manage the program, and moved it to a permanent home in the Parks and Recreation Department. The program has continued to evolve from a pandemic response measure to a permanent, climate workforce development initiative with equity at its core. With a strong focus on resident feedback, quality jobs, data collection and analysis, and wrap-around support, it is a model for Cities around the world looking to drive real progress on climate and equity goals.
Novelty: centering equity and residents’ lived experience led to an entirely different design; one where a living wage, supportive services, and strong career pathways are required for all programs under the ACCC initiative.
Impact & Results:
Key ACCC outcomes include 125+ paying, supportive opportunities for residents who what to pursue green careers. Residents have also had the opportunity to shape the program much more extensively than usual.

The City has gained new pipelines of qualified workers, an effective and efficient means of pursuing its climate and sustainability goals, and a successful, multimillion-dollar permeant equity-focused workforce development program.

Project/Initiative Name: Preventive Audit in Contracts: Expense, Bidding and Adhesion Analysis System (ANGELICA)
Description:
ANGELICA - Expense, Bidding and Accession Analyser System is a software-based robotic process automation (RPA - Robotic Process Automation), built in Phyton, developed with the purpose of monitoring the acquisition of goods and services and the budgetary, financial and asset execution, based on pre-established criteria, initially in the scope of the organisms and entities that are part of the Presidency and Vice-Presidency of the Republic.

The system was developed with a view to the timely examination of a large volume of information at a low cost, so as to provide relevant and timely information to public managers. It was not possible to generate information with the same level of efficiency before the adoption of the ANGELICA solution.

The solution RPA - ANGELICA was conceived to generate more agility to the monitoring of the acquisition processes, initially those elaborated by the Presidency of the Republic. The continuous examination of the activities involving bidding processes, as well as bidding exemptions and inexigibility, is essential to safeguard resources and avoid losses and damages to the public treasury. 

From pre-established criteria of materiality, risk, relevance and criticality, the ANGELICA indicates processes to be previously examined by the auditors. Considering that the realization of the electronic auction, for example, occurs within eight days after the publication of the public notice, the auditors start the checking of the elements that compose the monitoring of the process selected by RPA - ANGELICA.

The first and most relevant question to be verified is the adequacy of the estimated price, because its impact, when overestimated, may lead to the hiring of overpriced materials and services, which is the main risk factor of loss to the treasury in the contracting process. Next, the continuity of the analysis is directed according to the time remaining until the trading session is held. At this point, the following aspects are prioritized for analysis: size of the object, restrictive clauses, content and compliance with the recommendations of the technical and legal areas, adjustment and renegotiation clauses, and provisional and final receipt.
Novelty:
The RPA - ANGELICA solution is innovative because it performs research in large volume of goods and services acquisition processes for the public sector, through direct access to the government system Comprasnet Siasg (Integrated System of Administration of General Services), with extraction, separation and classification of data and availability of alerts, from predefined criteria and parameters (risk, materiality, relevance, criticality of the managing units or by purchase item). It is also possible to use the Annual Hiring Plan as parameters for the filters, with priorities indicated by the managers, as well as adapt the solution to state and municipal needs.
The alerts arising from the APR associated with prioritization of the scope of analysis allows the auditor to be assertive in his analyses, producing timely information for management. In addition, there is the practicality of operating the APR with limited equipment resources (computer connected to the internet).
Impact & Results:
The use of RPA - ANGELICA solution produced more accurate performance of the audit team in monitoring the acquisition processes with the generation of more timely information to the manager.
Audit report prepared in July 2021, after six months of use of the ANGELICA solution, brings the following description about financial benefits generated:
"48.	The financial benefits, derived from the timely performance of the Internal Control, with the aid of the ANGELICA System, were measured in a total of R$ 1.387.946,99 (>270,000 EUR) per year, being R$ 60.283,92 per year, in Public Procurement nº 6/2021, and R$ 1.327.663,07 per year, in Public Procurement nº 21/2021. 
49.	Knowing that these are service contracts and that they may be extended for up to 5 years, the financial benefits may be even greater, reaching a total of R$ 6,939,734.95. (>1,5 million EUR)"
In addition, there were non-financial benefits such as improved internal controls and improved governance of management processes.

Description:
The MTC2 education device is a hybrid ERP system (hardware and software) dedicated to pedagogy which assists a teacher to implement differentiated pedagogical practices in case of disruption of the pedagogical continuity or massification of teaching whatever the pedagogical modality (face-to-face and/or distance learning; group and or individualised) with an efficient, permanent and real time evaluation process.

It aims to respond to this observation by developing a collaborative educational system that capitalises on the possibilities offered by digital technologies. The system will enable secondary school students to benefit from differentiated courses that meet their needs. In terms of pedagogical aspects, the "MTC2" educational system proposes a multimodal, group and/or differentiated pedagogical approach. This device will also be equipped with a multimodal assessment process that will allow, in addition to the current assessment principles, to trace the learning process of a learner to the best of our knowledge, no current educational system is able to do so.

The MTC2 students will benefit from the innovations offered by the device in terms of distance learning, which proposes immersive virtual reality technologies, and in terms of evaluation, where the device is capable of tracing the learning process.

In its experimental phase (2020-2022), the device in the form of an experimental prototype "Cerpan 1" (hardware) and a proof of concept (software), about a hundred students could benefit from the innovation and validate the opportunity of the innovation device. The objective of the project is to have all 19,086 schoolchildren in the region benefit from this innovation within five years.

The success of the project will make it possible to study its institutionalisation and its integration into the architecture of the current system. It could then be disseminated in the different organic and structural strata of the national education system and in its territorial locations.
Novelty:
The MTC2 device proposes a new approach to education and redefines the context of the pedagogical relationship by proposing a new didactic organisation. In this organisation, all the learners in a class group are in a differentiated teaching situation. The mass pedagogical management of the learners is ensured by an evaluation device that combines new technologies (immersive and AI) and the results of research on cognitive sciences. To our knowledge, the performance of the evaluation system is still unequalled.
Furthermore, the system allows for educational continuity in crisis situations (pandemic, absence of educational structures, effects of natural disasters). 
Finally, the proposed system allows for an inclusive pedagogical approach and acts in favour of the intellectual and social emancipation of learners by accompanying them in their full acceptance of otherness.
Impact & Results:
'- The possibility of decompartmentalising teaching in secondary schools, demonstrated by the creation and experimentation of the "VAUBAN" proof of concept (03/2022/ Team of mathematics/history and technology teachers)
- A new alternative of distance learning allowing a better democratization of education ( 07/2020/ 1st experimentation of virtual mobility of a teacher between the city of Nice and Perpignan /experimentation conditions in the laboratory of the R&D department EPISTEMES and ( 03/2022/ 2nd experimentation of virtual mobility of a teacher /technological information/ 6 teachers /between the city of Lille (location of the CERPAN1 device) and Perpignan /actual conditions of use )

Project/Initiative Name: Innosprint - a design sprint for the public sector
Organisation: Public Sector Innovation Team of Estonia
Description:
How to bring new ways of working and problem-solving tools to the Estonian public sector? Over the course of a year and a half the Public Sector Innovation Team of Estonia developed a design sprint format suitable for the Estonian public sector. By constantly adapting and improving the format the Innovation Team has seen that it is a highly effective tool for spreading design thinking methods and generating enthusiasm around them.

An Innosprint takes place on five days spread over six weeks. What makes an Innosprint different from a traditional sprint is that there is time left between days 1 and 2 for all participants to conduct their own user research: interviews and, if possible, other kinds of field work. T sees this as an important step that changes the civil servants’ thinking and helps them understand the problem from the point of view of the user.

To date, four Innosprints have taken place, where altogether 381 people from 85 organisations have sought novel solutions in 53 project teams. Teams apply to the sprint by proposing an actual policy or service problem that they need to solve. It is important to emphasise that the vast majority of participants have never encountered design thinking before. The teams consist mostly of civil servants, but often include partners from the private or 3rd sector. Innosprints are centrally managed by Innotiim (a team of 4 people) and fully virtual (via Zoom and Miro), which does not prevent teams from having a physical war room and also allows people to take part in the sprint from all over Estonia.
Novelty:
Innosprint is innovative, because:
- it's a scalable and efficient method for upskilling public servants in design thinking and co-creation methods,
- all the participants conduct their own fieldwork. This has brought about a fundamental shift in mindset with many of the participants and given the civil servants a much better understanding of the problems they are solving
- it brings about innovative solutions that the public sector wouldn't otherwise have thought of
- it is a very practical learning method, as participants practice design thinking tools on actual problems that need solving
- the format empowers the participants to use design thinking in their day-to-day work and replicate the design thinking process on their own.
Impact & Results: We keep in touch with sprint teams and regularly check in to ask how are they faring with the sprint project and what kind of practices from the sprint have they taken away. Innosprint has improved the quality of procurements. Several public institutions have gotten the courage to use design thinking in-house to tackle problems. Many public institutions have taken co-creative methods and started using them in their day-to-day work. Participant feedback: ‘I have already used [the knowledge gained from the sherpa sprint] – for example, I am now developing the prototype in-house and trying to use the knowledge gained for this purpose and put small teams to work.’

Description:
Small and medium-sized companies often cannot afford investments in Artificial Intelligence technologies. Through AI Friends project, AI became more accessible, affordable, and familiar for entrepreneurs and citizens. AI festival, training, co-working spaces, and pilot solution consulting is operated through the cooperation of universities, research institutes, industries, government, and citizens over the last four years.

The primary objective of Science Technology Park(INNOPOLIS) AI Friends is to alleviate the barriers to AI-tech adoption for small and medium-sized enterprises(SMEs), which constitute of 97.3% of the companies in INNOPOLIS as of 2019. According to the Korea Development Institute’s survey of 500 Korean companies in 2020, the rate of AI solution adoption was only 3.6%, and the majority (91.7%) of companies that adopted AI solutions were large companies. 

Under the project name "AI friends", a total of four projects were operated to support the AI ecosystem - AI Clinic, AI spark Challenge, AI Start-up Co-working Space, and AI festival.
Novelty:
It focuses on establishing linkages between public laboratories and private industries at the nascent stage of AI solution development. 

it focuses on establishing linkages between public laboratories and private industries at the nascent stage of AI solution development.
Impact & Results: The AI Clinic program increased the usage of AI systems by operating an education program for the companies. Over the last two years, 376 people from the local companies in our science and technology park participated in AI courses. And last year, 72 companies participated in AI solution consulting and discovered the possibility of 29 companies applying AI in the company business. Currently, those companies are working on pilot tests with the help of AI experts from the research institutes

Project/Initiative Name: AI Convergence Reading System for Illegal Duplication Project
Description:
As South Korea, the 9th largest trade partner has increased K-brand power due to the spread of the Korean Wave to the world recently, damage cases in IPRs have accordingly increased from the inflow of illegal duplication imitating domestic products. NIPA and Korea Customs Service empathize with damage impacted on industry and people in 2020 in the case of the distribution of illegal duplication and have been developing AI-based reading solution to detect illegal duplication utilized customs clearance data through collaboration.

Starting with a demand survey for AI convergence tasks by inter-ministerial collaboration in February 2020, we made detailed business project proposal jointly through a number of consultations with NIPA-the Ministry of Science and ICT-Korea Customs Service. For smooth securing customs clearance data (image of actual goods, patent of design rights, etc.) which is essential for AI solution, business promotion & presentation are held every year for more than 100 companies of IPRs in cooperation with Korea Customs Service.

We secure reading accuracy and improve the efficiency of customs clearance by changing reading method to detect illegal duplication based on AI & customs clearance data from the existing visual inspection. We will achieve 93% of reading accuracy by 2023 and develop the solution to apply Incheon & Pyeongtaek Customs where Chinese items are imported most.
Novelty: This AI system achieves following two major innovations. First, we achieved 82.7% of reading accuracy which is above 80% of our target for initial AI solution models developed by 2021. Second, we can reduce the time to notify the right holder after detecting initial suspected goods, less than 5 minutes in the case of introducing AI solution from more than 1,440 minutes through customs inspectors visual inspection.
Impact & Results: We achieved 82.7% of reading accuracy which is above 80% of our target for initial AI solution models developed by 2021. Reading accuracy to detect illegal duplication is expected to improve by 93% from 0.4% of the existing visual inspection in the case of introducing AI solution. Further, we can reduce the time to notify the right holder after detecting initial suspected goods.

Description:
#MaFrance2022 is the largest online citizen consultation ever conducted for a public service media in France. It allowed one million French people to express their priorities and to engage in a direct conversation with the candidates in the run-up to the presidential election. This massive and unprecedented consultation thus sought to respond to the democratic urgency of placing the voice of the citizenry at the heart of political debates during a democratic high point like the presidential campaign.

Ma France 2022 is an initiative whose main objective was to fight against the democratic decline: 1) by mobilizing citizens before the 2022 presidential election, 2) by giving them a direct voice on their priorities for the future of the country and by placing their concerns at the heart of the public debate and the presidential campaign. 

From September 2021 to the beginning of March 2022, the French were invited to participate in a massive online citizen consultation, relayed by France Bleu and France 3, around a large open question: "What are the priorities for our country tomorrow? This consultation was relayed daily on the radio stations and digital spaces of France Bleu and France 3, but also on social networks, and allowed one million French people, from all territories, to express their priorities before the presidential election.

Indeed, Ma France 2022 has succeeded in involving the population to a very large extent. While there is an effervescence of participatory democracy tools today, some experiments have not succeeded in getting out of the pre-square of a few very committed citizens and are therefore not very useful in relation to the desired goal, if it is to rehabilitate the role of the citizen at the heart of public action. The price of the legitimacy of such a process is therefore to involve the population very widely, as this consultation did with one million participants.
Novelty:
This initiative is a democratic innovation and differs from other citizen participation projects in that it has succeeded in putting participatory democracy on the scale of the issues at stake. Ma France 2022 has indeed succeeded in involving the population in a very broad way, giving a voice to all, including those who usually feel distant from this kind of mechanism. 
The partnership with France Bleu and France 3, two major public service media, made it possible to extend the citizen consultation: participants were not only invited to submit their proposals and vote in the consultation, but they were also able to develop their ideas and arguments on radio and television, sometimes during direct discussions with candidates and political leaders. The initiative thus gave citizens a real voice and influence in the election campaign.
Impact & Results:
In 7 months, the "My France 2022" consultation has gathered 1 million participants, 34,000 proposals, and 7 million votes. One month before the first round of the election, the results of the consultation and the Citizen's Agenda made it possible to put 12 citizens' priorities at the heart of the public debate. 

The main indicators used to evaluate these results are the participation figures. Beyond this massive citizen participation, Make.org also made sure to have a certain representative balance in terms of age, gender, and region.

Description:
connectoo training aims to reduce the digital divide in Belgium by training public officials to better take into account citizens on the digital fringe. Accessible free of charge and 100% online, connectoo trains and certifies public agents in the challenges of digital inclusion and in the reflexes to adopt to help the most vulnerable citizens with online administrative procedures. Together, we are reconnecting digital public services to citizens. In 2022, the digital divide remains more than ever a reality in Belgium, where nearly one adult in two is in a situation of digital vulnerability.

Connectoo is a free online training accessible to all people working in a federal, regional or municipal administration, whatever their job. Our goal is to train and certify 10,000 public employees in Belgium by 2030. The training is aimed downstream at agents in direct contact with citizens (e.g. call center agents, counter agents) but also upstream at agents involved in the design of digital public services (e.g. communication managers, service managers). 

The training is structured around 5 modules combining theory, practical exercises and interactions with other participants: 
1/ The challenges of digital inclusion 
2/ Issues related to digital uses 
3/ Guiding users towards digital public services 
4/ Facilitating access to digital services 
5/ Designing inclusive services and supports. 

In addition, the training offers "applifocus", tutorials dedicated to the essential digital services of the federal administration (pensions, finance, health...) so that the agents in direct contact with the public can always be up to date with the latest functionalities offered.
Novelty:
To date, there is no equivalent to this training. Many initiatives are already in place at the regional and municipal levels, but most of them are aimed at training "front-line" staff. 

The connectoo training, on the other hand, makes it possible to act at two levels: 1) Downstream, by giving tools to the agents closest to the citizens in order to help them take the step to online public services, 2) Upstream, by raising awareness among second-line public servants of the importance of designing more inclusive digital public services "by design".
Impact & Results: The training went live in early summer 2022 on the federal government's e-learning platform. Nearly 1,000 people from more than 150 different institutions have already registered for the course. At the beginning of September, more than 100 public officials had already received their certificate. The satisfaction forms we receive are very encouraging as to the concrete applications in the field by the learners.

Description:
It is a project to support innovation that consists in labeling the Young Innovative Companies through a platform and digitalized process in order to benefit from measures instructed by the "Office of Transformation". The establishment of this innovative platform was set up to meet the need to identify and build a directory of innovative start-ups.

The platform meets the need to build a directory of Young Innovative Companies (Startups) in order to grant them a label that allows them to benefit from a set of measures instructed by the Office of Transformation. This is a unique device that allows public and private partners to collaborate in order to process applications received by companies offering high technological added value.

Since the launch of the device, more than 317 startups have benefited from this measure through the dedicated platform www.startuphub.ma. The platform allows to highlight the benefits of this measure and also allows the submission of applications through an online form.

The applications are then communicated at the platform to a committee of public and private experts who will decide on the quality of the applications and their innovations according to an evaluation grid integrated into the platform. 
The committee's votes are then consolidated to decide on the validation or rejection of the application. The validation of the application results in the generation of a certificate of labeling verifiable by QR code which is sent by email to the Startup and also downloadable through the platform.
Novelty: The Young Innovative Companies labeling system is an innovative and unique mechanism that brings together public and private partners in order to grant a label to Startups allowing them to benefit from a set of measures.
Impact & Results: The device has allowed to label more than 317 companies since its launch. The label has enabled them to benefit from the measures put in place by the Foreign Exchange Office and to further develop their activities at the national and international levels.

Organisation: New South Wales Department of Premier and Cabinet
Description:
Trend Atlas is a digital, interactive platform with rich insights on local and global trends and emerging signals to inform strategic planning, policy development and service redesign. It reduces research time, rework and duplication by breaking down information silos with centralised data and evidence. Built by policy people who upskilled in digital, it combines automation and curation with a sustainable contributor model to create a collaborative and trusted strategic intelligence service.

It brings together over 1100 fact and quality checked data sources to provide trusted and timely insights to users across NSW government. The platform provides individually curated information on a growing number of over 275 local and global trends. Each trend is comprised of a detailed analysis highlighting its drivers, impacts and possible developments. 

Multiple foresight and risk management taxonomies are also applied to the trends to enable effective user navigation and sense making. An horizon scanning database of over 3500 articles, gives users indications of weak signals of change and is the result of a comprehensive weekly scanning process that reviews over 5000 articles into a shortlist of 50 articles which are added to the platform each and every week.

While we had the vision for the platform, budgetary constraints meant we did not have the option to procure software developers and as a result we built the application as ‘citizen developers’, a policy team that upskilled in digital tools to make it all happen. We combined several off-the-shelf Microsoft (MS) applications into a seamless user experience that looks and feels like a website.
Novelty: It is the world's only government developed self-service, digital foresight and strategic intelligence platform for trend and horizon scanning. The platform is accessible across the NSW public sector to inform major public policy, service design and infrastructure decisions that shape our future without the need for costly consultants’ reports
Impact & Results: We continually measure platform usage and user engagement through surveys and platform analytics. The popularity of the Trend Atlas within NSW government has outstripped projections, the number of registered users exceeding 1000 users within 6 months of the product’s launch in 2022. 47% are active users having used the Trend Atlas more than once.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres: https://app.powerbi.com/links/8Aw4-Fjvsa?ctid=1ef97a68-e8ab-44ed-a16d-b579fe2d7cd8&pbi_source=linkShare       *** Please contact scott.perugini-kelly@dpc.nsw.gov.au to obtain access

Project/Initiative Name: Embracing and Enabling People of Determination (People with Disabilities)
Description:
A proof of concept has been created to incorporate design techniques in many elements of a people of determination's life cycle, planning for future demands and overcoming existing hurdles. The proposed strategy prioritizes the needs of persons with disabilities and helps them acquire the resources and services they need. It also helps families gain confidence in caring for a disabled kid. This helps people of determination engage, lead, create, and sustain life.

We wanted to understand the current status of People of Determination to learn about the lives of people with disabilities and their needs and experiences at a specific time. Desk research, interviews, social media listening, heuristic analysis of present services and products, and international benchmarking were used. Considering previous research, we could design three key personas and How Might We Questions to assist generate solutions for the personas and individuals of determination. The solutions were set out in a service blueprint, which was then translated into screen flows to explain the information architecture before moving on to the solution's real prototype phase and usability testing with actual users. 

Based on our segmentations and personalities, we produced two prototypes: one for the handicapped person and one for his/her guardian. The technique recognized the multiple life periods of a person with disability and emphasized crucial touchpoints. These range from having their handicap recognized at birth (or later in life) through retirement and developing an active community that enables them get peer support. At each touchpoint, the user is informed of available services, products, and support systems and their important life information is digitally preserved, including their digital ID, handicap card, and more.

Primary aims of this innovation:
1. Aligning with Goal 2030's vision of an ambitious country, flourishing economy, and lively society - The following PoD experience matches with a vision realization aim of integrating persons with disabilities into the workforce, promoting tenacity and persistence, and providing access to healthcare and education.
2. Few governments in the region engage in developing PoD, enabling KSA to pioneer inclusive government initiatives and empower under-represented groups.
3. Give people with disabilities centralized access to information, proactive services, and a supportive community. Supporting milestones.
Novelty:
1. The Proof of Concept focuses on benchmarking and using future trends to examine unknowable solutions.
2- We create a digital profile to make customized services available. We discover proactive government loan and aid options based on qualifying conditions.
3. It employs digital immersion, new technology, and other solutions to enhance the lives of industrious Saudis as the kingdom moves to a digital transformation phase. All while addressing accessibility considerations like sign language, voice-overs, and speech-to-text recognition.
Impact & Results: The project has been passed over to the development team with the relevant entities and is now in the implementation roadmap stage. Specific success indicators have been designed such as high satisfaction/relevance rating for recommendations provided, increased ratio of solved complaints through bot, community and counselors vs. in-person, and reduced time/effort/level of difficulty of the system.

Description:
A device is developed to trap mosquitoes with internet connection (IOT), which consists of LED lights to attract mosquitoes. This has a fan which sucks to the bottom, there is a camera connected to a mini computer raspberry pi which sends the photo and is deposited in a google drive.

A web page was also developed that has artificial intelligence capable of recognizing mosquitoes and differentiating those of the Aedes genus. The use of this trap would increase the sensitivity of Aedes detection and map the location of Aedes in real time.

Smarcacht: Low-cost IOT (Internet of Things) device with an image classifier, the project won a hackathon and was presented as a thesis, however it was discontinued. 

The goal of the project is to automate the monitoring and increase the accuracy of detection of the mosquito. This will lead to several improvements, saving costs for the government and improving the health of the citizens.
Novelty: Our technology is able to send photos and process them instantly and recognize them with artificial intelligence, which is highly innovative.
Impact & Results:
It allowed us to connect the municipality with different actors, laboratories, NGOs, universities.
We have used agile methodologies and with kpi, complying with them for technological development.
We want to validate the project with 6 more teams and then monitor the entire municipality.

Project/Initiative Name: Transitional Excess Profits and Losses mechanism
Description:
In 2021, SIRA clawed back $91 million in excess insurer profit earned on the sale of compulsory motor accidents insurance and returned it to NSW drivers. This move was made possible after the 2017 reforms introduced a transitional excess profits and losses (TEPL) mechanism that was designed to eliminate insurer ‘super profits’ of the past. To our knowledge, the activation of this mechanism to claw back excess insurer profit and return it to the people was a world first.

In 2017, NSW introduced a transitional excess profits and losses (TEPL) mechanism that provides the State Insurance Regulatory Authority (SIRA) with powers to control the level of profit insurers earn on the sale of CTP Green Slips. The purpose of the mechanism is to ensure that insurer profits are neither excessive nor inadequate.

Within the policy design is a provision called ‘innovation support’ that allows insurers to apply to retain up to an additional 3% of profit on Green Slips. This policy encourages insurers to innovate to invest in measures that improve outcomes for injured road users or road safety for motorists. Insurers must have evidence that the innovation has delivered measurable benefits to receive final approval and retain a share of profit.
Novelty: NSW is the first state in Australia to implement a CTP profit clawback mechanism and the design used in NSW is unlike any other in the world.
Impact & Results: In 2021, SIRA activated the mechanism for the first time to claw back almost $91 million in insurer profits. SIRA is currently completing its 2022 assessment cycle and the preliminary results show that excess profit was earned at an industry level. A decision will be made before the end of 2022 if the mechanism should be activated again.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital Boost (Small Business Digital Enablement) Programme
Organisation: Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment
Description:
New Zealand wants our small businesses to be the most digitally enabled in the world. Digital Boost helps owners and employees to build confidence and skills to digitally transform their businesses. Cynefin complexity theory, agile programme development, and public-private partnerships are used to deliver easily accessible learning and services. This includes bespoke playlists of video content, online diagnostic tools, peer-learning support and free or discounted digital equipment or services.

Instead of Government’s traditional detailed ‘one-off’ design and procurement process, Digital Boost was collaboratively designed and is constantly adapted through the application of Cynefin complexity theory and Agile programme management to respond to regular user feedback and research insights. 

The four key pillars/outcomes of the programme target SMEs at every stage of their digital journey:

1. Energise: small business owners are motivated and ready to adopt digital business tools and practices. videos showcase real-world case studies that offer an in-depth look at the experiences of small businesses that adopted digital tools and methods.
2. Educate: small business owners understand how digital tools and practices can enhance their business. free digital skills training platform available to any small business owner (or employee) who wants to accelerate their digital transformation
3. Equip: small businesses can access a wide range of trusted and relevant tools and services. Digital Boost Alliance is a collaboration between the Government and 47 private sector organisations focused on motivating and supporting small businesses, individuals, and communities to lift their use of digital technologies. 
4. Embed: digital tools, practices and thinking are embedded into small businesses. Digital Boost Checkable is a free tool that uses Artificial Intelligence and digital diagnostic tools to comprehensively analyse a business’ website and social media platforms.
Novelty: Digital Boost is a New Zealand first and follows an outcomes framework recognising key steps in a small business digitalisation journey.
Impact & Results: Evaluation results from August 2022 showed that 23% of businesses reported improved revenue after using Digital Boost, and for those who engage weekly with the platform, this figure increases to 39%. Additionally, 79% of users would recommend Digital Boost to others, 89% of users intend to continue using the platform, and long-term users note increases in digital capability.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres: http://www.digitalboost.co.nz (please login with digitalboost@mbie.govt.nz using password Test1472). Please note, our Checkable tool is geofenced for NZ users, but can be accessed via a VPN.

Description: The Carbon-Free Island JEJU by 2030 (CFI 2030) policy aims to transform Jeju into a carbon-free island by 2030. It is the first project in the world to target an area with a population of over 500,000 and has been ongoing since 2012. Home to the UNESCO-designated Biosphere Reserve (2002), World Natural Heritage Site (2007), and Global Geopark (2010), Jeju strives towards a carbon-neutral future utilizing state-of-art technology including renewable energy, EV, smart grids, and microgrids.
Impact & Results: Submission lists a lot of technical achievements in reduced carbon numbers.

Description:
Seocho-gu is the first district in Korea to offer a service like the “Seocho BookPayback Service” (hereafter, “BookPayback”), which reduces the waiting time for popular library books. A full refund is provided for a book purchased from a local bookstore that is returned (to the bookstore) within three weeks, after which the book is sent to a library in Seocho-gu. It is an innovative library service that allows residents to use library resources whenever needed, regardless of time or place.

Before this service, patrons who wished to borrow a newly-released book had to make a request to the library, which then purchased the book. This procedure took up to one month before the book was put into regular circulation.

To make BookPayback possible, Seocho-gu developed an independent service system. It also signed MOUs with local bookstores to create an environment that allows the stores to provide some of the functions of a local library.
Novelty: Seocho-gu was the first local government in Korea to devise BookPayback, which was recently patented.
Impact & Results: BookPayback increased the average amount of time residents spent reading. User numbers increased sharply in the early stages of the COVID-19 pandemic, reaching up to 1,991 book requests per month. In 2019, it received the grand prize in the Outstanding Civil Service Contest hosted by Seoul Metropolitan Government. In 2020, it received a ministerial citation from the Ministry of the Interior and Safety (MOIS) as an Outstanding Example of Improved Civil Service System that is Crucial to Citizens’ Lives.

Description:
Many students in villages and rural areas suffer from the lack of schools and teachers, in addition to the lack of textbooks. The first primary school students there also suffer from the lack of someone who reads them letters and words properly, especially since most of their parents are illiterate who do not know how to read and write. Further, many children are deaf-and-dumb, which prevents them from learning. The present innovation is the conversion of textbooks into programs and electronic games, to reduce dependence on the school, teacher and paper stationery, especially in rural and remote areas. 

The first interim step is converting the first-grade primary school book in Iraqi schools into a program and an electronic game. When you click on any letter or word, the program will pronounce it. In order for the program to be "talking" for deaf-and-dumb children as well, we have given each letter a special code, and when you press on the letter or word, the mobile device will vibrate according to the code of that letter, following Morse code.

In this program we have provided four types of virtual tools: digital textbook, digital blackboard, digital exams, and playground simulations. Thus, we produced a software simulation of the student’s school life and turned the textbook into an electronic program and game, reducing financial spending on the book, paper notebook and stationery, and dispenses with the presence of the school, teacher and parents in teaching the child to read and write. In addition, this innovation enables deaf-and-dumb children for the first time to sense letters and words through vibrations and not through pronunciation.
Novelty: This innovation provides students of remote areas an electronic and gamified version of the textbook. This also is able to teach deaf-and-dumb students how to read and write thanks to vibration.
Impact & Results: The number of users of the "Speaking Reading" program has exceeded more than 300,000 users, on the Google Play platform only, while the number of reviews and opinions of users has reached more than 500, and based on the opinions of those users, it is possible to know the extent of the program's impact and its positive results.

Description:
Implementation of free access to the world wide web (internet) through wireless access (Wi-Fi) with user authentication through login (gov.br) on the premises of administrative and operational units of the Federal Highway Police. The access will be made by the user, without the need to request a password to any server and/or employee connected to the agency, all that is required is a valid registration on the Gov.br platform. Access is granted for a period of one hour

Citizens who travel on federal highways cannot get fast and reliable internet access in the vicinity of the operational and administrative units of the Federal Highway Police. In many regions of Brazil, the 3G/4G internet signal is practically non-existent, and the Federal Highway Police units are, in some cases, the only points with access to the world wide web. Add to this the fact that some people simply do not have data plans or have limited data plans because of the costs involved in contracting internet plans with personal mobile service providers.
- The first problem to be overcome was to develop a solution that integrated the "GOV.BR" authentication with the wireless centralized management solution existing in the Federal Highway Police (Ruckus Controller).
- The second problem was to adapt the entire network infrastructure to maintain the security of the Federal Highway Police's internal network in all units of the agency. This adequacy involves changes in the logical network infrastructure, involving changes in settings of switches, routers and firewalls.
Novelty: This is a low cost initiative for the public administration, since it uses the existing information and communication technology infrastructure, and a high gain for the citizen, since the service is totally free, secure and stable. After implementation, there will be no increase in technology costs for the Federal Highway Police.
Impact & Results: PRF-PUBLICO wi-fi network available in 19 of the 27 Federative Units with complete implementation planned by the end of 2022.

Project/Initiative Name: EITA! - Public Challenges and the Encouragement of Open Innovation
Description:
Recife needed public policies to become a city open to innovation. The E.I.T.A! Recife was created and a process of cultural transformation is being conducted at City Hall to change the paradigm of how to do open innovation in public service for and with citizens. Recife was the first city in Brazil to sign 6 public contracts for innovation and to compose a hub of living labs. 

This movement is the Innovation and Open Transformation Squad of Recife's City Hall. From the creation of EITA Recife, a deep process of cultural transformation is being conducted among the departments of the City Hall in order to change the paradigm of how to do open innovation in public service. 

With this cultural transformation, a macro process was designed that goes from the identification of challenges, fundraising, launching of innovation cycles, to the management of the innovation process, based on four tracks: Innovation Cycles, Partnership with Academies, Hackathons and Living Labs. In a little over a year we are already having amazing results. EITA Recife instanced Design Thinking methods to involve all the secretaries, identify problems, hold interactive workshops to differentiate problems x challenges x solutions, prioritize and fill out the Canva of the public challenge (created by EITA Recife), in an agile, effective process that lasts approximately 3 months. Allied to all this, an open innovation policy with strategies based on OKR was elaborated. 

E.I.T.A! Recife is consolidating itself as an important hub of public innovation in the country. Recife was the first city in the country to sign at once, from a cycle of innovation, using the Legal Framework for Startups, 6 public contracts for innovation, with an estimated investment in the first year of 5.4 million reais.
Novelty:
Use agile techniques to implement a public policy of open innovation. To do this, we use John Kotter's 8 steps for change and culture management. We use the CANVA of exponential organizations to convey the vision of change clearly. 

The way of hiring innovative solutions, making a different bid, disruptive and unprecedented in the country (being the first city in Brazil to sign at once 6 contracts for innovation) using the legal framework of startups. The creation of a living labs, our EITA Labs, as a space for urban experimentation (regulatory sandbox).
Impact & Results: THE E.I.T.A! Recife is consolidating itself as an important hub of public innovation in the country. Six CPSI's have been signed, with an estimated investment in the first year of 5.4 million reais. A replication model was created to be used by other cities. Main deliverables: 1) Change management process; 2) Design to identify Public Challenges; 3) Open Innovation Policy; 4) Innovation Strategy using OKR; 5) Open Innovation Notice using LC 182; 6) Open Innovation Cycles Methodology; 7) Innovation Project Management Methodology; 8) 6 CPSI's signed; 9) Regulatory Sandbox; 10) Public Call Notice for urban experimentation; 11) Living Labs Hub - EMPREL and PTI; 12) 6 Innovation Solutions delivered to the city for experimentation: Integra. ai, L.I.A.H., Absens, SuperVision, Vamoo and Invisible Food.

Project/Initiative Name: Innovation Barueri - Technological development center, research and business incubator
