Organisation: Innovation and Technology Center of Barueri
Description:
The Smart City Barueri project is based on the definition that a smart city is one that uses Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) and other means to improve the quality of life, the efficiency of urban operations and services, and its competitiveness, while ensuring that the needs of current and future generations are met with regard to economic, social, and environmental aspects.

The CIT - Center for Innovation and Technology - has the role of promoting projects, partnerships and general actions that are in line with the above guidelines. The CIT has developed a Technology Master Plan, which contains more than 20 initiatives to be implemented or for the expansion of services in the city so that the citizen can, in short, obtain a better quality of life. All the initiatives of the Smart City Barueri project are aligned with the 17 UN Sustainable Development Goals, of which Brazil is a participant. 

Innovation Barueri - Technological Center for Research Development and Business Incubator is the initiative that concentrates, integrates and offers a set of mechanisms and tools for the development of scientific research, technology and innovation, also constituting a space for business-academic interaction for the development of solutions and provide socio-economic benefits to society, improve quality of life, leverage economic prosperity, promote the use of high-cost technologies and difficult access, encourage entrepreneurship and diversified development of creative solutions to the problems of the city.

Intended for technological research that meets the needs of the municipality, educational institutions, industries in the region, and residents. It contains a structure that encompasses: 1) SMARTLAB: Public Laboratory, 2) COWORKING: Sharing office space and resources, 3) TALENT LAUNCHING PROGRAM: Develops projects - public and private, 4) PRE-INCUBATION: Provides tools to support the user's project, 5) INCUBATION: In cycles, with groups selected among the pre-incubated, 6) COURSES AND TRAINING.
Novelty:
In the environment of Inovação Barueri several prototypes and new technologies are tested and deployed as the Metaverse. Currently, we have 4 environments in the metaverse (Innovation, Innovation and Technology Center and AAI - immersive learning environment in the metaverse). We also developed the avatar of the secretary of innovation and technology, Jonatas Randal, and all other secretaries of Barueri. 

In addition, we have Artificial Intelligence projects, which meets the main demands of Innovation Barueri within Messenger and Direct. We also have 3D Printer and Plotter, which perform projects daily, for example, we develop faceshields, mechanical hand, among others. We also use artificial intelligence to automatically create posts and videos for our social networks.
Impact & Results:
A September 2022 survey counted +160 Innovation projects, including: 
- 15 Innovators
- 11 Pre-Incubations and startups
- 20 projects with the 3D Printer
- 1 artificial intelligence in Messenger and Direct
- 4 virtual environments in the metaverse
- 13 projects in the metaverse
- 1 Smartlab with 3D printer, cutting plotter; toolboard, robotics kits, among others
- 7 augmented reality projects
- 528 thousand interactions/impressions on social networks
- 147 thousand views on the YouTube channel of TV Inovação Barueri
- 440 programs carried out
- 50 mentorships held with trainees

Project/Initiative Name: Development of an open innovation model for the Brazilian judicial sector
Novelty:
1) It promotes the application of user-centered methodologies for the development of innovative information technology solutions, which are not normally present in the software development context.
2) It enables the Superior Labor Court to add value to the software development sector, since the best practices applied in this program can be integrated into the court's software development process for future solutions.
3) Provides the development of new expertise for the resident students, previously the IT training had emphasis only on the software development process, and now the residents have expanded their knowledge in the application of innovative methodologies for product development, ranging from understanding the problem to validating the solution through the application of tools that aim to improve the user experience.

Project/Initiative Name: International Platform for Science, Technology and Innovation in Health (PICTIS)
Description:
The International Platform for Science, Technology and Innovation in Health (PICTIS), based in Europe, constitutes a new center for research, development, innovation and entrepreneurship. It materializes the first Brazil-European Union International Agreement fully structured based on the guidelines of the new Legal Framework for Science, Technology and Innovation. It is organized in laboratories specialized in generating innovative processes, products and services with special attention to the challenges of Global Health.

PICTIS is installed in the Science and Innovation Park (PCI Creative Science Park - Aveiro), with the objective of managing and participating in teaching, research, technological development, innovation and entrepreneurship activities in health, aiming at the generation of new products, processes and innovative services, as well as participating in the transfer and diffusion of new knowledge and technologies for the welfare of society.

The creation of this innovative International Platform established the presence of Fiocruz in a European Technology Park and already represents greater scientific, technological and productive articulation of Science and Technology Institutions in Brazil with other organizations of the innovation ecosystem in health in Portugal and other European countries, in line with the guidelines of the new Legal Framework for Science, Technology and Innovation in Brazil, especially with regard to the process of internationalization of federal institutions and also the strategic objectives of the European Union's Research and Innovation Program (Horizon Europe). 

PICTIS is structured in 6 sectoral laboratories specialized in generating processes, products and services with special attention to the Brazilian Unified Health System (SUS) and the Portuguese National Health Service (SNS).
Novelty:
The International Platform for Science, Technology and Innovation in Health (PICTIS) is the result of the first International Cooperation Agreement for Research, Development and Innovation in Health between Brazil and the European Union (EU), fully structured based on the guidelines of the new Legal Framework for Science, Technology and Innovation in Brazil and European Research and Innovation Program (Horizon Europe). 

PICTIS materialized Fiocruz's position in the EU in broad institutional dialogue with various global players and recorded a relevant and unique set of national and international supporters, as shown in the list of documents (letters of support and expressions of interest that are part of the International Agreement), as well as in official communications from the Delegation of the European Union in Brazil, the Embassy of Portugal in Brasilia, Brazilian and European universities, Organization of Ibero-American States (OEI), Directorate General for Research and Innovation of the European Commission (Brussels), among others.
Impact & Results:
The presence of Fiocruz through the International Platform at the PCI (Creative Science Park), in the Aveiro region, promoted greater scientific and technological articulation with organizations from the innovation ecosystem of Portugal and other European countries. 
PICTIS generated new exchanges of scientific knowledge, in the Brazil-EU relationship and expanded international participation in projects and scientific events of Fiocruz's Units, the integration with multilateral consortia of Research and Innovation, besides stimulating the cooperation already underway between Fiocruz, Universities and, especially, with institutions in Portugal and Spain, considering the performance of Fiocruz's researchers in projects at the University of Aveiro and its European partners.

Project/Initiative Name: Nidus' Problem-Focused Challenge Mapping Methodology and its aid for innovative procurement
Organisation: Secretariat of State Administration of the Government of the State of Santa Catarina
Novelty: The innovation proposed by the use of a proprietary methodology for mapping challenges and opportunities, allowed the problem to become the center of the process and not the search for "just one more system contract". With a deep immersion in the problem faced, a technical study that considers all the variables of the challenge brought by the public agency that seeks connection with Nidus, it was realized that most of these agencies did not need a new system, not even that their problems were susceptible to an innovative hiring, after all, most could be met by existing solutions on the market.
Impact & Results:
More efficient hiring, by applying a practical methodology for mapping challenges and opportunities that extracts the maximum amount of data about the problem experienced by the agency connected to the laboratory. The results were measured through questionnaires, surveys, and government indicators that measure the performance of the implemented actions. 

In numerical results, 64 challenges were run, 6 are in benchmarking, and 19 agencies have been attended to so far.

Project/Initiative Name: Artificial Intelligence Applied to the Decoding of Law
Description:
Creation of a functional prototype of a system, using AI, that assists the citizen in identifying and resolving issues related to consumer law and retirement status. INCM edits electronically the Diário da República (DR), the official newspaper of the Portuguese Republic, which is available exclusively on the portal Diário da República Eletrónico (DRE) at DRE.PT as a public service of universal and free access.

Access to the DRE allows consultation of all Portuguese Legislation, but it still has great potential for development in terms of Citizen services, particularly in services that allow the user to find the rules and procedures that relate to their particular need (the reason that led them to consult the legislation). 

The Project "Decoding the Legislation" results from partnerships between INCM and national scientific institutions and other government institutions, and intended to develop Artificial Intelligence tools for the DRE, facilitating consultation by citizens. Repositories were implemented with data sources and metadata from the DRE and, using natural language processing, the legal attributes are transformed into code language, and a web application was developed that allows intelligent queries to a particular law. 

The case studies chosen to test the AI tools are the Consumer Rights Legislation and the Retirement Statute. A proof of concept was carried out, which will be refined in the future, where the DRE can have an intelligent search mechanism where Citizens can ask questions about these sets of legislation and get answers in an automatic way.
Novelty:
The main innovation of the project is to make legal rules consumable by digital systems, so that they can be used by services and citizens. The instantiation of two concrete practical cases: the access to the Consumer Rights and the Retirement Statute, allows to give answers to questions that a normal citizen asks, without having great technical knowledge about the mentioned laws.

The adoption and availability of these new paradigms will be a key component in the digital transformation of governments, particularly as we seek to integrate service delivery, automate the exchange of information and decisions, ensuring government transparency, responsiveness and accountability.
Impact & Results:
Regarding the functional prototype of the Retirement Laws, a conversation tool was developed that will incorporate the most relevant conversation flows in the selected context; the conversation flows according to the rules derived previously and the development of a mechanism for detecting changes in legislation.

Regarding the Consumer Law prototype, further testing demonstrated that the Legal Semantic Search Engine (LeSSE) can improve, even without available query examples, using a technique developed in the context of this project. With this technique, a semantic representation is automatically extracted from legal texts, and then used to automatically generate sets of semantic pairs (questions/answers) that will be used later.

Project/Initiative Name: Collaborative Robotics in Security Document Production
Description:
Use of robotics to create an autonomous intralogistics system, allowing the personalization and automatic dispatch of security documents. The current INCM's production processes associated with the whole life cycle of the CC and PEP production need improvements, in terms of efficiency, going through the personalization phase of the PEP and the CC and ending with the expedition of these products to their respective destinations. The main efficiency losses are due to transition tasks between machines and operators and the current shipping logistics, which leads to increased costs due to poor optimization of times and processes. In this context, the biggest saving of this project comes from the possibility of incorporating in a single autonomous system different work packages of several production and shipping processes, with a greater impact on the savings related to shipping.

The project is based on the study and adoption of advanced collaborative technologies according to the man-machine paradigm in order to significantly benefit the efficiency of INCM's productive processes and also the work environment for the operators of the manufacturing area.

This project aims, therefore, to be at the international frontline in the modernization of traditional production processes (security printing and shipping, in this case), modernizing them with interactive machines and collaborative robotics, creating an agile and flexible production ecosystem, articulating people, robots and logical data systems in an integrated way, under the assumption of compliance with the quality and safety standards applicable to the production process and in force at INCM.

It is also noteworthy that the final solution of this project fits the new paradigm of Industry 4.0, by adopting modular and flexible industrial strategies of collaborative environments, joining human operators and autonomous robots and human-aware, to achieve demanding and efficient production goals.
Novelty:
The project becomes internationally innovative by validating the concept of using robotics to create an autonomous intralogistics system for managing the production cycle and shipping security documents, ensuring full integration in the production system of the INCM factory. 
Moreover, all this process is emulated in a Digital Twin of the existing processes/products, and of the factory area itself, allowing to configure and simulate the impact of changes that may be introduced in the life cycle of the process and production.
Impact & Results:
As main results achieved, it should be mentioned the complete integration with the INCM production system and the fact that the project deliverables are fully compatible with the Industry 4.0 standards (Digital Twin implemented, of the process and of the factory, and compatibility with the RAMI 4.0 Model).

A 100% rate was also achieved in the autonomy of the navigation systems and the total absence of human intervention in transport or material handling in the customization and dispatch of documents.

In analytical terms, the initial economic feasibility study, updated with the most recent assumptions (reduction of the number of documents initially planned and savings in shipping and human resources, changed accordingly), predicts the break-even of the project in a maximum of 14 years, a value considered to be the indicated for this type of products, but that may be reduced with the adoption of this technology in international projects, underway in INCM.

Description:
The relevance of public procurement in municipal activity led to the development of an internal portal - the Collaborative Procurement Portal - to bring together all those who work in this area. With this portal, Lisbon City Hall intends to foster an environment of trust, knowledge and sharing, and to strengthen the mission of its workers and the ties that unite them, even in a digital environment.

This Portal has been used since its launch to encourage employees to invest in the development of their skills, whether specific to public procurement or related areas such as sustainability, innovation, circularity and digital transition. In fact, the agility required for public procurement today requires equally agile and qualified means and resources. 

The Collaborative Procurement Portal has benefited all managers, lawyers, contract managers, interlocutors, among many others who participate in the procurement function and, ultimately, the dynamics of the Lisbon City Council itself.

In this Portal, three central characteristics stand out: flexibility, scalability and autonomy. 
- Since it was launched on May 16th, 2022, flexibility, one of the application's own characteristics, has already allowed the inclusion of additional pages to the Portal, such as the dissemination center of the ISO 20400 - sustainable procurement implementation project, developed with autonomy by the team itself. It has also allowed the redesign of the presentation of some content, making navigation more appealing and intuitive. 
- Scalability will allow other core areas of the municipality's activity, such as finance and human resources, to have their own portals and, in the medium term, the Collaborative Purchasing Portal itself will allow accommodating the dynamics of a new organizational model for municipal purchasing: the future municipal purchasing network. 
- Autonomy was acquired from the moment it was decided that the development of the Portal would use mostly native Microsoft Sharepoint tools, which means that the aforementioned changes have already been made by the portal's content managers, without external support.
Novelty:
The Collaborative Purchasing Portal is innovative because:
1) It involved workers in the co-creation of the prototype and in usability tests, allowing easy and intuitive navigation, both on pc and in its use via app on cell phone or tablet.
2) Exploits, at no additional cost, the applications included in the license that the lisbon City Council signed with Microsoft.
3) Centralizes, standardizes and shares knowledge of a neuralgic matter for the organization, as it is one of the 10 largest national public buyers and has an influential role in the supply chain of its suppliers, especially with regard to the pursuit of climate action and sustainable development goals.
Impact & Results: The Portal was launched internally on May 16, 2022, and since that date the daily unique accesses have been increasing, monitored using native SharePoint metrics. Other available monitoring is the average time spent and on what content. The results allow us to conclude that navigation on the Portal already includes regular consultation of central documentation for public procurement, as well as daily viewing of news released.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres: Being an internal Portal we do not have a web site available.

Description:
The Carioca Programmers is a public policy developed by the City of Rio de Janeiro based on a study that found that there is a deficit of 24,000 vacancies per year in the technology sector. Thus, the initiative aims to train and qualify socially vulnerable young people in the area of programming. The program is aimed at refugees and public school graduates with complete high school education, with priority given to blacks, women, and the transgender population.

The initiative to implement a program to train programmers stems then from a thorough analysis of the behavior of the employment sectors. Despite the scenario of falling economic activity and job numbers, not all sectors of the economy suffered a decline during the pandemic. The technology sector, for example, which had already been growing consistently in recent years and had a demand for professionals that exceeded supply, was boosted. A study by the Association of Information and Communication Technology Companies projects that in the period 2019-2024, the labor shortage in the ICT sector will be 24,000 professionals per year. Added to this already existing labor deficit, Rio's investment in the creation of a technology hub will increase the demand for programmers in the city. 

The Carioca Programmers aims to increase the speed with which new programmers graduate. This is intended to mitigate the labor shortage and generate a positive social impact for the city. The program will offer: i) an online platform that advertises job openings in technology, with information about the courses accepted as required qualifications, and information about courses and the financing options they offer; ii) for low-income youth with less ability to obtain there will be private financing to be provided by the city government to take proven courses.
Novelty:
The Carioca Programmers initiative will offer full and partial scholarships to refugees and public school graduates who have completed high school and are between the ages of 17 and 29. With priority given to blacks, women and transgender people, the goal is to train five thousand new programmers by 2024, with an investment of R$ 50 million. 

Besides receiving full or partial scholarships, the student also receives financial aid of R$ 500 for six months and a computer. In this first cycle (2022), the goal will be to train 1,200 professionals. Of these, 200 will receive full scholarships. Those awarded the 50% scholarship will pay the remaining amount after completing the course and, if they do not find a job within five years, the partner institution will assume the cost.
Impact & Results: The goal of the Program is to reduce the labor deficit in the technology area in the city of Rio de Janeiro. Impacts cannot yet be widely analyzed, since the classes have just started. However, we already have the first case of a student hired by a technology company.

Organisation: (011).lab - Innovation in Government Laboratory of the City of São Paulo
Description:
CopiCola addresses the problem of knowledge loss in the public sector by investing in the registration of initiatives that can inspire public servants, facilitate processes, and reduce risks. The program identifies, registers, and disseminates innovative practices with the potential to be adopted in different public management contexts. Knowledge is passed from one public servant to another, facilitating the connection between actors and enhancing learning from registered cases.

CopiCola was born from an effort to prototype a methodology for registering innovative initiatives at the São Paulo City Hall. After an experimental phase, the program was consolidated with the objective of documenting innovative practices in municipal public administration, focusing on facilitating its replication in other contexts and valuing the knowledge of civil servants.

The program identifies, registers, and disseminates innovative practices with the potential to be adopted in different public management contexts. The team seeks to understand the problem that the civil servants tried to solve, to map out what resources and processes were needed to develop the solutions, and to highlight the real challenges faced during implementation. These learnings are presented through didactic and attractive narratives, built in a collaborative way. 

The program was implemented in cycles:
1. Prototyping - development of the research methodology, based on interviews, questionnaires, conversations, and existing documents. 
2. Implementation Pilot - an initial three-month contract was made with a research institute to test the CopiCola shared management model.
3. Implementation - a public notice was issued to hire a civil society organization that could develop the program in the same shared management molds as the partnership experience.
Novelty: #NAME?
Impact & Results:
Quantitative:
- 174 participants
- 21 registered initiatives, involving 18 municipal bodies
- 13,300 views on the site from more than 276 cities
- 942 downloads of the materials
- 19 events of dissemination of the innovative practices
- 513 participants in presential events
- 1,311 views of the launching videos on Youtube

Qualitative
An initiative was fully replicated by the municipality of Niterói (Rio de Janeiro) through the guide "How to encourage choices through communication with the population? The Nudges case in the collection of IPTU". The replication of the initiative generates savings in resources, because it is possible to mobilize knowledge and evidence already registered, and it is not necessary to "reinvent the wheel" for the solution of the public problem.

Project/Initiative Name: 1st Round of Work for the Homeless Population of the City of São Paulo (Pop Rua Jud)
Description:
The INSS - National Institute of Social Security - built a team to receive administrative requests and to perform medical examinations, which took place at the premises of the Brazilian Bar Association - OAB, located nearby. The Federal Special Court of São Paulo also had volunteers to process lawsuits and perform complementary medical examinations. 

The initiative guaranteed to a large number of homeless people the access to citizenship, to public services and to the Judiciary, facilitating conciliation and speeding up the judicial provision. Sentences were handed down, judicial agreements ratified, and payment orders issued on the same day. The initiative has already been replicated by several other courts, will be repeated in São Paulo next November and is also being treated as an experiment to institutionalize a permanent flow of assistance to homeless people.
Novelty: The gathering of public agencies related to the provision of judicial services, such as issuance of documents/certificates, payment of social security/assistance benefits, assistance to immigrants, etc., by means of a single and iterative flow, enables referral to the various services that are in the same place. Volunteers direct people to the right services. Legal counseling, registration in social programs, job vacancies, and specialized counseling for women, LGBTQIA+, immigrants, and former inmates of the prison system were offered. The innovation occurs in providing infrastructure, communication and articulation for joint action of the various actors to serve the homeless population.
Impact & Results:
In the Mutirão held between March 15 and 17, 2022, almost 9,000 people were attended. Forms were filled out with various data. The data was then tabulated and a final meeting was held between the partners to assess what was positive and should be replicated, and the gaps that need to be filled. 

The network created for the Mutirão remains in full operation, continuing the collaborative work to identify the best way to serve the homeless population in a permanent way.

Project/Initiative Name: Acolhe Program - Private Social Investment Fund to End Violence Against Women and Girls
Description:
The Acolhe Program is innovative because:
- It offers lodging from the private network for women in situations of violence in more than 230 municipalities in Brazil in partnership with the municipal networks for confronting violence
- Besides offering hospitality, the program acts on the municipal referral flow, demanding a public response to the problem and offering qualification
- It offers customized responses for the women assisted

The Private Social Investment Fund was created to support women during the COVID-19 Pandemic. During the period of social estrangement, women faced even more difficulties in seeking protection in the public service. There was an increase in violence and feminicide in Brazil grew 20% in the worst period of the pandemic.
The main innovation of the Acolhe Program is the public-private partnership for hosting women in situations of violence on a national scale. This has never been done in Brazil before, connecting the public policy of confronting violence with a private network of hotels that accommodate women and their sons and daughters, while the public power prepares an action plan to make it possible for the victim to leave the situation of violence permanently.

The goal of the program is to strengthen public policies, offering alternative accommodation for women, helping the network to identify weaknesses in the assistance and reduce the recurrence of violence and feminicide. The program's goal is to donate 4200 hotel daily rates for women, reach 150 municipalities in Brazil, and implement the risk questionnaire in 100% of the municipalities that use the program. The program is implemented through the signing of Technical Cooperation Agreements with states with direct effect for the municipalities with Accor hotels, as well as nearby municipalities.
Novelty:
The Acolhe Program is innovative because besides offering lodging to women in hotels in more than 230 municipalities in Brazil, through public-private cooperation agreements, it serves as a gateway to delve into the barriers and weaknesses of the municipal networks that serve women in situations of violence.
Besides supporting and strengthening the public service and offering training to the agents of the network, the program also works as a social control when the network does not offer a response to the women victims of violence, triggering the responsible authorities and demanding an action from the public entity.
Impact & Results:
We keep close track of the attendances performed, but we also collect evaluations from the program users as well as the public partners.
Results so far:
- To date, 2145 accommodations have been donated, in 100 municipalities.
- 183 women with 223 companions have been hosted.
- The program has already provided 625 consultations to the participating municipalities.
- 1522 technicians from the networks have been trained in the use of the program and risk assessment
- 238 municipalities qualified to use the program, in 22 states

Description:
LA-BORA! gov arose from the need to improve the well-being and engagement of public servants. Created in December 2019, by the civil servants themselves, it is the first innovation lab in government focused on improving the civil servant experience. It is a space for learning and innovation with the purpose of supporting the creation of innovative solutions in people management, bringing servants closer to citizens, and supporting leaders to work with an empathetic vision of reality and focus on people.

People management areas are historically understood as bureaucratic and process-focused places. Moreover, most of the actions taken to improve the well-being of our employees refer to specific activities, such as yoga, meditation, and choir classes. They are important, but they work as mitigators, relieving stress, physical pain and mental suffering, for example, which have much deeper causes.

We have created an innovation lab in government, where employees are empowered and are part of the solution, create a sense of responsibility, and become more actively connected to the process. The result is greater engagement and productivity. The co-creation and the users' perspective were in the construction of the lab from the beginning: the name (LA-BORA! gov), the purpose, the values, and even the service charter were designed with the civil servants in interactive and immersive workshops. 

From the feedbacks of the civil servants, I understood that there is no lack of skills in the public sector, and no lack of interest from the civil servants in learning and transforming. We don't want to imitate robots at work. However, there is a lack of psychological safety environments for people and ideas to connect and for civil servants to put their socio-behavioral competencies into practice. Such innovative environments, fostering our human nature and skills, are not common in public administration.
Novelty: The solutions are simple and accessible to employees and co-created with them, using innovation approaches. Our actions are based on evidence and research that demonstrates the needs of public servants. An example of this was research with the People in Government Lab at Oxford University on well-being and engagement in remote work. Our services break with the old-fashioned perspective of punctual actions, such as extrinsic incentives and benefits as a form of engagement.
Impact & Results: Entre janeiro de 2020 e junho de 2022, o LA-BORA! gov realizou 214 serviços, entre oficinas, mentorias, palestras, cursos e rodas de conversa, nas modalidades presencial e virtual. Mais de 35 mil servidores foram diretamente impactados, de mais de 80 órgãos diferentes do Setor público brasileiro, além de mais de 120 mil visualizações assíncronas das palestras disponíveis no canal do laboratório do youtube.

Project/Initiative Name: Interinstitutional Virtual Platform for Environmental and Indigenous Demands
Description:
The Corumbá/MS Pilot-Project of the Federal Regional Court of the 3rd Region (TRF3) involved initiatives to prevent environmental damage and reduce the judicialization of conflicts, based on inter-institutional articulation, use of technology, and analysis of available databases in several platforms related to this matter.

The pilot project was inspired by the CNJ's Sirenejud platform, which brings data on deforestation, degradation, and fires in the Pantanal wetlands and its cross-referencing with data from forests and indigenous lands, information that rarely reaches the judicial process in a qualified and organized way, nor is it known by the institutions involved in the inspection, prevention, and repression of environmental violations.

The pilot project involved, among other initiatives, articulation in the TRF3's Virtual Interinstitutional Environmental and Indigenous Platform to enable the exchange of information about the measures taken by each agency to prevent and fight fires in the Pantanal region, as well as sharing technical data about the damaged areas, discussing the difficulties faced and jointly building solutions.

Similarly, the work done in the TRF3's Virtual Environmental and Indigenous Platform is essential to help the institutions involved in environmental litigation to seek solutions in a joint, articulated and preventive manner.
It is the Judiciary as a vehicle to encourage the joint action of the various federal entities and independent institutions involved with environmental issues. The Corumbá Project also signaled the importance of bringing together actions related to the same environmental damage, in order to obtain a consensual solution to the conflicts with priority to reparation.
Novelty: The project is innovative because the Judiciary acts as a vehicle to encourage the joint action of the various federal entities and independent institutions involved with environmental issues, making use of technological resources and the analysis and cross-referencing of data with other information, such as Sirenejud, land registry offices, or geo-referential reports.
Impact & Results: A group composed of technical and legal experts was formed to develop studies that will point out the relevant circumstances for the valuation of the climate damage and the quantification of eventual reparation.

Description:
The Secretariat of Management - SEGES provides an environment of inter-institutional cooperation for the implementation of the TransformaGov.BR Program through the creation of a collaborative network in the units of the federation. The TransformaGov.BR Network brings together representatives of the agencies and entities that have units outside Brasilia in order to promote the exchange of experiences, level governance and management practices, and to facilitate the implementation of administrative efficiency solutions.

The work through the implementation of management and cooperation networks in all the federal states over the last 3 years, through which an ecosystem capable of fostering state actions of a collaborative nature can be created, in interaction environments that involve decentralized units of organs and entities belonging to the APF, may prove to be an opportunity to consolidate the principles of efficiency and economy, optimizing the implementation of public policies, giving more quality to public spending, delivering more value to society, as well as establishing new ways of narrating results and managing projects, reducing heterogeneities within the APF.

The potential for continuity and strengthening of networked action by the Brazilian State and, also, the future possibility of expanding it to involve other federal entities and even representatives of civil society, may be an important step in the evolution of the State and the processes of process and project management by results focused on the citizen.
Novelty:
In support of the Strategic Management and Institutional Transformation Program - TransformaGov, which contemplates simplification and transformation measures in the organs and entities of the APF, SEGES, through its Government Transformation Department, created and maintains an inter-institutional cooperation environment: a set of collaborative networks that are currently present in 23 units of the federation, called TransformaGov.BR Networks. 

The Networks bring together representatives of the organs and entities with administrative units located outside Brasilia/DF, promoting the exchange of experiences and good practices in governance and management, as well as enabling the implementation of administrative efficiency solutions.
Impact & Results:
SEGES was able to test and confirm the relevance of the network model as a fundamental strategy to support the SUs in each of the states.
In addition, the promotion and diffusion of a collaborative culture acted strongly:
- Mobilization for administrative transformation and simplification
- Rationalization of resource use and cost reduction; 
- Knowledge management about public management in the decentralized units 
- Valuing knowledge, encouraging the sharing of experiences and good practices;
- Capacity building, workshops, impacted workers, affected agencies and partner organizations

Description:
Support local governments to develop transformative solutions to challenges related to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). With this purpose, the Brazilian Government created the Cities that Transform initiative, which combines the expertise of the National School of Public Administration in innovation approaches with the privileged knowledge of the Prefectures about the local reality, in projects aimed at generating concrete solutions with an impact on the lives of people in the cities.

GNova/Enap created an innovation journey inspired by design thinking to, together with municipal teams, explore public problems and challenges through immersion activities in reality, ideation and prototyping of solutions aligned with citizens' needs and demands.

To select the challenges to be supported, GNova/Enap used a simple and isonomic mechanism, through a public call for projects. To participate, municipalities of various sizes registered more than 100 initiatives, providing information about the public problem and its relationship with the SDGs; the potential for impact and innovation; and the existence of political and staff sponsorship in the municipality to participate in the initiative.
Novelty:
Cities that Transform is an innovative technical and methodological advisory service to municipal governments because:
1) generates agile and collaborative formulation of solutions (prototypes; governance mechanisms; services, etc.) to address complex problems in cities, with high potential to positively impact the achievement of SDGs;
2) develops innovation competencies in municipal employees, based on practical experience with field research techniques and methodological approaches such as design thinking
3) values municipal employees as protagonists in the development of innovative solutions to complex problems that affect their territories; and
4) connects municipal governments with the Agenda 2030 ecosystem, expanding their knowledge about sustainable development and how to internalize this agenda in the municipality
Impact & Results: Among the tangible results, we highlight the creation of solutions for the challenges presented: (i) the construction of a proposal for inclusive digitalization of public services (Ijuí); (ii) elaboration of an urban mobility agenda (Botucatu); (iii) sustainable development agenda (Abaetetuba); (iv) prototyping of a digital interface for application in municipal processes of social participation (Porto Alegre); (v) construction of governance mechanisms to coordinate municipal interventions for the reduction of territorial inequalities (Fortaleza). (The other projects have not yet been completed, so they do not have solutions developed).

Description:
Sandbox.Rio is the first controlled experimental environment in a Brazilian capital city to test innovative solutions that do not fit into the pre-existing regulatory environment. From the tests, information is collected that helps the City of Rio to understand the innovations, for a regulatory framework receptive and adherent to new technologies. In addition, the database generated assists the design of public policies in Rio, which establishes itself as a model smart city in Brazil.

The Sandbox.Rio is a project that aims to improve the regulatory framework of the Municipality of Rio de Janeiro through the development of a controlled test environment for experimentation with new technologies. Through it, services and products innovations are tested on a temporary basis, by means of removal from the applicable regulatory framework and under the control of a state entity, in order to observe their effects in practice and thus build a set of normative guidelines based on the evidence produced by the experimentation. 

Sandbox.Rio was thought as a solution to transpose the concept of sandbox to the reality of the Municipality of Rio de Janeiro. Its conception was made possible through research and dialogues that included (i) benchmark done nationally and internationally; (ii) meetings with members of academia and experts on the subject; (iii) dialogue with companies that work with innovation to verify the adherence to the program and (iv) analysis of the adequacy of the sandbox idea to the context of the Municipality. The approved projects, because they involve public areas to be ceded, are now going through a phase of definition of the implementation sites and form of monitoring.
Novelty: As mentioned, the project is the first sandbox to be implemented in a Brazilian capital city and does so without thematic restrictions, allowing companies from different sectors to participate. Furthermore, from the testing of innovative solutions, information is collected to help the City Hall in understanding the innovations, ensuring a regulatory framework receptive and adherent to new technologies. In addition, the data generated during the testing period will help public managers in the design of public policies of interest to the municipality.
Impact & Results: The sandbox makes it possible to reduce by up to 3 times the cost of creating and validating a product, service or process and launching it in the market. In addition, the thematic plurality of the projects demands specific monitoring and evaluation methodologies. All projects will be accompanied by interactive and simultaneous dashboards. From the reports generated, the data will be analyzed and the final technical report will adopt the most appropriate methodologies for each case, such as cost-benefit, cost-effectiveness, and multicriteria analyses, among others.

Description:
"Star Trek" is the JFPR's formative itinerary for Innovation. The name of the project is an allusion to the famous movie production, "Star Trek", for its classic and futuristic character, which matches the avant-garde spirit of JFPR and its significant translation of a path towards possible futures.

Star Trek" is the training itinerary in Innovation, for all the internal public of JFPR
The name is an allusion to the famous film production "Star Trek", classic and futuristic, allied to the significant translation of a path towards possible futures.

The Journey will develop in 3 axes/"chapters", until 2025:
- First Contact - beginners 
- No Boundaries - updating
- Next Generation - training new laboratorians

Heutagogy as basis: People manage their own route, choosing the themes according to their degree of maturity in innovation and knowledge gaps. Classes are always in workshop format, with deliverables at the end. Everyone benefits and the goal is to reach the entire staff of the Federal Justice of Paraná and, furthermore, share vacancies with other institutions.
Novelty: The uniqueness of learning, and its potential to expand and transform the culture of innovation through learning.
Impact & Results: Train 100% of the internal public and transform the culture for innovation.

Project/Initiative Name: The arts and the judiciary: clear communication at the service of people
Description:
This is the elaboration of a simplified version of a judicial sentence, with the objective of facilitating the understanding of an important decision issued by the judiciary, in the state of Maranhão - Northern Brazil.  The innovation solves the problem of the lack of understanding about decisions issued by the judiciary; the simplification of the language represents a way to enable people to have access to the right to understand.

The innovation aims to solve the problem of lack of understanding in communication between the judiciary and civil society. The innovation came from an experimental act: to use the graphic facilitation technique to facilitate the understanding of a judicial sentence, namely: a public civil action, therefore: a decision of interest to more than 50,000 people, inserted in the dimensions: environmental and public health.

Objectives and goals: to create a visual standard and implement it in a systematized way, we could standardize images in a system, for example: that generates a simplified version of the document. This would make it possible to replicate it on a larger scale.
Novelty:
It has two really innovative aspects:
- Aspect 1: it is an open innovation, fruit of the interaction between a judge of law and an artist. Therefore, the junction of Law and Art, Judiciary + Civil Society, two people connected by the intention of solving the problem of lack of understanding about decisions issued by the judiciary.
- Aspect 2: To provide understanding to people, in a context where the level of education of people is very low, therefore the visual language, the drawings are a form of social inclusion.
Impact & Results:
The innovation generated the impact of people understanding for the first time a decision issued by the judiciary.
The expected results and impacts are that: by increasing the level of people's understanding of justice, people will have more conditions and possibilities to exercise their rights and duties as citizens.
Another possibility is to standardize, through the use of information systems, this visual language on an institutional level.

Project/Initiative Name: Farishte Dilli Ke - Empowering Citizens Through Accessible and Reliable Emergency Healthcare Services
Organisation: Dialogue and Development Commission of Delhi
Description:
Farishte Dilli Ke (Hindi for Saviours of Delhi) uses innovative fiscal and non-fiscal incentives to encourage citizens to become Good Samaritans by helping victims of road traffic accidents, thermal burns and acid attacks without the fear of legal hassle or police enquiry. Under this scheme, the victims receive cashless treatment at any registered public or private nursing home or hospital across Delhi benefitting more than 18,919 people in the last 5 years.

Under the scheme, the Delhi government provides a reward of Rs. 2000 and a certificate of appreciation to the good samaritans who bring accident victims to the hospital. The accident victim can be admitted to any public or private hospital across Delhi, the government covers all medical costs of the victim and in case a hospital refuses to admit a patient, their license may be canceled. If the victim desires to change the hospital within the first 72 hours of the accident, then the government will pay for the bills at both the hospitals along with the ambulance expenses.

The sustainability of the scheme in the long run is ensured by the institutionalization of the Delhi Arogya Kosh, a state level fund set up under the State Illness Assistance Fund. The Delhi Arogya Kosh formalizes cashless treatment of medico-legal victims of RTAs, burns and acid attacks as one of the four fundamental schemes under its purview, thereby ensuring a sustained flow of financial and administrative support to the Farishte Dilli Ke Scheme in the long run.
Novelty: Farishte Dilli Ke is an innovative approach to road safety and emergency medical services that is less based on punishment of by-standers and more on positively incentivizing the good in people. Despite the monetary incentives for the good Samaritan, a key hurdle that disincentivised people from admitting road accident victims was the doubt around the bearing of medical expenditure. Under ‘Farishte Dilli Ke’, all monetary expenses of the victim are borne by the state Government, irrespective of whether the hospital is public or private, or whether the citizen belongs to Delhi or a different state.
Impact & Results: The direct impact of the Farishte Dilli Ke Scheme is currently measured in terms of the number of people who received cashless emergency medical services via the Delhi Arogya Kosh. Between 2018 to June 2022, more than 18,919 people have been benefited and numerous others rewarded for being good Samaritans. In the past five years, many citizens have also refused the monetary reward citing the invaluable feeling they experienced by doing the virtuous act of saving someone’s life.

Project/Initiative Name: Biciclando - Ecofriendly Employment Opportunities for Women in Recycling
Description:
Hermosillo faces two significant challenges: First, women are severely underemployed—it is estimated that pandemic-related job losses have impacted two women for every man. And, second, the city currently only recycles 2 percent of its waste. In response, the city is creating “Biciclando,” a green employment program for women that uses circular-economy principles to address both challenges through a single intervention. 

The application is promoted by the government of Hermosillo, where there are plans to transform the city's infrastructure to facilitate the work of female employees. It is a system of home collection of recyclable waste, through the use of an App, and a network of adapted electro-assisted bicycles, manned by women, who will serve as the recycling base of a sustainable, non-polluting fleet.
Novelty: It is an innovative proposal to connect the collection and recycling points by enabling a network and an app of women-led recycling team.
Impact & Results: The project is running its first pilot.

Project/Initiative Name: YH Rikx - A Marketplace to hire vulnerable residents
Description:
The city of Rotterdam partnered with the Rebel consultancy group to create a new digital marketplace "Rikx" that connects local social entrepreneurs with investors to incentivize hiring vulnerable residents. Rikx measures the impact that social entrepreneurs make with their projects and converts this into financial value with Rikx digital tokens which can be purchased by private-sector partners.

Companies with a CSR policy or SROI obligation can make a meaningful investment via Rikx in one of the projects of the participating social entrepreneurs, who are then guaranteed to make an impact with this investment in the places where it is most needed.

Rikx can serve as a supplement to the subsidy schemes and Social Return instruments that are currently used. In this way we are creating a society in which social entrepreneurship is the key to making the world a little better..
Novelty: A new digital marketplace that connects local social entrepreneurs to investors so that they can deliver innovative projects, while helping the city’s most vulnerable residents find work

Description:
Yes, We Rent! transforms private rental market by creating a new agent working in partnership with the municipality (hybridization of public policies). The project takes on two of the challenges facing local housing policy: How can private rental property – particularly property that has been vacant and off the market for an extended period – be used to provide affordable housing? How can the potential of community initiatives and cooperatives be harnessed to generate and develop affordable housing? As part of the Yes, We Rent! initiative, Mataró city council initiated and supported the creation of the tenants’ cooperative "Bloc Cooperatiu".

“Bloc cooperatiu” is an unprecedented cooperative that gather together tenants willing to search, renovate and rent collectively scattered empty flats, as a way to (self) generate affordable and stable housing targeting low-to-medium income households. It is already managing 61 flats and 105 members. It was created with the idea to become a new agent on the local housing market with the potential to reconfigure the balance of power between landlords and tenants.

"Bloc cooperatiu" consists of a "top-down" impulse by the city council to set up and provide start-up finance for a mixed multi-stakeholder cooperative; to generate small benefits out of the usage of private flats and to build capacity to increment market control with each new flat that is under the control of the coop; and to empower tenants to take over the cooperative once the housing scheme is running.
Novelty: Yes We Rent! proposes the idea of renovating empty flats of private owners and renting them out below market prices to tenats that form a cooperative. This new model of public-communitary cooperation increases the flats supply and the affordability of rental housing, faster than if the municipality would had to build a social houses.
Impact & Results: 61 flats have been renovated and rented with a rent minimum 30% below market prices (1m€ investment). 24 vulnerable youngsters have been trained in renovation skills. An unprecedented cooperative of tenants has been set up to manage the scheme (105 members so far) as new instrument for housing policies. An innovative public-communitarian governance scheme to generate affordable housing has been set up, empowering community to (self)generate affordable housing. Qualitative and quantitative evaluation has been conducted by the two research institutes involved in the consortium.

Project/Initiative Name: Marram-Ngala Ganbu, Koori (First Nations) Hearing Days
Description:
Aboriginal Children in Australia are 11.5 times more likely to be placed in out of home care then non-Aboriginal Children. Marram-Ngala Ganbu is an Aboriginal designed and delivered Court based program that seeks to provide a more effective, culturally appropriate and just response for Aboriginal families through a court process that enables greater participation by family members and culturally-informed decision-making.

Through consultation with families, many simple and common sense changes were made to the court process - including adapting the court setting to allow families and young people to sit around the table and speak at the same level as magistrates, and offering support before, during and after court from Koori staff who built relationships with families and into the community.
Novelty: Marram-Ngala Ganbu is an Australian first for culturally appropriate court process for Child protection matters.
Impact & Results: The Projects development was Koori led and in an independent evaluation in 2019 MNG was found to be providing providing a more effective, culturally appropriate and just response for Koori families through a more culturally appropriate court process, that enables greater participation by family members and more culturally-informed decision-making.

Project/Initiative Name: Dialogue between the city & its people: guide to civic participation in public space projects
Description:
By involving the public in the design of public spaces (parks, plazas, streets etc.) municipalities can create spaces that better suit society's interests. According to the data, 58 out of 60 municipalities in Lithuania recognize the benefits of public participation, but 50 % of them say they don't have the knowledge for it. The guide "Dialogue between the city & its people" is a step-by-step textbook for municipalities that helps execute the participation process from start to finish.

The Dialogue between the city and its people: a practical guide for municipalities on public participation" is the first Lithuanian textbook for municipality specialists that provides a methodology on how to involve the public in public space projects. The goal of the guide is to enable municipality specialists to plan & implement public participation as well as support them in the process of it. The guide is based on several other similar publications and policy recommendations in the field.
Novelty:
The guide "Dialogue between the city & its people" is innovative because: 

1) It has a practical step-by-step method which allows a specialist without a former background or experience in public participation to implement such a process. 

2) The guide addresses all the legal procedures set up by the national law that regulate the formation of public places. By doing so it shows how to design a quality public participation process that would complement formal procedures. 

3) During its creation, the methodology presented in the guide was tested in 5 public space projects by creating public participation plans and implementing 4 of them. The guide features these case studies and provides real-life examples of how the guide could look in practice.
Impact & Results:
The main results of the guide are actualised public participation events that were planned and organised following the methodology set out in the guide. Interviews with participants of these events have shown that all of the groups evaluate such meetings positively and would like to participate again. 

It is expected that the guide will be adopted and used in practice by 3 municipalities in Lithuania by the end of 2022. Also, the guide is expected to add to the cultural shift and support the Ministry of Environment in its plans to continue growing participatory culture in public space projects.

Project/Initiative Name: The SOLF-Seoul Olympic Legacy Forum, Beyond Olympic legacy, becoming global value
Description:
KSPO has created the SOLF, Seoul Olympic Legacy Forum as a platform where  Olympic legacy entities all around the world gather for the first time to share and collaborate on sustainable development plans for the Olympic Legacy. Through the SOLF, Olympic legacy entities can communicate and cooperate with each other to solve the long-term social problems after the Olympic games and to deliver intangible legacy of Olympic spirit and world peace to future generations.

Fears over financing, security and staffing threaten citizens in the Olympic host cities scheduled, they learn from the past that huge costs were required to maintain the stadiums and facilities after the Olympics. The Olympics is always shining from the opening ceremony until the closing ceremony. But looking into costs in a tough economy, organizers of the Olympics and citizens in the host cities are not positive about the future of the Olympic legacy, those stadiums and facilities could take shine off after the Olympic games. The Olympic host cities are still stuck in procuring the best options for utilizing the Olympic legacy.

The Seoul Olympic Legacy Forum in October 2022 is an international forum where IOC and some 300 Olympic legacy managers and experts from more than 40 countries will gather to discuss the present and the future of Olympic legacy, they will also find ways to revitalize the Olympic movement and make contributions to peace in the Korean Peninsula.
Novelty: The SOLF is the first platform where the Olympic host cities learn from KSPO how to revive the Olympic tangible assets after their own Olympics.
Impact & Results: The SOLF will be held from October 18 to October 19 in 2002. IOC President, Thomas Bach kindly will attend the forum and deliver a keynote speech and the 8th UN Secretary General, Ban Ki-moon will make a congratulatory speech. KSPO will share the one of 5 best practices selected by the IOC and will form a new consultative body with the Olympic legacy entities. This will lead 19 participant countries to the new era of the Olympic legacy. Even though wars are happening around the world, nothing can stop the Olympic games and current generations and next generations should enjoy the Olympic legacy.

Description:
TCS is part of the Government Data Architecture initiative to enable secure data sharing and usage across public service, built upon an integrated data management framework that manages the data lifecycle effectively from acquisition to destruction. It is a one-stop central sensor data platform that provides end-to-end data sharing services with exploitation environment.

TCS platform offers a seamless data request workflow that simplifies the user experience from data discovery to exploitation; users can view and request the dataset(s) directly with the routing of approvals being tied to attested identity sources within the government directory to fulfilment of request on analytics environment where the user can work directly on their scoped dataset. The platform greatly enhances the exploitation experience and contributes to faster turnaround in policy planning/analysis/design, operational service delivery and better enforcement outcomes.
Novelty: The GDA is a whole of government new initiative. Before GDA was introduced, government agencies mainly work off bilateral data sharing model. The model poses many fundamental issues, and it was time and effort consuming.
Impact & Results:
Within 2 years of operationalisation, the platform has supported more than 760 users across 37 agencies and fulfilled more than 170 data requests within 7 working days.
As the platform load is expected to increase over time with limited resources, the plan is to roll out progressive self-help features, which will result in a flatter but continued growth to 1500 users across 60 agencies by Mar 2027 and fulfilment of 100 data requests per year within 7 working days by FY26.

Project/Initiative Name: Inter-ministerial cooperation for inclusion in sport
Organisation: Ministry of Education, Science and Sport
Description:
It is easier to be said than done, but we have taken the first step and committed to inter-ministerial cooperation to support inclusion in sport. Together with the Ministry of Labour, Family, Social Affairs and Equal Opportunities and the Slovenian Judo Federation, as the first in a series of sports organisations, we have signed an agreement to support vulnerable groups to integrate into mainstream sport environments.

Even though many international and national documents state that inter-ministerial cooperation is desirable and necessary, large bureaucracies, the division of competencies and responsibilities makes it difficult. Organising an event 'on the ground' with athletes from vulnerable groups, is an innovation, as it requires a lot of coordination, learning, mutual understanding and exposure to new content. The Ministry of Education, Science and Sport had to familiarise itself with the status of people with disabilities and social inclusion before signing, while the Ministry of Labour, Family and Social Affairs had to familiarise itself with the field of sport and recreation. 

The biggest challenge in the area of inclusion in sports is posed by individuals who do not fit into the categories of para-sports. These include people with mental health problems, people who have suffered head injuries as a result of an accident, people with long-term illnesses, children and adolescents with behavioural and emotional disorders, socially disadvantaged individuals, the elderly, former top athletes, geniuses in a particular field, Roma, refugees and others. Most sports environments are currently not yet able to include such people, or only exceptionally so since additional skills and training are needed alongside an open heart. We stress out it is not only the vulnerable individuals involved who benefit but also others who can significantly improve the quality of their lives through awareness, acceptance, tolerance and respect for diversity.
Novelty: This innovation supports inclusion in sports thanks to the effort to overcome ministerial boundaries.
Impact & Results: One concrete outstanding effect of the coordination between the two ministries so far has been the record of the programme "inclusive sport for people with disabilities" in the new Resolution on the national social assistance programme 2022-2030. Currently, approximately 150 judo players of different abilities are involved in inclusive judo. There are around 20.000 children and young people in the education system in Slovenia who have been issued with written order on the necessary adaptations. There is also an estimate of the number of people with various forms of disability. They figure from 160.000 to 170.000 persons with disabilities. Almost 1.500 children and adults are involved in a pilot programme to link disability and sports associations at national level. Our aim is to provide long-term systemic support for their involvement in local sports clubs by at least 200 % through weekly exercise.
