To monitor the effectiveness of the course to grow both the awareness and capability of public service organisations in the use of AI in the delivery of public services, we survey the participants after the course and where allowed by the students, publish the results. This has further heightened awareness and enthusiasm within public service organisations to send their people on the course.

Description:
Social assistance for low-income families in Singapore are often largely premised on their needs, rather than the enterprise and creativity that families have. Empowered Families Initiative (EFI) is a developmental initiative that hopes to harness the strengths and abilities of low-income families and invest in their aspirations through grants, savings matching and group support to improve their life circumstances towards better socio-economic position and well-being.

The initiative consists of three essential components:
1. Families will have access to funds and linked to non-monetary resources support, based on their respective plans and goals to better their lives. They have the autonomy to choose how to utilize the resources for their goals.
2. Savings Matching – families are able to tap on the above funds to save each month and these savings will be matched 2:1 by the initiative.
3. Regular group meetings among families for mutual support, encouragement and accountability, towards increased social capital and network.

We imagine that EFI not as an end in itself, but as part of a greater movement in reimagining how we can reframe social assistance as ‘investments’ towards the potential of low-income families. With future iterations of this initiative and with greater funding, we hope to increase the amount of grants, create scholarships for low-income families who wish to upskill and create capability among social service professionals to have developmental, aspirational based conversations with low-income families, rather than merely focusing on the “here and now” needs.
Novelty: Unlike social assistance and programmes for low-income families that are mostly premised on basic needs, our project is innovative in the sense that it rewards families based on their motivation, harnesses and leverages on their assets and strengths. Further, low-income families with lived experience of being in poverty, are also co-creating this initiative together with us as part of the organising team and as co-facilitators.
Impact & Results: 4 families were onboard EFI in the first round - Danny, Lisa, Suzy and Fatima*. We measured the impact on each family through observations and qualitative means to evaluate any increase in income level or potential for income-generation and an increase in social support for the families. Lisa, Fatima and Suzy have their own home-based business, an informal way to earn an income. They used the grants as capital to buy equipment to increase their sales. Lisa and Suzy collaborated to open a food stall which doubled their incomes by the end of the iteration. Fatima was also able to save up $25k to open a car-washing business. Danny works as a food delivery rider. He does his delivery on his bicycle and used the grant to take a motorbike license to increase his ability to earn more by increasing his delivery orders. At the end of the initiative, Danny was able to complete half the requirements for his license. All 4 reported a higher level of social support from getting to know one another.

Project/Initiative Name: Aides Jeunes (Youth Aid) - Social aid and benefits simulator for individuals and those who accompany them
Organisation: Beta.gouv.fr via the Direction Générale de l'Emploi et de la Formation Professionnelle accompanied by the Direction interministérielle du numérique
Description:
Aides Jeunes, the aid simulator on the 1jeune1solution.gouv.fr website, was developed to combat the lack of access to rights in France. In just a few minutes, it allows people to see what aid they are entitled to, and to facilitate their aid application process. To date, it contains more than 700 forms of assistance and can be used to guide 80,000 people per month.

Aides Jeunes is one of the digital products created in the beta.gouv.fr ecosystem, alongside 120 other autonomous teams, also known as "State Startups". These teams participate in the co-construction of tomorrow's digital public services in France, by focusing on specific public policy problems raised by public agents. They are aimed directly at individuals as well as at companies, associations, and administrations. Some convincing achievements: the Culture Pass, the business creator simulator (mon-entreprise.urssaf.fr), an algorithm for detecting job openings for job seekers (labonneboite.pole-emploi.fr), etc. The Aides Jeunes simulator attempts to address the problem of access to the law.

How does the simulator work?
Following a series of questions (26 on average) about the person's situation (profile, home, housing, income and projects), the simulator evaluates the aid for which they are eligible. Information and useful links to help you prepare your application are associated with each type of assistance.

As part of the beta.gouv.fr ecosystem, we can capitalize on the expertise of other teams who also have agile, incremental and transparent practices. Their work is carried out in conjunction with the people who use their services on a daily basis. This is the case with rdv-solidarités.fr for online appointments and démarches-simplifiées.fr for online requests.
Novelty:
The simulator is updated daily to take into account legislative and technical changes in each integrated device. There was a recent work integrating a module for making appointments with caregivers and the progressive dematerialization and pre-filling of certain procedures also contribute to its innovative aspect. 

But it is mainly on these 2 aspects that we think we stand out: 
1) The progressiveness of the contribution: we allow institutions to contribute to the simulator according to their technical capacities. They can, at their choice or in a progressive way, integrate helpers with textual conditions, simple calculation rules, or complex calculation rules (thanks to OpenFisca).
2) Calculation rules modeled with OpenFisca can be reused for mass microsimulation, which allows for budget impact measurement during law reforms (leximpact.an.fr).
Impact & Results:
Our impact to date: 
- Nearly 140,000 visits / month (+70% in one year)
- More than 80,000 simulations completed / month (+70% in one year)
- 7.5/10 overall satisfaction index on the Observatory of the quality of online procedures
- 2/3 of people start at least one process (stable)
- 2/3 of them complete the process (stable)

Our methodology :
- Quantitative study 
    - Continuous monitoring of our usage statistics (number of visits to the site, simulations started, completed, exit pages, etc.) thanks to the open-source analytics software Matomo.
    - Implementation of site variations with A/B testing for relative performance evaluation
- Qualitative study 
    - Surveys sent, with their consent, to people who have done a simulation to ask them about their effective access to rights. Telephone and email exchanges with people who need support.

Description:
Using geolocation or an address entry, real verified users are shown their government representatives on their smartphones enabling direct one-to-one messaging on important events. Collected anonymized data enables evidence based decision making for the public and governments. iMatr (pronounced ‘I Matter’) provides an innovative technological solution to these problems.  It contributes to the evolution of democracy by providing unprecedented one-on-one connectivity to elected representatives. Finally, there is a system where one voice does matter in a collective infrastructure. 

Designed to address the need for transparent and productive political engagement, iMatr responds effectively and efficiently to the democratic challenges countries currently face, especially with youth participation in governance and the electoral processes. One need not look far to see how the internet has revolutionized information technology and with it social structures.  Social networks personified by entities such as Facebook and Twitter are evidence of world connectivity that is void of civil and respectful political engagement in virtual space. iMatr changes that. 

Through its Big Data, it clearly demonstrates what is important to communities and governments. Free and open to all, iMatr empowers government and legislators to act with evidence based outcomes; providing better and informed decisions reflective of what communities want. Moreover, IQed (pronounced ‘IQ-ed’) powered by iMatr, ignites youth interest and provides a lifelong journey of education, and civic participation. It brings the least participatory group of society into the process of governance through dynamic real time learning.
Novelty:
Democratic Technology is an innovation in society, education, governance and political engagement because: 
- It provides real, verified users in countries, structured real time engagement of politicians and government, and current news and events of the day in real time. 
- It empowers one-on-one contact with politicians on their smartphone/personal devices without third party filters: providing speed of access, convenience and the prevention of miscommunication and misdirection. 
- It presents Big Data 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, on what people want and where government and politicians stand.
- In addition to being a citizen engagement tool, iMatr is also an educational device that teaches young people about different government systems and local, national and world issues.
Impact & Results: iMatr has made an instant impact with government. When the world choses to engage habits of ‘destructive social media’ through trolls or fake identities, iMatr counters, and introduces ‘constructive social media and DT’ for the purposes of advancing humanity. Politicians who had not used social media to engage citizens, now use iMatr for this purpose. Over a 1000% increase has recently occurred in their participation.

Organisation: Politics for Tomorrow, Nextlearning, Centre for Environmental Politics, Free University Berlin
Description:
Transformation Wagen - "Daring Transformations" is a learning programme co-developed with and for public servants in the field of Environmental Politics in Germany. Aiming at enhancing tools for public administration to renew itself, it creates capacities and methodologies to handle major societal challenges and foster a transformative mindset to actively shape the future and not just react to what is happening. 

"Daring Transformations' ' is a learning and capacity building programme developed for civil servants in the field of environmental policy, allowing them to acquire mindset and transformative literacy to facilitate systemic change processes. The objectives are co-elaborated as follows: 1) Developing competencies within the political administration to empower public servants to be actors of change, 2) Creating collaborations and fostering working habits beyond departmental and organisational boundaries, 3) Developing knowledge and awareness on social and systemic dynamics, 4) Understanding policy development as a learning and agile field. Experimental learning makes transformation tangible.

Based on transformative literacy methods, on assessments of the political-administrative landscape’s innovation and organisational capacity and on arising cross-organisational needs, four main modules were developed during the early phases of the learning programme. They then serve as a framework for further on-going experimentations and transformative learnings.

The three of the four modules are as follows: 1) Systemic understanding of problems and transformative approaches, 2) Reflection on different perspectives and discovering different realities through methods such as the transformative actor analysis, 3) Self-reflection and empathy. Creativity and self-awareness are very much encouraged through methods such as creative cartwheel and future theatre.
Novelty: The development of the learning programme was innovative in the way it has been designed: collaboratively and based on challenges and needs of different personas from the German Environment Agency, the Federal Ministry for Environment and the Agency for Nuclear Safety. “Daring Transformations” - is unique as it encompasses the steps of transformation in a co-design manner, enabling participants to become themselves transformative agents. This is enabled through deep understanding of transformative literacy; through discovery and reflection of others’ perspectives and through self reflection and empathy.
Impact & Results: The implementation of the programme was evaluated both concomitantly and ex-post. Through the on-going evaluation and monitoring, the facilitation team continuously and systematically observed the progress of the participants. For example, direct feedback was taken into account in the further implementation phases of the programme. Ex-post surveys and interviews clarified the value of the learning experience, and summaries were offered to responsible leaders allowing them to share learnings and methodologies with their colleagues and further disseminate transformative literacy.

Project/Initiative Name: IQed - The Future of Digital Engagement for Youth and Governance
Description:
IQed (Inquiries/Questions in education) utilizes cutting-edge Democratic Technology in classrooms for the purpose of empowering students and educators in Digital Literacy, Civic Literacy, Dynamic Engagement, Critical Thinking/Problem-Solving Skills and Global Competency Education.  IQed enables every student to use their personal devices to explore, investigate and become informed on issues of governance and global matters, so that their voice can positively impact local and global communities.

Matr is Democracy for the Digital Age: convenient, relevant, and immediate. The platform not only unifies civility and politics, but it also additionally engages real world issues in real time. When the world engages in habits of “destructive social media'', iMatr counters, and introduces “constructive social media and technology” for the purposes of advancing humanity. 

With IQed’s "Let’s Discover" icon, students are first able to acquire the fundamentals of politics, prior to delving into more comprehensive issues and concepts of government. Once more, by accessing the "Let’s Discover" icon, students are quickly and conveniently informed on the essentials of governance, types of governments, governmental structures, democracy fundamentals, the power of “the vote”, civic duties, how laws are created and implemented, as well as the definitions of a multitude of political terms available to them at the touch of a button.  

Via the utilization of cutting-edge Democratic Technology, educators can now encourage students to embrace technology within the classroom for the purpose of dynamic engagement and meaningful learning. This innovation additionally promotes a greater degree of flexibility by allowing students to supplement submissions of written work with personal video testimonials as they relate to curriculum objectives.
Novelty:
For the very first time in history, a robust, cutting-edge educational technology now brings the issues of the world into the realm of the classroom, in real time, in a practical manner.  Students at every level can actively connect and collaborate with other students, community leaders and politicians on issues that matter to them. Utilizing Geolocation in its approach to Democratic Technology, students can now also conveniently discover who their governing officials are in their respective communities.   

IQed is Education for the future, with authentic, relevant application that facilitates student engagement and empowerment.  IQed is a timely and transformative educational conduit that develops critical thinking skills, which in-turn, facilitate bona fide and impactful learning.
Impact & Results: IQed is Education for the Digital Age: convenient, relevant, and immediate. The platform not only unifies civility and politics, but it also additionally engages real world issues in real time.

Project/Initiative Name: 'SYNERGASIA' Platform for Matching Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Resources with Local Needs
Description:
The innovation refers to a structured platform for cooperation between the Municipality of Amaroussion and the private sector for the public benefit. Corporate Social Responsibility resources are used to cover identified local needs in synergy with public interventions/services. Following a minor organisational reform, a team of officials has been trained to implement an innovative customised methodology that allows visibility and accountability and promotes the extroversion of public sector. 

The Municipality of Amaroussion developed the innovatory ‘SYNERGASIA’ Platform Project and proceeded to the necessary organisational reforms and staff training. The ‘SYNERGASIA’ Platform matches CSR resources with identified local needs in a mutually beneficiary way. Most importantly, it mobilises resources towards identified needs that cannot be covered by the City’s regular budget lines.

The Platform was piloted for eighteen months with remarkable and measurable results and today it has been mainstreamed into the routine operations of the Municipality. Regarding outcomes, the ‘Cooperation’ Platform was piloted during the pandemic, and it proved a valuable tool to attract necessary resources to cover urgent local needs. Apart from that though, in many cases our first contact with companies has resulted in relations of trust over the last two years.  

The range of activities funded via the Cooperation Platform is wide: accessibility infrastructure in school buildings, provision of IT infrastructure to school staff, meals and medicines provided via the City’s social service, seminars, art exhibitions, environmental projects, etc. So far, the population benefited are estimated to 25.000. The SYNERGASIA Platform Project can be scaled up from the local to the regional level with the proper customisation of procedures
Novelty:
The ‘SYNERGASIA’ Platform Project has brought into the Municipality of Amaroussion a new mentality about the benefits of working together with the business community. So far, cooperation was random, isolated from the City’s priorities and, usually, it had the form of in-kind contribution to vulnerable groups.  

Through the Platform, the CSR Office of the Municipality has acquired the know-how to operate in an extrovert, structured and effective manner in order to create lasting and trustworthy relations with the private sector, on the basis of specific criteria and code of cooperation.
Impact & Results:
The main objective of the Municipality of Amaroussion, was the design of procedures and tools that would enable the staff of the Municipal CSR Office to work with efficiency and efficacy.  That objective was achieved, through the adoption of the innovatory practices introduced in the context of the ‘SYNERGASIA’ Platform Project. 
The piloting of the Platform brought in considerable results and most importantly provided the ground of approaching and working together with large companies mainly from the IT, energy and health sectors.

Project/Initiative Name: Shanghai Data Exchange (SDE) - Initiative of Data Factor Market Development
Description:
Shanghai Data Exchange (SDE) has initiated a new business racing track of data factor market. SDE has independently developed a data trading system named niDts Intelligent Data Trading System, which provides efficient, convenient, transparent and secure data trading services, enabling the market development of data as a production factor, and fueling the digital transformation of economy, life and governance.

The founding of Shanghai Data Exchange the niDts system is committed to drive data factor circulation, release the digital dividend, and promote the digital economic development. With building data factor market and boosting digital assetization progress as its missions, Shanghai Data Exchange positions itself as the maker of data trading standards, Organizer of data trading market, promoter of data assetization, raiser of data trading ecosystem and taker of data trading policies. 

With corporate management system adopted, Shanghai Data Exchange builds a “1+4+4” system to achieve the target of building a key node of global data factor allocation. ONE POSITIONING refers to build a nation-level data exchange; FOUR FUNCTIONS refers to quasi-public service, omni-digital trading, whole-chain ecosystem building, systems and rules innovation; FOUR FEATURES refers to standardizing rights confirmation, unified registration, concentrated clearance and flexible delivery.
Novelty: It's a brand new market of Data Trading. Shanghai Data Exchange, as a key functional institution to promote the circulation of data factor, to release digital dividend and promote digital economic development, has made fruitful exploration in terms of data rights, data product resgitration, compliance assessment model of data products,  data product specification for consumers, data trading arbitration and etc.
Impact & Results:
The result is that more companies espeically the traditional companies are willing to do data governance, organize data products to trade, and explore more using scenes of data. 

The issues of data compliance, data value assessment, data security, data application, data governance and the like are all under research, and some of them are solved.

Project/Initiative Name: Driver Advisory Council for Uber India
Description:
Aapti's the Driver Advisory Council (DAC) represents a new framework for engagement between gig workers and platforms in the ride hail space - a first of its kind initiative in India. The larger aim of this innovation is to create a mechanism for platform governance that focuses on gig workers rights, enabling participatory action and reflexive praxis. To this end, we’ve engaged with public bodies such as India’s NITI Aayog to socialise the DAC and its novel approach to platform work.

Recognising the significance of drivers as the backbone of the industry, Aapti has instituted the 35-member Uber India Driver Advisory Council (DAC) - a unique, first-of-its kind effort in India. The creation of the council is aimed at promoting involvement of drivers in platform governance, through drivers’ participation in an independent third-party mediated forum.  

 The DAC is a means for driver partners to connect with Uber, and importantly, with peers from across 6 participating cities in India. 
To ensure independence from Uber, Aapti established the first DAC, alongside formulating practices and principles for governance of the Council. In establishing the DAC, Aapti reviewed ~4000 applications from driver partners in 6 cities ( Bangalore, Chennai, Delhi-NCR, Hyderabad, Kolkata and Mumbai).

To this end, regulators are growing alive to alternative models for platform governance and promotion of worker welfare, championing models such as DAC as plausible pathways for governance of the platforms of tomorrow. Such a move is particularly salient given the regulatory obfuscation and vacuum which have come to define policymaking for the platform gig economy. Specifically, regulators in India - such as the Competition Commission - have already instructed platforms to formulate self-regulation guidelines on pricing and transparency.
Novelty:
The DAC is innovative inasmuch as it represents a novel mode of engagement between platforms, gig workers and public agencies. More significantly, its innovative potential can produce gains at three levels:  

1) Business: The DAC helps enable increase in driver satisfaction leading to reduced attrition, provide avenues for channelling voice reducing risks of volatile actions and surface an opportunity to gather feedback and response 
2) Policy: Demonstrate self-governance through practice, intervene effectively in upcoming/planned policy changes with a body of evidence, frame the narrative on engaging with partners with learnings from experiences  
3) Societal: DAC supports conversations in safe environments for driver partner and rider communities alike, while enabling discussions on critical issues such as discrimination, health and safety
Impact & Results:
The results were evaluated through a framework for assessment developed internally by Aapti. This framework surfaces a rubric for Uber teams to prioritise the many issues highlighted during DAC meetings, furnishing appropriate timelines as well as explanations for their actions (whether an issue is resolved or not, and why).  

Notably, the DAC has also sparked conversations about gig workers' welfare to the extent that it has generated momentum for notification of Indian Government's proposed Code on Social Security. The CoSS introduces a co-pay system to cover health and other benefits for workers.

Project/Initiative Name: LAB JUSTICE – Innovation and Culture Program
Organisation: Justice institute for financial & equipments management
Description:
LAB Justice is the first executive education program, tailor-made for the Justice sector. With a customized learning journey, it was designed to help people working for Justice bodies address the challenges faced to deliver more efficient and people-centred services. Focused on three main areas – strategic management, digital transition and leadership in a context of change –, it aims to ensure the necessary skills to implement reform measures, namely those under the Recovery and Resilience Plan.

The Portuguese Ministry of Justice signed the first agreement with two of the major business schools in Portugal (Lisbon School of Economics and Management – ISEG and Nova School of Business and Economics – NovaSBE) to organize the Justice LAB, the first executive education program created from scratch and specifically designed to meet the real and current challenges of bodies and entities in the area of Justice. 

Focused on three main areas (strategic management, digital transition and leadership in a context of change), Justice LAB is an advanced program of innovation and culture to develop the resources and skills that guarantee the operationalization of change and innovation. It will promote governance models with the right management incentives, will provide support on how to incorporate emerging technologies (such as artificial intelligence), manage information and assure transparency, as well as to provide organizational frameworks on how to involve managers and employees throughout the decision-making cycle, from designing to executing change processes.

The unique character of the Justice LAB lies in the fact that the Program changes the public sector project cycle paradigm, moving from a “top-down” approach, with the PA bodies being mere executors of projects prepared at the political level, and for which teams of external consultants are often hired to carry them out, to a paradigm that also values “bottom-up” approaches and fosters “ownership” of the bodies, i.e., where the entities are committed to the design and management of projects, with less dependence external competence to carry them out.
Impact & Results:
The LAB Justice initiative has started very recently, and it is planned to be concluded in March 2023. So, it is still too soon to observe its impacts. 
During the program, the satisfaction of participants will be measured through questionnaires. 

By gathering one hundred professionals from 18 Justice bodies, it is also expected that this intensive education programme facilitates networking, and thus enhances collaboration and knowledge sharing.

Project/Initiative Name: Intergenerational Fairness Policy Assessment Toolkit and Future Check
Description:
Intergenerational fairness—the idea that we should meet the needs of the present without compromising rights of future generations or citizens—is a defining theme of our time. Although most politicians and citizens value fairness, society does not have a way to assess the impact we’re having on future generations and advocate for them. This Framework for Intergenerational Fairness contributes to this. The Framework provides a pragmatic solution, whether as a young citizen, or public servant. 

The Framework for Intergenerational Fairness is an enabling mechanism that contributes to this objective and intersects other mechanisms such as the UN SDGs (sustainable development goals). The framework consists of three inter-linked elements:  
1. Guidance for institutional ownership that provides legitimacy within the political system and accountability to the public.  
2. A blueprint for national dialogue to collectively consider society’s vision for the future.  
3. A policy assessment toolkit that applies latest best practice to provide useful clarity on the questions of intergenerational fairness in a specific policy or legislation.

It is designed to be a tool for thinking, communication and action, not just a “tick box” exercise. It has been designed on the best and most recent practices in policy assessment, risk management and strategic foresight. A focus on outcomes and practical recommendations. This was done with the aim to develop a practical and pragmatic tool, that is easy and initiative to use, and provides practical recommendation that can lead to changes in behaviour.  

Specifically, the tool has been piloted in Future Check, which is a citizen led service, organised by SOIF and All Parliamentary Group for Future Generations (APPG-FG). It is designed to help parliamentarians, policymakers and the public to consider the long-term impact of proposed government legislation.
Novelty: The intergenerational framework provides a unique practical and pragmatic solution that can be used by government and society to support intergenerational dialogue. It sets out an approach to bring together participatory approaches, strategic foresight, and intergenerational fairness as part of a national (or sub-national) dialogue, that can then inform decision-making and assessment. The assessment tool—an important part of the framework—is designed to be simple to use. Anyone from a Secretary of the UN, to a youth activist can use the tool.
Impact & Results: The Portuguese President, Marcelo Rebelo de Sousa, announced that he will champion intergenerational fairness, including training the Portuguese President’s staff in our intergenerational fairness assessment method. The pilot gained positive feedback from organisations including the Bank of Portugal, the national audit body, the Court of Auditors, and the Finance Council. We are currently building a network of users who are experimenting with the toolkit and will be capturing additional evidence of use, uptake and impact from the international community.

Description: Facilita is a comprehensive web platform for citizens' digital applications in public entities. There public entities can create their forms for procedures and surveys to the people and receive assessment. Similarly, since all procedures are digitalized it speeds up the service delivery and attention procedures.
Novelty: Integrated platform for procedures to the citizens that is of use of all/most public instances across all levels of government.
Impact & Results:
- 551 public entities of all levels of government 
- Almost 2,000 public servants are behind the platform use
- >2,4000 forms created by public entities
- almost 500,000 procedures

Description:
The initiative examines and delves into the current landscape of voice interfaces in India, with a focus on concerns related to privacy and data protection, linguistic barriers, and accessibility for persons with disabilities (PwDs). This project was undertaken with support by the Mozilla Corporation. 

With the aim that voice interfaces have the potential to democratise the use of the internet by addressing limitations related to reading and writing on digital text-only platforms and devices, the initiative mapped 23 voice interface developers between 2019 and 2020. It responds to the need that, for India, there is a need not just for the implementation of voice interfaces, but also for other accessibility measures to be introduced to enable every person to benefit from the digital world.

A common issue that the developers we interviewed highlighted was the scarcity of voice data in Indian languages. They noted that although there is now some data in Hindi and Indian English, there are several low-resource languages. If data on them was available, voice interfaces could be developed to help people access services in these languages via their phones. Additionally, they used publicly available information from the interface websites and from news reports. This infographic shows developers of voice interfaces organised based on the sectors that use their service, how they identify themselves, the underlying technology they use, the languages they provide voice services in and if they disclose in their privacy policy whether they are processing voice data or not: http://voice.cis-india.org/mapping-actors.html
Novelty: Initiative to mapp 23 voice interface developers in India to address the limitations related to reading and writing on digital text-only platforms and devices across the country.
Impact & Results:
Three case studies
Common voice: http://voice.cis-india.org/common-voice.html 
Indic TTS: http://voice.cis-india.org/indic-tts.html 
Niki: http://voice.cis-india.org/niki.html

Project/Initiative Name: Safeguarding through the Moderator Proposition - the early identification of children at risk
Description:
Safeguarding children at risk of abuse requires effective multiagency working throughout safeguarding agencies. Current arrangements have proved inadequate and have failed to deliver on the imperative for integrated working. The Moderator Proposition fully resolves these outstanding operational needs. 

Moderator employs AI technology, with an integrated organisational 'construct', to assemble a comprehensive 'picture of events' for conjoined working across agencies. 

Moderator resolves long standing problems for integrated working between 'siloed' agencies, fundamental for safeguarding children at risk. The proposition provides for the collation of review information delivering - multiagency working, interoperability, data/information sharing to provide early Information for early intervention. 

Information sharing is enabled through Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology, MicroFocus IDOL 12, to collate a 'picture of activity' on a child at risk. The AI engine assembles, interprets, analyses and reports on database records and associated information sources e.g. emails, texts, telephone calls, social media outlets - comprising so called 'unstructured data' - to assemble a comprehensive record of activity on any case.
Novelty:
Moderator is a unique process for integrated working, utilising advanced AI technology, conjoining disparate safeguarding agency and case related information in a manner not previously identified. It resolves long standing shortcomings in current safeguarding operations, in a process that's is both innovative and radical.

The underlying technology base, is currently deployed in government security agencies for other analytic purposes. It can be 're-purposed' for safeguarding without modification. No comparable initiative of its type exists, world-wide.
Impact & Results:
Safeguarding professionals will be provided with the means to make informed judgements, from a full and comprehensive collation of information available, at the earliest opportunity, in a manner not possible prior to Moderator, and independent of the need for co-located staff - currently considered a pre-requisite for effective working.

Analysis of published review data on safeguarding operations, indicate a Moderator based process would realise some £1.2B worth of efficiency savings in respect of current safeguarding operational costs - whilst providing for a dramatic improvement in recruitment and retention of sorely pressed staff -specifically social worker staff - a recurrent problem in safeguarding work.

Project/Initiative Name: Job Analysis in the Public Sector
Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description: Job Analysis in the Public Sector Project has been initiated to develop standardized and comparable job descriptions and specifications for all public sector positions. The project is supported through a web-based data collection system that includes artificial intelligence and machine learning. Its results will be used in disseminating modern human resources processes in Türkiye.
Novelty:
One of the most comprehensive HR projects regarding the number of positions analyzed and the number of participants. In addition, it serves as the first attempt to develop standardized job descriptions and job profiles covering the whole public sector. The project will utilize both qualitative and quantitative big-data analytics processes. 

With the Job Analysis tool supported by artificial intelligence and machine learning, modern human resources processes in the public sector, such as competency-based planning, recruitment, training and development, career planning, performance, and wage management, will be developed and disseminated based on data and technology.
Impact & Results: The job analysis project has three main objectives: to acquire detailed and ready-to-analyze quantitative data on the public sector job activities and requirements, to develop standardized job taxonomies based on activities and skills, to lay a foundation for more elaborate and standardized titles/positions, objective and data-driven planning, recruitment, training, career, and performance and evaluation systems.

Project/Initiative Name: Deterritorialization of the Federal Attorney General's Office
Organisation: Office of the Federal Attorney General - PGU/AGU
Description:
The deterritorialization of the Union's judicial representation consolidates the governance mechanisms of the Attorney General's Office (PGU) to develop effective, coordinated, and assertive defense of the Union's interests in court throughout the country. With substantial gains in performance and productivity, it seeks to expand the protection of public assets. 

Thematic and procedural specializations are determining factors for the success of judicial action. However, the entity's outdated bureaucracy, based on the territorial paradigm, have limited the ability of the PGU to advance in higher specialization in its work. In fact, in 2020, 45 of the 68 prosecutor offices of the PGU (66% of the total) had fewer than 10 active members and each office was organized according to its own rules for dividing and distributing legal work, making it difficult to allocate the work force efficiently.

De-territorialization has involved several actions, such as consolidating governance mechanisms, reviewing and redistributing attributions, creating and standardizing several work processes (previously executed in very different ways in the dozens of PGU prosecutors' offices), and creating new structures, the national and regional coordinations - more flexible and efficient in terms of workforce management, since they have attributions in all the courts in certain federal regions, or even throughout the country, This has promoted broad specialization in the work of the Federal Attorneys, equalization of workloads among the Federal Attorneys in each federal region and the rationalization of administrative activities in support of judicial actions, whose processes have been revised and improved. 

In summary, the legal representation of the Union, under the responsibility of the PGU, an agency of the Office of the Solicitor General of the Union (AGU), has undergone a major transformation, becoming a modern and agile institution to respond boldly to the challenges of Justice 4.0.
Novelty: The deterritorialization of the Union's judicial representation represents the overcoming of the territorial paradigm of work organization - whose cultural and administrative premises, formalized in hierarchical structures that limited the efficiency of administrative management in the face of the challenges of modernity, were relativized so that it would be possible to improve the judicial performance of the Union's Attorneys and promote, with greater efficiency, the distribution of the work force.
Impact & Results:
The volume of legal claims in the PGU has been growing at an ever-increasing rate, showing a consolidated increase of 54.8% since 2017. The average time taken to complete judicial and legal tasks within the PGU, on the other hand, has been falling year by year - the average time in 2021 is 7.65 days, almost four times less than in 2017, when it was 26.6 days. 

Another demonstration of the success of these initiatives and of the firm commitment of the Federal Attorneys in the PGU to the public interest can be seen in the evolution of the Judicial Success Rate in the period analyzed (2017-2021).

Project/Initiative Name: People's health: Mobile Health Program in Maceió
Description:
The Brazilian universal health program faces numerous barriers in quality and waiting time for services. To solve this deficit, the mayor of Maceió designed the People's Health Program, which is already the largest mobile health initiative in Brazil, aimed at low-income communities. This is a replicable and adaptable structure, easy to assemble and, more importantly, mobile to facilitate the access of the citizens. 

The program is innovative, seeking to perform local health actions for all the communities in Maceió. The People's Health program is expected to serve approximately 240 communities, prioritizing 100 areas in the city of Maceió.

There will be 120 work fronts in the program, with more than 400 professionals directly involved in the project. It is carried out through local health and promotion actions in the communities. It seeks to identify possible health problems, to invest in health promotion actions, to encourage self-care, coexistence, and citizenship actions, because a large part of health care is provided through social interactions.
Novelty: The initiative seeks new ways to provide health to the population. It is pioneering in the country and demonstrates care in all the implementation phases: the proposal is inclusive, seeking to take health to all communities, the project of an itinerant structure with an adaptable structure that can be transported to any part of the city, the network of multidisciplinary services, the implementation route, and the possibility of adaptability. The initiative guarantees the engagement of the citizens, who can choose the place where they will be attended to.
Impact & Results: The People's Health program reaches the mark of 51,193 services to the population of various neighborhoods of Maceió, mainly providing assistance to communities in situations of social vulnerability. In 38 days of operation, the program had already performed 4,863 consultations for Women's Health, 6,516 for Children's Health, 7,709 for Mental Health, 12,005 in the Animal Health fronts, and 20,100 home visits with the Health in the Grotas teams.

Project/Initiative Name: AGERSA'S Integrated Inspection Platform: Sanitation Regulation
Organisation: AGERSA - Sanitation Regulatory Agency of the State of Bahia
Description:
The INTEGRATED SUPERVISION PLATFORM contains digitally collected, georeferenced data, enabling remote supervision. To fulfill its purpose, AGERSA needs to conduct inspections in the water supply and sanitary sewage systems and then present the results in a technical report, with proposed corrective measures for the non-compliance found and deadlines for compliance.

With this initiative, the inspection could also happen in an indirect way, permanently, remotely from AGERSA's headquarters, through the analysis of data and information provided by the service provider. Through the management of this information, AGERSA creates conditions to evaluate, throughout time and through indicators, the performance of the Provider, in compliance with the legal basis and according to the attributions and obligations pre-established in the Contracts with the municipalities. 

The goal of the initiative is: 
- Ensure greater mobility in the field during enforcement actions, by the configuration and native use of platform elements.
- Enable Data Management to generate indicators and support decision making, from the collection, organization, and reliable maintenance of information about locations and details of assets and resources.
- Intensify permanent indirect surveillance by monitoring indicator dashboards combining data.
Novelty:
The inexistence of a georeferenced information database meant that the inspection depended on on-site inspections, since the information was collected at the provider's facilities, to identify factors that were generating or could generate damage to the provision of services, verify the pertinence of the information sent, possible non-compliance and try to create an AGERSA database, keeping it updated.
In the universe of 366 municipalities, this model made it take about 4 years to return with a new inspection in each municipality.
Impact & Results:
Objective 1: Deliver to the Managers a tool to follow up AGERSA's inspections.

Objective 2: Deliver to the Basic Sanitation Services Provider an automated information registration tool.

Objective 3: Deliver to the citizens a tool of access to information regarding the provision of basic sanitation services in the State of Bahia.

Organisation: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Description:
COVID-19 compelled for national lockdown in India too, the public had no means to consult doctors and specialists. eSanjeevani - a cloud-based population scale, indigenously developed transformative telemedicine platform was rapidly developed & operationalized nationally. eSanjeevani is an innovative solution that has brought about digital transformation (systemic) w.r.t. delivery of health services, the innovation has been a runaway success amongst patients & practitioners.

User-friendly interface, extensive broadcasting, round-the-clock support, as well as continual enhancement in the service, led to the creation of a first of its kind of expansive ecosystem of telemedicine/digital health in the country. Through its network of over 102,612 Health and Wellness Centres as spokes, 12,000 Hubs, 1120 online OPDs and with over 208,000 providers on board, it is serving over 225,000 patients every day by providing them safe, contactless medical consultations without having to travel to the doctors.

Direct benefits of the eSanjeevani include: 1. Enabling patients in rural/isolated communities to get health services from a Doctor/Specialist without having to spend time and money; 2. Helps to keep secondary and tertiary level hospitals less crowded, by providing an access to doctors/specialists accessible to rural patients through their local Health & Wellness Centres; 3. Creation of Health Records (electronic) at the grass-root level; 4. eSanjeevani has streamlined the process and made it more efficient as patients now get to see a Doctor/Specialist within minutes. 5. The patients can consult doctors and general physicians in the confines of their homes and consult super-specialists through contactless, safe and secured medical consultations
Novelty: eSanjeevani is first of its kind of a telemedicine service that is meeting the remote healthcare needs of the country from a single instance on the cloud. eSanjeevani has not only revolutionised primary healthcare in the country but it has also proved to be a life-saving modality for it is supporting specialised networks such as Tele-Stroke Management Network that connects secondary hospitals (as spokes) to a speciality (neurosciences) hospital as the hub.
Impact & Results: As per independent research conducted by a civil organisation, every consultation on eSanjeevani leads to a saving of up to USD 25 and also saves a journey of up to 18 km. The innovation has so far served over 62 million patients, it has also enabled digital health services in over 102,000 health and wellness centres (primary healthcare). Over 85% of beneficiaries so far are from rural India and over 50% of the beneficiaries are women. Medical specialists Over 40 medical specialities can be consulted remotely. eSanjeevani has set off a trend of digitalisation of health information, which is. beginning to aid policymakers to come up with effective and timely health policies.

Project/Initiative Name: Speak to Polling Station Members (Fala Mesária, Fala Mesário)
Organisation: Regional Electoral Committee of the State of Minas Gerais
Description:
The project "Fala Mesária, Fala Mesário" was created to improve access to support the Clerk onsite, with greater responsiveness in directing the demand, as well as improving the voter hotline service, with organization of the information repository and training for attendants in order to achieve an institutional alignment of content. It is innovative because the voter receives information efficiently and with institutional alignment.

The innovation occurred in the centralization of information capture and in the automatic routing to the competent sector that answers the voter in a fast and effective way. The integration of the sectors was carried out to build a layered service flow so that the voters are attended to with the greatest speed and assertiveness.

The "Contact Us" system was adapted to allow the person in charge to access the FAQ database and be directed, with a few clicks, to a form to submit a question (if he or she considers it necessary) that can be automatically directed to the electoral zone, to the volunteer bank for election monitors, or to the residual service sector (Mesários Section and Elections Center).

The agents of the Voter Call Center and the Ombudsman participated in a virtual workshop held jointly with the Judiciary School to promote integration among the sectors and to get to know the tool, as well as the content repository. In addition, a service and question forwarding flow was established, in case new demands arise from the external public.
In the project, besides the system's adequacy, the training of attendants and the sectors' alignment, an internal communication plan was carried out in order to raise the awareness of the zones and sectors to attend to the visually impaired with the agility and promptness that the electoral process requires.
Novelty: With a single entrance door, the voter can be attended directly by the competent sector, without a slow transit and with human intervention to direct the call. This speeds up and humanizes the service, which is received directly by the servers in the voter/monitor's areas of origin.
Impact & Results: The calls are being monitored and after the electoral cycle it will be possible to observe the agility in attending to the demand and the absence of manual call routing, which impacts the attendance time and causes communication noises. The measure is statistical and will be measured after the Elections.

Project/Initiative Name: Telework Review of Electoral Cases / Project CEP-AVE
Organisation: Regional Electoral Committee of the State of Minas Gerais
Description:
In 2020, some service stations of the Electoral Justice of Minas Gerais were extinct, so that their servers would have to be removed to other cities. However, due to the sudden problem of servers having to be removed to other locations, the Electoral Virtual Support Program - AVE was created, so that these servers can work in several Electoral Zones, but remotely.

The initiative aimed at supporting Electoral Zones located in greater cities, in terms of the number of cases and the number of servers, using as manpower the AVE program's servers, who work remotely. In order to know which Electoral Zones were more critical, an action plan was created, called Plan of criticality index of the Electoral Zones, in which several indexes are used to determine which Electoral Zone needs more support. 

The goal of this project is to create a highly specialized technical team to analyze the provision of accounts and, consequently, reduce the backlog of cases. Also, to keep the team working in the First Instance continuously, minimizing the bottlenecks of the processes of accounts, be it electoral or annual, since they are presented in thousands, without interruption, annually, by the parties, or in elections, also by political parties and candidates.

Thus, since the beginning of 2022, these servers have already supported 7 Electoral Zones, which enabled the analysis of more than 1,500 accountability processes, thus reducing the Minas Gerais Electoral Justice's backlog, as well as providing a breath of fresh air to the supported Electoral Zones. The method used to enable this support was the creation of a virtual environment for each Electoral Zone within the Microsoft Teams platform, where there are forums for questions, real-time communications, process monitoring spreadsheets, training for servers, statistics and data repository of all analyzed processes, enabling the work of analysis and action on the processes in any Electoral Zone of the First Instance, without the need for physical relocation of servers, per diem payments, transportation, territorial distance, etc., given its versatility.
Novelty: It allows to improve the performance of servers who are working remotely in various Electoral Zones of the State of Minas Gerais, without the barriers of distance preventing them from analyzing the files. In addition, a criticality index spreadsheet was created, so that it is possible to list which Electoral Zones need more help. Furthermore, it was possible to create a technical team highly specialized in accountability, ready to assist any Electoral Zone in the State of Minas Gerais and even simultaneously. Everything is monitored and managed remotely.
Impact & Results: Between April and October 2022, 7 Electoral Zones have already been supported, which has provided for a decrease of more than 1,500 cases in the collection of cases in the First Instance of Minas Gerais Electoral Justice. In addition, it was possible to create a highly specialized technical team to analyze the rendering of accounts, currently composed of 16 servers working remotely

Organisation: Small and Medium Business Development Agency of the Republic of Azerbaijan
Description:
MBS was established in order to provide the services of the Small and Medium Business (SMB) Development Agency of the Republic of Azerbaijan in an effective way. The MBS system is dealing with several corporate issues simultaneously: Business process design; Process monitoring; Accountability provision; KPI planning; Project assignments; Registration of thematic meetings; Registration of SME meetings; Formation of SME data; Processing of applications submitted by SMEs, individuals and etc.

MBS system concedes organisation of internal business process of Agency, as well as reviewing and responding to applications and letters of SMEs and individuals in a more agile way. The future role of MBS is planned by playing a fundamental role in the direction of digitalization of G2B and B2B services provided. Registration and monitoring of business processes for the work to be done based on the applications of business entities on e-KOB portal will be carried out through MBS.
Novelty: MBS system is being used by the Agency employees which creates a chance in monitoring and management of main business processes. It works as a useful platform that allows agile work.
Impact & Results:
MBS system is now being implemented as a pilot project. Its positive impacts are demonstrated by the fact that coordination among departments, process optimization and project Implementation control has been accelerated. Further, MBS system provides extra opportunities for a deeper analysis of the business environment.

Statistical data regarding the work done or in progress:
Total applications: 12021
Total initiatives: 8172
Total questionnaires held: 599
Total appeals: 7343
Redirections (forwarding): 4079
Total requests: 42
Thematic meetings (industry oriented): 237
Total events held: 748
Educative events: 695
Total ongoing projects: 23

Project/Initiative Name: SMB Houses – Small and Medium Business Houses
Organisation: Small and Medium Business Development Agency of the Republic of Azerbaijan
Description:
The purpose of SMB Houses is to ensure harmonized delivery of comprehensive services to SMEs under the one-stop-shop formats. SMB Houses provide a range of G2B (government to business) and B2B (business to business) services, as well as other public support tools over phases of planning, starting, operating and developing business activities. It enables businesses to save their time and operating costs while getting all necessary services collected under the one roof.

SMB Houses combines more than 200 services and more than 30 public or private service providers. SMB Houses also include SME Development centers where professional experts provide consultancy, training and mentoring services to businesses and those willing to start his/her own business. These support services are free of charge. Different financial institutions are also present at SMB Houses.

SMB Houses objectives for customers (entrepreneurs) is to facilitate their activities on: 1. Planning Business, 2. Starting Business, 3. Running Business, 4. Developing Business.
Novelty: SMB Houses are playing a role as a single platfrom where individuals are able to learn each phase of business running process (starting from zero), obtaining business knowledge through learning platforms (SME development centers) of the Agency and getting public or private sector services in term of business development, acquisition of permits, elimination of business obstacles and etc. Before entrepreneurs had to go to different places for different services.
Impact & Results: Currently, there are two SMB houses operating. In total, from January to September 2022, more than 50'000 services where provided to business, of which 95% G2B and 5% B2B. Around 200 trainings and 2000 hours of consultancy were provided to more than 2000 SMEs and startups. Relevant support was delivered for the preparation of a total of 110 business plans.

Project/Initiative Name: Governa MCOM - Governance Program of the Ministry of Communications 2020-2023
Description:
The objective of the Governa MCom program is to present the sensitive and critical points regarding non-implementation in the Ministry, to evaluate the governance model adopted and monitor and adjust it to the context and organizational objectives, communicating it appropriately to stakeholders. With the Governa MCom program, the Ministry created its control, monitoring and evaluation instances, incorporating them to its basic structure. The MCom's governance structure is supported by internal instances.

The Governa MCom program focuses on good governance, being crucial for the Ministry to increase the activities related to the search for improvements in the leadership, strategy and control of the intervention, allowing and contributing to the achievement of the objectives expected by the various internal actors in the Ministry, external bodies and the citizens themselves, focusing primarily on the transparency and ethics of its actions.

The innovation of this program is present in the form of establishing the benchmark (TCU questionnaire), establishing evidence (proof) of implementation, action plan, accountability and goals, and its implementation monitoring (Executive Secretariat and Ministerial Governance Committee - CMG), besides the engagement of the Ministry's top management. All these premises of the Ministry's governance design are based on the TCU's model. Moreover, the Governa MCom promotes the adoption of good public governance practices through strategic direction and management monitoring. Supervised by the Executive Secretariat of the Ministry of Communications, the MCom's decision-making and governance structure is evaluated during its term.
Novelty: The innovation of this program is present in the form of establishing the benchmark (TCU questionnaire), establishing evidence (proof) of implementation, action plan, accountability and targets and its implementation monitoring (Executive Secretariat and Ministerial Governance Committee - CMG), as well as the engagement of the Ministry's senior management.
Impact & Results: Despite the fact that the program has not yet been finalized, and its execution is until the end of 2023, it is already possible to see concrete results from its implementation. Thus, in comparison from one year to the other, we see the increase in the Ministry's governance, going from 45 indicators at an improved level in 2021 to 89 in 2022 and from 59 at an Inexpressive level in 2021 to 17 in 2022. Several graphs related to People Management, Strategic Management, Procurement Management, Budget Management, Internal Control and Leadership indicators show how the Ministry has evolved in governance (it is not possible to insert images in this document)

Description:
Guided by a vision with people at its heart, WFP is harnessing the benefits of blockchain technology in its operations. Since 2017, WFP and its partners have been applying blockchain technology through the “Building Blocks” (BB) project to help achieve its mission while working with the wider United Nations/INGO community to push for greater efficiency across humanitarian and development operations.

The idea behind Building Blocks is built on the concept of a common, equally owned, operated, and governed humanitarian blockchain network – where reputable humanitarian and development organizations can freely join the network and run their operations on it.

Through Building Blocks, various actors have a way of increasing the coordination of their responses, ensuring greater accountability, accuracy, and partnership with the prime objective of improving the lives of the people served in constrained environments.
Novelty: Powered by blockchain technology, Building Blocks provides a neutral space for multiple humanitarian actors to collaborate in a decentralized and independent manner. On BB, humanitarian organizations can deploy applications (smart contracts) to accommodate various humanitarian use cases. This solution presents a unique opportunity to overcome traditional barriers to inter-organizational collaboration at scale.
Impact & Results:
BB is operational in Bangladesh and Jordan, together BB has been serving 1 million Syrian and Rohingya refugees with USD 475 million through 20 million transactions. During the same period, BB has also saved USD 3 million in bank fees.
Additionally, BB was recently deployed in Ukraine. As a result, between 01 May and 31 August, 18 humanitarian organizations coordinated USD 337 million of cash to 3 million affected Ukrainians and prevented USD 35 million of unintended assistance overlap (deduplication). This enabled supporting an extra 185,000 thousand people for three months purely due to the inter-organizational collaboration on BB.

Project/Initiative Name: Energy Upgrade of the General Hospital of Veria
Description: There is general planning for energy efficiency (national goals set by the state). The innovation of the project is the installation and operation of a hybrid heat/cooling triple production system with a solar cooling system. The proposed combination of actions leads to the maximization of the saved energy which contributes to the reduction of the operating costs of the hospital, to the strengthening of its energy security and the noticeable reduction of its environmental footprint.
Novelty: This particular system, if not the only one in Europe, will be one of the very few similar systems. The combination of the operation of the triple production system, solar cooling and conventional heating - cooling and electricity supply systems and their control, monitoring and recording of their energy characteristics is an important innovation.
Impact & Results: We are expecting to benefit from the subject innovation within the near future. The plan of the proposed interventions after its implementation will achieve primary energy savings of 62.4% in relation to the existing operating situation, a significantly large percentage for a hospital the size of the Veria General Hospital, while the reduction of emitted pollutants which will occur as a result of the implementation of the energy upgrade plan, it is estimated at 651.88 tn CO2 /year.

Project/Initiative Name: Implementation of a Project Management System and Digitization of the Hellenic Competition Commission’s Services
Description:
The Hellenic Competition Commission (HCC) implemented an innovative teleworking system in a digitalized environment that was developed during the period of the Covid-19 pandemic. The innovation aims at managing the HCC’s activities and at the monitoring of the day-to-day work of its employees working remotely from their physical workplace and allowed citizens to access remotely all HCC services.

For the implementation of the teleworking scheme, a teleconnection platform was used which operates with secure connection and GDPR compliance protocols. The customized platform uses client server technology and supports employees’ remote access to their office computers, following a 2-step authentication. It also allows file and folder transfer to and from the employee's office computer. Combined with this platform, all available teleconferencing systems were also used.

Furthermore, in order to protect citizens’ as well as the HCC staff’s health in the midst of the pandemic, by maintaining social distancing and avoiding the overcrowding, additional digital services for citizens and companies were launched for the first time, such as teleconferencing with citizens, online complaint lodging, and online submission of requests for opinion/advice.
Novelty: The teleworking scheme put in place by the HCC is considered innovative as not only allows staff members to perform their tasks remotely, but also enables the Authority’s administration to remotely monitor the progress of its employees' work. At the same time, in order to protect citizens’ as well as the HCC staff’s health in the midst of the pandemic, digital services for citizens were launched for the first time.
Impact & Results: The results of the implementation of the innovation were reflected in the evolving progress of the HCC's work. For example, the average time of completion of the DGC’s case backlog dropped from 8 years in 2019 to 1.4 years in 2020 and 1.1 years in 2021, and the number of plenary decisions increased from 25 in 2020 to 42 in 2021. The results of an in-house survey addressed to the HCC’s staff members indicate that the majority of employees were positive about the way teleworking was implemented.

Organisation: University Hospital Center of Central Lisbon, EPE
Novelty: This project simultaneously touches on several dimensions, such as the guarantee of a service provision model (diagnosis and treatment) centered on the patient, optimization of the patient's pathway, fostering and consolidating multidisciplinary work, efficiency gains and profitability of human and physical resources, creation of a structure of excellence and increased satisfaction of patients and family/caregivers.
Impact & Results: The project is not yet long enough for this evaluation. The space requalification ended on September 29, 2022, particle and filter testing was performed on October 11, 2022, and intravitreal injections resumed on October 17, 2022. Testing will begin on November 7, and all appointments are currently being confirmed.

Description:
JeVeuxAider.gouv.fr is the French public platform for volunteering, the digital incarnation of the Civic Reserve. It was born out of the desire to create a more supportive and resilient society of commitment, where the collective spirit is stronger than individualism. It connects people who want to act in the general interest with general interest organizations that need volunteers. To date, the platform brings together more than 400,000 volunteers and 9350 organizations.

In 2020, the health crisis linked to the coronavirus has increased tenfold the willingness of French people to get involved: in a few weeks, more than 200,000 people have registered on the JeVeuxAider.gouv.fr platform with the desire to make themselves useful, by participating in missions to maintain social links via phone calls, help with shopping, or even distribute meals to the most needy.

Today, the JeVeuxAider.gouv.fr platform encourages commitment in various fields of action that concern access to culture, education, health, solidarity and integration, as well as environmental protection, with the aim of improving the quality of life together and defending the general interest. 

Here are some examples of solidarity support operations carried out by JeVeuxAider.gouv.fr :
- Support within the framework of a national operation 
- Mobilization to face a crisis
- Commitment to a priority public policy that is meaningful 

Since its creation, the API Engagement has become the reference platform for pooling the various public (Universal National Service, Civic Service, JeVeuxAider.gouv.fr, 1Jeune1Solution, 1Jeune1Mentor, Diagoriente...) and private (associative volunteer engagement platforms, private volunteer engagement platforms, dissemination partner...) mechanisms. Since the beginning of the year, more than 45,000 volunteer assignments have been distributed among these different platforms, and we have seen 450,000 redirections from one platform to another, and 25,000 applications for the proposed assignments.
Novelty:
The platform is a dematerialized solution that allows organizations to respond quickly and efficiently to the difficulties they face in recruiting volunteers. Our "start-up" approach is inspired by the working methods used in the private sector and is based on an agile organization mode, which allows us to mobilize specific and specialized skills. 

The constant improvement of the product is based on the feedback of our users. They guide us in the creation of new functionalities, especially in response to crisis situations. For example, the platform has been completely adapted to become the central place for French people to get involved in responding to the influx of people displaced by the war in Ukraine.
Impact & Results:
The main indicator is the number of volunteer participations validated by organizations on the platform, which currently stands at 100,000. The pool of volunteers currently represents 422,000 volunteers who can be mobilized very quickly. The youthfulness of this pool (half of them are under 30 years old), compared to the median age of traditional volunteering in France, appeals to sectors that are generally faced with an aging workforce. 

This year, the team has carried out an impact measurement in which we surveyed the volunteers and partners registered on the platform. The results of this study show that 73% of the associations questioned said that the platform facilitates the recruitment of volunteers, and 78% of the volunteers questioned think that jeveuxaider.gouv.fr facilitates the transition to action. For the year 2023, we would like to reach 460,000 volunteers and 130,000 connections.

Project/Initiative Name: Economic autonomy policy for vulnerable population of the city of Buenos Aires
Organisation: Ministry of Human Development and Habitat of the City of Buenos Aires
Description:
The social policy implemented incorporates an innovative factor that aims to lower poverty rates by promoting the economic autonomy of vulnerable workers and their households.the Undersecretariat for the Development of Human Potential develops policies to promote the popular and social economy to improve the living conditions of the vulnerable population of the City.the promotion of economic autonomy seeks to generate an ecosystem of sustainable development by building bridges between vulnerable workers and entrepreneurs in the city and the most developed private business sector.the promotion of economic autonomy seeks to generate an ecosystem of sustainable development by building bridges between vulnerable workers and entrepreneurs in the city and the most developed private business sector.the promotion of economic autonomy seeks to generate an ecosystem of sustainable development by building bridges between vulnerable workers and entrepreneurs in the city and the most developed private business sector.

The policy is based on the recognition of the existing value of the work of economically vulnerable populations.Since the opening of the Center for Labor and Entrepreneurial Development in Barrio Mugica in 2016, the value of territorial work has been recognized by working with neighbors to improve their economic situation through paid work and the strengthening of their enterprises.

The policy recognizes two main target universes: a) workers seeking to find employment, who are supported with technical training tools, soft skills and labor intermediation services, and b) enterprises and productive units facing production, financing and marketing difficulties.
Novelty:
Our project changes the paradigm of social policy from a model of containment to one of developing people's economic autonomy. 

The model is driven from the public sector but identifies the central role of the private sector for the sustainability of the project by creating incentives and specific tools that allow companies in the formal economy to work together with the vulnerable population, not from a social responsibility perspective, but as key partners. At the same time, the model also relies on the experience and knowledge of civil society organizations to build bridges and create an integral development ecosystem.
Impact & Results:
One year after the implementation of this model, more than 2,000 businesses in vulnerable neighborhoods have been strengthened, more than 1,000 have included digital payment methods and more than 300 are geo-referenced. We aim to double this impact by 2023, reaching 50% of the city's businesses.

More than 6,000 people received comprehensive job training, more than 1,800 were hired by 150 private companies that rely on our intermediation service. 

For the first time 41 external companies set up businesses in these neighborhoods creating 120 new jobs, we aim to increase these results by 80% by 2023.

In one year, the City purchased more than $300M from enterprises in the vulnerable sector of the economy and the private sector $62M. By 2023 we will double this impact.

Today we have financed the sector for $95M through FONDES and $25M in loans from other financial entities. By 2023, 1700 productive units will have access to $263M in productive credit.

Description:
The Kitchen Lab has innovated transversally by appointing two general directorates (DG) from different departments: 
- DG of Innovation and Agri-Food Promotion of the Government of Aragon and, 
- DG Open Government in Social Innovation of the Government of Aragon. 

These appointments seek to address topics of interest to the agri-food sector while taking them to the next level of debate. Challenges of the first cycle: 1)Promotions of the agri-food policies at the point of sale, 2)Technology inclusion, 3) Associationism 4) promotion of the Aragon Brand 5) Education. It aims to enhance the collective intelligence through workshops and the generation of outreach information. 

The application of the methodology has brought freshness to the relationship between the actors, ensuring a climate in which everyone speaks on an equal footing.
Novelty: Innovative because it breaks institutional barriers: led by two general managers and with the collaboration of three other general managers. The following Administrations have had a voice in these workshops: Agriculture, Livestock, Environment, Open Government, Tourism, Education, Commerce and the public sector (Department Stores, Hospitality, High School Students, Silver Economy, small producers, large entrepreneurs, exponents of the Aragon brand, regular councils, journalists, hotel and catering schools...).
Impact & Results:
Lessons learned from each workshop have been incorporated into daily promotional actions and are pending inclusion in future ones. 

We are compiling the results, analyzing in depth the most necessary and addressable requests. The presentation of these results will be made in a public event in mid-December. We have already extracted from the conclusions of each workshop the elements that are repeated, identifying points of common strategy in terms of 1) use of ICT 2) user experience 3) brand value 4) sustainability and 5) education.

Organisation: Lipor - Intermunicipal Waste Management Service of Greater Porto
Novelty: In addition to centralizing and streamlining LIPOR's and its Municipalities' goods and services procurement procedures, reducing expenses and allowing for a better management of public money, LIPOR's Purchasing Center stands out for including in its procedures criteria that may be innovative, both in terms of sustainability and circularity, which translates into significant gains and savings in sustainable procurement for the adhering entities.
Impact & Results: The main result was the high rate of adhesion. The project was initially formed by 9 entities and currently has 16 active entities. Another result is the total value awarded that is currently around 78,903,067.98€, obtaining economies of scale and savings of around 3,223,746.73€. The involvement of entities in the development of the Framework Agreements also allows the introduction of innovative criteria in the tendering procedures, such as: (1) calculation of the energy impact (2) insertion of the Product Life Cycle Cost criterion. Furthermore, the reduction of administrative costs, the increase of negotiating power and the reduction of litigation risks should also be mentioned.

Organisation: Digital Channels and Identity Department / Digital Government Secretariat/ Ministry of Economy
Description:
The main innovation is the integration, in the digital public service user experience, of the document signing stage, which was also fully digitized, thus eliminating the need for interruptions in the flow of service with displacement, physical identification at counters or manual signatures. This was made possible by the creation of the GOV.BR Signature, made available free of charge and with a high degree of security, using cryptographic processes, to millions of Brazilians.

As an example of services that require a signature, one can cite: opening, closing, and all corporate acts of companies of any size, all types of powers of attorney for INSS and Receita Federal services, and vehicle transfers, among others. 
  
In this context, the GOV.BR Signature was created to meet the real demand of Brazilian citizens, as it is secure, accessible and free.
