This project enables any public agency in the municipal, state, and federal spheres to integrate one or more of its public services to the GOV.BR Signature, thus facilitating and speeding up the Brazilian citizen's user experience. 

With a high degree of security, using cryptographic processes, totally digital and relying on a cloud solution, which eliminated the need for a token or card and allows access from any computing device, the GOV.BR Signature is now recognized, by law, for the vast majority of public processes that require the citizen's signature.
Novelty: GOV.BR Signature brings the delivery to the Brazilian citizen as a user experience of the digital service, which is fully integrated with the signing documents, which was also fully digitized, thus eliminating the need for interruptions in the flow of service with displacement, physical identification at counters or manual signatures, all free and secure, and made available to millions of Brazilians.
Impact & Results:
As a result, dozens of public services are currently connected, some of which are extremely relevant to the people and the economic development of the country. The platform has already enabled more than 13 million signatures, issued more than 2.3 million GOV.BR digital certificates, and resulted in savings of more than 500 million reais for the Brazilian society with the purchase of paid digital certificates, not including costs with displacements and notarization.

In addition, users have reported high levels of satisfaction with the tool, with an average score of about 4.7. The evaluation factors clearly show that the solution has been reducing bureaucracy for citizens, because it is possible to see high satisfaction in the time to complete the services, in being able to finish the service, and with the saving of time and money, among others.

Project/Initiative Name: International Congress on Social Security Management
Description:
It is innovative essentially because it is an academic event that is not being promoted by an academic institution. Although Congeps has the institutional and scientific support of a Brazilian federal university, the event was conceived and developed by INSS employees, which besides a proposal of scientific approximation and the creation of an academic research group in a public organizational environment, makes possible an initiative to transform theoretical studies into possibilities of experimentation.

The international academic congress on social security management (Congeps) promoted by an autarchy of the federal executive power (INSS) arises as an opportunity for dialogue benefiting INSS workers and the academic community (with learning and exchanges between theory and practice). In special the citizens, recipients of social security services and those mainly affected by the policies that may be implemented from the changes in the processes learned and discussed during the congress. 

The event is foreseen in the internal actions of the INSS' Education Policy for the year 2022 and aims to promote and position the INSS as an institution with a preponderant role in the diffusion and production of knowledge in matters of Social Security Management, providing the exchange and articulation of partnership networks with national and international organisms as well as constituting a unique opportunity for access to diverse contents, by means of academic, scientific, and professional social security dialog.

The congress is an educational and strategic institutional milestone because, besides meeting the four pillars of the current strategic map of this public organization, it resulted in the creation of a multidisciplinary research group with the support and partnership of four higher education institutions in the country (public and private) that will act jointly in the development of research and extension with a focus on queue management and in the three axes: Citizen / Services and Processes / People Management.

The nnovation is characterized as a model of inspiration because it is believed that it is from the improvement and innovation of processes and public services with implementation from the bottom up, from those who really know the organizational reality (front-line employees who know the demands) that structural innovation can be thought of.
Novelty: Besides being an academic movement promoted by the federal autarchy, the event brought together heads of state and representatives of international organizations related to social security activities, which aroused a strong interest of academic institutions to support and participate with their scientific exhibitions. This proximity of science and technology to the INSS' organizational environment generated conditions for changes and new ways to implement public policies, aiming at more organizational efficiency and social effectiveness, based on the aspects discussed in the congress.
Impact & Results:
There is the possibility to generate models and experimental processes with the creation of the multidisciplinary research group among civil servants and academics across several fields. 

- the central management of the INSS became aware of the progress of the academic research of its employees and could foresee possibilities of implementation, from the publication of the annals of the event with more than 50 academic abstracts.
- publication of articles in an international journal.
- presentation and debate of the studies of more than 70 scientific guests, 80 academic abstracts and good practices from a dialogued expositive method.
- 500 presential participants and 1200 virtual participants, 60 scientific guests

Organisation: The main implementing organisation is the Ministry of Health. The Digital Transformation Office of the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye ensures the system’s integration with the e-Government Gateway.
Description: e-Nabız is a personal health record system that Turkish Ministry of Health integrated all the information systems of all health institutions. e-Nabız is standalone portal but it has SSO integration to e-Government Gateway. Via e-Nabız, people can access their lab results, medical images, prescription and medication details, emergency information, diagnosis details, reports and health records containing all details of the examinations via smartphones, tablet PCs and PCs on 24/7 basis. People can also share their medical records with their doctor(s) and relatives within specific regulations. People can get medical appointments, report their claim to be blood donor and be an organ donor. Besides, via the wearable technologies infrastructure of e-Nabız, people can enter data manually via smart wristbands, blood pressure and blood sugar monitors with wireless features.
Novelty:
- Citizens managing their own health data and deciding which personal health data will be shared with which institutions and relatives. 
- Via the data sharing feature of e-Nabız, elimination of repeating medical imaging procedures, therefore savings in healthcare spending and less working hours for healthcare staff.
Impact & Results:
- As the milestone for combat against COVID-19 pandemics, e-Nabız provided services such as accessing COVID-19 test results and getting vaccine appointments. 
- e-Nabız is constantly updated with new features and is reshaped upon the actual and proactive needs of people and the healthcare workforce. 
- Effective change management strategies have been adopted to manage the transition period in a fruitful way which include effective project planning, capacity building activities, field studies, meetings with stakeholders and a pilot application in Istanbul with 18 hospitals.

Description:
Designing a reverse osmosis water desalination plant with a production capacity of 5000 liters, which can be developed to reach 10,000 liters per day, and this plant is distinguished from conventional desalination plants that it will be energy-saving by 60%, cost, maintenance and space as well.

Contribute to solving the problem of water salinity in the region to become potable water for various groups on the widest possible scale, as well as contributing to solving the problem of electricity and energy, while saving costs, maintenance, and the space needed for conventional plants.

Conventional desalination plants suffer from several problems, most notably the high cost in terms of set-up and periodic maintenance, as well as the large space required for them, the high energy consumption, the water consumption of the aquifer, and the noise generated during their operation.

The innovation lies in the design and development of a small desalination device (miniature plant) with a production capacity of 5,000 liters and may reach 10,000 liters per day after development and the introduction of some modifications, at a cost of installation, maintenance, operation and a two-year warranty of about 40% - 60% less than the traditional cost currently used.

Taking into account that the size of the station is much smaller and will not exceed (1 x 1 x 2 meters), it includes a desalinated water tank, and it can be used on any water source, whether a well or a public network water without the need to add chemicals (Antiscalant), and it can be operated The station is powered by a solar panel and two batteries of 100 amps each for 24 hours.
Novelty:

5000 liters and may reach 10,000 liters per day after development and the introduction of some modifications, at a cost

Taking into account that the size of the station is much smaller and will not exceed (1 x 1 x 2 meters), it includes Mashrabiya and a desalinated water tank, and it can be used on any water source, whether a well or a public network water without the need to add chemicals (Antiscalant), and it can be operated The station is powered by a solar panel and two batteries of 100 amps each for 24 hours.
Impact & Results:
On the social level, the advanced potable water to all segments of society, especially marginalized groups, in addition to contributing to reducing unemployment and creating new job opportunities for a number of technicians due to the ease of maintenance of this station and the absence of maintenance experts.

On the economic level, the effect is represented in material savings on individuals and institutions, which will help them to benefit from the savings in order to achieve other economic benefits.

Project/Initiative Name: The Integrative Unit Model (IUM)- Innovative vocational rehabilitation process for people with severe disabilities
Description:
The Integrative Unit Model (IUM) aims to help people with psychiatric and intellectual disabilities to realize their potential, achieve their goals, and join the open labor market. In addition, our employer-relations practices promote inclusive employment among small, medium, and large businesses. Since 2005, we have helped thousands of people with severe disabilities to find a job in the open labor market.

The IUM trains people with disabilities in Over 120 training units, which operate as innovative social businesses such as second-hand book shops, high-end second-hand women's clothing, dog walking companies, a chain of coffee shops, and more. These social businesses are located at the heart of the community, and, parallel to their training capacities, enables thousands of daily interactions that remove barriers between people with and without disabilities and encourage a smoother transition from our vocational training units into the open labor market.

The IUM consists of five main components:
1. Skills- providing service users with social and personal skills in order to thrive in the open labor market
2. Training- Acquiring relevant professional skills such as managerial competency, technical capabilities, customer service proficiency, etc.
3. Preparation for the open labor market, such as preparation of resumes and for job interviews
4. Placement into the open labor market
5. Progress of the service users' career & education
Novelty: The Integrative Unit Model (IUM) aims to help people with disabilities be successfully included in the free job market. Our units promote inclusive employment in the job market and assist people with disabilities to maximize their potential and life goals.
Impact & Results: As shown in the OECD 2019 Global Forum, the model's outcome, along with other factors, has prevented about 1 million hospitalization days in the past two decades, which is a 50% reduction that saved over €185 million.

Organisation: São Paulo State Court of Auditors - TCESP
Description:
The reason for creation was to improve inspection models of external control, which were based mainly on legality and conformity. IEG-M index is an instrument for measuring public area effectiveness, as it analyses the inputs, resources and processes of the local administration, evaluating its policies and activities. None of result indexes deal with the evaluation of processes to analyse the application of public money that impacts local communities. 

The index creation (IEG-M) established a new observation framework in public external control processes. It presents relevant elements in public management activities in 7 major areas: Planning, Tax Management, Education, Health, Environment, Protected Cities, Governance in Information Technology.

One of the main index objectives was to create technical control points in the management of the local administration to bring to the communities in need more quality in the public services of the local government such as Education, Health, Safety and Fiscal Management. It establishes annual measurement of processes in the budget execution of the local public administration with results presented in bands.

In addition, the questions answered go through control algorithms, so that the points outside the expected curve are presented to the audit teams, optimizing the time spent in the planning of control actions, given the diagnosis of the sensitive points of the management. Therefore, the simple methodology used (WEB questionnaire), adjusted to statistical mathematical models, allowed the IEG-M project to be used by a wide spectrum of actors.
Novelty:
There was no means index in Brazil that tried to measure inputs and processes of public management in this range. Existing indexes until then were essentially results. IEG-M observes how the local public manager executes its policy and if there was an alignment with the federal, regional plans and with local interests. 
The challenge was to improve audit reports so that more technical judgments would find, in the communities, the meaning of the expression quality public service.
Impact & Results: Index made it possible to detect sectors that deserve greater surveillance of inspection action. IEG-M became one of main criteria for risk matrix elaboration of inspection type by local government, it was included in inspection scripts for answers validation "in loco", notes generated by the index are analysed and corroborate reporting counsellor vote, with many cases of accounts rejection due to index poor performance.

Project/Initiative Name: Abu Dhabi Police Drone Cyber Attack (ADDCA) to attack wireless network of target criminals
Description:
The proposed idea of Abu Dhabi Police Drone Cyber Attack (ADDCA) will enable security authorities in Abu Dhabi to penetrate the wireless networks of the targeted criminals to collect the required evidences on time without any physical access, In preparation for their arrest with evidence in record time. The ADDCA will operate using Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Deep Learning and automated penetration systems which will transmit the results directly to the command center. 

Details of a new, fully automated cyber-attack have been revealed by researchers at Columbia University. The malware, dubbed "X-Agent," was created by Russian intelligence services to spy on the computers and smartphones of NATO military personnel, Ukrainian government officials, and journalists. Automated cyber-attacks are becoming more prevalent as malicious actors seek to exploit vulnerabilities in an automated way.

We will use the automated hacking tools that work in conjunction with Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Deep Learning technologies in small sophisticated drone cyber attack to penetrate the wireless networks of the targeted criminals.
Novelty: This invention will offer the ability to obtain real evidence in record time against any suspect threatening the state in preparation for his arrest using this evidence with a level of accuracy accounting for 99.8%.
Impact & Results: The Dhabi Police Drone Cyber Attack (ADDCA) would be able to gain access to criminal wireless network to collect the required evidences/information automatically without physical access or wasting long time remotely, with accuracy of 99.8%. Abu Dhabi Police Drone Cyber Attack (ADDCA) will work based on automated hacking tools that work in conjunction with Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning technologies in small sophisticated drone cyber attack.

Project/Initiative Name: Perioperative Patient Blood Management (PBM) Program for scheduled surgeries
Description:
The General Oncology Hospital of Kifissia (GONK) developed a Perioperative Patient Blood Management (PBM) Program (ppt slide1). The need to implement PBM programs has been recommended by the World Health Organization since 2010 (International Regulation environment,  ppt slides 2-6). The PBM Program for patients with scheduled surgery, enables the timely diagnosis of preoperative anaemia and prevents unnececessary harmfull and costly Blood transfusions. 

The Perioperative PBM program offers the possibility of the timely pharmaceutical correction of the patients’ anaemia which greatly improves the patient clinical condition. The Perioperative PBM program at GONK hospital includes: 
1) Provision of health services in the context of optimal Patient Safety with a coordinated multidisciplinary approach with specific Procedures and Protocols 
2) Optimization of patient clinical status with the treatment of reversible causes of anaemia.

The added value of this innovative Health Service in GONK hospital concerns the increase in Patient Safety, the corresponding contribution to the achievement of blood sufficiency (the country imports 25,000 units of blood per year from the Swiss Red Cross, at a cost of approximately $5,000,000) and consequently finally the economic benefit to the National Health System. At the same time, there is a plan to diffuse this innovation to four other hospitals in Greece aiming to create common PBM Good Practice Guidelines in our country.
Novelty: As far as we know, this is the first time that the Perioperative PBM Service has been established in a Greek hospital and at the same time this is also the first time for a collaborative partnership between five Greek hospitals to elaborate common Good Practice on this particular subject.
Impact & Results:
With the implementation of Perioperative PBM Protocol, in 2021 the perioperative PBM Service was provided to 34 patients with scheduled surgery, and a gradual increase in the attendance of patients in the PBM clinic has been achieved 18 patients in 2020, 15 patients in 2019). 

With the effort to implement good practices "Optimal Blood Use" which is part of PBM protocol, the GONK OI AGIOI ANARGYROI hospital has achieved zero postponements of surgeries due to lack of blood units.

Project/Initiative Name: Collective Complaint Resolution System of Anti-Corruption and Civil Rights Commission
Description:
ACRC’s collective complaint resolution effectively coordinates and mediates complicate large-scale complaints and suggests alternatives agreed by all parties. The system protects citizens from the infringement of rights and interests and prevents the occurrence of social conflicts in a four-step approach:

- Sharp rise in collective complaints with conflicting interests of various stakeholders: With the way of life changing rapidly and social relationship getting more complex, conflicts arising in the process of public policy implementation are intensified. 
- Public dispute resolution between citizens and governments: In the past, collective complaints were mostly about simple disputes between a group and an administrative agency.
- Flexible operation of collective complaint resolution according to characteristics: Depending on the characteristics of each collective complaint, ACRC uses various processes, from traditional complaint resolution
- Institutional improvement to prevent collective grievances: If ACRC confirms each collective complaint was caused by unreasonable laws, it conducts a targeted survey nationwide to prevent similar complaints and improves the regulations concerned.
Novelty:
- The system is the only collective complaint resolution in Korea that handles all disputes between citizens and governments through mediation and settlement. Other dispute mediation procedures in Korea are set up by sectors under individual laws. ACRC’s procedure covers all disputes across public administrations including construction. 
- The system uses alternative dispute resolution in public dispute resolution. Because public sector mediation and settlement were usually conducted by the interested agency, a fair resolution were hard to be expected. In addition, because they were used as public discussion tool to prevent conflicts in big public projects, they could not practically remedy the rights and interests of those affected.
Impact & Results:
'- Since its launch in 2008 to 2021, ACRC has received and handled 4,056 collective complaints and 1,362 of them were resolved through mediation (600) and settlement (702). For the last 5 years (2017~2021), ACRC received and handled 1,336 collective complaints, among them 569 cases were resolved, with a resolution rate of 42.6%. 282 cases were mediated, 205 reached a consensus, 35 were issued corrective action, and 47 received opinions, and a total of 188,600 citizens were relieved. 
- In 2021, the number of resolved collective complaints was 121, up from the previous year of 113. The resolution rate also rose to 57.6% from 54.8% of the previous year. People getting relief last year reached 48,200.

Project/Initiative Name: Conclusion of a Memorandum of Cooperation for the Support of Parents with Premature Newborns/Infants
Organisation: General Children's Hospital of Athens "Panagiotis and Aglaia Kyriakou"
Description:
FAINARETI aims at promoting the physical and mental health of pregnant women, new mothers, fathers and their babies through the provision of an array of clinical services and health education. The organization has incorporated in its perinatal care services specialized interventions with respect to parents of premature babies, offering midwifery care as well as mental health support.  

 More specifically, FAINARETI has been operating since 2009 (under the supervision of the Mental Health Directorate of the Ministry of Health) the first Day Center for the Care of the Mental Health of Women (Perinatal Mental Disorders) in Greece, having until now served over 20,000 expectant and new parents.  At the same time "FAINARETI" operates, since 2014, a National Perinatal Mental Health Helpline, having, thus far, received more than 75.000 calls for mental health support and midwifery counselling. 

The Laboratory aims to conduct research in the fields of Midwifery and Neonatal Care, through the application of good practices and interventions during the period before and after childbirth. More specifically, it aims through research, to highlight important public health through the development of protocols in the fields of Midwifery and Neonatal Care.
Novelty:
The specific Memorandum of Cooperation is an innovative action as for the first time 3 different entities (a public pediatric hospital, a non-profit organization and a university laboratory) are cooperating with a common goal: to pay all possible effort to optimally respond to the health needs and health care of premature newborns as well as to strengthen their family environment. 

Also important is that parents through a dedicated Helpline can receive free midwifery counseling on breastfeeding and psychological support by specialized midwives and psychologists. Plus, the innovation promotes research and training of health professionals and strengthens the promotion of the health of premature newborns and their families in society.
Impact & Results:
Through the partnership of the 3 agencies, an attempt is made to improve the satisfaction of users and customers. Through the: 
- provision of free counseling on breastfeeding to the mothers of premature babies hospitalized at the Hospital and free psychological support to their parents, breastfeeding is strengthened and parents are included in integrated and systematic perinatal care programs, plus their psychological support is supported throughout the newborn's hospitalization as well as after discharge from the hospital, 
- planning and implementation of joint research actions promotes Science and scientific knowledge, 
- joint undertaking of projects and actions for society, the health of premature newborns and their families is promoted.

Project/Initiative Name: Modernization of real estate transactions in Greece via global digital procedures
Description:
In order to provide integrated digital services and facilitate real estate transactions in Greece, a wide range of digital services has been developed and is currently expanding by the Hellenic Cadastre in electronic submission/processing of transactions and the provision of legal and technical research web services to the users. The paper archives of the Land Registries that gradually come under the Cadastre are digitized to allow for digital maintenance and remote access by the end-user.

The Hellenic Cadastre has embarked on an ambitious process modernisation scheme, which gradually led to the development of a large number of digital applications and services including the digital submission of deeds, the web-based research in the cadastral data, the on line application and delivery of certificates, copies, abstracts of diagrams and the provision of Interoperability tools for accessing cadastral data by other public sector agencies. 

Collectively, these initiatives have the potential to render real transactions more user-friendly, transparent, less time-consuming for the average property owner and to reduce on-site customer load in the regional offices. Overall, they are expected to transform the real estate market, as they increase the quality of service provided through reliable 24/7 electronic services, improve accessibility to the data and shorten transaction processing times. 

Two of the services released in the past two years that have been widely accepted by the public and the professionals to the extent that are now obligatory have changed drastically the way transactions are carried out: 1.	Web-based issuance of certificates, copies and diagrams, 2.	Digital submission of topographic and cadastral plans, 3.	Digitalization of the Land Registry archives.
Novelty: These projects aim to modernise the land registry function of Cadastre through the simplification and improvement of processes, the provision of extended e-services and the digital transformation of the Registry Offices, which operate on paper-based procedures. These Offices for the first time will provide a wide range of e-services to the professionals (lawyers, notaries, bailiffs, surveying engineers), the public agencies and organizations and the citizens.
Impact & Results:
- Web-based issuance of certificates. In 2021, 82,5% of certificates, copies and diagrams were digitally issued. The respective percentage as of September 2022 was 92,5%. A total of 85% of the digital certificates were issued automatically without human intervention. 
- During this period, this service has been operating on a 24/7 basis with user support help desk from 08:00 to 16:00. The total number of digital certificates issued suggests that more than 400.000 visits to the cadastral offices and land registry offices were prevented. 
- Digital submission of topographic and cadastral plans. Since 2020 more than 370.000 diagrams have been submitted and are available for processing. Operating cadastral procedures are rendered faster and more reliable. 
- Digitalization of the Land Registry archives. The project is in its initial implementation phase.

Project/Initiative Name: myDYPAlive: Consulting services to jobseekers via teleconference and the provision of interpretation
Organisation: DYPA (Hellenic Public Employment Service former OAED)
Description:
myDYPAlive is a communication channel via which DYPA employment counsellors offer consulting services to jobseekers and businesses via teleconference. It was developed in the midst of the pandemic in order to serve jobseekers and employers who could not visit the local PES services in person due to  coronavirus restrictions. myDYPAlive offers interpretation in selected languages and sign language/lip reading. 

DYPA in collaboration with the General Secretariat of Information Systems utilised and developed this specific service tool for both unemployed persons and businesses, initially by including employment consulting services and with the view of adding other services in the future. 

Using myDYPAlive is quite simple and it does not require high digital skills. Visiting www.dypa.gov.gr the citizen can gather information about the services offered and using his/her taxisnet credentials s/he can choose the service of interest, the date and time of the booking and fill in his/ her contact details or of a third person participating the teleconference. 

Apart from facilitating citizens who do not have access to local PES services due to COVID-19 restrictions mydypalive aspires to offer services to citizens who either do not speak Greek, or belong to a vulnearble social group (unemployed persons with disabilities, former substance addicts, etc). The digital teleconference channel is an example of an innovative business solution in the public sector, arising from the need to respond to the pandemic circumstances.
Novelty:
It is the first time such a service is offered in the Greek Public sector. It was a communication channel with population groups (unemployed persons and businesses) even in the midst of the COVID-19 pandemic.

What is innovative is that the channel facilitates the access to employment counselling services provided by the Public Employment Service via teleconference with the simultaneous provision of interpretation (in 14 languages as well as sing language/ lip reading).
Impact & Results: The permanent citizen satisfaction measurement mechanism in place (with the use of an automated short questionnaire) registers a high satisfaction rate. In particular in a 5 degree scale of satisfaction rate (completely, very much, moderately, slightly, not at all) 87% of the unemployed persons and 88% of the employers state that they are completely or very satisfied with the service.

Project/Initiative Name: The Neoteric Past For A Bright Future (NPFBF)
Description:
The Neoteric Past For A Bright Future (NPFBF). This project is comparable to the beginning of an entirely new era of technology involving people. This project's goal is to develop a head-cap that contains integrated circuits and silicon fibers, and it will function using artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, and deep learning. It will be able to read a person's memories from the current day, the day before, or the days before that while the person is sleeping. 

The NPFBF is worn by the individual while he sleeps, just like any other headgear. This hat is capable of communicating with brain signals, reading a person's memory, and transmitting that information to a computer for analysis. The computer then issues reports relating to the wearer's health and makes recommendations regarding what they should avoid doing the following day. Knowing that the invention has the potential to be utilized in the security fields to a significant degree for the purpose of determining whether or not a person has been convicted of a crime despite the lack of tangible evidence against him. With the help of this innovation, we will be able to transfer data not only from the computer to the human memory but also from the human to the computer; this will be a significant step forward for human life on Earth and potentially other planets.
Novelty: We will be able to transfer data both from human memory to the computer and from the computer to human memory as a result of this project.
Impact & Results: This project intends to read human memories, then transmit those readings to the data analytics machine works through machine learning, artificial intelligence, deep learning, and computer vision. Who will then make use of them to enhance human health and increase the likelihood that he will correctly anticipate future occurrences. Additionally, in the event that he is a criminal or a suspect, that information can be used against him.

Project/Initiative Name: (e)-access of citizens to extensive lab testing from Primary Health Care Centers at Regional Level
Description:
Equity of access to free, extensive & elaborated lab testing in Primary Health Centres around Crete without the need to address to a hospital away from home was the driving idea for the establishment of a network of public Primary Healthcare Centres and reference hospitals. Sampling is performed locally, biological material samples are tested centrally at reference hospitals and citizens/doctors have access to results via web. This regionally provided service is the only one operating in Greece. 

This technological ability facilitated the electronic ordering of lab tests to reference hospital’s laboratories while sampling of biological material is performed locally at healthcare centers. This way, citizens have the ability of carrying out a full set of laboratory tests for FREE, without having to commute to hospital. Furthermore, both citizens and healthcare professionals have access to the lab results through web without the need to seek them by a physical visit to the reference hospital. The reduction of flows to hospitals and the assessment/care of incidents by primary healthcare, the timely and valid information of citizens and medical personnel and the reduction of costs were also the objective of this action. 

The service of electronic lab test ordering and transfer of samples of biological material from Primary Healthcare units to reference hospitals includes nineteen (19) primary units (sample collection points) and five hospitals, which are interconnected operationally through a detailed weekly program and electronically via a web based service on a strictly monitored VPN network at regional level. Sample transport is carried out through a certified transport company through annual contracts. This transport network is also used for the transport of other samples of biological material in the context of, for example, the management of the pandemic (samples of COVID-19) and other surveillance networks.
Novelty:
The main technological innovations refer to the interconnection of all units under the public Sector's telecom infrastructure (SYZEFXIS), the parameterization and use of the Integrated Health Information System of Crete and the management of a large number of users in terms of training and status update.    

The services provided to both healthcare professionals and citizens are innovative in the way that they are delivered on site, without the need to access a secondary health unit away from domicile and results are delivered electronically via web.
Impact & Results:
The service of electronic ordering and transfer of biological samples from primary healthcare units to reference hospitals started on 13-2-2017.  

Patient satisfaction and out-of-pocket reduction were achieved, since they are served for free by a health unit near their residence. Economies of scale were achieved and the capacity of reference hospital laboratories was maximized. 

Indicatively, in 2018 161,914 samples of biological material were trafficked, while in 2019 the number was 213,649, an increase of 31.95%, indicative of the acceptance by citizens. In 2020 and 2021, despite pandemic, 214,044 and 252,746 samples were handled respectively.

Description:
The Regulatory Institute is focussed on regulatory quality assurance tools to address the problem of incomplete laws. Regulatory quality assurance tools assist lawmakers improve the quality of laws by triggering more conscious choices. Lawmaking public officials may use such tools as the Handbook: how to regulate?, regulatory articles covering over 80 policy sectors, model laws and the soon to be published Model Law Library as part of the quality assurance process all laws should complete. 

The Regulatory Institute has focussed since 2014 on regulatory quality assurance tools to assist lawmaking public officials improve the quality of the laws they make. Looking at the content of the law and its quality aspects, is distinguished from the work of other organisations involved in preparing or revising law or assisting with impact assessments. 

The regulatory quality assurance tools of the Regulatory Institute include the following: 
- The Handbook: how to regulate?, which presents an inventory of regulatory techniques from over 40 jurisdictions and a basic universal method. The Handbook is based on the idea that lawmaking public officials with an inventory of regulatory techniques have more choices and can develop better regulations. 
- Our regulatory articles at www.howtoregulate.org that compare regulatory techniques in different policy fields and from different jurisdictions to outline the various approaches to regulating a particular subject or issue to trigger more complete regulations. 
- The Model Laws created by the Regulatory Institute are designed to be used by lawmaking public officials as a toolbox, checklist, raw material or a basis for the development of an adapted law and optimised as such.
Novelty:
The Regulatory Institute's regulatory quality assurance tools are an innovation in lawmaking because: 
- They can be used by anyone irrespective of their lawmaking experience/qualification, which is particularly useful as not all lawmakers have a law background, and those with a law background may not have regulatory skills as this is not usually taught in law school. 
- The tools highlight important decisions to be taken by officials without pre-empting choices. This respects that every law has its own policy goals, and the tools highlight the regulatory techniques that help achieve those goals. 
- They can be applied to any policy subject or problem, which frees up the time of the official to focus on making conscious choices, possibly even permitting time for reflection. 
- The tools clearly outline the costs and benefits of each regulatory technique, which facilitates planning for implementation and enforcement of the law to counter the consequences of conscious choices.
Impact & Results: Several hundred laws are now more complete due to the regulatory quality assurance tools we have created as evidenced by elements in the final legislation coinciding with the approaches outlined in the tools or with the suggestions provided directly. The public officials who requested our assistance directly received clear responses to their regulatory issue, which ensured closer alignment between regulatory measures and policy goals. We have received written feedback from government officials (former Commissioner Andriukaitis) and civil society organisation (Indian anti-tobacco institute) about how our tools have helped them in their regulatory work.

Description:
We aim to create harmony and symbiosis between complex human behaviour and natural ecosystems. Singrass has developed multiple patented ecological technologies as a result of our passion for urban ecology.  

With a focus on the ecological optimisation of the indoor environment, Singrass endeavours to design green buildings with aesthetics in mind without compromising functionality. A cohesive ecological indoor environment has to be pleasing on the eye and calming on the mind.     
 
 Singrass is committed to provide green and innovative technological solutions to construct a harmonious urban ecosystem. By introducing our Indoor Smart Eco Farm to offices, we strive to contribute towards Singapore's "30 by 30" vision for food production “Three Zero” Farm.
Novelty:
Indoor Smart Eco Farm 
- Covers an area of only 0.3 square meters per unit, using the indoor free space, can be placed everywhere 
- The height of 1.7 meters is the aesthetic height of the screen, which is easy to maintain and harvest 
- Covers the CO2-rich area which is about 1500ppm 
- 157 planting cups, production is about 10kg /mon/unit 
- LED full spectrum light strip, 1:1 analog sunlight; not harmful to human eye 
- Only consumes about 1.2kwh of electricity per day, lighting system can be replaced by indoor smart eco farm, realizing negative power consumption

Project/Initiative Name: Tool for medical and nursing intervention for prevention of CLABSI at Pediatric Intesive Care Unit
Organisation: Children's Hospital of Athens "Panagiotis and Aglaia Kyriakou"
Description:
The Aglaia 's Kyriakou PICU implements the tool/checklist in which the nursing interventions that must be applied by the nurse to prevent central line associated bloodstream infections are recorded daily.  All children who admit in PICU are benefit. Organize and document the medical and nursing interventions for the deteriorating patients, can improve the staff compliance to international guidelines for prevention the nosocomial infections. 

The nurses' compliance to guidelines regarding the day care of CVC was inspected by the managing Tool "Interventions and Infections checklist". This document is a check list which reminds the steps that nurses need to follow daily in order to prevent CLABSI. This tool was submitted in scientific council with register number 16867/14-11-2014. 

The programme and the review of the CVC managment tool implemented in the middle of 2020, while in PICU were applying the actions for the insertion of CVC. The nurses' compliance regarding the documentation and usage of this tool for 2020 was 70% and the first 3 months of 2021 increased to 100%, while the compliance to guidelines occurred from 55% to 95%. During this time the incidence of CLABSI was zero.
Novelty:
The innovation include the introduction and implementation of a checklist  which are recording 1. Patient's informations, admission date, diagnosis, 2. Dates of specific medical interventions (insertion, implementation and management of CVC), 3. Date, type and results for the cultures which were taken, 4. Date that IV giving set needs to be changed (according the protocols) 5. Date of care for CVC, Hickman, was performed (according the protocols) 6.Continuous skin inspection and inspection, staging and care of pressure ulcers. 

The fact that all these informations can be recorded in one document and the continuing staff's education and observation played an important role in preventing nosocomial infections and especially infections related of CVC (CLABSI).
Impact & Results:
Pediatric Intensive Care Unit started the systematic recording of Central Line Associated Bloodstream Infection  (CLABSI) back in 2012, between 2012 and 2013 the incidence of CLABSI was 12.3 cases per 1000 catheter's day. 

The nurses' compliance to guidelines regarding the day care of Central Venous Catheter was inspected by the managing tool "Interventions and Infections document". This document is a check list which reminds the steps that nurses need to follow daily in order to prevent (CLABSI).

Project/Initiative Name: Public Policy Decision Architecture for the Design of Nudging Strategies in the Department of Casanare
Description:
This innovative research is developed from the approach of behavioral economics and has as its fundamental premise the recognition of cognitive and emotional factors on the social, political and economic decisions of individuals, groups and organizations. It is intended to find effective solutions to certain problems of the Casanare region and the country, regarding the design and implementation of social and environmental public policies. 
 
Currently, decision-making processes in public administration are assumed within the standard economic model from a conventional and reductionist perspective, where people are perfectly "rational" when interacting and deciding within the system, i.e., in most cases the psychological and cultural aspects are omitted, which is why public policies do not achieve a positive impact on the population, do not benefit the communities that need it most, and do not generate social transformations that lead to the welfare and development of individuals and social groups. 

Innovation through research seeks to generate transcendental implications for these real problems. In the department of Casanare there is some degree of dissatisfaction among the population with respect to the results of current social and environmental public policies, as well as a general feeling that these do not point to real and effective solutions, thus increasing the social gap. The fact of not having serious studies for the formulation of these public policies, which allow to give clarity and objectivity to the diagnoses and interventions. 

Consequently, given the absence in the department of Casanare and the country of an explanatory study that understands and assumes public policies, from the architecture of decisions and the use of behavioral sciences, we are currently working on the construction of an economic model of decision making based on this approach.
Novelty: The main objective of the innovation on which we are currently working is the construction of an economic model for decision making in public policies, from the understanding of the decision architecture and the use of behavioral sciences in the department of Casanare. The proposed economic model will be validated through a pilot in the department of Casanare to evaluate the possibility of transferring knowledge to the rest of the country.
Impact & Results: The recognition of the variables that influence decision making around public policies in the department of Casanare and the behavioral gaps of the citizens, are the object of study of this innovation. The results are able to influence the prognosis of individual and collective behaviors, thus providing a basis for the analysis and implementation of public policies, for the construction of an economic model of decision making, as a premeditated measure and with a positive impact on the decision architecture. In order to "push" people towards the most favorable alternatives to improve their own well-being and that of others.

Project/Initiative Name: Scheduling: organization and transparency to the process of castration of dogs and cats
Description:
The City Hall of São Gonçalo (RJ) started the process of digitization of services for the animals of the municipality, aiming, mainly, at the optimization of the servers' work processes and reduction of queues, paper use and costs for the municipality and citizens. The initial kick-off was in the castration of animals. In the Colab application, every month about 336 vacancies are offered for scheduling. The service brings more transparency and simplicity regarding the conditions and process of castration. 

The appointment management system, meanwhile, is allowing for a more automated, secure, space-efficient process, as well as smarter management of cancellations, reschedules, absences, and repressed demand through the use of data. In addition, the relationship with citizens is happening in a more direct and continuous way, through important communications (about the service or about the awareness of cancellations/absences) and reminders of the attendance dates. 

The expectation is that the municipality will perform up to 5,000 animal surgeries per year, with appointments taking place primarily via digital means. In-person appointments still happen for those who do not have knowledge or access to the internet.

Another evolution of the solution would be to have the patients' records and files also in digital format. Although the scheduling is conditioned to a "pre-registration", some information is still repeated and officially kept and considered on paper. To improve the quality of care for the citizen, it is understood that the diversification of digital channels is also important. Thus, making the service available via the web will be a way to further democratize access.
Impact & Results:
One of the main results was in relation to the adhesion of users to the platform since the launch of the service. The service was launched on 12/08/2022 and, from the launch until the end of August, we had the adhesion of 2991 users (21% of the total user base of São Gonçalo today). From the launch until 10/01/22, there was an adhesion of 4450 (31% of the total user base). It is possible to infer that the result of user acquisition was due to the launch. 

The usefulness of the service could also be observed, given that 336 places are sold out in less than 3 days, which also signals a repressed demand for the service.

Description:
The innovation was developed with the aim of more efficient management of professional training in public administration. The LMS platform is a unique information system for all public administration employees, which, apart from online training, also has developed modules for registration, records and generation of certificates. A virtual classroom, which automatically exchanges data with the LMS platform, is integrated.

The LMS platform established unique records in respect of trainings attended by employees in public administration. Each user has his own account, via which he/she can access training applications, training materials, online training content and electronically signed certificates in just two clicks. Linking the LMS platform with the Virtual Classroom had yielded two benefits: part of the work performed by employees at NAPA is automated, while the users have access to both systems with a single login.

Digitization of the training implementation process was also used in order to automate and simplify the procedures themselves. In short, a simplified, automated and digitized system for conducting trainings has been established.
Novelty:
Prior to the establishment of the system, the training participants registered for training electronically, but had to fill-in the same data for each training. With the introduction of this System, each training participant has his own profile and can register for Training in just two clicks. 

Now, the certificates are signed with a qualified electronic seal, and are available to training participants in electronic form just a couple of days after the training. In addition to the increase in efficiency, huge savings were also achieved because over 25,000 certificates have not been printed in the previous two years, and the services concerning their physical sending and delivery by mail were not paid for.
Impact & Results:
More than 14,000 employees from the public administration, local self-government units and bodies of autonomous provinces are currently registered on the platform. In addition to the user database, the platform also includes a database of implemented programmes, areas, topics, accredited implementers, bodies which conduct trainings via the system, authorities and contact persons, locations and resources.

Since then, almost 1,000 events have been conducted and recorded using the platform. There are currently 60 online training courses available on the platform.

Project/Initiative Name: Electronic System for Document Submission in real time
Organisation: Decentralized Administration of Macedonia and Thrace
Description:
Decentralized Administration of Macedonia& Thrace is a Public Organization which receives a plethora of documents daily both from citizens, private companies& public organizations, regarding its various sectors. The system's innovation lies in the fact that documents submitted through it are automatically inserted in the Organization's Document Management System,enabling the applicant to track the course of the application in real time and receive the answer through an electronic repository. 

The System is interconnected with the Organization's Electronic Document and Records Management System (EDRMS) "Irida". When using our innovative System, the document -  request is inserted electronically to EDRMS "Irida" and receives a unique protocol number automatically, without any secretarial staff's action.

Consequently the record is being electronically processed, through "Irida", in order to be assigned and fulfilled by the competent service. Each user aquires an electronic repository, through which can track the course of the request in real time, and monitor its assingnement within the organization. As soon as the request is fulfilled from the competent employee, the user is automatically informed through email and SMS on the mobile, that the response to the request is the electronic repository.
Novelty: The implementation of the Electronic Document Submission System is innovative because it has fully digitalized the process of submitting a request and fulfilling it, whether it comes from a private (employee - citizen) or an entity (company - public organization), without physical presence at any stage. Moreover security is ensured at login to the System either through tax records authentication or through multiple verifications when registered with email.
Impact & Results: The implementation of the System had led to full digitalization of processes concerning electronic document submission and online service of requests, submitted from employees of DAMT, citizens, private companies and public organizations. There has been significant reduction to the process time required to fulfill a request, as well as man-hours of the organization's human resources.

Description:
The innovation,  "ORPHAN AND NON-MARKETED MEDICINES  PLATFORM",  is a digital platform through which a doctor will be able to submit a request for an orphan/non-marketed medicine and to report the availability of an unused medicine. The innovation was developed because the current system in Greece is often outdated, time consuming and has a negative impact towards the patients. 

Non-marketed medicines are dispensed only from one EOPYY pharmacy in Attica (central pharmacy). The pharmacists of the central EOPYY pharmacy have created an excel document  in which they keep all the information related to the orders: 1) accurate order dates, 2) personal information of the patients (AMKA number, address, mobile, etc), 3) number and date of the pharmaceutical products delivered by IFET.

The innovation will benefit those in need of orphan and rare diseases drugs as well as patients with serious diseases requiring innovative medicines. A rare disease is a disease that affects fewer than 5 persons in 10 000. While 5 in 10 000 may appear to be very few, it still adds up to approximately 246 000 people in the EU for each disease. According to EOPYY central pharmacy's data,  approximately 476 patients received an orphan/non- marketed medicine until September 2022 and the total number of traded drugs was 144.
Novelty:
Our project is innovative, because: 
-it creates a new approach for patients suffering from orphan/life-threatening illnesses in order to receive their treatment on time 
-it reduces the pharmaceutical expenditure, by tackling the wasteful spending,  
-it focuses on specific categories of patients,  using innovative  monitoring technology to identify the actual needs (real life data) of prescribing innovative, highly-cost, new medicines.  
-it will develop multidisciplinary tasks among healthcare systems
Impact & Results:
Ιmproving the quality of health services by: 
-Receiving medication on time (better clinical result expected, better patient compliance) 
-Easier, direct procedure  (reducing bureaucracy) 
-Contributing drastically to the financial vialability of the healthcare systems by reducing healthcare expenditure  
- Improving the enviromental impact by reducing the amount of unused, expensive medicines  
-Facilitating interaction between countries and data exhanging (Cross-border platform) 
-Data source for epidemiological studies, referring to serious/orphan diseases 

Currently, it is estimated that, worldwide, more than 300 million people suffer from the 6,000 to 8,000 different rare diseases that exist, of which 50% affect children, with 30% of these children dying by the age of 5 years.

Description:
The Government of Turkey has activated a new digital platform, “Support to Investment” in order to facilitate accessing information related with state financed support programmes, incentive regime and local investment opportunities in an innovative way.  Main target group of the platform consists of potential investors considering to make investment in Turkey. 

The Investment Support platform consists of various modules that feed each other. Public Support Programs Search Module: By providing a minimum level of keywords, investors can easily access all kinds of financial support and grant programs implemented by public institutions. Users can check which programs are still active for applications, program priorities, eligible costs, example project topics, etc.  

nvestment Opportunities: Investors have free access to high-quality pre-feasibility reports prepared by DAs for private sector investment projects with growth potential, high value-added and in line with the region’s needs and priorities. New pre-feasibility reports are added to the platform periodically. There are 382 published pre-feasibility report as of February 2022. Investment Glossary: Investors can access definitions of important terms related to investment and commercial activities and their definitions. This glossary is updated regularly.
Novelty:
1-	It is practical and agile, providing investors up-to-date guidance and guidance in terms of financial incentive opportunities that investors benefit from without dealing with consulting companies.
2-	It is one of the best example in terms of rapid service delivery in the public sector.
3-	Various ministries and public institutions in Turkey have already some informative web portals for the programs they carry out.
Impact & Results: Since the platform has been operational, it has been met with great interest by users. According to latests Google Analytics records (As of the date 13/10/2022) there has been almost 940.000 unique site visit to the platform. Incentive wizard has produced 215.000 automatized incentive opportunity reports for users. Also with “Question and Answer” module 1950 questions of the investors were answered through the platform.

Organisation: General Directorate of Services for Persons with Disabilities and the Elderly, Ministry of Family and Social Services
Description:
The Ministry of Family and Social Services of the Republic of Türkiye has developed a new community based care service model, “Elderly Support Program”, to support ageing in place and older persons’ participation in social life without leaving their own living environment. Through this, the Ministry has raised awareness on ageing, supported older persons in their home environment by mobilizing local dynamics and supporting local governments.

The objectives of the Elderly Support Program are:
1) To develop and diversify community based care in line with the family-oriented social service policy.
2) To support older persons and families with a person-oriented service approach.
3) To mobilize local dynamics and support local governments to develop elderly care services based on regional needs.

Monitoring and Inspection Commissions, including Provincial Directorates of the Ministry have been established within the Governorship in the provinces. These commissions have been authorized to monitor the implementation stages of the projects that are carried out by the local governments. These commissions constantly monitor the implementation stages of the projects and submit reports to the Ministry in quarterly periods. As the projects’ activities are completed, financial supports are transferred in quarterly periods following the conformity report from the Provinces.
Novelty:
The Elderly Support Program is an innovation in community based care for older persons because:
1) It is the first project-based support program for older persons with the largest budget, transferred from the general budget, within the scope of community-based care services.
2) It contributes to an inclusive sustainable development goals giving the Ministry to select most needed and effective projects that a specific province need in terms of care services and allow the Ministry to develop care policy based on the regional conditions.
3) It raises awareness on ageing and promotes local governments to diversify and strengthen care services for older persons and allow them to provide unique opportunity to find innovative solutions for care problems of the region.
Impact & Results: Within the scope of ESP, a total of 36.092.000 TRY was provided for the projects conducted by the local governments in 6 years. 55 different projects have been carried out by 25 provincial municipalities and 6 district municipalities in 6 years. In this context 111.559 older persons in 76.497 households benefited from home care support services including psycho-social support and cultural activities. An additional 3 million TRY was transferred for the projects to be carried out in 2022.

Project/Initiative Name: National Museum Inventory System (MUES) of Türkiye
Organisation: Directorate-General Cultural Assets and Museums/Ministry of Culture and Tourism
Description:
What remains from the past is knowledge and experience transferred from generation to generation with written documents, and artistic expressions. As a great leap forward, the Ministry initiated MUES to take maximum advantage of information technologies to secure the past knowledge of arts and crafts by using it for the sake of future generations. MUES makes a major contribution to the cultural heritage field by improving accessibility, supporting better decision-making and real-time monitoring.

MUES is designed as an all-in-one web-based platform for recording the whole journey of historical objects, beginning with the first discovery on excavation site till its storage or exhibition. MUES databases include a vast amount of information consisting of technical, safekeeping, chronological, and production properties of artifacts. Including, details on custody and care of artifacts such as the room where the artifact is kept, the location within room, and the placement at the location. In addition, characteristics and quality of museum areas such as their climatic conditions and security infrastructures are also accessible. 

The database is equipped with an Image search function supported by Artificial intelligence. This feature is open for the accession of law enforcement in case of involvement of a specific artifact in a criminal issue. Further, a project phase has been completely dedicated to terminological and practical unity among museums. Lastly, a dashboard has been developed to enable executives for checking whether the system functions properly or not and to monitor the digitalization real-time.
Novelty:
1.Its country wide application scale. MUES is a digital transformation initiative rather than being just an asset.
2.Has a modular design consisting of 7 systems which are the core Inventory System, Anti-Smuggling, Commission and Valuation, Private Museums and Collectors, Restoration Laboratory, Excavation, and Museum Information Desk. Integrated Systems share information to support multipurpose institutional processes. This means, that once the data is entered into the Inventory System, other systems can retrieve the data
3. Datasets are created by expert groups countrywide.
4. Experts use drop down menues while making their routine inventorial works, this decreases the operators’ error margin almost zero. This also reinforces the comparative research functionality of the system.
5. Users access the system through e-government Gateway by using their citizenship IDs and passwords which makes any activity legally bonding. This also makes organizations more transparent and accountable.
Impact & Results:
MUES is one of 146 granted projects by the Presidency in line with the 2023 targets of Türkiye.
During standardization process 7000 lines of data have been standardized by the consensus of committee members.
Turkish Museums have currently digitalized 26% of the national inventory. Within 81 provinces in Türkiye, 110 Museum Directorates, 476 Affiliated Museums, and 1025 Museum Staff have been registered to MUES. Approximately 60 thousand publications have been associated with the digitalized inventory. The system is capable of designating very self-authorizations for different users with16 roles, in almost 6000 authorization fields.
The completion rate of Project has been reached 75% and the whole system will be ready to be activated by the end of 2023.
User training survey results showed that 92% of attendees considered that MUES is highly qualified, and 98% of them were highly satisfied with the training, and the performance of the trainers.

Description: ActInsight contributes to the collective efforts to integrate, analyse and visualize data across actors, at all levels, businesses and civil societies, and to understand their contribution and impact within the various climate initiatives.
Novelty: Better insights into collective climate actions. Interactive & visual approach to understand trends & patterns between actors. The platform is open and accessible to everyone. All code & data are open source, to encourage further collaboration & impact. Live data-sharing API, opening the platform's data layer for better collaboration, while leveraging climate data interoperability by following the OpenClimate Schema, designed & maintained by the OpenEarth Foundation team.

Project/Initiative Name: Electronic System for Document Submission in real time
Organisation: Decentralized Administration of Macedonia and Thrace
Description: Decentralized Administration of Macedonia& Thrace is a Public Organization which receives a plethora of documents daily both from citizens, private companies& public organizations, regarding its various sectors. The system's innovation lies in the fact that documents submitted through it are automatically inserted in the Organization's Document Management System,enabling the applicant to track the course of the application in real time and receive the answer through an electronic repository.

Project/Initiative Name: HITAP (Social Security Services Registration Program of Public Officials)
Description: Over 3 million civil servants nationwide are subject to detailed social security legislation. Before the HITAP, the manual entry of the Social Security information of public officials was preventing uniformity in practice and causing errors and confusions. Especially in the retirement phase, this caused time losses. The HITAP program, which was developed to solve these problems, minimized the margins of manual error thanks to the rules in the program, and shortened the retirement pension period.

Project/Initiative Name: The ACEP (Integration of Smart Technologies into Prisons) Project
Organisation: Ministry of Justice/General Directorate of Prison and Detention Houses, Ministry of Justice
Description: The ACEP Project is an innovative project that greatly digitizes prison practices. Project was developed to contribute to the rehabilitation processes of convicts by strengthening the ties of them with their families and relatives. With this system, for the first time in history of Turkish Penitentiary System, the convicts were able to have the opportunity to meet their relatives easily by means of the video-call through the multi media devices placed in their rooms or wards.

Project/Initiative Name: ELECTRONIC PUBLIC PROCUREMENT PLATFORM (EKAP) (ELEKTRONIK KAMU ALIMLARI PLATFORMU)
National Judicial Network Information System (UYAP – Ulusal Yargı Ağı Bilişim Sistemi)

Organisation: Investment Office of the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye
Description:
The digital guidelines are mapping the investment procedures via diagrams and tables. Through these guidelines, investors can access all the procedures they will encounter when they want to invest in Türkiye. After the investor determines which sector she wants to invest in, she can have information about all the necessary permits, approvals and licenses, as well as their processing times and costs.

Especially international investors are prone to gather information about all these procedures before making an investment decision. However, in many countries there are no online accessible systems about these investment procedures. For this reason, it takes a lot of time for the investors to learn these procedures, which prevents the investor from making an investment decision.

Project/Initiative Name: Strengthening Social Cohesion And Inclusion Through Sports Among Refugee And Turkish Youth

Organisation: Ministry of Environmental, Urbanization And Climate Change

The State Agency for Public Service
  and Social Innovations under the President 
  of the Republic of Azerbaijan
Description:
AMIS is a one-stop platform that lets citizens feedback on socially significant problems without having to figure out which government agency to contact. 

The platform allows complaints about public utilities, roads and transport problems, current maintenance, improvement of infrastructure to be sent by citizens to the relevant government agencies on an electronic map and to monitor the results of these complaints.

The purpose of establishing AMIS is to ensure citizen participation in the solution and management of public issues, timely and on-site solution of problems by the relevant agencies, improve the population’s social welfare and make cities and regions more livable. Government agencies ensure that information on the status of complaints received through AMIS, as well as documents on the outcome of complaints, are entered into the system.
Novelty: The project is innovative because it completely innovated the service process from collecting complaints to receiving feedback on resolved complaints. So innovative AMIS ensures citizen participation in decision making processes.
Impact & Results:
According to the application strategy of the AMIS , the creation of a comfortable city environment for life will be ensured throughout the country in cooperation with state institutions joined AMIS. 

The application statistics are as follows: - Number of applications sent to the program: 2674 - Number of applications sent to organizations: 1693 - Number of ongoing applications: 724 - Number of applications provided: 982

Description:
The innovation is an online hackathon which took place March 20–22. This online event focused on creating prototypes that can be sustained and can create impact beyond Germany. 28,000 participants showed up online and generated over 1,500 ideas for combating the pandemic.

These programs were designed to demonstrate solidarity and empower civil society to come up with innovative ideas on how to tackle the COVID-19 crisis. For this unique case, the first and foremost goal was not the sustainability of every project, but its feasibility and effectiveness. Many solutions might be critically important right now, but not in three weeks from now.

The Solution Builder focuses on developing prototypes into effective solutions as fast as possible. Over 150 projects made their way into implementation. The #WirVsVirus implementation programme enables rapid testing and user-centred development. The ideas from civil society unfold the greatest possible benefit for civil society. The #WeVsVirus concept is transferable to other challenges.
Novelty: Low-threshold and crowdsourced mechanism of creating solutions focused on feasibility and effectiveness to address the challenges of the COVID-19 pandemic

Project/Initiative Name: 18F - a technology and design consultancy inside the government
Description:
18F helps civilian and military federal agencies across the United States to deliver projects that fulfill their mission, stay within budget, and use leading technology practices.

18F does:
- Modernize software development processes while introducing good agile and human-centered design practices to your agency
- Improve public-facing services like websites or applications
- Digitize and streamline internal systems to save time and increase accuracy

It uses interagency agreements to set up the terms of the projects so they collaborate with other agencies to fix technical problems, build products, and improve how government serves the public through technology.

The scientific team of Dubai Police has designed and created special reagents that could be used worldwide in every forensic genetics laboratory to identify males, and even differentiate between closely related male individuals. The team from General Department of Forensic Science and Criminology is the first government law enforcement scientific teams to design and produce a multiplex system DNA kit to the market powerful enough to differentiate related male individuals.

The kit designed is called “Microread 26 RM Y-Plex ID system”, which is an innovation system that uses a 6 dye fluorescent technology and a co-amplification method to detect 26 rapidly mutating Y-STR alleles. Our kit is proven to be highly sensitive allowing it to obtain full profiles and effective locus information as low as DNA input of 0.125 ng. In addition to detecting trace amounts of male DNA when present in a large amount of background female DNA. It can generate a full profile in a mixed sample of male to female up to 1:3200 ratio. Also, the kit has a flexible application suitable for different sample types, full & clear could be obtained with extracted DNA and direct amplification of blood cards, saliva cards, swabs and more.

This novel kit showed the best practice and ideal methodology when elaborating the power of public/private partnership, as this kit was developed alongside the scientific team in Microread Genetics Bejing. It is important to note that, although this kit was released into the market in 2022, it is already being used in forensic laboratories in 5 different countries. The kit will continue to serve justice and solve crimes that would be impossible to solve using traditional male Y-STR multiplex kits.

Ultimately, the multiplex system developed has contributed to solving over 103 cases by April 2022, which included 7 homicide cases and 51 complicated sexual assault cases in Dubai.
Novelty: This innovation is a scientific tool that has been developed and validated for forensic DNA profiling purposes. The novely lies in the scientific sensitivity of the tool.
Impact & Results: The innovation has helped solving 103 cases in Dubai. Further, it has also been utilized to analyse 150 DNA samples extracted from skeleton remains of World War II victims that were found in Republic of Slovenia and Republic of Serbia, which could connect these remains to their family relatives and return those remains to their families. Lastly, 14 Forensic Laboratories around the world have officially approached Dubai Police to collaborate and implement the tool on their cases.

Project/Initiative Name: A cross-sectoral cooperation model for supporting young people in the NEET situation in local governments
Organisation: Education and Youth Board (Republic of Estonia)
Description: A cross-sectoral cooperation model was created in order to support youth not in employment, education or training (NEET) in local self-governments by cooperating even more effectively, considering current practices. The creation of the model is innovative, because the methodology of service design was used for this. Also 25 interviews of target groups young people or experts working with them provided valuable input. A result is a realistically usable framework for local governments.
Novelty: The model was completed in the process of service design together with many different industry partners, and its result was actually piloted. Innovative were the methods used in the process and the participants' own contribution in the form of homework. In the service design process, 6 personas were created, which help to better understand the stories of the target group of young people.
Impact & Results: During the piloting of the model, it has become clear which places of cooperation need to be developed in local municipalities, which networks to create, and how to do more across the region than before to support young people in NEET situation. The effectiveness of the implementation of the model can be evaluated after a longer period, including the reduction of the number of young people in the NEET situation.

Project/Initiative Name: A Sea Fog Forecasting System based on Artificial Intelligence (AI)
Description:
The Korea Hydrographic and Oceanographic Agency (KHOA) of the Ministry of Oceans and Fisheries of Korea has developed a Sea Fog Forecast System based on Artificial Intelligence (AI). This natural phenomenon, made worse by climate change, is a threatening factor to navigation safety at the ports and it is the biggest cause of vessel collision (23%). 

The innovative system developed by KHOA predicts the occurrence and dissipation of sea fog to prevent maritime accidents around ports. It is currently implemented to improve ship loading and unloading in nine major trading ports and in Haeundae Beach, one of the most famous in Korea.

The Sea Fog Forecast System based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) innovatively adopts this technology to forecast sea fog. It is able to provide unparalled rapidity in the forecasting calculations, it can take as inputs a variety of data (oceanographic, meteorological, satellite, CCTV) that are already collected by governmental and non-governmental organizations, and it is easy to install and to operate, as it can be used without a complete understanding of the complex dynamics of sea fog generation.
Novelty:
Before, due to the shortage of manpower and limited managerial resources, watching with the eyes and warning to the sailing ships was everything that port authorities could do. Now, the Sea Fog Forecast System based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) provides many novelties. Three major ones can be found:
1. Users are provided optimized information on short-term prediction (1, 3, and 6 hours) with unprecedented rapidity and accuracy
2. Users can easily access a user-friendly visualization of sea fog information through an online tool
3. Users are enabled to make efficient traveling schedules for passengers and sailors, and maritime management plans for the harbor workers
