Impact & Results: Regarding to the sea fog formation, the prediction performance measured by Success Ratio (SR), referred to Post Agreement (PAR) as well, has a range of 25~65% for nine ports. In the context of the project, two patents were issued by Korean Intellectual Property Office and five academic articles were published. Lastly, an increase of online users was found. Visitors increase up to 350% (from 37,091 hits in 2016 to 132,780 hits in 2018).

Organisation: Ministry of Economic Affairs and Communications
Description:
We detect wicked problems and turn them into new spaces for innovation. It is a platform for all Estonian ministries, the public and the private sector, experts and entrepreneurs to join forces and build the future that we deserve. We are untapping new markets with a powerful but lean public-private collaboration.

Here is how it works:
1. Through interviews and public events, we identify which individuals and ministries are open to rapid innovation. We only work with those that are currently willing to change.
2. With these ministries, we define the complex problems that need to be solved and which don't have a solution yet.
3. Then we create a call for innovators that offer possible out-of-the-box solutions to these complex issues.
4. In a competitive atmosphere, we figure out which of these innovators deserve a proper investment for validation and implementation.
5. For these few that are chosen, we offer a curated development of their idea with access to government, an R&D grant that matches the needs of a meaningful pilot, and the Accelerate Estonia brand to highlight the necessity and urgency of the experiment.
6. It is then up to the innovators and their public sector counterparts to prove they can push the edge of government where it has never reached before.
7. For those that make it, there will be follow-up investments available either from public or private sources, or as a combination of the two.
Novelty:
Accelerate Estonia is lending a solution from the corporate world - bringing in entrepreneurs-in-residence to understand and solve for complex issues. But we are only offering the solution in situations where know that the organisations are capable of innovation.

In such a way, we bypass the lack of innovation capacity by enabling innovation-friendly government organisations with partners that are willing to shake the status quo.
Impact & Results:
Funding has been secured and increased until 2025. Portfolio size has reached about 25 in total. Route to economic impact has been defined and ingrained into renewed strategy.

In addition to that, 20% of the portfolio has raised VC funding or is en route to do so. Another 20% have created special purpose vehicles to build on the achievements of the experiments. 20% have failed due to different reasons (team, timing, policy concerns). For some part of the portfolio, it is too early to indicate results.

Organisation: Ministry of National Planning and Economic Policy
Description:
Innovation was developed due to the problem of generating public policies with different elaboration methodologies but without an adequate development for their execution, implementation or operationalisation. Therefore, the innovation offers the opportunity to strengthen the process of public policy construction in the National Planning System of the Costa Rican State. It is innovative because it standardises and strengthens the work of public policy making.

The course of action began with a process of capacity building and training of human resources specialised in public policy and the development of an advisory guide for the elaboration of public policies and their action plans for all Costa Rican institutions, using the methods of problem tree definition, theory of change and implementation theory. This allows for innovation in the planning system with a results-based management approach for development in the use of institutional resources and the generation of strategic alliances for development.
Novelty:
The innovation presented strengthens the execution, implementation and operationalisation of new national public policies within the framework of the country's development priorities. This has allowed public policy to advance qualitatively and quantitatively in the execution of programmes and projects of the institutions.
Moreover, this innovation has transcended not only in the generation of national public policies, but is also having an impact on the formulation of policies at the cantonal, local or territorial level. This is aimed at strengthening the communication platform from the national to the local level.
Impact & Results:
Results are measured in terms of the number of public policies advised with the public policy formulation guide. This is measured through the National Planning System Repository.
Its effect or impact is that it generates proposals for good practices in the formulation of public policy, and also manages to generate sustained public policies over time, regardless of the political changes of each administration in office (change of authorities every 4 years).

Project/Initiative Name: AfricArXiv - community-led towards building an African-owned open scholarly repository
Organisation: AfricArXiv - The Pan African Open Scholarly Repository
Description:
AfricArXiv is a community-led digital archive for research made in Africa. Currently they are working towards building an African-owned open scholarly repository; a knowledge commons of African scholarly repository; and a knowledge commons of African scholarly works. The aim is to catalyze African position in the research landscape by partnering with established scholarly repository to provide a platform for African scientists of any discipline. 

They are currently building a map of institutions, researchers and topics to connect efforts among the African scholarly community to gain audience and bring about interest of different stakeholders.
Novelty: First community-led and digital archive for research made in Africa.

Project/Initiative Name: AirNow - EPA’s one-stop source for air quality data
Description:
AirNow is a one-stop source for air quality data. the recently redesigned site highlights air quality in a user's local area first, while still providing air quality information at state, national, and world views. A new interactive map let users access to several Air Data maps: interactive, fire and smoke, embassies and consulates, AirCompare. Moreover, has clustered all the data of the program in a new API that provides forecast and real-time observed air quality information across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. 

AirNow reports air quality using the official U.S. Air Quality Index (AQI), a color-coded index designed to communicate whether air quality is healthy or unhealthy for the citizens. Being a multi-partner initiative (including NASA, CDC, tribal states, among others), the centralized data system provides quality control, national reporting consistency, and the ability to distribute data to the public, researchers, businesses, educators, and to other data systems

AirNow receives real-time air quality observations from over 2,000 monitoring stations and collects forecasts for more than 300 cities.
Novelty: A one-stop source for air quality data.

Description:
The Alberta Technology and Innovation Strategy aims at helping position Alberta as an internationally recognized technology and innovation hub that attracts talent, business and job-creating investment from across Canada and around the world. 

It fulfils an Alberta Investment and Growth Strategy commitment to finalize strategies for key economic sectors and supports Alberta's recovery plan to put us back on course for long-term financial sustainability.

The Alberta Technology and Innovation Strategy sets 5 key goals to establish Alberta as a global technology leader: 1) Increase the depth of Alberta’s technology and innovation talent pool, 2) Increase access to private capital and public investments in Alberta’s technology and innovation sector, 3) Advance a system of supports that facilitate commercialization of Alberta research and innovations, 4) Optimize Alberta’s technology and innovation ecosystem, 5) Enhance Alberta’s reputation as a leader in technology and innovation.
Novelty: Policy and political frame to trengthen Alberta’s technology and innovation sector, helping to create jobs and diversify the economy.
Impact & Results:
- The Alberta Technology and Innovation Strategy seeks to create 20,000 new jobs for Albertans by 2030.
- Alberta’s technology companies could generate $5 billion more in annual revenue by 2030.

Description:
Seocho-gu neighborhood has a high building concentration and traffic volume, is more prone to extreme heat than other districts in Seoul.

Seoripul Wondumak is a fixed parasol that is installed in heat-prone outdoor spaces (e.g. crosswalks and traffic islands). It began from the suggestion of creating shaded areas for senior citizens waiting for the pedestrian signal on street on hot summer days. The parasols, which were created with attention to practicality, safety and design, serve as welcome “oases” that provide a brief respite from the sun and heated asphalt.
Novelty:
-  Practicality: made with mesh that is durable, water-repellent, blocks UV rays, and allows for airflow. Poles are installed where people usually stand to ensure space efficiency.
- Design: an elegant design that complements an urban setting
- Safety: Made to be foldable in the event of strong wind. Inside of parasol has safety hook holding it up so that it doesn’t drop even if the wire snaps. Pole is planted in a concrete base 60~80m underground for maximum sturdiness.
Impact & Results:
he parasols were first installed to protect senior citizens exposed to extreme heat. After two trial installations in 2015, 118 were installed in 2017 based on residents’ requests and donations from local companies.
Seocho-gu incorporated residents’ feedback from the earliest stages and worked with partners to create a parasol that is practical and safe. They have since been upgraded to include potted plants and chairs. In the winter, they are used to illuminate and beautify sidewalks (Seoripul tree). Furthermore, the Seoripul trees are decorated with lights that are bright but can’t be reached to ensure safety.

Project/Initiative Name: An Innovative Training Model for the Public Sector
Description:
What problem the innovation solves or what opportunity was taken advantage of
Measuring the effectiveness of training is a complicated task in the public sector for various reasons. For example, it is not always visible and measurable, courses are too generic, too short duration, among others.

The College has developed an approach, if implemented, can generate effective results and benefit stakeholders.

The objectives of the innovation are to:
1. design relevant courses for public officers
2. involve stakeholders in the process
3. Ensure that the training is effective

This is a continuous approach that is ongoing and adopted in designing all our training courses. International institutions such at the UNDP has funded the project and implemented in the 13 ministries.
Novelty:
Training institutions adopt some commonly used training models to ensure that training is impactful, for instance: 
-             The ADDIE model (Analyse, Design, Develop, Implementation and Evaluate), mainly to help the trainer capture accurate learning objectives and the learners’ learning needs),
-             Bloom’s 6-step Taxonomy (remember, understand, apply, analyse, evaluate and create),
-             Gagne’s Nine Events of Instructions, which is an inclusive process involving the learner at every stage, and
-             the Kirkpatrick Model (mainly for assessment). 
However, these models have shortcomings and sometimes difficult to adapt to training in the public sector
This is the reason the College is adopting a new approach to design and deliver training courses that are effective and make impact.
Impact & Results:
The process to develop relevant training courses are well understood by stakeholders. Training courses are well structured and designed. A huge improvement has been observed from the feedback from ministries, pubic officers and trainers.
Using the example of Business Continuity Management training, over 260 officers (20 from 13 ministries) have received the training. The Ministries are equipped with a BCP, which did not exist prior to the training.

Organisation: National Directorate for State Digital Integration and Processing (DNIYTDE) - Subsecretariat for Administrative Innovation (SSIA) - Secretariat for Public Innovation (SIP)
Description:
PAEC/AUTENTICAR is the first and only National State platform that offers a solution to ensure that the person is who he/she says he/she is in a digital environment. The Central Electronic Authentication Platform - PAEC/AUTENTICAR - provides government agencies and other jurisdictions with the service of authenticating individuals, interoperating with official identity providers, in a single standardised integration.

The solution provides improvements in terms of security by resolving the reliable validation of user data. The evolution of "Autenticación Ciudadana Argentina" includes the creation of a new digital identity for users, based on blockchain. It allows documents, certificates or identifiers to be issued in their own name and self-managed by the citizen, who can store them in a "document holder" application and present them to whomever they choose.

Already established and providing services, it continues to add new identity providers and forms of authentication, such as those based on biometric data (fingerprints, facial identification), asymmetric cryptography (Digital Signature) or decentralised identifiers (blockchain) in a transparent way for the Client Applications that have been previously integrated.

It is projected with the idea of generating digital identity solutions for the National State, the Public Sector in general, Provinces, Municipalities and organisms that require it.
Novelty:
A single gateway for all systems (Client Applications) that are integrated for the identity recognition of their users. 
The solution provides improvements in terms of security by resolving the validation of user data. 

MiArgentina : authentication of credentials for argentina.gob.ar
Nic.Ar-Network Information Center
Impact & Results:
The Central Electronic Authentication Platform - PAEC/AUTENTICAR - has 153 (one hundred and fifty-three) integrated client applications that obtain the standardised authentication service.
Around 300,000 effective authentications are performed per day, reaching in May 2022 a total of 13,164,216 interactions, totalling more than 200,000,000 (two hundred million) accumulated authentications since its implementation.
Impact - Tangible and Quantifiable Results
A)152 Customer Applications.
During the month of July 2022, 152 client applications were reached to validate the digital identity of their users.
B)202 Million authentications achieved. 
On 30 June 2022, 202 million authentications were reached, a growth of 67% compared to 2019.

Project/Initiative Name: Automizing services for citizens: from information and the submission of a form to receive a certificate in minutes
Description:
Because of the large number of issued certificates yearly, there was a need to develop a service for citizens that would allow them to obtain the certificate from criminal records electronically in a quick manner, which is the results of the interaction of three different criminal records information systems. This solution is innovative for citizens as it gives the certificate, in a matter of minutes, as well as relieves employees who were previously tasked with issuing these certificates.

After the implementation, an individual had to submit a single form on the eGovernment portal, to request a certificate. Any individual can request a printout of personal data contained in all three criminal records and relating to him. The citizens receive the certificate in minutes, if there is no criminal records in the database for the requester. In case they have a criminal record, it is directed by law, that the certificate needs to be served in person by mail.

For the future this solution has been a great precedence for future atomization of certificates. Because of the best practices this solution is a great example of what can be done, if the government optimizes its processes and becomes innovative for citizens and at the same time frees up the government.
Novelty: This solution is innovative and unique as it was the first digitally automated service in Slovenia regarding certificates from a central national database.
Impact & Results:
The biggest accomplishment of this innovation is that there have been more than 92.000 submitted applications and we believe more than 90.000 automated answers.
There has been an evaluation done in 2021 by the Sector for elimination of administrative obstacles, better legislation, and quality in the public sector, that estimates (according to the number of individuals that send online applications) that Slovenia has annual saving of 27.000 EUR for individuals and 35.000 EUR for the Ministry of Justice. If all applications would be sent electronically by individuals, the annual savings would be 161.000 EUR for individuals and 213.000 EUR for the Ministry of Justice.

Description:
BA Obras is a public works data opening tool that provides detailed information on more than 1,100 works in a simple and friendly way. It combines transparency, open data, accountability and collaboration with civil society and other cities around the world, giving rise to a new form of dialogue between residents and the Government. Furthermore, it allows residents to observe the impact that these works have on their quality of life.

Likewise, BA Obras became a tool for improving the internal management of government information. Currently, the BA Obras website provides information on more than 1,100 public works, including specific data for each one such as location, execution date, estimated deadlines, stage in which it is located, responsible area, company in charge, budget execution, and is periodically updated, providing the most recent and up-to-date information on all the works carried out by the City Government.

In its beginnings, BA Obras was an online transparency platform. Over the years, it has further developed into a website that shows, by the 48 neighborhoods, the transformation of Buenos Aires into a more resilient, inclusive and sustainable city.
Novelty: BA Obras is an innovative platform insofar as it made it possible to improve the management of information within the government, thus achieving a greater interrelation between the different levels of the government, unifying the dialogue and the information that is shared; on the other, it contributed to improving communication with residents, with a positive dialogue through public works, providing greater transparency on the projects that are carried out and influence the quality of life of citizens.
Impact & Results: The opening of data generated an improvement in government efficiency since it improved the internal process of management, collection, systematization, standardization and communication of government data. This had an impact on improving the quality of information and its centralization. At the same time, coordination and planning improved, by having evidence for the design of new public policies. Finally, by having information available to all, civil society was able to access information more easily, simply and quickly, and, consequently, requests for access to public information related to works have decreased.

Description:
Beckn Protocol aim to create an open and decentralized digital ecosystem. It is a set of specifications consisting of APIs, data models, reference architecture, transaction mechanisms, and global standards that when adopted by digital platforms, enable the creation of decentralized networks. Such networks allow consumers and providers to discover, identify each other and perform transactions with each other without the need for a central intermediary. It is a sector-agnostic protocol, meaning, any industry-specific taxonomy or knowledge model can be represented using the data model of beckn protocol.

This body of knowledge is a free public good and is being shared openly and made available as per the creative commons licence, CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 

Beckn Protocol specifications can be implemented by business-facing, customer-facing platforms and governments to create integrated digital networks across diverse sectors like e-commerce , mobility etc. The open codes specify a set of interoperable APIs that allows discovery, engagement, fulfillment and post-fulfillment of services and goods. The services and goods can be availed with or without commercial consideration. If required, beckn protocol allows commercial contracts to be established between two actors.
Novelty: An open and decentralized set of specifications consisting of APIs, data models, reference architecture, transaction mechanisms, and global standards that when adopted by digital platforms, enable the creation of decentralized networks.
Impact & Results:
Three examples of impacts using the set of specifications provided by Beckn Protocol.
First multi-domain cascaded round-trip transaction (simulated) on indias open network for digital commerce (ONDC)- worlds first ecommerce open network in the market ondc.org

Decentralized skills and education protocal (DSEP) using beckn launched www.github.com/beckn/DSEP-Specification

Decentralized Health Protocal (DHP) using beckn lunched continuing from the initial efforts in the may 2020 www.github.com/dhp-project

Description:
In its efforts to make it easier to do business in South Africa and to reduce red tape, the Companies and Intellectual Property Commission (CIPC) developed an online registration platform which is completely paperless. Through collaboration with the private sector and other government departments and its entities, it allows for the seamless registration and application for multiple services. The public benefits from the opportunity to apply for a company, tax number, business bank accounts in one go.

The Bizportal is an electronic business registration platform which integrates the company registration, tax registration, Broad-Black Economic Empowerment application (applicable in South Africa to address past imbalances), domain name (for .co.za applications), compensation and unemployment insurance fund (registration service required for businesses with employees and business bank account functionalities. 

The purpose or goal of this innovation is to introduce seamless service delivery, to have a virtual one-stop-shop and to make it easier for businesses to formalise and to access business support information through the click of one button. The Bizportal in essence is the beginning of e-government for the economic sector.
Novelty:
CIPC was the first on the continent to develop a digital registration platform that involves the services of multiple regulators and private sector partners. The President of the country launched this innovation in November 2019. Other jurisdictions such as the Malaysian Company Registration Authority came to benchmark the process and built a similar registration platform. 

Other innovations in the organisation include the use of biometric identity verification tools such as fingerprint scanners at it's customer facing centres. Another innovation from 9 years ago is a world first having banks offer company registration services.
Impact & Results:
CIPC is on a completely different trajectory as a result of Bizportal. Happier citizens as processes are quick and simple. Collaboration with other stakeholders result in even more innovations being identified and built.
20,000 companies are instantly registered monthly using this platform. Of these 10,000 apply for business bank accounts per month. This is directly related to economic growth and job creation..
When the additional services are released, even more efficiencies will be experienced with a greater uptake of these services.

Project/Initiative Name: Blockchain and the traceability of the charcoal production chain in Minas Gerais
Organisation: Secretaria de Estado de Planejamento e Gestão de Minas Gerais
Description:
The decision was made to develop a system that concentrated all the activities inherent to the business: MG Florestas. For the implementation of MG Florestas, the government of Minas Gerais chose to adopt the blockchain technology and, in general terms, this technology is composed of blocks protected by encrypted codes that store information. 

The goals are: through initial analysis, use of blockchain and agile methodology, integrate processes and information, contributing to a more secure, transparent, and faster process, both for citizens and industries in Minas Gerais; Encourage the most relevant economic activity in Minas Gerais (Charcoal and other forest by-products); Curb illegal and harmful practices to the environment; Understand the technological potential with the implementation of the system for the expansion of a blockchain network in other projects.

There are some general benefits: 1) in the use of blockchain, such as the increase of information reliability and transparency, besides the non-dependence on a central actor to mediate the transactions, which can drastically and dramatically reduce transaction costs. 2) The control of the charcoal chain and the use of forest products and by-products fits perfectly into these concepts, since this sector has multiple players, and needs strong regulation, since the irregular exploitation of forests has the potential to cause major environmental impacts. 

The MG Forestry project consists in developing a tool that guarantees the complete electronic processing and certification of the charcoal chain and other forestry by-products, from the georeferenced registration of the planted forest area, to the harvest, wood processing, commercialization, transportation and final consumption. 

The georeferencing functionality represents one of the most important innovations of the MG Florestas system, because it improves the user's usability, reducing costs and time to formalize the plantation registration, while allowing the State to exercise a more effective control over the origin of the wood, by aggregating in a single system the geospatial information from the Plantation Registers of the entire Minas Gerais State territory.
Novelty:
With this in mind, in recent years, the Government of Minas Gerais has adopted a strategy to understand and include this technology in the state's technological solutions framework. 

The development of the system introduces blockchain technology into the framework of technological solutions in the state of Minas Gerais. Being one of the pioneering states in Brazil in this matter.
Impact & Results:
Making the processes related to charcoal more secure and transparent, with accurate tracking of plantations and harvests: an intuitive GEO interface integrated with Google Earth was created, with presentation of satellite data, along the lines of the Spatial Data Infrastructure of the State System of Environment and Water Resources, which allows users to draw polygons freehand or upload a georeferenced file containing the data of the plantation plot(s).

Improved control of forest fee collection: in applicable cases, there will be a State Collection Document issued by the system itself, minimizing collection errors and optimizing the process.

It is expected to reduce the number of frauds in the woody material, since there is the obligation to register all the stages of the process in the system. Empirical results show the transformation of the process to a more transparent and faster format due to blockchain technology.

Organisation: Colectivo Legal y Tecnológico de Acción Social
Description:
The solution provides the state with a blockchain-based system that gives legal certainty and security to their cases and their life-tracking until they become adults, providing effectiveness and efficiency to all subject-matter related state processes. 

The instrument was born looking for a mechanism that facilitates, speeds up, articulates, collaborates, and guarantees the proceedings of the files, which mean the life of a person, in order to give legal certainty and avoid delay and impunity.

A Blockchain-based legal-digital tool is presented, which allows to identify all actors, structure procedural information, interoperate between responsible bodies, facilitate auditing, and enable secure communication. Its five main components are:
1. Identity
Unique identification ensures recognition of actors, bodies and data. Verifiable credentials and decentralised digital identifiers establish official sources of trust while maintaining sovereign control of data. 
2.	 Records
To streamline legal processes, a standardised sequence of legal actions records immutable references to cryptographically certified original documents in a distributed ledger.
3.	 Interoperability 
The responsible operators constitute a consortium of agencies that, according to their own internal rules and an inter-institutional consensus, allows for an efficient flow of information and actions.
4.	 Audit
Audit points, defined for these operational flows, show the speed of legal actions and the quality of their attention and results.
5.	 Communication
Private and cryptographically secure communication channels facilitate collaboration and cooperation within and across the network of all responsible bodies.
Novelty: Use blockchain to share information in real time, avoiding delays and speeding up processes. The tool will reduce impunity by articulating the institutions to exchange valuable information with the legal security of their acts, processes and resolutions between the actors of the bureaucratic, legal and judicial ecosystem, as well as with the private sector that collaborates with the State.
Impact & Results: Statistics on results are not yet available.

Project/Initiative Name: Brazilian Government's Single Sign-On for user authentication in Data Subject Access Requests received by the Superior Court of Justice
Description: The Ombudsman Office of Brazil’s Superior Court of Justice (STJ) decided to use the federal government’s Single Sign-On (SSO) technology for user authentication when a Brazilian citizen submits a Data Subject Access Request (DSAR). This technology is composed of resources and protocols which guarantee the authenticity of the requester and provide them privacy and inviolability of intimacy, honor and image. The implementation had no impact on public coffers.
Novelty: It is innovative and creative because by using a tool with a previously created source code, STJ solved a substantial issue for the institution: ensuring security to delivery personal data to an authenticated requester. It is also a simple and intuitive tool for the user and provides the public institution with all the necessary information on user authentication.
Impact & Results: Since STJ implemented SSO in order to diminish or even eliminate the risks of personal data leak, we could only have measurable results in case of a new hacker attack. However, it is possible to emphasize an important result for the institution, which is the simplification of the process for the user. With only one account - the one from Conta Gov.br - the user is able to submit a DSAR, skipping the “create an account” part of the previous process. It also makes the service more reliable for the user, since they are dealing with a federal technology that involves several high-level security protocols.

Organisation: Brown School of Public Health, Harvard’s Edmond J. Safra Center for Ethics
Description:
The dashboard initiative to establish a key metrics for suppression framework to help cut through the noise of information and conflicting advice for research, policymaking and public health decision making. By the time it was released the CDC had not published community levels and guidance, so this tool provided reliable information on how to interpret and act on the data that was available about COVID-19. The initiative aimed to provide guidance on the intensity of control efforts needed based on certain COVID risk levels (green, yellow, orange, red). 

The dashboard is powered by Microsoft's AI for health. 
The dashboard accompanying the framework continues to update daily case rates and risk levels based on the original metrics. The policy recommendations laid out in the framework, however, no longer apply as the pandemic has evolved, new variants have emerged, and vaccines and treatments are now available. They keep an archived record of these recommendations.
Novelty:
Integrative indicators with open source and official information available about COVID-19. 
Displays information at county and congressional district level.
Risk levels assessment framework

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
Career Gate, integrated with e-Government, is a digital platform developed by the Presidency of the Republic of Turkey Human Resources Office in order to share public institutions’ career and internship opportunities with the public more effectively and ensuring merit-based equality of opportunity in public employment.

CG supports sharing all part-time and full-time job postings in public institutions with citizens more transparently and effectively. Thus, citizens can reach career opportunities in public institutions easily on a single platform. Currently, all ministries and their subsidiaries use CG to carry out their recruitment processes. 

CG facilitates applications for candidates and provides employers with reliable data from central databases thanks to its integration with the e-Government system. While candidates make their applications, they can easily transfer their related information to CG. Candidates’ information is checked automatically by the verified information from the e-Government, and candidates who do not meet the criteria of the postings are not allowed to apply
Novelty: Career Gate is unique in terms of working with e-Government integration to facilitate applications for candidates and provide institutions with verified data from central databases.
Impact & Results:
All ministries, their subsidiaries, Presidential units, judicial authorities, and some public universities are the users and beneficiaries of the Career Gate Platform. They carry out their recruitment processes on CG. Also, they contribute to the improvement of CG by providing continuous feedback as users. 

By September 2022, 436 job postings of 101 public institutions were published on CG. More than 3 million people tried to apply for these postings but only 1.2 million applications that meet the criteria of postings were completed. About 1.8 million ineligible applications were automatically eliminated by the system since candidates’ verified information is checked by the system during their application.

Project/Initiative Name: Central Chronic Medication Dispensing and Distribution (CCMDD)
Description:
South Africa has experienced an unpredicted growth in patients requiring access to long term therapies. driven in part by universal access to Antiretroviral Therapy and steady increases in patients with non-communicable diseases. This has led to over extension of public health care facilities creating strain on available resources, contributed towards medicine shortages and challenges in the quality of care provided. The CCMDD programme provides an alternative mechanism to facilitate access to chronic medicine for stable patients. 

External pick-up points (PuPs) provide the patient with a more convenient option for the collection of their medicine which has been dispensed and distributed via the programme, thereby decongesting the facilities. The PuPs may be nearer to their homes or workplace and can result in reduced transport costs. The innovation digitises a patient’s public health prescription, at the public health facility The platform is a web-based solution that, can be deployed throughout the country. The electronic mechanism allows for duplicate prescriptions, or contraindicated medicines to be identified and adherence to treatment protocols. The courier pharmacy adds additional details to the patient record about the items dispensed, when it was dispatched and when it was delivered.
Novelty: The innovation provided the digitisation of prescriptions and created the electronic system, including parcel management. Further, with PuPs it reduced congestions and undexpected shortage of medicines.
Impact & Results: This innovation covers 3594 health facilities in 8 provinces and 5'173'295 patients are registered. Over 1 612 528 million patients are serviced through 2941 external PUPs. Patients have reported that they wait less than 5 minutes to collect their parcels from PUPs. Due to the electronic system clinicians spend less time writing prescription, and more time with the patients.

Project/Initiative Name: Cleveron 501 - the Robot Grocery Pick-up and AR
Description:
Cleveron 503 is a 2in1 solution – a refrigerated parcel locker for online grocery and standard parcel pickup. Cleveron 503 has three temperature-controlled modules: freezer, chilled and controlled-ambient. The locker acts as a self-service and hands out orders within seconds, thus creating a remote and safe experience for the end-user. 

With the exponential growth of e-commerce pushed by the COVID-19 pandemic, Cleveron has provided with mobile and on-site food delivery outlets. The preparation and installation of such places can be done by using a high-tech augmented reality installation tool. The worldwide inland and overseas travel bans due to COVID-19 pushed Cleveron to look towards innovative technologies for installations. Augmented reality or AR turns physical product installations into virtual, meaning that Cleveron’s technicians do not always have to travel on-site. Cleveron is the first to use augmented reality to install robotics-based parcel lockers.

The company offers a visual knowledge base solution that provides effective knowledge-sharing to conduct complex remote tasks such as installations, maintenance and repair, employee training, customer support, and more. Scope AR’s solutions are used by leading organisations like Johnson & Johnson, Toyota and Siemens and even cities such as Tallinn and San Francisco.
Novelty: Mobile and on-site food delivery outlets that can be prototyped with augmented reality.
Impact & Results: https://cleveron.com/news/robots-from-estonia-boost-supermarket-spirits-during-pandemic-media-mention

Description:
The Colombia Científica Program was developed by the Colombian government with the World Bank's support to create collaborative partnerships between regional, national, international Tertiary Education Institutions (TEIs) and research centers areas towards the common aim of enhancing the quality of regional TEIs.

The Colombia Científica program has two pillars:
1. Scientific ecosystems: this component is financing research grants for the implementation of research and innovation projects responding to regional productive sector needs presented by Institutional Partnerships (IP).
    a. These IPs will include at least 2 domestic TEIs (one leading accredited institution with proven research capabilities and one non-accredited institution), at least one international institution, and at least one private firm.
    b. The IP may also involve other partners, such as regional research centers. The funds will be allocated on a competitive basis.
2. Passport to science: this component will give scholarships-loans (part scholarship, part loan) to support postgraduate education abroad in the 500 Shanghai Ranking Universities.

To date, the program has involved 125 education institutions, more than 500 students and there were 245 beneficiaries of Passport to Science. Currently the scheme enters in its final year of execution and the World Bank have demonstrated interest in its results in order to evaluate the program´s feasibility for replication.
Novelty: Colombia Cientifica focusing on Science, Technology and Innovation schemes had achieved the development of the SDG principle of "Partnership for the goals" not only for the traditional triple helix participants (Academia, Business and Government), but also communities (from regions and vulnerable groups), which in a development environment is key to decentralize the quality of the education. In addition, it established an unprecedented successful collaboration between government teams.
Impact & Results: Currently the scheme enters in its final year of execution and the World Bank have demonstrated interest in its results in order to evaluate the program´s feasibility for replication. The great number of institutions (125), business (33), students (556), and researchers (1516) participating in it and benefiting from institution alliances (8), collaborative projects (78) and Passport to Science (245) seems promising.

Description:
Common Voice is a publicly available voice dataset, powered by the voices of volunteer contributors around the world. People who want to build voice applications can use the dataset to train machine learning models.

At present, most voice datasets are owned by companies, which stifles innovation. Voice datasets also underrepresent: non-English speakers, people of colour, disabled people, women and LGBTQIA+ people. This means that voice-enabled technology doesn’t work at all for many languages, and where it does work, it may not perform equally well for everyone. We want to change that by mobilising people everywhere to share their voice.

Mozilla is currently crowdsourcing an open-source dataset of voices. People are encouraged to donate their voice, to participate in validating processes of the accuracy of other people’s clips, and to make the dataset better for everyone. 

This is a six-step process until a dataset is released (one every 3 months): 1) Language Request - Someone asks for a language to be added, 2) Website Localization - The website text is translated into that language, 3) Sentence Collection - Sentences are collected for people to read aloud, 4) New Language Launch - We launch the Common Voice site in this language, 5) Voice Contribution - People come and contribute their voices, 6) Voice Validation - Other people validate those voice clips.
Novelty: Platform that collects voluntary people's contributions to build an open-source voice database that anyone can use to make innovative apps for devices and the web.
Impact & Results:
'- 24,000 hours recorded, 16,000 validated. 
- Languages (and some dialects) are included: Some languages have enormous variation in grammar, vocabulary and pronunciation. For this reason, we are introducing ‘Variants’ in 2022. This gives communities a way to distinguish their languages within the larger dataset.

Project/Initiative Name: Community Data License Agreement – Permissive, Version 2.0
Description:
Linux Foundation new permissive license designed to help foster for open data collaboration around AI and machine learning projects (CDLA-Permissive-2.0) aims to encourage data-sharing through licensing arrangements that clearly define how the data - and any derivative dataset - can and can't be used.

This new agreement has been crafted with the following objectives in mind: 1) A short, straightforward data agreement which enables data sharing and data innovation in a responsible way, 2) The use of data under the agreement carries no obligation, 3) The sharing of data under the agreement is operationally simple and requires nothing more than making available the text of the agreement, 4) Training an ML model based on data under the agreement, even copyrighted data, creates no obligation under the agreement for the use or the distribution of the trained model or for the insights it generates, 5) A data provider-friendly agreement, as the sharing of data under the agreement is designed to limit the liability of the data provider(s).
Novelty: It’s essentially a rewrite of version 1.0 but shorter and simpler to follow. Going further, it has removed certain provisions that were deemed unnecessary or burdensome and may have hindered broader use of the license.
Impact & Results:
This highlights how transposing a concept from a software license to a dataset license doesn’t always make sense, in part because laws and regulations usually treat data differently from software and other similar creative content.

Several companies have already revealed plans to make their existing open datasets available under the new CDLA-Permissive-2.0 license, including Microsoft’s research arm, which will now transition some its open datasets, including Hippocorpus, Microsoft AI initiative, GPS trajectory, IBM's Project CodeNet, among others.

Description:
Th City Commitments are a public contract between the Chief of Government and the citizens. Their purpose is to strengthen trust in institutions and promote a more open, transparent and efficient government, and align government departments under the administration’s strategic targets. Doing so also allows to tackle the growing complexity of the public agenda. The Commitments are made in 4 phases: formulation of goals, compliance planning, follow-up and citizen monitoring. 

The City Commitments are an initiative of the Chief of Government Horacio Rodríguez Larreta, who assumed his mandate in December 2015. After a period of survey, selection, and formulation, in February 2016, the Chief of Government publicly committed to the achievement of 20 priority, specific and measurable objectives, with a defined deadline for each one, and on which accounts would be periodically rendered. In August 2016, the City Government launched the City Commitments as the main accountability tool, an accessible platform with detailed and updated information on the goals and indicators of each Commitment, as well as the expected trajectories of compliance and their degree of progress. To date the Chief of Government has taken a total of 117 Commitments.

In the instance of formulation of Commitments, the participation of citizens is taken into account, using as data-input studies of public opinion, survey of information from social networks, direct communication channels with residents and co-creation roundtables with strategic actors. In turn, the Chief of Government participates in meetings with residents in which he receives queries and suggestions. Then, after compliance planning, follow-up and citizen monitoring are conducted with monthly or fortnightly follow-up meetings, review of progress and decision-making to correct deviations, subsequent monitoring of agreements, quarterly presentations in the cabinet of ministers and semi-annual balance of compliance.
Novelty: The Commitments are the first experience of these characteristics in Argentina and made Buenos Aires the first Latin American capital to publish all the information on its strategic priority goals online, to reinforce trust in institutions and promote the culture of citizen evaluation via a digital government tool.
Impact & Results:
As of August 2022, the website has exceeded 2,580,000 unique visits since its implementation. Further, the Commitments have generated important changes in local management, including the promotion of institutional coordination and more efficient internal processes, the strengthening of the follow-up and monitoring system of the actions and programs of the Government areas, and a substantial improvement in the analysis of public policies since the use of data and evidence began to be used to prepare proposals and carry out actions in a more sophisticated and precise manner.

In 2017, a public opinion study was carried out to find out the impact of the Commitments on citizen trust. The study carried out by Quiddity with 2,000 residents of Buenos Aires over the age of 18 consisted of a structured interview in which it was possible to observe the change of opinion in the respondents before and after browsing the Commitments website. At the beginning of the survey, they were asked a series of questions, then they were asked to access the web and were cross-examined. It was observed that, while at the beginning 30% of the people surveyed agreed that "The City Government does not inform the residents", that number fell to 22% after accessing the web. On the other hand, while at the beginning only 22% considered that "The City Government explains what it does and what is missing", that percentage rose to 36% after they entered the site. In summary, the study concluded that the initiative reinforces the confidence of citizens in institutions.

Project/Initiative Name: Computer programming in primary schools supported by open educational resources
Description:
Informatics and Computing was introduced as a compulsory subject in primary schools in Serbia in 2017. The Petlja Foundation has developed open educational resources to support innovative approaches to learning programming as part of the new subject, with focus on Python programming language. Those resources were vital for the introduction of textual programming into the official curriculum. Now every student in every primary school in Serbia has the opportunity to gain modern digital skills including computer programming.

All materials on the Petlja portal designed for primary and high school students, in addition to being free, are also published under a liberal open licence, which allows everyone to download, modify and, with citing the source, further distribute the materials. The result of these activities can be seen in most of the officially approved textbooks for the subject Informatics and Computing, which include references to these open educational resources developed by Petlja. The aim of all these steps was to provide every student in every primary school in Serbia with the opportunity to gain modern digital skills, including computer programming.
Novelty: Our open educational resources (OERs) use an innovative approach to the process of learning textual programming for students ages 11 and older. These resources were also a key enabler of the introduction of textual programming into the official curriculum for primary schools in Serbia at the time when Informatics and Computing was introduced as a compulsory subject.
Impact & Results: The impact of this innovation covers entire generations of students attending the upper grades of primary school (65,000 students per generation). During the peak of school activity, our online platform reached 200,000 monthly active users measured by Google Analytics. Most of them are primary school students.

Description:
Connected Papers is a unique, visual tool to help researchers and applied scientists find and explore papers relevant to their field of work.

To create each graph, Connected analyzes an order of ~50,000 papers and select the few dozen with the strongest connections to the origin paper. In the graph, papers are arranged according to their similarity. That means that even papers that do not directly cite each other can be strongly connected and very closely positioned. Connected Papers is not a citation tree

The metrics are based on the concepts of Co-citation and Bibliographic Coupling. According to this measure, two papers that have highly overlapping citations and references are presumed to have a higher chance of treating a related subject matter. The algorithm builds a Force Directed Graph to distribute the papers in a way that visually clusters similar papers together and pushes less similar papers away from each other. Upon node selection the platform highlights the shortest path from each node to the origin paper in similarity space.
Novelty: Algorithm based on co-citation and bibliographic coupling to find and explore papers relevant to a specific field of work in which a single paper of reference is based upon.

Description:
This research explores how to deliver ‘connectivity as aid’ in a dignified way while managing digital risks to refugees. It draws from an extensive literature review and on-the-ground fieldwork in displacement contexts in Uganda and Kenya. 

The report, which is targeted at humanitarian organizations interested in providing connectivity as aid as well as public and private sector actors involved in connectivity provision to refugees, identifies a number of digital risks—from online censorship to cyber threats, data protection risks, disinformation and privacy harms—which demand increased attention and action as connectivity as aid is mainstreamed as an essential form of humanitarian assistance.

The project aims to begin to fill this gap by exploring how to deliver connectivity as aid in a dignified way while managing digital risks to refugees. It draws from an extensive literature review and on-the-ground fieldwork in displacement contexts in Uganda and Kenya. This fieldwork included key informant and focus group interviews with refugees in urban and settlement contexts, observations of connectivity in use, and practical engagement in designing connectivity tools with risk-mitigating measures.
Novelty: This report is targeted at humanitarian organizations and the public institutions interested in providing connectivity as aid but for whom discussions on digital risk are still novel.
Impact & Results: The report on itself: https://www.unhcr.org/innovation/wp-content/uploads/2021/03/CWC-Managing-Digital-Risks-To-Refugee-Connectivity-Report.pdf

Description:
The Consumidor.gov.br is a public and free service that allows direct dialogue between consumers and businesses for alternative solutions to consumer disputes over the internet.

The main innovation of Consumidor.gov.br is to enable direct contact between consumers and companies, in a totally public and transparent environment, without the intervention of the Public Power in the individual treatment.

As it is a service provided and maintained by the State, with emphasis on interactivity between consumers and suppliers to reduce consumer conflicts, the participation of companies is voluntary and is only allowed to those that formally adhere to the service, by signing a term in which they commit to know, analyze and invest all possible efforts to solve the problems presented. The consumer, on the other hand, must identify himself properly and undertake to present all the data and information related to the complaint reported.

The data from the complaints registered at Consumidor.gov.br feeds a public database, with information about the companies that obtained the best solution and satisfaction rates in handling the complaints, about those that responded to the demands in the shortest terms, among other information. The performance of the participating companies may be monitored through the link Indicators.

Through the Consumer Reports tab, it is possible to read the content of the complaints, responses from companies and final comments from consumers, and it is even possible to search by: keywords, market segment, supplier, geographical data, area, subject, problem, period, classification (resolved / not resolved / not evaluated) and/or satisfaction rating, among other filters.
Novelty: Consumidor.gov.br provides essential information for the preparation and execution of public policies in defense of consumers, as well as encourages competitiveness in the market by improving the quality of products, services, and customer service. This service is monitored by the National Consumer Secretariat - Senacon - of the Ministry of Justice, Procons, Public Defenders, Public Ministries, Regulatory Agencies, among other bodies, and also by society as a whole.
Impact & Results:
Positive impacts.
Results of dispute resolution between claimant and claimant being resolved on average 80% of the time on the site itself, avoiding waste of public resources, mobility of the parties in conflict, and also the cataloguing of databases to generate rankings and transparency.

Project/Initiative Name: Corruption Risk Assessment for Preemptive Corruption Prevention
Description: As post-control system focusing on detection and punishment revealed its limit in effective corruption prevention, the need for preemptive corruption control system arose. In accordance with this need, the Corruption Risk Assessment was introduced as a primary system aimed at preemptively eliminating corruption risks in laws and regulations by identifying and removing corruption-causing factors inherent in those from the very stage of drafting.
Novelty: s or those that already caused corruption to take place. In contrast, CRA aims to identify and remove corruption-causing factors in laws and regulations that may have potential for corruption from the very stage of drafting so that it not only can proactively prevent corruption from occurring in the course of implementation but also can achieve simultaneous anti-corruption results when laws and regulations are put in place. As such, CRA can be regarded as a more innovative corruption preventive system compared to existing ones.
Impact & Results: Adoption of CRA, a corruption prevention system, has improved laws and regulations of various kind by eliminating corruption-causing factors inherent therein. In 2021, CRA was conducted on a total of 1,763 laws and regulations. Among them, 406 factors with potential for corruption were identified and removed from 182 laws and regulations.

Project/Initiative Name: Cost Calculator to see how supervision revocations can impact the state’s budget
Description:
The Cost Calculator provides a simple but robust projection of costs and savings to state corrections departments resulting from simple changes in probation and parole policies resulting in revocations or parole outcomes. This, at the same time, will impact on a state's prison population and costs. 

Projections are calculated by inputting previous years’ aggregated revocation population, revocation admissions, and average cost-per-person data into formulas developed to forecast the fiscal impact of changes in revocation populations.

To ensure the Cost Calculator can be applied across states using public data, it incorporates several simplifying assumptions, including (a) the prison population remains steady over time before the size of the revocation population is changed, (b) there is an idealized distribution of sentences for admissions, and (c) there is a constant average time served.
Novelty: First cost calculator that models future savings and population scenarios when applying changes in probation and parole policies.

Organisation: Ministry of National Planning and Economic Policy
Description:
The innovation was developed to consolidate the management for development results (MfDR) approach that Costa Rica has been promoting in the public sector. It seeks to promote a National Evaluation Agenda (ANE) contained in the National Development Plan as a mechanism to structure evaluations of public interventions with a strategic character; in order to optimise the design, management and results of these in order to obtain better public goods and services.

The evaluation agenda is the set of evaluations selected for evaluation during the government's term, reflecting the commitment to the evaluation of strategic public interventions, selected on the basis of pre-established criteria, under a multi-sectoral character.

Since 2015, a total of 35 evaluations of different public interventions have been carried out, which have contributed to improving different elements of the intervention cycle, through the implementation of action plans based on the recommendations of the evaluations that emerge from a series of technical aspects.

The development of evaluation processes in a systematic manner, supported by a solid normative framework, from the political constitution, planning and budgeting laws, as well as executive decrees and a specific public policy on the subject that guides a set of actors.
Novelty: It is a good governance practice, which grounds policies in solid evidence derived from rigorous analysis. The ANE is multi-sectoral and integrates different strategic public interventions, which are based on a "systematic assessment of the design, implementation and results of policies, plans, programmes and projects based on a set of pre-established value criteria". It generates recommendations to support decision-making and improve public management, as well as key information to enhance national debate and discussion on the legitimacy of public action to solve or mitigate social problems.
Impact & Results:
1. Increased consistency and effectiveness of public policies evaluated within the ANE framework.
2. Increased transparency and broadening of the spaces for accountability in public management.
3. Increased participation of public entities in ANEs - reflecting interest and technical-political will. 

Additionally, there are 2 measurements of the National Evaluation Capacity Index (2020 and 2022) that reflect progress on these aspects.

Organisation: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW)
Description:
Corona virus pandemic has impacted the world in big way and India is also impacted massively. To manage this Pandemic, MoHFW has developed a comprehensive real-time based IT platform (COVID19 INDIA Portal), to support health workers and administrators. It helps to provide surveillance, Track, Contain, Test and Treat corona infection. 

The application and its comprehensiveness has evolved with time. This platform provides various features such as BI tools based analytics, facts based decision support system, data collection upto facility level, Dashboards, Reports, integration with other applications etc.
Novelty: The Portal helped to tackle the Covid-19 pandemic taking into consideration a variety of data and inclusive predictive tools.
Impact & Results: More than 25'000 registered users. Used to support Coid-19 country-wide.

Description:
The multidisciplinary COVID-19 Research Review, launched by the Prime Minister's Office of Finland, compiles the most up-to-date research results related to 11 different dimensions of the pandemic, globally and from Finland, and presents them in a concise and informative manner. Compiled by a group of leading Finnish scientists, the review provides decision-makers and government officials with cumulative insights into the most relevant research on the crisis and increases situational awareness.

The review analyses medical, biological, social, economic, and political dimensions of the pandemic. A cross-disciplinary team of leading Finnish scientists was nominated to cover these areas, screen related research and select studies to be included in each review. Every three weeks the Review is distributed by email to all members of the Finnish Government and their cabinets, state secretaries, special advisers and leading civil servants in all ministries. It is also openly published in the web, and anyone can register to the distribution list.

By June 2022, 26 reviews had been published covering around 1400-1500 studies. The reviews had been downloaded over 35 000 times (on average over 1400 downloads per review). The Review raised a lot interest in and also beyond the Government. By June 2022, over 2000 persons had subscribed to the distribution list of which over 600 government officials. Among the subscribers there are a large number of key persons involved in the management of the pandemic in Finland. The review has received a lot of positive feedback from political decision-makers, civil servants and experts.
Novelty: This innovation provided a simple and perfectly effective solution to a novel challenge: how to keep up with the unprecedented flow of new research in the midst of global crisis? Further, it crowdsourced the scientific community, it improved the scientific literacy of readers (including government officials), and it allowed to make more scientific decisions.
Impact & Results: The review had 2000 subscribers, of which 600 government officials. It has been downloaded 35000 times (avg. 1400 downloads per review). Another key indicator is very positive feedback from the subscribers and readers of the review retrieved with meetings with the government but also with media and from social media.

Description:
Bangladesh identified its first COVID positive case on 8th March 2020. Being a densely populated country with inadequate healthcare infrastructure, it was a mammoth task to provide treatment to the daily increasing number of COVID patients keeping them in the hospital while maintaining social distance which could also lead to rapid community level transmission. So, COVID-19 Telehealth Center was established to ensure healthcare for COVID positive patients from their home.

As a2i, ICT Division had played a vital coordination role in planning, designing, developing, and implementing the e-Government intensively across the country supporting all the Ministries/Divisions/ Agencies of Bangladesh Government, therefore it has been advised by DGHS to prepare an effective and comprehensive plan on service coordination of COVID-19 cases in Bangladesh at this crisis. 

Based on this Mr. Forhad Zahid Shaikh, Chief Strategist, e-Governance played the lead focal role from a2i, ICT division by forming a team involving the domain/field experts from BSMMU-Bangabandhu Sheikh Mujib Medical University, DGHS, and IT industries for preparing a detailed strategic precise plan. This initiative started it’s journey in June 2020 for covid-19 positive patients.

Services:
1. Medical assessment, Doctor Consultancy, Counselling and treatment
2. Medical follow-up, counselling, treatment facility
3. Health information Service
4. Service through incoming call
5. Emergency service coordination (Ambulance info, hospital bed info, food & medicine buying support, funeral support etc.)

Patients were treated depending on 3 types of treatment plans.
-Treatment Plan A: Patients with Mild Symptoms
-Treatment Plan B: Patients with Medium Symptoms
-Treatment Plan C: Patients with Severe Symptoms
Novelty:
The most innovative catch of Covid-19 Telehealth Center was that it was the quickest and most cost effective possible solution available at that critical pandemic situation.
The objective of this initiative was to ensure effective and quick healthcare for citizens in every corner of the country during this crisis situation irrespective of their economic/ social status or physical location (urban or rural) so that no one is deprived from the basic healthcare right.
Impact & Results: Till 30 November 2021, COVID-19 Telehealth Center has provided 15,61,068 telehealth services to about 7,04,890 patients. In this way, doctors’ and patients’ distance was reduced, the cost of health treatment has also reduced, the tendency of taking antibiotics without doctors’ consultations has reduced, distant medical consultations have lessened the rapid community transmission of the virus, patients' satisfaction has increased.

Project/Initiative Name: Cultural Heritage Repair Records Meet Bim (building information modeling) Technology
Description:
Korean architectural wooden heritage are building, numbering in the tens of thousands, require periodic repairs. The repair of heritage buildings is a very specialized field, but there are many inconveniences as the repair history data is currently hand-distributed and managed. This project is a pioneering case of applying digital technologies such as BIM to the heritage field, and is the first attempt in Asia. We are digitizing the management system so that associates can use repair records comfortably. Through this guideline, it is possible to digitize wooden cultural properties into a three-dimensional shape that is easy to see at a glance, how to collect and verify repair records that have been distributed and managed by hand, and how to write vast amounts of information in the attribute information attached to each member. It sets standards for what it is.  We have built over 50 individual wooden cultural properties repair history HBIM from ‘21 to the present.

Through this guideline, it is possible to digitize wooden cultural properties into a three-dimensional shape that is easy to see at a glance, how to collect and verify repair records that have been distributed and managed by hand, and how to write vast amounts of information in the attribute information attached to each site. It sets standards for what it is.
Novelty: This project is a pioneering case of applying digital technologies such as BIM to the cultural heritage field, and is the first attempt in Asia. The Republic of Korea digitizes wooden cultural heritage buildings into a three-dimensional shape that is easy to see at a glance and collects, verifies, and systemizes repairs that were dispersed.
Impact & Results: The practice of repairing cultural property is made accessible to more people. In addition, through the HBIM data that has been constructed, the wooden cultural property can be repaired more authentically.

Organisation: Pakistan Customs Federal Board of Revenue
Description:
Complaint Management System is the first of its kind facility for the importers & exporters to lodge online complaints with Customs about any issue during their clearance. Pakistan Customs introduced automated clearance system in 2011. However, there was no such facility for the traders to lodge any complaint or raise their issues before the supervisory level of customs. The system shall reduce physical visits to customs offices saving time & cost for trade particularly at remote border stations.

The innovation is aimed to reduce the physical interaction between trade and customs; reduce the cost of doing business; quick redressal of issues and enhanced monitoring of clearances by supervisory officers. Generally all importers, exporters and customs brokers can utilize the facility. However, the major beneficiaries shall be the traders in far flung and border areas. The female segment of the trade shall also benefit from the system as they are usually reluctant to physically visit the offices of customs. As Pakistan Customs is in the process of launching a national single window linking all regulatory bodies with customs, the new system shall have a wider scope for the trade in the single window environment as they will be able to get online access to all regulatory departments.

Human-centered design tool has been used for development of new system which is a creative methodology to problem solving, starting with the people for which the system is being designed and ends with new solutions that are tailor made to suit their needs. In the Inspiration Phase, the specific issues faced by the stakeholders such as traders, customs brokers, women and young entrepreneurs were evaluated. In the Ideation Phase, the possible solutions were discussed with all the stakeholders and how they would like to solve it. Their inputs were taken and prototypes were designed based on their suggestions and assumptions. In the Implementation Phase, an Interactive Complaint Management System has been implemented.
Novelty:
The project is innovative because:
1. Prior to this innovation, no such online complaint management system was available for the traders and customs brokers.
2. The complaints are routed automatically based on data intelligence to the concerned customs officer instead of a general desk.
3. Live monitoring of complaints by senior management of customs.
4. Improve the service delivery of customs by providing visibility on the basis of average resolution time, categories and stages of complaints.
Impact & Results:
The complaints database has been evaluated and it is observed that 50% of complaints relate to delay in physical inspections, 40% relate to valuation and 10% relate to unnecessary notices for submission of supporting documents by assessing officers.
The quality of service delivery and resolution of complaints are being measured through customer satisfaction rating.
The system is expected to improve the overall efficiency of customs functionaries due to enhanced monitoring by supervisory officers through the system and have a positive impact on customs clearance time.

Description:
CVDB provides loans and manages the municipalities cash flows through a set of accounts in it. The work between CVDB and municipalities was done using paper. In addition, the municipalities did not know how much money they had in their accounts unless they visited one of CVDB's branches, hence they could not withdraw money. This project gave the municipalities the power to control their accounts without the need to visit any of CVDB's branches, which reduced the resources consumption (less time, allowances, CO2 emission since the need for transportation was reduced which reduce the fuel consumption). In addition, it increased customer satisfaction by the municipalities' customers and CVDB's customers.

Four parties benefited of this project: Central Bank of Jordan, since we helped to raising the percentage of financial inclusion, CVDB, more that 100 municipalities, and Municipalities' customers, who make up a large slice of Jordanian citizens or residents. For future and sustainability, CVDB started to think to integrate with the financial systems in all municipalities which will reduce the transactions' amount on the municipalities' employees and the reconciliation process beside easing audit and control process of CVDB on the municipalities.
Novelty: What CVDB did is currently used by commercial banks, but it is the first time that it being used by a development bank owned by government. Further, it was the first time the municipalities used such systems in Jordan.
Impact & Results: The new system and services reduced the service providing time, increased the customer satisfaction, and reduced the paperwork hence fuel consumption, allowances, CO2 emission and time consumption.

Description:
The Brazilian Chamber of Deputies has been servicing open data since the 2000s, but it was done in a not-for-everyone fashion with an increasingly unpopular technology. A team of IT and media servants designed and implemented a complete update to the service. Five years later, the Chamber's Open Data service became one of Brazil's most popular sources of data for academic studies, journalism, courses on data sciences and businesses.

A very conventional REST API was introduced for usage in mobile apps and websites, by large mirrored by a new set of files for download, primarily aimed at intermediate spreadsheet users, in five different open formats. No longer a programmer should be required for using open data. All published data follow a simplified definition of the main entities in the legislative process and a number of standards and recommended practices. Special care is given to uniformity in labels, parameters, data formats and structures used to represent the entities.

Open data remains machine-oriented, but the Open Data website provides some few tutorials and a "live API" page which allows users to know and experiment the endpoints and their parameters. A GitHub repository contains files with historic data which are not in the Chamber databases, and its "Issues" forum is used as a channel for technical questions and suggestions, replied by the mentioned journalist, with a very informal and practical language.

The service received an honourable mention in the 2017 eGov Award, and is mentioned as an example in courses promoted by OKBr, the Brazilian subsidiary of Open Knowledge Foundation. "Is is a very complete repository, and the effort has been a reference in state policy for other sectors, the Executive power included", says Fernanda Campagnucci, OKBr executive director.
Novelty: The Open Data service of the Brazilian Chamber of Deputies strives to make legislative data more friendly and accessible to users who are not computer programmers and citizens who are not acquainted with details and jargons used in the Parliament. Open formats and clear, simple language are thoroughly applied to make the data more easily processed into useful information.
Impact & Results: Being cited as a reference by courses from the Open Knowledge Foundation and at the Inter-Parliamentary Union Open Data Hub is a strong sign that the Chamber' service has been influential to many other open data services. The team has been also contacted by members of IT and administrative departments from regional parliaments.

Description:
DALL·E 2 is a new AI system that can create realistic images and art from a text description in natural language. The AI algorithm can combine concepts, attributes, and styles, as well as expand images beyond what’s in the original canvas, creating expansive new compositions. The tool makes realistic edicts from a natural language caption and has learned the relationship between images and the text used to describe them. 

The innovation uses a process called “diffusion,” which starts with a pattern of random dots and gradually alters that pattern towards an image when it recognizes specific aspects of that image. DALL-E 1 and 2 were released between 2021 and 2022 with an improvement that generates more realistic and accurate images with 4x greater resolution.

To leverage these representations for image generation, the innovation proposes a two-stage model: a prior that generates a CLIP image embedding given a text caption, and a decoder that generates an image conditioned on the image embedding. DALL-E decoders conditioned on image representations to produce variations of an image that preserve both its semantics and style, while varying the non-essential details absent from the image representation. Moreover, the joint embedding space of CLIP enables language-guided image manipulations in a zero-shot fashion. Diffusion models were used for the decoder and experiment with both autoregressive and diffusion models for the prior, finding that the latter are computationally more efficient and produce higher-quality samples.

Safety mitigations have been developed:
- Preventing Harmful Generations: They have limited the ability for DALL·E 2 to generate violent, hate, or adult images. 
- Curbing Misuse: Its content policy does not allow users to generate violent, adult, or political content, among other categories. 
- Phased Deployment Based on Learning: They began by previewing DALL·E 2 to a limited number of trusted users. As They learned more about the technology’s capabilities and limitations, and gained confidence in the safety systems, they slowly added more users.
Novelty: A new AI system that can create realistic images and art from a text description in natural language
Impact & Results:
From its caption matching and photorealism when evaluators were asked to compare 1,000 image generations from each model, the results were:
Version 2.0: 71.7% preferred for caption matching
Version 2.0: 88.8% preferred for photorealism

Project/Initiative Name: Development of a Scientific and Technological Observatory to promote the interaction of a public ICT with the productive sector
Description:
