The proposed innovation is the development of two integrated platforms, the first being an operational platform that aims to be a repository of primary data and information. That is, a common and shared environment, supported by the IT infrastructure, with all the institution's strategic data and information. The second is a platform that acts as an observatory of scientific and technological institutional results, with outputs monitoring panels (dashboards), through Business Intelligence - BI solutions, for the visualization of information dynamically (online), supported by strategic indicators to promote knowledge and decision-making. 

The specific objectives focused directly on the internal processes of the responsible areas (storage centres that handle the information generated by the results of the collaborators (publications, patents, PD&I projects, etc.); in the alignment with the strategy area and top management; and in the elaboration of indicators made available in the form of dashboards with the use of information technology tools for data analysis and visualization as a backdrop.

The project besides being innovative internally at the institution, there is no knowledge of the existence of a similar solution at another Research Unit of the MCTI. Thinking of a medium and long term strategy and with the proper contributions of development resources, project management and institutional interest, the realization of data mining studies (Data Mining), as well as the possibility of integration of the platform with other public databases that can add value to the platform. One way to scale up the project would be to evaluate the possibility of disseminating the solution in other institutions and create a third integrated platform that would have the proposal to act as a great scientific and technological hub of MCTI.
Novelty: The project is innovative within the Institution and unique among the MCTI's Linked Units, of scientific and technological basis. The project besides treating strategic data and information of the deliveries (dynamic mode), also has a performance monitoring environment. The Center for Strategic Studies and Management (CGEE), which is a Social Organization also linked to MCTI, and a reference in studies in the area of management in CT&I, has the Science, Technology and Innovation Observatory (OCTI). OCTI is a project of great complexity and scope, but its main goal is to monitor the scientific, technological and innovation production in the context of Brazil and the world and updated in regular time cycles
Impact & Results: It was observed a wide adherence of users, stakeholders and partners, but the project leveraged only after two years with the change of internal leadership of a partner who boosted the initiative in the strategic planning. At the moment, the engagement and positive perception of user satisfaction stand out and other metrics are being validated. It is expected in the near future that the second platform, planned to act as an observatory of scientific and technological results, is in minimum operating conditions to be disclosed, with the proper criteria, to the external environment. One of the results achieved was the deadline for the preparation of the management report on the strategy, which was reduced by approximately 30%.

Description:
Implementation of a document management system with advanced digital electronic signature, all the documentation of the municipality is "Born" digital, avoiding paper (saving + environmentally friendly), speeding up processes and improving communication between the different sections of the municipality.

The existing agreement with the general secretariat of government, which provides free advanced digital signatures, was taken advantage of and an integrated digital document management system was contracted, requiring this signature to be integrated, then all staff were instructed that administrative acts and all documentation should be generated by the system, and all documents were digitalised without physical copies.
Novelty: A document management system that tackles one of the main pains in public administrations, document bureaucracy.
Impact & Results: It has been possible to reduce documentation management times as well as a drastic reduction in the generation of associated paper. It has also made it possible to organise some processes that were carried out in an uncoordinated or untimely manner.

Organisation: The State Agency for Public Service and Social Innovations under the President  of the Republic of Azerbaijan
Description:
The E-Gov Development Center (EGDC) created the “Digital Local Executive Power” (LEP) portal to provide public services to the citizens through digital means. With the assistance of this innovation, the local executive powers started to function in more agile and efficient ways as they achieved an increased agility in the decision-making process, provided access to services from a single location, eliminated losing extra time for obtaining LEP services, and facilitated the application process.

In November 2021, the portal was first launched as a pilot project and initially, 3 socio-economically active regions were selected as pilot projects: Ganja, Masalli and Guba. Selection was based on the representation of each zone of Azerbaijan (North, South and West) and socioeconomic characteristics of the population. In March 2022, it was expanded into the whole country including 77 executive powers.

The Digital Local Executive Powers is a Web portal with various cabinets depending on the user. The portal was developed by web programming. It operates through the survey-based result demonstration system. In other words, the website asks the needs of citizens with simple questions and offers the services based on the results. Within the framework of this project, a number of modules have been developed for citizens: (i) E-Services module, (ii) Citizen Reception module, (iii) e-Participation module, (iv) News module.
Novelty: The portal not only facilitated the access of citizens to LEP services, but also accelerated the pace of service provision at the local executive powers. Along with introducing the agile ways of working at these institutions, this portal assisted to eliminate the issues such as the waste of extra time, additional paperwork, multilevel application processes.
Impact & Results:
The results of the project are:
- Number of registered users: 13 329
- Number of Local Executive powers: 77
- Total number of inquiries: 6031
- Number of e-services distributed to citizens: 13 (it is planned to increase the number to 20 by the end of year)
- Max age of the citizen: 91 The most critical impact of the project is that some executive powers have made the full transition into accepting requests from the online portal. This, in turn, allows the users to track their requests and diminishes the bureaucratic obstacles.

Project/Initiative Name: Digital public services models for Italian municipalities
Description:
According to the Digital Economy and Society Index, one of the main European index, in Italy there is a sensible gap between the supply of digital public services and their effective use made by citizens. However, the Italian Government is currently leading digital transformation processes with a clear strategic approach that is redefining the country: putting citizens at the heart of innovation, designing digital public service experiences able to enhance inclusion, transparency and trust.

Dipartimento per la trasformazione digitale (Department for Digital Transformation), with the Designers Italia project working group, has developed and made available to Italian municipalities a front-end model for institutional website and a series of design components (patterns) for modeling the flows of a wide range of digital services, according to five different typologies. Both website and digital service models are realized following user-centered design principles, thus focusing on the results of a user research carried out with citizens. A process who led the development of the models on the basis of user needs detected on the field.

Both models were designed according to the User Centricity and Transparency parameters of the eGovernment Benchmark method paper, thus introducing transversal functionalities into the website. Further, Designers Italia updated and made completely accessible the country's design system, ensuring that each HTML component of the institutional website and service models fully respects the WCAG standards on accessibility.

Thanks to the combination of design system’s components and standard models for website and public services, the Department for Digital Transformation has made available to Italian municipalities all the operational tools to successfully meet the challenges posed by digital transformation processes. Every municipality can freely download and use HTML templates of the website model and of the service flows, plus the design system’s components libraries, high-definition prototypes, content management tools and all the technical instructions to develop and standardize the offer of digital public services throughout the country
Impact & Results:
Although innovation is still in the making, there is a number who clearly describes the impact we expect in the next few months: more than 70% of Italian municipalities has already applied for the NextGenerationEU funding dedicated to improve the Citizen experience in digital public services. This means, as an outcome, that in the next few months innovation will dramatically increase in the whole country, thanks to the adoption of the standard models we provided as Department for Digital Transformation.

To measure such an impact, we set a series of KPI and at the same time we developed an App to automatically crawling and detect most of these features (eg: using a stepper to visualize procedure status, giving the citizen a possibility to leave quality feedback etc). According to European targets set by the NextGenEU program, by Q2 2026 we expect that at least 80% of Italian municipalities will adhere to the common standards provided for digital public services.

Project/Initiative Name: Dissemination of Knowledge to Enhance the Culture and Practice of Sound Financial Management in Public Agencies
Organisation: Ministry of Finance – General Directorate of Financial Audits
Description:
For the first time, the innovation proposal seeks to highlight best practices so that agencies can search and find solutions in a "guide" for effective and efficient management of more frequent financial issues that arise. The lack of know-how and, to a greater extent, the absence of a supporting framework so that actors can easily and quickly consult on best practices constitutes the reason for the birth of this specific innovation proposal. This is an unprecedented effort to create a repository of best practices through appropriate examples of sound financial management. An innovative approach to the data of the public administration is presented with the effort of spreading know-how through examples and subsequent formation of economic culture according to the criteria of economy-effectiveness and efficiency.

The innovation was developed by a five-member team of executives from the Ministry. of Finance under the guidance, supervision and approval of the General Director of Financial Controls, Mr. G. A. Georgiou. The added value lies in the formation of an economic culture and consequently in the maximization of efficiency and effectiveness indicators of public bodies. In addition, it aims to consolidate the terms of economy-effectiveness and efficiency
Novelty: The idea came from the need to align all public agencies with a "guide" of sound financial management with practical examples that have been drawn from the conducted audits of the GDDE. Due to the object of the GDDE and the gathering of many audit results, there is the possibility of distinguishing-marking of the most statistically significant errors related to sound financial management procedures. These are a guide to preventing future irregularities and implementing best practices
Impact & Results: Not provided by the author.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
Distance Learning Gate is a national learning platform that is put into service of institutions to equip public employees with appropriate knowledge, skills, and competencies. The platform, designed within the scope of Digital Türkiye, is aimed to provide equal opportunity in employee training, increase human resource efficiency, and the quality of training and development activities.

To determine the current situation of public institutions in human resources management, the Public Employee Satisfaction and Engagement Survey was conducted in 2019, in which training and development took one of the main parts. The data on the training and development activities in public institutions were analyzed to identify the quality and the quantity of employee training and, as a result, to offer data-based solutions. These data-based studies showed that the training activities were not widespread enough, and the existing training was not sufficiently inclusive and technology-based. In light of these results, Distance Learning Gate was proposed.

All public employees, starting with their candidacy period until they retire, can use Distance Learning Gate, and all public institutions can arrange their training activities via the platform. Distance Learning Gate increases the coordination and cooperation within all public institutions by sharing their best-practice implementation and learning materials. In that sense, it promotes the creation of training content in line with the fields of expertise of public institutions.
Novelty: Distance Learning Gate is an inclusive system as it includes all public institutions, namely the organization of the Presidency, 17 Ministries, 10 Directorates, and all affiliated, related, relevant, and coordinated public institutions and organizations, municipalities, and universities in Türkiye.  It is the first and most comprehensive platform designed in Türkiye, as it offers a common and centralized online platform to enhance public institutions' digital capacities.
Impact & Results: At the end of 2021, more than 4,000 training activities were carried out, and 1.5 million public employees participated in these training activities. The participation number in the training activities reached 11.8 million. In the first half of 2022, the total number of training activities held on the platform already increased by 42%. Compared to 2021, the number of public personnel who received training increased by 25%, and the number of training views increased by 48%.

Project/Initiative Name: E-government advertising campaign for 'adolescents smoking cessation'
Description: Korea came up with targeted anti-smoking campaigns directed at children and youth.
Impact & Results: Won various advertising awards.

Project/Initiative Name: eAmbulance - the eRegistration system to a network of regional hospitals
Description:
eAmbulance is the online registration system to a network of regional hospitals in the Vysocina area. The region has five hospitals and the platform enables to define slots for possible registration to various hospital departments. 

Through a rapid login to the system, patients/users choose and reserve their appointment. The system provides email and sms notifications, moreover, the data collected is exchanged through the network so patients experience a one-only registration process and a unified history platform. The system is also integrated with the National Identification Authority (NIA), so all citizens who already have national identity can login to eAmbulance without any barrier.

The service is designed with the aim to make hospital appointment booking and visits as easy as possible for the users/patients. It enables all users to choose the time and medical service based on their preferences with the all necessary cybersecurity requirements
Novelty:
The main impact of the service is that it enables patients to register online for medical service in hospital. For elderly people the service offers support by phone. In addition, the service minimizes the waiting time in the hospital.

From the hospital's perspective, it helps hospitals to manage their work more effectively and reduces the risk of spreading infectious diseases in hospital since patients spend minimum waiting time there.
Impact & Results: The system has >100,000 registered users. It was designed based on the Taiwanese example and in Vysocina region it is in operation 2012.

Description:
Tara Hospital identified a need for child care facility since employees were having difficulties caring for their children while they were at work resulting in high absenteeism. Parents struggling to find quality care are often absent, late and unproductive. unreliable child care leads to high absenteeism rate which affect service delivery.
Parents are now leaving children with capable, qualified, and trained teachers in a newly renovated, compliant, and safe environment within the hospital.

The project introduces the technologies of the Fourth Industrial Revolution and adding fun with learning, by creating a flexible learning environment for all groups, from anywhere and at any time.

A multi-platform application for computers, tablets and VR devices, which allows you to gather in 3D environments with learning colleagues from different Arab countries. These environments allow the users to interact directly and enhance virtual learning and also provide access to various scientific resources across the web in the world, break into small groups, use gestures and interactive effects, and explore. The users can either use the pre-made environments, or the educator or content creator in the next development stages can create their own 3D environment for students and the public to design their own free or paid environment.
Novelty: #NAME?
Impact & Results:
The process of starting the project and the progress of work on the four stages are summarized sequentially and gradually, provided that each stage takes its goals and expands in order to build the next stage, which is represented in:
- Academic platform: building the structural foundations in the project, human resource growth and market measurement.
- Transitional stage: expansion Horizontal, building knowledge resources and strengthening links and relationships.
- The hybrid model: preparation, preparation, provision of materials and tools, and the employment of digital commerce.
- The virtual educational city: integration between all stages and expansion.

Project/Initiative Name: Empathy-based Innovation Toolkit for Transforming Public Service Delivery
Organisation: a2i Programme, Prime Minister's Office, Government of Bangladesh
Description:
Cultivating empathy among civil servants shows them ‘why’ to do something rather than telling them ‘how’ to do it. Thereby, it taps into their innate desire to do good work and unleashing each individual’s unique innovation potential. A twist on the private sector’s ‘mystery shopping’ technique, the empathy-based innovation toolkit orchestrates for mid and senior level civil servants to step into citizens’ shoes and experience public service delivery/access process from citizens' perspective.

The project stems from adaptations of the work of Nesta, from whom we learnt that cost-effective public services hinge on an empathetic civil service who understand the difficulties of attaining services for citizens. The toolkit imbues empathy through providing essential training to officers responsible for public service delivery, placing them in orchestrated situations as “secret shoppers” trying to attain public services from ministries separate from theirs. This creates a deep sense of empathy among the civil servants, moving them to scrutinize their own public service delivery and examine how it can be improved. 

Civil servants are aided to do this, with a new set of simple parameters to gauge impact, by calculating the time, cost and visits saved from innovations. Moreover, they are trained to work towards service process simplification (inspired by the private sector’s business process reengineering, or BPR), mapping out their processes, removing unnecessary elements and redesigning it wholesale for citizens. Finally, a2i funds innovators across the public sector, private sector, and civil society, for any innovations that could be implemented in government.

The toolkit has a long road ahead. A2i has already been working towards its scale up across the entire government. In the first two years of toolkit implementations, divisional commissioners of government applied it in phases across several districts of the country. A2i has funded civil servants to implement the toolkit, but gradually ministries have established their own funding towards innovation. It is expected that the government will soon be able to implement innovations independently, with a2i expected only to provide resource persons. Already, implemented in 15 ministries, the aim is to scale up to all ministries by end of 2023.
Novelty: The most innovative aspect of the toolkit is its use of empathy as a tool to drive public sector innovation. It ensures that value is added to both the experiences of the public service provider and beneficiary, incentivizing speedier adoption of the toolkit across Bangladesh. Moreover, it also ensures that service innovations are not driven forward by institutions, but individuals involved with the service in question.
Impact & Results:
34,450 officials have been given empathy training, organizing over 1,589 innovation pilots. Over 600 service processes have been simplified.
In composite, using the TCV method, a2i has been able to save 7.43 billion visits, 12.23 billion work days, and $16.62 billion – on average, over a hundred dollars saved per person in Bangladesh, over seventy-six work days, and over forty-six visits a year.

Several processes spanning several ministries are to be examined using the toolkit. For instance, from digitizing social safety net payments, we anticipate cutting time spent by 58%, money spent by 32%, and visits by 80% - cutting millions of hours, dollars and kilometres spent by citizens for social security. The government also expects every ministry to digitize at least one service and simplify at least one service every year.

Project/Initiative Name: Empowering Parents of Children with Autism Spectrum Disorder
Description:
Parents of children with Autism Spectrum Disorder at Chris Hani Baragwanath Academic Hospital reported high levels of stress, isolation and anxiety related to their child’s diagnosis. The ASD multidisciplinary team realised there needed to be a shift from an individual to a holistic family-centred approach, thus the ASD Parent Program was established. The ASD Parent Program was created to empower caregivers through education, skill development and support.

This unique service consists of four two-hourly sessions including sessions on; the introduction to Autism Spectrum Disorder, Communication, Sensory and behaviour, activities of daily living and a counselling session with a representative from Autism South Africa to address feelings, emotions, and specific challenges that families are facing.
Impact & Results:
Caregivers reported via feedback forms that ‘the group had so much to share which was really helpful.’ Caregivers are able to learn from each other and be active participants in intervention. They further reported the program ‘allowed them to see things differently’ and that they were not alone; meeting other families on a similar journey was beneficial and a better understanding of ASD was gained. The support networks facilitated allowed for ongoing connections and support.
 
Therapists acknowledged an increase in caregiver confidence, ownership of goals and improved generalization to the home setting. The attendance rate improved to over 80% for sessions. Parents are able to better understand and respond to their child’s specific needs, which allows both parent and child to have more meaningful interactions.

Description:
The WCG developed a new, paperless solution – the Electronic Patient Screening and Observation and Monitoring (EPSOM) application, a tablet-based, web and mobile enabled application for recording vital patient statistics and which could accommodate the need for a sterile environment. Tablets enabled quick, easy access and sharing of patient information and could easily sanitised by wiping before use by someone else. Additionally, wifi was set up at health sites to enable quarantined patients to communicate with their families using video and calls. Launched in June 2020, the solution is still being used at Brackengate, with ongoing enhancements.

EPSOM allowed the nursing sister in charge to have a central view of all patients in the facility, enabling quick assessments of and responses to any health risks. Using electronic records meant less chance of infection, saved space by reducing storage needed for paper records and made it easy to share and retrieve patient information in future. A completely new system, it was developed in house, in three months, by a cross functional team – comprising infrastructure, development, active directory, security, network technologists – using an agile approach. The application was fully web and mobile device enabled, and so accessible from any location. Strong security ensured that only authorised and approved users could access the system.
Novelty: The system was developed and is maintained in house, using the existing skills of our staff. It was built on an existing and standard platform, which means there are no additional costs for specialised software or platforms. It is scalable and can easily be used in different contexts, for different use cases or for more users.
Impact & Results: This innovation reduces staff exposure, helping the containment of the viral transmission. Further, it improved health service in general while supporting the respect of patient's dignity and wellbeing.

Description:
First integrated and multi-country COVID-19 certificate that collects standardized information about: 
- Vaccination Certificate
- Certificate of Recovery
- Test Certificate

It works without tracking the user's personal data and it's possible to be used online.
Novelty: First COVID-19 pass to be used by every EU member state

Project/Initiative Name: Expansion of the capacities of the National Radio Spectrum Monitoring and Control System of the Argentine Republic
Description:
ENACOM is the national telecommunications regulator in Argentina. For spectrum control and monitoring, it has a dispersed technological solutions park -different brands, age, functionalities-, limited economic resources and difficulties to hire human capital. iSpectrum developed and implemented in ENACOM a software platform for spectrum control and monitoring, which expanded the capabilities and increased functionalities, automated tasks and processes. 

Given the need to enhance the functionalities of the various measurement instruments and move forward with the automation of tasks and processes, at the end of 2021 ENACOM set out to acquire a control software platform for the monitoring of the radio spectrum. Additionally, due to technological obsolescence and dispersion, a solution was required to integrate and expand the capabilities of the entire system. This was the main challenge: to implement a technology capable of interacting with different equipment with different characteristics. 

The iSpectrum App solution is a communication and control platform, which allows different spectrum measurement instruments to be managed from a single, equipment-independent environment, with a standard display interface for all instruments of different brands and market costs. The innovation lies in the fact that it reduces the customer's dependency on equipment suppliers - hardware and software - as it easily adapts the functionalities offered by spectrum measurement equipment from different manufacturers according to each user's own needs. This drastically reduces investment costs. The developed technology enables the integration of existing infrastructure, which is obsolete for other systems, including hardware (PCs, equipment). At the same time, with iSpectrum App the spectrum information is available anytime, anywhere. 

The iSpectrum technology was developed according to the recommendations of the ITU, the International Telecommunication Union. It can be adapted to different scenarios, providing quality solutions for interference identification, control and monitoring of the radio spectrum.
Novelty: iSpectrum App's software-defined spectrum monitoring technology is unique in the market, as it is based on customer requirements, flexible and compatible with equipment from different manufacturers and with different functionalities. The technology enables automation and systematisation of processes at highly competitive costs.
Impact & Results: Since the implementation of the iSpectrum technology, an increase in system functionalities has been observed, as well as an effective automation and systematisation of processes that, prior to the implementation, were done manually. This has resulted in a reduction of the human error rate.

Description:
The 5G SCF - Smart Campus FACENS is a living laboratory that seeks to solve real problems of society, supported by the 17 SDGs; connecting the academic community, market/companies, government and society, with a very strong focus on environmental protection, technology and innovation. Both proof-of-concept projects are developed, applied on campus and to external clients, and supporting full-scale implementation in the priority areas of the National IoT Plan.

The 5G SCF - Smart Campus FACENS began in September 2014, when it was idealized the unification of several activities developed in the college campus that were related to the theme of smart cities

This project gave rise to the MBA in Management and Innovation in Smart Cities, which aims to develop knowledge, skills and attitudes to inspire leaders to understand, analyze and propose disruptive solutions to multidisciplinary issues, directed to the management and innovation of smart and sustainable cities and clusters, thinking and acting in a systemic, human and holistic way in the dimensions of the Axes of the Smart Campus Facens.
Novelty: The 5G Smart Campus Facens, has 9 axes and the Governance one is responsible for the Smart Lab that acts as the integrator between the strategic, tactical and operational plans of the program, having the commitment to ensure that the objectives of the Smart Campus Facens are successfully achieved, integrating people, departments, technologies, partners, managing data and maintaining the traceability of applications within the campus. It takes care of the Dashboard which is a system that acts directly in the acquisition, monitoring and control of the data generated in the FACENS campus, whether from administrative, financial or academic systems, integrating IoT devices, AI, and other technologies; integrating the academy, startups, companies and local, state and federal governments
Impact & Results: Since 2014, more than 350 projects have been developed in the 9 areas (Education and Culture; Energy; Mobility and Safety; Health and Quality of Life; Industry and Business; Environment, ICT, Urbanization and Governance). This involved more than 98 professors, almost 1700 students (directly), 7,208 participations of undergraduate students in events offered for training in the theme of Human Cities, 17 companies and more than 12,500 citizens were impacted with the concept of Smart Cities, 32 scientific articles generated on the theme; savings of 480. 710kWh; 226,100 KWh generated with photovoltaic energy, 68,302 cigarette butts collected for recycling impacting 34,151.5 liters of water that were no longer polluted by incorrect disposal, and more than 10 tons of electronic waste that no longer pollute the environment.

Description:
The employer digital user-centered designed magazine "Faktor A" of the Federal Employment Agency (BA) moved completely online and provides a quick and modern introduction to the most important employer topics (i.e. certificates, validations, continuous learning). 

Employers and entrepreneurs in Germany have been able to find everything they need in one place on the web. Moreover, they have the possibility to be directly interconnected based on their profiles and interests. With a journalistic thoroughness and depth, the e-magazine presents employer topics exclusively on the web. It combines a mix of research-intensive journalism with digital processing applied to the employment sector. 

The aim is to respond to readers' new usage habits in connection with touchscreens. Users who go to the platform in search of inspiration can thus explore the website at will. For employers looking for specific topics, the sections "Finding employees", "Qualifying employees", "Shaping the working world" and "Leading properly" offer a quick and concrete introduction. In this way, the editorial team also caters to the different habits of "Faktor A" readers. The site can be used on all devices and offers a completely new experience. In addition, a bi-weekly newsletter is published that informs about current developments in the labour market.
Novelty: A digital magazine by design that connects employers with job seekers to inform about the developments in the job market, provide access to training, link directly employers/job-seekers in need.
Impact & Results: No impacts available.

Description:
The MDT Tracheostomy service was established with involvement from major stakeholders to ensure holistic family-centered care that alleviates prolonged hospitalization for pediatric patients. Prolonged hospitalization impacts on childhood and psycho-social development as well as education opportunities and additionally resulted in substantial financial consequences for families, the hospital and state. 

The Paediatric MDT Tracheostomy service consists of a weekly ward round (including all major stakeholders) that focuses on individualized tracheostomy care. It provides caregiver support and training on the successful management of all aspects related to tracheostomy care, to enable more cost-effective home-based care that facilitates early discharge from hospital. The service further involves post-discharge follow-ups and ongoing feedback.

The service is unique as it ensures that all components of the service is provided within a multi-disciplinary approach in a single appointment to ensure holistic management. It improves health outcomes and satisfaction for patients by reducing the socio-economic and financial impact of prolonged hospitalization and multiple appointments. It encourages effective communication and accountability of all stakeholders involved and more efficient use of hospital resources.
Novelty: The service reduces the impact on early childhood development, education and psycho-social development of pediatric patients as well as the burden on families both in terms of emotions, time, and finances. The service It improves hospital resource utilization which ensures increased access to quality basic services.
Impact & Results: The service has improved outcomes and reintegration into communities for patients and their families which allows for equal access and opportunities to all developmental, education, recreational activities. More than 55 pediatrics have been discharged home successfully since establishing the service, with length of hospital stay reduced to an average of three months. It has contributed to reducing the substantial financial burden on families and the hospital.

Organisation: Ministry of Finance, Investment and Administration of the Government of the State of Guanajuato
Description:
The "GTO Financial Management Strategy" project was based on the idea of transforming financial management from a passive vision of the process to a proactive vision, taking control of the situation.

The project consisted in the introduction of the auction concept in the state's financial processes and services, which allowed the State Government to broadly expand its portfolio of financial service providers, opening the range of business opportunities to all relevant banking institutions. 

The process was divided into three categories: bank account auctions, public debt procurement auctions and investment management auctions. In order to build confidence in the investment and management of public resources, and to ensure the transparency of the process, SFIA developed a comprehensive system of electronic auctions, which enabled the contracting of financing, the allocation of resources to be invested and the awarding of bank accounts.

The strategy has made it possible to successfully achieve the institutions' goals:
- To contract public debt at a surcharge of less than 100 basis points.
- Obtain an interest rate higher than the target rate set by Banco de México for financial investments.
- Transparency in the process of assigning bank accounts, in order to provide legal certainty and obtain the best financial conditions.
Novelty: The use of auctions as a strategy to maximise the return on public resources is one of the main innovation factors of this project, especially going back to the early 2010s, when it was conceived. The creation of this strategy was therefore an unprecedented practice in public administration in Mexico at all levels (local, regional and national).
Impact & Results:
'- The target for the surcharge on public debt contracting was exceeded by 55%.
- An interest rate 0.5% higher than the target rate set by Banco de México was obtained on the state's financial investments.
- Rates of up to 102% of the TIIE (Interbank Equilibrium Interest Rate) were obtained, which allows for transparency in the allocation of bank accounts, legal certainty and the best financial conditions.
As a result, the state has a high credit quality, reaffirming its position as one of the highest rated states to which a subnational government can aspire.

Project/Initiative Name: Flow - Focus on engineering, not updates
Description:
Flow is a technology startup that aims to accelerate teams work on the cutting edge of technology. It aims to eliminate unnecessary email updates, laboriously sharing data, and spreadsheet based collaboration. Specifically, the platform pushes the latests outputs of a user's work to other team members, keeping their models consistent. 

The platform streamlines specific/tailored engineering processes from requirements, to modeling, to verification. In that sense, it avoids data fragmentation across models in Excel, Python, MATLAB, among others. Their mission is to accelerate all engineering projects globally. According to their predictive models, applying their system may cut 20% of the costs off the next major carbon reduction project, or take 2 years off the timeline for fusion.
Novelty: Engineer and algorithm-based system to accelerate integration from requirement, to modeling, to verification of projects. This is to avoid data fragmentation.
Impact & Results: According to their predictive models, applying their system may cut 20% of the costs off the next major carbon reduction project, or take 2 years off the timeline for fusion.

Organisation: International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies
Description:
FBF is a strategy to enable anticipatory humanitarian action by releasing a pool of funds triggered by scientific forecasts of extreme climate-related crisis. This makes it possible to act and plan beforehand, thus avoiding human and financial losses and strengthening the resilience of the most vulnerable communities. 

The novelty gets implemented in the window between a forecast and a disaster. The approach forecasts climate-related hazards and humanitarian bodies collect information about them to set up an automatic system that triggers response and funds preparedness actions before a credible warning arrives. In short, FBF is based on preparedness (readiness), forecast-based response (anticipation), set ups on the field and distribute financial mechanisms accordingly. 

Forecasted information is used on pre-agreed activities which reduce risks and enhance preparedness and response. The model is based on forecasts thresholds that trigger early actions that aim at reducing the humanitarian impact of the disaster and allows the implementation of Early Action Protocols.

It is currently being piloted/executed in several countries such as Peru, Nepal, Bangladesh and Togo.
Novelty: Uses climate-related hazard forecasts to extrapolate resource allocation and improve the financial capacity to deliver early responses on the field

Description:
The idea of ​​the project depends on creating a smart trash can to work in the field of sorting and a rewarding system, and through it, people will motivate to preserve the cleanliness of the environment. Also, the ultrasonic sensor sends a text message to the collection center stating that the trash is full.

The idea is to create a smart trash can possible to be used to deal with the waste dumped by users. The user is given an RFID tag and the user is identified by the RFID tag provided. Once the user clicks the RFID tag in the RFID reader, the trash can lid is opened by checking the user ID through the database. Then the user can empty the trash into the trash.

When the user dumps the trash into the trash, he will get points, and the load cell also measures the weight of the waste that is dumped by the user and sends it to the database. If the dustbin is full, the ultrasonic sensor will detect it and send a text message to the collection center that the trash is full.

The project encourages people to keep the environment clean by getting points when throwing recycling waste, and so the project will motivate people and contribute to the preservation of natural resources.
Novelty: The recycling project is newly made, it is working in the source sorting and reward system because of it, it will contribute to the preservation of the environment and natural resources. The project encourages people to keep the environment clean by getting points for throwing out recycling waste.

Description:
In the trend of interacting with technologies in a disruptive way to effectively emerge to complex social challenges in confrontation with Sustainable Economic Development, Gender Technologies are urgently needed.

The discussions about gender permeate topics such as equality, violence, and discrimination. The social and economic sustainability tends to deepen in this complexity that are persistent in the systematization in front of the absence of the municipalities in the elaboration of public policies out of the local context through interfaces of Open Innovation in consolidation of the communication in Network.
Novelty: Gender Technology invents a new language of cultural patterns that provides regenerative change of digital communication; contributing to the reduction of inequalities and the redefinition of concepts and biases about social and institutional behavior patterns.
Impact & Results: Therefore Gender Technologies translates into a mechanism for efficiency in the optimization of investments, automation of public spending, and the effectiveness of emerging economic development services through gender interoperability; a Smart and Sustainable Cities approach.

Project/Initiative Name: Global Forests Watch to fight deforestation around the world
Description:
Global Forest Watch is an open-source web application to monitor global forests in near real-time. It incorporates crowdsourced data with live data from multiple sources (ie. NASA) to provide information to people and authorities to better protect forests. Through the platform it is possible to inform where and how forests are changing around the world.

It has been used to measure global deforestation rates and to detect and monitor illegal clearing activity, primarily in Indonesia. The platform engages user to help them by supplying with data and technology to improve forest management. They also engage with the private sector through research, advocacy and fieldwork. 

GFW is free and enables anyone to create custom maps, analyze forest trends, subscribe to alerts, or download data for their local area or the entire world. Users can also contribute to GFW by sharing data and stories from the ground via GFW’s crowdsourcing tools, blogs, and discussion groups.
Novelty: An interactive online forest monitoring and alert system

Project/Initiative Name: Global Government - ENet - (the next generation of internet)

Description:
The proposal is to create a worldwide organisation - Global Recycling Plant (GRP) that coordinates all the Recycling Plants that are created in each of the countries. Only the governments of each country have the monetary and infrastructural resources to create their own recycling plants. It will not be a business, it will be an exchange of processed materials between all countries.

It solves 2 problems 1) global waste pollution and 2) reduces the exploitation of natural resources. Regardless of their size, number of inhabitants and level of development, all countries produce waste. The innovation is about a Global Body that manages the recycling of the whole Planet. Goals: The GRP will be in contact with all the Governments of the Planet and its mission will be to verify the recycling compliance of all the rubbish that each country produces; in turn, each country will create policies of classification, collection, disposal, as well as the means or campaigns to achieve that each and every one of interested parties, take the rubbish that they produce to the treatment plant, have the resources both human and infrastructure to carry out the recycling and obtain all the possible materials, then the levels of production of the obtained materials and the way to exchange them between the different countries will be established; When each country has its stock of new recycled materials, the next step is within each of them and consists of selling these materials to the production, capital and consumer goods industries, etc., to obtain resources for maintenance, sustainability and innovation, not only for themselves, but also to contribute, depending on their size, to the GRP.
Novelty: This project is innovative in every way, especially because it ensures a step forward in the evolution of the human species, since its realisation requires a change in mentality at a global level, it will eliminate barriers and its main condition is the union of all the governments of the planet, it involves all citizens, because each one of us uses the resources of the planet, produces waste and is not renewing them, they are simply being thrown away indiscriminately everywhere.
Impact & Results: Some of the results of recycling are: saving energy, it is less expensive to recycle a material than to manufacture it from scratch. Recycling means that no new raw materials are extracted, and methods of extraction of natural resources, which are invasive and polluting, are avoided. Saving money, saving oil, saving water, etc. The expected result in the future is to recover 90% of the waste materials we produce. Clean up the oceans, no matter how titanic the work involved, reclaim the land, not allow any more species to become extinct.

Project/Initiative Name: GNova Lab - The government innovation lab at the National School of Public Administration (Enap)
Description:
The GNova Lab experiments with new ways of thinking about public problems and challenges and acting in government. GNova operates in three axes: 1- prospection of new methods and trends applicable to the public sector 2- experimentation in public policies, as a mechanism to discover the possibilities, test opportunities and identify the risks involved, and; 3 - dissemination of knowledge produced and support for the development of competencies, through events, networks, conferences and publications.

The creation of GNova Lab is part of a broader context of innovation promotion in the public sector, in which the Public Sector Innovation Network - InovaGov Network - deserves to be highlighted, bringing together more than a hundred participants to exchange experiences and develop joint activities. In its daily practice, GNova is guided by the promotion of prospecting activities, experimentation, and dissemination of innovations in the public sector, aimed at improving service delivery and better meeting the needs of citizens, businesses, and third sector organizations. 

The experimentation axis is predominant in the GNova Lab's work, gathering, during the first years, about 70% of its actions and projects. It includes testing new methodologies for the formulation, implementation and evaluation of public policies, serving as a space of freedom, autonomy and authorization for the development of creative processes, hypothesis validation, prototype testing and other agile ways to qualify proposals in a collaborative, iterative, fast and citizen-focused way.

For our innovation methodologies to be applied on a larger scale, our Dissemination strategy includes efforts to bring to public servants the learnings, discoveries and results of the lab's activities, its partners and the public innovation community. This occurs through events, organization of activities, curation of content, launching and distribution of publications, frequent dialog with people and organizations interested in public innovation to exchange experiences and information.
Novelty: The GNova Lab is guided by guidelines and values of openness to risk, networking, collaboration, public value creation, empathy, experimentation, and proactivity.  Its premise is to encourage the opening of possibilities to partner teams responsible for public problems or challenges from practical references. That is, not only to propose other ways, but to actually test such working hypotheses - focusing on the user and testing ideas early on together with the relevant actors (decision makers, implementers, beneficiaries, researchers) to be called upon to build understandings and address the problems.
Impact & Results: To quote the most recent experimentation figures, between 2019 and 2021, GNova Lab has executed 25 projects, 244 workshops, and 6 methodologies/approaches developed. Results are measured through mixed methods: field experiments, qualitative reports, pilot experiments. In the effort to disseminate project methodologies, GNova Lab has already directly and indirectly oriented dozens of laboratories in government in Brazil.

Project/Initiative Name: Hongseong's neo-endogenous rural innovation upon public-private partnership and local governance system of 'Hongseong-tong (HST)'
Description:
Korea's Hogseong County, located in South-Chungcheong Province, has led social innovation in sustainable rural development with multiple-stakeholders such as the central government, entrepreneurs, academia, civil society, and local communities. In particular, operating its open-access local governance system, "Hongseong-tong (HST)," the government has accelerated reliable public-private partnerships on which diverse local policies and projects have been (re-)produced with its citizens.

Since 2012 they have operated the HST governance system with the five principles described below. (i) Horizontal relations among the public-private participants without any authority. (ii) Instead, two coordinators undertake administrative tasks from the public-private respectively. (iii) Monthly meeting of the HST participants every last Tuesday. (iv) Sharing information in-between the public and the private and finding local problems and ideas. (v) If necessary to deepen specific discussion, any sub-committees of HST can be operated to deal with detailed issues.

Basically, The HST governance system is subject to benefit all of the residents in the public areas. For that, it is operating nine sub-committees by Sep 2022 divided according to different topic fields. HST does not have a proper institutional backbone yet. However, as it covers growing policy fields in-between the public and the private for the last ten years, the participant organizations have discussed efficient ways to advance their solidarity further, such as establishing HST cooperatives in the near future. At the same, the local government of Hongseong has also considered enacting an ordinance to support and develop the governance system itself.
Novelty:
1. Upon the principles of spontaneousness and autonomy, the public-private actors co-establish and co-operate the governance system by which they influence multiple policies of the central and local government.
2. The HST governance system pursues open-access and horizontal relations among the multiple stakeholders so that there is no limitation to discussing local issues in-between the public and the private.
3. Within the HST governance system, the local government Hongseong reflects its residents' needs on the ground by designing the local policies through EARD, which closely connects with multiple policies of the Korean central government.
Impact & Results: Since 2012, the HST governance system has operated more than 200 meetings in-between the public and the private. It contributed to designating Hongseong County as a special area for organic agriculture by the Ministry of SMEs and Startups (MSS) in 2014. Its school lunch system based on environmentally-friendly food was introduced through the HST governance system in 2015. In 2016, EARD and HST contributed to establishing the Local Community Support Center (LCSC) by enacting an ordinance. In 2018, HST worked on making an ordinance to support the youth and establish a team in the local government. Recently, EARD and HST helped to enact two ordinances to secure the rights to food and support public food provisioning and establish Food Policy Council. This September, Hongseong County is designated as a pre-Cultural City on Organic Intellectuals by the Ministry of Culture, Sports, and Tourism (MCST). This project is also coined through the HST governance system.

Description:
Victorian Government created the Human-Centred-Design Playbook, a design guide and training for Victorian public servants who are designing, procuring or managing human-centred design (HCD) projects. It has been developed to expand design literacy amongst the public service, to increase the success rate of design-led projects, and to promote the most optimal collaborations and outcomes with design teams embedded in government departments and external design agencies.

Three objectives drove the creation of the Playbook: (i) build human centred design capability, (ii) increase design literacy, and (iii) support the VPS with a range of precedents and case studies. To develop the playbook, a collaborative design process with 50 representatives from across academia, industry and government was carried out. A systematic review of existing toolkits, guides and playbooks from within and outside government provided inspiration and informed the evaluation framework against which innovations and products were measured during the design and development stages

The end result, the HCD Playbook, is comprised of three parts:
1. Process, tool and resources: The Playbook, published on the Victorian Government website and openly accessible. The playbook includes a new design model for government, design plans, tools and resourcing guidance. This part is designed to address budgetary 
2. Mentoring and training: Introduction to Human Centred Design training sessions facilitated by professional government designers to all public sector staff in an online, interactive and small group format.
3. Spaces and community: An online community of practice comprised of professional learning and peer-to-peer knowledge sharing.
Novelty: There are many HCD playbooks, guides and toolkits available. This guide is unique because it was written for the context of the Victorian public sector and the 345,000 members that make up the public sector. It seeks to make the design process more accessible by combining HCD methods and outputs into a set of design plans. The design plans are the key feature of this playbook and what separates it from others in circulation as they provide structural guidance and detailed methods to match design-led activities with the various resource envelopes (budgets and timeframes) and processes of government.
Impact & Results:
Since its release in early 2020 the HCD Playbook has been used by various teams across the Victorian government to develop services addressing family violence, mental health, bushfire recovery and bespoke technology products within government.
The rollout of the HCD Playbook has been accompanied by bespoke training also created by the Service Design Team for online delivery since 2020. To date, the Service Design Team has trained 1000+ Victorian public servants across 112 departments, public agencies and government entities in the usage and application of the design plans, methodologies and tools that are set out in the HCD Playbook.
The HCD Playbook and training have contributed to the capability uplift of public servants by increasing their creative confidence, demystifying human-centred design and de-risking the adoption of HCD into their work. Additionally, this number would be further increased by users of the Playbook online resource. 
Building upon the success of the Human Centred Design Playbook to date, the next series of training modules to be launched will offer staff training in prototyping and co-design approaches to further embed design across the Victorian Government.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
Human Resources Inventory aims to increase resource allocation efficiency by analyzing employees' work experience, educational attainment, and demographics. Hence, the project supports human resource planning processes in both public and private sector institutions. It makes all institutions' human resources management processes transparent, data-based, efficient and accountable and supports the efforts to increase efficiency and productivity in public institutions.

HR Inventory System supports human resource planning processes in both public and private sector institutions. 
Administrative data in different institutions have been combined anonymously to support policymakers' data-driven and evidence-based decision-making process. Within the scope of the HR Inventory System, the labor pool is visualized by using world-renowned big data analysis techniques to support people analytics practices and to employ qualified human resources in the public sector.
Novelty: The HR Inventory System is the first project in Türkiye that uses large administrative data sets which support evidence based decision making processes of institutions. It also improve human resources policies that institutions will implement with the analysis results it provides. With this system, it is possible to produce data-based policy and make decisions and effective planning. In this context, the HR Inventory System aims to encourage institutions to make strategic human resources planning and to support them with HR practices to be carried out in both public and private institutions. Thus, it is aimed to increase operational efficiency in the long term by ensuring more efficient use of human resources in institutions and organizations.
Impact & Results: HR Inventory System makes human resource planning processes of the institutions transparent, data-based, efficient and accountable for both public and private sectors. Thus, more efficient, data-driven and evidence-based HR strategies are conducted for short, medium and long-term human resource planning in Türkiye

Description:
The ICIJ database contains information on >810 thousand offshore entities that are part of the Pandora Papers, Paradise Papers, Bahamas Leaks, Panama Papers and Offshore Leaks investigations. The records cover more than 80 years up to 2020 and link to people and companies in more than 200 countries and territories.

The database strips away the secrecy that cloaks companies and trusts incorporated in tax havens and exposes the people behind them. This includes, when available, the names of the real owners of those opaque structures. In all, the interactive application reveals more than 750,000 names of people and companies behind secret offshore structures. 

The database come from leaked records and not a standardized corporate registry, so ICIJ is to provide the data as clean as possible. In some cases, companies are listed as shareholders for another company or a trust, an arrangement that often helps obscure the flesh-and-blood people behind offshore entities.
Novelty: Publicly available database that compiles offshore information of several "Papers scandals" related to potential illegal money flows.

Description:
ImmTune Therapies is a medical and biotech initiative that develops a platform for in-patient generation of cell and gene therapies, increasing the potency and reach of these therapies to all the patients who need them. Using novel delivery vectors, they are overcoming the challenges posed by current vectors which have limited the use of cell and gene therapies to ex-vivo manufacturing processes, and are developing a new way of manufacturing these therapies in-vivo at an affordable cost.

The innovation is pioneering the development of highly selective, safe and efficient delivery vectors for in-patient generation of cell and gene therapies. These include CRISPR and CAR-T immunotherapies. CAR-T therapy is a form of cancer treatment that uses a chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) to target killer T-cells towards tumour cells in the body. 

Their novel delivery technology allows them to safely, and effectively deliver genetic cargoes to targeted cells directly inside patients. This in-body generation of therapeutically active cells is cheaper, and produces more effective and longer-lasting curative products. This allows ImmTune to platform and to provide an affordable option for other disease indications including solid tumours, autoimmune and infectious diseases with high public and global health value.
Novelty: A medical and biotech initiative that develops a platform for in-patient generation of cell and gene therapies based on CAR-T therapies to provide affordable options for cancer and other disease indicators.
Impact & Results:
Several scientific prizes in 2022 for its approach to immune-oncology: 
https://www.pharmiweb.com/press-release/2022-03-16/astellas-and-we-are-pioneer-group-wapg-award-future-innovator-prize-to-innovative-life-science-startup-immtune-therapies

Project/Initiative Name: Implementation of the Quality Management System under ISO 9001:2015 of the Bridge Construction Management process
Description:
For the Provincial Government of Manabí it is a priority to work and develop an approach based on Quality Management Processes, the institution in its Territorial Development Plan has the goal of building 120 bridges in 4 years of management. currently, 81 bridges have been built. Innovation goes into how to effectively manage work activities to create value for citizens and improve their quality of life.

The project arose from the need to increase the quality and results of bridge construction in the province of Manabí. The lack of connectivity of the province's rural populations means that communities are isolated during the winter season. 

For the Provincial Government of Manabí, it is a priority to work and develop an approach based on Quality Management Processes, and for this it was necessary to innovate all its processes and to carry out an adequate change management. Faced with this challenge, the institution saw the need to innovate its forms and processes in institutional management.
Novelty: Since 2020, the Provincial Government of Manabí has started to have internal processes that guarantee employees a guide on how to execute their processes, ensuring quality, there are no more reprocesses, response times have been substantially reduced and the documentation of the Quality Management System is available to everyone on the institutional intranet.
Impact & Results:
- Increased internal and external user satisfaction
- Increased construction of more bridges per semester
- Reduced time from planning to construction of a bridge
- Decreased internal reprocessing - increased quality measurement
- To be the first public institution in Ecuador with innovative practices in all value-adding processes.
- Tangible results
- Bridge construction increased from 10 bridges per year 2018 to 26 bridges per year by 2022.

Description:
The ultimate goal is for the portal to ensure that it is easy to read and navigate from any device, that it uses clear language, that it guides the citizen through the process in a simple way and that it adapts to each person's situation. To this end, the citizen has been taken into account from the earliest stages, with the aim of creating the best possible experience through easy and user-friendly services.

The ultimate goal is for Importass to be the single portal that allows anyone to find out their personal information and manage their procedures with the TGSS. In order to meet this challenge, four main lines of innovation were chosen:

1) Methodologies for citizen involvement: through the implementation of initiatives such as social research, usability tests, contextual interviews, shadowing and focus groups.

2) Innovative design system consisting of a catalogue of standardised design solutions that make it possible to build the interfaces that the organisation needs to operate its services, facilitating the creation of new content on the Social Security web portal.

Agile methodology: Business and development experts worked with a design team as a translator of business needs.

4. Process modernisation: Processes that could be automated were identified in order to free civil servants from manual tasks to focus on those that provide greater value to the citizen, through the implementation of new RPA solutions.
Novelty:
Importass has meant a paradigm shift, putting the citizen at the centre of everything that is done, transforming the way of working when designing electronic services, using the most innovative methodologies.
This new way of working means that citizens, when interacting electronically with the TGSS, have the same security and certainty as if they were attended to by a civil servant, increasing the use of digital services.
Impact & Results:
- 2.6 M users of the Importass portal
- 28.3 M page views with an average duration of 6 minutes.
- 2.0 M requests for employment history.
In terms of the specific impact of the implementation of RPA technology, these are the results:
- 28,000 hearing procedures generated.
- 71% of data cleaning work has been eliminated, saving 43,000 working hours.

Organisation: United States Department of Commerce, National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA)
Description:
The Indicators of Broadband Need map is a multi-layered visualization effort that uses several different open data sources to show information on broadband availability within the United States. Layers in this map were created using data sourced from the American Community Survey collected by the U.S. Census, Ookla, Measurement Lab (M-Lab), Microsoft and the Federal Communications Commission (FCC). In addition, there is a layer in the map that displays the locations of higher education institutions eligible as Minority Serving Institutions (MSIs), and it shows layers of areas designated as American Indian, Alaska Native and Native Hawaiian Areas.

The innovation helps to illustrate the reality that communities experience when going online, with many parts of the country reporting speeds that fall below the FCC’s current benchmark for fixed broadband service of 25 Mbps download, 3 Mbps upload. The map also puts poverty and lack of broadband access on the same page.
Novelty: The public “Indicators of Broadband Need” tool released today puts on one map, for the first time, data from both public and private sources. This is the first map that allows users to graphically compare and contrast these different data sources.
Impact & Results:
The mapping platform allows these states and others to better inform broadband projects and funding decisions.
The dataset allows you to see where high-poverty communities are located and how that relates to internet usage patterns, as well as to a lack of computers and related equipment. The map also shows usage patterns in tribal communities, which have historically suffered from lack of internet access.

Description: To address massive logistical challenges with regard to nationwide COVID-19 vaccination, we rolled out a state-of-the art IT system. By leveraging potential of AI and data science, our system integrates the entire vaccine delivery chain and supports every step, from expression of interest and scheduling appointments, to tracking every vaccine vial and handling procurement, to vaccination, record keeping and issuing certificates. The system supported the delivery of about 9 million vaccines
Novelty: The initiative harnesses the potential of ICTs, AI and data science, to ensure quick, simple and equitable access to vaccines by supporting the entire nationwide vaccine delivery chain and enabling optimal coordination and informed decision-making. AI algorithm schedules vaccine appointments based on numerous criteria such as age, profession, health condition and preferred location.
Impact & Results: By September 2022, the system supported the delivery of close to 9 million COVID-19 vaccines, around 500.000 to foreign nationals with or without a registered stay in Serbia. Immunization reduced the risk of death caused by infection for persons willing to get the vaccine and allowed for returning to a new normal. An actual number of saved lives and benefits of immunization taking into account the economic and social implications of the crisis cannot be measured. Aside from this, the system facilitated the work of both medical professionals who use it daily, and decision-makers.

Project/Initiative Name: Information system to support the design and development of student transport routes in the Region of Western Greece
Description:
Aim of this tool is to improve the organization of the student transfer process in the Region of Western Greece by introducing a tool based on GIS applications, applying state-of-the-art technologies. The target audience for the tool includes: Schools / Students, Transport companies, Administrators in the Region. 

Students are expected to have transportation services for their daily commute to and from school based on various criteria provided by law, such as distance restrictions. The aim of the tool is twofold and focuses on the automatic application/request for funded transportation (based on the predefined rules describing under what conditions a student can use public transport for free) and on assisting the relevant actions of the Region (eg privision of a funcionality to assist on organizing chartered routes for transportation to school).

The system is used by all schools in Western Greece. During productive use, over 13 thousand valid student transportation requests, over 200 regular transportation lines supported by 7 transportation companies, and over 2 thousand user-generated leased routes in the Region of Western Greece are collected per school year, for students who they live in remote or hard-to-reach areas.

The benefits from using this sustem are as follows:
- Ability to speed up the process and avoid delays in the start of the school year
- Streamlining transportation costs by ensuring compliance with legislation.
Novelty: This innovation deploys ICT tools to improve the organization of the student transfer process in the Region of Western Greece by introducing a tool based on GIS applications.
Impact & Results:
The tool logged all user interactions while performing the tasks. Hence, we are able to quantify the number and type of interactions per type (previous school year):
- Number of Total Schools 629
- Applications 13485
- Standard routes 271
- Custom routes 2691

The results achieved can be summarised as follows:
- Efficiency improvement
- Automation of application collection process from schools
- Automated control based on the relevant legislation
- Special tool application used by admin users to create leased itineraries
- Improvement of user - transaction satisfaction
- Time improvement for route planning, Faster process assuring the implementation of the relevant legislation.

Description:
We need to accelerate growth of the Innovation Economy around the world, & the collaboration we are building has that goal as its primary purpose. It consists of more than 50 "InnovationEconomy" country code top level domains (cctld) such as InnovationEconomy.UK, .IN, .JP, .CA & many more with InnovationEconomy.Com or .Org promoting these sites as they promote the acceleration of innovation in their respective countries.  Target entities include innovation agencies, leading universities, technology companies and similar parties that significantly impact the growth of the global Innovation Economy.

Use of these Innovation Economy sites would not be limited to a specific type of innovation. The leadership group will determine what posts should be regularly added to the "InnovationEconomy" website regarding innovations in the applicable country and regarding associated commercial success levels, economic impact & other matters related to the featured innovations.
Novelty: Its focus is on global, country-by-country innovation and associated economic success. Subject to agreement by collaboration members, the emphasis will be on promoting & inspiring more innovation activities in applicable countries and on development of education materials associated with intellectual property (IP) and "open innovation" concepts.
Impact & Results: We are working to make the outcomes or impacts of this Innovation Economy collaboration an accelerated growth of the Innovation Economy in many (hopefully most) countries around the world. We expect that more and more millions of people around the world will think of themselves as knowledgeable regarding, and a part of, the #InnovationEconomy. Eventually we may build the metrics to measure economic growth that results from innovations. When these metrics get implemented, we will shoot for 1% growth at first and then grow from there.

Organisation: Criminal, Contraventional and Misdemeanours Court 10 of the Autonomous City of Buenos Aires
Description:
The public's distrust in the administration of justice brought with it the need for accountability in order to make government actions transparent. We were able to do this through the use of information and communication technologies, available and accessible to society.

We created a gender-sensitive dataset, in which we collected categorical information about the victim of violence and the context in which she or he is immersed. We collect data such as gender, age, nationality, education, income, family situation, among others. 

We also created a dataset that keeps a record of all the cases that are processed in the court. One of the purposes of this is to store, group and organise in a single document a series of identifying data of the court files, with the aim of having access to the information in a common, fast and efficient way. 

We created a "Guide to clear language and style", with the aim of contributing to modify the vocabulary and expressions we use, in order to simplify them; to reduce the use of unnecessary technical terms in order to prioritise more accessible terminology; to abandon androcentric and binarist language, to replace it with the use of words or expressions that include all people.

We are a court that shows its statistics to the world, always with the caution that people's sensitive data deserves, which we protect by anonymising it through the desktop application IA2 (its name comes from the union of open institutions and artificial intelligence). This is an artificial intelligence programme in which we load our sentences in ODT format, and it automatically anonymises the personal information of the people who were part of that sentence. This application was developed with the aim of being able to make our sentences public while protecting the identity of the persons.
Novelty: We are an innovative and pioneering court in the responsible use of information and communication technologies to generate mechanisms for citizen interaction and participation, openness and data protection, which led to the co-creation of an Artificial Intelligence tool to carry out this task.
Impact & Results:
One of the results of the implementation of the initiative is the co-creation of the first plain-language judicial document, which was achieved after making it available for a comprehension check. 

On the other hand, the call to participate in the hearings through social networks significantly increased the presence of the public at the hearings, reaching a total of 17 people.

Project/Initiative Name: Keep your eye on the ball: Our experience of learning to juggle out-patient team-based models to facilitate integrated patient care
Description:
Previously, patients who have experienced a stroke/head injury were booked into three separate appointment systems for speech therapy, occupational therapy and physiotherapy that resulted in increased waiting times, decreased therapist capacity and poor appointment utilization due to missed initial appointments/patient spontaneous recovery. We then implemented a trans-disciplinary screening service and an inter-disciplinary out-patient service developed by all three teams to reduce the barriers.

There are three coordinators (one representative from each discipline) appointed to ensure maintenance and sustainability of the services. The coordinators have a monthly face-to-face meeting to discuss matters and have general ongoing communication. Handovers of the coordination role take place should a new coordinator be appointed.
Novelty: The trans-disciplinary service allows improved use of clinical time as only one therapist screens for all three disciplines thus improving capacity for the therapists. A reduced waiting time means patients can access services faster thus, receiving more rehabilitation within the critical period (first six months after the stroke/head injury). The inter-disciplinary service allows all three professionals to attend to the patient simultaneously thus, allowing for person centred care where the patient is at the centre of attention and all therapeutic goals are directed by the patient and family with facilitation by all three therapists. This is a shift from how the services previously ran in silos similar to other public health institutions.
