The project facilitates access to health care services by reducing financial burdens by reducing transport costs for multiple appointments as singular appointments are implemented as well as caregiver burden as less leave days are required from work.
Impact & Results:
The screening service increases capacity for therapists (improves use of clinical time as one therapist only is needed) thus, decreasing waiting times for patients and allows attendance of singular appointments. 

Patients attend more appointments. The average attendance rate for the year 2020 was 52% compared to 71% in 2021 and reaching 100% in April 2021.

Description:
Kotikatuportaali, Home Street Portal is an online service developed by the public works department and serves residents by providing diverse information about public works and city planning in their neighbourhood. The purpose of the portal is to gather up-to-date information on matters and services "close to home." The website enables city residents to follow the changes and repairs in their neighbourhood and planned street projects.

This is a one-stop shop that centralizes information from different platforms about the streets, public works and city planning efforts in the city of Espoo. Through a map-based interface the citizens have access to, for example, visualize current street repairs, up-to-date snow ploughing, salting and sanding data, among others. 

The material used by the portal is automatically collected from the city's information systems and is of daily update. This data collection happens through direct feedback provision to the Public Works Department. The website has a high standard of information and data security: WCAG 2.1 level AA. Since the information utilised in the service is based on existing data available and there is no need for additional work to create data to the portal.
Novelty:
One-stop shop street platform that makes the information more accessible and user-friendly for the citizens. 
Cost-efficient to upkeep since the data is gathered from different sources.

Project/Initiative Name: LEGISInfo - Parliament of Canada's online research tool
Description:
LEGISinfo is Canada's Parliament web-based tool that allows tracking every piece of proposed legislation as it makes its way through the Senate and the House of Commons. It has a repository that allows tracing information backwards up to 1994. 

Citizens can find the full text of any bill, its sponsor, its status, parliamentarians’ speeches and more. It's a comprehensive tool that allows to scroll or search to find bills by number, keyword, date, current status among other categories. Also displays speeches and statements related to such bills. 

LEGISinfo is a collaborative service provided jointly by the Senate, the House of Commons and the Library of Parliament. It can be used in conjunction with the Senate’s Twitter account in plain-language summaries of bills and live-tweets their progress in the Upper Chamber. Furthermore, all the content is exportable in Open datasets in XML and JSON file formats. A customizable RSS feed is also available.
Novelty: Legislation follows the path of service delivery and delivery chains.

Organisation: National Penitentiary Procurator's Office (Procuración Penitenciaria de la Nación)
Description:
"Let's Try Talking" is a mechanism aimed at improving the human rights situation in prisons, contributing to their democratic management, for the benefit of workers, inmates and their families. It is based on the promotion and facilitation of dialogue by independent teams. It is innovative in that it promotes speech as a human right, emphasises prevention, collaboration and participation, and proposes that every member of the community be an actor of change.

This mechanism seeks to include, in a transparent and genuine way, the authorities of detention centres, persons deprived of their liberty and those who work in detention centres in direct contact with the population, so that they can become agents of change in favour of conflict prevention and transformation, fundamentally by exercising the "human right to speak", which we consider to be realised when people are genuinely heard.

This approach aims to benefit all the people who make up the prison communities reached, mainly through the transformative action of the people who participate in the dialogue, who are invited to take ownership of the spaces of conversation, to participate in their construction and in their care; to speak, to listen to each other, to respect each other and to put themselves in the place of others; to experience transformations in their relationships and in how they communicate, in their perspectives and in their capacities to practice dialogue and use it to prevent and resolve conflicts; to learn to control their reactions, to become more responsible and to take ownership of their lives.
Novelty: The "Let's Try Talking" project is making it possible to overcome the fatalistic view of the possibility of transforming custodial institutions, especially by encouraging, legitimising and empowering attitudes, behaviours and transformative practices, both of inmates and officials; overcoming approaches that emphasise the detection of shortcomings and the punishment of those responsible. It allows us to have a concrete approach to the problem of violence among detainees and to make real progress in the direction indicated by Mandela Rule No. 38. Among its strengths, we highlight its inter-institutional support network and its solid roots in the communities of confinement where it is developed, which have made it possible to give permanence to the device and to develop its own specific and flexible working method, in charge of trained facilitators.
Impact & Results:
The initiative's goals for the development of the dialogue are reasonably met: that the participants feel safe, agree on and comply with the rules for the dialogue, feel part of the space and that it belongs to them, that they speak, listen to each other, respect each other and put themselves in other people's shoes. 
At the same time, we observed considerable effects at the individual level, in an unspecified number of participants and cases of improved family ties; some positive changes, such as the attenuation of certain forms of ritual violence among young people and a softening of rivalries between groups and sectors in "maximum security" units; more dialogue between the population, the SPF and other authorities; cases of collaboration between rival sectors.

Organisation: LIPOR - Intermunicipal Waste Management Service of Greater Porto
Description:
LIPOR is the entity responsible for the management and recovery of municipal waste produced by 8 municipalities. LIPOR has been implementing an integrated waste management system since it was established in 1982. A pioneering strategy that dates back to the 90's is the Landfill Sealing, Recovery and Environmental and Landscape Enhancement Project. From the former Ermesinde/Baguim do Monte landfill, LIPOR's Adventure Park & Ecological Trail was born.

This innovation is aimed at the entire population: from the local population, offering spaces and equipment for recreation and leisure, to the young population, providing an active and diversified recreation, as well as for the school population and organized groups, providing organized visits motivated by contact with nature, sports and learning. 

Although admission is free, LIPOR invites all visitors to the Adventure Park & Eco-Trail to hand in recyclable waste as a "symbolic cost". As an Entity responsible for the management and recovery of urban waste, LIPOR, through this initiative, ensures that the recyclables delivered to us in this way are delivered at the right place (ecopoint) for a better recovery and incorporation in the value chain (recycling industries) as secondary raw materials.

In 2021, LIPOR's Adventure Park & Eco-Trail was awarded the Green Flag Award (a non-profit international accreditation programme that recognises and rewards well-managed parks and green spaces) in its first year of application, in addition to being recognised with the Special Award for Innovation for its innovative work regarding waste, recycling, environment and biodiversity.
Novelty:
LIPOR's pioneering strategy is innovative, as it has transformed a landfill into an Adventure Park & Eco-Trail, where more than 50,000 visitors per year are welcomed to explore and enjoy green spaces while learning about waste management, preservation and promotion of biodiversity and sustainable consumption. 

This infrastructure is based on the Organisation's policy and strongly contributes to the reinforcement of the LIPOR Brand and the good integration in the surrounding society, which reflects the thousands of people who have visited us throughout the 11 seasons.
Impact & Results:
In 2020, the 10th anniversary of the Adventure Park & Ecological Trail was celebrated. Since its inauguration, 3,607 environmental awareness and training, recreational and sports activities have been carried out, involving 377,768 visitors/participants. 

As a symbolic cost for entering the space, about 53 tons of recyclable waste have been delivered since 2011. These deliveries have been increasing annually, which represents an increase in the awareness of our visitors to the subject of recycling.

Description:
Citizen Connect is aimed to inform Little Rock's citizens understand what is happening around their homes or businesses at any given time. The site contains 311 data requests from Little Rock Police Crime Data, Planning Permits, Business Licenses and Housing & Neighborhood Programs' Land Bank information in one easy place to navigate.

Residents can sign up to receive emails from the site which will provide updates on the timetable and information they chose. Examples of updates which can be provided are when a Code Violation is entered into the 311 system or when there is a public safety incident in the neighborhood. The site also allows users to view where requests are coming in, do queries by date range and item type and shows some simple trend analysis on the data being viewed.

The tool is expected to contribute to collaborative efforts to guard the city and support the local authorities keep crime at the line.
Novelty: Near real-time information on Little Rock's incidences related to Code Violation across neighborhoods. Direct connection to the police and law enforcement authorities.

Description:
Little Rock's has transformed its repository for Open Data & Performance measurement. The site provides easy access to information about the city main stats and open data. The aim is to spark innovation, promote public collaboration, increase government transparency and inform decision making. Moreover, to support a range of outcomes (i.e. quality of life, government efficiency). 

Developers and overall citizens are able to access data as it is added to the system. In addition, private businesses, developers and programmers can freely access the same data and incorporate it into business processes, web or mobile applications.
Novelty: Open data that specific works for performance measurement and that targets city's main strategic goals.

Description:
We are thinking of developing a magneto-voltaic cell/battery (MCV/B) to improvise public service delivery formats in smart cities. Implicitly, we are thinking of launching an alternative to photovoltaic cells. This is the need of the hour because in this turbulent world affected by the climatic shifts and the depleting energy reserves where the reliance on solar power would be an eyewash given that "solar credits" are yet to emerge in the scientific jargon, MVC might be posed as better alternatives for refurbishing public service formats in smart cities as also elsewhere. 

As a synergistic tool between natural sciences and governance alongside information and communication technologies (ITC), MVC shall provide the much-desired alternative to the depleting energy reserves and prove to be a long-term and cost-effective alternative to the existing energy fuels.
Novelty: As a renewable, cost-efficient and reliable energy source, it is anticipated that MVCs shall provide the much-necessary succor to the already-pestered world where energy sources are fast depleting and alternative sources are being sought after.
Impact & Results: At present, the innovation is an idea. It is anticipated that besides powering the smart cities, MVCs shall also be a great help in the mainstream economic sectors for the refurbishment of the global order.
Other relevant URL(s) - eg reports, pres:
SP: suggest "No Review"
JB: Agree - not implemented

Project/Initiative Name: Malawi Local Authority infrastructure projects monitoring Information System
Description: The government of Malawi is implementing an infrastructure development projects monitoring information system (PMIS) which aims at aggregating LA-level development project information and enable real time feedback and monitoring. This platform will advance transparency of local authorities service delivery; enable real-time feedback and interaction with citizens; facilitate government responsiveness to citizen voices and provide an additional data point for evidence-based decision-making

Project/Initiative Name: Management Information System of Vocational High School (VHS) apprenticeship programs
Description:
Vocational High School (VHS) apprenticeship programs combine work-based, on-the-job learning with relevant technical education in the classroom. These duties in are supported by a complex Management Information System (MIS), named “Mathiteia”, built for the management of the whole lifecycle of Apprenticeships. It is through Mathiteia that students apply to apprenticeships posted by businesses and public bodies, enter into an Apprenticeship Contract with the employer, receive subsidy, obtain insurance coverage, and receive their certification. The complex institutional and legal framework of apprenticeship, the number and diversity of the users involved and the labyrinthine workflow of its processes, required a modern and innovative information system. The aim of this innovation led by the team of the Computer Technology Institute and Press "Diophantus" is the design, development, operation and maintenance of this complex information system.

Mathiteia MIS, the innovation they developed, supports a large set of heterogeneous users who are involved in its workflow: apprentices, private and public organisations, Regional Directorate of Education (RDE), VHS Director, teachers and supervisors, the apprenticeships administrator from the Ministry of Education and Science, and the financial administrators. This innovation provided a flexible and versatile system to reduce the bureaucratic burden of the processes, facilitate and encourage the stakeholders to participate, and it can be changed effectively according to new legislation. Moreover, the innovation provides an interface with other public MISs and the automatic verification and/or extraction of data in order to facilitate the filling of forms by its users and the validation of supporting documents, the design of easy-to-use pages that can be accessed by various devices (almost 80% of apprentices applied in 2022 via their phone), the ease of assigning roles to users by other users, etc.
Novelty: This innovation made the apprenticeship bureaucratic requirements less burdensome. It made the process more accessible and efficient.
Impact & Results: The first 3 cycles of Mathiteia (before 2020) were supported by a rudimentary MIS. Since the implementation of the current MIS, the participation and the results of Mathiteia have an increasing trend. The key indicators are the participation of apprentices, businesses, public bodies and VHSs and they have all increased in the last 2 years. These indicators are recorded on an annual basis, and in 2022 they are increased by 40%. Beneficiaries of 2021: 6264, Beneficiaries of 2022: 8751.

Organisation: Ministre de l’Économie, des Finances et de la Souveraineté industrielle et numérique
Description:
The French Ministry of Finance has started a digital skills development program linked to missions, jobs and professional skills. This program, still in its first phase, has enabled civil servants to become more efficient in both individual work and interdepartmental networking thanks to the appropriate use of ICTs and the development of competencies focused on specific lines of work.

This program, starting with a self-diagnosis and continuing with training courses, has enabled the mapping and the development of some professional and digital skills to allow a better allocation of resources. A list of digital hard skills thus corresponds to the standard skills associated with the "standard" job to which the job title is attached. These skills constitute a benchmark on which HR teams can rely during the various career management processes (recruitment, training, internal mobility) to gain objectivity and efficiency, and thus allow for better adaptability of civil servants.

The objectives of this program are in short term the identification of weaknesses and strengths through self-diagnostics, and then, thanks to these results, the creation of calibrated self-training programs.
Novelty: This innovation aims at mapping digital skills in the public sector in their link with professional skills with a self-diagnosis aimed at improving the abilities of public servants.
Impact & Results: Almost 100,000 civil servants have gone through self-diagnoses to find out their level in 16 different digital competencies. Further, the project resulted in the creation of digital ambassador’s communities and organization of digital PAC Num awards along with short, friendly communication events organised in the different premises of the French Ministry.

Description:
The CSET Map of Science is a map of scholarly literature constructed by clustering research publications and organizing research clusters (RCs) based on citation linkages. 

This map can be used for a range of analytic tasks. The publications in the map are drawn from a set of merged scholarly literature constructed from Microsoft Academic Graph, Clarivate Web of Science, the Chinese National Knowledge Infrastructure, Dimensions Digital Science, arXiv, and Papers with Code. The CSET Map corpus covers all years of each of these sources and content more than 260 million scientific documents. The resulting Map provides a visual representation of the landscape of science, with detailed information on more than 123,000 clusters of research including 130 million scientific papers.

CSET’s Map of Science user interface provides 17 filters (e.g. subject, dominant country, growth, percentage of AI-related papers) to search through the Map and find RCs of interest.
Novelty: Scientific and research map clustering research publications and organizing research clusters (RCs) based on citation linkages of more than 260 million documents.
Impact & Results:
- 123,000 clusters in a visual representation of the landscape of science. 
- 11 clusters identified and categorized.

Project/Initiative Name: MaskGoShare for people to crowdsource mask supplies
Organisation: Better.Sg (in partnership with the GovTech agency)
Description:
MaskGoShare:
This Civic-tech tackles the fear surrounding COVID-19 that led people to panic-buz large stockpiles of essential supplies (masks, sanitisers, others.)
Allows people to donate surgical mask to neighbours who do not have them. The system connects people in need and those with a surplus, allowing them to share their post code details and arrange drop offs in their mailboxes. The system autodeletes information after they have arranged a collection.
Novelty:
Web platform to match people's demands of essential needs with those overly supplied or with a surplus. 
For those with a surplus, the platform works on a postal code basis to browse through the requests.
For those with a shortage, they can register their flat information (1-2 bedroom flat) and hope to be connected.
Impact & Results: Not really as relevant/interesting anymore.

Project/Initiative Name: MaskGoWhere + FluGoWhere to inform about free masks collection and nearby clinics/hospitals
Description:
MaskGoWhere:
Web platform that allows Singaporeans to easily find where they can collect the four free masks being provided by the Government. The system was built in 24 hours with >8 million pageviews in its first week. The collection excercise concluded as of 14 June 2020. 

FluGoWhere:
Web platform to help Singaporeans identify Public Health Preparedness Clinics (PHPCs) and polyclinics specially prepared for the treatment of respiratory infections (COVID-19). Similarly, it informs about Combined Test Centres and Joint Testing and Vaccination Centres, as well as Swab and Send Home facilities (CTC, JTVCs, SASH respectively)
Novelty: Both platforms, and in particular the FluGoWhere, provide wholesome information about available public health facilities and private clinics near to the citizens/users. It addresses too FAQ and provides official information about COVID-19 and related topics. Furthermore, it provides the option for Teleconsultation with a doctor through a list of telemedicine providers.
Impact & Results: No longer as relevant, and too similar to efforts covered in 2020 trends report.

Description:
During the COVID-19 pandemic, infection and death rates rose beyond the capacity of state mortuaries and private undertakers. To deal with the surge in deaths, the Western Cape Government approved the construction of a field mortuary, including the development of a software system to process incoming demand through multiple processing stages. This project, the Mass Fatality System, was developed in an innovative way, following the Minimum Viable Product (MVP) Framework. With its emphasis on collaboration and continuous feedback, this approach was successful in keeping all stakeholders actively involved.

Following the MVP Framework: (i) the project team identified stakeholders and immediately engaged with them to get an in-depth understanding of the business problem, (ii) the collaboration and participation of business stakeholders with the development team was established at the early stage so that the project team was able to identify and agree on the scope of the Mass Fatality System project and application, (iii) the project team developed user stories to guide development and used various techniques to re-enact ‘real life scenarios’ of the user stories to ensure accuracy, (iv) screens were developed on the basis of the user stories and demoed to clients who would confirm and approve them,(v) various iterations of development, playback, testing and confirmation followed.
Novelty: The use of MVP Framework is the main novelty of the innovation.
Impact & Results: The MFMS project, because of its success, became the forerunner for using the MVP methodology in the current development efforts. This initiative culminated in the adoption of a new and more effective way of work by the development team – this includes the ability to adapt to change quickly; a thorough understanding of business and related environments; and iterative development process with values and prioritises user feedback. It also led a culture of collaboration between the development team and our clients which improved how we deliver IT services to internal and external stakeholders and enables us to deliver a high quality, relevant and useful solutions for departments and citizens.

Description:
The Citizen Participation Index was developed from the need to assess the territorial conditions, practices and effects of participation. This index measures the quality of participation in Medellín, its communes and corregimientos, benefiting all actors interested in citizen participation issues. Measuring the quality of citizen participation is a little explored issue. In the Colombian case, we are the first entity to carry out this type of measurement.

The CPPI is a composite indicator that gathers information from different variables that account for the quality of citizen participation from a multidimensional approach and whose dimensions allude to territorial conditions, actors and practices, and the effects of participation. Likewise, the CPPI is framed within a territorial approach that shows participation as a situated phenomenon, expressing at the same time the relations between the State and the citizenry in three ways; namely: State - Citizenship; Citizenship - State and Citizenship - Citizenship.

The measurement of the CPPI is under the mantle of Optimal Scaling and the Principal Component Method, statistical techniques that lend rigor to the innovative process. 
Although the MPCI has only been measured for the city of Medellín, it is hoped that in the long term it can be replicated as a good practice in other territories (national and international), which will contribute to the strengthening of democratic exercises around the world.

Given that the CPPI measures the quality of citizen participation and disaggregates the results on a territorial and population basis, the results benefit all those actors interested in participation issues, i.e. academic, institutional and non-institutional actors (such as social organisations and collectives, among others), as well as citizens in general (social leaders, etc.).
Novelty: The Medellín Citizen Participation Index (IPCM) is the only one of its kind in Colombia and in the world. Although citizen participation is widely addressed around the world, there are no exercises and/or projects similar to the MPCI that focus on the quality of citizen participation. In addition to the above, the IPCM assesses the quality of participation from three dimensions: territorial conditions, actors and practices, and effects. The IPCM understands that territorial conditions refer to the guarantees and opportunities for individuals and social organisations/collectives to exercise their right to participate in the different territories. Actors and practices refer to the processes of agency through which actors choose to participate and develop concrete experiences of participation, in specific scenarios and with a repertoire of strategies and resources; expressed in governance and leadership.
Impact & Results: For 2017, 2019 and 2021, the quality of participation in Medellín has been measured by applying more than 10,000 surveys in the last two measurements alone. The CPPI has also been taken into account in the formulation of Medellín's Municipal Development Plan, which, broadly speaking, is the main public management instrument. In addition, the results of the MPCI have been taken into account in the formulation of public policies aimed at improving the quality of participation in the city. In terms of citizenship, Citizen Laboratories and case studies have been carried out to deepen the results and, at the same time, encourage the social appropriation of knowledge. The process has also been awarded as an innovative process by the University of Antioquia (Colombia). It is hoped that the Index will be taken into account in the formulation of new public policies and that it can be recognised and replicated worldwide as an example of good practice.

Project/Initiative Name: Michigan's Courts Livestreams & MiCourt (a virtual courtroom directory)
Description:
The Michigan Supreme Court and the State Court Administrative Office launched two virtual platforms: 1) Court Live Stream, and 2) MiCourt - a virtual courtroom directory in order to keep the justice system open, transparent and available to the citizens. The court has also adapted to remote proceedings, provided training for judges and court staff, and implemented new technology to enhance both security and access. 

Statewide, >1,000 judges and officers are now using Zoom licenses and have assured >1 million hours of online hearings since proceedings were moved online. This is an initiative based in cost-effectiveness and promotes the engagement of the citizens with the judicial proceedings. 

About Courts Livestreams: in-person proceedings are typically held at the Michigan Hall of Justice and can be watched lived from the Courts livestream page or by using Zoom or Youtube. All proceedings are archived on YouTube. The hearings Court site have >30 thousand subscribers. 

About MiCourt: the virtual courtroom directory provides easy-to-find links to watch live and archived local trial court proceedings. The website allows an individual to search by county, or by judge. The directory has been used >80 thousand times.
Novelty:
Implementation of virtual court proceedings and the provision of virtual courtroom directories to build trust on the system.
Capacity building on judges and court staff to go online and use digital proceedings.

Description:
Midjourney is an independent research lab exploring new mediums of thought and expanding the imaginative powers of the people by converging design, human infrastructure, and AI. 

There are two ways to experience the tools: the Midjourney Bot, which people can use to generate images, and the web app at https://www.midjourney.com/, where people can find a gallery of their own work and other users' creations.
Additionally, the users can use the Midjourney Bot on the Discord server (https://discord.gg/midjourney) as well as on any other Discord server where it has been set up. I

Once users start using our tool, they will be able to create a limited number of images based on command or other queries (variations, upscales). These uses are also known as "jobs" or "GPU-minutes".
Novelty: An AI-based algorithm (text-to-image) to expand the imaginative powers of the people by converging design, human infrastructure, and AI.
Impact & Results: Community showcase: https://www.midjourney.com/showcase/

Project/Initiative Name: Milano Partecipa - Milan Citizens Participation Platform
Description:
Milano Partecipa is the digital platform for citizen participation of the Municipality of Milan. All resident citizens or city users, aged sixteen and over, are entitled to participate. 

The Milano Partecipa Platform’s objective is to empower citizens to voice their view on specific matters, allowing policymakers to collect ideas and feedback on policies and involve citizens more in the policy process of public services. it is based on the open source software Decidim and updated regularly by a community of practice.

The participation processes activated on the Milano Partecipa platform include both the institutions regulated by the Participation Act, such as referendums and petitions, as well as consultations, the participatory budget, the collection of observations or proposals - which are experimental initiatives of civic participation.
Novelty: Digital consultative platform used to pilot experimental initiatives of civic participation in the city of Milan.

Description:
Mobile application that allows you to have your digital health record information at your fingertips. It allows you to make medical appointments, view the information in your file, no matter where in the country you were treated, prescription of medicines, diagnoses, allergies, vaccinations, medical images, scheduled surgeries, risk conditions and pension information, available to the entire population, including people with total or partial visual impairment. Doing away with ID cards

The innovative nationwide application solves the problem of the population knowing what information is in their health record, which when it was on paper was almost impossible to see at the national level, allows empowerment of their health information, making them more informed users and better decision-makers about their condition. This was created through co-creation methodologies with citizen participation and was fundamental in the registration of information and risk conditions in pandemics, which made it possible to have real-time information on patients at the national level.
Novelty:
There is only one digital health record that covers the entire population and the entire country, with the facility to obtain and record information in real time, so that the patient has his or her record within reach and also so that the medical professional knows details of the patient's condition when he or she is not at the health facility.
It allows parents or caregivers to have the information of their children or dependents to be able to manage medical appointments from the same application, to also have information of the pension or their salaries, which provides a comprehensive approach from your mobile device, without having to queue, ask for copies of records, present cards, or not knowing their own information also provides the ability to share or use your information when required.
Impact & Results:
The app has had a total of over 5 million downloads, being available on the major Google, APPLE and Huawei shops, with over 6 million COVID questionnaires answered by the population, over 300,000 people have recorded vital signs remotely.
The app has remained the #1 downloaded app in the health and wellness category on both Google and Apple.
In the future, it is expected to have a greater number of users recording information and using it to a greater degree on a daily basis.

Organisation: National Council for the Evaluation of Social Development Policy (CONEVAL)
Description:
The instrument consists of the creation and implementation of an innovative proposal, the result of an exhaustive theoretical and empirical review on the subject. InMonitoring expresses its results numerically on a scale of 0 to 1, and additionally, for a better understanding, the results are classified in a category that considers four levels of monitoring: Adequate, Medium, Basic and Insufficient. This allows you to easily assess, compare and follow up your results.

The InMonitoring is comprised of two sub-indices: 1) Monitoring of Agencies and Entities; and 2) Monitoring of Social Programmes and Actions, which are fed by the seven dimensions that make up the SM: 1) Strategic Alignment; 2) Focus on Results; 3) Quality; 4) Sustainability; 5) Use; 6) Institutional Soundness; and 7) Fulfilment of Goals.

The main objective of InMonitoring is to improve social development policy and, consequently, social conditions in Mexico by providing the agencies and entities coordinating social programmes with objective and truthful monitoring information that allows them to make evidence-based decisions.

In turn, the index provides the National Council for the Evaluation of Social Development Policy with sufficient information to issue suggestions and recommendations for the improvement of social programme planning instruments in order to contribute to the consolidation and development of the SM.
Novelty:
InMonitoring is the first quantitative tool that provides an overview of the monitoring system in Mexico, for each of the agencies, as well as for the social programmes and actions they coordinate. It provides detailed information on each of the dimensions of analysis, which makes it possible to carry out specific analyses of strengths or areas for improvement.

In addition, the index allows comparing the results of the different dimensions studied among the agencies or programmes, which allows each analysed entity to know its current situation and have a point of reference. It also makes it possible to know the trajectory over time and to monitor its evolution.
Impact & Results:
The results of InMonitoring focus on the assessment of the Ministry of Finance in Mexico in order to drive an agenda for improving social policy based on the observed results. The results are not entirely the responsibility of the index, but of a joint effort as part of the Federal Government's Performance Evaluation System.

The follow-up of these efforts has resulted in identifying improvement in various aspects of social policy. Overall, it was noted that the 2018 change of government had a negative impact by changing some of the previous structures, however, the 2022 results indicate a gradual improvement in the elements assessed.

Description: MuktoPaath is the largest unique e-Learning platform for professional and skills development over multiple sectors in Bangladesh. It allows people to learn from online, offline or blended courses from experts, policy makers or academicians without any language barrier and also at the lowest affordable costs. The Government of Bangladesh launched MuktoPaath in 2016 with an aim to provide quality education and an opportunity of decent employment to the underprivileged community.
Impact & Results: An internal study has been done on MuktoPaath’s existing free online courses which are developed for teachers to understand its impact and incorporate the results for further development of the platform's newer versions. The result shows it saves 73.1% time; 86.3% cost & 100% visit. MuktoPaath had also offered an online course for migrant workers in a blended approach and minimized 30 days physical training to 12 days which saved TCV effectively. It is noted before the introduction of this platform, the average time for a visit to a district office took roughly 9 hours from a rural/remote area, which has been considered as the baseline.

Project/Initiative Name: Multimedia Talking book and Accessible Reading Materials for the students with Visual and Print Disabilities
Description:
a2i Program has facilitated a project named ‘Multimedia Talking Book’ specially designed for visually impaired students that converted all textbooks (grades I-X) into DAISY format (Digital Accessible Information System). National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB) partnered with a2i to ensure the revised multimedia talking books and other textbooks within 1st January every year in the hands of visually challenged children. 

In Bangladesh, the manual production process of Braille book isn’t efficient in terms of time, cost and volume of production. Hence, innovation of DAISY full text-audio multi-media books, accessible e-books and digital Braille books ensured accessibility of the students with visual disability, print disability and learning disability of class I-X. This has enabled disability inclusive publication and reduced the production cost by 70%. It’s free and available online for anyone in the world.
Novelty: Conversion on textbooks in DAISY format to support the education of students who are blind or have a print disability.
Impact & Results: Allowed many students to access education books thanks to DAISY format. Reduced production costs thanks to the use of digital tools.

Project/Initiative Name: Multivariate epidemiological surveillance tool based on municipal wastewater
Organisation: Regional Development Fund of Central Macedonia in collaboration with Aristotle University of Thessaloniki
Description:
The innovation concerns the design, development and implementation of a tool to assess the epidemiological status of a population for COVID disease with a focus on the concentration of viral load in urban wastewater. With the development of the innovation, internationally referred to as wastewater epidemiology, the epidemiological surveillance of a disease is decoupled from the medical surveillance of cases, while the environmental health of a population as a whole is monitored in a valid way.

Historical data on the quality characteristics of the wastewater at the entrance of the Thessaloniki Wastewater Treatment Plant, current corresponding data, measurements of the concentration of SARS CoV-2 viral load and its variants in wastewater and clinical surveillance data were used for its development. In the future, the infrastructure can be used to monitor the emergence of diseases with epidemic characteristics, the spatial distribution of viral load, environmental health and the assessment of the impact of climate change on it.
Novelty: The present innovation focuses on measuring the concentration of SARS CoV-2 viral load in wastewater samples by simple and rapid methods. As such, it supports clinical surveillance with a method that is cost-effective, simple, rapid and representative of the whole population.
Impact & Results:
Currently, the infrastructure has been developed for the city of Thessaloniki as regards wastewater analysis, results evaluation and dissemination. The results were reported and evaluated by the National Public Health Agency in the pandemic as a complementary means of the epidemiological picture of the city. It also functioned as such a tool for citizens, who showed particular interest, according to the readership of the articles in the daily press and media. 

The early diagnosis of the emergence of the delta mutation mobilised citizens in principle and helped to inform them to take protective measures. The same was the case for the receding and exacerbation phase of the Omicron wave.

Organisation: The State Agency for Public Service and Social Innovations under the President  of the Republic of Azerbaijan
Description:
The E-Gov Development Center has developed the MyGov portal (e-government portal) in order to facilitate the work of citizens and businesses, and provide transparency as well as efficiency through the presented electronic services. The portal functions based on the predictive government concept, thereby ensuring the transition from reactive services to proactive ones.

Functions of the portal:
1. Obtaining personal information by the citizens: citizens can access their personal, financial, educational, and other information.
2. Reminder notifications: In case a citizen's information is renewed or the validity period of the citizens' documents is expired, she receives reminder notifications via the personal cabinet.
3. Transmission of data to other institutions: citizen can upload his/her information in the form of a reference and pass it to other agencies.
4. Access of citizens to appeal to institutions: citizen can send his/her views, comments, complaints, and suggestions to the relevant body. At the same time, the citizen can appeal to the relevant entity about the wrong information she/he has discovered about herself/himself on the system.
5. The use of electronic services provided by government agencies: citizen can access the electronic services provided by government agencies by logging in to the personal account.

It is important to emphasize that the project was formulated on the basis of international experience. Especially, the best practices of the United Kingdom, The United States and Australia were utilized for developing the portal. The tools used to implement the project are specified below:
- Database servers - MS SQL, MONGODB, REDİS;
- Programming languages and platforms: Java (Spring Framework), Golang, JavaScript (Angular), Python
- Message queues : RabbitMQ
- ApiGateway: Traefik
As further steps to develop the project, it is planned to scale up and turn MyGov into a superportal by integrating the functions of the e-Government and Registry of Public Services (RPS) portals in the MyGov portal.
Novelty: MyGov is innovative because it is based on personal cabinet, it is in line with a predictive government approach, the portal is available not only for state agencies but also for private companies, transmission of data to any institution can be regulated by the citizen, and citizens can track a complete chronology of their activity of the Portal.
Impact & Results:
The results of the project:
● The number of users registered on the portal - 995 785
● Total number of portal uses - 52 471 711
● Total number of offered services - 710
● Types of notification services - 18

The Monitoring and Evaluation team employed following techniques to measure the results and impact of the project:
● Evaluation Surveys related to the satisfaction level and feedback of the users
● Statistics related to the project results
Moreover, it is anticipated that the registration and usage rates on the portal will encompass at least 70% of citizens by the end of 2023.

Organisation: Center of Policy Research and Governance
Description:
Center of Policy Research and Governance (CPRG), a civil society organization, has come up with the booklet "National Education Policy-2020 and Marginals" which paves the way for ensuring the inclusion of the Marginals in the mainstream education. The publication concocts a new way to understand policies from the perspective of the marginals. CPRG aims at empowering the marginal sections of the society by creating awareness about the provisions of NEP 2020 and providing them the information necessary for enhancing their accessibility to Education.

The booklet aims to make the society equitable by serving as a catalyst for the educational inclusion of the marginal sections of the society and ensuring social justice. Developing it, CPRG has worked towards the enhancement of government-citizen interface by making the marginalized sections of the society aware about the provisions of the National Education Policy paving the way for inclusivity, responsiveness and efficiency in governance processes. It not only helps in the promotion of participation of all the stakeholders in the process of policy implementation by keeping them well informed but also aids towards making the governance process reformative. It increases accountability of the government towards the citizens and gives shape to the concept of welfare democracy.
Novelty:
The innovation of the project lies in the fact that it serves as an instrument in understanding policy making from the perspective of the marginal sections of the society so as to lead a paradigm shift in the way policies are made. This project addresses the lack of awareness about policy initiatives at the grassroots level, which is considered a major cause of the difficulty in reaching marginalised segments of the population.
The booklet ‘National Education Policy-2020 and Marginals would ensure that the deprived sections of the society can utilize the provisions of NEP 2020 for their own advantage leading to the development of the deprived sections of the society in particular and the country in general.
Impact & Results: The innovation is creating a significant impact on the people of the country by making them understand the policies made by the government of India. The document is in the process of reaching every nook and corner of the country and aiding the policy makers to work towards making people centric policies so as to achieve the objective of promoting inclusive and welfare-oriented development in the country. The booklet has created awareness about the programmes that lead to better inclusion of SCs (Scheduled Castes), STs (Scheduled Tribes), OBCs (Other Backward Classes) and other marginal communities in the mainstream society which has made the government functionaries more conscious in following the stakeholder approach while formulating policies.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
National Internship Program, conducted by the Human Resources Office of the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye in cooperation with all ministries and their subsidiaries, and voluntary employers from the private sector, has been prepared for university students. It supports equal opportunity for all university students in accessing career openings with transparent, merit-based, traceable, and innovative evaluation methods.

All processes of NIP are carried out on a digital platform called Career Gate (CG) and each year a posting is published on CG enabling all students to apply for internship opportunities in public institutions through a single platform. The application process has a two-step structure. In the first step, students can easily transfer their information (contact information, university entrance exam scores, GPA, major, etc.) to CG. Secondly, they upload documents proving their competencies (sports/artistic pursuits, project competitions, etc.), which are important for employers to select interns. The accuracy of the uploaded documents is checked by university career centers and related authorized public institutions. Students’ competencies are scored by using the above-mentioned information, with an innovative and scientific method. This makes it possible to evaluate students’ long-term performance instead of using exams showing instant performance. Thus, it is ensured that all students are subject to fair, standard, and objective evaluation.

Students’ profiles, including Competency Scores, are added to a Candidate Pool which will be accessed by employers. To ensure merit-based recruitment and prevent gender bias; the identity (name, contact information, etc.) and gender information are specifically hidden from the profiles. Employers can select their candidates and send offers by filtering the Pool according to major, grade, foreign language proficiency, etc., and by viewing the competency scores and the achievements/activities that contributed to the scores.
Novelty:
NIP is unique in terms of inclusiveness, as it mobilizes internship opportunities of all institutions for all university students in the country. To this end, applications are standardized, made accessible through a single platform, and recruitment standards are made more transparent & traceable.

The evaluation method is also innovative as students’ Competency Scores are calculated and applicants are evaluated by their life-long performance instead of exams showing instant performance. Employers send their offers based on these scores without seeing the identity and gender of the students. Thus, gender biases are eliminated, and merit-based recruitment is ensured. NIP reverses a deep-rooted perception that “students are chosen by employers” to “students choose the employer.
Impact & Results:
In 2020, 15,868 students applied, and 6,481 of them completed their internships. In 2021, all public institutions participated in NIP and 44,930 students of the total 135,953 applicants completed internship. NIP with its innovative approach drew attention international organizations, and it has been reported as an exemplary practice in OECD's Governance for Youth, Trust and Intergenerational Justice Report (2020) as well as a policy note (2021) titled “What Did Countries Do to Support Youth in the COVID-19 Crisis?”.

This year, more than 289,000 students applied to NIP, and by September 2022, more than 110,000 students have started their internship.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Turkey Human Resources Office
Description:
The National Talent Fairs is a series of career fairs organized by the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office to make all young people recognize that they are talents, regardless of their region or university. It aims to support talents in all regions to have access to equal employment opportunities and meet face-to-face with HR professionals of public and private organizations which guide them in their career path and also raise career awareness. Moreover, The Project supports UCCs to have a network of employers, and increases their capacity to organize career events, thus helps them deliver equal career services.

As the central coordination entity, the Office groups universities based on their regions and assign University Career Centers to carry out the organizational processes of their region’s fair. In total, National Talent Fairs were conducted in 16 different cities, 371.746 undergraduates/graduates and 5.431 employers attended, and almost 2.000 career events were organized by participant companies. The project is unique in terms of being organized regionally and encouraging numerous universities all over the country to work in collaboration on a digital platform.
Novelty: The innovation provides a way to support university students' careers with a focus on equality of opportunity.
Impact & Results: The Fairs were conducted in 16 cities and 138 universities cooperated to perform within the standards set by the Office. 371.746 undergraduates/graduates gathered with 5.431 employers.

Description:
The initiative (is already terminated) sought to connect developers with medical professionals or people working to prevent or cure the COVID-19 virus. The digital support of our volunteers was continued on local levels and more individualized to the local needs and the network of our local chapters (the OK Labs). 

Now the OK Lab Berlin is a sub group of about 30 people that work in different groups on projects around Open Data and Civic Tech. They come from very different backgrounds, so the projects they work on deal with a wide range of topics.

Project/Initiative Name: Net Zero - From Pollution to Solution
Description:
(not qualified - only an idea)

The goal of this innovation is to design, install and commission a prototype Carbon Capture plant for the purpose of capturing CO2 that is being emitted from an anaerobic digestor, similar to that of a Sewage Treatment Plant. This project aims to achieve the “Zero Carbon Emissions”. This innovation is intended for implementation by Sewage Treatment Plants and by minimizing or elimination the emissions of CO2, it positively contributes to protecting Ambient Air Quality, and Public Health.

OONI Explorer is an open data resource on internet censorship around the world. Specifically, it sheds light on internet censorship and other forms of network interference worldwide by collecting data about networks in more than 200 countries and across >25 thousand networks. OONI is present since 2012, and in 2022 has launched the Measurement Aggregation Toolkit (MAT) to enable users and researchers to create customized charts based on aggregate views of real-time OONI data collected from around the world. 

The MAT provides a platform and several filters that enables the user to select the parameters they care about in order to plot charts based on aggregate views of OONI data. The platform can be accessed both via mobile or browser: https://ooni.org/install/
Novelty: Free, publicly available and open source measurement tools to shed light on internet censorship and other forms of network interference from governments around the world.
Impact & Results: Impacts can be seen here by scrolling down to display information across several countries: https://explorer.ooni.org/

Description:
"Code for Japan" is a general incorporated association that uses information technology to solve local issues with local governments and communities nationwide, collaborates with civic tech organizations and private services in various places. They started the project "OPEN EATS JAPAN" to open up restaurant information and share it widely with society.

In response to the situation in which many restaurants were forced to close to prevent the spread of the new COVID-19 infection, there are many projects to collect and publish information on restaurants that can take out and deliver to support restaurants in various places. To prevent such efforts from being a temporary one and to continue to cooperate with the region in disseminating restaurant information, the restaurant data collected in each region can be used widely in various applications and services. It created and published a standard format for restaurant as typification, menu, geo localization, among others.
Novelty: The open source codes and the platform gathers information about restaurants in Japan so that it can be used by developers to support local restaurants during the pandemic.

Description:
Open Terms Archive is free and open source software aiming at becoming a digital commons. This means its source code can be freely reused and built upon, as long as improvements are made available to the community under the same conditions. This also means that decisions on the evolution of the product will be taken in an increasingly democratic manner as the community that builds it will grow.

The Open Terms Archive community builds tools aiming to shift the power balance against big tech. For this reason, it records every change to the terms of digital services so that the community analyses these changes to try shifting the power balance. The process consists of four steps: 

1. Identification: The contractual documents of an online service are listed for tracking by volunteer contributors.
2. Archival: Several times a day, our robots download and publicly archive the listed documents.
3. Detection: When changes are spotted, they are recorded and exposed.
4. Publishing: All recorded versions are published in easy-to-analyse datasets.
Novelty: A free and open source software aiming at becoming a digital commons so that source codes can be freely reused and built upon.
Impact & Results:
Case studies with results are here: https://opentermsarchive.org/en/case-studies 
900+ documents classified along a taxonomy of over fifty types
200+ analysis published in the form of short stories and tweets
400+ services
spread over industries, languages and jurisdictions

Project/Initiative Name: Optimization and innovation of the harassment SOS helpline through a user-oriented approach
Organisation: Republic Agency for Peaceful Settlement of Labour Disputes
Description: The Republic Agency for Peaceful Settlement of Labor Disputes implemented a harassment SOS helpline which allows our citizens to initiate procedures promptly, simply and free of charge and get answers to questions about harassment. At the same time, conciliators and arbitrators of the Agency are able to handle cases more quickly and generate comparative reports and analyses.
Novelty: The Agency procedure base is unique and customized information system developed specially to meet the needs of employees, conciliators, arbitrators and beneficiaries of Agency services, the use of which is completely free. It was designed after comprehensive analysis of needs of all users. It integrates the whole procedure of processing a case where all parties in dispute can monitor the progress in real time even on a mobile phone. It is the only information system of this type developed in the region. Using modern technologies provided on our SOS harassment helpline citizens can get pertinent information on how to get protection from abuse at work round the clock, 24/7, completely anonymously and for free.
Impact & Results: The procedure base use and SOS harassment line resulted in increases number of cases brought before the Agency in comparison with the period before its implementation. Now, the average number of procedures is about 1000 per year compared to less than 500 per year before procedure base was implemented. At the same time 6979 citizens received information about how to protect themselves from abuse at work. Consequently, this has led to significant savings for citizens and businesses since procedures before the Agency are completely free of charge. The implementing Agency is today the only institution of this type in the region with developed information system for tracking and monitoring the procedure progress.

Description:
The Outcomes Graph is a collaborative, hyperlinked knowledge base that logs market and scientific research findings and points to the optimum path toward applying science to societal outcomes. Using the "discourse graph extension", the platform can write and synthesise knowledge as naturally as possible so the system recognizes the important nodes and relationships. Furthermore, the system helps making structured claims and arguments about the possibilities of applied science based on knowledge that is optimal for specific societal outcomes. 

The new language enables the users to quickly get to first principles and communicate across domains in a rapid, programmatic way. The logic of the outcomes graph is: 
1. Characterise outcomes with precision and granularity: Unlike technology roadmaps or technology trees, the units of information in the protocol are Outcomes. Outcomes are positive statements of capability or achievement, usually suggesting a motivating factor we want to occur in the future. Also they set dynamic logical operations (and/or) between outcomes. 
2. Have evidenced discussions: Throughout the process, they  want to state all of the relevant pieces of knowledge that inform their arguments i.e. how ‘strong’ is this knowledge or its relation to the constraint?
3. Identify optimal paths to achieving high-impact ventures: The logic, scoping language and its nested structure prevents everyone from going around in circles or getting stuck at one level. They can also detect areas of neglect. For example, a group of outcomes may be more fertile to explore if fewer companies sit below them. 
4. Discover opportunities for combinatorial innovation: Lastly the method captures not only outcomes but also knowledge entities which effectively makes the Outcomes graph a Wikipedia for invention. 

The method approach has seven steps until a company is successfully launched: 1) identify sector level areas, 2) partner with value-add industry leaders, 3) identify opportunity areas, 4) find the right founder, 5) deconstruct the opportunity area, 6) iteratively build out the team, 7) pre-incorporation.
Novelty: A collaborative, hyperlinked knowledge base that logs market and scientific research findings and points to the optimum path to solve complex social issues.
Impact & Results:
90% of founders form a company within 12 months
35 companies successfully built 
18 months on average to TRL4
List of companies: https://deepscienceventures.com/portfolio

Project/Initiative Name: Participatory regulation reform process - Public Toktok, Regulatory Talk
Organisation: ministry of the interior and safety
Description:
Whenever a new government is introduced, regulatory reform takes place. However, it does not go through sufficient discussions between the people and stakeholders, so awareness of regulatory reform cannot be high. Our project - Public Toktok, Regulatory Talk - strengthens public participation and increases the sense of regulatory reform through several public discussions. Through this, the regulation problem are resolved. In particular, through a long process of public debate, better regulatory alternatives are created.

The Public Toktok, Regulatory Talk provides a place for people to participate in regulatory innovation. The general public can directly identify unnecessary and unreasonable regulatory innovations experienced in daily life that are no longer useful. Experts from various fields can also participate. The Ministry of the Interior and Safety decides whether to accept the proposal in consultation with the relevant ministries. Agenda evaluated as useful suggestion is awarded and promoted. High-quality proposals are selected by comprehensively reviewing creativity (30%), feasibility (30%), and effectiveness (40%).
Novelty: This innovation provides a new way to involve citizens in policymaking with a novel focus on policies that are in place and no longer necessary.
Impact & Results:
The results and effects of PTRT are as follows:
- First, as the people directly participate in the regulation improvement process, interest in regulatory innovation and policy understanding are promoted. 
- Second, it increases the public's acceptance and efficacy of the government's regulatory innovation. 
- Third, new regulatory innovation measures can be created. 

PTRT has been held annually since 2016, and reviews thousands of proposals every year.

Project/Initiative Name: PeduliLindungi to Accelerate COVID-19 Pandemic Handling in Indonesia
Organisation: Center for Data and Information Technology - Digital Transformation Office, Ministry of Health of the Republic of Indonesia
Description:
Indonesia has a population of more than 270 million. This context urged the government to respond COVID-19 pandemic promptly to mitigate the spread of the virus in the community. PeduliLindungi was created as a national platform to accelerate pandemic handling through testing, tracing, and treatment (3T) efforts for public use. With its 3T features, PeduliLindungi helps Indonesians continue engaging in many socio-economic activities while adhering to the mandated health protocols.

The objective are:
1) To inform about the latest COVID-19 situation and regulation, vaccine location, and availability of hospital beds.
2) To digitise COVID-19 vaccine certificate and/or Antigen/PCR test results as a “digital passport” for travel requirements.
3) To trace the potential spread of the virus and ensure the safe entry in public spaces.
4) To provide teledoctor and medication for COVID-19 patients.

Project/Initiative Name: People’s Idea Box: Online platform that links people’s idea to government policies
Description:
The purpose of the platform is to harmonize government policies with people’s opinions. So far, it has been often the case that the government implemented policies and required citizens to follow them. However, such a government-centric policy implementation cannot realize a real democracy. And citizen participation is critical in policy-making procedure. Based on this understanding, we built this online platform. The Korean government and administrative agencies will continue to gather citizens opinions on the platform and advance public policies.

- When a citizen presents an idea for improvement related to public policies on the online platform, People’s Idea Box  (idea. epeople.go.kr), other citizens and experts develop the idea, and then the government use it for policy improvement.
- Administrative agencies, before implementing a policy, use the platform to ask the public for their opinions on the policy, and the collected public opinions are used in implementing better policies.
Novelty: The online platform is an open space where a single idea could be discussed by many people, with each adding his or her opinion and developing the idea. We built the platform because we believed that when many people think hard and discuss a single topic together, better alternatives could come out. The government and administrative agencies, for their part, could bring such public opinions together to come up with the best solution.
Impact & Results:
In 2021, a total of 7,700 ideas were suggested on the platform. Some of them were posted by citizens and others by administrative agencies. On the ideas, a total of 445,000 people added comments, expressing their opinions, mostly in the form of replies. Answering surveys that ask opinions of many people is also a way to present opinions. The idea that received the largest number of participations last year was “Install cameras in operating rooms.” A total of 13,000 people answered the survey, with 97.9% supporting the installment of CCTVs in operating rooms. In response, the National Assembly passed a bill that mandates surveillance cameras in operating rooms.

Last year, administrative agencies gathered public opinions on nineteen social issues on the platform. Among them were on government support for single-parent households, enhancement of COVID 19 quarantine measures, and improvement of the juvenile justice system. 

Such public opinion-gathering activities raised public trust in the government. As a result, in the OECD Trust Survey published every two years, South Korea in 20201 ranked 20th with 45% of people saying they trust in the government, which was a significant increase in 2017, with 32nd and 24%.

Description:
The platform displays the number of the amount, in parts-per-million, of CO2 in the planet's atmosphere. The number is taken live from The Keeling Curve, a graph of CO2 accumulation based on continuous measurements. 

Our planet has oceanic and terrestrial forests, both of which absorb gigatons of CO2 emitted by our civilisation each year. However, most of the world's terrestrial forests are in the Northern Hemisphere, and so there is a seasonal variation of about 5ppmv (parts per million variance) that represents plant life above the equator taking in as much CO2 as it can. During cold months in the North the number rises again, as there is less canopy to absorb it. In a sense, that up-and-down represents our planet's seasonal breathing.

This '2 steps forward 1 back' upward march is direct proof that we're emitting more CO2 than can be absorbed - too much - and so the rest is left to our atmosphere, warming the planet in turn. Were we doing things right, there would be a gentle tide, up and down, throughout the year; there wouldn't be a 'curve' at all.
Novelty: Easy way of displaying the risks of the climate crisis when it comes to the greenhouse effect and the issue of global warming.
Impact & Results: No publicly available information about impacts.

Description:
PetaBencana saw light in 2017 and was rebooted in 2020 prior a 5.0 magnitude earthquake that hit the west of Java. PetaBencana or Disaster Bot is an AI-based bot to report climate-related hazards or disasters near emergency areas by replying with #flood. The aim of the program is to crowdsource real-time maps of emerging and emergency disasters from social media users. 

The bot is able to turn social media chatter into a ground-level live view of emerging catastrophes, cutting through the chaotic flow of information that follows a natural disaster. Whereas Indonesian emergency management agencies used to send information in static PDF documents at six-hour intervals, PetaBencana is able to show in real time where help is needed, where roads are still passable, where hospitals are still operating, and where they have been knocked out. 

Data from PetaBencana.id is consumed by the National Emergency Management Agency’s (BNPB) InAWARE Disaster Monitoring platform to support decision making. BPBD DKI’s application Pantau Banjir also integrates real-time flood information from PetaBencana.id to push flood updates to residents. Residents of Indonesia can submit real-time flood reports by tweeting #banjir @petabencana, sending a Facebook message to @petabencana.id, or sending a Telegram message to @bencanabot.
Novelty: First (and biggest) bot in Indonesia to report climate-related hazards or disasters near emergency areas from social media users.
Impact & Results: PetaBencana’s success in Indonesia, where it is now used by government agencies, NGOs, and ordinary citizens, has led to it being adopted in the Philippines, Hong Kong and Vietnam

Description:
The Australian Electoral Commission provides electoral information in languages other than English to help people from non-English speaking backgrounds understand enrolling and voting in Australia.

Election information has been translated into 33 languages, this includes Your official guide to the 2022 federal election, and fact sheets on How to make your vote count and Checking the source of electoral communications.
These guides explain how to enrol to vote and how to vote at a federal election:
- An interactive tool to practise how to complete the House of Representatives and Senate ballot papers, with instructions in various languages.
- Voting during COVID-19
- A fact sheet that explains how to vote if you have COVID-19.
Novelty: Translation of key electoral processes and information to more than 10 languages.
Impact & Results: No impacts available

Organisation: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW)
Description:
The Pradhan Mantri National Dialysis Programme (PMNDP) launched on 05th May 2022 envisages the provision of providing dialysis services under the NHM to ensure the delivery of high-quality, cost-effective services and develop clinically safe and effective program. While the efforts would continue towards prevention and management of NCDs in initial stages, providing access to dialysis care to treat End Stage Renal Disease is the key priority.

The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Govt. of India has prepared an IT platform namely, https://pmndp.nhp.gov.in for PMNDP application which aims to provide smooth delivery of necessary dialysis services to every patient having unique 14-digit ABHA number (Health ID) based on Aadhaar Id of beneficiaries. Further, the key KPI indicators are monitored through PMNDP Dashboard for displaying bird eye view of program.

The objective of PMNDP portal is to integrate all dialysis facilities in a common IT platform to improve visibility & for capturing the details of all beneficiaries. Training is imparted to all dialysis facilities in-charge and the State Nodal Officers involved in providing dialysis services in their respective States / UTs including Service Providers through Public Private Partnership.
Novelty: PMNDP Portal was launched for provision of free and discounted good quality renal care services to all beneficiaries through all health facilities of the country in concurrence with National Health Policy 2017 which necessitated adoption of digital integration to create a National Digital Health Ecosystem to support 'Universal Health Coverage' by effectively tracking, reporting, monitoring, and promoting quality of health care at all levels in the country.
Impact & Results: PMNDP covers 1126 Centres, procured 7721 Haemodialysis Machines in 36 States/UTs & in 604 Districts. 14.37 Lakh patients availed Dialysis Services with a total of 152.20 Lakhs sessions held.

Description:
The Pre-bidding module enables the city of Mexico better communications between Purchasing Units and citizens, suppliers, and civil society groups interested in providing feedback to draft bidding documents. As such, it contributes to strengthening the technical, administrative, and financial elements of public procurement processes. The tool integrates functionalities that allow free, logical interactions and order between the different actors.

By disclosing the draft bidding documents to the public for comments, the Purchasing Units can obtain more information on market behavior, new technologies, ideas of efficiency and effectiveness that allow the acquisition of goods or the provision of services with better results, in addition to strengthening citizen participation, monitoring of high impact projects and transparency.
Novelty: Its uniqueness comes as it is the first product of its kind and it allows government bid redactors to improve and make better comprehensive bids that allow more suppliers to participate.
Impact & Results: The pre-bidding procurement module has been successfully implemented in the city of Mexico where it has been a success in helping agencies draft better bids. To date, the pre-bidding procurement module has thousands of comments and suggestions that have been implemented in more than 10 government bids

Description:
Qr Code attendance system, where students receive a unique code upon registration and when they arrive at school they can be scanned by any authorized person at the gate at that first moment. This is shared with the teachers, a checklist of absentees to be confirmed in class, speeding up the time spent on calls by 66%.

Problem solved: Losing teacher time in the classroom to perform administrative activity as is the case of attendance and impacts on a weak class and that the student can not get the necessary attention for the learning process.

What is in fact the innovation: decrease the time spent on making calls.
Objectives or goals of the innovation: implementation of the innovation with the control of students' entry and exit.

Who benefits from the innovation: any educational institution that needs to revamp its administrative process.

How is the innovation envisioned for the future? Connect the whole school community, both coordinators, guardianship councils, students' feeding can benefit from the insights generated in our platform
Novelty: Very low investment cost when compared to other technologies, the possibility to work offline and be available in more remote schools throughout Brazil, besides the easy adaptability to other technologies creating new directions for national education.

Project/Initiative Name: Prioritised Patient Care Reporting System for pre-hospital and Primary Health Care - SIREP
Organisation: Servicio Servicio Salud Viña del Mar - Quillota Mobile Emergency Care Service
Description:
1.	It is an automated information flow platform.
2.	It optimises the time taken to report cases captured in a call centre for medical emergencies and the delivery of information in real time to the competent levels for resolution and follow-up.
3.	Allows each health centre to be notified of relevant information on the solution needs that have been detected in their patients.

There is a high percentage of calls that do not correspond to medical emergencies but to primary health care. This innovation was developed to optimise the time it takes to register and send this information to the referrers (primary health centres, hospitals and specific areas of the health service). It tries to link a medical emergency level with primary care levels by connecting the patient and his or her primary care centre, after having detected a specific health continuity need.

This strategy is innovative since it detects a patient population with a high hidden demand that, by applying this tool, unifies, centralises and distributes the information to the corresponding levels.

The Prioritised Reporting System (SIREP) is a management tool, translated into an integrated platform of specific surveys related to the patient's health problem that are configured to be sent in real time to the email server of each person in charge of the different programmes of the primary health centres to which the patient belongs. The objectives of SIREP are to link the patient with his or her primary health care centre, provide continuity of care for the patient and his or her pathology, optimise human resources, and obtain traceability and statistics.
Novelty: SIREP is a unique system nationwide for screening, patient referral, follow-up and traceability.
Impact & Results:
The results of the project are mainly qualitative, because its implementation has generated; linkage between services and patients, reduction of patients with poly-consultations, definitive solutions to socio-health problems by primary care teams. Increased response from health to the community. The statistics are based on the reasons for the call, which allows us to profile the health needs. 
The impact we hope to achieve is that SIREP will become a national tool for managing the flow of continuity of care for patients, and that it will be used by hospitals and primary care centres.
In one year, more than 11,000 patients have been managed through SIREP. It facilitates inter-sectoral information and maintains production statistics in real time. More than 80 older adults have been rescued from poor socio-sanitary conditions. 80% of the patients' continuity of care is reported by other levels of care.

Description:
The Prison Management Program improves the operation of justice, for one must consider the current reality of the Brazilian penitentiary system where a large portion of the inmates is idle, without an occupation, and a high rate of illiteracy. In addition to expanding the potential for fundraising and the performance of Civil Society Organizations - CSOs related to the prison system and points evidence to subsidize the performance of the public power in the implementation of public policies.
