The Program arose in the change of direction of the penal establishment and together with the Public Ministry of Lagoa Vermelha where it was detected that the prison presented at the time, one of the worst rates of the penitentiary system gaucho, which was one of the highest rates of overcrowding of the state in the proportion of 230% above, as other problems, being: not having a UBS, a pavilion of work, technical-vocational school and the high rate of idle prisoners. It was known that the establishment receives prisoners from 03 (three) districts, being: Lagoa Vermelha, Sananduva and São José do Ouro. 

Totaling 15 municipalities. Understanding the co-responsibility of these municipalities, a project was started to establish the needs to be solved with the prison. Therefore, a management program was elaborated with the mayors, which aimed to apply the fomentation in the amount of R$2.000.000,00 (Two million reais), divided among the municipalities, and the division to be as fair as possible and was by the population of each municipality, according to IBGE and the division of the total amount was between 3%,6%,8% and 10,5%.
Novelty:
The great innovation of the practice is that this revitalization and restructuring of this penal establishment is done through the encouragement of municipalities that send convicts there, which previously did not have any resources. Besides having the possibility to destine to these municipalities apenados via Joint Action Protocol - PAC, to perform activities with them, in the most diverse areas, such as: tree pruning, landscaping, asphalt reurbanization, among others. 

Another major success factor of the practice was to establish a bond of trust for the implementation of the program and mainly and not more important to bring the eyes of society to a segment so unassisted by almost all sectors of society.
Impact & Results: The impact that the practice generated was in particular the regional mobilization around the theme of the penitentiary system. It brings as impact the possibility of building new horizons for people who are deprived of freedom.

Description:
Project CodeNet is a large-scale dataset with approximately 14 million code samples, each of which is an intended solution to one of 4000 coding problems. It contains samples designed to train AI to complete a range of programming tasks, including code search and clone detection.

Using CodeNet, data scientists can execute code samples to extract additional metadata and verify outputs from generative AI models for correctness. This is an AI-driven code completion and auditing platform. 

The code samples are written in over 50 programming languages (although the dominant languages are C++, C, Python, and Java) and they are annotated with a rich set of information, such as its code size, memory footprint, cpu run time, and status, which indicates acceptance or error types. The dataset is accompanied by a repository, where we provide a set of tools to aggregate codes samples based on user criteria and to transform code samples into token sequences, simplified parse trees and other code graphs.

The rich annotation of Project CodeNet enables research in code search, code completion, code-code translation, and a myriad of other use cases. IBM has also extracted several language specific datasets for benchmarking in Python, Java and C++ to drive innovation in deep learning and machine learning models in code classification and code similarity.
Novelty: Data scientists can execute code samples to extract additional metadata and verify outputs from generative AI models for correctness. This is an AI-driven code completion and auditing platform.
Impact & Results: Largest dataset of code samples >14 million which intend to solve >4000 coding problems across industries and sectors.

Organisation: Attorney General of the State of Bahia - PGE/BA
Description:
The Project Paid for What created a work routine that studies the causes and the cost of the lawsuits in which the State of Bahia was defeated, generating recommendations, which will be published annually through a report and bulletin. The initiative aims to combat the growth in judicialization, which was 58% in the last three years, and the public debt with precatory, which increased 110% between 2017 and 2020, and is now accumulated at 4.8 billion reais. This is a problem that affects all entities.

A two-stage analysis methodology was created. The first one was called Quantitative Analysis and analyzes the data using the Business Intelligence Panel. The second was called Qualitative Analysis and, based on a Protocol, conducts the study of the object of the conviction that generated the largest block of convictions for the State of Bahia, with a view to understanding the litigation and extracting possible learning for the preparation of recommendations aimed at preventing litigation.

This taxonomy was linked to the table of issues developed by the National Council of Justice (CNJ), so that currently one can categorize the information in the Business Intelligence Panel, both using the own taxonomy developed by the Committee, and using the CNJ's taxonomy, which allows a broader analysis of the issues.

The BI Panel displays the most relevant information in a dynamic, interactive and easy way, providing tools to generate comparative graphs according to the criteria desired by the user, such as: subject x value, subject x quantity, secretariat x quantity, secretariat x value, secretariat x quantity, and secretariat x subject.
Novelty: The initiative is innovative because before it there was no litigation prevention and knowledge management policy based on the collection, structuring and analysis of precatórios data by a public law agency. The existence of a new work routine with periodic disclosure of data on the cost of litigation, its causes and recommendations for prevention will serve as a kind of wake-up call to curb thoughtless and repetitive attitudes that cost the public coffers. The initiative has introduced a new work process, a new methodology, a new policy, and aims to bring about a new culture, based on the periodic warning of how much some managers' administrative decisions can cost the public coffers.

Project/Initiative Name: Protocol for data opening processes in the judiciary of costa rica
Description:
In 2017, the Judiciary had its first participation in AbreLatam and in the Regional Open Data Conference (ConDatos), events held on that occasion in San José. Staff from the IT Directorate had the opportunity to present the institutional open data portal, emphasising how some of the international standards were applied. 

In 2018, close coordination began to take place between the National Commission for the Improvement of Justice Administration (Conamaj) and the IT Directorate, which became in charge of the open data strategy. In September of that same year, staff from both offices travelled to Buenos Aires to participate in the International Open Data Conference (IODC), an event that brought together people from countries from different continents and in which they presented the progress that has been made in the Costa Rican Judicial Branch in terms of open justice and open data.

From 2019 to the present, the work on open data has strengthened collaborative actions and during this time the initiative has received support from civil society actors, academia and international organisations, such as: the Collaborative Laboratory for Innovation in Public Administration (Innovaap) of the University of Costa Rica (UCR), the Freedom and Clear Accounts Programme of Hivos Latin America, Open Contracting Partnership and the Latin American Initiative for Open Data (ILDA).
Novelty:
What makes this project innovative is the implementation of technology in legal and monitoring aspects from the Public Sector, specifically from the Judiciary of Costa Rica, using Open Data to increase transparency from the Organisations and also increases citizen participation and closeness from the judicial officials who are involved in this process.
Finally, in the case of this Protocol, it is the first in the country in this area, and with it, efforts to prevent corruption have been increased and, at the same time, internal processes of the Judiciary have been improved.
Impact & Results:
As a result, the alignment of internal processes between intermediary units of the Judiciary can be highlighted.
Communicators and infomediaries have better structured open databases that allow them to make more accurate reports and analyses with the information provided thanks to this protocol.
In relation to citizens, people have easier access to certain processes where they require very specific information.

Project/Initiative Name: Prototype of Sodium Carboxymethylcellulose and Poly(vinyl alcohol) Wound Dressings using a Mixer Torque Rheometer
Description:
(not interesting) Management in wound care requires care by the health team, but also investment in technologies for the production of more effective and accessible dressings. In its absence, the damage to populations with little access is notorious: loss and/or worsening of health, with emotional cost for patients and families and also a financial cost for the Public Health System

The National Institute of Technology (INT – Brazil) developed a new prototype of a polymeric film made of poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) and sodium carboxymethylcellulose (NaCMC), free of toxic crosslinking agents, whose material processing was performed in a mix torque rheometer. Through this, it was possible to obtain a prototype for use as a dressing for the treatment of wounds, primarily in Health System from low-income and middle low-income countries, such as The Brazilian Unified Health System.
Novelty: This prototype eliminates the use of toxic chemical crosslinkers, allows the incorporation of drugs, such as IBU, and meets a universal public health proposal due to the low cost of the process for the treatment of patients with wounds.
Impact & Results: Due to the purpose of this innovation, additional human trials should be performed for the development of a health product. From that, the impacts will be measured.

Description:
In June 2018, the new BESOP system, an online communication tool between parties entering into public works or public works-related services contracts, was put into operation; different actors are involved. With the new update, the transparency of its use between the parties is guaranteed, in addition to supporting the tasks of monitoring and compliance with the regulations applicable to the execution of public works carried out by the resident, superintendent and/or supervisor users, in terms of the use of the electronic public works logbook.

With the BESOP's operating structure, both federal and state public servants are served at three levels, starting with the Global Administrator, who is staffed by personnel from the Ministry of Public Administration, and then assigning the profiles of Public Entity Administrators who, in turn, empower the Local Administrators who are responsible for registering contracts and related users, within the scope of their competence.

In the new physical and financial progress module, the resident is obliged to record the monthly physical and financial progress, in accordance with the estimates authorised in the month prior to the month in which the record is made, until the final settlement is recorded; this new module allows the various consultant users, whether they are managers of the entities or of the auditing areas, to know the status of the work and its compliance with the work programme established from the start of the works.
Novelty: This new version of the electronic logbook is made up of two main modules: 1) The electronic logbook module which is made up of 55 pre-established format notes, attached to the events that the regulations refer to as obligatory to register, and which are organised according to the execution process of the work. 2) The physical and financial progress module, to which only the resident has access, who is responsible for recording the progress of work and financial progress on a monthly basis, is linked to the registration of the work programme recorded at the beginning of the contract, which allows monitoring of the programmed against the authorised in the process of execution of the works.
Impact & Results: To have a digital historical repository of information on public works; online system that allows online registration of the parties that enter into contracts with the state and federal government and contractors, 24/7 availability, security, through the use of electronic signature, locks and validations that guarantees the quality and security of the information. It provides information for consultation and extraction of data from the auditing bodies regarding contracts and their progress; timely information in real time, of the works that are in process.

Description:
(not interesting) In 2013 over 45% of households in the Western Cape did not have a single member that had access to the Internet (Stats SA, General Household Survey). Accordingly, the WCG decided to leverage the broadband connectivity by implementing a separate Public Wi-Fi hotspot network so that citizens could benefit from the broadband infrastructure at the WCG provincial buildings and connect to the Internet. Since all WCG buildings are located in the vicinity of citizens, installing a Public Wi-Fi hotspot at the WCG building and providing limited free access to the Internet would enable the surrounding community to access the world of information and opportunities that the Internet presents.

The service provides any Wi-Fi-enabled device (mobile phone, laptop, tablet, computer) with: (i) unlimited free access to government websites, (ii) a monthly allocation of 3GB of free data to surf the Internet, and (iii) a free basic digital literacy course. The project prioritises the provision of access to the most underserved and rural communities. Focus has been placed on ease of use, so there is no registration process or login required to access the network. Inappropriate websites are inaccessible. The service does not provide unlimited free access to the Internet as the WCG does not want to promote a culture of completely free services and also expects its citizens to take responsibility for improving their own lives.
Novelty: The Public Wi-Fi hotspot project was the first initiative to transform provincial government’s approach to its own connectivity requirements by extending the infrastructure to provide large scale Internet access to citizens across the entire region.
Impact & Results: Over 2,4 million devices have connected to the service since inception, and each month over 300,00 users consume more than 250 terabytes of data. In quantifying this further, we can use mobile data prices, which is often the only other connectivity option in the communities that the network serves. At an average price of R100 per gigabyte of data, the usage of the Public Wi-Fi network equates to savings of over R25 million each month for the Western Cape citizens.

Project/Initiative Name: PubPeer - to aggregate people's comments as formal scientific criticism
Description:
PubPeer enables scientists to search for their publications or their peers publications and provide feedback and/or start a conversation about it anonymously. The overarching goal of the Foundation is to improve the quality of scientific research by enabling innovative approaches for community interaction. This is an online platform for post-publication peer review.

The post-review process happens once a scientific publication is released and discussed on the PubPeer browser or through the plugin which highlights on any web page, in particular PubMed. Operating since 2014, PubPeer recently has incorporated the Zotero plugin which is a free reference manager that makes possible to mute or prioritize comments by particular users. Similarly, they have introduced the option to link to high-quality blog posts, curation initiatives and expert comments by invoking the PubPeerBot. The PubPeerBot based on AI operates like a twitter automata that activates when tagging @PubPeerBot including the DOI or arXiv.
Novelty: Through an AI-based bot incorporates comments from Twitter as reviews of scientific papers/publications. This is to create a larger scale of opinions and representation as an important source of "scientific commentary"

Description:
Quarantine Heroes is a platform that coordinates neighborly help in the Corona pandemic. People seeking help are brought together with volunteers and referred via the homepage and alternatively by telephone. Since the COVID-19 pandemic is under control, the platform has been used to support campaign for refugees from Ukraine in Berlin and across Germany. 

The process is web-based and simple: 1) people in quarantine can ask for help and ask for assistance with shopping or errands, 2) people with time to support can log in and browse the closest neighbor. 

The platform is available in three common languages where the most refugees come from (Arabic, Turkish, and Russian) and German. It also includes a hotline available 24/7.
Novelty: Platform that connects aid seekers and providers at the neighborhood level during pandemic and war-crisis and refugee times.

Project/Initiative Name: Random fleet inspection skips at the start of operation
Description:
The high percentage on immobilization of the fleet in operation that generated risk for users due to mechanical failures, poor perception of service and impact on mobility. In order to mitigate this situation, a statistical methodology of random skips was implemented for fleet inspection at the beginning of the operation. Random sampling is performed on a dynamic sampling frame, which is not conventional in random sampling.

It was proposed to increase the proportion of vehicle immobilizations at the beginning of the operation, reduce vehicle inspection times in the daily inspection and randomly select vehicles for inspection. It was expected to provide a better service, based on fewer accidents and less impact on travel times due to mechanical failures, which also aimed to improve the perception of the service by users of the Integrated Public Transportation System of Bogota (SITP).

The innovative proposal to carry out fleet inspections at the beginning of the operation through random inspection jumps was included as a methodology in the technical annex of the audit for future contracts, in order to optimize the resources available for this purpose.
Novelty: Novel way of carrying out fleet inspections with the adoption of random sampling technique.
Impact & Results: As part of the results, it was found that the percentage of immobilizations in operation was reduced to 70% and the punctuality of dispatches increased by 4.53%. The results also showed an increase of 157 dispatches that represent an additional 7850 vacancies offered, without investing any resources.

Project/Initiative Name: Randomnized fleet inspection jump at start of operation
Description:
It was proposed to increase the proportion of vehicle immobilizations at the beginning of the operation, reduce vehicle inspection times in the daily inspection and randomly select vehicles for inspection.

The innovative proposal of inspecting the fleet at the beginning of the operation by means of random inspections was included as a methodology in the technical annex of the audit for future contracts, in order to optimize the resources available for this purpose.

The planning of the project involved a diagnosis of the fleet, service provision and operation in each of the operating yards/workshops, which identified opportunities for improvement and the need to innovate in the selection of vehicles. The uncertainty regarding the inspected vehicle generated a change in the fleet management and readiness culture prior to the start of the operation, which increased the amount of fleet available to provide the service.

Several fleet inspection processes were adapted according to the number of departures, platforms and personnel available in the 32 yards/workshops: to speed up the selection and reduce bias, a random number for the first vehicle inspected and a random inspection skip between zero and two, which varies between vehicles, were proposed.
Novelty:
Random sampling is based on the principle of probability of selection of fully identified and available elements of the sampling frame. In the inspection process the fleet was chosen by the concessionaires at their convenience, and parked at the exit of the yards/workshops hindering the normal flow of the beginning of the operation, additionally the concessionaires were allowed to adjust the readiness of the fleet in the middle of the inspection process, delaying it even more.

Several fleet inspection processes were adapted according to the number of departures, platforms and personnel available in the 32 yards/workshops: to speed up the selection and reduce bias, a random number was set for the first vehicle inspected, and once the inspection is completed, the next vehicle is chosen with a random skip that varies between zero and two according to the required sample size.
Impact & Results: The percentage of immobilizations in operation was reduced to 70%, the punctuality of dispatches increased by 4.53% and 157 dispatches were increased, representing an additional 7,850 vacancies offered, with no investment of resources.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description: The Model has been developed to improve holistic, merit and equal opportunity based, transparent, sustainable, accountable, data-driven, and technology-oriented human resources processes in the public sector. In the framework of the Model, functions and organization of HR units of ministries have been changed in a modern way. With the effective and innovative use of digital HR tools and methods, a paradigm shift has been achieved, and innovative public HR management reform has been implemented.
Impact & Results: With the Reference Model, productivity-oriented, merit and equal opportunity based, transparent, equal-opportunity, and data-based methods have come to life. Capable HR units are developed in Ministries for extensive collaboration and coordination.

Description:
The Regional Medicine Price Observatory was developed to support public health institutions in optimising public spending on medicine procurement. The problem identified was that there was no database of public medicine procurement prices that would allow comparisons between countries and determine fair market price trends. The innovation focuses on the methodology for coding and comparing medicines through a digital platform.

The ORPM is a tool for monitoring and analysing medicine prices that, through access to accurate information, contributes to reducing information asymmetries in procurement prices and, above all, to improving the effectiveness of public health spending.

The ORPM presents standardised medicine price information that allows comparison between different public sources in the region and the world, serving as a support instrument for informed decision-making based on reliable data and methodological rigour backed by public health experts and procurement specialists. The database contains market price information on more than 1,000 critical medicines from 21 countries, obtained through a permanent process of referencing and monitoring medicine prices. It contributes to improving the efficiency of public health spending and promotes access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines, contributing to greater universal health coverage.

The innovation of the ORPM focuses on the methodology for comparing medicines across countries. A universal coding was developed that allows correlating descriptions and coding across countries to compare procurement prices. In addition, an open data mining algorithm was developed to automate the data collection process. Finally, the digital platform allows the user to consume data and at the same time feed information through structured data templates to ensure that the information is kept up to date.
Novelty: It is innovative because there are no existing medicines procurement data platforms that have been able to codify and harmonise medicines procurement descriptions across countries. The collaborative working model among countries requesting access to the Observatory's platform is based on reciprocity of providing their medicines procurement data while consuming information from other countries.
Impact & Results:
The ORPM has provided information for negotiation processes for patented medicines. In Mexico, the procurement team was able to reduce about 23% of spending on high-cost medicines.
Three governments (Panama, Ecuador and Mexico) have voluntarily requested to join the Observatory to access international price information and have agreed to provide data on procurement in their countries. Discussions are underway with the governments of Peru, Costa Rica, Guatemala and Honduras.
Impact results are measured under a first approximation of the level of public spending with prices before access to the Observatory and after using the information contained therein. 
Currently the database contains data for 1563 medicines and approximately 297,000 prices of public procurement transactions.

Project/Initiative Name: Reviving millets in farms and on plates with focus on gender equity, nutritional security and climate resilience of small and marginal farmers of Odisha
Organisation: Department of Agriculture and Farmers' Empowerment, Government of Odisha
Description:
As per FAO, 3 cereals namely rice, maize and wheat contribute 50% of global energy intake. With expected yield drop around 25-30% in rice and wheat in next 25 years due to climate change, food security is at risk in India. Odisha Millets Mission is a uniquely designed multi stakeholder government led sustainable food system intervention to diversify the cereals to deliver food and nutritional security to at least 5 million households of vulnerable and indigenous communities in Odisha.

In order to design the intervention, a consultation was organized with farmers, collectives, NGOs, experts, research institutions and others to develop a framework for revival of these forgotten foods (Millets). Consensus emerged that to revive millets there is a need to work comprehensively on all aspects such as campaigns for revival of millets in household consumption, promoting local processing enterprises, improving productivity of millet crop systems and developing markets.

The Department of Agriculture and Farmers Empowerment, Government of Odisha launched Odisha Millets Mission (OMM) in 2017 in 30 blocks covering 7 districts. It was later expanded to 84 blocks covering 15 districts. It is further extended to 143 blocks covering 19 districts. Budgetary support was increased from 8.04 million USD for 5 years in 2017 to 353 million USD for 5 years in 2022. It is proposed to further enhance income support to the millet farmers and expand OMM to all the rainfed blocks of the state.

OMM is implemented by farmer collectives/women collectives at the village level with support of community resource persons and local NGO partners. A Gram Panchayat (cluster of villages) meetings are convened with the community and the concept of the project is shared. Participatory approaches such as Free and Prior Informed consent (FPIC) are adopted to identify the constraints and village wise list of interventions in Gram panchayats. Through organizing such meetings, a block level action plan is prepared. Representatives of agriculture departments and people representatives participate in these meetings. Based on the action, a month wise year wise activity plan is submitted to District Administration and implemented accordingly.
Novelty:
OMM is one of its kind multi-stakeholder sustainable-food-system intervention which focused on revival of millets through increasing household consumption and diversifying the cereals through assured procurement and distribution in government food security schemes such as Public Distribution System (PDS), Supplementary Nutrition Programs (SNPs) for pregnant women, etc.

Further, it is innovative because it is a participatory project that involves custodian farmers, local collectives, NGOs and research institutions to promote biodiversity and celebrate millet food cultures through awareness campaigns, food festivals and women collectives led entrepreneurship on food outlets etc.
Impact & Results: OMM has adopted the Objectives-Means-Target-Indicators approach to comprehensively evaluate results, outcomes and impacts. The value of millet produce per household has increased from 48.50 USD to 153.05 USD, whereas the value of produce per hectare has gone up from 115.80 USD to 253.85 USD. Prior to the project implementation, the amount of production of finger millet per farmer for one acre was 0.17 and 0.26 MT respectively. However, during the post project implementation, the amount of production per farmer for one acre has increased to 0.56 and 0.6 MT respectively. In 2021-22, 41286 farmers sold 15810.85 MT of finger-millet and were paid 13.41 million USD. OMM model will be scaled up through supporting 3000 women's collectives and 143 farmer collectives through established urban millet cafes, millet street food shops in rural/urban areas. OMM will upscale SNPs in 15 districts covering 1.48 million beneficiaries. Millet procurement of 95000 MT and benefiting 0.3 million farmers.

Project/Initiative Name: RIA - The Regulatory Implication Analysis portal
Description:
The current process for Regulatory Impact Assessment (RIA) incorporates several individual reports that quantify the impact (financial, societal, organizational, etc.), as well as the legality and implementation reports, which allow to gather all the facts needed for evaluating the impact of a regulatory reform. Until recently this process took place offline and included the communication of electronic documents through email, without any versioning or changes' tracking. As a result, the process lifecycle was lengthy and the final output was an electronic document (PDF) with lots of information that was hard to analyze and process, at the data level.

The new RIA platform allows multiple stakeholders to collaborate in the writting of the RIA report, to have continuous tracking of the changes and versions and at the same time allows the higher-level administratives and control committees to have the final word on the report before signing. It supports multiple report types (e.g. legislative act, urgent act, international conventions, amendments), handles the process from creation to publishing of the report, supports printing into PDF and at the same time provides report statistics and user and role management.

The application is developed following a three-tier logic, with a form-based interface in the front-end written in HTML and Javascript, a business logic layer in the middle that orchestrates the RIA report lifecycle and is written in NodeJS and ExpressJS, and a MySQL database that stores all the report data in the back-end.

The editors of the RIA reports and all the administrative committees that are responsible for creating and managing a report are benefitted from the platform as they can now contribute their data at any moment from any place with this web-based platform, they can have full control of the RIA report editing lifecycle and get notified about the need for their contribution. The ministries and the General Secretariat of Legal and Parliamentary Affairs are now able to monitor the progress of all RIA reports and reach the final result much faster and with the exchange of much fewer emails. Finally, they can export the RIA report at any time in PDF and also check the differences between the various versions of a report that is processed.
Novelty: The RIA platform uniquely supports the digitization of the RIA process, providing multi-user and multi-role access management, monitoring of changes and versioning. Minimizes the exchange of emails and offline editing in the whole RIA composition lifecycle and leads to the creation of a database with all data from all RIA reports. For the first time ever, the General Secretariat of Legislative and Parliamentary Affairs has full control of the impact assessment process and has a digital tool for monitoring the progress of reports, producing the final report online, and visualizing previous RIA data, planning and optimizing future legislative actions. All the stakeholders, ministries, parliament members, and the committees of the Secretariat, are now aware of the progress of an RIA report at all stages and can collaborate asynchronously to compile the final report.
Impact & Results: The impact of the innovation is limited so far since it is still in the pilot phase. However, the first training sessions with more than 30 employees from the ministries revealed the power of the platform to cover their basic needs and facilitate them in the composition of the RIA report, saving them time and resources. Further evaluation of the impact of the RIA platform will follow best practices for impact assessment in various axes, including micro and macro-economic impact (e.g. cost-benefit analysis, and ROI analysis), productivity, societal impact, etc. Surveys and questionnaires will be developed for this purpose.

Project/Initiative Name: Risk Analysis with Focus on Port Security
Description:
The context in which port terminals are inserted, characterised by a large volume of information and threats, produces the need to define strategies that ensure the reduction of port vulnerabilities based on technical knowledge. It is in this context that this innovation is inserted, by presenting a methodology focused on Risk Analysis with Focus on Port Security, aligned with national and international standards that is, at the same time, technical and transparent. By force of international resolutions and norms, port security must involve several practices and routines, which, consolidated, generate the Port Security Plan (PSP). However, to be valid, this Plan needs a previous analysis: it is the Risk Assessment Study.

By contemplating a study of the internal and external environments, this technical planning instrument defines actions aimed at defense and physical security, being the basis for the consolidation of a Port Security Plan. In the search for adequate protection, the Risk Analysis emerges as an indispensable element in this scenario given its real capacity to subsidize the Port Security Plan for the balanced and effective treatment of the security of vulnerable categories, such as human resources, security of areas and facilities, security of the monitoring center, security of equipment, security of documentation, security of communications and computing, security of operations and security of information.

The Risk Analysis enables the selection of appropriate security measures that allow, at the same time, that the port facility is not immobilized to the point of becoming operationally slow and inefficient, and that have protective measures relevant to the threats that permeate it. In other words, the Risk Analysis seeks to identify the necessary balance between security, functionality, and cost.
Novelty:
The various methodologies used until then were superficial and, in general, did not meet the legal requirements, both in the public and private sectors, which led to distortions in meeting security requirements, hesitation of terminals regarding the inspections carried out by the public sector and inadequacy of the procedures required. 

The Risk Analysis with Emphasis on Port Security presented here meets the international standards' requirements and guidelines by employing a structured methodology, capable of ensuring, from a systemic viewpoint, the effectiveness and suitability of the necessary measures and procedures.
Impact & Results: The innovation has already been implemented and standardized and has already had an impact on the way port terminals have started to manage their risks. No other impact measurement is available.

Description:
The innovation was developed as part of the digital transformation process and as a response to the social isolation situation posed by COVID 19. It consists of enrolling citizens, Peruvian individuals, in the Taxpayers' Registry 100% virtually, using remote fingerprint biometrics from a cell phone or the SUNAT Portal. It is innovative because it is a disruptive response to the citizen providing a totally virtualized service.

The procedure implemented is simple, you only have to enter the APP Personas SUNAT from your cell phone (available for free download on Google and IOS stores) or the SUNAT Portal, perform the remote fingerprint biometric verification, which allows you to check the identity of individuals, complete the necessary information and generate your own SOL Key. The RUC Digital is obtained virtually 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, avoiding the need to go to a Taxpayer Service Center. 

The RUC DIGITAL is innovative because it is the disruptive response to citizens for their enrollment in the RUC, allowing the taxpayer's identity to be authenticated completely virtually and providing a 100% digital service, so that they no longer have to go to the taxpayer service center; and thus not only has one of the main services provided by SUNAT been virtualized, but it has also allowed future entrepreneurs to become effective with quick access to formality. In the future, this innovation should be scaled towards the use of other biometric technologies, such as facial or face biometrics.

All the functionalities are implemented in a library, which is integrated to the main application and is called by the APP when the innovation is used. An API Gateway interface is provided where a REST service is exposed as a router for the use of all the microservices to be used. This REST service is authenticated by tokens. In the integration with the biometric web service, it first performs the query with a microservice provided by the RENIEC, "best fingerprint" in order to request the 4 fingers or thumb of the right or left hand, then proceeds to ask the citizen the hand or thumb and runs the fingerprint validation microservice and thus closes the identification process.
Novelty: Terminates the face-to-face scheme, allowing tax enrollment with a remote fingerprint identification service (from a cell phone) never before used by a public administration as a means of identifying individuals and using a mobile application to complete the necessary information for the taxpayer's registration. Likewise, it allows the delivery of the access key to SUNAT's virtual services (Clave SOL) also using this innovation, in a single transaction, significantly reducing the time of individuals from almost 4 hours in person to 5 minutes and saving US$10 (travel expenses and work permits for the attention during office hours) to US$0. In this way, all the services of the tax administration, included in the tax life cycle of an individual, are totally virtualized.
Impact & Results: Up to this moment, almost 500,000 people, since August 2020, have used the RUC Digital to register to the RUC and obtain their SOL key. Quantitative results are measured on a daily basis and to verify the quantitative results, post-contact studies have been used to verify the degree of satisfaction of the people who have used the innovation. In the future we expect to optimize the innovation by providing a geo-referencing service for the location of your address and the alternative use of identity verification with an identification gateway (fingerprint, face).

Description:
Satu Data Indonesia (SDI ) is a government data management policy that aims to create quality data, easy to access, and can be shared between Central and Regional Agencies. This policy is contained in Presidential Regulation no. 39 of 2019 concerning One Indonesian Data. Through SDI, all government data and other relevant agency data can lead to the One Data Indonesia Portal (data.go.id).

The Satu Data Indonesia Portal is the official open data portal for Indonesia managed by the Central Level Secretariat of One Data Indonesia, Ministry of National Development Planning / Bappenas. Through the Satu Data Indonesia Portal, we are making full efforts to improve data governance for the realization of government transparency and accountability, as well as supporting national development.

Satu Data’s vision is to make decisions based on “data that are accurate, current, integrated, accountable, accessible, and easily shared”. In that way, the initiative introduces common principles for data accuracy, metadata, interoperability and referencing across every government. Without these, ministries used inaccurate and inconsistent data to make policies. 

Satu Data’s first step is to tackle existing databases of information, particularly those addressing government funding for poverty, education and healthcare. With Indonesia’s population of 271 million, “poverty is dynamic, and there are government limitations in terms of budget”, highlights Suharso. Linking databases would reduce discrepancies and ensure that funding is distributed accurately. Additionally, as a Law and regulatory framework, the Satu Data law introduced new roles and structures to ensure the standards are maintained in every agency. Centrally, the initiative is overseen by a board from seven agencies, chaired by the Minister of National Planning (Bappenas).
Novelty: A government data management policy that aims to create quality data, easy to access, and can be shared between Central and Regional Agencies through standards, a law and frameworks of data.
Impact & Results: More than 77,5 thousand publicly available datasets on government policy, near 5 thousand datasets on activity management, 4,4 thousands datasets on territorial development, 42,9 datasets not yet classified.

Project/Initiative Name: School of Virtual Reality - Cambui Online
Description:
A system that recreates the school environment in virtual reality and enables interaction between teachers and students, integrating tools such as: augmented reality, immersion process, systems integration, application of tests and exams, presentation of slides and videos.

The project reduces school dropout, increases learning capacity, includes technology in education as a teaching tool and expands the capacity of teaching tools for teachers.

It is a software that reassembles a teaching environment, Room or School Unit, in virtual reality ( Metaverse) and through mixed technologies and immersive process allows students and teachers to be in the same digital environment. 

Objectives or goals of the innovation;
1- Technological inclusion in public education, reduction of school drop-outs, recovery of post-pandemic educational development.
2- Evolution of teaching methodologies, innovation in education to prepare students for the technological market, increased knowledge assimilation through cognitive activation of students.
3- Generate new jobs based on the demand generated by the implementation of innovation, move the local economy of the regions where the implementation is activated.

The solution serves each educational unit individually, which allows it to be scaled according to the needs of each city
Novelty:
With the system, architectural designs of school units will be created.
Each school unit will hold: 1) 20 classrooms with capacity for up to 45 students; 2) Structuring of the educational content to be presented by teachers for execution of classes in the system as: videos, slides and holography; 3) The user will create their personalized avatar that will give unique access to the
educational environment; 4) Speed in the application and correction of tests and exams by teachers, 5) Technical training of teachers to use the system, and methodological training for the development of teaching methodologies.
Impact & Results:
With innovation the first impact generated so far are: 
1- The involvement of students and teachers with the innovation process.
2- The commitment to learning and the increase in attendance during actions.
These are measured with satisfaction and adherence surveys.
The expected results for the near future are the implementation of a complete IFRS - Erechim unit, the creation of new courses in virtual reality being able to attend more than 1.000 (One Thousand) students.

Project/Initiative Name: Senior shared housing - Cohousing and Coliving
Description:
We detected the need to develop a legislation for senior shared housing, based on a proposal for a shared senior housing sandbox, applicable at the municipal, state and federal levels, thinking of a temporary normative with a minimum period of 2 years, for case validation and later universalization of the right, with the power of scaling. Housing that is assigned to this senior population by social leasing, with usufruct rights and that returns to the state in case of death to be assigned to other seniors. This sandbox indicates the need for a new regulatory framework that encourages and promotes shared housing for active seniors, relaxing the regulatory requirements of the financial market aimed at this age group, minimizing real estate risks, bringing companies closer to regulatory agencies, encouraging the credit line program for active senior housing, establishing specific urbanistic and regulatory standards for shared housing, providing fiscal support to institutions for the development of this type of housing; promoting public policies and housing programs for the active 60+ age group in situations of social vulnerability, linked to social and health policies aimed at active aging.

- Remedy the lack of adequate housing to serve the active elderly population in the public sector.
- This is shared housing for people over 60 who are independent and autonomous. 
- Focus on social technology innovation where active aging, social relations and cooperativism, and the exchange of experiences are encouraged, resulting in improved health and quality of life. According to the WHO, it is a successful model in Europe and the US with positive health outcomes for the population.
Novelty:
The proposal aims to build shared housing or use unoccupied public buildings with retrofit. A way of living based on sharing, where the environment promotes mutual care among residents, adding the concept of "Aging in Place", which collaborates in the permanence of the elderly in this community as long as possible, postponing to a later stage the entry into an LCI (Long-Term Care Institution) according to each case.
A social leasing policy linked to this innovation of housing for autonomous elderly people allows the resident to enjoy the benefit, and is responsible for carrying out the relevant maintenance of the property, generating a sense of ownership in usufruct status, and the benefit is extended only to the spouse.
Impact & Results:
The implementation of shared housing will bring positive results for the Brazilian population in the following points:
1) Economic - the implementation of sustainable measures in construction reduces production costs, with these savings being passed on to future residents of the development, and the sharing of various services.
2) Social - health and well being, where the sharing of activities and spaces promotes socialization and better physical and mental conditions already proven by the WHO.
3) Environmental - good practices of environmental sustainability in the implementation of the houses (alternative renewable energy, rainwater collection for reuse, intelligent and healthy architectural solutions, vegetable gardens, etc.) that will bring savings and contribute to the environment.

Description:
Seongdong-gu introduced an innovative process called the Seongdong Mobile Directory, the world’s first system capable of accurately managing visitor information in real time and safely storing personal information thanks to ICT technology through consultation with an IT venture company.

You can access the website to enter personal information with a mobile phone through the NFC tag (or QR code) provided when entering institutions and facilities. Then the authentication message is sent to the corresponding cellphone. Once you put the authentication number received, self-authentication is completed. After that, if you are additionally checked for fever, respiratory symptoms, and overseas travel history, the information is stored in the visitor information cloud and the entry is registered.

Project/Initiative Name: Singapore sends daily Whatsapp updates on coronavirus
Description:
The Whatsapp tool enables coordinating Singapore's response to the COVID-19 outbreak by providing citizens with two to three daily updates on cases via the messaging app. This is to tackle fake and false news by using the same interface where they are typically propagated. 

The system had to be upgraded to meet the scale and time sensitibity to cope with new demand, and get information out rapidly before missinformation could sow discord. The Whatsapp platform was adapted by incorporating three main tools: 1) AI translation of the information in English, 2) Tailored sing-up process, 3) fast updates up to 500 messages per second to cover the whole population in less than 30 minutes. Whatsapp is used by >4 million Singaporians, that's why the Government has been used the app for updates on different topics since 2019.
Novelty:
It's a massive communication system to reach out to the people with official information about COVID-19 that are relevant to the people. 
Three tools are integrated simultanously:
- AI translation: rapidly translate messages from English to the other three official languages (Chinese, Malay, Tamil) to be vetted by civil servants before sending out on Whatsapp. 
- Easy to sing up: people specify their desired language. Using Form.SG to launch mailing lists to connect government systems (end-to-end encrypted and in the cloud)
- Fast updates: a jump from 10 messages per second to 500. Postman system replaced MCI and connected other systems like sing-up forms, translation systems, and automated subscriber lists.
Impact & Results: - Capacity to reach out to >4 million Singaporeans in less than 30 minutes per Whatsapp message

Description:
Singpass, Singapore’s national digital identity (NDI), is the cornerstone of the country’s Smart Nation vision. As a foundational digital infrastructure, NDI has created endless possibilities for government and businesses to provide innovative and user centric digital services, basing off APIs made available by NDI. What started off as an identity and access management system for government services, has grown into an ecosystem of products and services adopted by 97% of the population who are at an eligible age to use Singpass. This is possible because the team tirelessly focus on users’ needs, prototype, and improve the product offerings iteratively.
 
Singpass includes many different services:
- Login - verifies users’ identity online in a secure and trusted manner when transacting online
- Myinfo - consolidates data that government have of users from various single source of truth. These data are released to public and private organisation based on the respective users’ authorisation. The product started with the objective of users having to only “tell government once” when updating information
- Identiface - provides a stronger method of authentication by matching users’ selfie captured during transaction with the latest enrolled facial image recorded with the Immigration and Checkpoint Authority
- Verify - enables users to release verified data when performing in-person transactions
- SafeEntry - during the recent pandemic, Verify was pivoted within a short period of time to support contact tracing
- Sign - seeks to digitalise transactions that still require wet ink signature by providing secure electronic signature capabilities for all Singpass users
- Notify - provides a platform for government to reach out to citizens quickly and securely
Novelty:
Singpass aims to provide growing opportunities for digitalization and value creation to the everyday lives of residents and businesses by enabling end to end digital transactions. What make Singpass innovative is that it permitted to:
1. create an ecosystem of trust that has enabled other public and private organisations to rely on and further innovate to deliver wonderful services to users.
2. build a product platform that can iterate rapidly to meet evolving needs within a very tight timeline (SafeEntry to support contact tracing)
3. eliminate the need for multiple online identities and passwords, through the use of Singpass authentication methods (Login, Identiface, Verify)
4. empowers users with greater and more convenient access to their own data, allowing personal and corporate data to only be shared upon explicit consent using Myinfo
5. enable trust services through digital signing, remote authorisation of transactions and digital credentials stored in a digital wallet.
Impact & Results:
Singpass has largely impacted organisations, since on-boarding to Singpass APIs, businesses have reported:
• 97% usage by eligible residents
• 80% reduction in transaction time for digital transactions
• 10 mins reduction in waiting time through the use of Singpass Face Verification.
• up to S$50 savings for financial sector companies to acquire each customer online using Singpass Myinfo
• 15% increase in approvals due to better data quality
An independent study done by Deloitte has estimated the current economic impact of Singpass to be $385 million. What is even more exciting is that the potential economic impact can be above $1 billion, when new and existing product and features have been adopted by various sectors.

Project/Initiative Name: Small changes that generate great impacts
Organisation: FOGAPY: Fondo de Garantía del Paraguay
Description: In 1995 in Paraguay, a Guarantee Fund for MiPymes companies was created, which 21 years later had no significance. Between years 2016 and 2018, numerous innovations were generated: legal, economic, technological and governance in the regulations of the FUND, from which it results in an exponential use and thus even the bankruptcy of thousands of MiPymes is avoided, by making it possible with the use of the guarantee granted by the FUND, with transparent processes that are 100% online.

Allocating Housing to citizens in need is often understood as a transactional service with long waiting list. However, when using systems thinking to understand the service from a different perspective, we recognise that this is in fact a complex service that does not just involve Housing. When we then design Housing using systemic design, a far more effective way of dealing with people in need emerges, and the waiting list disappears.

The team realised that the application form could not be a reliable method for understanding people’s real problems. Only other human beings can absorb the variety of complex people issues. 

The team and the head of service, during redesign, developed a way of working that allowed front line staff to deal with the work, without needing the supervisor approval and checking that occurred in the old system. The new system being far more rapid, end to end resolution was often a matter of days, and the supervisor was more aware of what was happening in her team than before. The team created new ways of learning, and they reviewed cases with colleagues when they needed assistance. They became a true self managed team.
Novelty: The innovation comes from using a systems thinking lens to view the service and its relationship with citizens. Starting from the complex needs of citizens, and naturally incorporating what matters to them, to directly link to the work that the wider public sector can do to help the citizen to remedy their situation. This is based on recognising that the local government office is there to help and collaborate with the citizen in the way that is most appropriate to deal with the many causes of the problems that the citizen faces.
Impact & Results:
The waiting list dropped from 6293, to 309
Failure demand dropped from 62.7%, to 3%
Appeals from citizens dropped from 324, to 1 a year
Citizens used to be frustrated and aggressive, to engaged and compliant.
Staff were very demotivated, to very motivated.
Housing Allocations department was 22 , reduced to 15 staff.

Project/Initiative Name: SOS Wristwatch - Innovation of Ship SOS Signal System
Description: So many peoples related to a matter of marine activities are concerned about boat accidents threat human lives such as crash, sinking of boats. KCA developed an watch type of SOS signal repeater (‘SOS wristwatch’) connected to VHF-DSC equipped in a boat cabin. When a person cannot push the distress button on the VHF-DSC directly in a state on a boat accident, they can send SOS signals by touching top of the SOS wristwatch and be saved by the coastal guards.
Novelty: Prior to our innovative case, boat accidents were causing life damages(death, disappearance) because crew had to reach the Distress Signal Facility located inside the cabin on an urgent situation such as crash and rollover is difficult, resulting in casualties without being able to be rescued. Using the SOS Watch, survivor can activate a quick life-saving innovative system which are able to transmit distress signals by immediately pressing the rescue button on the watch regardless of any type of maritime accidents.
Impact & Results:
It has been recognized by the government and the public for its excellence in innovation by winning the Korea Knowledge Management Prime Minister's Award and selected as the best case for innovation in the public sector.
 It also saved about 10.4 million dollars in social expenses for the search and rescue of survivor on marine accidents.

Project/Initiative Name: Strategic Plan for Federal and Cross-border Interoperability
Organisation: National Directorate for State Digital Integration and Processing (DNIYTDE), Subsecretariat for Administrative Innovation (SSIA), Secretariat for Public Innovation (SIP), Office of the Chief of Cabinet of Ministers (JGM)
Description:
The "Strategic Plan for Federal and Cross-Border Interoperability" consists of a set of plans, proposals, concepts and specific software products to achieve the high levels of information exchange between public bodies required for efficient and intelligent management.

As a first step, the deployment of the MSI (Modules for Interoperable Services) tool was developed and started. The second stage is the expansion of the capabilities of this network of nodes, with the possibility of integrating ecosystems that use other tools (XRoad, for example).

This upgrade implies a strong commitment to the concept of "Digital Coexistence", and it allows communication between different Interoperability Ecosystems, maintains technological neutrality in terms of free choice of software or environment used, and enables both federal-level exchange between the different jurisdictions of our country, as well as cross-border exchange, opening the door to connectivity between different states. The plan contemplates another 42 (forty) new nodes to be installed, 2 (two) of them in pre-production status.

A specific module is also being developed for connection with X-Road ecosystems, to be approved by NIIS (Nordic Institute for Interoperability Solutions, Estonia) so that the certificate profile of the Argentine PKI (Digital Signature Infrastructure) can be recognised and thus interconnect MSI/INTEROPERAR networks with XRoad networks, both at national and cross-border level.
Novelty:
A) The element of this project identified as unprecedented in the country and the region is the strong commitment to the concept of "Digital Coexistence", materialised in the MSI module "MSI/X-Road connector".
This has been developed to allow communication with XRoad-based installations or networks, in order to extend its scope and facilitate the exchange of information with ecosystems that use other interoperability solutions.
Its main function is to allow any data service exposed in the X-Road Platform to be interpreted by the MSI Platform (and vice versa), being transparent to the applications that use it, maintaining in both ecosystems the characteristics of security, encryption, non-repudiation and traceability.
B) Regarding the MSI tool, one of its components is the "Translation Module". It stands out for allowing the conversion of pre-existing services in SOAP protocol and structured data of any kind (databases, csv files).
Impact & Results:
INTEROPERAR strengthens efficiency in the public sector, streamlines citizens' dealings with the State, minimises bureaucracy and reduces the amount of information that citizens need to provide in order to carry out their procedures, avoiding repeated documentary verifications and duplication of records.
It allows the administration not to generate data replicas, to keep the original source securely and to make available to public bodies the data required for the fulfilment of their tasks.
The current deployment consists of 16 (sixteen) nodes, used by 27 (twenty-seven) entities, exposing 67 (sixty-seven) data exchange services, with 165 (one hundred and sixty-five) systems using them.
The plan contemplates another 42 (forty-two) new nodes to be installed, 2 (two) of them in pre-production status.

Organisation: Department of Education - Taipei City Government
Description:
Taipei City CooC-Cloud and virtual reality (VR) is an integrated parent, teacher, and student platform and part of the city Smart Campus 4.0 strategy. The platform was initially stablished in 2016 to boost e-learning, it gained relevance during the COVID-19 pandemic under the Stay-at-home economy development strategy. 

Taipei CooC-Cloud (臺北酷課雲) e-learning center has served both local and overseas expat students providing teachers, students and parents in Taiwan an alternative approach to teach, learn and communicate. The digital learning platform is a place for students from elementary to senior high school to access educational materials under the competency-based educational framework. Apart from compulsory knowledge classes such as Chinese, English and mathematics, there are also optional competency classes that equip students for daily life and everyday challenges. For those preparing for university, some experiential and technology-driven modules are up including IoT design or introduction to Chinese medicine. 

The platform has also become a tool for the education department and Overseas Community Affairs Council (OCAC) to promote Taiwan’s digital education worldwide. About 390 teachers from all over the world have received training and have run a course using CooC-Cloud.
Novelty: City's e-learning center to provide an integrated parent, teacher, and student remote experience and articulated with the national competency-based educational framework.
Impact & Results:
Total number of visits: 111,535,229
Number of registered accounts: 1,091,413
Number of videos online: 4,131 (Other videos: 6,828)
Number of open questions: 43,821
Other resources: 2,125
Number of e-books created: 2,875
Total number of titles: 29,659
Total number of ideas: 11,604
Total number of certified reading questions: 46,267

Organisation: United Arab Emirates Climate Change Special Envoy
Description:
Terrenesat-1 is a scientific research satellite in a medium earth orbit, studying the Earth’s changes in surface temperature, atmosphere and climate. The objectives of the satellite are to calculate an environment's susceptibility to wildfires. The satellite is provided with a system that allows it to do so by analysing environmental data points e.g: temperature, perception, humidity, wind speed, rainfall, and elevation to build a GIS database. The system looks for patterns, allowing it to predict when and where wildfires could break out. 

The intent of innovating this satellite is to alert environmental protection agencies about the present-day and near-future conditions of possible intensification of wildfires at a current location. Terrenesat-1 is developed with the newest imaging tactics, presenting real-time forecast updated data and weather reports based on influences of temperature and patterns. The satellite issues warnings ahead of time, especially to home ignition zones.
Novelty: With VNIR imagery, the spatial resolution is high, the world can better monitor global climate change, in a more effective and efficient way, that includes, depletion of wildlife habitat, air pollution, disturbed ecological balance, and low environmental protection; with the support of SWIR imagery, the satellite will work to identify and map areas with hotspots. Areas affected by global warming can get an advanced warning ahead in order to reduce risk to human life.
Impact & Results:
As we all turn our attention to the global goals, we notice that satellites help us reach those Earthly targets.

1. Rapidity: This satellite can provide up-to-date and real-time data, a five-day forecast that can give accurate adjustment predictions to weather.

2. Visibility: With advanced high-tech like multi-spectral imaging system and Advanced Baseline Imager (ABI), terrensat-1 could provide adequate visibility even under harsh conditions.

3: Reliability: The satellite is trusted to deliver warnings well ahead of time of annual probability of wildfire burning in a specific location and time, thus, managing wildfire risk.

Project/Initiative Name: The 2021 State of the Octoverse Survey
Description:
For the first time, this research combines telemetry from 4M+ repositories and surveys from more than 12,000 developers. This approach reveals current trends and also provides predictive results (i.e., it is now possible to see with more precision how to achieve successful outcomes through data and coding for developers, teams, organizations, and communities.)

Specifically, they incorporated augmented telemetry from four million repositories with survey responses from over 40,000 developers. This allows to  improve performance and well-being by developing code, creating documentation, and supporting communities in smarter, more sustainable ways. 

To all the institutions using GitHub, their model showcase that sharing information through READMEs, contribution guidelines, and issues are open source projects’ secret sauce: they invite new contributors and make developers 55% more productive. This report and findings are valuable, in particular for teams that work with open source and stack data technology.
Novelty: Incorporation of new telemetric approach to go through >4 million repositories and surveys to improve performance and well-being by developing codes and creating open source documentation.
Impact & Results: Visualization here: https://octoverse.github.com/static/octoverse-report

Project/Initiative Name: The AuroraAI: A Human-Centric and Life-Event Based Public Sector Transformation
Description:
In a typical society, societal operations are based on resource efficiency and organizational orientation, in the public sector mandated by law and in the private sector driven by business initiatives. This results in task-oriented services and organizations, where it is up to individuals to discover and navigate through the different services they want to use. In contrast to this customer-oriented task-centricity, the National Artificial Intelligence Programme AuroraAI aims at offering citizens personalised services at the right time in different life situations and events, in line with a Human-Centric AI Transformation model.

A human-centric and life event -based operating model supported with the life-event service ecosystem technology enables the authorities to produce value directly in people's life events through the cooperation of other service providers in a timely and proactive manner. The planning of activities and service paths is guided by different situations and events in peoples’ lives and the well-being of people is supported through extensive cross-sectoral cooperation. Digitalization and the use of AI aims at improving the flow of life. Data interoperability enables a well-being-enhancing data economy and people's ability to manage information about themselves. 

In the initial phase, there are 10-20 services connected to the AuroraAI network. The goal is to include strategically selected service offerings based on different life and business events during the next government term (2023-2027).
Novelty: Currently, the dominant operating model for developing public administration service production is customer orientation, where the digitalization of service processes is still planned from the service tasks of the authorities. Resources are allocated based on legislation to the customer-oriented implementation of each administrative task, which only accelerates the running of people from one service to another, and no one is still interested in a person's overall situation. A Human-Centric Service Production changes the starting point of public administration operations and also the planning of services to people's life events, in which case the authority can only succeed in the implementation of its responsibilities in close cooperation with other authorities and service providers and by understanding people's real needs in different life events.
Impact & Results:
The AuroraAI Network is still in implementation phase, hence there is no empirical evidence of its impact and results yet. Still, this innovation is supposed to have numerous beneficiaries, the most important of which are citizens and companies. The innovation guides the public administration to offer genuinely useful services in life events together with private sector service providers. At the same time, the innovation improves the matching service offering to the real needs of the people and thus reduces failure demand, where citizens use the wrong services at the wrong time. 

The developed human-centric transformation and operating model and The AuroraAI Network supports the national implementation of life event approach and improve the effectiveness of service offerings for people in the next governmental term (2023-2027). The first results should be available by the end of 2022.

Organisation: 10 Tooba - Applied Research on the Built Environment
Description:
The Built Environment Observatory (BEO) is a web-based portal of open knowledge. It covers a range of urban issues with a focus on fair housing, spatial justice, and, access to basic infrastructure such as electricity and water. The initiative aims to support scholars, communities, civil society, development agencies and officials in their pursuit for the equitable development of the built environment, through the production of knowledge in the form of:

- State of Housing series that offer in-depth analyses of the impact of legislation, policy, and, public spending, on housing and cities
- Laws and legislation explainers and commentaries
- Housing Statistics fact sheets and analysis of key data
- Social Housing commentaries and analysis of projects and strategies
- Commentaries on wider issues of housing and the city

Example of Series - Who owns Cairo?: https://marsadomran.info/en/category/state-of-housing/2022-who-owns-cairo/ 

They work with deprived communities throughout Egypt with a focus on those in informal areas, rural and peri-urban villages, and historic urban cores. Also, they produce a range of participatory action plans, from recommendations to full outline plans based on: Mapping assets, needs, Negotiation between stakeholders, Consensus building among stakeholders
Novelty: Open knowledge platform with a focus on housing/property topics and intersects them with gender, ownership, investment, legal topics and more.
Impact & Results: Series of evidence-based reports about the state of the built environment in Cairo.

Description:
What motivated the ideation of the campo amigo project scope was due to the problem of scarce resources, inequality in agribusiness, irrational use of water resources for irrigating crops, as well as the issue of the need to increase the production of food for internal and external supplies in an intelligent and sustainable way, in which, there are organic and technological resources accessible for free energy generation for farms, fertilization via waste management. 

Goals and Objectives: Elaborate, integrate public private partnerships with small and medium products, such as, foment the production/purchase of biodigesters and solar panels, as well as, engineering services for the construction of cisterns for irrigation. 
Deadline: There is no set. 

Final Objective: Sustainability, greater food supply for internal and external supply and the reduction of inequalities in the field, giving autonomy and profitability to small and medium farmers, as well as the cheapening of food in Brazil and in the world.

Implementation of Cisterns, Biodigesters and Solar Energy Panels for a self-managed farm in terms of electricity and water.
1. Cisterns: Water supply in response to droughts and alternative irrigation channel.
2. Biodigesters: Energy supply via Biogas from methane that generates energy. Still on Biodigesters, it would provide reusable water in case there is a need for an alternative for irrigation: It is worth mentioning that there is a lack of studies on how harmful or not this water is for this purpose (Coming from Biodigesters). To finalize the biodigesters part, the product and solid of his process, would be reverted in fertilizers
3. Small Solar Panels: the question would be for an electrical energy reinforcement for the farm to be fully compensated and self-managed
4. The suggestion is for irrigation and fertilization by small drones.
Impact & Results: There is no way to measure something that is in scope.

Project/Initiative Name: The Continuity of Higher Education in Brazil during the Covid-19 pandemic: Connected Students Project
Organisation: Ministry of Education - Secretariat of Higher Education
Description:
The pandemic had impacted the federal education network (69 universities and 40 technical institutes). The Ministry of Education (MEC) guided educational institutions through legislation that authorized the replacement of face-to-face classes with classes in digital media, except for medical courses that require internship and laboratory practices. The project's challenge was to quickly enable, through mobile data, access to the internet for students in a situation of socioeconomic vulnerability by sharing monthly data packages and monitoring the data consumption of each student.

Further, it was port of the mission of Connected Students to obtains economic gains of scale resulting from the negotiation with Brazilian telephony operators. Thanks to the dialogue with Brazilian cell phone operators Algar and Oi, the price was set at US$ 2.00 for a package of 20 Gigabytes, whereas this same data package negotiated individually by the student would be around US$ 20.00. Therefore, the result of the economy of scale is proven.

A federal educational institution guaranteed its participation in the Connected Students by sending the expression of interest to the National Teaching and Research Network (RNP). RNP later sent the Term of Adhesion to the federal educational institution, which included the rules and conditions for participation and had to be signed by the highest director of the educational institution. Then the federal educational institution publicly selected students in a situation of socioeconomic vulnerability who expressed an interest in obtaining the benefit.
Novelty: This innovation, thanks to the dialogue with the private sector, managed to provide students the means to connect and continue their education during the pandemic. This also meant pushing the private sector to develop a new product in the Brazilian market: mobile data packages, exclusively (no calls nor messages).
Impact & Results: The project, created in 2020, made it possible to grant a cell phone chip with an internet access package of up to 20 GB per month to 165,000 students in a condition of socioeconomic vulnerability. This action covered the entire Brazilian territory, allowing these students to develop and continue their academic activities outside the campus of their educational institution in the most severe periods of the new coronavirus pandemic (from January 2021 to June 2022).

Description:
With the help of AI, the platform automatically tags Belgian politician when they use their phone on the daily livestreams.

Every meeting of the flemish government in Belgium is live streamed on a YouTube channel. When a livestream starts the software is searching for phones and tries to identify a distracted politician. This is done with the help of AI and face recognition. The video of the distracted politician are then posted to a Twitter and Instagram account with the politician tagged.

The software is written in Python and is using machine learning to detect phones and facial recognition to identify the politician. The software started running on July 5, 2021. If there is no live meeting the software will check older uploaded videos on the YouTube channel.
Novelty: Use of AI for face and mobile device recognition for political accountability within the Flemish Parliament.
Impact & Results: No publicly available information about impacts.

Description:
The General Index is based in an underlying corpus to extract underlying content of articles that are not yet released. The General Index is available for free download with no restrictions on use. This is an initial release, and the hope is to improve the quality of text extraction, broaden the scope of the underlying corpus, provide more sophisticated metrics associated with terms, and other enhancements.

The General Index is an invaluable utility for researchers who wish to search for articles about plants, chemicals, genes, proteins, materials, geographical locations, and other entities of interest. The General Index allows scholars and students all over the world to perform specialized and customized searches within the scope of their disciplines and research over the full corpus.

The index doesn’t contain the full text of articles, but only sentence snippets up to five words long, releasing it does not breach publishers’ copyright restrictions on the reuse of paywalled articles.

Project/Initiative Name: The Largest Digital and Educational Inclusion Program of a Subnational Government
Description:
The entire population of the state of Minas Gerais, about 21 million inhabitants, in its 853 municipalities, will have access to the digital signal on Open TV by 2023. With 97% of homes with TV, it will be an impacting innovation in the access to information and content in an unequal country. And the highlight will be two exclusive channels for EDUCATION, seven days a week, 24 hours a day. With modern and innovative content, universality for learning and professional qualification will be guaranteed.
