The innovation is in this successful program with the totality of the digital signal and the guarantee of access to two exclusive channels for education, regardless of the municipality or social class of the people. It is certainly a state policy with impacting results in the short, medium, and long term.

The planning, work plan, and action plan are already underway. With financial resources guaranteed and tenders completed, the equipment is already being installed and internal and external meetings are underway to define partnerships with public and private entities for modern, attractive, and innovative content for the people of Minas Gerais. The goal is to partner with excellent content that, today, is restricted to access via internet platforms. And soon on broadcast TV, in almost all homes in Minas Gerais.
Novelty:
The program presented is innovative and with excellent prospects for satisfactory results, mainly because:
- The current globalized world and with a brutal technological development is very unfair when we analyze an unequal country like Brazil. The unlimited access to information offered by the internet divides the rich and the poor even more. Therefore, policies that enable access to the most needy of information are indispensable.
- There is no way to develop a country without quality education. While the most advantaged have ample opportunities, the poorest are limited, compromising their futures and that of the country. The initiative aims to make quality content accessible to all.
Impact & Results: The process of installing the equipment began just three months ago. According to the initial planning, by March 2023 this phase will be completed and equipment turned on. The content partners are already getting ready, with definitions of themes, periodicity, possibility of certification in some cases, and recording of classes.

Description:
The Markup citizens browser provide information on how Facebook's recommendation algorithm siloes information on the platform according to the users' preferences. Facebook’s recommendation algorithm shows different news, groups, and hashtags to different users. 

Using user data collected between December 2020 and June 2022, the dial compares how often the content was shown to one grouping over the other. They call this effort monitoring of algorithmic broadcasting to social media users.

For the pilot phase, a custom standalone desktop application was distributed to a panel. These panelists provided the platform with demographic information about themselves—gender, race, location, age, political leanings, and education level—and connected the Citizen Browser application to their personal Facebook accounts. The application periodically captures data from the panelists’ Facebook feeds.
Novelty: First effort for monitoring of algorithmic broadcasting to social media users.
Impact & Results: Several results can be accessed from here: https://www.niemanlab.org/2020/11/how-much-political-news-do-people-see-on-facebook-i-went-inside-173-peoples-feeds-to-find-out/

Organisation: National Infrastructures for Research and Technology (GRNET S.A)
Description:
The neighbourhood babysitters platform aims at matching available higher education graduates willing to work as babysitters with parents/families who search baby-sitters. Beneficiaries of the same neighbourhood, who meet specific conditions (i.e single parent families, low income families etc. ) have the right to apply for funding from the Neighbourhood Babysitters platform https://ntantades.gov.gr to cover part of the babysitter's service fee.

The platform has 3 modules: (a) the babysitter registration, (b) parents' registration and selection of curator, (c) application for a voucher if the family meets the subsidy conditions. The innovation of the application lies in the extensive use of interoperabilities of public organizations. When completed it will interoperate with over thirty (30) Information Systems of the public administration, indicatively: systems of the Ministry of Digital Governance, Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Education, Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Ministry of Health, Ministry of Justice etc.

This innovation strongly focuses on gender balance as it aims to promote equality between men and women in employment by harmonizing the professional and family life of working or job-seeking parents and supporting the access and position of women in the labor market.
Novelty:
The project is an innovation in many ways:
1) The platform was developed as an open software application and the code is available at gitlab. It can be reused as it is and easily customised, to meet the needs of any other public or private organization that develops similar services.
2) In its current version, it uses eleven web services provided by public agencies, ministries, public health agencies, etc.
In its final version, it will make extensive use of the web services offered by almost the entire spectrum of the public sector, e.g. public organizations and ministries of digital governance, education, health, labour, justice, foreign affairs etc.
Impact & Results: Since June 2022, when the first module was launched, 679 babysitters have enrolled in the platform. The qualitative assessment will be based on written and oral reports of all involved stakeholders (babysitters, parents, digital and printed press, etc.) but its results are not available yet.

Project/Initiative Name: The Public Employee Satisfaction and Engagement Survey (KAMU-VERI)
Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description: KAMU-VERI is a Public Employee Satisfaction and Engagement Survey that was developed to collect public employees’ opinions and suggestions on current human resources processes in their institutions. KAMU-VERI is a scientific and contemporary tool that all the components of which ranging from data collection to data analysis, are originally developed and assessed by the Office, itself.
Novelty: KAMU-VERI is developed to collect public employees’ opinions and suggestions on current human resources processes in their institutions as well as their satisfaction and loyalty to their profession and institution. KAMU-VERI is a scientific and contemporary tool for surveying personal data all the components of which ranging from data collection to data analysis, are originally developed and assessed by the Presidency of the Republic of Turkiye Human Resources Office, itself. When this data collection tool is compared to its counterparts in the world, it has surpassed its peers with the number of participants and the data volume obtained. As a result of all these studies, the Human Resources Office provided the opportunity to obtain data from the perspectives of citizens, academics/researchers, the private sector and other countries' public sectors.
Impact & Results:
KAMU-VERI Survey achieved the followings:

- It was implemented for the first time in Türkiye and surpassed its peers with the number and rate of participants.
- KAMU-VERI results has been analysed on basis of institutions. 
- It was used by the Office in order to:
   -Eliminate the lack of current human resources management practices in the public sector,
   -Organize the human resources units of the institutions to meet the needs,
   -Create and develop the human resources staff that will perform the function,
   -Develop a system where human resources practices in institutions can be monitored online and instantly,
  -Prepare a Reference Human Resources Management Model which will serve as a guide for the public institutions in the transition to modern human resources management.
- Cutting-edge human resources digital platforms such as Distance Learning Gate and Career Gate has been developed and disseminated in the public sector by the Office.

Description: The Pudding's audio register of Mexico's City streets is an audio/visual story exploring the sounds of the streets. They explore the sonic code of it. They aim to make visible their impact on the city, as well as to shed light on their experiences and their presence - an estimated 800,000 “vendedores ambulantes” (roughly translated as wandering merchants or peddlers) currently make their living on the streets and sidewalks of Mexico City.
Novelty:
Voice and sound recorder to explore the diversity of Mexico City. 
Data journalism and visibilization of vulnerable urban communities

Project/Initiative Name: The Right to Data Portability in the GDPR
Description:
The right to data portability is one of the most important novelties within the EU General Data Protection Regulation, both in terms of warranting control rights to data subjects and in terms of being found at the intersection between data protection and other fields of law (competition law, intellectual property, consumer protection, etc.). 

It creates a valuable case of development and diffusion of effective user-centric privacy enhancing technologies and a first tool to allow individuals to enjoy the immaterial wealth of their personal data in the data economy. 

The right to data portability can be approximated under two different perspectives: the minimalist approach (the adieu scenario) and the empowering approach (the fusing scenario), which the authors consider highly preferable. The most significant innovation here is the concept of "the right to be forgotten" to control the erase of personal data from the digital space.
Novelty: Incorporates several citizens "rights" to regain control of their data and privacy in the digital ecosystem/space.

Description:
TraceTogether is a digital solution that facilitates community-driven contact tracing through the logging of encounters between users via Bluetooth technology. The app does not rely on the user’s memory and works with unacquainted contacts who are also TraceTogether programme participants. It therefore attempts to plug the gap of close contact with unacquainted contacts, by recording who you have been in contact with, but not where so the privacy of users remains protected.

The mobile application's key features include: 
- Bluetooth technology: connecting Bluetooth-enabled devices to arrive at estimates of the proximity and duration of a user’s contact with other users.
- It also approximates the duration of contact by interpolating successive communications between devices. The more often two devices communicate with each other, the longer the users are likely to have been within each other’s vicinity.
- Data security and privacy: the data is anonymised, encrypted and stored only on users’ devices, and uploaded by the user to share with the Ministry of Health, which is automatically deleted after 25 days. 

The contact tracing app also incorporates an AI-powered thermometer to provide an integrated screening and reporting option. The app could screen up to 30 people a minute in the vicinity. Moreover, since not everyone has access to mobile devices, the Ministry of Health has developed physical tokens that function in a similar manner. Users have the option to use the token which has embedded Bluetooth technology to send and detect signals to estimate a user-s contact proximity. The battery is designed to run for 6-9 months without charging.
Novelty: A digital App and physical token-based solution that facilitates community-driven contact tracing through the logging of encounters between users via Bluetooth technology
Impact & Results:
The use of digital contact tracing enabled the government to go from taking an average of 2-3 days to do activity-mapping, to issuing a quarantine order in about one day.

Post-launch, over 60 countries or cities have indicated interest in adopting a similar solution or learning from the team, with Australia, Canada, and Poland adopting the open-source version of TraceTogether.
At present, over 99% of people in Singapore are currently using TraceTogether to help stop the spread of COVID-19.

Project/Initiative Name: Tracka - to enable citizens to follow up on budgetary capital expenditure
Description:
Tracka is a community of active citizens tracking the implementation
of government projects at the community level to ensure service deliver in a three-step methodology: 1) project/expenditure identification, 2) following and tracking of projects, 3) citizens engagement. 

The aim is to address critical issues surrounding social and economic development in Nigeria, specifically the lack of citizens inclusion in developmental projects, an ever-increasing number of abandoned projects and poor execution of the few projects implemented. Moreover, Tracka’s goal is to expand citizens’ understanding of the budget, in particular on the contextual issues affecting them, to raise demand for citizen information about government budgets and budget implementation.

Initially launched in 2014, nowadays the innovation has incorporated a chatbot to guide users through the results and, as well, has updated its methodology. It has presence in 586 local government across 26 states in Nigeria. So far they have tracked over 15,859 projects in 7,589 towns and organised over 7,612 town hall meetings across the country. They plan to scale to all the 36 states of the federation.
Novelty: Budget review across different levels of government in Nigeria, plus a chatbot and method to approach citizens to the information

Project/Initiative Name: TransCoder - Deep learning to translate between programming languages
Description:
TransCoder is an entirely self-supervised neural transcompiler system that can make code migration easier and more efficient. This is the first AI system able to translate code from one programming language to another without requiring parallel data for training.

Meta's AI neural transcompiler is a system that converts code from one high-level programming language like C++, Java, and Python into another. It’s unsupervised, meaning it looks for previously undetected patterns in data sets without labels and with a minimal amount of human supervision, and it reportedly outperforms rule-based baselines by a “significant” margin and had high computational accuracy above 65%-90% depending on the programming language. 

TransCoder relies exclusively on source code written in just one programming language, rather than requiring examples of the same code in both the source and target languages. It requires no expertise in the programming languages, and it is easy to generalize TransCoder’s approach to additional programming languages. In this sense, this could be useful for updating legacy codebases to modern programming languages, which are typically more efficient and easier to maintain.
Novelty: First entirely self-supervised neural transcompiler system that can make code migration easier and more efficient.
Impact & Results: In our evaluations, the model correctly translates more than 90 percent of Java functions to C++, 74.8 percent of C++ functions to Java, and 68.7 percent of functions from Java to Python. In comparison, a commercially available tool translates only 61.0 percent of functions correctly from C++ to Java, and an open source translator is accurate for only 38.3 percent of Java functions translated into C++.

Project/Initiative Name: TTSH microlearning platform to provide bite-sized training modules to hospital staff
Description:
The microlearning platform hosts bite-sized training modules on Unleap (this platform currently no longer available) of about 15 minutes that TTSH's staff can complete on-the-go. 
Around 80% of TTSH courses were conducted face-to-face. The platform halved this number to 40% and quickly pushed out new modules and address pressing challenges such as employee burnout. It comprises of five modules that teach doctors and officers how to manage suicidal ideas and difficult situations, and use active listening to provide emotional support. Additionally, it provides quick training for nurses in dealing with violent and aggressive patients. 

Amid of the pandemic the microlearning platform provided quick guideline and training on changing procedures (i.e. how to don and remove personal protectice equipment). Content is created by doctors within the relevant departments and each content is supervised by the Department's health check.
Novelty:
- time reduction of learning sessions to 15 minute
- from 70% to 40% was the reduction of training programs in TTSH
- addresses mental health issues related to the medical service provision during the pandemic and provides  relevant information "on the go" that also changes quickly

Description:
The Global Assembly is a UN citizens-led initiative consisting in two assemblies: 

1. Core Assembly: a proportionally representative group of 100 people from around the world; a snapshot of the planet's population. This group will learn about the climate and ecological crisis, be facilitated in deep deliberation, decide what they think should happen, and present proposals at the United Nations' COP26 climate conference in November 2021.

2. Community Assemblies: people all over the world can run their own Community Assembly, using a step-by-step toolkit. They can be run in any community, such as workplaces, or schools and its decisions will contribute to a Global Assembly report in 2022.

Moreover, the The Global Assembly wiki contains all of the content from the Community Assembly Toolkit and the Information Booklet. Communities across the world will be translating these resources into multiple languages, so as many citizens as possible can use them. These translations will be openly available on the UN wiki.
Novelty: Global citizens assembly to provide opinions, solutions and political statements about the climate crisis to be up taken to UN's general assembly and COP26.

Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description:
Uni-Veri is a national project evaluating university graduates' labor market performances by field of study. The project aims to analyze school-to-work transition and to guide policy recommendations to strengthen the structure of human capital in Türkiye by using administrative micro data sets. Moreover, it helps students to make informed decisions about their careers before enrolling in a tertiary education program and provides information to professionals in the education system.

UNI-VERI project contributes to efforts to strengthen human capital accumulation and human resource planning in Türkiye by providing six key indicators of the labor market performances of the new graduates. In that respect, administrative micro data sets are first anonymized and then analyzed using various statistical methods and modern tools provided by data science. These six indicators listed below: 
-         Entry Level Wage Distribution
-         Duration Until First Employment
-         Skill Mismatch
-         Employment in Public Sector
-         Sectoral Distribution
-         Firm Size

UNI-VERI aims to help professionals, policymakers, high school students (who are at the stage of choosing a university major) and university students by analyzing the school-to-work transition of new graduates and providing data-driven results to its target audience. Using these results, young people can choose their majors based on evidence and know how employers will value them after graduation. Furthermore, policymakers may use UNI-VERI results to determine the quotas for higher education programs.
Novelty: The UNI-VERI project was the first project that used the higher education and employment information of almost 1.5 million graduates in Türkiye. By matching administrative data sets, UNI-VERI achieved the most comprehensive education-labor data. UNI-VERI also enables its users to compare the 81 department groups in key performance indicators, thus helping high school students who are indecisive in choosing their university departments. Furthermore, UNI-VERI was the first project that presented the indicator of skill mismatch on such a large scale. Therefore, the match between higher education attainments and the expectations and labor market needs can also be seen with the skill mismatch indicator in the UNI-VERI project.
Impact & Results: The website of the UNI-VERI has been visited approximately 1 million times, and it contributes to many users’ decision-making by providing data-driven results, thus contributing to Türkiye’s strategic human resource planning to be more productive in the future. Furthermore, the results of UNI-VERI for universities were also made available on another platform where counselling and guidance teachers get evidence-based information on departments and universities. Thus, UNI-VERI supported students in their decision-making process with more detailed information.

Description:
The Unified System for Public Procedures (SUT) is the official repository of administrative procedures and services provided exclusively by Peruvian public entities from the national level.

It is a web-based IT tool of mandatory implementation for State entities that allows registering, integrating and optimizing the processes for the elaboration, approval and publication of the Single Text of Administrative Procedures (TUPA).

The SUT improves the predictability, efficiency and timeliness of administrative procedures and exclusive services and, thus, the quality of service to citizens. It is of easy access to every Peruvian public servant and incorporates step-by-step information, for example, to register procurement orders or to conduct tailored impact regulatory analysis to certain procedures.
Novelty: First of its kind as an official repository of administrative procedures in Peruvian public sector.
Impact & Results: >100 public entities from the national level are on board

Description:
The Unified Tech Fund is the first and a novel attempt of NHS to set out clearly the funding available while have a single approach to bidding for it among healthcare and science-related researchers. It is a technology-based fund and it is available to Integrated Care Systems. 

The initiative brought together several national technology funds. This amounts to £938 million available to NHS organisations throughout the financial year 2021 to 2022.

The funding is intended to clarify how the funding process will work and what a researcher needs to do to bid. All of this intends to provide clarity to those who are applying. The information obtained on its site is for financial year 2021 and ahead.
Novelty: Integration of technology-based funds

Project/Initiative Name: United for Smart Sustainable Cities (U4SSC) Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for Smart Sustainable Cities
Organisation: International Telecommunication Union – the UN specialized agency responsible for ICTs
Description:
The U4SSC Key Performance Indicators initiative is a consistent and standardized method for cities to collect the necessary data to measure performance and progress which is supported by 18 UN Agencies. These indicators have been formulated to provide cities and communities with a consistent and standardized method to collect and report the data needed to quantify, measure, report and monitor performance and progress towards achieving the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), becoming a smarter city, and becoming a more sustainable city. 

There are 91 KPIs that cities can measure and report. Each KPI falls under one of three dimensions of smartness and sustainability in a city: Economy, Environment, Society and Culture. Each of these dimensions provides a separate view of progress in its area, and when reported together, the dimensions provide a holistic yet comprehensive view of a smart sustainable city. 

The KPIs can be used to baseline and benchmark a city’s performance to help track its year-over-year progress; perform longer term trend analysis; and benchmark its performance to strategic goals. Over 100 cities worldwide are already implementing the U4SSC KPIs, including Dubai, Singapore, Riyadh, Santa Fe, Moscow, Valencia, Pully, Ålesund, Kristiansand and Bizerte, with many more aspiring smart cities interested in the initiative.
Novelty: The U4SSC supports cities on their digital transformation journey to meet their goals and targets and create the cities and communities of tomorrow. The U4SSC KPI program has developed a complete program to assist cities with implementing the KPIs, verifying the KPI data through an independent verification process, and providing cities with benchmarking visuals that show progress to meeting the SDGs and versus the performance of leading cities.
Impact & Results: Over 100 cities worldwide are already implementing the U4SSC KPIs, including Dubai, Singapore, Riyadh, Santa Fe, Moscow, Valencia, Pully, Ålesund, Kristiansand and Bizerte, with many more aspiring smart cities interested in the initiative. Public reports are available on the U4SSC website.

Project/Initiative Name: University Career Centers Supported with Talent Gate Digital Career Platform
Organisation: Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office
Description: Establishment and strengthening of University Career Centers project has been initiated by the Presidency of the Republic of Türkiye Human Resources Office with the aim to provide equal opportunity in every region of the country and to increase students’/graduates’ career awareness, school-to-work transition and employability. UCCs are provided with trainings, coordination meetings, a handbook, a digital career platform (Talent Gate) and many other projects to increase their capacities.
Novelty: The project is unique as the establishment and consolidation of all University Career Centers (UCCs) in all universities have been coordinated by a central government authority (the Office) to provide equal career services in all universities. Thus, university administrations noticed their importance and started taking the necessary steps within a quite short time to strengthen them.
Impact & Results: Fully equipped career centers are established in all universities in a short period of time

Description:
Visor Urbano, a digital platform for land management that allows the processing of building permits and business licenses. It makes public information available to citizens in a simple, transparent and free way. Its objective is to fight corruption and make public information on urban settlements transparent, highlighting any irregular construction or commerce, giving people tools to participate in the development of their city.

Visor Urbano solves licensing challenges by facilitating the complete management of business and construction licenses digitally, eliminating corruption and providing citizens with the information they need to make better decisions. 

The platform has benefits for both governments and citizens. For public servants, it is a system that serves to serve people in a more agile way when they go to the offices to carry out their procedures, organising and systematising their documents and information for a better search and safeguarding of data, creating a digital file. 

It allows citizens to identify the official list of requirements for opening new businesses, a catalogue with all the government programmes and support to start or promote their businesses, and to carry out a commercial analysis that provides information on existing businesses and locate the most competitive area in their vicinity.
Novelty:
Digitise the cadastral information of the city's properties, even available in 3D, for free consultation by citizens. 
- 3D mapping of more than 250 hectares of Guadalajara's historic centre, making it the largest in Latin America. 
- To make available the zoning and land use information of Guadalajara. 
- Georeferencing of construction and business licenses. 
- Completely online management of procedures (land use report, issuance of business and construction licenses).
Impact & Results:
Visor Urbano reduced bribe requests by municipal officials by 74%, reduced bribe requests from illegal intermediaries by 85%, and optimised the time taken to issue licences by 84%.
Likewise, with the digitalisation and systematisation of construction and business procedures, physical contact between officials and public servants, which was conducive to corruption, was reduced by 80%.
Through Visor Urbano, nearly 180,000 business licenses and 60,000 construction licenses have been geo-referenced, and more than 40,000 technical reports on the Urban Development Plans of the municipalities of the Metropolitan Area have been downloaded free of charge.

Description:
Urbem is a cloud-based software platform to digitise online procedures and services for the public audience. It helps to implement government agencies processes in a short time and at low cost. It is ideal for any organisation that offers procedures or services to the general public, recommended and ready to use especially in Public Notaries and Local or State Government outlets. 

It provides the following features: 
- Online payment of taxes and services: Offer citizens the option to pay online (by card, transfer or self-service payment). Connect with the citizen's information systems to automate the allocation of costs and discounts. They will be able to link and reconcile payments with their accounting system.
- Online consulting: Provide security by enabling remote consulting and eliminating travel. Allows the client's to schedule a video call at scheduled times, to guide them through the next steps of the process they wish to carry out.
- Business permits and certificates: Request all the information a citizen requires through an online form and speed up the digital review of the documents provided. Provide the option of online payment and extend a QR code that provides the necessary information to the auditors.
- Company Incorporation: Provides a comprehensive, secure and streamlined service. It maximises the use of the single file to be more efficient in the use of the information and/or documentation required to draw up and design the bylaws, notarise the articles of incorporation, as well as register the company with the Public Registry of Property.

As an additional feature, it may include an artificial intelligence chatbot in conversations with the audience on social media and on the city's or public institution's website.
Novelty: It is a cloud-based software platform to digitise online procedures and services for the public audience.
Impact & Results: Case of impact: https://blog.civica.digital/sam-petrino-chatbot-gana-premio-a-innovacion-2020-8aca354f683c

Project/Initiative Name: USDR American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) Grant Report
Description:
The USDR ARPA Grant Reporting Tool is an excel-based tool suite that easily and intuitively guides users through complex reporting, designed specifically to address the requirements in United States federal grants guidance. Validation routines embedded in the tool assure error-free submission to a central grants administrator. The tool automates data management, validation and aggregation, producing the exact format and content required for upload to the U.S. Federal reporting system.

Used today in Ohio, Rhode Island, Kentucky, Oregon and Connecticut, the tool has transformed their federal grant reporting. The tool can be scaled to any size government entity and tailored to its needs within days. All of the beneficiaries - citizens in need, state and local government, federal government - benefit from time saved, increased accuracy and lowered risk, and money saved and more people served.

U.S. Digital Response is continuing to innovate in partnership with state and local governments to address immediate challenges with grants reporting and ARPA. The same approach can be replicated across other complex federal grants programs, such as programs included in the recent infrastructure laws, with USDR involvement. At scale, the relationships USDR has developed both with state and local governments, as well as the federal government owners of grants programs can help shift the same methodology into the federal government to achieve the ultimate scale in the United States - all federal grants programs with reporting requirements.
Novelty: The tool and its approach are innovative because it (1) is free of charge to government (2) provides a fast, easy-to-use, complete toolset for error-free reporting from program managers and government accountants; (3) is excel-based and simple to implement, requiring no IT investment; (4) has been built and enhanced in collaboration with government partners; and (5) demonstrates a technology approach that is extensible to any complex grant program requiring reporting on expenditures, performance, outcomes and impact.
Impact & Results:
As of the date of submission in 2022, U.S. Digital Response has worked with 22 state and local jurisdictions in the United States to implement federal grants management tools. The validation is now centrally hosted by U.S. Digital Response with governments retaining all control of their own information. Three main positive results can be found: 
1.  Time Saved
2. Accuracy Boosted, Risk Lowered. 
3. Money Saved and People Served.

Project/Initiative Name: Using Checklists and Audit Tools to Improve Care in Hemodialysis Facilities
Description:
At the hemodialysis Unit of 'P. & A. Kyriakou' Children's Hospital, an infection control program is implemented based on the use of Checklists and Compliance Tools (Audit Tools). The use of Control Tools reduces morbidity and mortality and contributes decisively to the prevention of Hospital Infections. At the same time, it creates a culture of safety for patients and staff.

The surveillance data revealed the picture of the practices already applied, as well as their possible differences from the scientifically documented ones. The results were communicated to the staff, existing limitations and shortcomings were discussed, evaluated and subsequently, a relevant training program was implemented in order to improve compliance with good practices and achieve the desired standardization of procedures.

The program was carried out in collaboration with the Hellenic Nephrology Association of Nurses (ENEN). The experience and know-how of each side were deposited and a field of joint action and application of international guidelines in clinical practice was sought, with the aim of providing optimal health care to hemodialysis patients.
Novelty: The project is innovative because it improves efficiency. The standardization of the process ensures that all the elements and actions mentioned have been correctly executed. The reduction of errors caused by lack of up-to-date knowledge and inconsistency. Lastly, the project increases patient safety by creating a greater sense of confidence that the process has been completed accurately and thoroughly.
Impact & Results: From the evaluation of the results, it emerged that the compliance with the good practices was: 98% when connecting the hemodialysis catheter, 97% when disconnecting the hemodialysis catheter, 87% during hemodialysis catheter exit point care. No more results available.

Project/Initiative Name: Voluntary Expansion of Calorie Labeling on Alcohol Initiative (VECLAI)
Organisation: Ministry of Food and Drug Safety
Description:
Voluntary Expansion of Calorie Labeling on Alcohol Initiative (VECLAI) is to provide Korean consumers with calorie information of alcohol, and alcohol producers or importers with practical way to reach to labeling calorie information on their alcohol products. 

To provide consumers with calorie information of alcohol, instead of downward regulating from government to industry, this time we found a way of multilateral cooperation amid government, industry and consumer organization. This will make consumers help choose proper products for them. Besides, VECLAI aids alcohol producers or importers to set plans to label calorie information considering their own packaging material inventory situations.

After 2025, Korean consumers will be able to compare and select their preferable alcohol products with calorie information on them. This is because more than 72% of circulating alcohol products will bear calorie information.

Four parts of government, industry, experts and consumers gathered and committed to communicating and making progress for 9 months to lead to VECLAI. Each sector tried to find the most reasonable way to please everyone without ones side's sacrifice.
Novelty:
VECLAI is an innovation in government policies because:
1) It represents new way that we can solve a problem based only with regulating industry, but also with understanding, cooperation and trust. It made alcohol producer and importers voluntarily join the initiative.
2) It does not burden minor producers nor importers which doesn’t join the VECLAI. However, it’s certain that they will be motivated by the VECLAI to try to label their products better to be chosen by consumers.
3) Government, industry and consumer organization, each part has its own role in the VECLAI. Three parts will cooperate, check and support each other to get outcome.
Impact & Results: Government, industry and consumer organization has signed multilateral Memorandom of understanding on VECLAI on 7th Sep. 2022. According to present predictions, 72% of circulating alcohol products will bear calories on them until 2025 -- only 6 products which is almost 0% of alcohol have been labeled since 2017.

Description:
The Western Cape Government Contact Centre (WCG-CC) is the first point of contact for citizens, providing access across 14 channels – Call Centre, Fax, SMS, Please Call me, Walk-in-Centre, Snail Mail, E-mail centre, Presidential Hotline, 4 Social media channels, eService portal and the First Thursday programme – for all provincial departments. The Covid-19 pandemic led to a hugely increased demand for WCG-CC services. With more than 1 500 patients being diagnosed daily during the first wave of the pandemic, the WCG Department of Health and Wellness (DOHW) struggled to contact all Covid positive cases daily. The WCG-CC was subsequently used to provide telephonic care to patients, leading to the first large scale telehealth initiative in the province – the Covid Track and Trace, Quarantine and Isolation (TTQI) programme, a critical part of the response to the outbreak in the province. The WCG-CC had to rapidly adopt new processes to support the provincial telehealth initiatives. It extended its operating hours, from 08h00 until 16h00 on weekdays to being operational 7 days a week – 08h00 to 20h00 on weekdays, 08h00 to 16h00 over weekends and Public holidays. Further, the new processes were mapped into its Citizen Relationship Management (CRM) System and daily meetings between the WCG-CC and DOHW officials were held to manage the caseload and identify and address challenges. These interactions enabled the WCG-CC to improve and refine processes over time. The WCG-CC was migrated to a cloud-based connectivity solution to allow for scaling up of agent resources from 25 to 125 and to provide a work-from-home solution so that agents could socially distance themselves.

As a result of the project, the WCG-CC could effectively:
• provide accurate information and a timely response to citizens and Covid patients, recognizing when patients were struggling and routing them to medical assistance and care, reducing its query resolution targets significantly
• ensure that agents were competent by providing onboarding regular CRM and business-specific training in partnership with the DOHW
• deal sensitively and caringly with grieving – agents were given specific training for this, and support programmes were put in place to assist agents with managing the emotional toll the work took
• enable an innovative cloud-based connectivity system that could easily scale up to support more resources
Novelty: This project provides a benchmark to the WCG and DOHW (Department of health and wellness) of the speed at which a new project can be developed and implemented as the same principles and approach could be applied to future telehealth services, such as Tuberculosis, HIV and other disease and patient management initiatives. As telehealth services is likely to be increasingly important in SA, the project has enabled us to make major strides towards ensuring that a larger base of the population can have access to healthcare, which while different, is no less effective than the traditional way of seeking care by visiting a health facility.
Impact & Results:
To date, the WCG-CC handled over 400 000 COVID cases, with working continuing. Its work on the Track and Trace programme and other health initiatives had a significant impact by freeing up medical resources to deal with citizens requiring medical assistance and undoubtedly directly contributed to saving lives. Further, the work with DOHW has proved so successful that the department is now exploring other telemedicine/telehealth interventions.

The WCG Contact Centre entered the Provincial Service Excellence Awards in 2021, and it was recognized as one of the top 5 projects under the category: Lead Programme or Project.

Project/Initiative Name: Whole-of-Government Application Analytics (WOGAA) - Improve Government Services with Data in One-Stop
Description:
WOGAA is a groundbreaking project that allows the tracking of Singapore Government-wide digital services. Launched in 2017 under the Smart Nation plan, WOGAA offers real-time monitoring of user interactions with government digital services. As of 2021, WOGAA has tracked over ~ 1.1 billion visits to 104 agencies. 

With WOGAA, public service can identify gaps in digital services delivery holistically, and ensure that public informational needs and user experiences are optimised and prioritised.

By integrating just two lines of JavaScript code onto agencies’ websites, WOGAA provides an end-to-end toolbox for officers to monitor and maintain government digital services holistically.
Novelty:
WOGAA’s uniqueness: Its engineering capabilities and strategy of monitoring over 1,100 websites in a centralised WOG platform (a number unheard of in the private sector, where a company typically manages fewer than 10 websites), transforming traditional manual data collection and analysis work into an efficient and effective workflow by leveraging state-of-art technologies.

In traditional web analytics, agencies will just get a single aspect of the overall digital footprints of their users and manage multiple different analytics platforms/software to get the overall performance of their digital services. 

WOGAA provides an end-to-end toolbox for public officers to monitor and maintain government digital services holistically, all in one package.
Impact & Results:
WOGAA now serves 104 agencies, tracking and monitoring 1,146 websites, 2,725 Transactional services, and 46 mobile applications.

All government agencies implement the same web analytics solution, and the data can be viewed easily through a centralised dashboard. This saves each public agency SGD 325,000 a year, which is the cost of building a similar tool.

Organisation: La Marocaine des E-services (Ministry of Interior, Directorate General of Local Authorities)
Description:
“Wraqi” is a Moroccan "endogenous" and innovative solution that first allows citizens and businesses access to over 2000 public notary offices remotely via a mobile application. It uses a set of technologies to guarantee identity, protect documents and sign them electronically. This solution allowed Moroccans to complete 55+ million signature notaries and 80+ million document copy certifications remotely. 

Wraqi is able to: eliminate the need to be physically present, eliminate the chances of fraud or corruption, eliminate or reduce the need for printing documents, eliminate the need for distribution and use of stamps on documents, eliminate the need to manage cash in public offices, and give all parties involved a digital tool to use for KPIs, billing, tax purposes. 

Wraqi is now in 4 offices and waiting to be deployed in all 2000 offices as well as Moroccan consulates. Wraqi has contracts with the Ministry of Interior, the Ministry of Foreign Affaires as well as the Customs Administration. It is developed as a public-private partnership and, for it, the government does not allocate funds. The private provide public administrations with this service for free. Citizen-users are asked to pay a small fee per each transaction (10 Moroccan Dirhams).
Novelty:
Wraqi is innovative in many ways:
1. Local solution: Digitizing public notary services was not on the government action plan. Morocco like any developing country was importing solutions from the West. This is made by Moroccans for Moroccans and can be adopted by other countries.
2. Pushed regulatory changes: Laws had to be changed to make it possible for users to benefit from Wraqi
3. Advanced technology: The app uses Blockchain, Biometrics, Cloud Computing, IoT and AI to provide all its services
4. Bottom-up: A bottom-up approach to e-Gov instead of top down. 
5. Business model: The solution is free for governments and the end users pay only if they use the app remotely. If they come to the government office, they use Wraqi for free
Impact & Results:
The innovation is not fully implemented yet, so minor results can be found. A major impact we observed while working with the administrative agents during our first pilot site is how quickly they were able to adapt to the technological aspect introduced by the innovation knowing that their only prior experience is handling the procedures with pen and paper, thus becoming the ambassadors to “Wraqi”, advertising the platform to citizens and other offices.
The positive results that can be identified up to now can are, for instance, the high number of people that registered to the platform without grand-scale advertising, and the fact that is it likely that all the administrative annexes will adopt our solution and most people will be using it, especially companies to do their paperwork. A good indicator of that is the current statistics related to the currently deployed pilots in Casablanca: more than 7600 citizens have registered to Wraqi, 5500 of which completed their registration by depositing their signature at the notary office. Also, more than 11000 documents have been processed through the platform.

Project/Initiative Name: Xonai - Democratizing access to accelerated computing for data processing platforms
Description:
XONAI goals are to build a Universal Compute Fabric (UCF) and to democratize access to accelerated computing for data processing platforms. The innovation aims to 1) solve the current data platform issues - proprietary data platforms are exceedingly intrusive, 2) incorporate a new paradigm in the provision of such services - premium and instant optimization is achieved at no expense/taxed to leverage new technologies. 

Their approach is three-fold: 
- Seamless integration: Rather than pushing users into invasive, intricate and time-consuming adoption processes, the solution is built from the groundup to power the underlying data infrastructure as it stands, and provide optimization opportunities not possible in legacy proprietary platforms.
- End-to-end optimization: decoupling optimization process from the compute platforms such technologies supports, achieving optimal execution through end-to-end optimization of data pipelines and intelligent selection of hardware resources.
- Scalable technology: tackle the current problems on a fundamental level by leveraging novel compiler technologies to build scalable solutions to maximize the performance of demanding workloads.
Novelty: Algorithmic technology to provide a universal computer fabric to democratize access to accelerated computing for data processing.
Impact & Results: No visible impacts.
